raw_text: ABBOTT, ARTHUR WILLIAM.—B. 1893; ed. Blundell's; entd. Crown Agents' office after compet. exam., Oct., 1912; asst. head, finance dept., 1929; dep. head, 1933; asst. sec., E. African currency bd., 1922; sec., 1930.
ABBOTT, CHARLES, Barrister-at-law (Gray's Inn).—Res. mag., Kenya, Feb., 1930; crown coun., Oct., 1930; pres. dist. ct., Cyprus, Sept., 1932.

ABBOTT, MYLES JOHN.—B. 1906; ed. King's Sch., Canterbury; admitted solr. (Eng.), Oct., 1929; 2nd lieut., 9th Bn., Middx. Regt. (T.A.), Mar., 1933; lieut., Mar., 1936; junior legal offr., and asst. land offr. and D.R.M., Hong Kong, Mar., 1936; asst. crown solr., in addn. Mar., 1936.
ABDUL HADI, RUII BRY, O.B.E.—B. 1885; held various offices in the Turkish diplomatic service, 1905-20; admve. offr., Palestine, 1920; asst. sec., 1930.

ABRAHAM, ARTHUR ALEXANDER.—B. 1883; agrl. instr., Br. Guiana, 1906; snr. do., 1916; horticultural supt., 1918; sec., B.G.sec., Br. Empire Exhibn., Wembley, 1924; asst. supt., botanic gardens, 1928; asst. agrl. supt., 1930; agrl. supt., rel. comm., Mar., 1920; ag. supt., topo. br., June, 1927; ag. supt., surveys, Penang Feb., 1928; Empire conf., survey offr., Southampton and London, July, 1928; sen. asst. supdt., topo. br., Jan., 1929; Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. Medal, Nov., 1929; senr. asst. supt., topo. br., May to Dec., 1930; do., survey dept., F.M.S. and S.S., Feb., 1932; supt., survey dept., S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1933.

ABRAHAM, JOHN CONRAD, M.A. (Oxon.), M.B.E. (1918).—B.1889; ed. Keswick and Wadham Coll.; Oxford; ast. res., Nyasaland, 1911; O.C. Details, Fort Johnston; attd. 1st K.A.R., 1914; sp. serv., carrier transport, N.F.F., 1915-18; men. in despa., three times; admin. offr., 1st gde., 1923; ag.asst.ch. sec., Nov., 1926; N. Nyasa native reserves comm., 1928; sp. duty, Tanganyika, Nov., 1929; ast. sec., native affrs., Nyasaland, Jan., 1931; ast. ch. sec., July, 1931; chmn. native languages bd., 1931; supt., census, 1931; ag. ch. sec., May-Nov., 1932; gov.'s dep., July, 1932; senr. prov. commr., Nov. 1933; nom. mem., exec. and leg. couns.

ABRAHAMS, Sir SIDNEY SOLOMON, Kt. Bach. (1936), N.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1885; ed. Bedford Modern Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Middle Temp., June, 1909; Midland circuit; town mag., Zanzibar, June, 1915; crown counsel, Kenya, Nov., 1915, but did not proceed; seconded for serv. with Mesopotamia civil adminstr., Jan., 1920; advoc. gen. and registr. of companies, Baghdad, Apr., 1920; pres., ct. of first instance, ct. of sessions and ct. of criminal appl., Basrah, Jan., 1921; atty.-gen., Zanzibar, June, 1922; atty.-gen., Uganda, Jan., 1925; atty.-gen., Gold Coast, 1928; ch. just., Uganda, Feb., 1933; do., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1934; do., Ceylon, 1936; joint edr., Zanzibar law repts., 1868-1918; sole edr., Zanzibar law repts., 1918-22; 2nd cls. Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1924.

ABRAMSON, MAJOR ALBERT, C.B.E. (1927), O.B.E.—B. 1876; war serv. with E.E.F.; O.B.E., 1918; twice ment. in desps.; mily. gov., Hebron, 1918; chmn., land comm., and dir., land dept., 1920; sh. br. rep., Transjordan, 1921; dist. gov., southern dist., 1922; dist. comsrr., northern dist., 1925; comsrr., lands, 1927.

ACHESON, A. B.—B. 1895; ed., Dulwich Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1913); on mil. service, Rifle Brigade and Machine Gun Corps, 1914-19; served in France, Belgium and Germany; ment. in desps., Croix-de-Guerre; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; sec. to comte., on the development of scientific training and research, 19th Nov., 1920; sec. to comte., on trade and taxation for Br. W. Africa, Oct. to Dec., 1921; priv. sec. to permanent under-secret., 3rd Oct., 1923; ag. prin., Jan., 1926.
ADAMS, CYRIL OCTAVIUS.—B. 1907; ed., Sherborne; sub. inspr., Jamaica constab., 1930; 3rd cls. inspr., 1932; asst. comsrr., pol., Nigeria, 1935.
ADAMS, F.V., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.).—B. 1904; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1928.

ADAMS, G. H., A.S.A.A.—B. 1891; C.A. office, Nov., 1911; acctnt., Nyasaland, July, 1915; cashier, 1915; asst. comsrr., income tax, 1921; senr. asst. treas., Apr., 1925; ag. dep. treas., in 1921, 1925 and 1927; dep. treas., Oct., 1929; ag. treas., May to Dec., 1930; dep. treas., Palestine, 1933; mem., rly. bd., road bd., harbr. bd., cent. town planning comm., wages comte., 1935; ag. treas., mem., exec. and advisory couns., currency offr., June-Oct., 1934.

ADAMS, JAMES WILMOT, F.R.C.S. (Eng.), B.A., M.B., B.C. (Cantab).—B. 1884; ed., Tonbridge schl. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; med. offr., S.S., Feb., 1913; surg., Tan Tock Seng's hosp. and gen. hosp., 1915 to June, 1917; surg., Penang, Aug., 1926; ag. ch. med. offr., Penang, June, 1929 to Nov., 1929.

ADAMS, THORODER SAMUEL, C.M.G. (1937)—B. 1885; ed., King's Schll., Canterbury, and All Soul's Coll., Oxford, B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; asst. dist. offr., Klang, 1911; dist. offr., K Langat, 1912; dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, 1914; supt., lands, Kelantan, 1918; ag. 1st asst. sec., 1919; ag. 1st asst. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., 1920; ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, 1920; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1925; dist. offr., Larut and regisr. titles, Perak, Nov., 1926; offr., cls. Ia, chmn., assy. bd., K. Lumpur, June, 1928; mag., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1928; ag. under sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; Br. res., Selangor, Dec., 1933; ch. comsrr., N. Provs., Nigeria, 1936.

ADAMS, THOMAS, F.S.I.—B. 1871; ed., Corstorphine and Edinburgh; pres., Young Scots' Soc., 1900; sec., Garden City Assoc., 1901-1904; sec. and man., First Garden City Co., 1903-1906; town planning adviser to Marquis of Salisbury, Earl of Lytton, Sir Richard Paget, Bt., and other landowners, 1906-1909; town planning inspr., L.G.B. of England and Wales, 1909-1914; examr. in civic design, Univ. of Liverpool, 1912-1914; rep. of L.G.B. on arterial roads; Confere. of Greater London, 1913-1914; prep'd. offr. rept. on housing and town planning investig., in U.S., Germany, Sweden, and other countries and on first town planning schemes in Britain, 1908-1918; organised cheap cottage exhibts., Letchworth, 1905, and Wolverhampton, 1908; housing and town planning adviser to Canadian Gov't., since 1914; hon. mem. Instn. of Mun. and Cnty. Engrs. (England); mem. of American Soc. of Mun. Improvement; pres., Town Planning Inst. of Britain, 1913-1914; and mem. of coun., 1914-1919; mem. bd. of govt., American City Planning Inst., 1915-1918; chmn., Town Planning Inst. of Canada, 1919.

ADAMS, MAJOR WILFRED CARNE, M.C.—B. 1894; supt., constab., N. Borneo, Dec., 1914; adjt., Apr., 1922; ag. offr., commdg., constab. and offl. mem., leg. coun., May, 1927; commdt. and offl. mem., legis. coun., Apr., 1931.

ADAMSON, SIR JOHN ERNEST, KT, B.A. (1924), C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1867; ed., in London; Litt.D., M.A., London Univ.; principal, Normal Coll., Pretoria, 1902; dir. of educn., Transvaal, 1905; M.L.C., Transvaal; mem. of coun. of Cape University; dir. of educn., Transvaal Prov.; vice-chmn., Univ. of S. Africa; master, Rhodes Univ. Coll., Oct., 1925-31; vice-pres., educul. sec., Br. Assn. (centenary meeting).

ADDERLEY, CHARLES J. H., B.A., B.A.I., A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.Inst.C.E.I.—B. 1884; Chinese govt. rly., 1908-16; war serv., R.G.A., France, 1916-19; road survr., Nigeria, 1920; exec. engr., 1923; senr. exec. engr., 1926; ag. asst. dir., in 1927-30; ag. D.P.W. in 1927-35; ag. dep. D.P.W. in 1927 and 1929; ag. supr., nat. admin. wks., 1931 and 1933; asst. D.P.W., 1934.

ADDIS, WILLIAM.—B. 1901; ed., Rugby and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (hons.), 1925; asst. dist. comsrr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1924; ag. asst. sec., clk. of couns., edr., offl. gazette, Mar., 1925; to June, 1930; ag. res. mag. and dist. regist., Pemba in 1931 and 1932; dist. offr., Pemba, Apr., 1934; seconded to D.O., June-Oct., 1933; dist. comsrr., Pemba, Nov., 1934; dist. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1935.
ADDO, J. A., B.A.—B. 1903; 4th cls. teacher, Gold Coast, 1922; 2nd divn. teacher, 1923; cadet inspr., schls., 1927; inspr., schls., 1928.
ADELAIDE, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. ARTHUR NUTTER THOMAS, D.D., Cantab.—B. 1892; rector of Guisborough, Yorks., 1901-1906; bishop of Adelaide since 1906.

ADSHEAD, PERCY WILLETS, A.C.A.—B. 1892; ed., Tettenhall Coll.; R.F.A. (T.F.), 1914-19; France, 1915-19; capt. and adjt., 1916-18; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Uganda, Nov., 1926; mem cotton enquiry comsrr., 1929.

AGAR, ARTHUR KIRWAN.—B. 1877; ed., at Brighton Coll., called to bar, Grays Inn, May, 1919; temp. lieut. R.A.S.C., Oct., 1915; ag. capt., R.A.S.C., Aug., 1916; staff capt., W.O., Feb., Dec., 1919; dist. mag., dep. judge, ct. of summary juris., coroner, regist. of sup. ct. secheator gen., provost marshal, regist. gen., regist. of titles and legal adviser, to govt. of Montserrat, 1st Oct., 1920; dist. comsrr., Belize Br. Hond., Nov., 1923; ag. atty-gen., Dec., 1923. to Apr., 1925; res. mag., Jamaica, Oct., 1925; ch. just., Br. Hond., 1936.

AHEARNE, CHRISTOPHER DOMINIC.—B. 1886; ed. Christian Schla., Cork, and Trinity Coll., Dublin, scholar 1907; senr. moderator, B.A., 1909; cadet, S.S., 1910; India, studying Tamil, 1911; asst. supt., Indian immigrants, Selangor, 1912; 1st asst. contr. lab., Penang, 1913; dep. contr. lab., Klang, 1914; dep. reg. sup. off., Penang, 1916; dist. off., Balik Pulau, 1916; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, S'pore, 1917; asst. reg., impts. and exps. (war dept.), 1918; off., cl. iv., Jan., 1919; asst. adv. to gov't. of Johore, Batu Pahat, 1919-21; dist. mag., Singapore, Mar., 1922; dep. contr. lab., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1922; dep. treas., S.S. (cl. Ia.), 1928; contr., lab., Malaya, 1929; leader S.S. deleg., Simla shipping confere., Feb., 1931; chair., S.S. retrenchment confere., Feb., 1932; leader Malaya deleg., to gov't. of India, New Delhi, 1932; to Indian immigr. to Br. Malaya, Oct., 1933; fedl. sec., F.M.S., Feb., 1936.

AINLEY, ALFRED JOHN.—B. 1906; ed. St. Bees Schol. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1928; practised on Northern circuit, 1928-35; dist. mag., Gold Coast, Feb., 1935; crown counsel, Feb., 1936; ag-dist. mag., Aug., 1936.

AINSLEE, JAMES ROBERT, B.Sc. (Oxon.). Diploma of Forestry (Oxford). F.L.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. at Graeme Walker's (Aberdeen); Aberdeen Univ. and Oxford; probationary asst. cons., forests, Imp. Indian Forest Serv. (Oxford), Aug., 1907; asst. cons., forests, Ceylon, Nov., 1910; on active serv., 1914-17 (Egypt and France); attd. to W.O., timber supplies dept. (XI Forest Divn. staff), Aug., 1917; dep. cons., forests, Ceylon, May, 1919; senr. cons., forests, Nigeria, Feb., 1922; dep. dir., forests, Nov., 1928; dir., forests, May, 1929; oh. conserv., forests, 1935; author of "The Physiography of Southern Nigeria and its effect on the Forest Flora of the Country" and "Timber Resources of Nigeria."

AITKEN, ERIC COLQUHOUN.—B. 1884; searcher, gen. regis. office, Jamaica, 1902; asst. treas., 1907; ditto, secretariat, 1911; 2nd cls. clk., 1913; 1st ditto, 1919; conf. clk., 1920; prin. clk., 1921; av. col. sec. on several occasions; audr.-gen., 1932.

AITKEN, JOHN, B.A., LL.B. (1906).—B. 1885; ed. Giggleswick Coll. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1909; asst. dist. off., S. Nigeria, 1913; station mag., Nigeria, 1914; crown counsel, Gold Coast, Sri Nov., 1920; asg. solr.-gen., Dec., 1920 to Apr., 1921; July to Sept., 1921; July to June, 1922; July to July, 1922; ag. puisne judge, Sept. to Oct., 1921, and from July to Sept., 1922; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1925; atty.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1929; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1933; ditto., S. Stilima, 1935; judge, Seremban, Nov., 1935.

AITKEN, JOHN BANKS.—B. 1903; ed. Stewart's Coll., Edin. and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1925; asst. conserv., forests, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1926; asst. conserv., forests, Br. Guiana, Nov., 1928; utilization offr., May, 1934.
AITKEN, W. J., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (Liv.).—B. 1899; mily. serv., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1926.

AKBAR, MAAS THAJOON.—K.C., B.A., LL.B. Cantab., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1880; ag. crown coun., Ceylon, Aug., 1907; crown coun., June, 1909; ag. 2nd addnl. dist. judge, Colombo, and as solr.-gen. on several occasions; solr.-gen., Oct., 1924; ag. puisne just., Nov. to Dec., 1925; ag. atty.-gen., Dec., 1927; puisne judge, sup. ct., Apr., 1929.

ALABASTER, CHALONER GRENVILLE, O.B.E. (1918), K.C. (1922).—B. 1880; ed. Tonbridge; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1904; ag. atty.-gen., Hong Kong, 1911, 1912, 1928, 1930; confirmed in app't., 1931; unoffl. mem., leg. coun., 1919, 1924, 1925; stdl., legal advisory staff, C.O., Aug.-Sept., 1934; edr. of "Laws of Hong Kong 1844-1912."
ALBURY, JOSEPH JOHNSON.—B. 1881; Out Island commr. (4th div.), Bahamas, 1910; 4th grade commr., 1912; 3rd grade, Jan., 1918; 2nd grade, 1926; 1st grade, Mar., 1931.

ALBURY, WILTON GRANT, M.B.E. (1931).—B. 1877; public school teacher, Bahamas, 1896; resigned, 1906; re-app'd, 1909; head-master, boys' central school, 1914; inspr. of schools, 1915; ag. S. and C. mag., June to July and from July, 1926.

ALCOCK, WILLIAM D. E.—B. 1892; ed. King Edward's schl. and Birmingham Univ.; B.A. 1913 (1st class, two distinctions); cadet, Fiji, and Western Pacific oiv. serv., 1915-20; asst. polit. offr., 2nd grade, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; ag. dist. offr., 1927; seconded as lab. offr., 1929; dist. offr., July, 1931.
ALEXANDER, JOHN EDWARD.—Amt. land ranger, R.A.P., Oct. 1912; lands offr., Nyassaland, July, 1922.

ALLAN, ALBERT, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1893; war serv., 1914-19; station supt. and traffic inspr., F.M.S. rly., Feb., 1920; asst. sec., G.M.R. oiv. office in 1921, 1923, 1925 and 1929; asst. sec., bd. in 1924, 1927 and 1930; divnl. supt. (W.), K. Lumpur, Aug., 1932; do. (Mid.), Germania, Mar., 1934.

ALLAN, PETER.—B. 1886; ed. George Heriot's Schol., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ., M.B., Ch.B. (Hons.), 1910; D.P.H., 1912; M.D., 1920; R.A.M.C., France, 1917-18; capt., R.A.M.C.; med. inspr., Union of S. Africa health dept., 1920; major, S.A.M.C., 1921; med. supt., Nelspoort sanatorium, 1923; seconded to tuberculosis research comte., 1927-30; reassumed M.S., Nelspoort sanatorium, 1930; asst. health offr., Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1936.

ALLARD, JULIUS.—B. 1889; ed. Nicolet Coll., Quebec; lawyer; elec. to legis. assem., Quebec, 1897, 1900, 1904; apptd. to legis. coun. and min. of pub. wks., Quebec, 1905; min. of agric., 1907; min. of lands and forests, 1909-1917; re-elec. to legis. assem. at bye-election, 1910 and g.e., 1912; re-called to legis. coun., 1916; prothonotary of court of superior ct., Montreal, 1917.

ALLDREN, HENRIETTA.—B. 1873; 5th clk., C.S.O., Barbados, 1892; 4th do., 1898; 3rd do., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., P.O., 1904; ch. clk., do., 1908; ch. clk., audit off., 1913; acted on several occasions as clk. of the exec. coun. and comte., as acctnt., in P.O., and as auditor-gen.; sec. to Barbados quarantine coman., 1903, to West Indian quarantine confce., 1904, and to confce. on trade relations between West Indies and Canada, 1910; sec., Barbados civ. serv. comsn., 1920.

ALLEN, ARTHUR AUGUSTUS.—B.1890; ed. Christ Hospital; examr. Exchequer and Audit dept., Nov., 1911; asst. audr., Nigeria, June, 1914; seconded, rly. constrn., Oct., 1922-July, 1929; senr. asst. audr., Nigeria, Nov., 1930.

ALLEN, BERTRAM WEDGWOOD.—B. 1888; ed. Repton and King's Coll., Lond.; pol. probationer, S.S., Nov., 1909; asst. supt. pol., Aug., 1914; dep. comsnr. pol., Muar, Johore, June, 1918; asst. supt., pol., Penang, June, 1920; asst. comanr., pol., Kedah, Jan., 1923; supt., pol., S.S., July, 1928; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, Oct., 1929; comdt., pol., depot, Singapore, Dec., 1930; ch. pol. offr., Penang, 1932.
ALLEN, CONWAY PHILLIPS, M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M. & H. (Liverpool)—B. 1901; anaesthetist, F.M.S., July, 1927; med. offr. in ch., offr., in admn., Aug., 1932.
ALLEN, E. M.—Ed. St. Etheldreda Coll., Norwich; temp. mistress, kindergarten schl., Tamale, Gold Coast, 1927; mistress, 1928; inspr., schls., 1933.

ALLEN, EDWARD CUNNINGTON.—B. 1902; clk., secretariat and reporter, legis. coun., Nyasaland, 1925; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1930; ag. finan. asst., prov. admin., Feb. to June, 1933 and July, 1934-Mar., 1935.

ALLEN, FREDERICK HAROLD, M.I.Mech.E., M.Inst. C.E.—B. 1890; engnr., asst., Port of London Authority, 1910-19; dist. engr., Br. Guiana, 1919; dist. engr., Florin, Nigeria, 1923; exec. engr., 1924; native adminstr. engr., 1925; mech. engr., Br. Guiana, 1926; exec. engr., P.W.D., Jan., 1930; ag. D.P.W. and M.L.C., May to Nov., 1934.

ALLEN, GEORGE VANCE, M.D., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Hons), D.T.M. and H.—B. 1894; asst. bacteriologist, Kenya, Feb., 1921; senr. bacteriologist, Sept., 1925; bacteriologist, F.M.S., Nov., 1927; ag. prin., med. coll., Singapore, June, 1929; prin., Mar., 1930; pres., Raffles Coll. in admn., Apr., 1932; mem. dental bd., S. S., Sept., 1933; mem., pharmacy bd., 1934; offr. del., 9th cong. Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Nanking, Sept., 1934.

ALLEN, H.—Cashier and acct., E.A.P., July, 1914; on active serv., E. Africa, 1914-16 and 1917-18; tempy. asst. dist. comanr., E.A.P., Apr., 1919; asst. treas., Uganda, Aug., 1920; asst. treas., Zanzibar, Dec., 1930; ag. treas., in 1932, 1933 and 1934; dep. treas., Uganda, 1935.; dep. currency offr., 1936.

ALLEN, HON. SIR JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1926), K.C.B. (1917), M.A., Camb.—B. 1855; entd. New Zealand Parlmt., 1881; heut.-col. in command Otago Div., New Zealand Garrison Art., 1902; min. of defence, 1912; min. of finance, min. of stamp duties and min. of educ., 1912-15; min. of finance and min. of external affairs, 1919-20; high comanr. in England, 1920-26; M.L.C., 1927.

ALLEN, JOHN WILLOUGHBY.—B. 1904; ed. Westminster and St. John's Coll., Oxford; 2nd class hon. mods., 1925, 3rd litt. hum., 1927; B.A., 1929; M.A., 1932; dipl. O.S., 1933; supt. educ., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; offr., examr., Swahili, 1934.
ALLEN, LEONARD GILBERT.—B. 1901; entered C.O., 1917; apptd., after exam, asst. clk., 23rd Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 17th Sept., 1929.

ALLEN, LUCIEN ARTHUR, Q.B.E. (1931).—B. 1888; ed. Merchant Taylors' Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; passed cadet, June, 1915; asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1918 to Aug., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, June, 1920; Brit. res., Brunei, Mar., 1921; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Jan., 1924; dep. pub. pros., N. Sembilan and Pahang, Dec., 1924; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1929; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Nov., 1931; offr., cls. I.B., 1932; do., Kinta, Nov., 1933; comanr., lands and mines, Johore, M.
ALLEN, PERCY DE VERE.—B. 1891; ed. Melbourne, Australia; on mily. serv., E. Asia, Egypt and France, 1914-19; lab. inspr., Kent, Apr., 1925; lab. offr., Jan., 1928; prim. hbr., 1936.

ALLEN, SIR STEPHEN SHEPHERD, K.B.E. (1933), C.M.G. (1919), D.S.O. (1917); w/o (1918).—Volunteer serv., Cambridge Univ., 1904 and in N.Z., 1911-15; left N.Z. with gunnforcements N.Z. Exped. Force, with major, 1915; lieut.-col., Jan., 1917; in com. 2nd batt., Auckland Regt., Dec., 1916 to 1919; comdr., Auckland Infy. Brig. (T.F.), 1920 to May, 1925; col., Feb., 1921; in com. Hauraki Regt., Sept., 1926 to Apr., 1928; O., Apr., 1928; admtr., W. Samoa, 1928; chmn., transport co-ordination bd., 1934-36; timesment in despe.; hon. A.D.C. to gen., N.Z., Jan., 1925 to May, 1928.
ALLEN, W. R., B.A.—B. 1895; w/o 1914-18; ret. with rank of capt.; inspr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1929.

ALLEYNE, HERBERT MASSY.—B. 1895; Blundell's and R.M.C., Sandhurst; com. Connaught Rangers, Dec., 1918; transf. Own Royal Regt., 1922; ret., 1929; admtr., offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929; dist. offr., Aug., 1931.

ALLEYNE, JAMES DOUGLAS, M.R.E., M.D. (Tuft's Univ., U.S.A.)—Ag. pol. med. dist. A., Barbados and visiting physician, asylum, 1917; asst. port health offr., 1918; supt., lunatic asylum, 1919; port health, 1921; ex officio mem., quarantine board.

ALLIES, E. J.—B. 1891; apptd. asst. Scottish Educn. Dept., 1910; apptd. after exam. clk. of the 2nd divn. of the cv. National Insurance Audit Dept., 1913; cv. serv., 1917-19; assigned to the C.O., Aug.; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; rank 3rd Mar., 1937.
ALLISON, HERBERT BRUCE.—B. 1885; Neilson Schl., Paisley and R. Tech. Coll.; aast. engr., F.M.S.rly., Aug., 1919; dist. grade II., Aug., 1924; do., grade I., 1928.
ALLISON, J. S.—B. 1879; ed. Glasgow and Edinburgh Univ.; M.A. (Edin.); (Cape); Capelaw dep., 1903; Univ. dep. of affrs., 1918; under sec. for native affrs., 1918.

ALLUTT, RICHARD BUSWELL, B.Sc.—B. 1907; ed Malvern Coll. and S. Eastern Coll., Wye; B.Sc. (agr.) (Lond.) and dipl. 1928; crop. observn. offr., Norfolk agr., 1928-30; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930.

ALLSOP, HERBERT HENRY.—B. 1886; Gen. East Africa campaign, 1914-16; asst. polit. Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1916; inspr. admtrive offr., Aug., 1922; ag. dep. prov. comm. (W. Prov.), Oct., 1934—Dec., 1935.

ALUWIHARE, RICHARD.—B. 1896; ed. oomn. serv., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct. 1920; oomn. to dir., agr., Feb., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Dist. gamuwa, Oct., 1923; pol. mag., Pt. Tedd, 1923; ditto, Panadure, Nov., 1924; 2nd inspr., surv., est., Dec., 1926; oomn. requests, nat. and adult. dist. judge, Kegalla, Nov., 1929; ast. stitlt. offr., Oct., 1929; dist. judge, Sri Eliya, Oct., 1931; contr., finance and Mar., 1934.
ALVAPPILLAI, KOVINAPPILLAI.—B. cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1929; attd., Asiapura kach., Jan., 1929; attd., Matara Sept., 1929; attd. office ast., Puttalam

AMBLED, CAPT. GOODMAN, M.C., V.D., R.G.S.—B. 1885; ed. Steyning Grammar Schl., Ighton Grammar Schl. and St. Mark's Ill., Chelsea; asst. mast., Victoria Inst., Lumphur, F.M.S., Jan., 1910; war serv., France and Gallipoli, 1914-19; rejoined Victoria st., 1919; sec., Selangor cadets with rank of pt., Mar., 1924; Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. ed., Mar., 1924; headmast., Outram schl., S'pore, Apr., 1926; lieut., S.V.C., May, 1926; pt., Aug., 1930; transf'd to S.S.V.F., R. of O.; nr. normal instr., S'pore, Dec., 1931; headmast., Outram schl., Nov., 1932.

AMBLED, LLOYD EDWARD.—B. 1894; olk., stal dept., Fiji, June, 1909; 4th cls. clk., ids dept., Aug., 1913; served with 1st Batt., R.R.C., France, 1916-17; Indian Army Res. mand., Oct., 1917; Baluchistan, 1917-19; 1 cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1916; d. lands dept., Fiji, Sept., 1919; ag. 2nd cls. clk. and sec., mining bd., Oct., 1919-Oct., 20; ag. sub-inpr. constab. (during Indian strike), Feb., 1920; ag. 1st cls. clk. and olk., exec. d leg. couns., Jan. to Apr., 1921; regy. clk., Eastern Pacific high comm., May, 1921; ag. ch. sept., 1922 to Feb., 1923; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 27; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., on various occasions, 1923-31; ag. sec., Western Pacific high comm., June to Aug., 1931; ch. clk. and acct., Jan., 1932.

AMERASINGHE, SAMSON FELIX, B.A., intab.—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., July, 26; attd., Kalutara kach., Aug., 1928; office stv. govt. agt., E. Prov., Dec., 1927; pol. mag., Indagamuwu, Sept., 1928; ditto, Negombo, Dec., 28; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, June, 30; office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., June, 32; pol. mag., Kandy, Nov., 1932; addnl. asst. vt. agt., Colombo, Oct., 1935.

AMERY, LIEUT.-COL. L.C.M.S.—B. 1873; at Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; late fellow of All Souls, Oxford; Ouseley Scholar Imp. Inst. in Turkish; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; on editorial staff of The Times, 1909-1909; served during the war in Flanders, Salonika, and the Near East; on the staff of the Imperial War Coun. at Versailles, and as asst. sec. the War Cabinet and Impl. Cabinet to Jan., 1919; under-sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; acted as sec. of state during Lord Milner's absence in Egypt, Nov., 1919 to Apr., 1920; First Lord of the Admiralty, Oct., 1922; S. of S. for the Colonies, 7th Nov., 1924; also S. of S. for Dominion affrs., 9th July, 1925 to June, 1929; capt. 14th Ist B'hm Batt., Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1914; lieut.-col. on general staff, 917; M.P. (U) for S. Birmingham, May, 1911; itto Sparkbrook div., Dec., 1918; edited "The Times History of the South African War"; author of "Problem of the Army," 1903; "Fundamental Fallacies of Free Trade," 1906; "The Great Question" (with J. M. Robertson, I.P.), 1909; "Union and Strength," 1912.

AMES, CECIL GRAINT.—B. 1897; ed. Elstree Prep. Schl. and Dover Coll.; (schol.); 5th Somerset Light Infy., 1916-19; Law Soc.'s final exam. (hons.), 1921; Nigerian admstive serv., 1922; ag. ch. regist., sup. ct., Nov. 1932—July, 1933; admitted, solr., Nov., 1933; attd. judl. dept., Jan.—Mar., 1934; asst. judge, high ct., Apr., 1934.
AMHERST, HON. HUMPHREY WILLIAM.—B. 1903; ed. Charterhouse and R.M.C., Sandhurst; regular army, 43rd Light Infy., 1924-26; cadet, Gold Coast, 1929; asst. dist. commr., 1930.

ANDERSON, ADAM, B.E., B.A. (R.U.L.)—B. 1888; ed. Foyle Coll., Londonderry and Queen's Coll., Belfast; asst. land surv'r., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; asst. engrn., 1919; dep. port engrn., port development dept., 1924; exec. engrn., 1926.

ANDERSON, ADMIRAL SIR DAVID MURRAY, K.C.B. (1930), C.M.G. (1918), M.V.O. (1910).—B. 1874; ed. Stubbington and H.M.S. "Britannia"; served with Brass river expdn. and M'Wale expdn., 1905; Ashanti, 1906; Great War, 1914-19, including opereas in Rufiji river, 1915, and with Grand Fleet, 1918; capt. supt., Pembroke dockyard, and senr. naval offr., Milford Haven, 1920-22; rear-adm'l., 1922; naval A.D.C. to the King, 1921-22; senr. naval offr., Yangtze river, 1923-25; C. in C., China station, Apr.-June, 1925; Africa station, 1927-29; vice-adm'l., 1927; high comm., S. Africa, June-Dec., 1928; adm'l., 1931; adm'y. rep., L. of N. perm't. advisory comm., 1929-31; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Newfoundland, 1932; do., New South Wales, 1936.
ANDERSON, LIEUT.-COL. E. L. B., O.B.E. (1936), D.S.O.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Oct., 1919; pvt. sec. to ag. gov., 1926.

ANDERSON, JAMES HENRY (Lieut. R.A.K.O. Seaforth Hrs.)—B. 1895; 2nd cls. clk., treasy., Nyassaland, 1920; transf'd. to secretariat, 1922; senr. clk., 1924; ag. ch. clk., secretariat and edir., Govt. Gazette, 1927; asst. treas., Gold Coast, Aug., 1928; ag. dep. treas., Mar.-July, 1935.

ANDERSON, CAPT. (local liut.-col.), ROBERT CHARLES BECKETT, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Rugby and Trinity Coll., Oxon.; 2nd liut., Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders, May, 1915; served, Great War, France and Belgium, 1916-18; lieut., Aug., 1916; capt., Apr., 1923; adjt., 1931-34; adjt., Hong Kong Volunteer Defence Corps, Oct., 1935; commdt. and local liut.-col., Aug., 1936.

ANDERSON, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1895; ed. Kilmarnock Acad. and Tech. Coll.; active serv. (France) 1914-19; dep. storekeeper, Hongkong, Feb., 1925; ag. supt., acc'ts. and stores, P.W.D., 1926; sup't., acc'ts. and stores, Jan., 1928.
ANDRE, WILFRID JOSEPH.—B. 1890; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1912; mag., St. Andrew, &c., Trinidad, Nov., 1923; mag., Caroni, Dec., 1924.

ANDREWES, LANCELOT RUGGLES.—Ed. Fort Street, Sydney; war serv., 1915-18; admitted solr., sup. ct., London, 1924; asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, 1928; J.P., 1931; ag. dep. registr. in 1932-33; dep. regist., sup. ct., 1935.

ANDREWS, NORMAN FREDERICK STEWART.—B. 1902; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and Oxford; schol., Brasenose Coll., 1919; Bridgemian Prize, 1923, 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1924, B.A., 1924; M.A., 1928; cadet, adminstrn., Uganda, 1925; asst. dist. offr., 1927; temp. serving in C.O., 1927-28; seconded to secretariat, Uganda, 1929; sec., cotton enquiry comm., 1929; asst. sec., N. Rhodesia, 1935; clk., leg. coun., 1936.
ANGWIN, JOHN.—B. 1892; land surv'r., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1922.

ANNECKE, D. H. SIEGFRIED.—B. 1895; ed. Grey Univ. Coll., Bloemfontein; B.A. (lit. and phil.), 1914; Univ. C.T., M.A. (physiology), 1918; Guy's Hosp., Lond., 1919-23, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1921; all house appts., Guy's, 1921; M.B., B.S., Univ. Durham, 1924; D.P.H. and B.H., Univ. Durham, 1925; research schl. and asst. dept. protozoology, Lond. Schl. Hyg. Trop. Med., 1925-28; D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), 1927; joint educ. grant, govt. S.A. and Health Section L. of N. for study of anti-malarial methods in Italy, 1927; senr. govt. pathologist, Union health dept., Durban, 1928; med. inspr. (malarin), 1931.

ANSIDELL, GEORGE ARTHUR REES WETTENHALL.—B. 1894; ed. Lisard High Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; war serv., Egypt, 1916; Turkey in Asia, 1919; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1921; passed lower Swahili, 1922; admin. offr., 2nd gde., 1924; passed law, 1925; ag. dist. offr., 1925; seconded as lab. offr., 1928; A.D.C. and P.S. to govt., Apr. to Nov., 1927 and Dec., 1928 to Jan., 1929; dist. offr., 1934.
ANTEBI, SOLOMON, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1895; agril. offr., S. dist., Palestine.

ANTHONY, PHILIP ARNOLD, C.M.G. (1918), M.I.C.E.—B. 1873; ed. at Mill Hill Schl.; served in engineering dept., G.W. Rly., 1894-1910; gen. man. and chief engr., F.M.S. Rly., 1910; services lent to ministry of munitions, May, 1915 to Nov., 1916, resumed duty in F.M.S., Feb., 1917; services lent temporarily to govt. of South Aust., Apr., 1918; resumed duty in F.M.S., Jan., 1919; ret., Dec., 1924; reported on Palestine rly., 1925; ag. ch. engrn. (design), C.A., Nov., 1925 to Dec., 1926.

ANTROBUS, MAURICE E., B.A.—B. 1895; ed. Winchester, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; math. exhib., Trinity Coll., Dec., 1913; 2nd lieut., 6th King's Royal Rifle Corps, Aug., 1914; lieut., June, 1915; capt., Jan., 1917; served in Flanders, Salonica and France, 1915-19; 2nd cls. math. tripes, pt. I., 1919; 2nd cls. hist. tripes, pt. II., 1920; B.A., 1920; temp. 2nd cls. elk., C.O., Aug., 1920; appntd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., 7th Feb., 1921; asst. pvtc. sec. to S. of S., 2nd Nov., 1925; seconded as pvtc. sec. to govt., Ceylon, July, 1927; prin., D.O., 23rd July, 1930; seconded as polit. sec. to High Commr. for the U.K. in the Union of S. Africa Jan., 1936; ag. High Commr. for U.K., May-Oct., 1936.

ANTROBUS, SIR REGINALD L., K.C.M.G. (1911), C.B. (1889).—B. 1853; ed. at Winchester and at New Coll., Oxford; scholar of New Coll., 1872; 1st class in classical mods., 1874; 2nd class in final classical school, 1876; B.A., 1876; appntd., after an open compet. exam., to be a elk. in the C.O., May, 1877; asst. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, Dec., 1880 to Dec., 1882; priv. sec. to the Earl of Derby, Dec., 1882 to June, 1885; to Col. the Right Hon. F. A. Stanley, M.P. (afterwards Earl of Derby), June, 1885 to Feb., 1886; and to Earl Granville, Feb. to Aug., 1886; administ. the govt. of St. Helena, Nov., 1889 to June, 1890; priv. sec. to Mr. Meade, Feb., 1892; and to Mr. S. Buxton, M.P., Aug., 1892; sen. elk., Jan., 1894; princ. elk., 1896; asst. under-secr. of state, Oct., 1898; crown agent for the cols., 1909; ret., 1918.

APPLEGATE, F. W.—B. 1887; ed. Bath Coll. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1911; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1915; junr. asst. col. sec., May to Sept., 1915; travelling commr., Neawam, Oct., 1915; 1916, and from Apr., 1917; ag. dist. commr., Kwahu, 1919; dep. prov. commr., 1929; prov. commr., 1932.

ARCHER, CLYDE VERNON HARCOBTY, B.A.—B. 1904; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Cambridge Univ.; called to bar, Grav's Inn, 1927; elk. to atty.-gen., Barbados, 1934; ag. surv.-gen., and as pol. mag., dist. "A", various periods, 1930-34; pol. mag., dist. "B", 1935.

ARCHER, SIR GEOFFREY FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1920), C.M.G. (1913).—Asst. collr., East Africs Prot., Dec., 1902; attached secretariat, Jena, 1902 to Mar., 1903; dist. commr., Apr., 1911; spec. serv., Northern Frontier, East Africa, 1919; offr.-in-charge, May to Dec., 1911; awarded Cent-berk Pratt Grant by the R.G.S., 1918, for surveys of E. Africa connecting Major Gwynn's Abyssinia triangulation with the triangulation of E. Africa; transf'd to Somaliland Prot., June, 1912; admin. govt., July to Oct., 1912, and from June to Oct., 1913; dep. commr., Mar., 1913; commr. and comdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., May, 1914; assumed govt., May, 1914; present at operations against the Dervishes at Sumbe; Berri, Feb., 1915 (African General Service Medal and clasps); member of British Mission on occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu of Abyssinia, Feb., 1917 (Star of Ethiopia, 1st clas); gov., Somaliland Prot., Nov., 1919; directed operations against the Mullah, 1920, resulting in complete destruction of Dervish power in Somaliland; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Uganda Prot., Sept., 1922; gov., Sudan, 1924-26.

ARCHER, NORMAN E.—B. 1892; ed. R.N. Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth; midshipman in Mediterranean and Home Fleets, 1909-12; sub-lieut. (six 1st cls. certif.), Sept., 1912; lieut., June, 1913; H.M.S. Warrior in Mediterranean and Grand Fleets, 1912-15; qualified, specialist course, as torpedo lieut., 1915; served with Russian Navy in Black Sea, &c., 1915-17 (Order of St. Stanislas, 3rd cls.); passed exam. as Russian interpreter, 1918; intell. divn., adm'y., H.M.S. Lowestoft in Adriatic, minelaying squadron, Grand Fleet, and H.M.S. Malaya, 1917-20; appntd. under reconstruction scheme asst. prin., min. d. lab., Sept., 1920; transfd. to C.O., Dec., 1921; pvtc. sec. to perm. under sec. for Dom. affrs., Sept., 1927; seconded as sec. to Br. economic mission to Australia and visited Australia, N.Z., and Canada, 1928-29; pvtc. sec. to parl. under sec. for Dom. affrs., June, 1929; prin., D.O., 4th Sept., 1929; seconded as sec., office of his commr. in Canada for H.M.G. in U.K., 1st Apr., 1932; ag. high commr., Sept., 1934 to Jan., 1935.
ARCHER, W. S.—B. 1907; ed. the Leek Schl., Barbados, B.W.I.; chief elk., adm'r's ofce., D'ca., 1931; prinl., C.S.O., Antigua and elk to exec. and leg. couns., Leeward Is., 1932.

ARCHIBALD, EDGAR S., B.A., B.S.A.—B. 1885; recd. pmv. educn. at Yarmouth pub. schl. and Yarmouth acad.; grad. Acadia Univ., 1908; also grad. from the Nova Scotia hort. schl. in the same year, and from Nova Scotia agric. coll. in 1906; recd. deg. of Bach. of Scien. Agric. from the Ontario agric. coll., 1908; inst. of agric. and experimt. at the Nova Scotia agric. coll., 1908; prof. of agric. and farm supt. of the same coll. in 1910; entd. the serv. of the Fed. Dept. of Agric. in June, 1912, as Dominion animal husbandman at the Cent. Exptnl. Farm, Ottawa; dir. of Exptnl. Farms, 1918-1919; dir., June, 1921.
ARDRON, HAROLD GRAHAM.—B. 1890; mily. serv., 1916-21; educn. dept., Gold Coast.

ARNOLD, WILLIAM ERIC.—B. 1882; ed. Napier public schl. and Napier high schl., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. govt. life insurance dept., 1901; dist. man., 1920; senr. clk., actuarial br., 1925; office examnr., 1927; acotnt., 1929; sec. and dep. comsnr., 1932; comsnr., 1933.

ARUMUGUM, JOSEPH NALLAH, B.Sc.(Lond.).—B. 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Anuradhapura kach., Dec., 1921; itg. pol. mag., W. Prov., May, 1924; pol. mag., Chilaw, Aug., 1927; dist. judge, Tangalle, Jan., 1929; do., Badulla, Nov., 1932; astt. govt. agt., Anuradhapura, Feb., 1935.

ASHMORE, JACK HALIBURTON.—B. 1900; 2nd lieut., The King's Own Royal Lancaster Regt., Apr., 1919; col. pol. offrs. course, R.I.C., May, 1920; sub-inspr., pol., British Guiana, Oct., 1920; dist. inspr., July, 1923; travelling mag. and warden, Nos. 3 and 4 mining dists., Aug., 1923 to Apr., 1924; passed colloquial Hindi exam., 1926; county inspr., 1927; ag. detective inspr., for various periods in 1926-28 and 1930-32; local commdt., pol. in ch. crim. invesgn. dept., Cyprus, Dec., 1932; ag. ch. commdt. pol. and inspr. prisons in 1933, 1934 and 1935; dep. ch. commdt., pol. and inspr., prisons, Jan., 1935.

ASHTON, DANIEL ASPINALL.—B. 1869; ed. at Manchester gram. schl. and Owens Coll.; M.B., B.Ch., Victoria Univ.; oiv. surg., S. African war, 1901-1902 (medal and three clasps); col. med. offr., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1903; served in Cross River expdn., 1904 (medal and clasp); med. offr. at Abe and Obubura.

ASHTON, MAJOR-GEN. E. C., C.B., C.M.G., V.D., M.D., C.M.—B. 1873; ed. Trinity Med. Coll., and graduated in med., Trinity Univ., Toronto, 1898; comsnr., Dufferin Rifles of Canada, 1893; gradually promoted to lieut.-col. comdg., 1907; at outbreak of war organised and commanded 36th batt., C.E.F., 1915; col. Canadian Militia, May, 1916; after various commands promoted brig.-gen., Apr., 1917; rtd., Canada to be adjt.-gen., Nov., 1919; major-gen., Jan., 1918; qmr.-gen., Canadian Militia, Aug., 1920; do., defence dept on reorganisation, Jan.; D.O.C., M.D., No. 2, Jan., 1930; do., No. 11, Oct., 1933; ch. gen. staff, dept. of nat. defence, June, 1935; three times ment. in despatches for war services.

ASHWORTH, HENRY TWEEDALE, M.R.E. (1934), M.M.—B. 1885; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., G.P.O., 1902; on mily. serv., Oct., 1914 to May, 1919; served in Gallipoli, Egypt (Canal Defences) and France; passed supplementary clsks. exam., Jan., 1921; services loaned to C.O., 1st May, 1922; apptd. cler. offr. and assigned to the C.O., 1st Sept., 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1927; staff offr., 1st Apr., 1934.
ASKEW, G. G.—B. 1895; ed. St. Bees Schl., Cumberland; on mily. serv., 1914-19; "1914" Star, ment. in desps.; dep. colliery man., Nigeria, 1924.

ASPINAL, SIR ALGERNON EDWARD, KT. BACH. (1928), C.M.G. (1918), C.B.E. (1926).—B. 1871; ed. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; R.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; sec., West India Comtee. since 1898 and of the Impl. Coll. of Trop. Agr. since 1921; pres. of W. Indian Club; mem. of comtee. on Col. blue-books and blue-book repts., 1917; mem., W. Indian chipping comtee., 1919; hon. sec., Trop. Agril. Coll. comtee., 1920; hon. sec., W. Indian Contingent comtee., 1915-21; mem., W. Indian currency comtee., 1923.

ASSER, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Joseph John, K.C.B. (1924), K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C.V.O. (1917), C.B. (1915), and many foreign decorations.—B. 1867; served with Nile expdn., 1897-9; in command of Kordofan expdn., Soudan, 1910; A.G., Egyptian Army, 1907-18; served in European War, 1914-19; base commdt., 1914-16; lieut-gen., 1919; gen. offr. commdg. Br. troops, France and Flanders, 1919-20; gov. of Bermuda, 4th May, 1922; assumed govt., 9th Aug., 1922; ret., 1927.

ASTON, Arthur Vincent.—B. 1896; ed. King's Schil., Chester, and Queen's Coll., Oxford open maths. schol.); R.F.A., 1915-18 (M.C.), 1917); cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; collr., land rev., July, 1922; sec. to Br. adv., Kedah, Feb., 1930; A.D.C. to O.A.G., Nov., 1930; attd., land office, Penang, Nov., 1931; collr., land rev., Penang, July, 1932; ag. res. conn., Penang, Apr., 1933; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Aug., 1935.

ATHILL, Samuel Lauchland.—B. 1885; ent. civ. serv., Leeward Is., Sept., 1901; clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Aug., 1906; sec. to legal affairs inquiry commr., Montserrat, 1911; ag. regar., Montserrat circuit, Feb.-Mar., 1915; deputy coroner, dists. "A" and "B," Antigua, Oct., 1917; mag. dist. B., Antigua, July, 1924; mag., dists. "A" and "B," 1930.

ATHLONE, First Earl of (created 1917), Alexander Cambridge, K.G. (1928), G.C.B. (1911), G.C.V.O. (1904), C.M.G. (1917), D.S.O. (1900).—Served in Matabele campaign, 1896-7; S. African war, 1899-1900; European war, 1914-18; maj., 2nd Life Gds., 1911-19; brevet-col., 1919; G.S.O., 1st grade, 1915; ret. with hon. rank of brig.-gen., 1919; personal A.D.C. to the King from 1919; gov. gen. and commdr.-in-chief, Union of S. Africa, 1923-30; chan. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George (1934); Grand Cordon of Order of Leopold of Belgium, Croix de Guerre (Belgium); G.O. Leg. Hon. and Croix de Guerre (France); Knt. of Justice of St. John of Jerusalem.

ATKINSON, Vivian Buchanan.—B. 1886; N. Staffs. Rly. Co., 1907; A.M.I.C.E., 1916; asst. engr., Uganda Rly., Jan., 1912; dist. engr., Tanganyika rly., Oct., 1920; asst. ch. engr., Kenya and Uganda rly., and harbrs., 1930.
ATTERBURY, H. W.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 14th Oct., 1921.
ATTERBURY, William Albert.—Brit. post office, 1910-19; war serv., 1916-19; sub-tel. engr., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; tel. engr., 1926.

AUBER, Egerton Lancloz.—B. 1889; ed. Wesleyan High Schil., Freetown, Sierra Leone; supervm. clk., cust. (indoor), Gambia, Feb., 1908; 3rd clk., treasy., Nov., 1908; 2nd clk., Nov., 1911; re-graded 4th grade clk., 1915; 3rd grade clk., July, 1916; 2nd grade clk., Dec., 1918; ch. clk., treasy., Oct., 1920; seconded for duty as marine acctnt., Feb., 1927 to July, 1929.

AUCHINLECK, Gilbert Graham.—B.Sc. (McGill), F.C.S., B. 1884; ed. West Indies and McGill Univ., Canada; 1st cls. hons. in chemistry and botany, McGill Univ.; govt. laboratory, Leeward Is., 1902-04; McGill Univ., 1904-08; science and 2nd mast., St. Kitts-Nevia gram. schl., 1908-09; supt. of agric., Grenada, 1909-14; asst. dir. of agric. and chemist, Mauritius, 1914; ag. dir. of agric. and regar., C.C. societies, Dec., 1916 to May, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, Jan.-May, 1917; reported on Is. of Rodrigues to the Mauritius govt., 1919; Mauritius del. to Imp. Entom. Confce. in London, 1920; divnl. agric. affr., Ceylon, 1920; seconded to Seychelles to report on the possibilities of sug. cultivation, 1921-22; dep. dir., agric., 1926; sent to Java and Sumatra to rep. on sugar industry, Apr. 1926 to Jan., 1927; dir. of African agric. confce., Nigeria, Mar., 1927; author of papers on land stimt. and various agric. subjects; dir. agric., Dec., 1928.

AUCHINLECK, Maurice M.—B. 1888; asst. cust., Antigua, June, 1906; col. secret., Antigua, 1912; clk. to commr. and exec. offr., Montserrat, 1915; ag. postm. and harbr., Antigua, 1919-20; ch. clk., treas. and compt., Antigua, 1921; ag. treas., Dominica, 1921; treas. and compt., cusk., Antigua, and treas., Leeward Is., local chmn., exec. commrs., Antigua; mem., exec. and leg. commrs., Antigua, and mem., exec. and leg. commrs., Leeward Is., 1926-30; served on hurricane comtce., 1928; J.P. and visiting just. of peace, mem., city commrs. and road bd., Antigua; senr. asst. recr. gen., Gambia, 1931; J.P., recr.-gen., 1933.

AUDEN, Francis Thomas.—B. 1890; Shrewsbury; M.D., Edinburgh Univ., 1901; M.B., 1891; civil surg., S. African war, 1901 (medal and clasp); dist. commr. and med. offr., Turks and Caicos Islands Dist., med. offr., Jamaica, 1917; Keny., Bahamas, 1924; Cayman Is., 1925; St. Lucia, 1926; Seychelles, 1927.
AUSTEN, Harold Cholmley Mansfield, C.B.E. (1935).—B. 1878; tempy., harbr., Mauritius, 1923; gen. man., rly. and engr., May, 1931.
AUSTIN, T. A., L.R.C.P. and S.I., (Dub.), D.T.M. and H. (Liverpool).—ed. Royal Coll. of Surgeons, Dublin; East Ind. med. serv. (Zanzibar), May, 1924.
AYLES, James Arthur, M.C.—B. 1883; Col. post and tel. serv., 1898-1906; Univ. Africa post and tel. serv., 1911-13; Brit. post and tel. serv., 1913; asst. P.M.G., Trz., 1930; P.M.G., 1936.

AYLESWORTH, Hon. Sir Alles B., K.C.M.G. (1911), K.C., B.A. (1874), (1875).—B. 1854; educ. at Newbury school and Toronto univ.; Prince of Wales' prizeman; bencher of law soc. of Canada; called to bar, Q.C. (Ontario); H.M.'s commrs. for settlmt. of Alaska in 1903; elected to the H. of C. for North-West Territories, 1904; postm.-gen. of Canada, 1905; just., 1906; British ag. internat. arbitr. at the Hague, 1910; resigned and retired, 1911; mem., privy coun. of Canada; called to the Senate, 1923.
AZEEZ, Abubucker Mohamed Abdul, B.A. (hons.), London.—B. 1911; cadet Ceylon corp., Feb., 1935.

BABER, Edward Alfred.—B. 1888; oq't survey dept., B. Honduras, 1905; appnt. ditto, 1906; passed exam. in theory of surveying; 2nd cls. clk., Aug., 1910; ag. clk., 1911; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s dept., 1913; clk., roads and rivers bd., 1914; ag. clk. and clk. of coun., 1914-15; ag. inspr. of clk. and clk. to bd. of educn., Nov., 1915; sen. col. sec.'s dept., Feb., 1917; ag. clk., for various periods, 1917-21; ag. asst. col. Nov., 1921; ag. priv. sec. to Sir Errol H. May-Aug., 1922; ch. clk., Apr., 1921; am. sec., June, 1923.

BACKWELL, Henry Fleming, B.A. (Cantab.), B. 1884; ed. at Charterhouse (jun. and scholarship), and at King's Coll., Cambridge; exhibr.; asst. mast., Portsmouth gram. sch., 1907-8; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1908asst. dist. offr., Jan., 1914; dist. offr., 2nd cls., Aug., 1917; res., July, 1925.

BADCOCK, RICHARD DORIAN, M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Dulwich Coll.; mil. serv., 1914-19; major, R.A.; asst. ch. of staff, O.E.T.A. (S.), 1919-20; sub-dist. gov., Palestine, 1920; sec. Near East Conf., Cairo, 1921; admve. offr., Safad, 1924; Nablus, 1926; Jaffa, 1928; dep. dir. dept. of imigr., 1930; asst. dist. comanr., S. dist., 1934.

BADDELEY, SIR FRANK MORRISH, K.B.E. (1930), KT. BACH. (1928), C.M.G. (1925).—M.A., Magdalene Coll., Cambridge, 21st wrangler, Math. tripos, 1896; cadet F.M.S., 1897; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1913; recd. thanks of govt. in connection with Ulu Selangor riots, Feb., 1902; revenue auditor, Pahang, Jan., 1904; ag. offl. assignee, Singapore, Apr., 1907; apntd. a currency comanr., May, 1907; dist. offr., Kuantan, June, 1908; supt. govt. monopolies dept., S. Sttlmts., Sept., 1909; P.M.G., S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1916; food controller, Oct., 1920; under-secr., S. Sttlmts., July, 1921; ag. col. sec., S. Sttlmts., Apr.-Sept., 1922; ag. M.O.Y., 1923 and from Sept.-Nov., 1924; ch. sec., Nigeria, Nov., 1924; O.A.G., Nigeria, in 1925, 1927 and 1928-29; govts. dep. for various periods in 1925, 1926 and 1927; ret., 1930.

BAEZA, JOSHUA ISADORE, M.B., B.Ch. (Edin.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Oxon.), certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1890; jr. res. surgn., Barbados gen. hosp., Oct., 1913; med. offr., W., Africa, May, 1914; war serv., 1914-16; med. offr. and ag. bacteriologist, Trinidad, 1919-22; ag. sr. health offr., Penang, June, 1923; ag. ch. med. offr., Malacca, Feb., 1924; health offr., S'pore, Mar., 1924; sr. health offr., Kedah, June, 1929; ag. state med. and health offr., Pahang, Sept., 1934.

BAGOT, EDWARD.—B. 1892; pol. probnr., F.M.S., Nov., 1912; asst. comsnr., Oct., Oct., 1916; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, Dec., 1918; adjt. pol. depot, June, 1920; 1st ast. comanr., Selangor, June, 1922; ast. comanr., pol., Ipoh, Feb., 1925; offr. i/c detectives, Perak, Mar., 1925; ast. comanr., pol., fed. hqrs., Dec., 1929; ch. pol. offr., Pahang, Jan., 1930; ch. pol. offr., Perak, Apr., 1930; dep. comanr., pol., F.M.S., July, 1930; comanr., pol., Trengganu, Nov., 1930; (do.), Johore, Jan., 1933.
BAILEY, A.N.—Ast. dist. comanr., Kenya, Jan., 1920; junr. sec., native affrs. dept., 1926; pvt. sec. to ag. gov., 1927.

BAIN, ARTHUR HERBERT.—Ck., Sept., 1895; examr., deeds, April, 1912; 1st gr. examr., deeds, Oct., 1919; ast. regtr., deeds, King Williamsstown, Nov., 1919; ast. regtr., deeds, Natal, Jan., 1926; ast. regtr., deeds, Cape Town, Oct., 1928; regtr., deeds, Natal, Jan., 1932.

BAIN, JAMES, M.C., M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1892; ed. Edin. Univ., European mast., S.S. Raffles Inst., S'pore, Jan., 1921; headmast., Geylang English Schl., Jan., 1923; ag. inspr., schl., 1923-28; ag. supt., educn., Kedah, Apr., 1930; senr. educn. offr. (B.), Jan., 1932; ag. headmast., Penang Free Schl., Oct., 1933; headmr., Eng. Coll., Bahru, Aug., 1934.
BAIRD, ROBERT DOUGLAS, Lieut., R.N.R.—B. 1905; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast and H.M.T.S. "Conway"; merch. serv., 1921-28; marine offr., Kenya, 1929; harbmr. and marine supt., British Guiana, 1932.

BAKER, ALAN CUSTANCE, M.C., B.A., Oxon.—B. 1885; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1908; attached to land office, Malacca, Jan., 1909; officiating asst. supt., monopolies, Penang, June, 1919; officiating asst. offl. assignee and dep. regar., sup. et., Penang, Aug., 1919; capt., Penang Monopolies Nov., 1919; offr., cls. III, Oct., 1920; collr. land rev. and asst. treas., Malacca, Mar., 1923, comanr., trade and cust., Johore, June, 1927; cls. IB; ag. Br. advr., Kedah, Apr., 1932; Br. advr., Kelantan, July, 1934.

BAKER, ERNEST LIDLAUW.—B. 1880; ed. Australia; ck., col. sec.'s office, Fiji, 1901; shorthand writer, legis. coun., 1906; sec., Albert Park Bd. (joint apptnt.), 1908; 1st ck., col. sec.'s office, and ck. to legis. coun., 1909; ck., exec. coun. (joint apptnt.), 1910; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., 1913; ag. ast. col. sec., 1920; dist. comanr., 1st grade, 1930; nom. M.L.C., 1934.

BAKER, FRANCIS HORACE.—B. 1887; ed. Stratford-on-Avon Sch.; admitted solr., sup. ot. England, 1910; practised in England until 1913 and in Hong Kong, Jan., 1914; on mily. serv., 1914-19; comanr., R. Artillery, 1915; staff capt., 1919; mily. mag., Nablus dist., Apr., 1919; mily. mag., Jerusalem dist., May, 1919; pres. Jeruslaem dist. ct., Aug., 1919; also pres., Beersheba dist. ct.; puisne judge, Nigeria, 1935.

BAKER, HERBERT ARTHUR, D.Sc., D.I.C., F.G.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1885; ed. Univ. Coll. and London Day Training Coll., Univ. of London, 1905-08; B.S. hons.; B. of E. teacher's certif., 1908; M.S., London, 1918; D.Sc., London, 1920; dipl. of membership, Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Technology (D.I.C.), 1920; R.F.C., France, 1915-16; warrant offr., R.N., 1916-19; govt. geologist, Falkland Is., 1920-22; headmaster, Grenada boys secondary schl., 1924.

BAKER, JOHN ERNEST.—B. 1895; ed. St. Paul's Sch.; junr. draughtsman, off. supdt. civ. eng., H.M. dykd., Roysth, 1914-15; R.F.C., France, Mesopotamia, Persia, 1916-20; studied Seale-Hayne Agr. Coll., 1920-24; agrl. offr., Zanzibar, 1925; del., Imp. Agr. Res. Confe., 1927.
BALDWIN, JOSEPH MARON, M.A., D.Sc.—B. 1878; ag. govt. astronomer, Victoria, 1915; govt. astronomer, July, 1919.

BALDWIN, THOMAS HAYWARD, B.A.—B. 1896; ed., Stamford Schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, Oct., 1921; ag. prin., higher coll., Katsina, 1930 and 1932; do., Kaduna Coll., 1936.

BALE, EDGAR GEORGE.—B. 1891; ed. King Edward's Schl.; Imp. cust. and excise serv., 1912-21; ast. to comanr. of cust., Kenya and Uganda, Mar., 1921; dep. comanr., cust., April, 1923; comanr., cust., May, 1936; chmn., harbr. advr. bd.; chmn., K.R.N.V.R. advr. comtee.
BALFE, R. S.—B. 1892; cler.-ast., Basutoland, 1912; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1915; war serv., 1915-19; inspr., 1927; dep. ast. comanr., 1930; dist. comanr., 1936.

BALFOUR, DOUGLAS HASTINGS.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1911; ag. pol. mag. P. Pedro, May, 1911; extra office ast., Jaffna kach., Nov., 1911; office ast., Jaffna kach., 1912; pol. mag., Matara, July, 1913; ast. comanr. excise, June, 1917; dist. judge, Kegalla, July, 1923; ag. ast. govt. agt., Mannar, Apr., 1925; dist. judge, Negombo, June, 1925; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, July, 1927; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Oct., 1929; ag. dist. judge, Jaffna, Sept., 1930; dist. judge, Negombo, Oct., 1933.

BALFOUR, STEPHEN FRANCIS, B.A.—B. 1905; ed. King's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., 1930; 1st ck., mag., Kowloon, 1932; asst. head, navy dept., Feb., 1933; 2nd pol. mag., Oct., 1933 and Feb., 1936; lat clk. and lag., May, 1935; J.P., 1935.

BALLARD, EDWARD, B.A. (Cantab.), F.R.Entom.Soc.—B. 1888; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; gov't entomologist, Nyassaland, 1911; Indian agril. serv.; gov't entomologist, Madras, 1914; on war serv., 1916-18 (France and Italy); advry. entomologist, Long Ashton research station, 1921; Commonwealth of Australia cotton pest research, 1924-25; visited Papua and New Guinea for survey of potential cotton areas, 1925; dir., Plant prot. sect., Egypt, 1929-32; chm., Egyptian deleg., to Internat. confere., on plant prot., Rome, 1929; entomologist, Palestine, 1932; author of numerous papers on insect pests from S. India, also from England, Australia, New Guinea and Egypt.

BAMFORD, Lieut.-Colonel H.W.M.C.B.E., 1933, O.B.E. (1919); M.C. (1916).—B. 1882; Cape Mounted Riflemen, 1900-1906; S. African War, 1900-1902; Transvaal Mounted Rifles, Zulu Rebellion, 1906; Natal Police, 1907-1912; dep. sheriff N. Zululand, 1913-1914; adjt., Natal Light Horse, S.A. Rebellion and G.S.W., Africa, 1914-1915; adjt. 2nd S. African Infantry, Senussi Campaign, W.F.F. Egypt (wounded Hazalin Jan. 23rd, 1916); to France with S. African bde., 9th Scottish Div. (wounded Delville Wood, July 15th, 1916); bde-major, 90th infy. bde., France, 1917; comdg. 2nd S. African infy., 1917-1918 (very severely wounded Beaurevoir, Oct. 8th, 1918); service in France includes Somme, Arras, Messines, Ypres, defence of Hazebrouk, Le Cateau; three times mentioned in despatches; O.B.E., M.C., Croix de Guerre avec palme, commandant of police and inspr. of prisons, Malta, July, 1919; commdt., constab., N. Territories, G. Coast, July, 1922; inspr.-gen., pol., G. Coast, Aug., 1924.

BANCROFT, OSWALD LAWRENCE, M.A.—B. 1888; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1909; called to bar Inner Temple, 1910; admitted barr., Barbados, 1910; barrister-at-law and solr., sup. ct., Br. Columbia, 1911-24; pol. mag., dist. A, Barbados, 1925; dep. registr., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1926; crown coun., N. Rhodesia, 1928; pol. mag. and coroner, Barbados, 1931.

BANNERMAN, CHARLES EDWARD WOOLHOUSE, O.B.E. (1924).—B. 1884; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, Nov., 1913; admitted to Gold Coast bar as solr. and adv., Jan., 1914; practised before sup. ct., G. Coast up to 31st July, 1919; pol. mag., sup. ct. 1st Aug., 1919; senr. pol. mag. 1920; ag. puisne judge sup. ct., on various occasions, 1925-1931; circuit judge, Ashanti and N.T., Gold Coast, Sept., 1934; puisne judge, sup. ct., Gold Coast, July, 1935.

BANNISTER, ADRIAN ALLAN—B. 1894; ed. Codrington Coll., Barbados and Rawle Training Inst. for Teachers, Barbados; B.A., 1928; asst. mast., Berbice High Schl., Br. Guiana, 1920; ag. asst. inspr. schls., 1924; asst. mast., teachers' training centre, 1928; asst. inspr., schls., 1935.
BANTING, WILLIAM VILLIKERS.—B. 1903; ed. Haileybury Coll.; chartered acctnt. (England and Wales), 1928; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1930.

BARBADOS, BISHOP OF (founded 1824), RIGHT REV. DAVID WILLIAMS BENTLEY.—Ed. Durham Univ.; B.A. (1910), M.A. (1913), D.D. (1919); asst. curate, St. John's, Barrow-in-Furness, 1906-10; do., St. James-the-Less, Bethnal Green, 1910-14; vicar, St. Mark's, Plaistow, 1914-16; warden, St. Peter's Coll., Jamaica and examining chaplain to bishop, 1917; canon missioner, 1918; asst. bishop of Jamaica, 1919; bishop of Barbados, 1927.

BARLAS, WILLIAM.—B. 1888; ed. Pitlochry High Sch. and Thomson's Acad., Dundee; travelling teacher, Falkland Is., Feb., 1908; ag. gov't schoolmr., June, 1913; asst. teacher, gov't schl., July, 1914; mag., &c., S. Shetlands, Nov., 1914; junr. clk., C.S.O., Apr., 1915; mag., &c., New Is., Mar., 1916; mag., &c., S. Georgia, Apl., 1918; dep. mag., S. Georgia, Jan., 1920; ag. mag., S. Georgia, on various occasions, 1921; ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk. of couns., Mar., 1922-Feb., 1923; ag. col. postmaster and local auth., June, 1926-Feb., 1927; mag., S. Georgia, Sept., 1928.

BARLEY, JACK CHARLES.—B. 1887; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford, B.A.; cadet, R. Solomon Is. Port, 1912; ag. treas. and collr. cust., 1913; dist. offr., 1915; ag. res. commr., various periods, 1917-32; seconded to serv. in Fiji, Mar., 1924; 1st dist. offr., 1925; chmn., lab. commr., 1928; res. commr., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., Oct., 1933.
BARNARD, Hon. WILLIAM EDWARD.—B. 1876; ed. Levin State Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; mem. N.Z. H. of R. since 1921; war serv., 1916-19; speaker, H. of R., 1936.

BARNES, ARTHUR CHAPMAN, C.M.G. (1936), B.Sc. (Hons.), F.I.C., A.M.I.C.H.E.—B. 1891; ed. Deacon's Schl., Peterborough, Munic. Coll. of Tech. and Victoria Univ., Manchester (schol. computer, survey dept., E.A.P., Oct., 1914; ag. analyst, Apr., 1916; chem. offr., Kenya, 1919; ag. dir., chem. research, May to Aug., 1920; ag. chem., Nigeria, Apr., 1923; asst. dir., ag. Zanzibar, June, 1921; ag. dir., agr., Jan.-Sept., 1928; prov. offl. mem., leg. coun.; dir., agr., Fiji, July, 1929; offl. mem., leg. coun.; chmn., coconut comm., chmn., livestock record assn.; dir. agr., 1930; dir., agr. and island chemist, Jamaica, 1933.
BARNES, HAROLD CHARLES VERNON BLAKE.—B. 1905; ed. Uppingham Schl.; asst. audt., Kenya, July, 1928.

BARNETT, ERNEST ARTHUR.—B. 1884; clk. and pay and qtrmr., 1900; sub-inspr., Fiji constab., 1906; attached to R.I.C. for training, 1908; inspr., Fiji constab., 1911; higher grad., 1914; asst. adjutant to local forces, 1914; capt., Fiji Def. Forces, 1914; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab. and sheriff, June to Dec., 1916; J.P. for the col., 1916; press censor, 1917; chief inspr., 1918; ag. inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1918; dist. commr., 1921; ag. dist. commr. and prov. commr., Ea., May, 1921-June, 1922; dep. inspr.-gen., constab., Jan., 1922; prov. mem., leg. coun., Nov., 1925; ag. inspr. gen., constab. on various occasions; title changed to asst. commr., pol., 1936.

BARNHILL, WILLIAM WILSON, B.A. Hon. (Cantab.).—B. 1903; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1926; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1926; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., Feb., 1930; seconded to Gold Coast as asst. col. sec., 1934.

BARRACLOUGH, FRANK CLARANCE.—B. 1893; ed. Gram. Schl., Burton-on-Trent; St. Mark's Coll., Chelsea; mast., Victoria Inst., K. Lumpur, May, 1921; headmr., Serangoon Eng. Sch., S'pore., Aug., 1930; do., T. Kurral, Apr., 1931; asst. inspr., schls., Aug., 1931; headmr., Westlands Schl., Penang, Sept., 1934.

BARRETT, ARTHUR LUTLEY.—B. 1882; ent. native affrs. dept., Cape, Feb., 1901; clk., native appl. ct., sec. to several deptl. comtees, and as pvtv. sec. to Sir Walter Stanford; prot.; natives Kimberley, 1913; prin. clk., ch. native comsnr., Natal, 1920; mag., Ngomakwe, 1925; ch. clk., office of ch. mag., Umata and sec. and treas., Transkeian Territories gen. coun., 1928; ch. clk., dept. native affrs., Pretoria, 1929; seconded as comsnr. of conciliations, 1930; dir., native lab. (Union) and ch. native comsnr., Witwatersrand, 1932.

BARRATT-LENNARD, SIR FIRNNESS CECIL ARTHUR, KT. BACH (1926)—Served in Anglo-Boer War, but invalided through wounds recd. in action, 1890-1900; judge, sup. ct., Gold Coast, and ex officio of sup. ct., Nigeria, Sept., 1913; served with local mily. forces and as judge, ct. of appeal for eastern Africa and the high ct. of Uganda, Feb., 1915 to Nov., 1916; judge, sup. cts., S. Stiltsm. and F.M.S., Mar., 1920; judge, ct. of appeal, Johore, June, 1920; judge, ct. of appeal, Kedah, Dec., 1921; ch. just. and keeper of records, Jamaica, Nov., 1925; ret. 1932.

BARRON, GEORGE DESMOND, M.C., B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1891; surv. prob., F.M.S., Dec., 1913; surv., grade II, rev. surv. br., Jan., 1915; ditto, topo. br., July, 1915; on mil. serv., Nov., 1916-Apr., 1919; M.C., Oct., 1918; asst. supt., rev. surv. br., Jan., 1919; asst. supt., rev. surv., Kedah, Sept., 1922; do., Johore, Jan., 1925; do., Selangor, Oct., 1929; supt., surveys, F.M.S. and S.S., 1933; do., Kelantan, Dec., 1933.

BARRON, JACOB MAURICE, B.A.—B. 1891; ed. High Schl., Dublin, 1899-1910; sen. modr., nat. sci., 1914, T.C.D.; cadet, F.M.S., 1914; labr. dept., 1915-17; passed in Tamil, 1917; dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, 1917; labr. dept., 1917; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1917; supervisor of cust., P. Swettenham, 1917-20; labr. dept., Apr., 1921; supernmy. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1921; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Oct., 1922; dep. contr., rubber expts., Jan., 1924; town planning admstr. and valuer-gen., K. Lumpur Jan., 1926 and in Mar., 1927 (in addn.); offr., cls. III; ag. dep. contr. lab., F.M.S., Aug., 1928; offr., cls. II; dep. contr., lab., F.M.S., Nov., 1931; ag. dep. contr., lab., Malaya, Mar., 1933.

BARRON, WILLIAM DOUGLAS.—B. 1887; ed. Aberdeen Univ., M.A., 1909, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; passed cadet, Dec., 1913; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1914; asst. dist. offr., K. Kubu, Selangor, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Rembau, N. Sembilan, Aug., 1920; dist. offr., Nov., 1920; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1923; asst. adv., B. Pahat Jan., 1925; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1928; collr., land rev., Seremban and regisr., titles, N. Sembilan, Apr., 1929; state valuer and town-planning admstr. in addn.; chmn. sany. bd., Seremban in addn., Mar., 1930; chmn., European unemployment local comtees, N. Sembilan, Oct., 1930; instast. sec.(A), S.S., Nov., 1931; ag. sec. to high comsnr., Apr., 1933; offr., cls. IB, 1934; ag. Br. advr., Kelantan, Feb., 1934; do., Selangor, Jan., 1935; comsnr., cust. and excise, S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1936.

BARROW, JOHN—B. 1903; ed. Rugby and Caius Coll., Cambridge (B.A. 1926, M.A. 1929) abroad and Univ. Coll., Lond.; cadet, Hong Kong, Jan., 1927; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., Mar., 1929; asst. col. sec., June, 1929; pvtv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1929 and to O.A.G., Feb., 1930; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1930; pvtv. sec. to O.A.G., 1932; last clk. magistracy and mag., 1932; asst. col. sec., 1933; asst. supt., impts. and expts., May, 1933.
BARRY, CHARLES EDWARD, K.C.—B. 1878; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; admitted Inner Temple, 1905; admitted advoc., Pretoria, 1906; judge, sup. ct., 1926.

BARRY, EDWARD, M.B.E. (1936).—B. 1896; ed. Mount St. Mary's; served in Army, Apr., 1915-Oct., 1927; expedy. forces, European War, Aug., 1915-Nov., 1918; 3rd K.A.R., Nov., 1922 to Oct., 1927; commr., 3rd gde., Somaliland, Mar., 1928.

BARRY, HON. JEREMIAH HAYES, K.C.—B. 1858; ed. at public schls. of Saint Mary's and Fredericton; mem. of Senate of Univ. of New Brunswick; admitted to the bar, 1882; K.C., 1898; judge, sup. ct., New Brunswick, 1909; King's lanch division, 1913; ch. just., King's Bench divn., 1924.

BARRY, RICHARD JAMES.—B. 1876; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; clk., C.S.O., Cape, 1895; C.S. Law, 1899; clk., mag. dept., 1901; asst. mag., various places; relief staff, 1904; ch. clk., master's office, Cape (insolv. brch.), 1906; Natal, 1912; Transvaal, 1912; master, sup. ct., Natal, 1915; ditto, Cape, 1923.

BARTH, SIR JACOB WILLIAM, KT. BACH (1922), C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Wadham Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1895; M.A., 1898; barrister-at-law, Mid. Temp., 1900; registr. and prin. registr. of documents, E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1902; Crown advoc., Oct., 1902; apptd. to serve on land coms., Oct., 1904; judge, high ct., E. Africa Prot., April, 1905; atty.-gen., E.A.P., Feb., 1914; ag. chf. sec., Aug., 1918; ch. just., Kenya, Aug., 1920; ret. 1934.

BARTLEY, THOMAS DOUGLAS MURRAY.—B.A. Trin. Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1925; war serv., Aug., 1914—Jan., 1920; adminstr. offr. (cadet) Tanganyika Territory, May 1920; asst. sec., May, 1922; ag. senr. asst. sec., Sep. to Nov., 1923; asst. adminstr. gen. and asst. registr. of companies, June, 1924 and Nov., 1925 to Jan., 1926; res. mag., Oct. 1925; ag. adminstr.-gen., ag. registr.-gen., births, deaths and marriages, ag. registr., companies, ag. liquidator German savings bank, ag. registr., trade marks, Jan. to Aug., 1926; asst. judge, high ct., Nigeria, 1934.

BARTLEY, WM., M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1885; B.A., Dublin; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1908; passed cadet, July, 1910; offr. of cls. V., Jan., 1912 passed in Javanese, Feb., 1912; dist. offr., Dindings, May, 1912; asst. registr., impts. and expts., S.S., Dec., 1915; sec., food control comte., June, 1917; mem., shipping comte., Apr., 1918; sec., food contrlr's. comte., Dec., 1918; agent, food controller, Singapore, June, 1919; services recog. by S. of S., Aug., 1919; sec., profiteering coms., 1920; supernmy. offr., cls. III, Nov., 1920; sec. to advising comte. on allowances, in addition, Dec., 1920; asst. adviser, Kelantan, Apr., 1922; sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1926; offr. cls. Ib comte., lands, S.S., Dec., 1926; cls. Ia; under-sec., S.S., 1930; ag. col. sec., S.S., May to June and Aug. to Sept., 1930; pres., mun. comsrs., Singapore, Mar., 1931; supernmy. offr., cls. Ia, S.S., 1932; supernmy. staff offr., S.S., July, 1934.

BARTOLO, PROF. HON. SIR AUGUSTO, KT. BACH (1929), LL.D., B. Lit., F.R.H.S.—B. 1883; matric., Malta Univ., 1901; B.Lit., 1904; called to bar, 1907; edr. for many years of "Daily Malta Chronicle"; prof., hist. of legislation and examr. in law and lit., Malta Univ.; chmn., Malta branch, Empire Parly. Assocn.; Malta Univ. del. at congress of Univa. of Empire in Gt. Britain, 1921; el. for 2nd divn. in leg. assem., Oct., 1921; re-el., June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for educn. and migr., Aug., 1927; judge, 1934; author of several works on Maltese hist. and Imp. affrs.

BARTON, A. E. V., C.B.E. (1936), O.B.E. (1933).—Imp. cust. serv., 1912; cust. ast., Kenya, Sept., 1919; collr. of cust., Apr., 1923; comprtr. cust., B. Guiana; collr., gen., Jamaica, 1927; collr., cust. and excise, Trinidad, 1930.

BARTON, C. J. J. T., O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1891; ed. Denstone and Downing Coll., Cambridge; history and law triposs.; B.A., 1913; M.A., 1920; ast. dist. comnrs., E.A.P., Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comnrs., 1917, 1919, 1921; ast. nat. affrs., 1923; ag. senr. ast. sec., 1924-26; ag. ast. col. sec., 1927; ag. prin. ast. col. sec. and clk. to exec. coun. on various occasions, 1928-33; senr. ast. col. sec., 1932; ag. dep. col. sec., 1934; col. sec., Fiji, 1935; ag. gov., Fiji and ag. high comnrs., W. Pacific, May-Nov., 1936; author various papers on Kenya tribes and local history.

BARTON, CECIL MOLYNEUX, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1883; ed. Shrewsbury and Trinity Coll., Dublin (hons., classics, law); barrister-at-law, King's Inns, Dublin, 1906; ast. dist. comnrs., E.A.P., 1913; res. mag., 1914-21; ag. judge, sup. ct., Apr., 1921 to Oct., 1922; legal adviser, Gambia, Dec., 1922; comnrs. to enquire into double inoculation of cattle, 1918; chmn. of comnrs. to enquire into inoculation of cattle for rinderpest by vety. dept., 1919; ag. judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1923-28; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; circuit judge, Gold Coast, 1929; puisne judge, 1934.

BARTON, GEORGE SAMUEL HORACE, C.M.G. (1935), B.S.A., D.Sc.A.—B. 1883; ed. Vankleek Hill High Sch., Ontario Agr. Coll., Toronto Univ. (B.S.A.), Laval Univ. (D.Sc.A.); ast. animal husbandry, 1907; lecturer, 1910; prof., 1911; dean, faculty of agr., Macdonald Coll., Metisill Univ., 1925; comdcr., Agricole Merite, Quebec govt., 1929; dep. min., agr., Canada, 1932.
BARTON, LANCELOT ALEXANDER.—B. 1894; acct. treas., Hong Kong, 1921; cashier, 1924.

BARTON, MAJOR PERCIVAL FILLER, O.B.E. (1935), V.D.—Ast. treas., G. Coast, Mar., 1907; addl. col. sec.'s office, Oct.-Nov., 1907, and Sept.-Nov., 1910; senr. ast. treas., Nov., 1908; ch. ast. treas., Apr., 1917; ag. treas. and currency offr., July-Oct., 1919 and Dec., 1920 to Aug., 1921; dep. treas., Jan., 1920; commanded G. Coast Volunteers, 1914 and during War; ment. in desps., Aug., 1917; col. treas. and currency offr., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1922; treas., Dominica, 1932; treas. and collr., cust., St. Helena, Mar., 1933.
BARTON, T., M.A.—B. 1904; qualified teacher Scottish educ. dept.; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Oct., 1928.

BARWELL, HON. SIR HENRY NEWMAN, K.C.M.G. (1922), LL.B. (Adelaide Univ.), Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1877; ed. St. Peter's Coll., Adelaide; mem., H. of A., S. Australia, Stanley dist., 1915-25; atty.-gen., 1917 and 1918-20; premier and atty.-gen., 1920-24; fedl. Senator, 1925; agt. gen. for S. Australia, 1928.

BASSETT, RALPH HENRY.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1920; addl. to Ratnapura Kach., Jan., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Jaffna, May, 1922; ast. stimlt. offr., July, 1925; pol. mag., Colombo, June, 1930; ast. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1933; comnrs., agr. marketing, Jan., 1935.
BATEMAN, G. S., L.D.S., R.S.C., Eng.—Serv. with Forces in E. Africa, 1915 and 1916; dental surg., Uganda, May, 1912.

BATES, ISAAC GRAINGER.—B. 1886; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1909; equity bar, 1909; mag. and legal adviser, Br. Solomon Is., with jurisdiction as a judl. commr. for the West Pacific, Nov., 1913; ag. res. commr. in admin. ing 1919; seconded for serv. as ch. pol. mag., May, 1920 to Dec., 1921; senr. mag., Tasman Territory, Dec., 1922; ag. judge, high ct., various periods, 1924-33; ag. solr.-gen., to Oct., 1927; puisne judge, Apr., 1931.

BATHO, CHARLES PHILIP ARTHUR, M.I. (1920).—B. 1883; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey and Victoria Coll., Jersey; control st. office, Cape Colony, 1900; sec., tenderbl. transf. Transvaal prisons dept., July, 1929; clk., July, 1910; prin. clk., Union prisons Nov., 1912; ch. clk., Nov., 1917; ast. dir., pris., Apr., 1926; is also commr. of oath for Cap. gr. grade supt., Pretoria cent. prison and Princ. gaol and outstations and warden, Baviaanspoort farm col. and inebriate reformatory, Sep., 1930; do., Johannesburg gaol and outstations warden, Leeuwkop farm col., Jan., 1934.

BATTERBEE, SIR HARRY F., K.C.M.G., K.C.V.O. (1927), C.M.G. (1918), C.V.O. (1918), B. 1880; ed. at Grammar Sch., Faversham and at Oxford; classical scholar, Harv. Coll., 1899-1904; 1st cls. class. moda., 1901; cls. math. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1901; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., ord. and audit dept., Jan., 1905; 2nd cls. clk., Oct., May, 1905; ag. 1st cls. clk., 4th June, 1916; cls. clk., 6th July, 1917; sec. to W. Afr. lands comtce., 1912; ast. priv. sec. to Mr. (now Lord) Harcourt, 9th Dec., 1912; priv. to Lord Irlington, 12th Oct., 1914; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, 31st Oct., 1915; priv. sec. to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Nov., 1916 to 13th Jan., 1919; priv. 1st Apr., 1919; ast. sec., 1st Mar., 1923; polit. sec. to Admin. commg. sp. serv. squadron during Empire tour, 1923-24; ast. sec., 12th Dec., 1924; polit. sec. to H.R.H. the Duke of York during his visit Australia and N.Z., 1927; ast. under sec. state for dom. affrs., 25th Jan., 1930; dep. imp. Confco., 1930; regnr., Order of St. Michael and St. George since 1930.

BATTERSHILL, WILLIAM DENIS.—B. 1878; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914-19; cadet, Ceylon, 1920; addl. to Colombo cust., May, 1921; addl. to Badulla kach., Jan., 1921; pol. office, Badulla kach., Mar., 1921; pol. mag., Panadura, Oct., 1922; dep. fiscal, Colombo, June, 1930; addnl. astl. col. sec., Jan., 1925; 4th astl. sec., Mar., 1925; 3rd astl. col. sec., May, 1925; 2nd astl. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun. May, 1925; astl. col. sec., Jamaica, 1928; ag. col. sec., various occasions, 1929-32; col. sec., Cyprus, 1934; ag. gov., Mar. and June-Oct., 1935 and July-Sept., 1936.

BAVIN, HON. SIR THOMAS RAINFORD, K.C.M.G. (1933), K.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1874; ed. Auckland, N.Z. and at Sydney Univ.; called to bar, 1897; prof. of law, Univ. of Tasmania, 1900; sec. to prime min. C. of A., 1901-4; M.I.A., N.S.W., from 1917; premier and col. treasurers, N.S.W., 1927-30.

BAXTER, JOHN BABINGTON MACADURY, K.C., D.C.L.—Elected to New Brunswick legislature, 1911; atty.-gen., 1914; leader of provincial opposition, 1920; min. of cust. in Dem. govt., 1921; prim. min., New Brunswick, 1925; judge, sup. ct., N.B., 1931; ch. just., N.B., 1935.

BAYLES, HERBERT LAURENCE.—B. 1886; ed. Dulwich Coll.; clk., C.A.'s office, 1905; etc. cust. serv., Nyasaland, 1909; P.W.D., 1910; Nyasaland Field Force, 1914; astt. finan. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1916-17; astt. treas., Zanzibar, 1921; dep. treas., Kenya, Mar., 1924; treas., Trinidad and chmn., agrl. bank, May, 1931; treas., and currency offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1933; treas., and currency offr., Nigeria, 1935.

BAYNES, EDWARD WILLIAM, C.B.E. (1928), O.B.E. (1920).—B. 1880; clk., G.P.O., Antigua, Jan., 1899; clk. to commr., Virgin Islds., Jan., 1901; priv. sec. and clk. to adminstr. Dominica, Aug., 1909; transf. to Impl. civ. ser. and apptd. 2nd cl. clk., National Health Insurance Commn. (England), June, 1912; 1st cl. clk., May, 1913; priv. sec. to sec., ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917; sec., National Health Insurance Joint Comtee., Oct., 1919; priv. sec. to parl. sec., ministry of shipping, Apr., 1920 to Mar., 1921; suptg. clk., Min. of Health, Oct., 1920; sec., "Discovery" comtee., C.O., July, 1923; col. sec., Leeward Is., Oct., 1925; admstr. govt. of Antigua on numerous occasions; sg. gov., Leeward Is., on various occasions, 1930-34; admstr., St. Lucia, June, 1935; ag. gov., Windward Is., June-Oct., 1936.

BAYNES, THOMAS EDWIN PERCIVAL, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1884; ed. St. Kitts Grammar Schl. and Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; clerical astt., col. sec.'s offr., Trinidad, 1903; 6th clk., C.S.O., 1903; 5th clk., C.S.O., 1906; 1st clk., post office, Antigua, 1907; 3rd clk., C.S.O., 1911; ag. clk. to adminstr., Dominica, 1912; 2nd clk., C.S.O., clk. of exec. and legis. couns., Antigua, and supt. of govt. printing office, 1912; clk. to adminstr., and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Dominica, 1919; offr. in ch. (later, warden), N. dist., 1924; temp. mem., leg. coun., Dominica, Feb., 1926; O.A.G., Montserrat, in Mar., 1926 and Apr., 1928; O.A.G., Virgin Is., Aug. to Nov., 1926; offr. mem., leg. coun., Dominica in 1927 and 1928; ag. astt. col. sec., Leeward Is., Apr.-Sept., 1927; treas., Dominica, June, 1928; offr. mem., exec. coun., Dominica, June, 1928; O.A.G., Montserrat June-Sept., 1929; O.A.G., Dominica various occasions, 1929-36; commr., Montserrat, 1932.

BAZELL, CHARLES—B. 1885; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar 1904-08); B.A. 1908 (2nd hon. mods., 2nd lit. hum.); astt. mast., Raffles Inst., S'pore, 1914-17 and 1922; headmast., Malay Coll., K. Kangsar, 1923.

BEARDMORE, ERNEST.—B. 1882; ed. Manchester and Capetown; B.A. (1907); LL.B. (1911); clk., Cape civ. serv., 1900; prof. astt. and ch. clk., solr.-gen., Cape, 1920; solr.-gen., Cape, 1931; crown pros., Witwatersrand, 1933; atty.-gen., O.F.S., 1933.

REATLEY, FRANK EDWARD MAMERTUS, O.B.E. (1937).—B. 1882; ed. Lucton Grammar Schl.; traffic supt., Mauritius govt. rly., 1914; dep. traffic man., Sierra Leone rly., Jan., 1925; traffic man., 1930; gen. man. and traffic man., 1933.

BEATTIE, Lieut.-Col. ALEXANDER ELDER, C.M.G. (1933), C.B.E. (1920), M.C. (1915), ret. pay (rea. of offr.).—Entd. Army (Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders), 1906; transf. on promotion to the Queen's (now The Queen's Royal) Regt., 1908; served in Aden and India; seconded for serv. with W.A.F.F., 1911; astt. adjutant, Southern Nigeria Regt., 1912; ag. regt. adjutant, S.N. Regt., 1913; adjutant, 4th Bn. Nigeria Regt., 1914; capt., 1914; Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914; staff offr. to various field columns, 1914-15; ment. in desps., 1914; M.C., 1915; severely wounded, 1915; astt. staff offr., W.A.F.F., Col. Office, 1915; staff offr. (gen. staff offr.), W.A.F.F., 1916; temp. major, 1916; offg. staff offr., K.A.R., 1915; promoted major by brevet, 1918; tempy. lieut. col., 1918; C.B.E., 1920; accompanied inspr.-gen., W.A.F.F., on tour of inspection, Nigeria, Gold Coast, Sierra Leone and Gambia, 1920; C.O. rep. on various inter-depl. comtees, 1915-21; ret. with rank of lieut.-col., Sept., 1922; recd. thanks of S. of S. for services under the C.O.; astt. sec., Gibraltar, Sept., 1922; res. inspr. of schls., Oct., 1922; clk. of exec. coun., Oct., 1922; sec., bd. of educn. (ex officio), 1922; local secy., Cambridge Univ. exams. (ex officio), 1922; ag. D.P.W. on two occasions; col. sec., Falkland Is., 8th Oct., 1925; ag. gov. from May, 1926 to Apr., 1927; ch. astt. col. sec., Cyprus, 8th Aug., 1927; ag. col. sec., on various occasions, 1927-29; col. sec., Gibraltar, Jan., 1930.
BEATTIE, JAMES, O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1891; European War, 1914-18; Somaliland Camel Corps, 1918-20; M.C., 1920; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, 1920; dist. commr., 1922; comdt., pol., Oct., 1928.

BEATTIE, Sir JOHN CARRUTHERS KT., (1920), F.R.S.E. and S.A., D.Sc., LL.D. (Ed. and Wits).—B. 1866; ed. Moray House, Edinburgh Univ.; Vans Dunlop schl. in Physics, 1851 Exhibn. Schl., Unvls. of Munich, Vienna, Berlin and Glasgow; prof. of physics, S. African Coll., 1897; medallist S. African Assncon. for Advancement of Science, 1910; crossed Africa from Cape Town to Cairo with Prof. J. T. Morrison on magnetic survey; chmn., survey comsn., 1921; pres., S. African Assncon. for Advancement of Science, 1928; prtn., S. African Coll., 1917-18; prin. and vice-chanc., Univ. of Cape Town since 1918; prin. publns.: rept. of a Magnetic Survey of S. Africa, Cambridge, 1909, and (in conjunction with Prof. J. T. Morrison) "On Magnetic Work in Southern and Central Africa," Washington, 1913.

BEATTY, Sir KENNETH JAMES, KT. BACH. (1926).—B. 1878; ed. Univ. High Schl. and Melbourne Univ.; qualified Victorian bar, 1900; Transvaal law certif. (including Roman Dutch law), 1904; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1908; served in S. African war as lieut., V.M.I., wounded in action (Queen's med. and 4 clasps); apptd. pub. pros., Transvaal, Nov., 1902; addtnl. astt. res. mag., Mar., 1903; astt. res. mag., Dec., 1906; attd., Natal Carbineers, Natal rebellion, 1906; recd. thanks of Natal govt., med. and clasp; pol. mag., master of sup. ct. and registr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Sept., 1908; has acted as ch. just., circuit judge and atty.-gen., Sierra Leone; on mil'y. serv., 1915-18; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1921; ch. just., Bermuda, 1924; ch. just., Bahamas, 1927; dep. gov. in 1928.; ch. just., Gibraltar, 1931.

BEAUBRUN, IGNATIUS CECIL, O.B.E. (1936).—B. 1889; treasy. clk., St. Lucia, 1906; astt. audit clk., 1907; rev. offr., St. Vincent, 1912; ag. audit clk., 1910 and 1912; rev. offr., Grenada, 1915; 3rd treasy. clk., 1916; ch. clk., treasy. and commr. of income tax, 1922; astt. treas., 1929; col. treas., collr. of cust., supt. of inld. rev., ch. of excise, Grenada, June, 1930; ag. col. sec. and registr.-gen., June, 1932.

BEAUCHAMP, 7th Earl (creat. 1815), Visct. ELMLEY (1815), Baron BEAUCHAMP (1806, U.K.); K.G.; K.C.M.G. (1899); WILLIAM LYGON; B. 1872; succeeded his father, 1891.—Ed. Eton and Chr. Ch., Oxford; D.L. Worcestershire; Mayor of Worcester, 1895-6; mem. London schl. bd., 1897-9; gov. N.S.W., 1899 to 1900; capt. of hon. corps of Gentlemen-at-Arms, 1906; first commr. of works, 1911-14; lord-pres. of the coun., 1910-15.

BEAUCHAMP, ARTHUR KENNEDY.—B. 1888; ed. Worcester Cath., King's Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford, B.A., 3rd cls. math. hons., 1910; headmst., gram. schl. and sub-instr., schls., Dominica, Sept., 1912; ag. instr., schls., Leeward Is., July-Oct., 1916; instr., schls., St. Lucia, Jan., 1921; supt., educ., S. Prov., Nigeria, 1926.

BEAUCHESENE, ARTHUR, M.A., K.C., LL.D., Litt.D., F.R.S.C.—B. 1876; ed. St. Joseph's (class course), Memramcook, N.B.; grad. as B.A., M.A., and Litt.D. valedictorian, 1895; studied law, Laval, Montreal; admitted to bar, Quebec, 1904; K.C., 1914; legal adviser, justice dept., 1913-16; clk., asst., House of Commons, Canada, and comsnr. to admstr. oath of allegiance to M. of P.; clk., House of Commons, 1925; author of "Beauchesne's Parliamentary Rules and Forms," "Écrivains d'Autrefois," etc.

BECK, DAVID MACDONALD HERBERT, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. Weymouth Coll. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; temp. lieut., W.A.F.F., Feb., 1917; admstr. offr., Nigeria, Jan., 1919; dep. res., Apr., 1933; res., Aug., 1934.

BECK, EWALD EGERTON HERBERT.—B. 1894; ed. Weymouth Coll., Dorset; pol. probtr., F.M.S., Oct., 1914; 1st asst. comsnr., pol., Selangor, Feb., 1923; supt., convict estabt., Taiping and instr., prisons, F.M.S., Sept., 1923; offr., i/c, pol. dist., Kuantan, Aug., 1927; comdt., pol., depot, K. Lumpur, Mar., 1929; supt., pol., S.S., Oct., 1931; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, Feb., 1932.

BECKETT, HAROLD.—B. 1891; ed. at Monmouth Grammar Schl. and Oxford; exhibitioner at Wadham Coll., 1910-1914; also Symons exhibitioner, 1912-1914; 1st cls. Class. Meds., 1912; 3rd cls. Lit. Hum., 1914; B.A., 1914; appnt. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 30th Nov., 1914; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Milner, 14th Jan. to 16th Mar., 1919; ag. 1st cls. clk., 17th July, 1919; priv. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir G. Fiddes), 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; mem., W. Indies currency comtec., 1923; sent to Mauritius as spec. comsnr. to enquire into and rept. to S. of S. on questions of currency and exchange, Apr., 1925; returned to C.O., Oct., 1925; ag. asst. sec., 28th Sept., 1931.

BECKETT, HERBERT JAMES RUST, A.C.A.—B. 1880; treas., N.
