Borneo, Feb., 1911; dist. treas., Apr., 1911; mag., 1st cls., Apr., 1914; seconded for mil. serv., 1916-19; dist. offr., Aug., 1919; asst. comsnr., cust. and excise, Mar., 1921; asst. comsnr., excise, Feb., 1926; auditor, Mar., 1929; finance contr., Dec., 1930.
BEDDOW, HERBERT HOWELL, B.Sc., hons. (Wales).—B. 1898; flying offr., R.A.F., 1916-19; Hong Kong educn. dept., 1927.

BEDDINGTON, GUI CYRIL, A.M.I.C.E., F.S.I., M.I.M. & Cy. E., E.S.I., M.R.San.I., Cltd. Civ. Engr. and Chtd. Survr.—B. 1889; ed. Univ. of Bristol; ast. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., July, 1913; exec. engr., Ipol., 1922; ag. senr. exec. engr., Kuantan, 1927; ast. ch. hydric. engr.'s office, K. Lumpur, Nov., 1929; ag. senr. exec. engr., waterworks, K. Lumpur, Oct., 1930; sr. exec. engr., F.M.S. & S.S., Klang, 1932; sr. exec. engr., wks. and bldgs., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1934.

BEGGIE, RALPH STOCKMAN, M.D., M.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. and H. (Edin.).—B. 1901; health offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1930; ag. malariologist, 1931; ast. bacteriologist, 1932; ast. bacteriologist, 1934.
BEKKER, SIMON POTGIETER.—B. 1882; sol., 1906; M.P.C., 1928; mem., ex. co., 1928; M.P., 1933-34; admstr., Transvaal Prov., 1934.

BELCHER, SIR CHARLES FREDERIC, Kt. Bact. (1931), O.B.E. (1923), M.B.E. (1919), M.A., LL.B.—B. 1876; ed. at Geelong Gram. Schl. and Trin. Coll., Melbourne (final scholarship in classics and comp. philology, Shakespeare scholarship); admitted to practise, Victoria, Oct., 1902; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, June, 1909 (cert. of honour); conveyancer, Uganda Prot., July, 1914; custodian of enemy property, Uganda, Apr., 1915; mag., Uganda, June, 1916; ast. judge, Zanzibar, June, 1920; judge of H.E. ct. of appeal for E. Africa, Aug., 1920 and June, 1925; atty. gen., Nyasaland, Sept., 1920; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ag. judge, high ct., Mar., 1921 to Jan., 1922; judge, high ct., May, 1924; ct. just., sup. ct., Cyprus, May, 1927; ch. just., Trinidad, 1930.

BELGRAVE, WILLIAM NORMAN CUMMINGS, B.A. (Cantab.), Camb. Univ. Dip. of Agr., B. 1891; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Antigua; J. Coll., Cambridge; ast. mycologist, agr. dept., F.M.S., Oct., 1914; plant physiologist, Jan., 1920; ch. divn. of soil and plant physics, Feb., 1927; deleg., 2nd imp. mycologia confere., London, 1929; ast. todir., in addn., Jan., 1930; in ch., divn. of agr. chem., in addn., Sept., 1930; ch. resch. offr., Sept., 1931.

BELL, ALAN MALCOLM.—B. 1905; ed. Warwick, Ireland and Bedford; junr. ast. grn. laboratory, Leeward Is., 1923; clk., col. sec. office, 1924; sub-instr., pol. and adjt. lea. forces, 1927; sub-instr., constab., Trin., 1928; A.D.C. to govt., 1930-31; local commr., pol., Cyprus, 1934; ag. comsnr., Feb., 1935 to Aug., 1936.

BELL, EDWARD PETER STUBBS.—B. 1902; clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, 1920; snr. clk., 1927; clk. to atty. gen., 1927; clk., ex. and leg. couns., Antigua, 1927; pvt. sec. to govt., Leeward Is., June, 1929 to Apr., 1930; seconded for dist. relief measures, Antigua, June-Nov., 1931; mem. dist. F and afterwards dist. E, Dominica, Nov., 1931; mag. dist. D and addnl. mag., dist. C, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1934; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1934; ag. comsnr., Montserrat, July to Sept., 1938; ag. crown atty., St. Kitts, May-June, 1938; ast. gen., St. Lucia, 1936.

BELL, MAJOR G. H., M.C., Officer Lea. d'Honneur.—B. 1885; entered army, 1906; India, 1907-9; Nigeria, W.A.F.F., 1911; ast. dir. of surveys, Nigeria, 1912-14; served in France, 1914-16; Palestine and Syria, 1917-18; dep. ch. polit. offr., Egyptian Expdy. Force, 1919; G.S.O., Intelligence, E.E.F., 1920; dir. topographic branch, Gold Coast Survey, 1920.

BELL, SIR HENRY HESKETH JOHNSON, G.C.M.G. (1925), K.C.M.G. (1908), C.M.G. (1908)—B. 1865; ed. in Paris; 3rd clk. gov.-in-chief's office, Barbados, May, 1882; transferred to treas. dept., Grenada, 1883; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Jan., 1890; sen. ast. treas., G. Coast, Aug., 1891; ag. dist. comsnr. and sheriff, Accra, 1892; ag. col. treas., 1893; rec-gen., Bahamas, Dec., 1894; represented Harbour Isld. in House of Assem., 1895-6; ag. col. sec., July to Oct., 1897, Apr. to Nov., 1898; admstr. of Dominica, Aug., 1899; ag. gov., Leeward Is., Aug., 1904; and from June, 1905; comsnr. and comdr-in-chief, Uganda Prot., Jan., 1906; gov., ditto.
ELL, P. S., M.B., B.S., M.R.C.P. (Lond.), C.S., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1897; military, 1915-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory,

ELL, THOMAS STEWART, B.A.—B. 1911; ed. staff High Schl. and Univ. Coll., S. Wales and mouthshire; 1st cl. hons., Greek; 2nd cl., liv., hons., Latin; Gwilym mem. pr.; Univ. Yales teacher's dipl.; Sach's student, Br. at Athens, 1933-34; admire. offr. (ass. surv.), Cyprus, Jan., 1935; comemt., Nov., 1935.

ELLANTI, J. J., LL.D., Malta Univ., 1910.— 885; ed. St. Ignatius Coll., Malta; matric. place in hons. list), Malta Univ., 1902; clk., serv., Malta, 1907; mem. of coun. of govt., asst. crown advocate, Gozo, 1914; assessor axes, Malta treasy., Apr., 1923.

ENNETT, ARTHUR GEORGE.—B. 1880; loc. serv., Aug., 1893; cust. offr., Falkland Is., 1911; spec. serv. as whaling offr. and gen. offr. to Antarctic Dependencies, 1913-27; Dependencies, 1921-23 and 1925-27; seal offr., 1921; asst. to govt. naturalist, 1922; govt. nat., 1924; author of "A List of Birds of the Falkland Is. and Dependencies," "Whaling Antarctic," and various scientific papers on whales, some in conjunction with "U.S. Discovery."

ENNETT, HON. SIR JOHN ROBERT, K.B.E. (6).—M.H.A., St. John's West, Newf'dld., M.E.C., 1913; col. sec., 1913; mem. ence comte., 1915; deputy chief censor, telephones and cables, 1914; press censor, 1915; min. militia, 1917.
ENNETT, JOHN SLOMAN.—B. 1914; ed. Liberty Schl., Romford and Magdalene Cambridge (schol.); 1st cl. hist. tripso., 1934 and pt. II, 1935; apptd. after compet. m. ast. prin., C.O., 2nd Nov., 1936.

ENNETT, MAURICE CHRISTMAS, F.S.I.— ved in Great War, 1914-19; ment. in desps.; R. of O.; joined O.E.T.A., Nov., 1918; orbed in civil adminstr., Palestine as ast. dir., de F. dept., lands and surveys, July, 1920.

ENNETT, RT. HON. RICHARD BRADFORD, P.C. P.C. (Can.), LL.B., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. pub. high schls., N.B., Dalhousie Univ., N.S.; n. degree LL.D., Dalhousie Univ., Queen's univ. and Univ. of Alberta; admitted to bar of P., 1903; mem., legis. assem., Northwest Teritories, 1898-1905; legis. assem. of Alta., 1909; Calgary in H. of C. Canada, 1911-17; min. just. and atty.-gen., 1921; min. of finance in high admstr., 1926; leader of conserv. party, 1927; prime min. of Canada after g.e., June, 1930; leader of opposition, 1936.

ENNETT, SYDNEY CHRISTMAS, A.M.I.C.E., M.Inst. Mun. and Cty. Engrs.—Ed. Sherborne Schl.; astt. engrn., P.W.D., E.A.P., 1914-15; pioneer, E. A. Pioneers, 1915-16; 2nd lieut., A. Rly. Corps., 1916-17; it., 1917-18; ment. in desps., "1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory medals; exec. engrn., Kenya, 1921.

ENNETTS, ALBERT THOMAS, A.C.S.M., A.I.M.E., M.A.I.M.—B.1890; ed. Camborne Schl. of Mines, 1908-11; medallist of the Royal Cornwall Polytechnic Soc'y., 1910; 1st cl. hons. and silver medallist, City and Guilds of London Inst., 1911; 1st cls. diploma, Camborne Schl. of Mines, 1911; mines dept., F.M.S., 1912-25; ast. ward., mines, Perak, Nov., 1929; senr. inspr., mines, Jan., 1931; ast. ward., mines, Selangor, Mar., 1932; sk. ward., mines, Pahang May, 1932; do., Selangor and N. Sembilan, Sept.,

BENSON, GEORGE ALLEN RALSTON.—B. 1879; 6th cls. offr., cust., Br. Guiana, 1901; 5th cls., 1904; 4th cls., 1911; 3rd cls., 1915; 2nd cls., 1924; 1st cls., 1925; ast. survr., cust., 1926; survr., cust., B. Guiana, 1927.

BENSON, SIR J. HAWTREY, KT. BACH. (1912), A.B., M.D., F.R.C.P. Irel.—Ed. Trim. Coll., Dublin; 1st hons. maths., graduated, 1864; consulting physician to Royal City of Dublin Hosp. and to the Monkstown Hosp. Fellow of Acad. of Med., Ireland; late censor and examin., Royal Coll. of Phys., Ireland, and late external examir. in med., Dub. Univ.; is now med. advir. for Ireland to C.O.; author of numerous papers and articles on technical med. subjects; ret., 1926.

BENTINCK, LILLIE JOSEPHINE, M.B., B.Ch., certif. L.S.T.M.—Lady med. offr., B. Gajah, May, 1921; Ipoh, July, 1921; K. Pilah, July, 1923; Taiping, Apr., 1925; K. Kangsar, June, 1925; lady med. offr., infant welfare centre, Taiping, Aug., 1925; Seremban, July, 1926; K. Lumpur, Sept., 1927; Ipoh, Nov., 1927.

BENTINCK, MAJOR WALTER GUY, BARON, C.M.G. (1912), D.S.O. (1902).—Ed. at Marlborough Coll., Jena Univ., Germany, and Royal Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; joined Rifle Brig., 1885; capt., 1894; major, 1902; ret., 1905; intelligence dept., W.O., 1899; served throughout S. African war, 1899- 1902; (ment. in desps., Queen's medal with six clasps, King's medal with two clasps); dist. comsrr., Vereeniging, 1900; res. mag., Wakkerstroom dist., Transvaal, 1901-1907; Transvaal mem. of Natal-Transvaal boundary comsrr., 1902; sec. in office of high comsrr. for S. Africa, 1907; ag. Imperial sec., Aug., 1907, to Mar., 1908; ast. Imperial sec., Apr., 1908; chmn. Swaziland water comsrr., 1909.
BENZIES, DOUGLAS, M.A., B.Sc.—B. 1899; ed. Heriot's Schl., Edinburgh; Edin. Univ., 1919- 25; M.A., B.Sc.; France, June-Dec., 1918; demob., Jan., 1919; ast. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

BERENDSEN, CARL AUGUST, C.M.G. (1936), LL.M.—B.1890; ed. Australian pub. schls., Gore Dist. High Schl. (N.Z.) and Victoria Univ. Coll.; entl. N.Z. educ. dept., 1906; lab. dept., 1916; chf. clk., 1917; dep. regir., industrial unions, 1919; Imperial affrs. offr., prime min.'s dept., 1926; perm. head, prime min.'s dept., 1935; also sec. for external affrs., from 1928; mem., N.Z. deleg. to L. of N. assembly, 1936.

BERNE, CAPT. JAMES LKO. O.B.E. (1918).— B. 1885; ed. Dulwich Coll.; 2nd Lieut., Royal Irish Regt., 1904; capt., 1910; served with Sierra Leone Batt. R.W.A.F.F., 1907-1912; served with Franco-Liberian bndry. comsrr., 1908 (reed. thanks of French govt.); served with Anglo-French bndry. comsrr., 1911; ast. dist. comsrr., Somaliland Prot., Sept., 1912; dist. comsrr., March, 1914; served with Admiralty war staff, intell. div., Apr. to Aug., 1916; accompanied mission to Abysinnia on the occasion of coronation of Empress Zauditu, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia, 1917; sen. ast. sec. to govt. of Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1921; ax. ast. ch. sec., Sept., 1922 to May, 1923, Dec., 1923 to March, 1924; ag. prov. comsrr., Apr., 1930 to May, 1931 and Feb., 1932- Mar., 1934; dep. prov. comsrr., Feb., 1931; mem. leg. coun., 1931; prov. comsrr., Sept., 1934.

BERTEAU, F. C., I.S.O. (1914).—Ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, 1865-72; ent. Newfoundland civ. ser. as clk., H.M. Customs, 1878; collr. of cust., Labrador, 1881; clk. in col. sec.'s off., 1889; ch. clk., 1890; J.P. for col., 1893; mission to Ottawa to inquire into Canadian fin. system, with view of applying same to Newfild., May, 1898; audr.-gen. Newfild., July, 1898; ag. cashier, Newfoundland savings bank, 1911.

HERTRAM, SIR ANTON, KT. BACH. (1916); K.C. (1913).—Ed. at City of London sch., and Camb.; scholar of Caius Coll., 1887; Fellow, 1891; 1st cl. class. trip., part I, 1890; ditto, part II., 1891; Powis medal for Latin verse; Chancellor's medal for classics, 1891; B.A., 1890; M.A., 1892; president, Union Soc., 1891; Tancet law studentship, Lincoln's Inn, 1890; called, 1893; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1902; elected to H. of Assem., June, 1903; ag. col. sec., May to Sept., 1903; admstd. govt., Sept. to Oct., 1905; ag. ch. justice, Sep.-Nov., 1906; chrmn. of comm. on the Out Is., 1906; puisne judge, Cyprus, 1906; atty.-gen., Ceylon, 19th May, 1911; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. ch. justice, Apr., 1918; ch. justice, July, 1918; services lent to govt. of Palestine, Dec., 1920 to May, 1921; ret., 1925; joint author, "Handbook to Workmen's Compensation Acts, 1897 and 1900."

BESSBOROUGH, 9TH EARL OF (creat. 1739), Rt. Hon. VERS BRABAZON PONSONBY, P.C. (1931), G.C.M.G. (1931), C.M.G. (1919), B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1880; 2nd lieut., Bucks, I.Y., 1903-8; temp. capt., Suffolk yeo. and adjutant, 1915; served in Great War, 1914-18; L.C.C., 1907-10; M.E., Cheltenham, 1910 and Dover, 1913-20; J.P., Co. Kilkenny; gov.-gen. of the Dominion of Canada, 1931-35.

BEST, HON. SIR ROBERT WALLACE, K.C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1856; vice-pres. of the exec. coun., Australian Commonwealth; leader of the Deakin govt. in the Senate, Feb., 1907; min. for trade and customs, C. of A., 1909; now retd.

BEST, SIR THOMAS ALEXANDER VANS, K.C.M.G. (1932), K.B.E. (1926), C.M.G. (1916), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1870; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; Taylorian Univ. scholar; gold medalist of French Ministry of pub. instruction; asst., B. Cent. Africa admstn., 1896; judicial offr., Mar., 1898; ag. ch. judicial offr., Nov., 1899, to Mar., 1900; ag. vice-consul, Chinde, Aug., 1902, to Apr., 1903; ag. dep. consnr., Apr. to Dec., 1903, and June, 1905, to Jan., 1906; dist. res., 1st cls., Apr., 1906; col. sec., Falkland Is., Oct., 1909; ag. gov., Dec., 1909, to Dec., 1910; attended Imp. Edcn. Confce, 1911; col. sec., Leeward Is., Jan., 1913; ag. gov., May to Sept., 1914, Apr. to Aug., 1915, and from Jan., 1916, to Mar., 1919; recd. thanks of H.M.'s Govt. for services during disturbances in Antigua in Mar., 1918; col. sec., Trinidad, Aug., 1919; ag. gov., June-Aug., 1920, June, 1921 to Jan., 1922, June to Oct., 1923 and from Aug., 1924; lieut.-gov., Malta, 1925; O.A.G. for various periods in 1926, 1927, 1928 and 1929; gov., Windward Is., Sept., 1930; ret., 1933.
RETANCOURT, NELSON, M.I.P.T.—B. 1887; sworn survr.; 2nd asst. inspr. of mines, Trinidad, April, 1909; 1st inspr., mines, Apr., 1921; ag. inspr., mines, in 1927, 1933 and 1934-36.

RETHEL, CHARLES PERCIVAL, L.S.O. (1929).—B. 1891; ag. 4th clk., post office, Bahamas, Apr. and Nov., 1909; cadet, Outislands, Feb., 1910; 3rd clk., C.S.O., and clk. to pub. bd. of wks., New Providence, Sept., 1912; 2nd clk., C.S.O., Feb., 1914; ch. clk., C.S.O., Aug., 1919; enlisted for army, Apr., 1919; resumed duty, ag. clk. in charge, col. sec.'s office, July, 1923; ag. col. sec. and temp. men's med. on various occasions, 1923-34; ag. inspr., admstr., Sept. to Nov., 1925; ass't educ. July, 1928; ag. col. sec. and temp. men's med. coun., various periods, 1934-36.

BETHELL, WILLIS H.—B. 1859; teacher, Bahainas, 1887; customs officer, 1901; ag. chief clk., audit dept., 1912; chief clk., audit dept., Apr., 1913; audr. pub. accs. for various periods between 1923; audr., Dec., 1925; audr., Dec., 1925.

BETTINGTON, DIGBY ROWLAND.—Ed. at United Serv. Coll., Western Cape; served with Natal Mounted Pol., 1889; 2nd Kitchener's Fighting Scouts (dangerously wounded, med. in despatch, and 5 clasps); inspr., mil. pol., 1903; inspr., pol., Sierra Leone, 1906; pol. and sheriff on several occasions; asst. of pol. and dep.-supt., prisons, Gold Coast base comdt., Togoland Field Force, 1914-15; "Star, War and Victory medals; 1st dep. inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, inspr.-gen., pol., Gold Coast on various depts. of pol. and prisons, 1920; kn. med., 1920; ret., 1924; commdt., 1924; resigned, 1926; chief of pol. Res.

BEVIR, ANTHONY, M.A.—B. 1895; Ed., Eaton, 1909, and of Hertford Coll., 1913; M.A., 1923; served with 7th Bn., Liverpool Regt., July, 1915 to Apr., 1919; ment. in despatches; asst. prin., C.O., 9th accompanied Mr. Ormaby-Gore on his tour of West African Colonies, Jan.-May, 1920; sec. to Mr. Ormaby-Gore, Feb., 1920; Apr., 1929; sec., C.O., confce., 1930.
BEWES, GEORGE PRICK HOPE.—B. 1891; Clifton Coll. and Royal Naval Coll., 1909; dist. consnr., Gold Coast, 25th Aug., 1911.
BEYERS, LT.-COL. LEONARD.—B. 1891; staff, Union defence forces, 1914-31; commdt. Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1932.

BHAROJAI, NAVROJI MANEKJI.—Tab.—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv.; addnl. pol. mag., Kurunegala, June, 1910; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Apr., 1910; Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1917; pol. mag., Balapitiya, Nov., 1917; Balapitiya, Nov., 1917; judge, Tangalla, Mar., 1922; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, Jan., 1922; dist. judge, Kalutara, Dec., 1932, do., Badulla, July, 1933.
BICKLE, W. H.—B. 1885; appt. compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div., and to C.O., 17th Mar., 1906; oler. offr., 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., 4th Sept., 1922.

BIDEN, CLAUDE ERIC DE BER—ed. Radley Coll.; asst. audr., S. Nigeria on active serv., attd. Nigeria Overseas German K. Africa, and W. African Service, 1917-19; senr. asst. audr., Nigeria, 1919; audr., Gold Coast, 1930; mem. regiment comtees, 1931; ag. audr., May, 1931; Palestine and Trans-Jordan, May, 1933.
BIDMEAD, KENNETH ANDREW.—pol. probnr., Hong Kong, Nov., 1929; pol., Aug., 1932; attd. Punjab pol., 1935.

BIENEMAN, G. A. J., B.A.—R. 1888; Close, Cheltenham and Emmanuel bridge; junr. supt., educn. dept., 1935. uly, 1913; head of training coll., Oct., 1921; ch. aspr. educn., N. Provs., Nigeria, 1929; ag. nat. dir., educn., N. Provs., 1931, 1932, 1933 and 34; ag. dir., educn., Nigeria, 1934; ast. dir., Iaan., N. Provs., 1936.

BIGELOW, HENRY VERDIER, K.C., B.A., L.B., M.A.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schs., Truro, S., and Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1894; LL.B., 1896; M.A., 1897; admitted to bar of N.S., 1896, practised law at Truro, N.S., and Regina, Sask.; C., Sask., 1913; judge, King's Bench, Sask., eb., 1918.

BIGG, WILFRED JOSEPH.—B. 1897; boy clk., P.O., 22nd Nov., 1912; apptd. after compet. ram. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to C.O., 1st Dec., 1914; on mil. ser. from 31st Aug., 1914, to 8th May, 1919; cler. ffr., higher grade, 1922; ast. prin., 1st Aug., 1925; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec., D.O. (Sir Ed. Harding), 3rd Feb., 1930; prin., 22nd June, 1931.

BINNIE, THOMAS REGINALD.—B. 1886; ed. din. Acad.; warehousekeeper, K. Lumpur, June 1921; supr. cust., Oct., 1922; supt., cust., Oct., 1923; sr. supt., trade and cust., Jan., 1931; prpvr., ext. duties, F.M.S. and asst. supt., rubber ont., S'pore in addn., Feb., 1935.

BIRCH, HUGH MASSEY, M.I.C.E.—Ed. privately at Kelvedon Col., Ldn.; trooper, M.M. Pol., 1897; exec. engr., P.W.D., E.A.P., 1907; local ast., E.A. Forces, 1916-18; exec. engr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1918; B.S.A. Coy.'s, Br. War and Victory medls.
BIRCH, W. C.—Ast. analyst, E.A.P., July, 914; senr. chemical offr., Apr., 1923.

BIRCH, WILLIAM SOMERSET, M.C., M.R.C.S. Eng., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), B. 1889; ed. King's Coll. and King's Coll. Hosp.; served R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1914-19; sen. ast. med. offr., Jamaica genl. hosp., 1923-24; med. supt., do., 1925-26; ast. med. supt., lunatic asylum, Barbados, 1927; med. supt., do., 1928; ast. ch. med. offr. for various periods, 1933-34.

BIRD, EDGAR CLARENCE FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. Xaverian Coll.; ast. ch., cust., Zanzibar Prot., Oct., 1914; ast. ch., cust., Zan. bar to Aug., 1915, May, 1916 to Feb., 1917, and Dec., 1917; marshal, H.B.M. Court for Zan. bar (in Prize), Jan. to Aug., 1915 and from Dec., 917; immigr. offr., Sept., 1915; dep. comptr., cust., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1920; ast. comptr., cust., on various occasions, 1921-29; ast. comptr., cust., Nigeria, 1930; comptr., Nov., 1931.

BIRD, REGINALD.—B. 1893; ed. Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1919; war serv., 1914-19; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; ast. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, June, 1920; ast. dist. judge, S'pore, Aug., 1922; ast. sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1924; dist. offr., K. Pilah, July, 1925; ast. advr., B. Pahat, Aug., 1928; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1929; ast. stamp and prob. offr., S'pore, Aug., 1933; ast. lep. treas., S.S., May, 1934; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1934.

BIRKINSHAW, FRANK.—B. 1887; ast. agrl. supt. and sc. mast., gram. schl., St. Vincent, Aug., 1912; agrl. instr., Mauritius, Jan., 1916; inspr., co-op. credit socs. in addn., Oct., 1915; agrl. supt., Sept., 1919; ast. agrl. instr., Perak, F.M.S., June, 1920; agrl. field offr., Perak North, Dec., 1924; ditto P. Wellesley and Penang in addn., Apr., 1926; sr. agrl. field offr., Perak, Sept., 1931; state agrl. offr., Jan., 1934.

BIRKITT, FRANK.—B. 1885; Imp. postal serv. (tels.) 1898-1902, mails, 1903-07; admstve. (cler. and acctg.) 1908; staff record offr., 1909; postmtr., Nyasaland Prot. (various offices) 1912; (active serv., 1914-15, N.V.R., Nthn. Rhodn. Forces), ag. ast. P.M.G. various occasions; 1st clc., 1920; ch. ast. and stkpr., 1923; P.M.G., Br. Guiana, 1927; temp. M.L.C. in 1932-34; ch. acct., Kenya, Uganda and Tanganyika Territory postal serv., 1935; senr. dep. postmtr., do., 1936.

BIRSE, ARTHUR LOUIS.—B. 1893; cadet, S.S., Apr., 1921; colnr., income tax, S'pore, Sept., 1921; treas., exec. omtte., Br. Malaya, Br. Emp. Exhibn., in addn., Jan., 1923; recvr., Alsagof Concession, Kubub, Mar., 1926; offr., ol. III, June, 1930; offr., cls. II, June, 1935; dist. offr., Larut and registr., titles, Perak, Feb., 1936.

BIRTWISTLE, WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Schl., Blackburn; ast. naturalist (fisheries), min. of agr. and fisheries; directed research in dept. of oceanography, univ., Liverpool; fisheries economist, S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1924; offr.-in-ch., fisheries dept., S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1928; attended 5th Pacific Science Congress, Vancouver, B.C., as S.S. and F.M.S. govt. deleg., June, 1933.

BISCOE, VINCENT FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. at Cheltenham Coll. and Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A., June, 1908; 2nd lieut. London Rifle Brig. (Territorial Force), June, 1909; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1910; ast. res., N. Nigeria, Aug., 1911; 2nd cls. dist. offr., Dec., 1919; ag. station mag., Dec., 1924.

BISHOP, CECIL WILLIAM EDWIN, B.Sc.(Eng.), A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.Inst.W.E.—B. 1899; ed. Reigate Grammar Schl. and King's Coll., Lond., Univ.; war serv., 1918-19; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1922; ag. ex. engr., waterworks, 1935.

BISSET, WILLIAM DAVID, O.B.E. (mil.), A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1889; ed. Gordon Schl., Huntly, Aberdeenshire, and Allen Glen's Schl., Glasgow; engr. lieut., R.N., Jan., 1914; engr. lieut. comdr., R.N., Apr., 1919; engr.-in-ch., Misida dockyard, Malta, 1916-19; H.M.S. "Egmont," 1920; ast. engr., marine dept., Nigeria, 1920; prin. engr., 1933; ag. supt. engr., in 1934, 1935 and 1936.

BLACK, ARCHIBALD MAULE.—Ed. S. African Coll., Cape Town; ent. oiv. serv., Cape Colony, 1898; addnl. ast. regisr., sup. ct., Cape Colony, 1903; ast. regisr., 1904; regisr. and taxing mast., sup. ct., Cape Town and high sheriff for prov. of Cape of Good Hope, 1928; on active service, 1914-19; ment. in desps.; awarded V.D., 1921; major, Union R. of O. (arty.).

BLACK, JOHN ALAN.—B. 1891; cadet F.M.S., 1914; offr., cls. V., 1917; offr., cls. IV., 1921; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1926; ast. prot., Chinese, S'pore, Dec., 1926; ast. prot., Chinese, S'pore, Nov., 1929; do., Dec., 1930; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1931; ast. immigr. offr., Jan., 1933; ast. pres., munic. comsrs., Penang, Nov., 1934.

BLACK, JOHN GRAHAM.—B. 1896; cadet, S.S., May, 1920; pol. mag., ast. dist. judge and dep. regisr., sup. ot., Malacca, 1920; passed cadet, June, 1921; coroner, Malacca, Mar., 1923; colnr., land rev., May, 1923; offr., cls. V., May, 1923; priv. sec. to O.A.G., S.S., May, 1925; ast. dist. offr., Tapah, June, 1926; ditto, Kroh, Feb., 1927; offr., cls. IV, May, 1927; ast. advr., Trengganu, Nov., 1927; res., Labuan, Feb., 1930; offr., cls. III, May, 1932; ast. ist. ast. estab. offr., M.E.O., Oct., 1933.
BLACK, THOMAS.—B. 1894; supt., accs. and corresp., port developmt. dept., Hong Kong,

BLACK, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1872; ed. pub. high schls., Ont.; grad. in agr., Toronto Univ.; editor, "Farmers' Advocate," Winnipeg, 1902-04; dep. min., agr., Manitoba, 1904-06; pres., Manitoba Agric. Coll., 1905-15; sec., economic and development comm., Canada, 1915-16; comm., of agr., Canada, 1916-18; cmm., soldier attnt., bd., 1918-20; dep. min., immigr. and colonization, 1921; man., colonisation dept., Canadian National Rly., London, 1923; ch. colonisation dept., C.N. rly., Montreal, 1924.

BLACKABY, ERNEST JAMES, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1902; ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp., London; house surg., R. Infirmary, Sunderland, Feb., 1924; res. surg. offr., dist. infirmary, Ashton-under-Lyne, 1924; house surg., London Lock Hosp., 1925; ast. med. offr., Tonbridge Infirmary, 1926; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, July, 1928; ag. surg. spec., in 1929 and 1930; med. offr., E.A.M.S., Nyasaland, 1931; med. offr., Zanzibar, 1936.

BLACKALL, HENRY WILLIAM BUTLER, K.C., B.A., L.L.B. (1st place).—B. 1889; ed. Stonyhurst and Trinity Coll., Dublin; Victoria prizeman, jurisprudence and internat. law; 1st of 1st cls. hon., mod. history; John Brooke scholar, King's Inns; called to Irish Bar, 1912; crown counsel, E.A.P., Oct., 1919; M.L.C., Apr., 1920; crown counsel, Nigeria, May, 1923; ag. solr.-gen., on various occasions, 1923-31; mem. comm. on riots, S.E. Provs., 1930; atty.-gen., Cyprus, Jan., 1932; atty.-gen., Gold Coast, 1936.

BLACKWELL, BERNARD FLETCHER.—B. 1906; ed. Woodford Acad., N.S.W.; R. Australian Navy, 1924-30 (paym.-lieut.); ret., 1930; corres. elk., New Hebrides, Sept., 1931; ag. acct., Jan.-June, 1933 and Aug., 1935-Apr., 1936.

BLACKWELL, CAPT. FRANKS.—B. 1885; Impl. P.O., 1901-13; ast. supt., posts and tels., F.M.S., Nov., 1913; on mily. serv., 1914-21; personal ast. to dir., posts and tels., F.M.S., Feb., 1921; ast. supt., posts and tels., S.S. and F.M.S., Oct., 1926; contrlr., ditto, Jan., 1929; contrlr., Perak and Dindings, Nov., 1929; ag. contrlr., posts and tels., S'pore., Nov., 1930; contr., do., Nov., 1932; ag. dir., posts and ag. sec., Malayan postal bd., June, 1934.

BLACKWELL, KENNETH RAY.—B. 1896; ed. Shebbear Coll., N. Devon; war serv., R.F.A., July, 1915-Nov., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; attchd., Pahang secr., Apr., 1921; ast. supt., chandu monop., Ipoh, June, 1922; supvr., cust., Johore, Aug., 1923; offr. cls. V. ast. dist. offr., Gopang and Lampar, Nov., 1923; passed inter. L.A.A., Dec., 1925; ast. dist. offr. Kroh and ast. supvr., cust., Jan., 1926; offr. cls. IV, Nov., 1927; ag. supt., convict estabt., F.M.S., in addn., Feb., 1928; offr. cls. III, Nov., 1932; state treas., Perak, Feb., 1934; chmn., sany. bd., Larut in addn., Oct.-Nov., 1934.
BLACKWOOD, A. MC.A., M.B., Ch. B. (Glasgow).—B. 1885; lieut., R.A.M.C., May, 1917; capt., 1918; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1919; senr. med. offr., Jan., Nov., 1932.

BLAINE, CHARLES HERBERT.—Ed. St. Andrews Coll., Grahamstown, and Dover Coll. (Eng.); served, S.A. War, 1901-02; Great War, 1914-18; clk., Christiana, Transvaal, civ. rec. clerk; pub. proc., mag.'s office, Johannesburg, 1908; ch. f. clk.; ast. clk., A.R.M., Germiston, Jan., 1908; ditto and ast. mag., Krugersdorp, 1909; ditto, Barberton, 1911; senr. clk. and senr. pub. pros., Johannesburg, 1919; addnl. mag., Roodepoort, 1920; ditto, Durban, 1923; ditto, Johannesburg, 1924; 1st gde. mag. and native comm., Marico at Zeerust, 1929; senr. gde. mag., Zeerust, 1931; under sec., justice, Pretoria, July, 1934.

BLAIR, HON. ARCHIBALD WILLIAM.—B. 1894; ed. Wellington Coll. (N.Z.), and Canterbury and Victoria Univ. Colls.; judges associate, 1894; admitted barrister and solr., 1899; experience in legal offices, 1898-1910; in practice, 1910-20; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1928.
BLAIR, HAROLD ARTHUR.—B. 1902; R. Lancing and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; ast. Gold Coast, Apr., 1928; ast. dist. comm., Nov., 1928.

BLAIR, HON. SIR JAMES WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1935), KT. BACH. (1930)—B. 1871; called to bar, 1894; M.L.A., Queensland, for Ipswich, 1926; atty.-gen. in Morgan and Kidston ministry, 1930-37; also sec. for mines for most of that period; sec., pub. instr., Denham ministry, 1937; puisne judge, Apr., 1922; ch. just., Queensland, 1925.

BLAIR, ROBERT WESTERP, A.R.C.S., F.I.C.—B. 1885; ed., George Watson's Coll., Edin.; R. Coll. Sc., Dublin; ast. chem., Inst., 1905-06; ast. chem., Metro. Water Bd., Lond., 1906-11; 1st ast. chem., Wellcome Resch. Lab., Khartoum, Sudan, 1911-12; ast. chem., Inst. for Med. Resch., K. Lumpur, F.M.S., 1912; chem., Inst. for Med. Resch., K. Lumpur, F.M.S., 1913; 2nd lieut., Lancs. Regt., 1915; tempy. lieut., 1916; brigade R.E., 1916; ment. in desp., 1918; 2nd capt., R.E., 1918; chem., Inst. for Med. Resch., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1919; 2nd lieut., M.S.V., Dec., 1922; 1st lieut., July, 1924; extra A.D.C. to high commnr., Nov., 1924; ch. chem., Inst. for Med. Resch., Mar., 1927; capt., M.S.V., May, 1927; hon. A.D.C. to high commnr., May, 1930.

BLAKE, GEORGE STANFIELD, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.R.S.M. Lond., M.I.M.M.—B. 1876; R. Sch. of Mines, London; on teaching staff, 1896-97; Imp. Inst. (mineral survey of colls.), 1901; Newfoundland and Canada, 1915; munitions inspect., Eng., 1916-19; staff imp. Coll. of Sci., 1920-22; geol. adviser, Palestine govt., 1922.

BLAKELY, DONALD QUENTIN.—B. 1875; ast. atty.-gen.'s off., B. Honduras, Apr., 1888; regisr.-gen., May, 1906, to Mar., 1907; 1st clk., regar.-gen.'s office, Apr., 1907; ag. regisr.-gen., Oct., 1908, to May, 1909; commr. of colls., B. Hond., Mar., 1908; ag. regisr.-gen., 1908, to May, 1909, and Aug., 1912.

BLAND, CHARLES HEBER.—B. 1886; ed. high and high schl., Pembroke, Ont., and Queen's Univ., Kingston, Ont.; B.A. (1907); ent. Canadian civ. serv. at first compet. exam., 1909; sec. with Can. Field Art'y., 1917-19; ast. sec., ent. comm., 1919; ch. examr., 1921; civ. ser. comm., 1933; chmn., 1935.

BLANDFORD, HON. SYDNEY D.—B. 1876; M.H.A., Newfoundland; elected for Bonavista, 1904, 1908 and 1909; M.E.C. and min. of agric. and mines, 1909; M.L.C. and min. of agric. and mines, 1914; sheriff of Newfoundland, 1917.

BLANDY, CAPT. R. D.—B. 1891; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; ent. Indian pol., 1911; Inc. Army, R. of O., Apr., 1915; active serv., S.W. Frontier and Mesopotamia; returned India pol., Jan., 1919; dist. ast., pol., 1920-24; ch. international pol., Tangier, July, 1928; asst.

BLANKENBERG, SIR R. A., K.B.E. (1920), O.B.E. (1918)—Rhodesian service, 1895 to 1902; conf. clk. to lieut.-gov., Transvaal, 1902; ag. priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., Aug., 1902; ch. clk. to agt.-gen. in London, 1st June, 1907; ch. clk. to high comsnr. for Union of South Africa, London, 1910; asst. sec. to high comsnr., 1912; sec. to high comsnr., 1919; ag. high comsnr., 1920; ret., 31st Mar., 1926.

BLAXTER, KENNETH WILLIAM.—B. 1895; ed. Malvern and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.); milly. serv., France and Italy, 1915-19; asst. prin., miny. of transport, 1920; sec., royal comsn. on compensation for suffering and damage by enemy action, 1923; asst. prin., C.O., 1924; pvt. sec. to perm. under-sec. of state for the cols., 10th Dec., 1928; pvt. sec. to parly. under sec. of state for cols., 17th Sept., 1929; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

BLEDISLOE, 1ST BARON (cr. 1918) RT. HON. SIR CHARLES BATHURST, P.C. (1926); K.B.E., (1917).—B. 1867; ed. Sherborne, Eton, Univ. Coll., Oxford and Royal Agrl. Coll., Cirencester (gold medallist and late chmn. of govs.); chancery barrister and conveyancer, 1894-1910; M.P. South Wilts Divn., 1910-18; mem. of coun. of Duchy of Lancaster; parly. sec. to miny. of food, 1916-17; chmn., R. comsn. on sugar supply, and dir., sugar distribution, 1917-19; parly. sec. to miny. of agr., 1924-28; chmn., R. comsn. land drainage, England and Wales, 1927; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, New Zealand, 1930-35.

BLOFELD, THOMAS GUEST.—B. 1902; ed. Hilton Coll., Natal and Bedford Sch.; supt. power stations, elec. dept., Ceylon, 1928; asst. engr., elec. dept., F.M.S., 1933; inspr., machinery, mines dept., Gold Coast, 1936.

BLONDIN, LIEUT. COL. THE HON. PIERRE EDUARD.—B. 1874; ed. Seminaire de Nicolet and Laval Univ., Montreal; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1908; re-el., g.e., 1911; el. dep. speaker, 1911; min. of inland rev., 1914; sec. of state, 1915; P.M.G., Canada, 1917; overseas, 1917-18; apptd. to senate, 1918; re-apptd. P.M.G., 1920; speaker of senate, 1930.

BLOOD, HILARY RUDOLPH ROBERT, C.M.G., (1934), M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1893; on milly. serv., Oct., 1914-1919; tempy. employed at C.O., Mar.-Dec., 1919, Aug., 1924 to May, 1925 and Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1930; cadet, Ceylon, Jan., 1920; attd. to Colombo Kach., Feb., 1920; Anuradhapura Kach., Nov., 1920; attd., Kandy Kach., Apr., 1921; office asst., govt. agrt., Cent. Prov., June, 1921; 4th ass't col. sec., Apr., 1922; 3rd ass't col. sec., June, 1925; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1926; asst. govt. agrt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., Colombo, Mar., 1930; col. sec. and regisr. gen., Grenada, 1930; O.A.G., Windward Is., June to Aug., 1930; admtd. govt., Grenada, on several occasions; col. sec., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1934; ag. gov., Oct.-Nov., 1934 and May-Oct., 1936; govs. dep. on several occasions, 1935.

BLOUNT, AUSTIN ERNEST, C.M.G.—B. 1870; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir Chas. Tupper, Bart., 1896-1901; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir Robt. L. Borden, prime min. of Canada, 1901-1917; olk. of parlms. and clk. of senate, 1918.

BLOXHAM, CAPT. HUGH FITZHERBERT.—B. 1893; ed. Bradfield Coll.; 4th batt., Wiltshire Regt., 1914-15; 102nd K.E.O. Grenadiers, I.A., 1915-24; served, Mesopotamia, 1915-19 and Afghan War, 1919-20; twice ment. in desps.; asst. supt., pol., Hong Kong, Feb., 1924; asst. supt., prisons, Apr., 1925; ag. supt., prisons, Apr., 1925; ag. asst. supt., pol., Mar., 1926; ag. supt., prisons, Mar., 1929; dep. dir., prisons, Gold Coast, Oct., 1930; prison supt., S.S., 1932.

BLUMER, REV. REGINALD CHARLES, M.A., B.Sc., Dipl. Ed.—B. 1889; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ.; Caird schol. in chem.; B.A., 1910, 1st cls., hon., Latin, 2nd cls., hon., maths.; B.Sc., 1910; M.A., 1912; dipl., ed., 1921; deacon, 1912; priest, 1913; curate, St. John's, Parramatta, 1912-17; curate, Holy Trinity, Dulwich Hill, 1918; asst. mast., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1921-25; vice-prin., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

BLUNT, DENZIL LAYTON, M.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1891; ed. Shrewsbury and King's Coll., Camb.; served under court of wards, Madras, 1913-14; served H.M. Forces, 1915-19; lieut., R.A.S.C.; senr. agrl. offr., Kenya, 1926; asst. to dir., agr., Kenya, 1930; dir., agr., Cyprus, Aug., 1935.

BLYTHE, WILFRED LAWSON.—B. 1896; ed. Birkenhead Inst. and L'pool Univ.; war serv., 1916-18; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; priv. sec. to ch. sec., F.M.S., Feb., 1925; offr., cl. IV, Apr., 1928; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; ag. prot., Chinese, Penang, Sept., 1933.
BOAST, HENRY WALTER—B. 1887; clk. Swaziland, 1909; registr. and mast., sup. ct. and sheriff, 1928; dep. asst. comsnr., 1930.

BODENSTEIN, HELGARD DEWALD JOHANNES, LL.D. (Leyden), barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, —Clk. mag.'s offices, Krugersdorp, Lydenburg, Pietersburg, 1909; Piet Retief, 1910; sec., judge pres., J. de Villiers, 1910-12; ch. clk. and professional asst. to atty.-gen., Bloemfontein, Apr., 1912; prof., Roman Dutch Law, Univ. of Amsterdam, Aug., 1912; asst. edr., "Die Burger," 1920; prof., Roman Dutch Law, Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1922-1927; sec. to prime min. and for external affrs., 1927.

BODILLY, COMMDR. RALPH BURLAND, R.N.—B. 1884; ed. Bradfield Coll. and H.M.S. "Britannia," 1899; midshipman, China station, 1901; sub. lieut., Atlantic Fleet, 1905; lieut., Home Fleet, Jan., 1907; invalided, 1909; called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1913; on war serv., Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1919; lieut. commdr., Jan., 1915; promoted commdr. for war services, Nov., 1918; res. mag., Jamaica, Feb., 1925; ag. judge, Kingston ct. on various occasions, 1927-31; acted in appellate ct. on various occasions, 1928-31; pres., marine and water cts.; ch. mag., Palestine, Oct., 1932; author of "Inter-action between Vessels."

BODKIN, GILBERT EDWIN, F.E.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1886; ed. at Repton Schl., Derbyshire, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1908; Cambridge Univ. diploma of agric., 1910; govt. economic biologist, dept. of science and agric., Br. Guiana, 1911; Carnegie scholar in economic entomology, and as such toured the prin. agrl. stations of the United States, 1912-1913; rep. Br. Guiana at W. Indian Agrl. Conf., 1913; asst. dir. of sci. and agr., and dep. chmn., bd. of agr., Br. Guiana, 1919; editor, jnl. of the bd. of agr., 1918; joint editor, Br. Guiana Handbook, 1921; govt. entomologist, Palestine, June, 1922; offr.-in-ch., locust campaigns, 1928-30; pres., Internat. Locust Bureau, Damascus, 1930-31; dir., agr., prin., coll. of agr. and regisr., co-op. credit socs., Mauritius, 1932; author of numerous papers on the insects of Br. Guiana and Palestine, scientific rept. to govt., etc.

BOILEAU, LEWIS HAROLD.—B. 1888; ed. Monkton Combe Sch., Bath and Garriock Chambers (Schoones); asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; ag. prin. asst. sec., Apr., 1923; ag. prin. asst. sec., S. Prov., in 1925, 1926 and 1927; admstv. offr., cls. I, Aug., 1930; prin. asst. sec., Dec., 1930.

BOISSIER, CAPT. JOHN SCOBELL, B.Sc. (hons.), M. Inst. W.E.—B. 1894; ed. Exeter Schl. and Birmingham Univ.; asst. engnr., F.M.S., Aug., 1920; sec. for ser. in Johore, Jan., 1924-Sept., 1929; exec. engnr., P.W.D., Aug., 1929; exec. engnr., rural, S'pore., Nov., 1934.

BOK, DR. WILLEM EDUARD, JUNE.—Priv. sec. to prime min., Transvaal, June, 1907; sec. to prime min. and clk. to exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, May, 1910; law adver., Feb., 1916; sec. for just., Aug., 1918; judge, high ct., S.W. Africa, Feb., 1930; judge, sup. ct., S. Africa, Griqualand West local divn., 1933.

BOLAND, FREDERICK MALCOLM, LL.B.—B. 1891; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1913; mag., County of St. Patrick, Trinidad, Mar., 1926; mag., eastern dist., Jan., 1927; asst. mag., St. George West, 1933; mag., Victoria, 1934; ag. puisne judge, May-Dec., 1935.

BOLTON, ARTHUR, B.A., Dipl.—B. 1897; ed. Rugby and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (class. exhib.); B.A. (hons. chem.), 1920; dipl. educn., 1921; lab. asst., H.M. facty., Sandycroft, 1916; research, Queen's Coll., Oxford, 1921; served in France, 1917-18; wounded; lieut., O.T.C., 1923; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

BONAVIA, WALTER JOSEPH.—B. 1895; on mil. serv., 1914-1916; clk., secretariat, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1917; asst. polit. offr. (cadet), Oct., 1920; admstv. offr., 2nd grade, Oct., 1923; ag. ditto, 1st grade, Feb. and Nov., 1923 to Aug., 1925; ag. dist. offr., in 1926, 1928, 1929-31; dist. offr., Oct., 1931.
BOND, MAJOR B. W., M.C.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, May, 1920.

BOND, RALPH NORMAN, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1900; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. Putalam kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, Hambantota kach., Oct., 1924; office asst., Badulla kach., Jan., 1926; pol. mag., Gampola, Jan., 1930; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Oct., 1931; do., Kandy, June, 1933; do., Kurunegala, Dec., 1934.

BONE, MAJOR CHANDOS ERIC M.C., F.S.I., L.S. (Vict.).—B. 1889; survr., 1st grade, rev. surveys bch., Perak, Oct., 1913; lieut., R.F.A., May, 1916; capt., Apr., 1918; major, Aug., 1918; asst. supt., rev. surveys Selangor, Nov., 1920; supt., surveys, Trengganu, May, 1925; asst. supt., surveys, S'pore., Sept., 1927; supt., surveys, F.M.S. and S.S., 1933; ag. asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S., Oct., 1933; ag. dep. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., May, 1934; asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S., Apr., 1935.

BONE, MAJOR THOMAS, O.B.R., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1879; London Schl. of Trop. Med., 1924; 13 yrs. army; 3 yrs. A.D.V.S. army divn.; O.C., vety. hosp.; senr. vety. offr., remount depot; dir., vety. dept., Mozambique Territory; govt. vety. surg., Br. Guiana, 1928.

BONHAM CARTER, Lieut.-Gen. Sir CHARLES, K.C.B. (1935), C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1919). D.S.O. (1917).—B. 1876; ed. Clifton Coll., Sandhurst and Staff Coll.; served S. African war, 1900-01 (Queen's med., 4 clasps); European war, 1914-18; (D.S.O., C.M.G., bvt. col., officer, Legion of Honour, American D.S.M.); maj.-gen., 1926; dir., staff duties, W.O., 1927-31; comdr., 4th divn., 1931-33; lieut.-gen., 1933; dir.-gen., Territorial army, 1933; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Malta, 1936; col., Queen's Own R. Kent Regt., 1936.

BONYUN, E. C. B.—B. 1885; ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados and privately; copyist, po. mag.'s courts, Dist. "A," Barbados, Jan., 1906; 2nd clk., registn. off., Barbados, Sept., 1906; dep. registr., from Jan. to Feb., and Sept. to Nov., 1910; ch. clk., registn. off., Nov., 1913; supervisor of customs, Nigeria, July, 1914; asst. recvr., sup. ct., Nigeria, for the purpose of winding up affairs of German and Austrian firms Nov., 1914; hon. asst. sec. to West India Contingent Comtee. (whilst on vacation leave of absence, and with permission of the S. of S., Sept. to Dec., 1916; ag. supt. of customs, 1st grade, Feb., 1916; asst. censor, Bonny, Feb., 1916; censor, Bonny, May, 1916; ag. recvr. of enemy estates, Nigeria, June to Dec., 1916, and Jan., 1917; collr., 2nd grade, headqrs., Jan.-Mar., 1917 and Sept., 1917, to June, 1918; hon. sec., comtee, Nigerian comforts for overseas forces, Sept., 1917 to June, 1918; in charge of Port Harcourt cust., June-Nov., 1918; ag. supt., 1st grade, Degema, July, 1919 to Jan., 1920; asst. collr., Lagos, Feb.-May, 1920; ag. senr. collr., Lagos, May-July, 1920; collr., Victoria, July, 1921.

BOOCOCK, JOHN JAMES.—Enlisted in Car Mounted Rifles, 1897; forester, Transkeian conservancy, Cape, 1903; supt. of plantations, 1904; asst. dist. forest offr., Umtata, Feb., 1905; Butterworth, Dec., 1908; Kambi, Oct., 1909; Umtata, Apr., 1912; dist. forest offr., Kologha, Oct., 1913; conservator of forests, Natal Conservancy, May, 1920; served with Cape Mounted Rifles during Anglo-Boer war, 1899-1902; served with S. Africa Heavy Arty., Apr., 1917 to June, 1919; conservator, Eastern conservancy, 1926; do., Western conservancy, 1931; forestry inspr., W. insprorate, 1932.

BOON, HERBERT M.B.E. (1935).—Compr., treasy. dept., St. Kitts, May, 1908 to Mar., 1916; 2nd clk., admstr.'s office, Apr., 1913; ch. clk., post office, St. Kitts, May, 1915; clk. to admstr. and clk. exec. and leg. couns., Deer., 1924; has held various ag. appts.; treas., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1935.

BOOTHE, LEONARD HORACE VERTUE.—B. 1866; ed. R. Naval Coll., Osborne and R. Naval Coll., Dartmouth; European War, 1914-18; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, Dec., 1919; asst. supt., Feb., 1922; ag. astb. harbrmr., Apr., 1922 and Feb., 1926; supt., pol., 1931; asst. dir., criminal intelligence, 1931; ag. dir., do., 1932, 1934 and 1935.
BOOTH, R. W.—B. 1879; stock. inspr., Basutoland, 1911; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1915; inspr., 1925; staff offr., 1929.

BORDEN, Rt. Hon. Sir ROBERT Laird P.C. (1912), G.C.M.G. (1914), K.C., LL.D.—B. 1854; ed. Acadia Villa Acad., Horton, N.S.; called to the bar, 1878; head of firm Borden Ritchie and Chisholm, Halifax; pres. Nova Scotia Barristers' Society, ten years; Q.C., 1906; LL.D., Queen's and St. Francis-Xavier Unive.; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g. e., 1896; re-elec. 1900; defeated g. e., 1904; re-elec., bye-elec., 1905 and g. e., 1908, 1911 and 1917; leader opposition in H. of C. 1901-1911; president of the King's privy council for Canada and prime minister, Oct., 1911; prime minister and S. o.f. for external affairs; mem. of the Peace Conference of Versailles, 1919, as representative of the Dominion of Canada; resigned premiership, 10th July, 1920. ADIAN REP. TO ARMAMENTS CONFEE., WASHINGTON, ONT.; CHAN., QUEEN'S UNIV., KINGSTON, ONT.

ORG, ANTHONY J., M.D., MALTA.—B. 1889; I.C.OFFR., FIJI, 1916; D.M.O. AND M.O.H., 1917; AG. R.M.O., MED. SUPT., PUB. LUNATIC LUM AND VISITING MED. OFFR., SUVA GAOL, 1919; I.O., NADROGA, 1920; D.M.O. AND M.O.H., 1921; DITTO, LEVUKA, 1923; DITTO, LABASA, 1924; D.M.O. AND DIST. COMSRR., RA (CONJUNCT), 1925; D.M.O., BA, 1934.

OSMAN, ANDREW MURRAY.—B. 1888; ED. INDRACHT HIGH SCHL., PRETORIA; ONTARIO AGRIC. SCHL., UNIV. OF TORONTO; LECTR., ANIMAL HUSBANDRY, POCHFESTHOF SCHL. OF AGRIC., 1913-15; VICE PRINC., FACULTY OF AGRIC., UNIV. OF PRETORIA, 1917-33; CH., DEPT. OF AGRIC. EDUCATION AND EXTENSION, DEPT. OF AGRIC., FORESTRY, AND PROF., ANIMAL HUSBANDRY, UNIV. OF PRETORIA, 1934.
OSTOCK, H. K.—B. 1892; ED. REPTON; & N.W. RY., CREWE, 1909; ON MILITARY SERV., 1914-19; DIVNL. SUPT., NIGERIAN RY., 1928; SUPT., ELECTRIC POWER AND EQUIPMENT, 1934.

BOSTON, HUGH MERRICK.—B. 1893; ED. ST. ALBANS H.E. SCHL., NEW BRIGHTON, LIVERPOOL RY., M.B., CH.B. (LIV.), 1925; SERVED WITH 2ND LANCERS (98TH COUNTY PALATINE) FIELD AMBULANCE (T.F.), 1914-19; MED. OFFR., ACHIMOTA COLL., COAST, APR., 1928.

BOTHAMLEY, CHARLES MILDMAY.—B. 1891; ED. TERRACE SCHL. AND WELLINGTON BOYS' COLL., N.Z.; COMTEE. CLK., N.Z. H. OF R., 1917; LEGIS. COUN., 1919; 2ND CLK. ASST., LEGIS. COUN., 1924; DEP. CLK. OF PARLIAMTS. AND CLK. ASST., LEGIS. COUN., 1931; CLK. OF PARLIAMTS., CLK., LEGIS. COUN., AND EXAMR., PETITIONS FOR PTE. BILLS, 1934.
BOTTOMLEY, JOHN HUBERT, A.M.I.Struct.E., F.R.S.A.—B. 1899; ED. THE SALT SCHL., SHIPLEY, BRADFORD SCHL. OF ART. AND BRADFORD TECH. COLL.; ARCHIT., P.W.D., HONG KONG, 1924; CAPT., H.K.V.D.C.

BOTTOMLEY, SIR WILLIAM CECIL, K.C.M.G. (1930), C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1921), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1878; ED. OWENS COLL. AND TRIN. COLL., CAMBR., ENTRANCE JUR. SCHL., 1896; SCHL., 1898; B.A. AND 3RD WRANGLER, 1900; ENT. C.O. AFTER COMPET. EXAM. AS 2ND CLK., 22ND OCT., 1901; SEC. TO COL. SURV. COMTEE., 1905; SEC. TO INTER-DEPT. PENSIONS COMTEE., 1907; ASST. PRIV. SEC. TO LORD CREWE, 16TH APR., 1908; PRIV. SEC. TO SIR F. HOPWOOD, 1ST FEB., 1909; PRIV. SEC. TO COL. SEELEY, 1ST JAN., 1911; PRIV. SEC. TO LORD LUCAS, 24TH MAR., 1911; PRIV. SEC. TO LORD EMMOTT, 26TH OCT., 1911; 1ST CLS. CLK., 1913; PRINCIPAL CLK., 8TH DEC., 1917; ASST. SEC., 1ST APR., 1920; MEM., E. AFRICAN CURRENCY BD., 1920; ASST. UNDER SEC. OF STATE, 1927.

BOUCAUD, JOSEPH ERROL.—B. 1904; ED. ST. MARY'S COLL., TRINIDAD; ADMITTED SOLR., 1927; PRIV. OFFR. AND 2ND DEP. MARSHAL, SUP. ET., TRINIDAD, 1933; DEP. REGISTR. AND MARSHAL, JAN., 1935; REGISTR.-GEN., 1936.

BOUCAUD, JOSEPH ERWIN ADOLPHUS, M.R.C.S. (ENG.), L.R.C.P. (LON.), M.B., B.S. (LON.).—B. 1892; ASST. RES. SURG., COL. HOSP., PORT OF SPAIN, TRINIDAD, 1919; RES. SURG., COL. HOSP., SAN FERNANDO, 1923; RES. SURG., COL. HOSP., P.O.S., 1928; SEN. MED. OFFR. (SURG.), COL. HOSP., P.O.S., 1935.
BOULDERSON, G. H. C.—AASST. DIST. COMSRR., E. AFRICA PROT., MAR., 1912; 1ST GRADATE ADMTVE. OFFR., JAN., 1924; PRIV. COMT., 1934.

BOURDILLON, SIR BERNARD HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1934), K.B.E. (1931), C.M.G. (1924).—ED. TONBRIDGE AND ST. JOHN'S COLL., OXFORD; ASST. MAG., UNITED PROVS., INDIA, NOV., 1908; UNDER-SEC. TO GOVT., MAR., 1913; REGISTR., HIGH CT. ALLAHABAD, MAY, 1915; APPTD. TO I.A.R.O., SEPT., 1917; J.T. MAG. (SECONDED), MAR., 1918; JUDL. ASST., PERSIAN GULF, JUNE, 1918; SETTLEMENT OFFR., BAGHDAD, MAR., 1919; POLIT. OFFR., BAQABA, OCT., 1920; POLIT. SEC. TO HIGH COMSRR., JAN., 1921; SEC. TO HIGH COMSRR., DEC., 1921; SEC. TO HIGH COMSRR., JAN., 1922; COL. SEC., CEYLON, NOV., 1929; O.A.G., APL. TO SEPT., 1930; CH. SEC., JULY, 1931; GOV. AND COMD.-IN-CH., UGANDA, 1932; DO., NIGERIA, 1935.

BOURNE, JOHN DUNBAR MACDONWALL.—B. 1886; EXAMINER, E. AND A. DEPT., FEB., 1906; ASST. AUDITOR, N. NIGERIA, AUG., 1909; ASST. AUDR., BR. GUIANA, FEB., 1920; AUDR., SOMALILAND PROT., JULY, 1921; AUDR., B. GUIANA, OCT., 1923; SENR. ASST. AUDR., NIGERIA, JULY, 1927; AUDR., SIERRA LEONE, 1930.

BOVELL, SIR HENRY ALLEYNE, KNT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1854; GILCHRIST SCHOLAR (LONDON UNIV.), JAN., 1873; LL.B. (LOND.), JAN., 1877, WITH 2ND CLASS HONOURS IN JURISPRUDENCE AND ROMAN LAW; CALLED TO THE BAR, LINC. INN., NOV., 1876; SOLR.-GEN., AND EXCHEQUER GEN. OF BARBADOS, AND J.P., MAR., 1882; M.L.C., JULY, 1883; MEM. OF COMAN. TO REVISE LAWS OF BARBADOS, MAR., 1886; CHANCELLOR OF THE DIOCESE, 1884; ATTY.-GEN., OCT., 1886; ATTY.-GEN., BR. GUIANA, 1896; AG. LEGAL ASST., C.O., DEC., 1900 and 1901; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1902; ret., 1912.

BOVELL-JONES, LIONEL CLIFFORD.—B. 1897; ed. Christ's Hosp.; enlisted 1915; served in France, Feb., 1916-Aug., 1918; demob., 1919; sub inspr., pol., British Guiana, July, 1920; dist. inspr., 1924; offr.-in-ch., punitive expd. to Venezuelan bndry., 1925; passed colloquial Hindi exam., 1927; county inspr., 1931.
twin_text: BOVELL-JONES, Lionel Clifford.—B., 1897; ed. Christ's Hosp.; enlisted 1915; served in France, Feb., 1916-Aug., 1918; demob., 1919; sub inspr., pol., British Guiana, July, 1920; dist. inspr., 1924; offr.-in-ch., punitive expd., to Venezuelan bindy., 1925; passed colloquial Hindi exam., 1927; county inspr., 1931.

raw_text: BOWEN, F. C. P.—B. 1896; copyist, audit dept., B. Honduras, 1912, also depts. of col. sec. and regiar-gen.; 3rd cls. clk., atty.-gen's off., 1915; 2nd cls. clk., ditto, 1918; clk. to regiar-gen., 1919; secy., riot inquiry comm., 1919; ag. regiar-gen. and cust. enemy ppty. and contrl., L.C.O., 1920; secy., yellow fever inquiry comm., 1921; ag. regiar-gen., 1921, 1923 and 1925; regiar-gen., regiar., lands titles, supt., vice-adm'y. and bankcy. cts., companies, patents, births, marriages and deaths, 1927; J.P.; stamp commr., dir., pub. offrs. guarantee fund; commr., income tax, 1927; sec. in arbitrn. between govt. of U.S.A. and Guatemala, 1930; dist. commr., Belize, offr., recr. in bankruptcy, offr., admstr., estates, coroner, 1933; ag. ass't. col. sec., 1934; postmr. Rhodesia, Oct., 1919; asst. native commnr., Feb., 1922; native commnr., Oct., 1928; dist. offr., grade III, Apr., 1929; do., grade II, Apr., 1931; sec. as asst. sec., Aug., 1931; asst. col. sec., Jamaica, Aug., 1935.

BROWN, GEORGE PRITCHARD.—B. 1885; served with E. Africa pol. service batt., 1914-17; capt., Aug., 1916; Turkana expdn., 1916 (A.G.S. med.); asst. supt. of pol., Kenya, Apr., 1916; supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1923; staff offr. to commnr., pol., 1926; ag. dep. commnr., pol., July to Oct., 1931; offr., order of St. John of Jerusalem; asst. commnr., pol., Jan., 1933; ag. commnr. pol., June, 1934 to May, 1935.

BROWN, GERALD HORACE.—B. 1875; ed. Ottawa pub. schls. and Coll. Inst.; journalist and parly. correspondent; asst. dep. min. of labour, Canada, 1909-17; sec., reconstruction and development comttee; re-apptd. asst. dep. min. of labour, 1920; Canadian del. League of Nations, Internat. Lab. Confce., Geneva, 1921.

BROWN, HARRY WHIDDEN, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1868; ed. Acadia Coll., and Dalhousie Univ.; called to Nova Scotia bar, 1893; ent. civ. serv., Can., 1896, as priv. sec. to min. of militia and defence; dir. of contracts, militia dept., 1904; commnr., war purchasing commn., 1919, returning to militia dept., on the dissolution of that commn., 1920; asst. dep. min. of militia, 1920.

BROWN, HON. JAMES T., K.C., B.A.—B. 1871; ed. pub. and high schls., Huntington, Quebec, and McGill Univ.; called to bar, 1896; K.C., 1907; elec. to Sask. legislature, 1905; judge, sup. ct. of Sask., 1910; ch. just., ct. of King's Bench, 1918.

BROWN, JAMES VICTOR RAEBURN.—B.1897; ed. Falkirk High Sch. and Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin.; B.Sc. (Agric.) Edin., F.S.I.; Agr. and Suth. Hldrs., attd. R.F.C., Egypt, France, Italy, 1916-19; E.C.G. Corp.; post-grad. stud., Camb., 1925; prov. supdt., agr., Sierra Leone, 1925; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika, 1931; seconded, Zanzibar, 1931.
BROWN, JOHN BATHURST.—B. 1904; ed. Shaftesbury and H.M.S. "Worcester"; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

BROWN, KENNETH VINCENT.—B. 1890; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1915; mag., eastern dist., Trinidad, Dec., 1924; asst. mag., county of St. George West, 1926; mag., county of Victoria, June, 1930; do., St. George West, 1932; ag. judge in 1933, 1935 and 1936; 3rd puisne judge, June, 1936.

BROWN, MONTAGUE, M.B.E. (1933), B.Sc. Econ. (Lond.), A.C.I.S., A.L.A.A.—Office supt. and sec., gen. agrl. coun. and comttee. on agrl. economics and marketing; office, commrs. of income tax and super tax, 1915; 38th Bn. R. Fusiliers, 1918-19; Palestine govt. treas., 1920; dept. of agrl. and forests, 1922; sec., Palestine trade delegn. to Egypt, 1935.
BROWN, PHILIP PENTON, B.A.—B. 1903; ed. Rugby and Trinity Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1924; 2nd cls. hon., philos. and econ.; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

BROWN, RALPH PHELPS.—B. 1877; ed. pu. and high sch., Nova Scotia and Boston; 2nd cls. elk., militia dept., Can., 1904; 1st cls. elk., 1907; asst. acctnt., 1910; asst. acctnt. and paymaster, 1917; ch. acctnt., 1918; gen. audr., 1920.

BROWN, ROBERT ALFRED.—B. 1888; draughtsman, Kano and Kaduna waterworks, Nigeria, 1914; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1915; senr. exec. engrn., 1927; ag. asst. D.P.W. 1929-34; ag. dep. D.P.W., 1934; ag. supt., nat. adminstr. wks., 1935; asst. D.P.W., 1936.

BROWN, ROBERT ALLAN.—B. 1897; ent. P.O. savings bank, Dec., 1912; trans. to gen. bd. of control for Scotland, Feb., 1914; apptd. asst. dir. after compet. exam., Dec., 1914; junr. dir., 26th Apr., 1918; on mily. serv., July, 1915 to Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; trans. to C.O., 27th Apr., 1922; seconded as acctnt., treasury, Cyprus, 1928 to 1930; treasury, accnt., Gambia, Aug., 1930; asst. recor. gen., 1933; ag. recor. gen., June to Oct., 1934; J.P., 1936.

BROWN, THOMAS ALGERNON.—B. 1900; ed. Marlborough Coll. and Wellington Cadet Coll., S. India; 2nd lieut., 16th Cav., India Army, Feb., 1920; 1st lieut., Feb., 1921; active serv. Waziristan Field Force, Feb., 1920-May, 1922; lieut. regular army R. of O., June, 1922; B.A. (Oxf. Coll.), Oxford; barrister-at-law; 2nd cls. hcs. jurisprudence; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1926; crown coun., Gold Coast, July, 1933; ag. solr.-gen., Aug., 1934, Nov., 1935 and June, 1936.
BROWN, WILLIAM TWEEDIE STODART, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1903; ed. St. Bees Schl., Edinburgh Univ. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; asst. comm. forests, Gold Coast, 1926.
BROWNE, GEORGE GILHAMSTONE, A.C.A.—B. 1898; acct., survey dept., S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1924; acct., P.W.D., S.S., Apr., 1932; sec. acct., F.M.S., Aug., 1934.

BROWNE, RAYMOND HAMILTON.—B. 1901; ed. St. Paul's Schol. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; B.A., LL.B.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1924; Midland circ.; advoc., Uganda bar, 1928; dist. mag. and coroner, Antigua, July, 1929; ag. atty.-gen., Leeward Is., on occasions; cls. mem., legis. coun., Antigua; res. mag., Jamaica, Nov., 1930; ch. regist., sup. ct. and prot. ct., Nigeria, 1936; regist., W. African ct. of appe., 1936.

BROWNE, ROLAND PAUL LAMBERT.—B. 1887; ed. at Marlborough Coll.; served as "élève externe" at British Consulate, Danzig, June, 1904 to July, 1909; asst. to res. commn., New Hebrides, Feb., 1912; commn., Cyprus, Jan., 1924; attd. col. sec.'s office in 1932 and 1934; admstr. offr., Jan., 1935; clk., exec. coun. in 1935 and 1936; ag. ch. ass't sec., various periods, 1936; ag. col. sec., July-Aug., 1936.

BROWNE, VERNON DENNISON.—B. 1892; secretariat clk., N.E.R. admstrn., May, 1909; mag. clk., N. Rhodesia (Fort Jameson) Ac., 1911; treas. clk., Mar., 1913; asst. acctnt., Mar., 1914; served in R.A.F., 1918-19; senr. asst. treas., N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1930; ag. dep. treas. on various occasions, 1927-34.

BRUCE, HON. R. RANDOLPH, B.Sc., C.E., F.R.G.S., LL.D. (Glas.).—B. 1863; ed. The Gymnasium, Old Aberdeen and Glasgow Univ.; emigrated to Can., 1887; on eng. staff, Can. P.Ry., 1887; silver-lead mining Br. Columbia, 1897-1926; lt.-gov., Br. Columbia, 1926-31; pres., Mine Owners Assn. of Interior, 1923-25; hon. col., 16th Can. Scottish; do., 29th Vancouver Regt.; Canadian minister, Tokyo, 1936.

BRUCE, Capt. The Rt. Hon. Stanley Melbourne, C.H. (1927), P.C. (1923), M.C. (1915).—Member of the H. of R. for Flinders since 1918; served in European War, 1915-17; capt., R. Fusiliers, Dec., 1914; twice wounded and invalided from the Army, Apr., 1917; Croix-de-Guerre, 1916; resigned chairmanship of firm of Paterson, Hughes ministry, Dec., 1921; prime min., Commonwealth of Australia, 1923-29; hon. min. and res. min. in London, Lyons ministry, 1932; leader Australian delegation to Ottawa conference, 1932; resigned from miny. and apptd. high commr. in Gt. Britain, 1933.

BRUCE, Thomas Dundas Hope, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1886; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; final hona., schol. of jurisprudence, 1906; admitted solr., sup. ct. of England, 1911; crown solr., Fiji, Nov., 1913; ag. atty.-gen., Jan. to May, 1915; ag. chief pol. mag., ag. egistr. of sup. ct., ag. reg. of titles, ag. curator of intestate and vacant estates, ag. public trustee, May to July, 1916; ag. atty.-gen., Dec., 1916; empty M.L.C., Aug., 1917; on active serv., Dec., 1917 to May, 1919; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1919 to May, 1920; dep. commr., Western Pacific, May, 1920; ag. res. commr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., May, 1920 to May, 1921; ag. atty.-gen., Fiji, May to Dec., 1921 and Mar. to Apr., 1922; apptd. a judi. commr. for the W. Pacific, Dec., 1921; seconded for sp. serv. with the W. Pacific High Commr. to preside over certain murder trials in the Solomon Is., Dec., 1921 to Mar., 1922; res. mag., Jamaica, July, 1922; called to bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1925; solr.-gen., Kenya, Apr., 1927; ag. atty.-gen., in 1932 and 1933; King's proctor, Dec., 1928; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1936.
BRUCE, Thos. Robert.—B. 1862; 2nd clk., sol. sec. off., St. Helena, Jan., 1898; postmaster, St. Helena, Apr., 1898.
BRUCE-JONES, Douglas.—B. 1903; ed. Univ. Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1928; res. mag., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1935.
BRUEN, John Patrick, B.E. (Dub.).—B. 1904; asst. engr., Dr. Guiana, 1930; asst. engnr. to sea defence bd. of commrs., 1934.
BRUMAGE, Douglas O.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1919; 1st grade admstve. offr., Kenya, Dec., 1930.
BRUNNEN, John Ewart.—B. 1893; admn. and dockyard schls.; H.M. Navy, 1914; admn., 1915; engnr., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. asst. elec.engnr., 1922; ag. sen.asst.elec.engnr., 1924.
BRUNT, Abraham de Villiers.—B. 1879; Cape civ. serv., 1897; Tvl. civ. serv., 1901; commr., Robben Is., 1915; oh. clk., health dept., 1919; under sec., health dept., Union of S. Africa, 1932.

BRUTON, Charles Lamb, O.B.E.—B. 1890; ed. Radley Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford, B.A., 1913; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, 1914; 1st gr. admstve. offr., 1924, senr. dist. offr., 1935; prov. commr., 1936.

BRYAN, Sir Herbert, K.B.E. (1925), D.S.O. (1917), C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1865; 2nd lieut. Linc. regt., June, 1892; lieut., Jan., 1894; capt., Manchester regt., July, 1899; brev. major, Nov., 1900; served in W. Africa, 1897-8; employed in Lagos Hinterland; also in operations on the Niger, including expdtion to Bassema (ment. in desp. medal with two clasps); served in N. Nigeria, 1900, slightly wounded (ment. in desp., clasp); served in operations in Ashanti, 1900; on the staff, D.A.A.G. (ment. twice in desp., medal); chief staff offr., Gambia expdt., 1901 (ment. in desp., medal with clasp); staff offr., W.A.F.F., Oct., 1901; employed at C.O., May 1902, to Nov., 1903; ag. col. sec., G. Coast, Nov., 1903; col. sec., G. Coast, Feb., 1904; ag. gov., on various occasions, 1904-09 and 1911-12; col. sec. and rev. commr., Jamaica, Aug., 1914; served in France, Flanders and Salonika, 1915-18; Constantinople, 1918-19; bvt. lieut.-col., bvt. col., D.S.O.; ag. gov., Jamaica, on various occasions, 1919, 1922, 1924-26; ret., 1925.

BRYANT, G.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apr., 1918; served with K.N.V.R., Sept., 1918, to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 24th Aug., 1927.

BRYANT, George William.—B. 1887; ed. Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (math. scholar); B.A., 1909; M.A., 1916; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; passed final exam. in Malay, Dec., 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Mar., 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, June, 1913; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, S.S., Apr., 1915; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Mar., 1917; sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Aug., 1918; offr., cls. IV, 1st Jan., 1919; sec., tin mining commr., in addition, Aug., 1919; addl. for sp. duty, Federal secretariat, Kuala Lumpur, July, 1922; ag. asst. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Aug., 1924 and Apr., 1927; temp. asst. prin., C.O., May, 1926; ag. dist. offr., Raub, Mar., 1927; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1927; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1927; offr., cls. I B, June, 1930; pres., munic. commr., Penang, Oct., 1930; ag. res. coun., Penang in addn., July, 1932; offr., cls. IA., res. coun., Malacca, Oct., 1934.

BRYCE, George.—B. 1885; ed. George Heriot's Schol., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; B.Sc., D.Sc.; Abyssinia, 1907-09; Madagascar, 1910-12; asst. botanist and mycologist, dept. of agr., Ceylon, 1913-23; served with H.M. forces, France, Germany, 1917-19; dir., agr., territory of New Guinea, 1923-26; del. to pan-Pacific confce., Australia, 1923; dir., Rubber Research Inst., Malaya, 1926-29; del. to pan-Pacific sci. congress, Java, 1929; dep. asst. dir., agr., Nigeria, 1930; asst. dir., agr., 1936.

BRYSON, Hugh Patterson, M.C.—B. 1898; ed. Royal Schol., Armagh; war serv., 1916-19; 2nd lieut., Tank Corps, 1917; France, 1918-19; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attached to dist. office, Kinta, B. Geiab; offr., cls. V, 4th asst. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Apr., 1924; offr., cls. V, attlt. offr., Trengganu, Apr., 1928; ag. sec. to res., N. Sembilan, May, 1931; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; asst. advr., Segamat, Feb., 1935; do., Kedah, Apr., 1936.

BUCHANAN, John Cecil Rankin, M.D. (Edin.), M.R.C.P. (E.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.)—B. 1896; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; mily. serv., 1915-19; ment. in desps., 1916; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925; med. offr., Somaliland, June, 1928; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1931; senr. med. offr., Somaliland Prot., 1936.
BUCHANAN, Lawrence Alexander Cockburn, M.A. (Edin.), B.A. (Oxford).—B. 1903; ed. Edinburgh Acad., Edin. Univ. and Oxford Univ.; teacher's dipl., London Univ., 1929; supervsr. educn., Zanzibar, 1929.

BUCHANAN-SMITH, Sir Walter, Kt. Bach. (1935), C.M.G. (1930), M.C. (1918)—B. 1879; ed. Repton Schol.; Br. N. Borneo civ. serv., 1903; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1909; ag. commr., lands, 1912, 1914 and 1916; addl., Nigeria Regt. in Cameroons 1914-15 and in E. Africa 1916-18; ag. commr., lands, 1916; 1st cls. dist. off., 1918; res., 1921; ag. prin. astt. sec., S. Prov., 1921, 1923 and 1925; ag. sec., S. Prov., 1923 and 1925; staff grade, 1926; ag. lieut.-gov., S. Prov., 1928-29 and 1930; lieut.-gov., S. Prov., 1930; O.A.G., Nigeria, Sept.-Nov., 1930; ret., 1935.

BUCKLAND, LANGLLOT LIDDLE RORKE, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1897; ed. Oumlle Schl., Univ. of Cape Town, S.A.; Artists Rifles, 1918; astt. engnr., irrig. dept., S.A., 1919-28; astt. engnr., Tanganyika Territory, 1928; exec. engnr., 1929.

BUCKLE, DOROTHY M., O.B.E. (1951).—B. 1883; ed. Howard Gardens Munic. Secondary Schl., Cardiff and Whitelands Training Coll., Chelsea; 1st cls. B. of E. teachers certif.; astt. prin., Raffles girls schl., Singapore, 1908; ag. prin., 1909; prin., 1911.

BULKELEY, GEORGE VICARY OWEN, A.M.I. Mech. E.—B.1892; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey and Owen's Coll., Manchester; 1st cls., rly. operating, Lond. Schl. of Economics; port man., Kenya and Uganda rlys. and harb., 1926; gen. man., Nigerian rly., 1933; dir., transport, Nigeria, 1936; author "Mechanical Appliances for handling Railway Traffic" (1922); "Railway and Seaport Freight Movement" (1926).

BULL, HAROLD ROBERT, M.A., Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1888; ed. Framlingham and Queen's Coll., Camb.; open entrance scholar, Queen's Coll., 1907; foundation scholar, 1908; 1st cls. hons., Nat. Sci. Tripos, Part I, 1909; 3rd cls. Maths. Tripos, Part I, 1910; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1911; attached to Chinese protectorate, Dec., 1911; passed cadet; offl. collr., stamp duties, Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, Apr., 1924; dep. contr., lab., Dec., 1924; sheriff, Singapore, Mar., 1925; 2nd mag., do., Oct., 1925; astt. offl. assignee, do., Jan., 1928; offr. cls. II, Nov., 1929; ag. dist. judge, Singapore, Apr., 1930; offr., cl. IB, Mar., 1932; dist. judge, Singapore, Sept., 1932.
BULL, OSWIN BOYS, M.A.—B. 1882; dir., Lerotholi tech. schl., Basutoland, 1931; dir., educn., 1935.
BULLOCK, G.—Ast. traffic manager, Uganda Railway, Apl., 1919; senr. astt. supt., transportation dept., Mar., 1927.

BULMAN, JOSEPH WAUGH, A.M.I.Loco.E.—B. 1893; ed. Petershill Pub. Schl., Glasgow; served in Navy, 1914-19; loco. foreman, Cyprus govt. rly., Nov., 1919; astt. loco. supt., Oct., 1927; astt. rly., July, 1935.

BULSTRODE, B. P. E.—B. 1887; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1913; astt. priv. sec. to gov., Leeward Is., 1907-1909; chief clk., govt. office, and clk. of couns., St. Lucia, Oct., 1914; ag. inspr. of schools, St. Lucia, Jan. to May, 1915; priv. sec. to admstr., Feb., 1916 to Apr., 1917; seconded from govt. office for sp. duties, Feb., 1917; priv. sec. to ag. admstr., Apr., 1917; trans. to Gambia as 2nd astt. recvr-gen., Oct., 1918; offr.-in-charge, secretariat on several occasions between 1920 and 1926; oz. clk., leg. coun., Oct., 1920; 2nd astt. col. sec., Jan., 1921; clk., exec. and leg. couns., on many occasions; ag. inspr., schls., Apr. to Sept., 1926; astt. col. sec., Gold Coast, 1931.

BUNBURY, CECIL MOLESWORTH.—Ed. privately; employed at Crown Bridge Works, W. Bromwich, 1895-1898; Great Central, Great Northern and City Rlys., 1898-1900; served in S. African War, 1900-1902 (medal with 4 clasps); CentraS. African Rlys., 1902-1904; astt. engnr., Uganda Rly., 15th Jan., 1906; maintenance engnr., Jan., 1925; ag. ch. engnr., 1925.

BUNNY, JOSEPH, F.L.S.—B. 1865; Reading Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; astt. conserv., forests, S. Nigeria, 1917; sec., 1918; senr. conserv., 1922; attended intercolonial confce., in Australia and N.Z., 1928; Nigeria and Sierra Leone, 1928; ag. director, Nigeria in 1929.
BUNTING, BENJAMIN, N.D.A., F.L.S.—B. 1889; astt. agriculturist, F.M.S., Malaya, do., Dec., 1914; agriculturist, Mar., 1915; sp. duty in Ceylon, Jan., Feb., 1930.
BUNTING, ROBERT HUGH, F.L.S.—B. 1892; of agriculture, Sierra Leone, 1913-15; astt. agric. and govt. mycologist, Gold Coast, 1915; ag. dir. of agric. on several occasions.
BURDETT, HENRY LEATHAM, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; war serv., Nov., 1914 to Aug., 1919; astt. engnr., Basutoland, 1921; transferred to Tanganyika as road engnr., 1929.
BURGE, G. H. K.—B. 1890; cadet, Royal Naval and Ellice Islands, Jan., 1915.

BURGESS, CLAUDE BRAMALL.—B. 1890; Epworth Coll. and Christ Ch., Oxford; B.A., greats; cadet, Hong Kong, Oct., 1932; sec. for Chinese affrs., Feb., 1933; astt. sec. of impts. and expts., Aug., 1935; admin. urban coun., Mar., 1936; pol. mag., June, 1936.
BURKE-GAFFNEY, E.M.O.D.—B. 1890; Belvedere Coll. and Univ. Coll., Dublin; astt. engnr., F.M.S., P.W.D., Jan., 1920; engnr. Jan., 1929; ag. state engnr., K.C.A., Nov., 1934.
BURKE-GAFFNEY, H.J.O'D., M.D.—B.A.O. (Dub.).—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925; astt. bacteriologist, 1929; senr. pathologist, 1935.
BURNER, ERIC ANDERSON.—Ed. at King's Schl.; comand., R.F.A., France, Belgium, Germany, 1915-18; astt. dist. commr., Coast, 1920; dist. commr., 1924.

BURNETT, BERTRAM DAVIDSON.—Ed. Port Elphinstone Pub. Schl. and Int. Acad.; war serv., 6th Batt. Gordon Highlanders, 1914-19; foreman of wks., 2nd grade, Port Gold Coast, Feb., 1920; headmast., trade schl., Apr., 1924.

BURNETT, CAPT. FREDERICK, M.C.—B. 1890; ed. Ashwater Schl., Devon, and Univ.; war serv., R.E., France, 1914-19; bar; divl. agrl. offr., Ceylon, 1920; regt., co-op. socs., 1926; vice-prin., agt., Peradeniya, 1926; dep. dir., agt., Br., 1930.

BURNS, SIR ALAN CUTHBERT MARSHALL, K.C.M.G. (1936), C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1879; appd. to treasy. and cust. dept., St. Kitts, Leeward Is., Mar., 1906; clk. to govt., 1909; ag. mag., Anguilla, 1910; dep. commr. and J.P., St. Kitts, 1911; ag. clk. and govt. to admstr., Dominica, 1912; supervisor of S. Nigeria, Nov., 1912; astt. sec., Nigeria, 1914; col. sec., Bahamas, Feb., 1924; mem., H. of A., 1925-28; del. to W. Ind. confce., London, 1926; astmtd. govt. on various occasions, 1924-28; dep. ch. sec., Nigeria, 1929; acting ch. sec. and gov't. dep. on various occasions, 1929-34; gov't. and commr.-in-chief, Honduras, 1934; on active serv. with W. Afr. Regt., 1914-15; adjutant, Nigeria Land Corps, 1915; served with Egba Expeditionary force, joint compiler, Index to Laws of Leeward Is., 1910; compiler, Nigeria Handbooks, 1911; author of "A History of Nigeria" 1929.
BURNS, R. E., LL.B., London (1908); clk., registr. & off., St. Kitts, June, 1898; agt.,

BURT, ROBERT HILLHOUSE.—B. 1896; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to C.O., 15th Mar., 1915; cler. offr., higher grade, 30th Apr., 1923; staff offr., 1st Oct. 1936.
BURTON, GERALD JOHN LLOYD, M.C., B.A.—Plant breeder, agrl. dept., Kenya, Jan., 1921; senr. plant breeder and experimentalist, 1935.

BURTON, WILLIAM, Barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn.—B. 1884; B.A., Cantab., 1st cls., div. III, clas. trip., 1906; 2nd cls., pt. II., hist. trip., 1907; cadet, F.M.S., 1907; ast. dist. offr., K. Pilah, cls. V, cont. as mag., K. Baharu, and harbour., T. Anson, and ast. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, 1911; ast. dist. offr., B. Padang, 1913; mag., Ipoh, 1913; ast. regis., sup. ct., Ipoh, 1914; mag., Seremban, 1916; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1918; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1919; regis., sup. ct., K. Lumpur, 1920; offr. assignee in addn., May, 1921; regis., sup. ct., July, 1921, cont. offr. assignee in addn.; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1924; do., cls. I.B, dep. legal advr., F.M.S., May, 1926; temp. judge, F.M.S., Mar., 1928; puisne judge, S.S., Apr., 1929; judge, Johore, Jan., 1936; ag. ch. just., S.S., May, 1935.

BURTON, WILLIAM ELLIOT.—M.D., C.M., McGill Univ., 1910; ag. temporary res. surg., Barbados general hosp., 1910; ag. med. offr., St. James' parish, Barbados; ag. visiting physician, leper asylum; ag. surg. to police, dist. II., Barbados, 1911; mag. and med. offr., Anguilla, Leeward Is., 1912; inappr. public health, St. Kitts-Nevis, Aug., 1918.

BUSH, BERTRAM OLIVER, Assoc.M.Inst.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engr.—B. 1890; ed. Hoe Gran. Schl. and Plymouth Tech. Schl.; ast. engrnr., Perak, F.M.S., Jan., 1920; ditto, K. Kangsar, Feb., 1921; ditto, K. Pilah, Dec., 1924; ditto K. Pilah and Jelebu, Oct., 1925; exec. engrnr., D.P.W.'s office, Jan., 1929; sr. drainage and irrign. engrnr., Perak, Jan., 1932; ag. ast. advr., drainage and irrign., Malay States in 1933 and 1934.

BUSHE, SIR HENRY GRATTAN, K.C.M.G. (1936), C.B. (1932), C.M.G. (1927)—B. 1886; called to the bar, 1909; Western circuit, 1910-11; S. Eastern circuit, 1911; Herts and Essex sessions, 1916; sec. to royal comsn. on the contracts between the War Office and Sir John Jackson, Ltd., 1916-17; ag. legal astt., C.O., Nov., 1917; legal astt., 1st Jan., 1919; legal adviser to D.O. and C.O., 1st Apr., 1931.

BUSTARD, FRANK HARCOURT.—B. 1888; joined Northumberland Fusiliers, May, 1903; seconded to K.A.R., Aug., 1913; commissioned in field, May, 1916; coy. commdr., Apr., 1917; ment. in desps, 1918; Somaliland campaign, 1920; capt., reg. army R. of O.; temp. astt. sup., Tanganyika pol., May, 1925; cadet, Zanzibar pol., Oct., 1925; supt., pol., May, 1926; ag. astt. comsnr. and ag. comsnr., pol., 1935-36.

BUSWELL, HENRY HOPE.—B.D. (Serampore, Calcutta); L.C.P. (hons. English and French), Fellow of L'Institut Francais du Royaume Uni.; ast. chaplain, Quatre Bornes and Moka, Mauritius, 1908; civ. chaplain, Beau Basin and Rose Hill, Mauritius, 1909; civ. chaplain of Seychelles, 1919; sub. dean, St. Paul's Cathedral, Victoria, Seychelles; chmn., Seychelles free library; mem., bd. of educn., Seychelles.

BUTCHER, JAMES WILLIAM.—B. 1889; ed. N.Z. state schl. and Waitaki Boys' High Schl.; ent. registr.-gen.'s dept., 1906; statistical branch, 1912; ch. compiler, census and statistics office, 1915; asst. govt. statistician, 1929; govt. statistician, 1932; N.Z. rep. at Br. Empire Conf. of statisticians, Ottawa, 1935.

BUTCHER, PERCY GEORGE, B.A. (hons. Oxon.).—B. 1890; ed. Perse Sch., Cambridge and Exeter Coll. Oxford; war serv., Devonshire Regt., 1915-19; France, 1916-19; adm. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, Aug., 1919; Arabic dipl. Schl. of Oriental Studies, 1927; ag. res., Bornu Prov., various periods since 1928; dep. res., Aug. 1934; adm. offr., cls. I, 1936.
BUTLER, CHARLES OWEN.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., 1914-19; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. commnr., 1924; asst. col. sec., 1928; ag. sec., asst. col. sec., Apr.-Sept., 1932.

BUTLER, CHARLES RICE.—B. 1885; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin, and at the Sorbonne, Paris; B.A.; supt., educ. dept., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; attchd. Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; ch. inspr., S. Provs., 1930; ag. asst. dir., educ., S. Provs., 1933 and 1935; prin., Higher Coll., Yaba, 1933.
BUTLER, ERNEST CECIL.—B. 1900; clk., Basutoland, 1920; sub-insp., B.M. pol., 1927; war serv., Apr., 1917-Mar., 1919.

BUTLER, FREDRICK BERRY LEWIS, F.L.S.—B. 1890; ed. Shaw Schl. (Berks) ent. R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1912; served in European War, 1915-19; agr. instr., E.A.P., 1919; man., Mazeras and Kibos experimental farms, E.A.P., 1921; agr. supervisor, Kenya, 1923; grader and inspr., Kenya, 1925; ch. grader and inspr. of produce, Cyprus, Jan., 1935.

BUTLER, G. G., M.B.E., B.A., M.D., B.C., Cantab. (hons. nat. sci. tripus), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), certif., L.S.T.M. (with distinc- tion).—Apptd., W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, Apl., 1911; seconded for sp. investigation under yellow fever commn., May, 1913 to June, 1914; served Cameroons Expdy. Force, Jan., 1916 to Apl., 1916; temp.-capt. (gen. list) Jan. to Apl., 1916; seconded for serv. with R.A.M.C., 1916-19; dir. of laboratory, Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1920; ag. D.P.M.O., Mar., 1924; dir., med. research inst., Gold Coast, May, 1928.

BUTLER, HON. RICHARD LAYTON, M.P. (S. Australia)—M.P. for Wooroora 1915-18 and since the elections of 1921; leader, party, liberal party and of opposition, Dec., 1925; premier, treas. and min. of rlyw., 1927-30; now leader of opposi-

BUTTERFIELD, HARRY ROBERT FRDERICK.—B. 1896; ed. Westminster City Schl.; 2nd lieut., Midd'x. Regt., Mar., 1915; served Great War, 1914-19; 6th K.A.R., Tanganyika Territory, 1922 to 1925; ret., 1926; admin. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1927; asst. dist. offr., May, 1929; capt., 6th K.A.R. R. of O., July, 1930; ag. res. mag. and dist. registr., June, 1931-Sept., 1932; author, "Index to Laws of Tanganyika Territory, 1935."

BUTTERS, HENRY ROBERT.—B. 1898; ed. Glasgow High schl. and Glasgow Univ., 1st bursar, 1916; cadet, Hong Kong, Jan., 1922; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1924; dist. offr., North, 1925; ag. dep. registr. and 2nd pol. mag. in addn., 1925; 3rd asst. col. sec., 1926; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk. of couns., June, 1928; ch. ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1929; 2nd pol. mag., 1930; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., 1932 and 1933; pol. mag., Kowloon, Sept., 1932; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., Oct., 1933; extra asst. col. sec., Jan., 1934; dep. clk., couns. in addn., Sept., 1934; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., couns., Sept., 1935; 1st pol. mag., Dec., 1935; P.M.G., Mar., 1936.

BUTTERS, SIR JOHN HENRY, KT. BA., M.I.E.E., M.A.M.I.C.E., etc.—B. 1882; Taunton Schl. and Univ. Coll., Southampton; engnr. and gen. man., govt. hydro elec. dept., Hobart, Tasmania; chmn., Tasmanian com. of the C'wealth advisory com. of science and industry; pres., Tasmanian Inst. of Engnr., chem., econmr., fedl. cap. comnr., Canberra, Nov., 1924; ret.

BUTTON, FRANCIS JENKINSON, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Burnley Gram. Schl.; war ser., East Lancs Regt. and R.T.C., Aug., 1914-Jan., 1919; asst. engnr., F.M.S., Apr., 1920; engnr., Apr. 1929; ag. sr. exec. engnr., J.B., Nov., 1932.
BUXTON, C. E. V.—Asst. dist. commn., Africa Prot., Oct., 1919.

BYNOE, ALLAN KENNETH.—B. 1881; ed. civ. serv., Barbados, 1900; ch. clk., pol. zct., dist. "A," 1922; ag. pol. mag. of vari- dists., on several occasions, 1922-26; pol. dist. "C," 1927; do. dists. "D" and "F," 1928.

BYRNE, BRIG.-GEN. SIR JOSEPH A., G.C.M.G. (1934), K.C.M.G. (1928), K.B.E. (1918), C.B. (military 1916).—B. 1874; joined R. Inniskilling Fliers, 1893; served in S. African War, 1899-1902; wounded; served with central judicial comm. Pretoria, 1903-06; major, 1914; bt. lieut. comm. 1915; brig.-gen., 1916; asst. adjutant-gen., W.O., 1916; dep. adjutant-gen., Ireland, 1916; (C.B. and desps.) inspr.-gen., R.I.C., 1916; ad. to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1922; gov., Seychelles, 21st July, 1922; assumed govt., 26th Sept., 1922; gov. and commr.-in-chief, Sierra Leone, 1922; gov. and commr.-in-ch., Kenya and high commr. for transport, 1931; ret., 1936.

BYRON, FREDERICK ANGUSTUS WILLIAM.—B. 1900; served in R.E. and Royal Corps Sign., 1918-1924; awarded M.S.M. and ment. in ser. for serv. on N.W. Frontier, India; wireless on Gold Coast, 1925; electrician in charge of services, Falkland Is., 1930; broadcast on Sierra Leone, 1933.
CABABE, THALES.—B. 1887; clk., P.W.F., Cyprus, 1910; ch. clk., secretariat, 1931; asst. postm.-gen., 1934; ag. P.M.G. in 1934 and 1935.

CADE, REGINALD COURTNEY.—B. 1891; Academie de Caen and in Germany; joined ser., Aug., 1914; served in France, June, 1915 to Sept., 1916 and Jan., 1917 to Mar., 1918; lieut., list, H. Qrs., Home Forces, till Mar., 1918; invalided out of army; junr. examr., clearances (enemy debts), Sept., 1920; senr. examr., June, 1921 to Dec., 1929; temp. adm. offr., O.C.O., Feb., 1930.

CADMAN, SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (civil), C.M.G. (1916), M.Sc., M.I.M.E., F.G.S.—B. 1895; ed. High Schl., Newcastle-under-Lyme; Durham Univ. Coll. Sc.; Staff, C.C. sch., 1895; B.Sc. (Dunelm.), hons., 1899; M.Sc., 1901; pres., Union soc., 1898; certif. colliery man., 1901; asst. gen. man., Silverdale coal and iron works, Staffs., 1900; asst. agt., Walter Scott, Ltd., Collieries, Durham, 1901; H.M. inap. of man. (H.O.), East Scotland, 1902; ditto, Staffordshire, 1903; govt. mining engnr., Trinidad and Tobago, 1904; ret., 1922; author of prize papers on "The Ore Deposit" before Inst. of Mining Engrs.

CAHAN, HON. CHARLES HAZLITT, B.A., LL.B., LL.D., K.C.—B. 1861; ed. Yarmouth Seminary and Dalhousie Univ.; leader of conserv. party in Nova Scotia legis., 1890-94; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada at g.e., 1925; re-el., 1926, 1930 and 1935; sec. of state in Bennett cabinet, 1930.
CAIN, Henry Oliver.—B. 1892; Europ. ar., 1914-18; 21st Lancers 1910; Indian Sig. rv., 1916-23; qmr. st., Somaliland pol. force, 25; pay and qmr., 1928.

CAINE, Sydney, B.Sc. (Econ.)—B. 1902; ed. arrow County Schl. and London Schl. of Economics; B.Sc. (Econ.), with 1st cls. hons., 1922; ptd. after compet. exam., ass't inspr. of taxes (land Rev.), 1923; apptd. after compet. exam., st. prin., C.O., Nov., 1926; prin., 8th Nov., 31; visited W. Indies and B. Guiana as sec., Indian sugar coman., 1929-30; sec., managing dir., Imp. Inst. of Entomology, Oct., 31.

CAIRNS, Thomas Crawford, B.Sc.—B. 1900; I. Edinburgh Acad.; B.Sc. Edinburgh Univ., 24; entomologist, Empire Cotton Growing corp.; seconded to divn. of entomology, Union S. Africa; cotton investigator, Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1928; dist. agrt. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1930.

CALDECOTT, Sir Andrew, K.C.M.G. (1937), T. Bach (1935), C.M.G. (1932), C.B.E. (1926).—B. 1884; ed. Uppingham (schol. and exh.), and Oxford (school), 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1907; B.A., Exeter Coll., 1907; cadet, F.M.S., 1907; dist. offr., Selangor, 1911; dep. contr., labr., 1913; 2nd asst. sec., Fed. Sec., 1916; seconded for sp. duty in connexion with Br. Empire Exhibn., while on leave in England, Jan., 1923; town planning adminstr. and state valuer, Ipoh, Mar., 1926; chmn. housing and P.W.D. enquiry comtees, Dec., 1926; dep. contr., lab., Malaya but continues ag. under sec., S.S., July, 1927; offr. cls. Ia. sec., postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1928; ag. Br. res., N. Sumatran, Dec., 1929; ag. Br. res., Perak, Oct., 1930; ag. Br. res., Selangor, May, 1931; ag. ch. to govt., F.M.S., July, 1931; supernumerary staff offr. of rank of ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1932; col., sec., S.S., Dec., 1933; O.A.G., S.S. and high commr., Malay States, Feb.-Nov., 1934; gov. and commr.-in-ch., Hong Kong, Dec., 1935; gov. and commr.-in-ch., Ceylon, 1937; Knight of Grace, Order of St. John of Jerusalem, 1936.

CALDER, James.—B. 1898; ed. Glasgow Univ.; M.A.; war serv., 1916-19; cadet, S.S., Apr., 1921; attd., treasry., S'pore, June, 1921; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; asst. supt., govt. monop., S'pore and asst. contrlr., rubber in addn., Aug., 1926; ditto, Penang, Mar., 1927; offr., cls.
