IV, Apr., 1928; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Dec., 1928; asst. dir., co-opern., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1929; ag. asst. atvr., B. Pahat, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; mag., Seremban, May, 1935; do., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1935.

CALDER, Hon. James Alexander, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1868; ed. Ingersoll pub. schls., pub. and high schls., Winnipeg; Manitoba Coll.; hon. grad. in science, Manitoba Univ., 1888 (silver medallist); called to bar, North-West Territories, 1906; prin. of Moosejaw high schl., 1891-94; inspr. of schls., N.W.T., 1894-1900; dep. commr. of educ., N.W.T., 1901-05; elec. to Sask. ass. at first g.e., 1905; prov. treas. and commr. of educ., in Scott min., Sept., 1905; unsucc. cand. for Milestone div. at g.e., 1908; elec. for Saltcoats by bye-elec., Dec., 1908; min. of immigr. and colonization in federal union govt., Oct., 1917; elec. to H. of C., Canada, for Moosejaw, Dec., 1917; pres., Canadian privy coun., July, 1920; called to senate, Sept., 1921.

CALDER, John A., M.A.—B. 1889; ed. at Harris Acad., Dundee and Edin. Univ.; Vans Dunlop scholarship in history, 1910; M.A. with 1st cls. hons. in history, 1911; 1st cls. hons. in philosophy, 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Nov., 1912; ag. 1st cls. clk., 8th Dec., 1917; on military service from 16th Sept. to 10th Dec., 1918; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; visited S. Africa, N. and S. Rhodesia, Nyasaland, Tanganyika, Zanzibar, Uganda and Kenya as sec., E. Africa Parly. Coman., Aug. to Dec., 1924; asst. sec., 27th June, 1933.

CALDWELL, Reginald Percival.—B. 1894; held various appts., Fiji, 1910-17; served with 1st Batt., K.R.R., France, 1917-19; 1st cls. clk., treas. and sec., returned sailors and soldiers land settlement scheme and returned sailors and soldiers advances bd. of control, 1922; asst. commr. for Fiji, Br. Emp. Exhib., 1924; col. audlt. dept., Dec., 1924; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925; ag. senr. asst. audr., June, 1925; asst. audr., Uganda, July, 1930; ag. senr. asst. audr., Aug., 1930; senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Dec., 1935.

CALLAN, Hon. John Bartholomew, K.C. (1934), B.A., LL.B.—B. 1882; ed. Christian Bros. schl., Dunedin, and Otago univ., N.Z.; pty. legal practice many years; served in war as capt., 3rd batt., N.Z.R.B.; lecturer in torts, Otago univ., 1912-33, and dean of faculty of law, 1924-33; mem., coun. of legal educ.; formerly pres. Otago law soc.; judge, sup. ct. of N.Z., 1933.

CALTHROP, Lancelot Henry Calthrop.—B. 1904; ed. Blundell's; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, Nov., 1924; seconded as junr. dist. offr., Weihiwei, 1926; asst. supt., pol., 1928; J.P., 1928; passed in Mandarin, 1928; attd. Punjab pol., 1930-31.
CALVERT, Hubert, C.S.I., C.I.E., I.C.S. (ret.), B.Sc.—B. 1875; regis. co-op. socs., Ceylon, Mar., 1935.
CALWELL, H. G., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Hons.) (Belf.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.) (Gold Med., Diseases of Children, Belfast).—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1931.

CAMACHO, Fabien Joseph.—B. 1898; ed. St. Wilfred's Coll., N. Staffs.; army, France 1917-19; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1923; mag. and legal advr., Montserrat, Nov., 1930 to May, 1932; mag., E. counties, Trinidad, Nov., 1932; ag. solr.-gen., Aug., 1935; ag. mag., Victoria, 1936.

CAMACHO, Maurice Vivian.—B. 1885; ed. St. Edmund's Coll., Herts.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1906; ag. atty.-gen., Leeward Is., in 1919 and 1920; atty.-gen., Dec., 1920; K.C., 1924; judge, West Indian ct. of appeal for hearing of certain appeals, 1924; solr.-gen., Trinidad, 1927; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1928 to Apr., 1929 and Dec., 1930, to Jan., 1931; ag. judge, sup. ct., Apr. to June, 1929 and Jan. to June, 1931; atty.-gen., Jamaica, 1931.

CAMBRIDGE, Ralph.—M.A. Hertford Coll., Oxon.; B. 1878; 3rd asst. mast., Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, Sept., 1901; 1st asst. mast., Apr., 1918; 2nd mast., July, 1920; prin., Queen's Royal Coll., Sept., 1:27.
CAME, Frederic Harold Roberts, M.B.E. (1936), A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1900; ed. Taunton Schl. and Impl. Coll. of Sci., S. Kensington; asst. engr., P.W.D., Uganda, 1925.

CAMERON, Sir Donald Charles, G.C.M.G. (1932), K.C.M.G. (1926), K.B.E. (1922), C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1872; ed. at Rathmines School, Dublin; entd. Br. Guiana civ. ser., 1890; 5th class clk., secretariat, 1891; 4th ditto, 1895; 3rd class and despatch clk., 1895; 2nd class, 1899; priv. sec. to ag. gov., 1896, 1897, 1898 and 1901; ag. ast. gov. sec. and clk. of councils, 1900-1901, principal clk., secretariat, 1901; priv. sec. to gov. of Newfoundland (with permission of S. of S.) whilst on leave, 1902; ast. col. sec., Mauritius, July, 1904; ag. col. sec. in 1904-5-6-7; temp. transf., to S. Nigeria as ast. sec., Jan., 1908; principal ast. sec., 1911; sec. to S. Nigeria liquor trade inquiry comte., 1909; ag. prov. commr. in 1910, 1911 and 1912; ag. col. sec., 27th Feb. to 24th Aug., and 5th Sept. to 3rd Oct., 1912, deputy gov. in 1921-23; cent. sec., Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1914; ch. sec. to gov., 1st Jan., 1921; ag. gov. in 1921, 1923 and 1924; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1924; ditto, Nigeria, 1931.

CAMERON, SIR EDWARD J., K.C.M.G. (1916), C.M.G. (1905).—Ed. at Shrewsbury Schl., Clifton Coll., and Merton Coll., Oxon; priv. sec. to Sir C. C. Lee, gov., Bahamas, Jan., 1883; ast. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, Nov., 1883; J.P. for the Sttlmt., and visiting justice, Freetown Gaol; ag. col. sec. and treas., Dec. 1885, to July, 1886; pres., Virgin Is., Feb., 1887; mem. Leeward Is. legis. coun., 1887, and exec. coun., 1888; comnr., Turks Is., Mar., 1893; ag. judge sup., May to Dec., 1893, Jan. to June, 1895, and Apr. to July, 1898; admstr., St. Vincent, May, 1901; admstr., St. Lucia, Apr., 1909; ag. gov. Windward Is., June to Oct., 1909, July to Dec., 1911, and in 1912; representative of St. Lucia, and chmn. of W. Indian delegates at reciprocity conf. at Ottawa, Mar., 1912; gov., Gambia, Feb., 1914; assumed govt., Apr., 1914; ret., 1920.
CAMERON, KATHERINE ROSS, M.B.E. (1926).—Nursing sister, Basutoland, 1911; ag. matron, 1914; nursing sister, Nyassaland, 1919; matron, 1923.

CAMERON, MAJOR SIR MAURICE ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1914); C.M.G. (1900), late R.E. B. 1865; first commn. in Roy. Engrs., Ang., 1874; dep. col. engr., S. Sttlmts. Dec. 1883, to Apr., 1892; during which he acted about two years and a half as col. engr., and survr.-gen., and was a mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; apptd. one of the crown agts. for the colonies, 1895-1920.
CAMILIERI, LUIGI A., LL.D.—B. 1892; matric., Malta Univ., 1907; LL.D., 1913; sl. for legis. assem., 1921; mag., 1924; judge, 1930.

CAMELLE, VRE BERNARD.—B. 1898; ed. Malvern Coll. and Royal Milv. Acad., Woolwich, 1915; R. Arty., 1915-21; active serv. (France), 1916-18; ast. dist. comnr., Nyassaland Prot., 1927-30; ast. treas., 1930-36; seconded, Sonali-land Prot., 1936.
CAMPBELL, ALBERT BERNARD.—B. 1883; ast. store keeper, Baro Kano rly., 1909; seconded, Nigerian Regt., 1917; ast. ch. storekeeper, 1920; ch. storekeeper, Nigeria, 1928.
CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD, A.M.I. Mech.E.—B. 1891; ed. Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; N.B. Rlv. Co. 1906-11; engrg. design dept., C.A., 1914; war serv., 1916-18; ast. engrg., 1921; dep. engrg. (mech.), 1935.
CAMPBELL, COLIN BRUCE, B.A., M.C.—B. 1890; ed. Leatherhead Schl. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, May, 1921.
CAMPBELL, DAVID CALENDER.—B. 1891; ed. Foyle Coll., Derry and Edin. Univ.; ast. sec., Tanganyika, Dec., 1919; ast. ch. sec., 1933; dep. ch. sec., Uganda, Feb., 1936.

CAMPBELL, COL. GEORGE FREDERICK COOK, C.M.G. (1919), V.D. (1908).—B. 1858; ed. Govt. Coll., New Zealand; entd. New Zealand serv., 1874; dep. commr. of taxes, 1894-194; dep. supt., advances to settlers' office, 1894; dep. valuer-gen., 1897-1904; valuer-gen., 1904; gen. man., state fire insurance office, 1904; commr. of taxes, 1910-2; sec. to treas., rev. gen., pay-master-gen. and supt. of state savings office, 1913; contr. and audr.-gen., 1922.

CAMPBELL, JAMES EDWARD FRANCIS, C.B. (1937), M.A.—B. 1879; ed. Christ Church, Oxford; double lit. cls. hon., called to bar, Middle Temple; Order of the Nile; served throughout war; major, R. Artillery, 1916-18; ent. Pay-admtn., Mar., 1919; ast. dist. gov., Jerusalem-Jaffa dist., 1920; dist. commr., Jesuitari, 1932.
CAMPBELL, J.M., M.B., Ch.B. (Ed.), D.P.H. (Camb.), D.T.M. (Liv.) (Alan Milne med., 1897; med. offr., Kenya, Nov., 1923; health offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1923.

CAMPBELL, LACHLAN BAIN, M.C., M.A., M.I.C.E. (Lond.).—B. 1882; ed. Auckland Grammar Schl. and Canterbury Univ. (N.Z.); 30 years' serv. in N.Z. pub. works; comnr., borough of Napier (during recent period following earthquake), 1931-33; sec., same, 1933.

CAMPBELL, OWEN NEIL.—B. 1884; ed. Auckland gram. schl. and Auckland univ. (N.Z.); ent. N.Z. lands and survey dept.; served overseas as lieut., N.Z. Engrs.; 1914-15, ch. drainage engr., lands dept., 1922; ch. comnr. of crown lands, 1927; dep. under-comnr. of crown lands and survey dept., 1934; native trustee, under-sec., native dept., 1935.

CAMPBELL, S. McNEILL.—B. 1893; ed. Hamilton Acad., and Glasgow Univ.; M.A. clia. hona. Classics) 1915; G.U.O.T.C. 1914; on military service, Jan., 1916 to Mar., 1919, with Cameronians (Scottish Rifles) Div. and R.A.F., Egypt and Palestine; July, 1917; capt., R.A.F., Nov., 1918; temp. clk., in C.O., Apr., 1919; apptd. by exec. commrs., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. sec. (Sir J. Masterton Smith), Ap., 1921-23; ag. prin., 3rd Oct., 1923; prin. 1st Oct., 1925; sec., W. Indies Closer Union Com., Nov., 1932-Mar., 1933.

CAMPIGLI, LIEUT.-COL. GEORGE MEE (R. of O.), M.C., M.Inst.T.—B. 1890; ed. Vict. state rly., 1905-14; served in Aust. Forces in Gallipoli and Palestine, 1914-18; in dep.; capt. (staff capt.), May, 1917; ast. dir., rly. traffic, 1918; ast. dir., dist. offr.-in-ch., Palestine milv. rly., 1919; ast. to civ. govt. as ast. gen. man. Palestine, 1920; lieut.-col. R. of O., July, 1921; man. in 1922, 1924, 1926-1927-28; mem. ad. bd. to Palestine govt. for commerce and indus., Mar., 1928.

CAMPION, LIEUT.-COL. SIR WILLIAM ROBERTS, K.C.M.G. (1924), D.S.O. (1917), T.D. (1914-1870); ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford; lieut.-col. 4th Batt., R. Sussex Regt., 1915; col. M.P., Lewes divn., Sussex, 1910-24; gov. Australia, 1924-31.

CAMELLE, CHARLES, C.M.G. (1937), B.Sc., LL.D., F.R.S.C., F.G.S.A.—B. 1875; St. John's Coll., Winnipeg; grad., Man. Univ., 1894; post grad., geol., of Queen's Univ., Kingston, Harvard, and Massachusetts Inst. of Tech., Boston; geol., Algoma Cent. Rly., 1901- Canadian Northern Rly., 1902-03; geol., geological surv. of Can., 1904; geol., investigations and explns. in Br. Columbia and N. Can., 1904-20; in charge of B.C. and Yukon br. of the geol. surv., 1918; dep. min., federal dept. of mines, 1920; apptd., Dom. fuel bd., 1921.

CANNON, HON. LAWRENCE ARTHUR DUMOLIN, B.A., LL.L., LL.D.—B. 1877; ed. Commercial Coll., Arthabaskaville, Que. (Can.), Quebec Seminary and Laval Univ.; gov.-genl.'s med., A.B., 1896; LL.L., 1899; LL.D., 1927; admitted to bar, 1899; K.C., 1910; M.L.A. (Que.), 1916 and 1919; mem. of coman., to revise and consolidate statutes of Quebec, 1923; bataillonier, Quebec bar, 1924; hon. sec., Canadian Bar Assn., 1927; judge, King's bench (Que.), 1927; judge, sup. ct., Canada, 1930.

CAPETOWN, ARCHBISHOP OF, MOST REVEREND FRANCIS ROBINSON PHELPS, D.D., Ed. Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1886; M.A., 1889; D.D., 1916; bishop of Grahamstown, 1915; archbishop of Capetown and metropolitan of the church of the province of S. Africa, 1931.
CARBERY, JOHN EDWARD DONTON, LL.B., McGill.—B. 1893; served in Great War, 1915-19; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1925; clk. ota., Jamaica, 1927; ag. res. mag., 1927-32; res. mag., Nov., 1932.
CARBONELL, JOHN ROUSE.—B. 1908; ed. Shrewsbury Schl. and St. Catharine's Coll., Camb.; 3rd cl. geog. trip., 1929; 3rd cl. anthrop. trip., 1930; B.A., 1930; supt., Tanganyika Territory, 1930.

CARDIN, HON. PIERRE JOSEPH ARTHUR Richelieu.—B. 1879; ed. Sorel Comm. Coll. and pvt. teachers; studied law, Laval Univ.; bataillonier Richelieu bar sec.; first elect. to H. of Canada, at g.e., 1911; re-elec., g.e., 1917 and g.e., 1921; mem., privy coun. and min. of marine and fisheries, 1924; re-elec., by accl. after assuming office, 1924; re-elec., at g.e., 1925, 1926, 1930 and 1935; resigned portfolio with King Cabinet, 1926; re-apptd., min. of marine and fisheries, Sept., 1926; min. of pub. wks. in King cabinet, 1935.

CARDINALL, ALLAN WOLLEY, C.M.G. (1937).—B. 1887; ed. Winchester Coll. and Heidelberg; cond. Matric., 1914; asst. dist. comsnr., Ashanti, S. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 1st Jan., 1921; ag. comsnr., S. Prov., N.T., Apr. to Aug., 1928; ag. comsnr., N. Prov., N.T., Jan. to June, 1929; ret., 1932; comsnr., Cayman Is., 1934.

CAREW, WILLIAM DENMOND.—B. 1899; ed. Longowes Wood Coll., Ireland and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd lt., 13th D.C.O. Lancers, Indian Army, 1918; served N.W. Frontier, 1919; cadet, Fiji, 1921; dist. comsnr., 1925; ag. ch. pol. mag., or various periods, 1928-31; called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1930; ag. atty. gen., sup. ct. criminal circuit, Sept., 1931 and Sept., 1932; dist. mag., N.W. Viti Levu, and dist. registrar, sup. ct., inch. of Lautoka dist. regv., 1932-34; ag. ch. pol. mag., regis. sup. ct., pub. trustee and off. rec. in bankruptcy, Dec., 1934; jud. commr. for W. Pacific, New Hebrides and ag. Br. judge of joint internat. ct., condominium govt., New Hebrides, Mar., 1935.

CAREY, CECIL WILLIAM VICTOR.—B. 1887; B.A., LL.B., Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to bar, Dublin, 1910; asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda, Oct., 1915; mag., 1918; admst.-gen., custodian of enemy property, off. rec. in bankruptcy, 1919; crown coun., Nigeria, 1921; ag. solt.-gen. in 1923-30; puisne judge 1930.
CARLTON, ERIC JAMES WATTS.—B. 1908; ed. Bedford Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; passed in sch. of agr., B.A., 1930; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1931; asst. dist. ofir., 1933.
CARMICHAEL, FRANK BERNARD.—B. 1883; loco. supt., Trinidad govt. rly., June, 1928; ag. supt., rly., July, 1934 to Jan., 1935.
CARN CROSS, HON. SIR WADE CHARLES FREDERICK, KT. BACH. (1922).—B. 1885; ed. privately; mem., N.Z. House of Reps., 1890-1902; M.L.C. since 1903; chmn., comte., of L.C., 1910-18; speaker, 1918.
CARR, EDWARD THOMAS WHITMORE, B.A., T.C.D.—B. 1883; sub-inspr. of constab., Trinidad, Oct., 1907; inspr., July, 1916.

CARR, FREDERICK BERNARD.—B. 1893; admstve. ofir., cle. IV., 1919; asst. sec., S. Provs., secretariat, 1925, 1930 and 1931; asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1928, 1929 and 1930; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1930; sec., coman., of inquiry in S.E. prov., 1930; ag. prin. asst. sec., S. provs. secretariat, 1930 and 1932; admstve. serv., cls. II, 1933; ag. res., 1928, 1933, 1934, 1935 and 1936; admstve. ofir., cls. I, 1936.

CARR, JAMES BENJAMIN.—B. 1889; ed. Bedford Mod. Sch. and Culham Training Coll. (Oxford Diocesean); European ast. mast., Victoria Inst., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1911; with H.M. Forces, Oct., 1914 to Feb., 1920; Raffles Inst., S'pore, Jan., 1921; headmast., T. Kurau govt. eng. schl., June, 1926; asst. inspr., schl., S'pore, in addn., Sept. to Nov., 1926; headmast., K. Pilah govt. eng. schl., Dec., 1928; headmast., Rangoon Rd. schl., S'pore, Dec., 1930; group supvr., S'pore, June, 1935.

CARRIE, WILLIAM JAMES, M.A., B.Sc. (1914).—B. 1891; ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin., and Univ. Edin.; cadet, civ. ser., Hong Kong, 1914; passed in Cantonese, 1917; on military service, 1918 to 1919; asst. col. treas., 1920; J.P., 1922; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1922; sec., Sui On piracy coman., 1922; sec., stocks and shares coman., 1925; asst. supt., impts. and expts. dept., 1926; head, sany. dept., Nov., 1927; asst. col. sec. and clk. of coun., Oct., 1928; head, sany. dept., 1929 and Nov., 1934; sec., salaries coman., 1928 and 1929 in addn.; supt., census, 1930-31; senr. asst. col. treas., Nov., 1931; ag. P.M.G., 1933; chmn., urban coun., Jan., 1936; ag. sec., Chinese affrs., and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Mar., 1936.
CARROW, LIEUT.-COMM. JOHN HINTON, R.N. (Ret.), D.S.C.—B. 1890; ed. Clifton Coll.; admstve. ofir., cls. IV., Nigeria, June, 1919; ditto, cls. I., Dec., 1932.
CARRUTHERS, ANGUS WILLIAM.—B. 1879; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edin.; Impl. serv., 1897; O.F.S. serv., 1904; asst. dir., census, U. of S.A., Feb., 1918; dir., census, Jan., 1934.

CARSON, ANDREW HOWARD, A.M.I.E.E., M.I.R.E., Chtd., Elec. Engnr.—B. 1895; ed. High Sch., R. Acad. and Educat. Inst., Inverness, Univ. Tech. Coll.; Br. P.O. eng. dept., Jan., 1912; asst. teleg. engnr., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., Mar., 1921; engnr., P. & T., S.S. & F.M.S., Oct., 1926; ag. sr. engnr., on various occasions, 1932-34; sr. engnr., and sec. to Johore as ch. teleg. engnr., July, 1934.

CARSON, JAMES COBURN, M.B., B. Ch., B.A.O. (Queens Univ., Belfast), D.T.M. (Meth. Coll., Belfast).—B. 1894; 11th Battn. R. Irish Rifles and 126th Baluchistan Inf., 1915-18; med. ofir., S.S., Apr., 1924; gen. hosp., Penang, May, 1924; ag. prof., anatomy, med. coll., S'pore, May, 1926-Jan., 1927; med. offr., S'pore, June, 1927; ag. ch. med. offr., Malacca, Jan., 1928 and Sept., 1931; med. offr., P. Wellesley, Jan., 1932; do. S'pore, June, 1933; do., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1933; ag. ch. med. offr., Penang, July, 1935.
CARSTAIRS, CHARLES YOUNG.—B. 1910; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edin., and Edin. Univ.; M.A. (hons. in hist.); apptd. after compet. exam. asst. prin., D.O., 24th Oct., 1934.

CARTER, CYRIL WALTER, Assoc. M.Inst.C.E., M. Int. Nav. C.—B. 1900; ed. Berkhamsted Schl. and Crystal Palace Engnr. Schl.; mil. serv., 1918-19; seconded to Tanganyika Govt., 1927; dep. res. engr., Egyptian govt., 1929; exec. engr.-in-ch., harbr. wks., Tanganyika, 1930; asst. engr., harbr. dept., Nigeria, 1934; asst. port engr., 1935; port engr., 1936.
CARTER, EDWARD STANLEY.—B. 1888; ed. Carpenter's Co. Tech. Inst.; war serv., 1914-19; asst. engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1920; seconded, Kowloon-Canton Rly., 1926-27 and in 1929.
CARTER, HUGH CLAY, M.C., M.E.I. (Canada).—B. 1884; ed. St. Albans Schl.; asst. engr., Br. Honduras, Jan., 1921; ag. dir., pub. wks., Apr., to Dec., 1923; D.P.W., Br. Honduras, May, 1924.
CARTER, WILLIAM HOWARD.—B. 1887; asst. inspr., schls., Barbados, 1934.

CARTER, SIR WILLIAM MORRIS, KT. BACH. (1919), C.B.E. (1918), B.A., B.C.L.—Ed. at King's schl., Canterbury, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; open exhib., in mod. hist., Jan., 1892; class. mods. (honours), 1894; 2nd cls. hon. jur., 1896; certif. of honour, bar exam., 1899; awarded spec. prize in English constitutional law and legal hist. by coun. of legal educn., 1899; called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, Nov., 1899; regis. and prin. regis. of documents, E. Africa Prot., Jan., 1902; ag. town mag., Mombasa, Apl., 1902; mag., 1st Oct., 1902; judge of H.M. high ct. of Uganda and H.B.M. ct. of appeal for E. Africa, Nov., 1903; ag. prin. judge and legal adviser, Apl. to Sept., 1904; ag. prin. judge, Mar. to Oct., 1907, Aug. to Dec., 1909, and Aug., 1911, to Jan., 1912; apptd. consmr. to enquire into native land tenure, 1906; mem. of committees to consider land legislation, 1906; and to draft land legislation, 1909; president, native land settm. commit., 1911; chief just., Uganda Prot., July, 1912; joint compiler of first edition of laws of Uganda Prot., 1909; ch. just., Tanganyika Territory, 12th Oct., 1920; ret., 1924; chmn., cotton enquiry comm., Uganda, 1929.

CARTMEL-ROBINSON, HAROLD FRANCIS, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1889; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Merton Coll., Oxford; probtnr., N. Rhodesia, Sept., 1912; asst. native consmr., 1914; war serv., 1917-19; native consmr., 1918; asst. mag., 1926; ag. prov. consmr. for short periods in 1928, 1931, 1932 and 1933; prov. consmr., 1934; ag. senr. prov. consmr., 1935.
CARTWRIGHT, CYRIL GEORGE FOX.—B. 1911; ed. Winchester Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A.; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1934; dep. consmr. (temp. and provvn.), 1934; ag. sec. to gov't., 1936.
CARVER, J. F. A.—Asst. conserv., forests, Nyasaland Prot., Scott., 1921; conserv., forests, Mauritius, Sept., 1936.

CASEY, NORMAN BHANDRETH, M.B.E. (1934).—B. 1882; ent. Fiji civ. serv., 1908; gaoler, 1917; ag. inspr., 1920; ag. inspr. gen., prisons, 1920; CASEY, HON. RICHARD GARDNER, D.S. M.C., M.A., M.P.—Politic. liaison offr. in London between Br. and Australian Govts., 1924; M.P., Corio, H. of R. of C'wealth of Australia since 1931; asst. treas., 1933-35; treas., 1935.

CASGRAIN, HON. PIERRE FRANCOIS, B.L., LL.M., K.C.—B. 1886; ed. St. Mary's Coll. at Laval Univ.; practised law, Montreal, since 1908; el. to Canadian H. of C., g. el., 1917; m. g. el., 1921, 1925, 1926, 1930 and 1935; chair. whip, 1925-35; sl. speaker, H. of C., Feb., 1935.
CASHIN, HON. SIR MICHAEL PATRICK, K.B.E. (1918); B. 1866; M.H.A., Newfoundland; ass't for Ferryland since 1893; mem. exec. com. of min. of finance and customs, 1909; mem. of finance, Jan., 1918.
CASTONGUAY, JULIUS.—B. 1882; asst. office of clk. of crown in chancery for Canada, 1908; asst. ch. electoral offr., 1920; ch. elect. offr., 1927.
CATCHPOLE, CAPT. R. K.—B. 1889; dist. desian pol., 1910-16; K.A.R., 1915-20; ass't pol., Kenya, Dec., 1920; supt., 1928.

CATOR, GEOFFREY EDMUND, C.M.G., B.A.—B. 1884; ed. King's schl., Bruce and Selwyn Coll., Camb. (Scholar); cadet, F.M., Nov., 1907; passed cadet, June, 1908; offr., V. Mar., 1911; ag. res., Labuan, in addn., 1917; offr. cls. IV., Jan., 1918; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1919; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1924; mem. ex. advary. comtes. on use opium, Malaya, 1926; temy. mem., legis. council, S.S., 1927; offr. cls. I.B., dist. offr., Klang, Jan., 1928; ag. Br. res., Perak, in addn., June-July, 1928; offr., cls. I.A., June, 1930; supt., gtr. polices, S.S., 1930; M.L.C.; Br. res., Selangor, July, 1932; Br. res., Perak, Dec., 1933.
CATTANACH, A.—B. 1880; on mil. ser., Feb., 1915 to Feb., 1918; asst. cashier and cad. Disposal and Liquidation Coman., 12th May to 22nd July, 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 24th July, 1922.

CAVENAUGH, HERBERT JAMES LAWRENCE.—Ed. Ardingly Coll. and King's Coll., Lond.; attd., R.I.C., Dublin for training, May, 1914; sub-inspr., Trinidad constab., Dec., 1914; serv. with H.M. Forces, Sept., 1915 to 1919; capt.; G.S. and Victory Med., 1919; of the Nile (4th cl.); ag. staff offr., forces, Aug. and Sept., 1919; ag. inspr., Trin. constab., Sept. to May, 1920; supt. of police, Honduras, 1st June, 1920; ag. dist. constab., Belize, Mar.-May, 1921; ag. supt., present addition to substantive appt., Sept., 1921; Mar., 1926; dep. dir., prisons, Gold Co., 1927; dir. ditto, 1930.

CAVENDISH, RICHARD CHARLES ALEXANDER.—B. 1885; served with B.S.A. pol., S. Rhodesia, 1906-11; asst. comantr., pol., S. Nigeria, 1912; King's pol. med., 1926; senr. comantr., pol., Provs., Nigeria, 1927; asst. inspr.-gen., pol., Provs., Nigeria, 1930; dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Nigeria, 1931; comantr., pol., Kenya, 1931.

CENTLVRES, ALBERT VAN DE SAMPEL, B. (Cape and Oxford), K.C.—Rhodes Schl., 1919; S. African Coll. and Coll. Oxford; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1910; advoc., Cape divn., sup. ct., S. Africa, 1911; advoc., high ct., S. Rhodesia, 1913; partly, draftsman, part. Union of S. Africa, 1920; ag. judge, high ct., West Africa, 1922; ditto, Cape prov. divn., 1933; judge, do., 1935.
CHALMERS, 1ST BARON, OF NORTHAMPTON (1919); RIGHT HON. STN. ROBERT CHALMERS, P.C. (Ireland) 1916; G.C.B. (1916); K.C.B.
AMBERLAIN, GEORGE DIGBY.—B. 1898; St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; war serv., 1914; lieut., reg. R. of O.; asst. dist. commr. Coast, Apr., 1925; seconded to C.O., as prin., Oct., 1930.

AMBERLAIN, KENNETH SEYMOUR JAMES.—B. 1901; treas., Kenya, 1924-33; dep. treas., Apr., 1933; ag. treas. and consmr., inland June, 1933-Feb., 1934; treas.; Gibraltar, sec., July, 1902, to Feb., 1903, and Apr. to Oct., 1903.

CHAPLIN, SIR FRANCIS DRUMMOND PERCY, G.L.E. (1923), K.C.M.G. (1917).—B. 1866; ed. Harrow and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A.; barrister, Lincoln's Inn, 1891; pres. of chamber of mines, Transvaal, 1905; M.H.A., Transvaal, 1907-10; M.L.A. for Germiston, Union of South Africa; admstr. of Southern Rhodesia, 24th Dec., 1914, and at N. Rhodesia, March, 1921; resigned both appts., Sept., 1923.

CHAPMAN, GEOFFREY WALTER, B.A. and Dipl. in Forestry (Cantab.).—B. 1908; ed. Cranbrook Schl. and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; hon. exhibn. in hist.; hon. nat. sci., 3rd class; post-grad. probationers' course at Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford; asst. conserv., forests, 1st grade, Cyprus, Sept., 1930.

CHARLEWOOD, Lieut.-Comdr. CLEMENT JAMES, D.S.C. (1914), R.N.R. (Ret.)—B. 1888; ed. Newcastle Prep. Schl., Newcastle-upon-Tyne, and on board H.M.S. "Worcester" from May, 1903 to July, 1905; midshipman in R.N.R., 4th Aug., 1905; employed by British India Steam Navigation Co., Ltd., July, 1909 to Aug., 1914; comd. small craft taking part in blockade and subsequent capture of coast of German E. Africa, being selected by Rear-Adm. E. Charlton to rec. surrdr. of Dar es Salaam on 4th Sept., 1916; attached to Zanzibar govt. to suprintend docking and repairs to Zanzibar govt. steamers "Cupid" and "Khalifa," July, 1917 to Apr., 1918; apptd. to H.M.S. "Orcoma" for escort duties in Nth. Atlantic, Apr. to Nov., 1918; asst. port offr., Zanzibar, Apr., 1919; port, offr., 5th Dec., 1923; lieut. comdr., R.N.R. (ret. list), 10th Apr., 1925; Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 3rd cls., 1934.

CHARLTON, FREDERICK RAYMOND.—B. 1886; ed. Brisbane Grammar Schl.; surr., Kedah, 1913; resigned, 1916; surr., lands dept., Fiji, 1921; seconded to regr. titles office, 1925; asst. commr., lands and crown surv., 1926; ag. commr., lands, crown surv., chm., mining bd., and conserv., forests, 1930; commr., lands, crown surv., chm., mining board, ag. chm., native lands commn., 1935; nom. mem., leg. co., 1935.

CHARLTON, WALTER DUNCAN, Lieut. Reg. Army, R. of O. Worces. Regt.—B. 1897; serv. with army and R.A.F., France, Flanders, India and Egypt, 1914-19; asst. audr. grade A. F.M.S., Aug., 1927; transf'd S.S. estab., Feb., 1932; asst. audr., S.pore., Dec., 1934.

CHARNAUD, Cecil ARTHUR ROY, Lt.-Commdr. R.N. (Retd.).—B. 1903; ed. R.N. Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth; lieut., 1925; ret., 1927; lt.-comdr. (retd.), 1933; cadet, N. Rhodesia, July, 1929; dist. offr., Grade III, Aug., 1931; seconded as asst. sec., secr't., Jan., 1934; attld., C.O., Feb., 1935.

CHARTER, ARTHUR ERNEST, O.B.E. (1922).—B. 1882; Transvaal land sttlmt. bd., 1901; land dept., 1902; priv. sec. to commr. of lands, 1903; ag. ch. clk., land and irrigtn. dept., 1908; ch. clk., adminstr.s' off., June, 1910; prov. sec., Natal, Feb., 1928.
CHASE, Lisle AUBERT.—B. 1889; entd. civ. serv., Barbados, 1908; war serv., 1917-19; supt., govt. indust. schls., 1932.

CHASEN, Frederic Nutter.—B. 1896; trooper Norfolk Yeomanry (K.O.R.R.), Apr., 1915; 2nd lieut., Suffolk Regt., Mar., 1919; asst. curator, Raffles Museum, S.pore., Jan., 1921; curator, Mar., 1924; dir., Raffles Museum and Library, S.pore., Mar., 1932.
CHASEY, Lieut.-Col. Lionel Cecil, M.C.—B. 1890; asst. engnr., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; exec. engnr., Nov., 1929; do., S'pore, May, 1931; do., Penang, Jan., 1934; do., Kedah, April, 1934.
CHAU, Kwan Lam, M.A. (Durham), Dip. Ed. (Lond.).—B. 1890; mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1923; inspr., Eng. ptye. schls. in addn., 1926; ag. headmnr., Wankai Schl., 1936.

CHAYTOR, C. J.—B. 1889; ed. King's Schl., Worcester; served in Royal Navy, 1906-12 (asst. paymr.); asst. res., N. Nigeria; attd. as lieut., Cameroons Expedy. Force, Aug., 1914 to May, 1916; seconded to Admy., Aug., 1917; served as paymr. lieut.-commdr., H.M.S. Liverpool, Adriatic, Mediterranean and Black Seas; demob., Maj., 1919; 1st cls. interpreter, French and higher standard Hausa, 1919; lower standard Arabic, 1924; temp. attd. to C.O., 1927.

CHAYTOK, Maj.-Gen. Sir Edward Walter Cleveaux, K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C.V.O., C.B. (D.), A.D.C. to the King.—B. 1868; jnd. Marlborough Hussar Vol. (N.Z.), 1886; lieut., Oct., 1888; capt., Mar., 1893; maj. in N.Z. Militia, Mar., 1901; lieut.-col., Jan., 1902; temp. col., Jan., 1911; transf'd. to N.Z. Staff Corps, Jan., 1911; prom. col., June, 1913; maj.-gen., Apr., 1917; commd., N.Z. mil. forces, Dec., 1919; ret., Mar., 1924; served in S. African War, 1900-1902 (severely wounded); mentd. in desps.; (Queen's med., three clasps, King's med., two clasps); left N.Z. with main body N.Z. Exped. Force 1914 as A.A.G. with rank of col.; temp. brig.-gen., Dec., 1915; temp. maj.-gen. while employed as a divi. comdr., Apr., 1917, and maj.-gen. in N.Z.E.F., Apr., 1917; apptd. G.O.C., N.Z.E.F. in Egypt; Order of the White Eagle, 3rd cls. (with swords) (Serbian), 1916; Order of the Nile, 2nd cls., 1918; mentd. in desps. (6).

CHEFSEMAN, Harold Ambrose Robinson.—B. 1889; ed. Perse Schl.; European mast., Penang Free Schl., Sept., 1907; instruc., Penang normal classes in addn., Aug., 1908; senr. instruc., ditto, Apr., 1909; heatmast., Northam rd. schl., Aug., 1922; inspr. schls., Penang, Jan., 1923; supt., educ., Johore, Mar., 1923; inspr., schls., Singapore and Labuan, 1934; del. to Imp. confc. on social hygiene, July, 1934.

CHELMSPORD, 3rd Baron (U.K.), creat. 1856, Frederic John Napier Thesiger, G.C.M.G. (1912), K.C.M.G.—B. 1868; ed. at Winchester and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. law), 1891; M.A., 1894; Fellow of All Souls, 1892; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1893; mem. of London schl., 1901-4; mem. of L.C.C., 1904; gov. of Queensland, 8th Aug., 1905; gov. of N.S. Wales, 16th Mar., 1909, to Mar., 1913; ag. gov. gen. of Australia, Dec., 1909, to Jan., 1910; chancellor of the Order of St. M. and St. G., 1914-1916; viceroy of India, 1916; first lord of the adm'y., Jan.-Nov., 1924.

CHERRY, William Thorpe.—B. 1896; mily. serv. W. Indies and France, Aug., 1914-Mar., 1916; supt., govt. printing office, S'pore, July, 1926; 2nd lieut., S.S.V.F., Feb., 1928; lieut., Feb., 1930; capt., June, 1930; mem., fool cont. advy. cunbe., Nov., 1934.

CHEVERTON, Reginald Leslie, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1901; casualty house surg., 1927; house surg., 1927, and house physician, 1928, gen. hosp., Birmingham; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, Dec. 1928; ag. dir., health, Aug., 1933; senr. specialist, June-Sept., 1933; senr. med. land Is., 1935; do., Br. Honduras.
CHILCOT, Thomas Charles—B. 1883; C.A. shipping agts., 1902; apptd. to C.A. at 1912; asst. head, gen. dept., Mar., 1922; head, pay dept., Nov., 1933; accnt., R.M. currency bd., 1920.
CHILTON, Noel, B.A., B.M., B.Chir. D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1890; ed. borough Coll., Brasenose Coll., Oxford; St. Bartholomew's Hosp.; med. offr., India Territory, Mar., 1929.
CHINTAMANI, B.—B. 1878; apptd. to Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1934; office ass't., Jaffna kach., Feb., 1934; extra office ass't., Mar., 1935.

CHISHOLM, Sir Joseph Andrew, K.C.M.G. (1935)—B. 1863; ed. pub. schls., St. Anselm Univ. of St. Francis Xavier, Antigua, and M.A., and Dalhousie Law Schl., E. (LL.B.); admitted to the bar, 1886; m. Halifax, 1909, 1910 and 1911; pres. of Canadian Municipalities, 1910; K.C.M.G.; puisne judge, sup. ct., Nova Scotia, Feb., ch. just., June, 1931.

CHRISTIAN, John Harold Smith—B. 1887; ed. Alderman Newton's schl.; apptd. after compet. exam., clk., G.P.O., on mil. serv., 4th Aug., 1914 to 8th June, 1919; commd. in desps., Feb., 1915; commandant corps signals, 1919; loaned to C.O., 17th bde., apptd. cler. offr. and assigned to C.O., in 1921; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, July, 1925.

CHRISTIANI, Henry Perot, M.R.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1879; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; clk., cust., Br. Guiana, 1897; 5th cls. clk., 1903; 3rd cls. offr. and govt. surv., land mines dept., 1904; 2nd cls. offr. and govt. surv., 1908; drawing office asst., 1924; sec., land mines dept., 1927; sec. and acctt. dept., 1929; ag. commr., lands and mines, 1933.

CHRISTIE, James, C.M.G. (1931), M.A. (N.Z.).—B. 1880; ed. State schl. and Univ. Coll., N.Z.; schl. teacher, 1897; treas. dept., 1901; crown law office, 1907; drafting office, 1907; asst. law draftsman, 1916; law draftsman, 1918.

CHRISTIANI, Fred Hamilton, M.R.E. (1918).—Asst. treas., G. Cosst., 1901-1906; commr., army pay dept., 1915; K.A.M. dept., July, 1916; ag. ch. paymr., K.A.M., 1917; asst. treas., Truc. Territory, Dec., 1919; senr. treas., 1930; ag. dep. treas., Oct., 1932-Apr., 1934.

CHRISTOFFELZ, Arthur—B. 1890; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. serv., 1915; extra office ass't., Jaffna kach., Jaffna, Jan., 1917; office ass't., Jaffna kach., Mar., 1919; pol. mag., Dandaga Oct., 1920; ditto, Kurume gala, Sept., 1921; attltm. offr., Oct., 1926; ag. dist. judge, N. Mar., 1927; asst. attltm. offr., Dec., 1927; govt. agt., Kegalla, June, 1931; do., Pa and Chilaw, Sept., 1933.
CHRISTOFFELZ, Basil Morris.—P. cls. V, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1931; offr., Kalutara kach., Feb., 1931; do., Matara Jan., 1934.

CHURCHILL, William Foster Norton—1898; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sidg. Coll., Camb. (exhbr.); B.A. (1921); Sw. R.F.A., Aug., 1917; served in France, 1917; F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attd., dist. office, Kaj 1921; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; asst. dist. Churchill, Right Hon. Winston Spencer, P.C. (1907). — M.P. for dham, 1900-6; for N.W. Manchester, 1906-8; Dundee, 1908; late lieut. 4th Queen's Own Hussars; ed. Harrow, Sandhurst; entd. military, 35; served with Spanish forces in Cuba, 1896; t.c.l.s. (Spanish) Order of Military Merit; served attached 31st Punjab Infantry, with Malakand field force, 1897; present at operations in Bajaour, including actions of 16th and 30th Sept. (despatches); served as orderly offr. to Sir Lockhart with Tirah exped. force, 1898 (clasp); served, attached 21st Lancers, with Nile exped. force, 1898; present at battle of Khartoum (medal with clasp); contested Oldham (C.) 1899; served lieut. South Africa Light Horse; acted as press repdt., "Morning Post," South Africa, 1899-1900; taken prisoner, action 15th Nov., but escaped 12th Dec.; present at actions of Acton, Venter's Spruit, Hussar Hill, Gingolos, Monte Cristo, and at battles of Spion Kop, Vaal Reitzen, and Pieters; also operations round Deetendorp, April, 1900; passage of Sand River, 10th May; engagements of Johannesburg and Diamond Hill, and capture of Pretoria (medal with clasps); partly under-sec. of state for the Colonies, Dec., 1905; visited Uganda and E. Africa, 1907; pres. of Bd. of T., 1908; Homsec., 1910-11; adm. lord of the Admiralty, 1911; chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, 1915; min. of munitions, 1917; sec. of state, War Office and Air Ministry, 1919; S. of S. for the Colonies, 14th Feb., 1921 to 25th Oct., 1922; chancellor of the exchequer, 7th Nov., 1924; author of "The Story of the Malakand Field Force"; "The River War"; "Savrola"; "London to Ladysmith, via Pretoria"; "Ian Hamilton's March"; "Life of Lord Randolph Churchill." Churns, W. H.—B. 1890; apptd., after exam., asst. library attendant, C.O., 6th Apr., 1911; on military service from 5th Aug., 1914, to 19th Jan., 1919; paper room clk. in library, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Apr., 1921.

CLARKE, ARTHUR GRENfell, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Mountjoy Sch., Dublin and Trinity Coll., Dublin; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Dec., 1929; passed cadet, Jan., 1932; asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., Feb., 1932; J.P., 1935; ch. asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., Aug., 1935; 2nd asst. to do., Oct., 1935.

CLARKE, CAPT. CHARLES NOBLE ARDEN.—B. 1898; ed. Rossall Sch.; on mily. serv., M.G.C., 1916-20; cadet, Nigeria, 1920; asst. dist. offr., 1923; dist. offr., 1928; ag. prin. asst. sec., Nigerian secr., 1933; govt. sec., Bechuanaland Prot., 1936; res. consnr., 1937.
CLARKE, F. H.—Asst. game warden, E.A.P., 1919; ag. game warden, Kenya, 1932.

CLARKE, LION. SIR FRANCIS GRENVILLE, K.B.E., M.L.C.—B. 1879; ed. Scotch Coll., Melbourne, Melbourne Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; M.L.C. for N. Prov. (Vic.); min. for lands and water supply, Nov., 1917 to Oct., 1919; min. of pub. wks. and water supply, Oct., 1919 to July, 1923; pres., leg. coun., Victoria, July, 1923.

CLARKE, HON. SIR FREDERICK JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1911), C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. Christ Church Mid. Sch., Barbados, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1879; M.H.A., Barbados, 1887; pres., Barbados agric. soc'y., 1905; mem., cent. poor law board; M.E.C., 1903; J.P.; O.C., Barbados vol. force, 1906; speaker, H. of A., Barbados, 1898-1934.

CLARKE, FRANCIS PRIESTLEY COURTNEY.—B. 1879; ed. Haileybury and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A., Oxford, 1901; LL.B., Cape, 1909; advoc., sup. ct., Cape, 1910; ent. Cape civ. serv., 1903; served in depts. of justice, native affrs. and forests; ch. clk., S.W.A., admntr., 1923; asst. sec., 1925; sec. for S.W.A., 1934; chmn., S.W.A.—Angola bndry., and Cunene Water comm., 1929; rep. Union govt. at Geneva at mtgs. of Mandates Comsn., 1930.

CLARKE, GEOFFREY T. S.—B. 1879; clk. to P.M.O., Basutoland, 1913; clk., treas., 1914; clk. to asst. consnr., 1919; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Police, 1921; war serv., 1916-19; asst. dist. consnr., 1936.
CLARKE, JOSEPH SHERWOOD, B.A. (Harvard).—B. 1903; ed. Rossall Schl. and Harvard Univ.; supt., educn., Nigeria, Apr., 1927; headmstr., Grammar Schl., St. Vincent, 1931.
CLARKE, ROBERT WILLIAM, F.I.C., diploma, food and drugs, agric. chem., biol. chem., and bacteriology.—Analyst to sany. comans., Gibraltar, Oct., 1913 to Jan., 1921; govt. analyst, Cyprus, Jan., 1921.

CLARKE, STEPHEN GRAINGER.—B. 1894; 5th cls. clk., P.W.D., Fiji, 1914; 4th cls. clk., audit dept., 1915; on active serv., 1915; 3rd cls. clk., audit dept., 1922; 2nd cls. clk., 1922; acctnt., Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1924; dep. consnr. (temp. and prov'l.), 1926; treas., collr., cust. and ch. postmtr., 1928; dep. consnr., 1929; ag. res. consnr., 1935.

CLARKE, THOMAS LEEVE EVELYN, M.D., C.M., McGill (1911).—B. 1885; ed. at the Lodge Schl. and Harrison Coll., Barbados, and McGill Univ., Montreal; junr. res. surgeon, general hosp., Barbados, Aug., 1911; med. offr., Virgin Is., May, 1912; ag. consnr. and max., May, 1912; mem. of quarantine bd., health offr., mem. of exec. coun., and J.P., Virgin Is., July, 1912; ag. consnr., Virgin Is., Apr., 1913 to Jan., 1914; ag. consnr., mag. and dep. judge, summary jurisdiction et., Virgin Is., from Dec., 1914 to May, 1917, and from July, 1917 to Oct., 1918; pres., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1918; provost, fed. leg. and exec. coun., Leeward Is., 1919; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1919; ag. consnr., 1919-16; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1916; pres., Overseas Contg., 1916-18; temp. btr., German and S.W. campaign, 1918-19; Overseas Contg., 1916-18; temp. btr., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; Traralgon supt., cust., Feb.-May, 1930; mem., cust., 1935.

CLARKSON, CHARLES FRANKLIN.—Boys' Model Schl. and Boys' High Schl., admitted atty., Natal prov. dist. court, 1915; offr., Durban Light Inf., and adjt., R.F.A., 1915; mem., exec. coun., 1915; supt., cust., 1930; min., posts and tele. and pub. tel., 1933.

CLARKSON, FRANK CECIL, O.B.E., M.B.E. (1930)—B. 1882; P.W.D., S. Africa, 1905-07; ch. asst., S. Nigeria, 1907; European War, Gallipoli, Egypt, 1914-19; ast. supt., pub. wks., and treas., Nevis, Jan., 1921; ag. comm., mag. Is., Apr., 1922; col. engr., Dominica, Jan., 1923; mem., leg. com., mag., etc., Virgin Is., Nov., 1926; judge, Turks and Caicos Is., Aug., 1927.

CLAUSON, GERARD LESLIE MACDONALD (1933), O.B.E. (Mily.), 1919-28; R. of Eton, 1903 and C.C.C., Oxford; Sanskrit Scholar, 1911; 1st cls. cler. Mode., 1912; Hall-Houghton Sch., 1913; 2nd cls. Greats, 1914; B.A., Mew Arabic Scholar, 1920; appd. for exam. 2nd cls. clk., B. of India, 1914, but released for mil. service; 7th Som. L.I., 21st Aug., 1914; par. list, 19th April, 1915; served in W. Gallipoli, Egypt and Mesopotamia; Staff, 26th Dec., 1915 to 12th Aug., 23rd Apr., 1918, to 28th Feb., 1919; in despatches Mesopotamia, 1915; French Croix de Guerre, with Palms, clk. in C.O., 1st Mar., 1919; par. list, 1920; on sp. serv., Lausanne, for treaty, June to July, 1923; acc't., Permanent Mandates Com., at various times, June, 1926 onwards; stud. Defence Coll., Jan.-Dec., 1927; currency comte., 1930; visited S.S. Kong as mem. and sec. of Hong-Kong comte., Feb. to June, 1931; dep. U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Ottawa, July to Aug., 1932; ag. dir., Dec., 1934.

CLAXTON, Herbert William.—Weymouth Coll., 1909-16; 2nd lieut., 1917; served Palestine, 1917-18; chartered accnt. (Eng.), 1924; hon. treas., Somaliland, 1924; ast. treas., Territory, 1925; senr. ast. treas., 1929; treas. and ch. of cust., 1938.

CLAY, GEOFFREY FLETCHER, O.M.C., B.Sc.Agr. (Edin.), N.D.A., X. Lane Dip. in Agric.—B.1895; ed. Duke of Wellington's Regt., 1914; Uganda, 1924; geneticist, Amami, 1927-28; senr. agr. offr., Uganda, dir., agr., 1931; ag. dir., agr., 1935; mem., sugar coman., 1935; agr., Nigeria, 1937.
CLEARE, LAURENCE DELANEY.—F.L.S.—B. 1893; ed. Cornell Univ., Br. Guiana, 1919; econ. biologist.
AND, JOHN STOCKWIN, M.B.E., L.A., M.I.A.—Draughtsman, P.W.D., Natal, Mar., 1909; asst. archt., P.W.D., of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; archt., Sept., sec. for pub. wks., June, 1932.

MENTI, SIR CECIL, G.C.M.G. (1931), G. (1926), C.M.G. (1916)—Ed. St. Paul's and Magdalen Coll., Oxford (demy., 1894; 898; M.A., 1901); hon. mention Hertford 895; 1st cls. mods., 1896; hon. mention and Craven schl., 1896; Boden lit scholar, 1897; prox. acc. Gaisford Prose, 1897; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1898; acc. Chancellor's Latin essay, 1899; Hong Kong, 1899; passed cadet, 1900; col. sec., and ag. clk. of councils, 900, to Oct., 1901; asst. regisr.-gen., Aug., nem. bd. of examrs. in Chinese, Apr., 1902; ad for spec. serv. under govt. of India, May, (P.), June, 1902; ag. asst. col. sec., and clk. cils., Sept., 1902, to June, 1903; sec. interpre. bd., Dec., 1902; seconded for famine relief Kwang Si, April to June, 1903; mem. of New Territories, Dec., 1903; ag. ast. land nd pol. mag., New Territories, May, 1905, t., 1906; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1907; attended Internat. Opium Conf. at hai, Feb., 1909; priv. sec. to H.E. the r., Apr., 1910; ag. col. sec., and mem. and legis. couns., on various occasions, 2; col. sec., B. Guiana, 1911; admstd. 8th June, 1916 to 14th Apr., 1917, Apr., 1919 and June to Oct., 1921; col. sec., 1, Nov., 1922; admstd. govt., Nov., 1922, 1923 and Apr. to Oct., 1925; gov., Hong 1st Nov., 1925; Knt. of Grace of St. Johnusalem, 1926; hon. I.D.H., Hong Kong 1926; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Straits ts. and high comsnr., Malaya States, 1929; ed., 1934.

MENTI, LOCKHART.—B. 1881; served in Mounted Rifles; sub-inspr., Basutoland td Pol., 1905; inspr., 1913; served in Boer War; staff offr., 1926; dep. asst. tr., 1929; asst. comsnr., 1930, dist. comsnr.,
EMENTS, JOHN BURTON, B.Sc. Forestry.)—B. 1891; for offr., Nyasaland, Sept., ag. ch. for offr., Oct. to Dec., 1920 and to Dec., 1923; ch. for offr., Dec., 1923.
EMMEY, A.V., M.A., M.B., B.Ch. (Oxf.), C.S., L.R.C.P., M.M.S.A.—B. 1901; med. Tanganyika Territory, July, 1928.

ENDINNING, J. B.Sc. (Lond.), A.M.I.C.E. 1888; ed. Queen's Univ., Belfast; asst. dir., Gold Coast, Oct., 1919; dep. dir., central branch, Jan., 1920; dir., do., Dec., dep. survr.-gen., Apr., 1924; ag. survr.-gen. various occasions, 1921-26; survr.-gen., Oct.,
EVERLEY, HERBERT NORMAN.—B. 1894; ser., 1914-19; called to bar, Inner Temple; admstve. ser., Nigeria, 1919; station mag., asst. judge, Prot. high ct., 1934.

LIFFE, ARTHUR LEWIS, M.A.—B. 1896; schol. of Eton, 1915 and of Balliol Coll., ord., 1921; Craven schol., 1929; 1st cls. house, 1923; Ireland schol., 1923; 1st cls. lit. div., 1925; apptd. to C.O. after compet. exam., Oct., 1925; sec., managing comte., Imp. Bureau of Mycology, 1926; seconded as pte. sec. to gov., Nigeria, 1927; pte. sec. to perm. under sec., C.O. (Sir Samuel Wilson), 3rd Feb., 1930; temp. ag. prin., 1st Oct., 1931; asst. pte. sec. to S. of S. for the cols. (Sir Philip Cunliffe-Lister), 9th Nov., 1931; prin., 4th Aug., 1933.

CLIFFORD, CAPT. HON. SIR BEDE EDMUND HUGH, K.C.M.G. (1933), C.B. (1931), C.M.G. (1924), M.V.O. (1920).—B. 1890; served, European War; pte. sec. Lord Novar, gov.-gen. C'wealth Australia, 1918-20; sec., H.R.H. Prince Arthur of Connaught (gov.-gen. and commdr.-in-ch., Union of S. Africa) 1921-23; sec. Earl of Athlone, gov.-gen. and commander-in-ch., Union of S. Africa, 1924; Imp. sec., S. African high comsnr., Apr., 1924; rep. of H.M. Govt. of Gt. Britain in the Union of S. Africa, 1928; gov. and commdr.-in-ch. of the Bahamas, 1931.

CLIFFORD, SIR HUGH CHARLES, G.C.M.G. (1921), G.B.E., (1925), K.C.M.G. (1909); C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1866; joined Perak service, 1883; passed in Malay, Feb., 1885; collr. land rev., Kuala Kangsar, Mar., 1885; on special service to Pahang, 1887 (recd. thanks of S. of S.); ag. govt. agt. there, 1887-8; supt., Ulu Pahang, 1889; ag. Br. rea. Pahang, 1890 to 1891, and from Feb., 1893; took a leading part in suppression of Pahang rebellion, 1892; sec. to govt., Selangor, Dec., 1894, but continued to act as res. of Pahang; visited Cocos-Keeling Is. as special comsnr., June, 1894; led armed exped. into native states of Tréngganu and Kelantan to effect capture of fugitive rebel leaders, Mar.-June, 1895 (recd. thanks S. of S.); res. Pahang, July, 1896; gov. of North Borneo and Labuan, 1899 to 1901; Br. res. Pahang, 1901; ag. col. sec., Trinidad and Tobago, Sept., 1903, confirmed, Sept., 1904; sent on deputation to B. Guiana in Nov., 1905, to enquire into municipal institutions; admstd. govt., Mar. to Aug., 1904, and from Apr. to Oct., 1906; col. sec., Ceylon, May, 1907; admstd. govt., on various occasions, 1907-12; gov., G. Const., Dec., 1912; gov., Nigeria, July, 1919; gov. of Ceylon, Nov., 1925; gov. S. Sttlmts. and high comsnr., Malay States, June, 1927; ret., 1929; concluded with M. Noufflard, the lieut.-gov. of Dahomey, in Aug., 1914 an agreement for the temporary partition between the British and French Govts., and for the provisional administration of Togoland; admstd. Br. sphere of occupation, Togoland, concurrently with the G. Comt., from Aug., 1914, to Aug., 1919; chmn., comte., on W. African trade and taxation, Oct.-Nov., 1921 (received thanks of S. of S.); is part author, with Sir Frank Swettenham, of a Malayan dictionary, and author of more than a dozen books, mostly on Malayan subjects, and of a translation of the penal code into Malay; also of a history of the G. Coast regt. in the F. African campaign.
CLIPSHAM, PHILIP RICHARD.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 31st Sept., 1920; seconded as regisr. on staff of high comsnr. in Canada for H.M.'s govt. in Gt. Britain, 1932.
CLIVE, CAPT. J. H.—Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, May., 1920.
CLOSE, R. W., B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1867; ed. S. African Coll., Cape Town; M.P. for Rondebosch, 1920-29; Mowbray, 1929-33; leader, Cape bar, min. plenip., Washington, Jan., 1934.

CLUBB, HON. WILLIAM REID—B. 1884; served on Morris munici. coun., 1916-20; mem. leg.assembly for Morris, Manitoba, 1920-29; re-elec., July, 1922; min. of public works, Manitoba, 8th Aug., 1922; re-elec. for Morris by accl., 26th Aug., 1922.

CLUER, REGINALD MONTAGU.—B. 1891; ed. Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. (hons. schl., jurisprudence); barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1915; served Great War, 1914-16; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1932.

CLUTTERBUCK, P.A., M.C.—B. 1897; schol. of Malvern Coll. and of Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, 1915; Coldstream Guards, 1916-19; capt. and adjt., 3rd batt., Oct., 1917; M.C. and ment. in despa., 1918; appntd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., G.P.O., 1st Sept., 1919; transf. to C.O., 13th Feb., 1922; sec., managing comte., Imperial Bureau of Mycology, 1923-25; addl. pvt. sec., to party, under sec., (Lord Arnold), June-Oct., 1924; sec., Milner-Lovat agric. comte., 1924-25; sec., Donoughmore comte., on Ceylon constitution and visited Ceylon, 1927-28; pvt. sec. to perm.-under sec. for Doms. affrs., 23rd Aug., 1928; prin., Doms. Office, 14th Nov., 1929; U.K. delegation to L. of N. assembly, 1929, 1930 and 1931, and to reparations comte., Lausanne, 1932.

CLUVER, COL. EUSTACE HENRY, M.A., M.D., B.Ch., D.P.H.—B. 1894; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S. Africa, Hertford Coll., Oxford and King's Coll. Hosp., London; Rhodes schol. for Stellenbosch; Burrey Yeo, schol., King's Coll.; senr. demy, Magdalen Coll., Oxford; 1st cls., final hon., nat. science (physiology); served with 1st S. African field ambulance in France; major, S.A.M.C., Active Citizen Force; lecturer in geology, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, 1914; prof. of physiology, Univ. of Witwatersrand Johannesburg, 1919-26; asst. health offr., Union of S. Africa, 1926; senr. asst. health offr., do., 1934; transf. R. of O., 1929; lieut.-col., R. of O., 1932; D.D.M.S. defence headqrs., 1933; D.M.S. with temp. rank of col., 1934; comdr. Order of Hosp., of St. John of Jerusalem, 1935; as-sec. edtr., Journal of Industrial Hygiene (America); published many papers in tech. jnl.s. on subject of physiology and hygiene.

CLUVER, FRANKRICK WILLIAM PAUL, B.A. (Cape), M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Cape Town). Fellow of the Royal Institute of Public Health; major, S.A.M.C. (res. of offrs.).—Ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Edinburgh Univ. and Univ. of Cape Town; res. med. offr., gen. hosp., Pretoria, and med. offr. to S.A. Air Force (major S.A.M.C.), 1924; O.C., mily. hosp., Wynberg, 1926; O.C., mily. hosp., Roberts Heights, 1927; asst. health offr., Union health dept., Pretoria, 1932; do., Durban, 1933.

COAKER, HON. STR WM. F., K.B.E. (1923).—President, fisherman's protective union, Newfoundland; M.H.A. for Bonavista, 1913; M.H.A., Twillingate, 1914; mem. of tonnage comte., 1917; M.E.C., 1917; min. without portfolio, 1918.

COALES, FRANCIS GEORGE, M.I.C.E., M.I.S.E.—B. 1889; ed. Elstow Schi., Bedford; ch. draftsman, P.W.D., Perak, 1914; as-t. engnr., 1917; ag. exec. engnr., grade III, 1919; ag. exec. engnr. and asst. to D.P.W., 1922; exec. engnr., 1923; ag. asst. D.P.W., 1925; exec. engnr. in ch. deptl. tech. schl., 1925-27; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., 1926; exec. engnr., Larut, 1928; lieut., M.S.V.R., 1929; senr. exec. engnr., Jan., 1930.

COATES, RT. HON. JOSEPH GORDON, P.C. (1926), M.C.—B. 1878; ed. privately; mem. of New Zealand parl. since 1911; min. of justice, 1919-20; P.M.G., 1919-25; min. of pub. wks., 1920-26; min. native affrs., 1921-1928; min. rly., 1923-1928; prime min., 1925-1928; min. external affrs., 1928; min. mines, 1928; min., agr., 1928; ret. from office, 1928; joined coalition min., 1931; min., pub. wks., 1931-33; min., transport, 1931-35; min., finance, 1933-35; min., cult., 1933-36; N.Z. rep. at Imp. Conf., 1928; at Ottawa conf., 1932; served with N.Z. Force as capt. and major, 1916-1919; awarded M.C. with bar.

COATS, ROBERT HAMILTON, B.A., F.S.S. (Ips.), F.R.C.S.—B. 1874; ed. Toronto Univ.; banker scholarship in pol. econ., and Wye prize English; on staff of "Toronto Globe," 1897-1900; editor, Labour Gaz., and ch. statistician, dept. of labour, 1901-12; mem., comm. on cost of living, 1913-14; dom. statistician and compt. census, 1914; author of "The Labour Movement in Canada" (Canada and its Provinces); part author of life of James Douglas (Makers of Canada series); offl. del. to Br. Empire Statistical Conf., 1920; mem. of comm. on statistics under League of Nations, 1920; mem., International Statistical Inst.

COBON, HUGH HORACE, A.S. (Australia), M.Q.I.S.—B. 1892; Aus. Imp. Focs., 1914-17; authd. surv., Queensland, 1920; surv., F.M.S. rly., Nov., 1920; surv., Penang, Sept., 1920; do., Kedah, Feb., 1924; do., F.M.S., July, 1925; asst. aspt., Johore, Feb., 1930; do., F.M.S., Oct., 1933; ag. aspt., Johore, June, 1934.

COCHRANE, CAPT. BASIL WILLIAM DUNSTERALD.—B. 1891; Cape Mounted Riflemen, 1914; served S.W. Africa campaign and Mesopotamia (R. of O.); "Dunsterforce" (Azerbaijan), Nort. Persia, 1918-19; South Persia Rifles, Kermanshah Brig., capt. and adjt., 1st Cavalry, 1919-20; "1914-15" star, Br. War Victory and L.G.S. medal (bar, S. Persia); constable, Kenya police, 1921; asst. aspt., pol., 1925; aspt., do., 1933.

COCHRANE, EDGAR, M.B., Ch.B. (Glasgow, 1925), D.P.H. (1931).—B. 1887; on war med., 1915-19; ag. dist. surg., Jesselton, Br. N. Borneo, 1925-26; asst. govt. med. offr. of health and deport health offr., Br. Guiana, 1932; med. offr., Gold Coast, 1936.
COCKAYNE, ALFRED HEIDE.—B. 1880; sci. biologist, N.Z. agric. dept., 1904; biologist, 1910; dir., fields div., 1923, also dir., plant research, 1927; asst. dir. gen., agr., 1929; dir.-gen., 1930.

COCKEY, C. E. E.—Ed. St. Edwards Schi. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; M.A.; served in France and Salonica; 3rd Devons, atchd. 2nd Glouc. Reg., May, 1915, to Jan., 1919; ag. capt.; menal. despa.; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, June, 1919.

COCKMAN, HERBERT JAMES, D.F.C.—B. 1898; ed. St. Paul's Schi. and Merton Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1921; attd. Fed. secret., Jan., 1922; offr. cls. V, Dec., 1924; offr. cls. IV, dist. offr., Pekan, Dec., 1928; offr. cls. III, dist. offr., Lipis, Dec., 1933; ch. asst dist. offr., Kinta, Nov., 1934.

COCKRAM, BEN.—B. 1903; ed. Victoria Taunton's Schi. and Oxford; hist. schol., Queen's Coll., 1921-25; 1st cls., mod. hist., 1925; goldsmith's sen. student, 1926; 1st cls., med. and mod. hist., London, 1923; fellow, Michigan, 1925-26; Ph.D., 1926; apptd. after compet. exam. as prin., Dominions Office, 4th Nov., 1926; prn. to the party, under sec. for dom. affrs. (Mr. Lum), 1st Oct., 1930; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec. state for Dominion affrs., 22nd June, 1931; prn. 21st Nov., 1934.

COCKS, HUGH CEOL, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1919; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; Colombo kach., Jan., 1928; attd. secre., May, 1928; office asst., Nuwara Eliya kach., 1929; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, May, 1930; asst. attnt. offr., June, 1930.
CODD, LESLIE EDWARD WASTELL, M.Sc.—B. 1908; ed. Natal Univ. Coll., S.A.; plant breeder, Br. Guiana, 1931.

COE, THOMAS PEARSON, M.C.—B. 1887; ed. King Edward VI Sch., Norwich and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; (schol.) 2nd cls. class. mods., 1908, 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1910; B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; passed cadet, Deo., 1912; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1913; on war serv. with Norfolk Regt., 1915-18; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; demob., Feb., 1919; 2nd asst. sec., Fed. secretariat, Nov., 1919; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec. in add., Nov., 1919 to June, 1920; 2nd lieut. M.S.V.R., Apr., 1921; ag. capt., Nov., 1921; capt., Aug., 1922; supernumy offr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; major, M.S.V.R., Jan., 1923; dist. offr., July, 1924; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1927; sec. to res., Perak; asst. adviser, Kelantan, Sept., 1928; ag. gen. advr., Johore, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. I.B.; asst. adviser, Muar, Johore, Feb., 1930; ag. Br. advr., Kelantan, May, 1931; ag. sec., postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., Mar., 1933; ag. dir.-gen., posts and tels., Malaya, Jan., 1934; offr., cls. I.A., Jan., 1936.
COGLAN, B.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O.(Dub.), D.T.M.(Liv.).—B. 1899; I.F.S., A.M.C., 1922-23; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1926.

COHEN, ANDREW BENJAMIN.—B. 1909; ed. Malvern and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls., 1st pt., class. tripods, 1st cls., 2nd pt., class. tripods; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., inland rev., Oct., 1932; transf. to C.O., 2nd Oct., 1933.

COHEN, EZECHIEL, M.B.E. (1924), I.S.O. (1936).—B. 1882; clk., Cape govt. rly., May, 1900; transf. to Imperial Mil. Rly., Nov., 1900; transf. to Bech. Prot. service and attached to office of high commnr. for S. Africa, Aug., 1901; asst. Imp. acctn., Apl., 1918; prin. clk., Nov., 1923.

COLE, ARNOLD KIRTLAND.—B. 1884; sec. to bd. of educn., Bahamas, 1907; ag. res. justice, Rum Cay, 1908; ag. inspr. and genl. aspt. of schools, 1910, 1911, 1913; 3rd cls., treasry. dept., 1913; 1st cls., P.O., 1914; ch. cls. and asst. examr. offr., cust. dept., Aug., 1921; ag. contr., cust., in 1922, 1923, 1924 and 1928; asst. contr., and examining offr., Dec., 1923; survr., shipping, Mar., 1925; ag. contr. cust. and regis., shipping from Apr., 1926; ag. harbourm., Mar. to Apr., 1927; contr., cust., Sept., 1928; M.L.C., Dec., 1931; del., W. Indies cust. confce., Bermuda, 1934.

COLEBATCH, HON. SIR HAL PATRISHALL, KT. BACH. (1927).—B. 1872; ed. Goolda, S. Australia; M.L.C. for E. Prov., W. Australia, May, 1912; col. sec., min. for educn., min. of pub. health and leader of legis. coun., 1916-19; premier, W. Australia for short period, 1919; min. of educn. and pub. health, 17th May, 1919; agt.-gen. for W. Australia, 1923-27; mem. Australian constitution royal comm., 1927-29; C'wealth senator for W. Australia since 1929.

COLEMAN, EPHRAIM HERBERT, K.C.—B. 1880; ed. Pub. and High Schls., Annpriod and Carleton Place, Ont., and Univ. of Manitoba; sec.-treas., Canadian Bar Assoc., 1919-33; dean, Man. Law Sch., 1929-33; bencher, Law Soc. of Man., 1928-33; under-sev. of state, Canada, 1933.

COLEMAN-DOSCAS, APOLLO ERNEST, Dip. Agr., W.A. Sc. Teach., Claremont, W.A.; Univ. of W.A.—B. 1893; asst. agrl. inspr., F.M.S., July, 1920; inspr. agr., Johore, Aug., 1922; A.F.O., Malacca, Apr., 1925; do., F.M.S. (Perak South), Jan., 1928; ag. sr. agrl. offr., Perak, Sept., 1931; A.F.O., N. Sembilan, Sept., 1933; state agrl. offr., Johore, Feb., 1935.

COLEY, CAPT. C. F. (Reg. Army, R. of O.)—B. 1897; ed. Oxford Univ. and Université de Paris; served with Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., Devon Regt., and R. Corps of Signals in European War, 1914-18; diplomatic serv., 1918; cadet, Nigerian adminstr. serv., Mar., 1926; ag. asst. dist. offr., Apr., 1926.

COLGAN, EDWARD PATRICK.—B. 1883; R.I.C., Sept., 1901; inspr. pol., F.M.S., Jan., 1910; hdg. inspr. pol., Jan., 1925; secd. to Kedah, Aug., 1927; asst. commr., F.M.S., Nov., 1929; offr. supt., pol. cir. K. Lumpur, Sept., 1931; do., Perak, South, Jan., 1935.

COLLENS, ARCHIBALD EDGAR, F.I.C., F.C.S.—Lab. asst., and chem. demstr. to the prof. of chem., Queen's Royal and St. Mary's Colls., Trinidad, 1900-08; lect., agrl. chem., govt. training schls., 1902-05; asst. analyst, govt. lab., Trinidad, 1908-16; offr. in charge, rubber tapping experiments, dept. of agr., 1911-17; scientific asst., bd. of agric., 1908-10; science master, Queen's Royal and St. Mary's Colls., Trinidad, 1906-08, 1910, and 1914; ag. prim. asst. anal., govt. lab., 1914-16; supt., field experiments, 1915-16; examr. in pharm. chem., to med. coun., Trinidad, 1908-17; examr. in agr., bd. of educn., Trinidad, 1917-19; govt. chem. and supt. of agr., Leeward Is.; chmn., granary bd., Antigua; mem., food control prices bd., Antigua, 1917; mem., Barbuda bd., pub. lib. trustees; mem., Fed. exec. and leg. couns., and leg. coun., Antigua; gov. and on exec. coun., Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agr., 1921.
COLLET, J. W., M.R.C.P., M.R.C.S.—Med. offr., S. Nigeria, 1901; author of "Text Book of Physiology and Hygiene."

COLLIER, C. W. H.—B. 1892; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; cler. asst., immigrn. dept., Br. Guiana, 1909; sec. to various col. comtees, and gradually promoted to 1st cls. offr., 1920; immigrn. agt., 1926; sec., colonization (500 family scheme) comtee, 1927; senr. immigrn. agt. and immigrn. agt., Demerara county, 1928; ag. immigrn. agt., gen., 1929; senr. clk., col. sec.'s office, 1930; prin. clk. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., 1932; asst. col. sec., 1934.

COLLIER, HON. PHILIP.—B. 1873; M.L.A. for Boulder, W. Australia, since 1905; min. for mines and rly. in Scaddan govt., 1911-14; and of mines and water supply, 1914-16; leader, partly, labour party, May, 1917; premier, W. Australia, Apr., 1924 to Apr., 1930.

COLLINS, CHARLES HENRY, B.A., Lond.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1910; pol. mag., Puttalam, Sept., 1912; office asst., Kandy kacheheri, Julr., 1913; asst. land setttn. offr., May, 1915; asst. land setttn. offr., Sept., 1915; ag. asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, kach., Sept., 1916; office asst., Colombo, kach., Oct., 1916; 2nd asst. col. sec., Oct., 1917; 3rd asst. col. sec., Sept., 1920; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1922; prin. asst. col. sec., Feb., 1924; addnl. asst. col. sec., Oct., 1925; dep. collr., cust., Dec., 1925; ag. dep. cl. sec., Dec., 1932; govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Feb., 1933; dep. finan. sec., Feb., 1934; offr., cls. I., grade I., 1935; ag. finan. sec., May-Nov., 1935.

COLLINS, F.—B. 1882; clk., Bri. tel. serv., 1899; clk., Cape post and tel. serv., 1900; engr. asst., 1911; inspr., traffic, 1912; traffic supt., 1920; chf. supt., 1923; chf. clk., 1925; contr., teles. and telephones, 1933; under sec., teles., 1936.
COLLINS, JOHN VICTOR, M.A., Sc.M. (Dublin), F.I.C.—B. 1897; ast. govt. analyst, Ceylon, Jan., 1925; dep. do., Oct., 1927; govt. analyst, May, 1934.

COLLINS, W. H., Ph. D., F.R.S.C.—B. 1878; ed. Univ. of Toronto, 1904; Heidelberg Univ., 1907; Chicago Univ., 1909; Univ. of Wisconsin, 1911; ast. geologist, geol. surv. of Can., 1905; jun. geol., 1912; geol., 1914; fellow Royal Soc. of Can., 1918; dir., geol. surv. of Can., 1920; ex-off. mem., Can. Inst. Min. Met.; mem., Geol. Soc. of America, 1920; has pub. numerous papers and memoirs on pre-cambrian geol. and petrology.

COLLISSON, PERCIVAL LORIMER, O.B.E. Mil., 1919.—B. 1883; ed. at Warminster and King's Coll., London; examt., E. and A. dept., 1901; col. aud. branch, Sept., 1905; examr. of accts., O.R.C., Jan., 1906; rejoined col. aud. branch, Jan., 1908; ast. auditor, N. Nigeria, Sept., 1908; 2nd cls. clk., col. audit., 1914; mil. audit., E. Africa Exped. Force, 1916 (twice, in despatch); senr. ast. auditor, E.A.P., Apr., 1919; ag. audr., E.A.P., 1920; audr., Fiji and W. Pacific, 1922; audr., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1924; audr., Uganda, Sept., 1927; audr., Hong Kong, Aug., 1930; J.P., 1932.

COLLYMORE, ERNEST ALLAN, M.A. (Oxon.), K.C. (1933)—B. 1893; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Merton Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. hons. final schl. of jurisprudence, 1914; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1920; serv. in E. Lanes. Regt., 1914-19; capt., Oct., 1915; capt., Barbados Vols. and O.C., cadet corps of the island, 1921-26; sen. mag., Bridgetown and dist. A, June, 1925; recvr. of wrecks, Aug., 1925; ag. judge, ast. ct. of appeal in 1925 and 1926; stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas and coroner for New Providence, July, 1927; ag. atty.-gen., in 1927 and 1928; atty.-gen., Barbados, June, 1928; ch. just., July, 1936.

COMBE, SIR RALPH MOLYNEUX, KT. BACH. (1920).—Ed. at Haileybury Coll. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1897; Crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 4th Aug., 1905; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1907; atty.-gen. E.A.P., 1912; atty.-gen., Nigeria, 1913; ch. just., Nigeria, 1918; ret., Mar., 1929.

COMMISSIONG, TERENCE BERTRAND.—B. 1893; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Grenada, Apr., 1909; 2nd clk., gov.'s office, June, 1915; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1919; ag. clk., col. sec.'s office, clk. of couns., med. regis., and man. govt. printing sub-dept., Feb.-Sept., 1920; seconded for active serv., Sept., 1915 to Oct., 1919; awarded meritorious serv. med. in German E. African campaign, June, 1916; apptd. J.P., Grenada, in recog. of war serv., July, 1919; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, clk. to exec. couns., med. regis. and man., govt. printing dept., May, 1921; ast. col. sec., Grenada, Jan., 1928.

CONIBEAR, JAMES HANFORD.—Reg. army, 1897-1919; sub-inspr., pol. and keeper, prisons, St. Lucia, Nov., 1920; ag. ch., pol. and inspr., prisons, 1922; prison supt., Gold Coast, Jan., 1924; lieut., G.C. Volunteers, Oct., 1924; ag. adjt., 1924-25; capt., G.C. Defence Force, Mar., 1933; supt., grade I, Sept., 1934; senr. prison supt., Jan., 1935; ag dir., prisons, June-Sept., 1935.
CONNELL, W. K., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), B. 1893; mily. serv., R.A.M.C., 1916-20; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1924; junr. surg. specialist, 1934.

CONNOLLY, HON. SIR JAMES DANIEL, KT. BACH. (1920).—B. 1870; ed. Warwick Pub. Schl. and Catholic Sch.; M.L.C., Western Aus., 1902; col. sec. and min. of com. and int. col. sec. and min. of pub. health, 1910; leg. coun. from 1906 to 1911; M.L.A. for 1914; ast. genl. in London for Western Aus., 1917-23; ast. gen. in London for Malaya, 1923-26.

CONRADIE, DAVID GIDEON, RA., LL.B. 1879; ed. Prince Alfred Hamlet and Schola, Stellenbosch and Trinity Coll., Wes- sen, mod. and gold medal, LL.D., 1906; Cape Town and Bloemfontein, 1907-09; dept., O.F.S., 1909-15; atty., Reitz ad. fonteine, 1915-33; mem., prov. and gen. adv., 1920-26; M.P., Bethlehem and Lindley, admistr., S.W. Africa, 1933.

CONRADIE, JOHANNES HENDRIK.—B. 1886; ed. Calitzdorp, Robertson, pte. sch., Stellenbosch; schlmaster, Christiana, Boshof, 1890-95 and Grey Coll. Sch., 1895; admitted as atty.-at-law, 1905; finan. sec., Reformed Ch., Cape Prov., 1903-12; Kakamas lab. col., 1912-1920; organis. Nationalist Party, Cape Prov., 1921-29; leg. assem. for Gordonia, 1924-29; admistr. Prov., Sept., 1929.

CONWAY, WILLIAM EWAN.—B. 1886; High Schl., Dundee; govt. travelling schl., 10th Dec., 1913; 2nd clk., C.S.O., Mar., 1914; clk. and priv. sec. to govt. of Falkland Is., 1916 to Apr., 1917 and Sept., 1917 to Apr., collr., cust., G. Coast, Oct., 1920; cust., June, 1928.

COOK, MAJOR DUDLEY STAFFORD.—B. 1879; ed. Elston Schl., Bedford; solt., Jun., 1909; dist. offr., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; mag. Cameroon, 1914; G.E.A., 1917-18; mag. ag. commr., lands, Nigeria, 1924-25; dist. offr., 1924; ast. commr., lands, S. Prov., 1924; dep. res., 1933; ag. commr., Colony, 1934.
COOK, JAMES, L.R.C.P. & S. L.R.F.P. & S. (Glas.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), Durham)—B. 1904; med. supt., col. Port of Spain, Trinidad, Oct., 1936.

COOK, RT. HON. SIR JOSEPH, P.C. G.C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1860; mem. of H. Commonwealth of Australia, since 1911; gen. gen., N.S. Wales, 1894-1898; min. for agric., 1898-9; min. of defence, C. of A., 1909; prime minister, C. of A., June, 1914; min. for the Navy, 1917; dite., Feb., 1918; high commr. in London for C. Nov., 1921; ret., 1927.

COOKE, ELEANOR MARGARET.—R. matron, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, May, 1917; accompanied S. Aulian patrol, 1917-18; polit. offr., Turkan, A.G.S. med. and clasp.; dist. commr., Jul., 1922; dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jul., 1922.

COOKE, WILLIAM ERNEST, M.A., F.R.S. Cadet, Adelaide observatory, 1878; B.A. Univ., 1883; ast. astronomer, Adel. observ., M.A., Adel. Univ., 1889; govt. astronomer, W. 1896; govt. astronomer, New South W., 1912-26; prof. of astronomy at Sydney Univ., 1926.

COOLS, LARTIGUE, ALEXANDER RAPHAEL B. 1899; ed. at St. Mary's Coll., St. Lucia Dominica Grammar Schl.; called to bar, C. Inn, 1924; admitted to bar, Leeward Is., practised at bar, 1924-35; ag. crown atty. mag., dist. "F", and mem. exec. and leg. Dominics, Oct. 1931 to Jan. 1932; ag. crown Dominics, and mem. exec. and leg. couns., 1933 to Feb., 1934, and from July, 1934 to 1935; visiting just. of prisons and J.P. crown atty., and mag., dist. "E", and mem. exec. and leg. couns., Dominica, Jan., 1938; comsnr., sup. ct., Leeward Is., June, 1936.

COOLS-LARTIGUE, LOUIS.—B. 1905; ed. Dominica Grammar Sch.; asst. mast., Dominica Grammar Schl., Mar., 1923-Aug., 1924; 2nd clk., adm'r's. office, Dec., 1924; junr. clk., do., Aug., 1927; senr. clk., reg'rs. office, Apr., 1929; prin., adm'r's. office as ch. clk. and clk. of couns., etc., June, 1932.

COOLS-LARTIGUE, THEODORE.—B. 1867; ed. at Mount Saint Mary's Coll., Derbyshire; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1889; admitted to the bar, St. Lucia, 1889; ditto, Leeward Is., 1894; ag. registr., St. Lucia, 1891; ag. mag. in 1903-4-5; ag. registr., St. Lucia, 1906-7; ag. mag., 1911; elective mem., Castries town bd., 1903-1912; reg'rs. and prov. marshal, Dominica, and also supplementary mag., Roseau, Dominica, Feb., 1912; offl. mem. leg. coun., Dominica, visiting just. of Dominica prison and J.P., 1912; govt. mem.—Roseau town bd., 1914; valuation comsnr. for assessing of taxes, 1916; ag. crown atty. and mem. exec. coun., 1916-19; crown atty. and registr., sup. ct., Oct., 1919; ret., 1929; re-apptd. ag. addnl. mag., dist. E, 1930.

COOMARASWAMY, CHINNAPPAH.—B. 1887; ed. Hindu Coll., Jaffna, and Royal Coll., Colombo; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., April, 1910; extra office asst., Batticaloa kach., May, 1912; pol. mag., Matare, May, 1913; do., Puttalam, June, 1913; do., Chilaw, Feb., 1914; do., Point Pedro, June, 1915; ag. dist. judge, Batticaloa, Feb., 1918; ditto, Chilaw and Puttalam, Mar., 1920; addnl. astt. gov't. agt., Chilaw and Puttalam, in addn., Aug., 1920; comsnr. of requests, Colombo, May, 1922; dep. comsnr., stamps, May, 1925; dist. judge, Kalutara, Apr., 1926; dep. comsnr., stamps, May, 1926; registr., motor cars, Jan., 1928; ag. registr.-gen., Sept., 1931; dist. judge, Kurunegala, June, 1932; do., Jaffna, Oct., 1933.

COOMARASWAMY, VALUPILLAI.—B. 1892; Cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1913; ag. addtnl. office asst., Anuradhapura Kach., Nov., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Puttalam, Feb., 1914; pol. mag., Negombo, July, 1917; ditto, Dandagamuwa, July, 1918; office astt. to gov't. agt., S. Prov., Feb., 1919; pol. mag., Panadura, Oct., 1920; pol. mag., W. P., July, 1923; dist. judge, Kegalla, June, 1924; astt. gov't. agt., Hambantota, July, 1927; astt. gov't. agt., Puttalam and Chilaw, Jan., 1931; clk., state coun. and sec. bd. of ministers, July, 1932; ag. gov't. agt. E. Prov., June, 1933.

COOMBES, H. P.—B. 1894; clk., gov't. sec's. office, Nyasaland, May, 1917; senr. clk., Aug., 1923; ag. astt. sec. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Dec., 1926 to Mar., 1927; ag. senr. astt. sec. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Apr. to Dec., 1927; ag. office supt., secretariat and clk., exec. and leg. couns., in 1929 and 1930; office supt., secretariat, Apr., 1931; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Apr., 1931 to Dec., 1933.

COOPE, ARTHUR EGERTON.—B. 1888; ed. Fonthill Schl., St. Edward's Schl., and Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A. Oxon.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; passed cadet, June, 1913; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1914; astt. dist. offr., May, 1915; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; astt. dist. offr., Apr., 1919; dist. offr., Mar., 1921; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1923; offr. astt. advr., lands, Kedah, May, 1924; town planning adm'r. and state valuer, Ipoh, Oct., 1928; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928; ag. astt. dist. offr., Ipoh, in addn., Apr. to May, 1929; ag. mag., Seremban, Sept., 1929; ag. chmn., sany. bd. in addn., astt. astt. res., Berbera, July, 1898; vice-consul, adviser, Muar, Johore, Mar., 1930; mag., K. Lumpur, July, 1931; offr., cls. I B, Nov., 1931; ag. legal advr. and judl. comsnr., Kelantan, Mar., 1933; comsnr., trade and cust., Johore, Dec., 1934; contr., rubber, Malays., Mar., 1936.

COOPKR, BERTRAND, M.N.Z.I.S.—B. 1882; mounted inf'y., S. Africa, 1901-02; licensed survr., N.Z., 1906-07; survr., Pahang, Apr., 1921; astt. supt., rev. surveys, F.M.S., Jan., 1926; astt. supt. (field), Kinta, Aug., 1929; ag. sr. astt. supt., rev. surveys, Kinta., June, 1930 to Feb., 1931; astt. supt., surveys, Malacca, May, 1933; do., Kedah, Mar., 1934; seconded as astt. contr. rubber, June, 1934.

COOPER, EVANS GUSTAVUS.—B. 1875; astt. clk. of courts, Jamaica, 1893; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1912; clerk of cts., Hanover, Dec., 1912; J.P., 1913; ag. res. mag. on various occasions; supernmy. res. mag., June, 1923; res. mag., June, 1924.

COOPER, COMDR. GUY EDWARD, R.N. (ret.),—B. 1891; ed. R.N.; Great War, ment. in desps.; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1923; legal adviser, Seychelles, Apr., 1928; ag. ch. just., May to Nov., 1928 and Nov., 1930 to May, 1932; pol. mag., Gold Coast, Nov., 1932; attd. legal advy. staff, C.O., July-Aug., 1936.

COOPER, WILLIAM GERALD GROVES, Ph.D. (Lond.), B.Sc. (Lond.), A.R.C. Sc., F.G.S., A.Inst. M.M.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Schl., Crediton, Devon; Battersea Polytechnic Inst. and Imp. Coll. of Sc. and Technology, 1911-14; res. engrn. on location and constrn. of Canada Northern Ontario Rly., 1911-14; war serv., 1914-18; geologist, Gold Coast geol. survey, Nov., 1919.

CORAY, EDMUND JOSEPH, B.A., LL.B. (Lond.), Barrister-at-Law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1907; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1931; attd. Colombo kach., Nov., 1931; office astt., Ratnapura kach., Mar., 1934; do., Maunar and Mullaitivu kach., Mar., 1935; do., Kegalla kach., Nov., 1935.

CORBETT, ALAN FREDERICK.—B. 1878; ed. K.H.S., Coventry, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1902; barrister-at-law (Inner Temple), 1903; advoo., sup. ct., Cape Colony, 1904; sup. ct. (T.P.D.) Union of S.A., 1919; clk., res. mag., Worcester, July, 1903; clk., mast. sup. ct., Cape, Jan., 1905; clk., res. mag., Beaufort West, Dec., 1905; clk., civil. comsnr., Kimberley, May, 1906; clk., inland rev. dept., Apr., 1911; ag. recr. of rev., Germiston, Oct., 1915; senr. clk., inland rev. dept., Nov., 1916; prin. clk., inland rev. dept., Jan., 1921; astt. comsnr. for inland rev., Apr., 1929; comsnr. for inland rev., Sept., 1929.

CORBETT, GEORGE HAMBLIN, B.Sc., F.R.E.S.—B. 1887; ed. King Edwards Schl., Bath, and Edin., Cornell and Harvard Univs.; govt. entomologist, F.M.S., Apr., 1920; deleg. of F.M.S. and S.S. govts. at 2nd Imp. Entomological Conf., London, June, 1925; constg. entomologist, Inst. Med. Resch., and Rubber Resch. Inst., in addn., June, 1930-Aug., 1932 and Mar., 1935.

CORDEAUX, MAJOR SIR HARRY EDWARD SPILLER, K.C.M.G. (1921), C.B. (1904), C.M.G. (1902).—Ed. at Cheltenham Coll., and St. John's Coll., Camb.; minor scholarship, 1889; class. tripods, 1892; passed in Hindustani and Persian, 1895; Somali, 1899; B.A., 1892; entd. Indian Army, 1894; probationer, Bombay polit. dept. as 4th astt. polit. res., Aden, Apr., 1898; vice-consul, Berbera, Oct., 1898; consul, Nov., 1902; admstg. govt. of Somaliland Prot., in 1900, 1902, 1903 and 1904; served in operations against the Somali Mullah 1902-04, as polit. offr. (ment. in desps., medal and 3 clasps); dep. comsnr., Somaliland Prot., 1904; admstg. govt., June, 1905; comsnr. and comdr.-in-chief, 15th May, 1906; gov., Uganda, 1st Feb., 1910; gov., St. Helena, 22nd Nov., 1911; assumed govt., 21st Feb., 1912; gov., Bahamas, 1920.

CORDOAN, JEAN ALBERT.—B. 1887; olk., cust., Durban, 1904; Pretoria, 1911; 1st grade clk., 1916; senr. clk., 1919; prin. clk., 1921; acct., 1922; ch. clk., cust. and excise, Pretoria, 1930; ch. inp., cust. and excise, 1933; dep. comsnr., 1933.

COREA, CARLTON VICTOR DEMETRIUS SENEWIRATNE.—B. 1903; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; attd., Matare kach., Mar., 1927; ag. office a-st., Kegalle kach., Nov., 1928; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Mar., 1930; office a-st., Galle kach., Nov., 1931; asst. govt. agt., Badulla, June, 1933; asst. settlmg. offr., Dec., 1934.
CORNELL, CHARLES ALBERT.—Called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1928; asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, Feb., 1921; ag. dist. comsnr., 1925; dist. comsnr., 1927.

CORNEY, LEONARD GEORGE.—B. 1886; B.A. Cantab., class. exhibtn., St. John's Coll., Camb.; class. tripes., 1908; examn., col. audit branch of E. and A. Dept., Feb., 1910; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1910; ag. audr., July, 1913, July to Dec., 1914, and Apr. to Oct., 1916; ag. asst. col. sec., Dec., 1916 to Jan., 1917; seconded to G. Coast Regt., Apl., 1917 to Jan., 1918; senr. asst. audr., Jan., 1919; ag. audr., Jan. to Oct., 1919; dep. audr., Jan., 1920; asst. dir. cent. office (Col. Audit Dept.), Feb., 1930; dep. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1931; ag. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1932; audr., do., 1936.

CORNISH, HENRY HAVELOCK, K.C., M.A. (double 1-t cls., hons.), LL.M.—B. 1887; ed. Kaitangata Sch., Otago Boys' High Sch., Otago Univ. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; pte. legal practice many years; pres., Wellington dist. law soc'y., 1926; mem., N.Z. law soc'y.; coun. of law reporting, rules comm., etc.; prof., English and N.Z. law, Victoria Univ. Coll., 1930-34; solr.-gen., N.Z., 1934.

CORNWALLIS, COL. SIR K., K.B.E. (1929), C.M.G. (1926), C.B.E., Mily. (1919), D.S.O. (1917), Commander of the Order of King George I. of Greece, Order of Nahdhadh (2nd cls.), Order of the Nile (3rd cls.), Order of Majidje (4th cls.), three times mentioned in desps.; Sudan civ. serv., 1906-14; Egyptian civ. serv., 1914; dir., Arab Bureau, Cairo, 1916-19; dep. ch. polit. offr., Egyptian Expdy. Force, 1918; asst. ch. polit. offr., ditto, 1919; attd. to Amir Faisal in London, 1919; attd. to F.O., 1920-21; accompanied Amir Faisal to 'Iraq and seconded to 'Iraq govt. for two years, June, 1921; adviser to min. of interior, 'Iraq, September, 1921.

CORKIE, OWEN CECIL KIRKPATRICK, M.C.—B. 1882; ed. Monckton Combe Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (maths. exhib.); on active serv. in France, Belgium and Palestine with N. Somerset Yeomanry and R.F.A. (T.F.), 1915-19; M.C. and ment. in desps.; judl. offr., Nablus, O.E.T.A. (S.), Mar., 1919; vice-pres., ct. of appeal, O.E.T.A. (S.), Oct., 1919; vice-pres., ct. of appeal, Palestine, July, 1920; senr. Br. judge, sup. ct., Sept., 1924; ag. ch. just. on various occasions, 1922-25; ch. just., Pi., 1925; judl. comsnr., W. Pacific, 1936.

CORRY, WILFRED CHARLES STEWART (Oxon).—B. 1900; ed. Cheltenham (co. Wadhams Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., 1923; attchd secr., Taiping, Jan., 1923; land office, J. Bahr, Apr., 1924; passed Jan., 1926; offr. vls., Dec., 1926; passed land rev., K. Lumpur, Mar., 1927; offr. vls., Dec., 1930; pr. sec. to govt. S.S. and comsnr., Malay States, July, 1931; offr. vls., Dec., 1935; ag. 1st asst. sec., S.S., July, 1936.

CORSER, LIEUT. E. E.—Ed. Shrewsbury. 2nd lieut., 4th Batt. King's Shropshire Light Infantry (T.), Aug., 1914; lieut., Jun., 1915; served in France, 1917-18; asst. dist. sec., G. Coast, 3rd Dec., 1919.
CORSON, JAMES FREDERICK, O.B.E., M.B.E., M.D., Ch.B. (Manch.), D.P.H. (Coast), Jan., 1913; pathologist, Dist. Nigeria, May, 1923; Tanganyika Territory.

COSTAR, NORMAN EDGAR.—B. 1899; Battersea Grammar Sch., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; hist. schol., 1928-32; cl. I div. tripes., pts. I and II; B.A., 1931; Kellems man, 1931; apptd. after compet. exam., prin., C.O., Apr., 1932; pte. sec. to gen. under sec., D.O. (Sir Ed. Harding), 21st Oct., 1935.

COTCHING, HOUGHTON ALGERNON, A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1887; serv. in mech. engr., N.E. Ry. Coy., 1903-15; on mil. ser., seconded for duty with R.E. (landward and docks), 1917; major, D.A.D.R.T. and Chemin de Fer du Hedjaz as loco. sup., ch. mech. engr., Palestine rly., 1920.

COULSON, NORMAN.—B. 1891; ed. Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; (M.A. in cls. and mod. hist.); 2nd lieut., 10th Res. Cav., Sept., 1914; attd. 8th (K.R.I.) Hussars, Sept., 1915; (France and army of occupation) cadet F.M.S., Apr., 1920; passed cadet, 1922; attd. to treas., Penang on sup. duties, 1922; dist. offr., Dindings, Apr., 1923; offr., Raub, Feb., 1925; seconded for ser., Kelantan, Aug., 1926; offr., ola. III., Feb., 1928; supt., lands, Kelantan, Apr., 1928; ser., Seremban, Mar., 1930; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, June, 1931; offr., cls. II, Feb., 1933; sup., Penang, Apr., 1934.

COURT, PHILIP FRANCIS SHIELDSLEY, F.R.C.S. (Ed.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (London), 1900; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong; med. offr. in ch., Kowloon and New Territories, 1930; ditto, Victoria Hosp., Mar., 1932; med. offr. in ch., Kowloon Hosp., Mar., 1933; do., Victoria hosp., Feb., 1936.

COUTTS, JAMES MAGNUS.—B. 1896; ed. Heriot's Sch., Schol. of Medicine and Univ., Edinburgh; L.D.S., R.C.S. (Edin.), 1924; served in war with Cameron Highlanders, 1915-19; M.M., 1916; dental surg., Falkland Is., Oct., 1925; loc. audr., Apr., 1926-Apr., 1927; lieut., Falkland Is., Def. Force, 1927; capt., 1929; ag. O.C. No. to Nov., 1929; 2nd in comm., 1927-31; dental offr., Straits Settlements, Aug., 1931; dent. offr., S.S. and dent. surg., S'pore, Jan., 1934; dent. hd., S.S. in addn., Feb., 1934.

COWAN, JOHN ARCHER, M.B., B.S. (London), D.T.M. (Liverpool).—B. 1890; bacteriologist, Stiltsm., Apr., 1925; govt. pathologist, Penang, Aug., 1926; ag. govt. pathologist, Coll. of Medicine, S'pore, Mar.-Aug., 1935.
COWELL, HUBERT RUSSELL, B.A., C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1877; ed. Malvern and Cambridge.

COX, LIONEL, A.S.A.A.—B. 1890; ed. St. Stephens Gram. Sch., Dublin; acctnt., govt. opium shops, Spore, Feb., 1926; offr. i.c., govt. opium shops, Spore, Jan., 1934; acctnt., med. dept., S.S., Sept., 1935.
