COX, MAJOR-GEN. SIR PERCY ZACHARIAH, G.C.M.G. (1922), G.C.I.E. (1917), K.C.S.I. (1915), K.C.M.G. (1920), K.C.I.E. (1911), C.S.I. (1909), C.I.E. (1902), F.R.G.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1864; ed. Harrow and Sandhurst; joined army 1884; served with 2nd batt. Cameronians till 1889; joined Indian Staff Corpsa, 1889; Indian polit. dep., 1890; vice-consul, Zaila, Somali Coast, 1893; Berbera, 1894-5; consul and polit. agt., Muscat, 1899-1904; consul-gen., Bushire, 1904; polit. res., Persian Gulf, 1909; sec., foreign dept., govt. of India, 1914; ch. polit. offr., Indian Expedy. Force "D," 1914; ment. in desps., 1916; ag. British min. to Persia, 1919-20; high commnr., Mesopotamia, 1920, ret., 1923.

CRABB, RICHARD BRUCE—B.1891; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; asst dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1915; served European War, East Surrey Regt., 1916-18; dist. commnr., 1922; asst. col. sec., 1923; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1926; ag. clk., couns. in 1927 and 1928; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., Apr. to Sept., 1932; senr. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1932.
CRAGG, WILLIAM GILLIAT, D.S.O. (1918), Major, R. of O. Loyal Regt.—B. 1883; ed. Shrewsbury and R.M.C., Sandhurst; asst. dist. offr., N. Nigeria, Aug., 1920.
CRAIG, ERNEST DUNCAN, M.A., B.Sc., F.Z.S.—B. 1888; ed. Aberdeen Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. instr., educn., N. Provs., 1936.

CRAIG, GEORGE KENNETT, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dublin).—B. 1886; ed. Foyle Coll., Londonderry and Trinity Coll., Dublin; M.O., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1911; apptd. to W.A.M.S. (Gambia), Sept., 1920; transf. to Nigeria, Feb., 1929; S.M.O., Aug., 1929; A.D.M.S., June, 1936.

CRAIG, JOHN.—B. 1884; ed. at Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., lst cl. hona., Classics; univ. prize in Latin, 1906; and at Christ Church, Oxford; B.A., 2nd cl. hons., lit. hum., 1908; after exam., examr., E. and A. dept., Jan., 1909; asst. auditor, S. Nigeria, May, 1909; temporarily transf. to N. Nigeria, Aug., 1909; joined col. audit dept., Jan., 1910; auditor, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1912; priv. sec. to gov., Sir W. Collett, K.C.M.G., Nov., 1914; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to ag. gov. R. Walter, C.M.G., gov. W.Hart-Bennett, C.M.G., and gov. Eyre-Hutson, C.M.G., 1915-19; lieut., B. Honduras Territorial Force; audr., Mauritius, Apr., 1920; col. audit dept., 1st Apr., 1924; dep. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1926; ag. audr., Mar.-Nov., 1927; treas., Fiji, Apr., 1930; commnr., inland rev. and stamp duties, Nov., 1930; chmn., economy comtee., 1931; chmn., tariff revision comtee., 1932.

CRAIG, RUPERT BERCHMANS, A.M.Inst.C.E., M.R.San.I.—B. 1897; apprentice, P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1913; served with H.M. Forces, 1915-19; ment. in desps.; ag. astt. engr., and ch. draughtsman, Oct., 1921-July, 1922; astt. draughtsman, P.W.D., Jan., 1924; seconded to consultants, Georgetown improvement schemes, June-July, 1923; seconded to harbour bd., as engr.-in-ch., Berbice River training wks., Jan. to Oct., 1925; astt. engr., P.W.D., 1926; dist. engr., P.W.D., 1929.

CRAIGIE-HALKETT, MONTAGU C., O.B.E. (1934).—Ag. priv. sec. to gov. Falkland Islds., Mar., 1897; writer, col. sec.'s off., Apr., 1897; priv. sec. to admrs., Apr., 1897; clk., col. sec.'s off., Oct., 1897; treasy. clk., 1899; J.P., Nov., 1909; postmtr., man., savings bank, regiar-gen., and regir. of sup. ct., and offl. admtr., 1911, temp. mem., leg. coun., 1911 and 1914; supt. census, 1911 and 1921; local audtr., 1916-26; regir-gen. and mag., 1926; temp. mem., exec. coun., 1923; treas. and colir., cust., Aug., 1926; has acted as mag. and regir., sup. ct., and as col. postmtr. on various occasions; ag. col. sec., May-July, 1933 and Feb.-Sept., 1934; ag. gov., July, 1933 to Feb., 1934; dep. gov. in 1932, 1933, 1934, 1935 and 1936; ag. gov., Sept., 1934 to Feb., 1935; col. sec., Jan., 1935.

CRAN, CAPT. PETTER McLellan, O.B.E., B.Sc., M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1886; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; 2nd lieut. R.E. (S.R.), 1911; astt. engr., govt. ryls., N. Nigeria, 1911; dist. engr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1912; astt. offr., R.E., Forth and Clyde Defences, Scottish Comd., Mar., 1914; div. offr., do., Oct., 1914; astt. to comding. royal engr., do., Nov., 1916; ment. in desps., Feb., 1917 and Aug., 1919; exec. engr., P.W.D., Sierra Leone, 1928; temp. astt. engr., P.W.D., Cyprus, Jan., 1931; divnl. engr., Mar., 1931.

CRANE, ALFRED VICTOR, LL.B. (Lond.), Hons.—B. 1892; solr., sup. ct., judic. Eng. and Br. Guiana; el. mem. Br. Guiana legislature, 1926-33; dep. mayor, Georgetown, 1923; mem. several select and other govt. comites.; mem. Georgetown sewage and water bd.; mem. bd. of govs., Queen's Coll.; sen. mag., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1933; called to bar (Inner Temple) 1935; author of "Law of Unlawful Possession," "Law of Workmen's Compensation."

CRANSTON, T. D.—M.A. (Glasgow); astt. mast., training instn. for teachers, Accra, G. Coast, Apr., 1912; astt. censor, in addn., Mar., 1915 to Jan., 1916; ag. qrtmr., Gold Coast Volunteers, Nov., 1916; lieut., ditto, Apr., 1917; ag. prin., Accra Training Coll. on several occasions between 1917 and 1925; ag. prov. inspr., schls., Apr., 1926; astt. dir., educ., Nov., 1926; ag. dir., educ., in 1930, 1932, 1933, 1934, and 1936.

CRAREY, WILLIAM RAYMOND DUNSTAN, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1900; ed. Gresham's Schl., Holt and London Univ.; astt. engr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, Apr., 1926; ag. dist. engr., in 1930, 1931, 1933 and 1934; astt. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1935.

CRAWFORD, JOHN GERALD.—B. 1894; ed. King's Schl., Chester and Merton Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1920; lieut., Cheshire Regt., T.F., Nov., 1915; capt., June, 1916; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; astt. to dist. office, K. Selangor, Apr., 1921; astt. to office of conserv., forests, Jan., 1922; passed cadet, Feb., 1923; supernw. offr., cls. V, Feb., 1922; astt. dist. offic., B. Padang, Nov., 1923; ditto, Kroh, May, 1924; offr., cls. IV, Feb., 1926; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, June, 1927; astt. to co-op. socs. dept., Oct., 1928; ols., III, Nov., 1931; astt. dist. co-op., Ap., 1932.
CRAWFORD, MATTHEW, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., 1901; astt. vety. surg., Ceylon, 1874; Imp. Agr. Research Conf., as cls., 1927; govt. vety. surg., Nov., 1931.

CRAWSHAW, EDWARD DANIEL W., B. 1903; ed. St. Bees and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1926; admitted solic., N. Rhodesia, 1930; astt. admin. gen. of Uganda Territory, 1933; ag. res. mag., Tanga, Nov., 1935.
CREAN, B. A.—Barrister-at-law, of titles, Kenya, Oct., 1920; res. mag. at puane judge, sup. ct., Cyprus, Apr. to prez., dist. ct., June to Sept., 1930; ag. ch. just., Br. Guiana, Apr., 1934.
CREASE, LEONARD GEORGE, M.A., M.R.S.T.—B. 1891; ed. Oxford Rugbyschol., Oxford; educn. appts. in 1908-26; war serv., 1915-17; Ceylon schls., Br. Guiana, 1931; ag. dir., educious occasions.

CREASY, GERALD HALLEN, B.A., Rugby schol., King's Coll., Camb., 1916; with R.G.A., 1916-19; astt. prn. c., July, 1920; pvt. sec. to parl. under Dominion affrs., 1925-27; dep. sec., July, 1927; prim., 1927; accom. partly, I.N. of theCols. (Mr. Ormsby-Gore) to Ceylon and Java, 1928; sec., Col. Agr. Com., and of confce. of dirs. of agr., 1930; partly, U.S. of S. for the Cols. (Lord I.) to W. Africa, 1935.
CREED, HENRY.—B. 1871; ag. exam., 3rd class messenger in C.O., 1904; 2nd class, 1st Feb., 1904; King's med., Jan., 1911; office keeper, 1924; med. Br. Empire, 1931.

CREMER, ARTHUR STANLEY.—B. 1894; Durham schl.; col. audit dept., 23rd Jan., 1913; astt. audr., G. Coast, 1913-13; astt. Nyassaland, 1913-16; astt. audr., Uganda, 1916-19; astt. audr., Tanganyika, Oct., 1919; andr., Mauritius, July, 1924; Gold Coast, Jan., 1933.

CRERAR, HON. THOMAS ALEXANDER, pub. schls. and Collegiate, Portage la Prairie, Manitoba; teacher, farmer, grain elevator; invited to enter Union govt., 1917; Canada, 1917; min. of agr., 1917-19; Marquette, Manitoba, 1917-25; leader, progressive party, 1921-22; M.P., Brandon, 1921-22; min. of ryls. and canals, 1929-30; defeated, re-el., g.e. 1935 as M.P. for Churchill, mines, min. of immigr. and colonization, of interior, and sup. gen., Indian affairs Mackenzie King cabinet, Oct., 1935.

CRESSALL, PAUL EWART FRANCIS, M.B., B.Ch., ed. Cranleigh Schl.; ent. cir. Br. Guiana, 1911; cler. astt., secretariat, inspr., B. Guiana pol., 1912; ag. dist. inspr., 1914-15; 2nd lieut., B. Guiana Militia, 1914; served in B.W.I. Regt. as capt. and Palestine, 1915-19; dist. inspr., B. Guiana pol., 1920; J.P. and coroner, 1920; as inspr., 1920; passed final law exam., 1921; B. Guiana local forces, 1922; ag. astt. to Guiana local forces, 1922; called to Gray's Inn, 1923; county inspr., pol. consnr., for oaths and revising barrack stip. mag., Apr., 1924; ag. stip. mag., town, Aug., 1924 to Dec., 1924 and Dec., 1925; neeming mag., 1926; dist. c., June, ing pre-CR.

SWELL, COL THE HON. FREDERIC HUGH D.S.O.—B. 1866; ed. Bruce Castle, Derby and R. Sch. of Mines (grad. 1st cls.); arrived Africa, May, 1893; mining in Rhodesia, 1893- Durban Roodepoort Deep Mine, 1895ved in Imp. Light Horse during Anglo-Boer Village Main Reef, 1902-03; passed exam. on expulsion of Chinese labour; electo tion parl., 1910, and led Labour Party; 2nd md., Rand Rifles, S.W. Africa, 1914; 2nd md., subsequently commdg., 8th S.A. Infy. German E. Africa campaign; mem for min. of defence and labour, 1st and 2nd miny., June, 1924 to Mar., 1933; M.P. Iville, May, 1933.

SWE, 1ST MARQUESS OF (cr. 1911); BARO F.S.A.—B. 1858; Baron Houghton of Houghton in the county of York, 1863; Earl swe (cr. 1895); Earl of Madeley (cr. 1911); Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; asst. priv. sec. for foreign affairs (Earl Granville), lord-in-waiting to Queen Victoria, 1886; lt.-jnt. of Ireland, 1892-95; lord pres. of the 1905-08; S. of S. for the Colonies, Apr., Lord Privy Seal (in conjunction with taryship of State), 1908; S. of S. for India, accompanied H.M. King George V, on occasion of the Royal visit to India, 1911; pres. of the coun., 1915.
ICHLLOW, NATHANIEL.—M.B., Ch.B.; offr., Solomon Islands, Oct., 1914; ag. senr. offr. on several occasions; travelling med. July, 1927.
LOCKER, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1890; ed in R.A.S.C. (M.T.) in Mesopotamia, and India; asst. govt. printer, Gold Coast; ag. govt. printer, 1925, 1927, 1928, 1930; supt., govt. press, 1928.

ROCKETT, HON. OSWALD SMITH, LL.D.— 868; ed. pub. and high schls., Fredericton, Canada, and Univ. of New Brunswick; 1886; atty., sup. ct., N.B., 1891; called to 1892; M.P. (Can.) for York County, N.B., judge, king's bench div., sup. ct., N.B., judge, sup. ct., Canada, 1932.

RONKEN, BRIAN.—Ed. Dulwich Coll.; ed B.S.A. pol. and Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 2nd grade clk., 1921; ag. sub-inspr., 1922; asst. res. mag., 1926; passed 2nd grade wana exam., 1923; ag. res. mag., 1927; 1st de clk., Apr., 1928; sub-inspr., Bechuanaland pol., 1929.

CROOK, WALTER VERNON.—B. 1900; ed. Berlborough Coll., and R. Mily. Coll., Sandhurst; insd. Reg. Army, East Surrey Regt., Dec., 1918; ved N. Russia, 1919; seconded under C.O., Mar., cadet, Uganda, 1920; asst. dist. offr., Uganda, June, 1928, and placed on Reg. Army, of O.

CROSBIE, HON. SIR JOHN C., K.B.E. (1919). M.H.A., Bay de Verde dist., Newfoundland, 1908; M.E.C., 1909; mem. defence comtec., 1915; rtn., tonnage comtec., 1917; min. of militia, 1917; min. of shipping, 1918.

CROSBIE, ROBERT EDWARD HAROLD, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1886; ed. Cheltenham and Penbroke Coll., Cambridge (schol.); B.A. (class. tripos, hon. cls. II), 1908; Egyptian civ. serv., 1910-19; seconded for mily. serv. with E.E.F., 1915; apptd. to O.E.T.A. (S.), 1918; dep. mily. gov., Brisban, 1919; dep. mily. gov., Tiberias, 1920; dep. dist. gov., Jenin, 1920; asst. gov. (continuing as asst. dist. comsnr.), S. dist., 1922; passed advanced Arabic exam., 1924; dist. comanr., Southern dist., 1931.

CROSS, GRAHAM.—Ck., native ct., Johannesburg, May, 1902; asst. res. mag. and asst. mag., native ct., Witwatersrand dist., Aug., 1908; mag., Nov., 1915; under sec. for lands, Sept., 1923; sec. for lands, Aug., 1932; chmn., pub. serv. comsn., Jan., 1934.

CROSS, MATTHEW ARNOLD.—B. 1881; ck., deeds, Transvaal, Mar., 1902; examnr., deeds, July, 1904; 2nd grade examnr., deeds, Pretoria, Oct., 1919; 1st gr. ditto, Nov., 1919; ch. examnr., July, 1922; asst. regisr., deeds, Bloemfontein, Nov., 1927; ditto, Pretoria, Oct., 1928; regisr., deeds, Bloemfontein, Apr., 1933; do., Pretoria, Feb., 1934.

CROSSE-CROSSE, WILLIAM WICKHAM RED- DER.—B. 1899; ed. Blundells schl.; 2nd lieut., Welsh Guards, 1918; lt., 1920; served with 1st and 2nd K.A.R., in Tanganyika Territory, 1922- 24; R. of O., (Welsh Guards), 1924; cadet, (Ganda Prot.), 1924; asst. dist. offr., 1926; asb. lab. comsnr., 1929; temp. asst. prin. C.O., 1930-32; dist. offr., 1936.
CROUCHER, HERBERT HARRY, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.I.C.—B. 1904; ed. Reading Univ.; lect in chemy. and soil sci., Impl. Coll. of Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1926; agrl. chemist, Jamaica, 1931.

CRUICKSHANK, ARTHUR HOWARD, M.I.Mar. E.—B.1888; jnr. mech. engnr. and river survr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. mech. engnr., 1921; transfld. as engnr., transport offr. to motor transport dept., Aug., 1922; ag. ch. mech. transport offr., 1924; ag. ch. transport offr. for various periods, 1926-34.

CRUIKSHANK, JOHN MERRILL, M.D., C.M., McGill Univ. Canada 1925, Licentiate Med. Coun. of Canada, 1925.—Med. dir., Alexander sanatorium (mental diseases) San Francisco, 1927- 28; med. offr., Bahamas, 1928; res. surg., 1929; ag. dir., med. services in 1930; ch. med. offr. and res. surg., 1931.

CRUTCHLEY, ERNEST TRISTRAM, C.B. (1935), C.M.G. (1932), C.B.E. (civ.), 1926, O.B.E. (mily.), 1918.—B. 1878; lieut.-col., R.E., T.F. Res.; asst. survr., G.P.O., 1908; dep. dir., Army Postal serv. (T.F.), 1914-19; staff offr., grade I, mily. of transp., 1919; ag. prin., ch. sec.'s office, Dublin, 1921; Irish office and mem., Irish grants comte., 1922; C.O., 1923; ag. finance offr., overseas settlement dept., 1925; Br. govt. rep. for migrn. in the C'wealth. of Australia, 1928; rep. in the C'wealth. of Australia of H.M'a. Govt. in the U.K., 1931.

CRUX, RICHARD MILLETT.—B. 1880; ed. in India; dept. of Transvaal prisons, dir., prisons office, Jan., 1904; asst. warden, Houtpoort Reformatory, Apr., 1916; prison supt., 1923; supt., prisons, Pretoria commd., 1924; war serv., Anglo-Boer War, 1900-02; Great War, G.E.A.,
CRYAN, ROBERT.—B. 1889; asst. elec. engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924; elec. engnr., 1934; ag. ch. do., 1928, 1931, 1935.

CUBITT, MAJOR-GEN. SIR THOMAS ASTBY, K.C.B. (1931), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O. (1903)—B. 1871; ed. Haileybury and R.M.C., Woolwich; ent. army, 1891; capt., 1900; served W. Africa, 1898 (med. and chap.); staff offr., W. Africa (despa., brevet-major, med. with clasp) 1901; W. Africa, (despa., clasp), 1902; Kanokokoto campaign, 1903 (despa., clasp, D.S.O.); European war in commd., troops, Somaliland, 1914-15; in commd., divn., France, with rank of major-gen., 1918; G.O.C., Presidency and Assam dist., 1920-24; 53rd Welsh Divn., T.A., Shrewsbury, 1927; G.O.C., 2nd Divn., 1928; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Bermuda, 1931.

CUFF, CYRIL CHARLES HENRIETT, O.B.E. (1936). M.B., B.S. (Durham), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1888; ed. Guy's and Durham; lieut., R.N.V.R., 1914; passed Ed. of Trade exam., for master, 1916; transfd. to R.A.M.C., 1918; house surg. and bacteriologist, St. Lucia, 1921-23; resigned; re-en. col. serv. and apptd. to Seychelles as sen. asst. med. olfr., res. surg. and visiting mag., outlying islds., 1924; ag. ch. med. olfr., June to Nov., 1924; dist. med. olfr., Cyprus, Apr., 1926; surg. specialist, Dec., 1928; ag. dir., health on various occasions in 1928 and 1932-33; ag. senr. med. olfr., 1934.
CULLEN, JAMES REYNOLDS, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1901; ed. Tonbridge and Balliol Coll., Oxford; Hertford schol., 1919; 1st hona. mods., 1920; 2nd lit. hum., 1922; dir., educ., Cyprus, Sept., 1930.

CULLEN, HON. SIR WM. PORTER, K.C.M.G. (1912), KT. BACH. (1911), K.C., M.A., LL.D.—Barrister-at-law; M.L.A., New South Wales, July, 1891; M.L.C., Aug., 1895; fellow of Senate of Sydney Univ. since 1896; vice-chancellor, 1908; resigned from legis. coun. and apptd. chief just. of N.S.W., Jan., 1910; lieut. gov., N.S.W., 30th Mar., 1910; ret., 1925; chancellor of Sydney Univ. from Sept., 1914.

CUMARASWAMY, SUBRAMANIANI DAVIS.—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1929; attd., Badulla kach, Feb., 1929; attd. Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1930; office asst. to asst. gov't. agt., Matale, Mar., 1930; attd., Kandy kach., Sept., 1933; office asst., Kandy kach., Feb., 1934.
CUMMINGS, D. J.—Many years serv., N.Z. pol. force; apptd. consmr., pol., 1936.

CUNLIFFE-LISTER, HON. SIR PHILIP, G.B.E. (1929), K.B.E. (1920), P.C. (1922), M.C., M.P. (Hendon).—B. 1884; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; called to bar, 1908; served in army, 1914-17; joint sec. of Miny. of National Serv., 1917-18; chmn., perm. lab. comte., of War Cabinet war priorities comte., 1918; mem., select comte., on nat. expend. and select comte., on high prices and profits, 1919; parly. sec. to B. of T., 1920-21; sec., D.O.T., 1921-22; chmn., Imp. Economic Conf., 1923; pres., B. of T., 1922-23, 1924-29 and Aug.-Nov., 1931; S. of S. for the Cols., 9th Nov., 1931; S. of S. for Air, 14th June, 1935.

CURLE, ALEXANDER TANCRED, M.B.E. (1936).—B. 1900; army, 1918-29; commr., grade III, Somaliland, 1929; ag. vice commr., Jigjiga, 1930; ag. commr., Harar, 1933; polt. olfr. and asst. commr., Br. Somaliland-Ethiopia bndry.commun., 1933-39; attd., Somaliland Camel Corps as polt. olfr., 1935-36.

CURLEWIS, JOHN STEPHEN, B.A., LL.B. (Cape).—Cape civ. serv., 1883-1888; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Colony, 12th Oct., 1887; advoc., high ct. (late S.A.R.), May, 1888; counsel for Imp. govt. in arbitn. with late S.A.R. re Asiatic question, 1895; ag. judge, high ct. (late S.A.R.), Apr., 1899 to Jan., 1900; pres. crim. ct. for Witwatersrand, Nov., 1899 to Jan., 1900; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, Sept., 1903; puisne judge, sup. ct., S. Africa (Transvaal Prov.), Div., May, 1910; judge pres., July, 1924; judge of appeal, 1927; O.A.G., Union of S. Africa during absence of Lord Clarendon, 1933.

CURMI, CAPT. HENRY C. O.B.E. (1937).—B. 1890; ed. Malta Lyceum, Univ. and pvt. tutors; matric. (Italian, French and Spanish), 1904; in place Malta civ. serv. exam., 1908; served in asst. lieut. gov't., treas., educ. and charitable institutions dept.; 2nd lieut., K.O. Malta Regt., 1914; mobilised 1914 and transfd. to R. Malta Art., 1915; with Med. Expedy. Force at Mudros at Anzac from Sept., 1915 to evacuation; mer. despa., Egyptian Expedy. Force, 1916-17; demob., Apr., 1920; served shipping office, sec., charitable institutions comte., 1921; sec. to min. for posts and agr. and in charge of bureau, 1921; sec., emigr. comte., 1922; sec. and offr.-in-charge, tourist comte., 1923-24; comte. for lab., Apr., 1928; comsmr. for lab. in Australia, 1929 and 1936.

CURRY, H. D.—B. 1883; ed. Dulwich; served in Fiji, 1911-15; ag. 2nd cls. cik., C.S.U., 1915; cadet, Solomon Islands, 1915; ag. asst. olfr., Gizo, 1915; on mily. serv., 1917-19; 2nd lieut., 1919; priv. sec. to gov. of E. Honora, 1919; resumed duty Solomon Is., 1920; ag. asst. olfr., Shortlands, 1920; ag. sec., 1920; ag. olfr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1923; sec. as lab. olfr., 1926-31; olfr. in ch., lab., N. prov., 1932; ag. dist. olfr., 1935.

CURRY, WILLIAM CLIVE, M.C.—B. 1895; City of London Schol.; mily. serv., 1916-18; M.T., 1917; called to bar, Middle Temple, May, 1917; asst. land olfr., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; asst. admistr.-gen., 1928; Br. mag., Palestine, 1932.
CURTIS, CECIL VIVIAN.—B. 1903; Wes. Guards, 1920-23; Br. Palestine gdsnt., 1923; Tanganyika Territory pol., 1926; staff, July, 1934-Jan., 1935; supt., pol., Zanzibar, 1935.

CURTIS, RICHARD JOHN FROUDE.—B. 1876; ed. Blundell's Schol. and R. Mily. Acad., Wich; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., 1917; lieut., 1920; of O., 1920; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; olfr., V. Nov., 1923; dist. olfr., N. Tebal, Dec., 1923; olfr., cts. IV., Nov., 1927; Br. res., Brunei, 1928; res., Labuan, Aug., 1929; A.D.C. to O.I., Apr., 1931; A.D.C. to gov., June, 1931; olfr., III., Nov., 1932; priv. sec. to gov., Dec., 1932; 2nd mag., Spore, Aug., 1933; 3rd mag., Sep., 1933; mag., Seremban, Nov., 1934; collr., rev., Penang, Apr., 1935.

CURTLER, ERNEST ALFRED, M.A. (Camb.), Camb. Univ. Dip. Agr.—B. 1895; ed. by Gram. Schol. and St. John's Schol., Camb.; agrclat., F.M.S., Nov., 1921; 2nd lieut., M.S.V., June, 1929; on sp. duty Ceylon and India, 1930; lieut., M.S.V.R., June, 1931; olfr. de intnl. dairy cong., Copenhagen, July, 1931; agrl. olfr., Cameron Highlands, Nov., 1931.

CUSACK, CAPT. HENRY VERNON (Res. Offrs.), F.R.G.S.—B. 1895; ed. Arvon and Royal Coll. of Sci., Ireland; served European War, Flanders and N. Russia, 1914-15; asst. audr., Sierra Leone, July, 1920; asst. Oct.-Nov., 1921; asst. audr., Nigeria, Oct., 1922; senr. asst. audr., Nyasaaland, May, 1928; audr., Oct., 1928 to Oct., 1929 and Nov., 1932; asst. dir., col. audit dept., 1933.
CUSACK, JOHN RALFE.—B. 1902; ed. R. Schol., Armagh; apptd. to col. audit dept. AUDR., NIGERIA, JUNE, 1929; AUDR., LEEWARD MAR., 1935.

USSEN, RICHARD CECIL.—B. 1887; B.A., LIN; BARRISTER-AT-LAW, GRAY'S INN; CADET, S.S., 1911; AG. DIST. OFFR., JELEBU, OCT., 1912; ED. CADET, JUNE, 1914; OFFR. CLS. V, DEC.; ASST. DIST. OFFR., B. PAHANG, FEB., 1915; OLS. IV, JAN., 1919; MAG., SEREMBAN, JULY; OFFR., CLS. III., D.P.P., PERAK, NOV., 1923; CLS. II., NOV., 1928; DEP. LEGAL ADV., F.M.S., CLS. II., APR., 1929; AG. LEGAL ADV., F.M.S., 1934; AG. JUDGE, F.M.S., FEB., 1935; JUDGE, S.S., 1936.

UTHBERT, MORTON.—ED. EDIN. INSTITUT. AND GOW AND WEST OF SCOT. TECH. COLL.; APPRENT. IR. TO MESSRS. DICK, KERR AND CO., LTD., LONDON, 1895; ELECTR. WITH GLASGOW CORP'TN. ELECT., 1899; ASST. ENGR., GOVERNMENT CORP'TN. ELECT., 1904; CHIEF ASST. ENGR., OBAN CORP'TN. ELECT. DEPT., 1904; ELECTR. ENGR., B. HONDURAS, 1905; EXEC. ELECTR. ENGR., P.W.D., B. HONDURAS, 1909; B.H.T.F., 1917; AG. UTTERIDGE, JOSEPH OLIVER, M.B.E. (1932), P., F.R.G.S.—B. 1887; ED. BOROUGH ROAD, ISLEWORTH, LONDON, 1906-08; TEACHER'S CERTIF. (4 DISTINCTIONS) BD. OF EDUC., LONDON; A.C.T., BD. OF EDUC., LONDON; STAFF-CAPT., H.M. SERVICES; INSPR. Q.M.G. SERVICES; MATHS AND DRAWMASTER, JUNIOR TECH. SCHL., LIVERPOOL, 1919; PRINC., TRAINING COLL., TRINIDAD, JAN., 1921; MEM. BR.-COL. UZEN, ALLAN LECKIE.—SERVED IN R.E., 1912; S. AFRICAN WAR (MEDAL AND FIVE CLASPS); POLICE, 1902-6; BECH. PROT. POLICE, 1906-14; PASSED CAPE CIV. SERV. LOWER LAW EXAM., 1914; CLK. TO ASST. COMSR., N. DIAT., BECH. PROT., 1914; AG. ASST. RES. MAG., DITTO, 1915; PASSED 2ND LEVEL SECAWANA EXAM., 1916; 1ST GRADE (B), DITTO, 1917; AG. RES. MAG., 1917; AG. ASST. RES. MAG., 1921.

ABNER, CHARLES J.—B. 1876; ON MILY., FRANCE AND FLANDERS, SEPT., 1914 TO MAR., 1919; CLK., ARMY PAY OFFICE, R. ENGLAND, MAR. TO 1919; CLK., ARMY PAY OFFICE, COMMAND MR., OVERSEAS BASES, OCT., 1919 TO JULY, 1922; OFFR., C.O., 10TH JULY, 1922.

AKERS, COLIN HUGH, M.C.—B. 1897; ED. TECH. GRAM. SCHL.; WAR SERV., 1915-19; CADET, I.S., NOV., 1920; OFFR., CLS. V, NOV., 1923; PR. LIQUOR SHOPS, F.M.S., SEPT., 1925; 2ND T. DIR., EDUC., (CHINESE), S.S., APR., 1926; CLS. IV, NOV., 1927; OFFR., CLS. III., NOV., 1928; ASST. DIR., EDUC., CHINESE SCHLS., F.M.S., CLS. I., 1932; AG. PROT., CHINESE, SELANGOR AND PENANG, APR., 1933; DO., PENANG, AUG., 1934; SELANGOR AND PENANG, MAY, 1935.

ALE, WILLIAM LEONARD.—B. 1906; ED. MERS COLL., HULL; LL.B., LONDON; HOLT. SCHOL., GRAY'S INN, 1929; CALLED TO BAR, 1932; N. EASTERN CUIT, N.R.Y. AND E.R.Y. SESSIONS; LEGAL ASST., O. AND C.O., FEB., 1935; AUTHOR OF "LAW OF THE PARISH CHURCH" (1933).

DALLARD, BERKELEY LIONEL.—B. 1889; RANGIORA HIGH SCHL. AND VICTORIA UNIV. COLL., Z.; ENT. N.Z. GOVT. SERV., 1907; STAMP DUTIES, COMSR., ON EDUC., QUEBEC, 1910; EL PRES., 6TH DEPT., 1907-13; AUDIT OFFICE, 1913-18; ADVISORY ACCTNT., BD. OF TRADE, 1919-23; INSPR., PUB. SERV. DALLEY, JOHN DOUGLAS.—B. 1900; POL. PROBR., F.M.S., NOV., 1920; PROBRY, A.C.P., JAN., 1922; ON DEPN. TO KEDAH; A.C.P., NOV., 1923; LAT. A.C.P., SELANGOR, DEC., 1924; C.P.O., JOHORE S., APR., 1926; A.C.P., K. KANGAR, JUNE, 1931; A.S.P., S'PORE, SEPT., 1932; COMDT., POL. DEPOT, NOV., 1933; DEP. COMSR., POL., F.M.S., AUG., 1934.

DALLIN, THOMAS.—LATE LIEUT. 3RD BATT. ESSEX REGT.; SERVED IN S. AFRICAN WAR (MEDAL AND TWO CLASPS); HON. 2ND LIEUT. IN THE ARMY, OCT., 1902; JOINED COL. AUDIT DEPT., FEB., 1904; EXAMR., EXCHEQUER AND AUDIT DEPT., JULY, 1906; AUDR., S. NIGERIA, OCT., 1906 TO FEB., 1910; REVERTED TO EX. AND AUDIT DEPT., FEB., 1910; ASST. AUDR., HONG KONG, JULY, 1910; AG. AUDR. IN 1911, 1912, 1913 AND 1914; ASST. CABLE CENSOR, AUG.-OCT., 1914; ON MIL. SERV., MAR., 1915 TO JULY, 1916; AG. AUDR., AUG., 1916 TO DEC., 1917; J.P., 1918; ATTD. TO FINAN. BRANCH, W.O., SEPT., 1918 TO MAR., 1919; AG. AUDR. ON VARIOUS OCCASIONS IN 1920, 1922 AND 1924-30.

DALTON, ALFRED, A.M.INST.T.—B. 1892; G.S. AND W.R., IRELAND, 1909; ASST. TRAFFIC SUPT., TANGANYIKA RLYS., NOV., 1920; TRAFFIC SUPT., 1930; ASST. SUPT., LINE, KENYA, AND UGANDA RLYS. AND HARBOURS, 1936.

DALTON, LLEWELYN CHISHOLM.—B. 1879; ED. MARLBOROUGH AND TRIN. COLL., CAMB.; B.A., 1900 (HIST. TRIPOS); M.A., 1905; CALLED TO THE BAR, GRAY'S INN, 1901; LEGAL ASST., LAND SETTLM., BD., O.R.C., OCT., 1901; ASST. RES. MAG. AND J.P., JUNE, 1902 TO NOV., 1910; ADVOC., SUP. CT., 1904; REGISTR., B. GUIANA, NOV., 1910; JUDGE ADVOC., LOCAL FORCES, 1914; AG. PUISNE JUDGE, NOV., 1912 TO FEB., 1913; NOV., 1914 TO JAN., 1915; JULY, 1916, TO JUNE, 1917; JULY TO SEPT., 1918; MAR. TO JUNE, 1919; AG. SOLR.-GEN., MAY TO AUG., 1914; SEPT., 1914 TO JAN., 1915; AG. ATTY.-GEN., AUG.-SEPT., 1914; FEB.-APR., 1918; AG. CHIEF JUST., JULY-SEPT., 1917; JUNE-OCT., 1919; PUISNE JUDGE, B. GUIANA, JUNE, 1919; PUISNE JUDGE, GOLD COAST, FEB., 1923; PUISNE JUDGE, CEYLON, MAR., 1925; AG. CH. JUST., JUNE-NOV., 1933 AND APR., JULY, 1936; CH. JUST., TANGANYIKA TERRITORY, 1936; COMPILED AND EDITED "STATUTORY RULES AND ORDERS (B. GUIANA)," TO 1914, 2 VOLS.; EDITOR, B. GUIANA LAW REPORTS, 9 VOLS.; AN ASST. EDITOR "BURGE ON COLONIAL AND FOREIGN LAWS," 2ND EDIT., VOL. IV.; COMPILED DIGEST OF CASE LAW OF BRITISH GUIANA, 1910-1920; AUTHOR OF "CIVIL LAW OF BRITISH GUIANA."

D'ANDRADE, WILLIAM ALBERT.—B. 1884; CLER. ASST., SURG. GEN'L'S OFFICE, BR. GUIANA, 1903; 6TH CLS. OFFR., CUST., 1903; 5TH DITTO, 1906; 4TH DITTO, 1913; 3RD DITTO, 1921; 2ND DITTO, 1925; CH. CLK., CUST., 1925; COMPTR., CUST., 1927.

DANDURAND, HON. SENATOR RAOUl, K.C., LL.D., P.C.—B. 1861; ED. MONTREAL COLL. AND LAVAL UNIV.; LL.B., 1882; LL.D., 1909 AND HON. LL.D., MCGILL, TORONTO, AND QUEEN'S UNIV.; PRES., UNIV. OF MONTREAL, FEB., 1934; CALLED TO QUEBEC BAR, 1883; K.C., 1898; CALLED TO SENATE OF CANADA BY LORD ABERDEEN, 1898; SPEAKER OF SENATE, 1905-9; P.C., 1909; MIN. OF STATE AND LEADER OF KING GOVT. IN SENATE, 1921, ALSO IN 2ND KING GOVT., 1926, AND IN 3RD KING GOVT., 1935; LEADER OF LIB. PARTY, SENATE, 1930; SERVED AS R. assem., L. of N., Geneva, 1925; del. of Canada to coun., L. of N., 1927-30; apptd. by pres. of U.S.A., arbitr. under treaty of peace with Brazil.

DANEEL, Josias, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1884; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Edin. Univ.; admitted F.R.C.S., Edin., 1922; med. asp., Rietfontein hosp., Union of S. Africa, 1927.

DANIEL, EDWARD WELLEY, B.A., (Lond.), F.R.S.A., M.R.S.T.—B. 1885; ed. St. Paul's Coll., Cheltenham; war serv., 1914-18; Trinidad local forces, 1927-32; asst. mast., high schl. for boys, Hereford, 1921-27; prin. and lect. in pedagogy, govt. training coll. for teachers, Trinidad, 1927; sec., for external exams., 1927; asst. dir., educn. and ch. inspr., schla., 1934; ag. dir., educn., ohmn., bl. of educn., and temp. mem., leg. coun., Mar.-Oct., 1936.

DANIEL, FRANCIS de FOREST.—B. 1886; ed. Marlborough Coll.; admitted solr., 1910; asst. acctnt., Lagos rly., July, 1912; transf., cust. dept., May, 1913; asst. dist. offr., N. Provs., Nigeria, July, 1914; res., Mar., 1930.

DANIEL, REGINALD YOUNG, M.A. (Oxon).—B. 1894; on mily. serv., 1914; cadet, Ceylon, civ. serv., Oct., 1920; ag. office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Matara, Sept., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Galle, Jan., 1922; office asst., Hambantota, kach., Mar., 1922; ditto to govt. agt., N.Prov., Nov., 1922; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Dec., 1923; pol. mag., Kalutara, Dec., 1926; pol. mag., Kandy, Apr., 1928; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Aug., 1930; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Sept., 1930; do., Ratnapura, Oct., 1931; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Mar., 1933; do., Matale, Oct., 1934.
DANKS, FRANK SCOBIE, B.Sc. (Forestry), Dipl. in Agr.—B. 1896; on war serv., 1915-19; asst. conserv., forests, 1st gr., Cyprus, Oct., 1926.

DANNATT, ROBERT MALCOLM, M.B., B.S. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1891; med. offr., F.M.S., Oct., 1925; surgn., F.M.S., Mar., 1926; do., Selangor, Nov., 1933; ag. senr. surg., S'pore, Aug., 1935.

DARLING, JOHN SINGLETON.—B. 1898; ed. Marlborough Coll.; served in Indian Army 1917-22; war serv., Palestine, 1918; Waziristan, 1921; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929; asst. dist. offr., Aug., 1931.

DARRELL, ARTHUR LLEWELLYN.—B. 1883; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; asst. clk. to registr., St. Vincent, 1900; 3rd clk. treas., 1901; 2nd clk. to gov., Windward Is., (Grenada), 1905; pvt. sec. and A.D.C. to gov., Windward Is., 1913; treas. asst., 3rd grade, Nigeria, 1914; treas. asst. 2nd grade, 1919; senr. asst. treas., 1921; ag. treas. in 1924, 1933 and 1935; dep. treas., 1934; treas., Dominica, 1936.

DASH, PROF. JOHN SYDNEY, B.Sc. (Agr.), McGill Univ.—B. 1887; various posts, dept. of agr., Barbados, 1905-09; plant pathologist, Br. Columbia, 1913; asst. supr., agr., Barbados, 1914-15; dir. station agronomique, Guadeloupe, F.W.I., 1918-21; tobacco specialist and botanist, seed br., dept. of agr., Canada, 1921-22; prof. of agr., Impl. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1922-27; dir. agr., Br. Guiana, 1927; M.E.C. and M.L.C., 1928; rep. col. at various col. agril. confs., 1930-33.
DAVENDPORT, CECIL THORNHILL, M.A.—B. 1892; ed. Malvern and Oriel Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. comsrr., Kenya, Jan., 1920.

DAVIDSON, EDWARD ERNEST.—B. 1878; extra clk., C.S.O., Ceylon, July, 1898; inspr. of post offices, Jan., 1900; office asst. to dir. of public instruction, Jan., 1906; ag. inspr. schla., Apr., 1911, and May, 1912; apptd. to en. V. of civ. serv. Jan., 1913; ag. asst. dir. of educn., Apr., 1920; addnl. asst. dir. of educn., Oct., 1920; office asst. to dir. of educn., Nov., 1922.

DAVIDSON, GERALD LABBOOM DEAS.—B. 1898; on mily. serv., Sept., 1917; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1921; attd. Colombo rch Dec., 1921; attd., Badulla kach., Jan., 1922; office asst., Ratnapura kach., Oct., 1924; dinx Kurunegala kach., Oct., 1926; pol. mag. DAVIDSON, WALKER WHEATLEY, M.C. (Cantab.), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1886; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1914; on mily. serv., 1916-19; exec. engr., July, 1923; senr. exec. engr., Nov., 1932; do., Larut, Matang and Krian, Apr., 1934.

DAVIES, D.—B. 1889; ed. at Llandover and Worcester Coll., Oxon.; asst. dist. comsrr., Gold Coast, Apl., 1915; junr. asst. col. sec., May-July, 1915; ag. dist. comsrr., Addah, Jan.-May to May, 1916; ag. dist. comsrr., Accra, Sep., 1916 to Sep., 1917.

DAVIES, DAVID, A.R. San. I.—B. 1887; sec. dept., Hong Kong, 1914; seconded for m. ser., 1917-20; storekeeper, army dept., 1918; lst. clk. and asst. sec., army bd., 1923; sec., army bd., 1927; ch. clk., senr. cler. and actg. sec., col. secretariat, 1928; J.P., 1930.
DAVIES, EUSEBEZ Dyer.—Clk., educn. dept., Natal, 1902; senr. clk., 1913; prin. clk., 1913; educn. dept., Natal, 1922; ch. clk., admin. dept., Natal, June, 1932; asst prov. sec., Jan., 1935.

DAVIES, EDWARD HAROLD, B.A. (Wales)—B. 1895; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Col., Oct., 1920; ag. pol. mag., Matara, Jan., 1922; ditto, Panadura, May, 1922; office asst., Badulla kach., Oct., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Ngombo, Feb., 1924; asst. dir., statistics, Mar., 1924; off. asst., Kandy Kach.; Oct., 1925; asst. dir., statistics, Feb., 1927; ag. dir., ditto, Aug., 1927; asst. govt. agt., Colombo, May, 1928; asst. land comsrr., July, 1931; sec., Colombo prov. comsrr., Nov., 1931.

DAVIES, EDWARD JOHN.—B. 1898; ed. Llandover Coll. and Univ. of Wales, B.A., LL.B. (Wales); served with Artists' Rifles and West Yorks. DAVIES, RICHARD MORGAN, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1892; on mily. serv., Dec., 1914; cadet, Ceylon, Sept., 1920; office asst., Kegalla kach., Jan., 1922; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1923; dep. rubber com., May, 1924; asst. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Ser., 1925; ditto, Trinco malee, June, 1926; asst. sttlmt. offr., Dec., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Kap., Aug., 1927; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, May, 1928; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, May, 1930; pol. mag., Matale, Nov., 1931; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Mar., 1932; asst. sttlmt. offr., July, 1932; asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Jan., 1935.

DAVIES, WILLIAM ALFORD NOEL.—B. 1883; ed. Monmouth Gram. Sch.; admitted solr. of sup. ct., England, Apr., 1906; private practice in E. Africa, 1906-1907; private practice in Sierra Leone, Dec., 1908; passed lower standard Mendi exam., June, 1911; ag. dist. commnr., Dec., 1911, to June, 1912; ag. pol. mag., June to Dec., 1912; acted as crown prosecutor on several occasions; ag. dist. commnr., 1913-14; master and regis., sup. ct., S. African Service Brig., Oct., 1918; demob., Dec., 1918; resumed duties as regis., sup. ct., Sierra Leone, May, 1919; regis., sup. ct., S'pore, Nov., 1920.

DAVIS, SIR CHARLES THOMAS, G.C.M.G. (1928), K.C.M.G.(1923), C.M.G.(1915).—B. 1873; ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon, and Oxford; classical scholar, Balliol Coll., 1892-6; 1st class classical mods., 1894; Jenkyns exhibn., 1896; 1st class lit. hum., 1896; apptl., after compet. exam., 1st cl. clk., acct-gen.'s dept., admiralty, Oct., 5th, 1896; transf. to sec.'s dept., inland rev., Mar., 1897; 2nd cl. clk., col. office, Apr., 15th, 1897; went to Gibraltar as sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir C. R. Thompson on a mission to inquire into the currency question, May and June, 1898; sec. Pacif. cable comittee, 1900; priv. sec., to Sir M. Ommannay, June, 1902; 1st cls. clk., 1st Apr., 1903; principal clk., 4th June, 1916; senr. asst. sec. to Imperial Conference; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. under-sec. of state, 1st Dec., 1921; perm. under-sec. of state for dominion affrs., 9th July, 1925; ret., 1930.

DAVIS, HON. HENRY HAGUE.—B. 1885; ed. Brockville, Ont., Can.; Collegiate Inst., Toronto Univ. (B.A. 1907, gold medallist in polit. sci., M.A., LL.B.); Law Sch., Osgoode Hall, Toronto, 1911; called to Ontario bar 1911; K.C., 1928; judge, supreme ct., Ont., 1933.

DAVIS, LIONEL STUART, F.C.S.—B. 1890; lab. asst., Br. Guiana, 1908; 3rd asst. analyst, 1914; 2nd asst. do., 1915; 1st do., 1922; examin. in chem., to pharmaceutical bd., 1921-33; ag. gov't. analyst, 1929 and 1933; ag. gov't. analyst, Trinidad, July, 1933; ag. gov't. do., July-Dec., 1933.

DAVIS, RICHARD PRITCHARD, M.I.M. & C.E., M.T.P.I., M.R.S.I.—B. 1888; town planning supt., Selangor, F.M.S., Dec., 1928; town planner, Selangor, July, 1932; do., Perak, Selangor and N. Sembilan, Oct., 1933.

DAVIS, SIR STEWART SPENCER, KT. BACH. (1930), C.M.G.(1919)—B. 1875; rev. off., St. Kitts, 1893; 1st clk., treasy. and cust., 1898; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis def. force, 1898; ag. adjutant, 1899; att. col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., July, 1901; acctnt., treasy., G. Coast, 1901; asst. treasy., 1902; ch. ast. treasy., Nov., 1908; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1915; treasy., civil admnstr., German Africa (occupied territory), Nov., 1916; treasy., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ag. sec. to admnstr., June to Aug., 1919; dep. admnstr., Mar.-Apr., 1920; treasy., Palestine, May, 1922; chmn., economy comittee, 1922; ag. ch. sec. on various occasions, 1923-25; chmn., currency comittee., 1924; del. for Palestine at Constantinople in connec. with Ottoman pub. debt, Oct. to Nov., 1924; chmn., comittee on finances of Orthodox Patriarchate of Jerusalem, Dec., 1925; chmn., standing comittee for commerce and industry from 1928; O.A.G. Palestine and ag. high. comittee., Trans-Jordan, July-Sept., 1930; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., St. Helena, 1932.

DAVIS, THOMAS ARTHUR WARREN.—B. 1899; ed. Haverfordwest and Keble Coll., Oxford; Indian army, 1917-22; dipl., forestry (Oxon.), 1923; asst. conserv., forests, Br. Guiana, 1925; ag. conserv., forests, various periods, 1930-36.

DAVSON, SIR EDWARD, 1ST BARONET (b. 1927), KT. Bach (1919)—Ed. Eton; pres., Associated West Indian Chambers of Commerce, 1916-26; chmn., Imp. Cocoas Conf., 1924; mem., Imp. Economic Comittee, 1925; chmn., federation of Chambers of Commerce of Br. Empire, 1925-28; chmn., W. Indies Conf., 1929; advr. on col. trade to S. of S. for the Cola., Ottawa Conf., 1932 and World Economic Conf., London, 1933; rep. of cols. on Imp. Communications Advisory Comittee, and Imp. Economic Comittee.; a trustee of Imp. Inst., and gov., Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agric.

DAVSON, GEORGE CHARLES WOLSELEY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll., and Birmingham Univ.; asst. engnr., Nigeria, July 1913; asst. engnr., Nigerian Rly., 1915; cadet as S.M.E., Chatham, 1916; lieut., R.E., 1917; ag. capt., 1919; asst. engnr., S.S., June, 1919; ex. engnr., Nov., 1923; senr. ex. engnr., Sept., 1933.

DAWE, ARTHUR JAMES, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1891; ed. Berkhamsted Schil. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; scholar, 1910-1914; R.A.; war service, 1914-18; staff, 1917; lieut., R.N.V.R. (R.N.D.); temp. appt. in C.O., Jan., 1918; apptd., under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., June, 1919; asst.prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to Mr. Amery, 18th June, 1920; priv. sec. to Mr. E. F. L. Wood, 1921; prin., 1st Dec., 1921; dep. sec. to Imp. Economic Conf., 1923; visited Sierra Leone as sec. to comittee of enquiry into affrs. of Freetown municipality, 1926; accom. parly U.S. of S. on visit to Palestine and Cyprus, 1930; sec. to Malta royal comittee, 1931; sent on mission to Malta, 1933-34; asst. sec., 6th Apr., 1936.

DAWE, MORLEY THOMAS, O.B.E. (1926), F.L.S.—B. 1880; asst. botanist, forestry and scientific dept., Uganda, 1902; head of dept., 1903; dir., agric., Mozambique, 1910; agric., advr. to Colombia, 1915; comand. by C.O. to visit and report on agric. conditions and needs of Gambia, 1920; consultg. agric. expert to Fomento Geral de Angola, 1921; comittee, lands and forests, S. Leone, 1922; comittee, repg. S. Leone at British Empire exhib., 1924; comittee, repg. S. Leone at Canadian Nat. exhib., Toronto, 1926; dir. agric., Cyprus, 1928; mem., leg., May, 1928 to Nov., 1931; headed Cyprus trade mission, June-July, 1930; rep. gov't., 9th Internat. horticultural conf., London, 1930, confere on fruit production, London, 1930 and 5th Internat. botanical conf., 1930; dir., agric. and forests, Palestine, 1933; mem., advy. coun., chmn., eng. agril. coun. and mem. standing comte., for commerce and industry.

DAWSON, CHRISTOPHER WILLIAM, Barrister-at-Law (Gray's Inn).—B. 1896; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (class. schol.), 2nd lieut., 5th Batt., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; served India (N.W. Frontier), Mesopotamia; demobilised with rank of capt., 1919; cadet, S.S., Mar., 1920; 3rd mag., S'pore, July, 1920; 2nd mag., S'pore, Oct., 1922; 2nd asst. advr., B. Pahat, Nov., 1923; 1st mag. and regis., sup. ct., Johore, Mar., 1924; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Feb., 1925; offr., cls. IV, Mar., 1927; called to bar (Grays Inn), June, 1929; dist. judge, Malacca, Sept., 1929; 2nd ast sec., S.S., Jan., 1930; 1st ast sec., S.S., Mar., 1932; ag. legal advr., Kedah, July, 1933.
DAWSON, ERNEST EDWARD.—B. 1886; B.A. (engg.) Cambridge with 2nd cl. hon., M.A.C.I.E.; Canadian N. rly., 1910; astt. engrg. Tanganyika rly., Jan., 1921; dist. engrg., Nov., 1928.
DAWSON, PERCY LEE.—B. 1890; ed. Needham House (Ely), Camb., office astt., Nigerian Rly., 1920; astt. traffic supt., 1921; dist. traffic supt., Feb., 1935.

DAY, SIR ALBERT CECIL, KT. BACH. (1932), C.M.G. (1925), C.B.E. (1919).—Astt. priv. sec. to gov. of New Zealand, 1910-1912; offr. sec. to gov. gen., 1913-36; apptd. to staff of N.Z. high comm., London, 1936.

DAY, EDWARD VICTOR GRACE.—B. 1896; ed. Christ's Coll., Christchurch and Timaru High Schl., New Zealand; cadet, S.S., Sept., 1921; attd., col. sec. office, Nov., 1921; astt. sec., Malaya Borneo Exhibn., Dec., 1921; A.D.C. to O.A.G., June, 1922; sec., sny. bd., A. Star in addn., Feb., 1923; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; ag. 1st ast sec., S.S., Nov., 1933; ag. ast treas. and collr., land rly., Malacca, Nov., 1935.

D'CRUZ, ALBERT WALTER JOSEPH, L.M.S (S'pore).—B. 1885; astt. surg., Tan Tock Seng's Hosp., S'pore, Apr., 1908; ag. health offr. quarantine, June 1910 and Nov., 1918; astt. health offr. port, Aug., 1911, Sept., 1915, Mar., 1919 and Apr., 1921; ag. med. offr., Labuan, Dec., 1920; dep. health offr. port, S'pore, Jan., 1923; do., but sr., Jan., 1930; health offr., port, S'pore, Dec., 1930.

DEAKIN, CHARLES STINEY, B.A.—B. 1897; ed. at Orme Schl., Newcastle-on-Tyne, (Christ's Hosp. and Queen's Coll., Camb., schol., Hughes Eng. essay prize, 1st cl. mechan. tripods, engnr., 1923; research grant, bridge oscillations, 1923-24; junr. engrg. dept., science and indust. research, bridge stress comm., 1924-25; John Wimbolt prize, engnr. research, 1925; R.A.S.C. (M.T.), 1916; Iraq, 1917; attd., polit. dept. as Q.M.S., Native Militia and hd. clk., Zubair, 1919; demob., 1920; astt. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

DEANE, SIR GEORGE CAMPBELL, KT. BACH. (1930).—B. 1873; ed. at St. John's Coll., Oxford; M.A., Oxon (honours in jurisprudence); called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1898; practised at the bar, B. Guiana, 1898-1903; ditto, Trinidad, 1903-1910; stip. mag., Arima, Trinidad, Sept., 1910; ag. S.J.P., Port of Spain, Trinidad, May to Dec., 1911, and from May to Oct., 1912; stip. mag., Port of Spain, 1913; ag. puisne judge, on various occasions, 1914-17; twice apptd. commr., under ordce, 1919; capt. and adjutant, Trinidad Light Horse; 2nd puisne judge, Apr., 1920; ch. just., Leeward Is., Jan., 1923; puisne judge, S. Sttlments. and judg. commr. F.M.S., Nov., 1924; ch. just., Gold Coast, 1929; pres., W.A. ct. of appl., 1930; ret., Dec., 1935.

DEANE, Lieut.-Col. ROBERT., O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1879; S.A. War, 1900-02; S.A. constab., 1901; Transvaal pol., 1908; S.A. Permt. Force (Staff), 1912; German S.W.A. (staff capt. and brig. machine gun offr.), 1914; W.F.F., Egypt (brig. machine gun offr.), 1st S.A. Inf. Brigade, 1915; France, 1916; ch. of pol., St. Lucia, 1919; dep. inspr. gen. pol., Mauritius, 1922; inspr. gen. pol. and supt., prisons, 1926; M.L.C., 1926.

DEAR, EUGENE CLYDE.—B. 1885; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; ent. civ. serv., Barbados, 1902; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1923; clk. to atty.-genl., 1924; ag. sol.-gen. for various periods, 1926-28; regar., 1930; judge, astt. ct. of appl., and regis. of friendly socs., 1931.

De BOER, HENRI SPELDENGHE, M.C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Cantab.), D.T.M. and H. (Lond.).—B. 1889; ed. privately and at London Hosp.; served with R.A.M.C., 1915-20 (Gallipoli, Sinai, Palestine and Syria, ment. in desps.); med. offr., Kenya, May, 1920; senr. health offr., E. African med. serv., Jan., 1926; dep. dir., san. services, N. Rhodesia, Mar., 1931; do., med. services, 1933; dep. dir., med. services, Uganda, 1933.

DE CARTERET, VALENTINE EDWARD ROSS.—B. 1883; astt. commr., pol., Gold Coast, May, 1915; commr., Jan., 1922; col. pol. offr.'s course, R.I.C., 1915; and senr. pol. offr.'s course at New Scotland Yard, 1927; senr. commr., pol., 1929.

DE CHAIR, VICE-ADMIRAL SIR DUDLEY RAWSON STRATFORD, K.C.B. (1916), K.C.M.G. (1933), M.V.O. (1908).—B. 1864; mid. of the "Alexandra," at bombardment of Alexandria, 1882; landed with naval brig., naval attaché to courts of the Maritime Powers, 1902; and to N. and S. America, 1903-4; A.D.C. to the King, 1911-12; naval sec. to 1st lord of the adm'y., 1912-14; adm'l. of training service, 1914; comdg. tenth cruiser squadron, 1914-16; naval adviser to F.O., 1916; vice-adml. comdg. battle squadron, 1917-18; vice-adml., 1917; adm'l. comdg. coast guard and reserve, 1919; gov., New S. Wales, Oct., 1923; ret., 1930.

DE COMARMOND, HENRI MAXIME.—B. 1899; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1922; ag. dist. and stip. mag. for Mauritius and Rodrigues, Aug., 1925; ag. addnl. subst. procureur and advoc. gen., on various occasions, 1925-28; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Jan., 1927; 2nd addnl. subst. procureur and advoc.-gen., Jan., 1928; subst. procureur and advoc.-gen., Dec., 1932; ag. procureur and advoc.-gen., May, 1933; mem., exec. coun. and coun. of govt.

DE FREITAS, SIR ANTHONY, KT. B.A. (1927), O.B.E. (1918), B.A. (Clare Coll., Camb.), B. 1869; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn; ag. chief just., St. Vincent, May-Oct., 1904; stip. mag., 1st Dist., St. Lucia, Oct., 1904; ag. atty-gen., St. Lucia, July, 1906; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, Apr., 1907; chief just., St. Vincent, May, 1913; chief just., St. Lucia, Sept., 1919; ag. atty-gen., St. Vincent, 1909, 1915, and Mar., 1917, to Dec., 1918; ag. col. sec., St. Lucia, on seven occasions; ag. adminstr., St. Lucia, on 15 occasions, including July, 1914, to May, 1915; puisne judge, Jamaica, Aug., 1921; ag. ch. just., Jamaica, July, 1922 to Sent., 1923 and Jan., 1925 to Feb., 1926; ch. just., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1927; ret., 1934.

DE GLANVILLE, R. J. A. P. G.—B. 1885; called to the bar, Bahamas, 1914; notary pub., 1914; priv. sec. to H.E. Wm. Hart Bennett. Esq., C.M.G., adminstr. of Bahamas, 1909; also to H.E. Sir W. Grey-Wilson, K.C.M.G., gov., 1912; ag. coroner for New Providence, 1911 and 1913; confirmed, 1914; 2nd clk., H. of A., 1912; chief clk., 1913; ag. regisr., sup. ct. from 1913; ag. stip. and circuit mag. and coroner on various occasions, 1921-31; ag. atty.-gen., advoc. gen. and admly. advoc., contr., local clearing office and temp. mem., exec. coun. on various occasions in 1923, 1924 and 1925; regisr.-gen., Apr., 1923; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1922-35.

DE HEVENINGHAM, N.W.P.—Ed. King's Coll., London and Worcester Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law (Inner Temple); ent. home civ. serv. (open compt.), 1901; served H.O.L.G. Bd. and min. of munitions; practised at bar, 1918-24; ch. mag. and legal adviser, Br. Solomon Is.; mem., advisory coun. and a judl. comr. for W. Pacific, 1924; dep. regisr., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1928; ag. regisr., May-Dec., 1931; res. mag., 1932; ag. judge, high ct., 1935.
DE JAGER, SAMUEL JACOBUS, K.C., B.A., LL.B., Camb.—B. 1879; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1902; admitted advocate, O.R.C., 1903; atty.-gen., O.F.S., 1911; ditto, Cape Prov., 1929.

DE KOCK, GILLES VAN DE WALL.—B. 1889; ed. Stellenbosch, Pretoria, Capetown and London; M.R.C.V.S., Lond. (1913); vety. research offr., Onderstepoort, 1914; prof. of anatomy, Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1920; prof. anat., T'vaal Univ. Coll., 1920; nr. vety. research offr., Onderstepoort, 1920; sub-dir., vety. services, Onderstepoort, 1927; D.Sc., Witwatersrand Univ., 1928; dep. dir., divn. of vety. services and animal husbandry, 1931.

DE KOCK, MICHIEL HENDRIK, B.A. (Cape), M.A. and Ph. D. (Harvard).—B. 1893; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Harvard Univ., U.S.A.; sen. lecturer, economics, Univ. of Cape Town, 1923-24; mem., bd. of trade and industries, Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1924-29; mem., diamond control advisory comttee, 1930; S. African Reserve Bank, 1931; dep. gov., S. African Res. Bank, 1932; author of "Finance Of the Union of South Africa," "Government ownership in South Africa," and "Economic History of South Africa."

DE LA HARPE, PETER HENRY, I.S.O. (1935).—B. 1877; apptd. to cls. V., Ceylon civ. serv., June, 1923; extra offr., asst., Galle Kach., June, 1923; ditto to col. sec., Aug., 1928; office asst. to col. sec., Sept., 1929; do. to ch. sec., July, 1931.

DE LA WARR, 9th Earl (cr. 1761), HERBAND EDWARD DONALDSON BRABBSY SACKVILLE, J.P.—B. 1900; ed. Eton; served in the Navy during the War; partly, under-secr., W.O., 1929-30; lord-in-waiting to H.M., 1929 and 1929-31; Mayor of Bexhill, 1932-33, 1933-34 and 1934-35; partly, sec. min. of agr. and dep. min. of fisheries, 1930-31 and 1931-35; parl. U.S. of S. for the Colts., Sept., 1936.

DEL TUFO, MOROBE VINCENZO, B.A. —B. 1901; ed. Trin. Coll. Camb.; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1923; passed cadet, May, 1925; fedl. examr. in Tamil, July, 1926; offr. cls. V, asst. contr., lab., Klang, Dec., 1926; do., Penang, Dec., 1928; offr. cls. IV, Dec., 1930; fedl. examr. in Telegu, July, 1932; offr. cls. III, Dec., 1933; asst. to attny.-gen., Jan., 1936.
DE MEL, VIDANELAGE SAMSON MANUEL, B.A. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1910; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1934.
DE MEZA, JOHN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1885; vety. offr., Nyasaland Prot., 1914; vety. bact., 1921; ch. vety. offr., 1935.

DEMPERS, HENDRIK LOUW.—B. 1886; ed. Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch, S. Africa; asst. comttee, olk., H. of A., Union of S. Africa, 1913; war serv., S.W. Africa, Ireland, France and Belgium, 1914-19; olk. of papers, 1917; serjeant-at-arms, 1937.

DENHAM, SIR EDWARD BRANDIS, G.C.M.G. (1930), K.C.M.G. (1931), K.B.E. (1927), C.M.G. (1922).—B. 1876; ed. at Malvern and Merton Coll., Oxford (exhibtnr.) B.A., 1899; cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1899; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1902; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., May, 1904, Mar., 1905; asst. govt. agt. N. Prov. for pearl fishery work, Mar., 1906; 2nd asst. col. sec. and sec., agric. bd., May, 1905; priv. sec. to Sir A. Ashmore, O.A.G., Sepk., 1905; organising sec., agric. bd., Dec., 1905; landing survr., cust., Colombo, June, 1906; dist. judge, Negombo, Sept., 1908; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1909; and priv. sec. to Sir H. Clifford, O.A.G., June, 1909; seconded as supt. of census, Aug., 1910; priv. sec. to Sir H. Clifford, O.A.G., June, 1911; attached to C.O., 1st Jan. to 13th Oct., 1913; prin. asst. col. sec., Ceylon, 25th Apr., 1914; chief censor, in addition to his own duties, Aug., 1914; capt., administrative section, town guard, Colombo, 1915; ag. govt. agt., E.Prov., Sept., 1915; offr. of cls. I., grade II. on appnt. as dir. of educn., Aug., 1916; dir. food production, Mar.-Dec., 1920; col. sec., Mauritius, 14th Dec., 1920; admstd. govt. in 1921, 1922 and 1923; col. sec., Kenya Col. and Prot., Aug., 1923; admstd. govt., Feb. to Oct., 1925 and from Jan. to Aug., 1927; gov., Gambia, 1928; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Br. Guiana, 1930; capt.-gen. and gov.-in-ch., Jamaica, 1934; joint author of "Rubber in the East."

DENMAN, LORD.—3rd Baron (cr. 1834), Rt. Hon. Sir Thomas Denman, P.C. (1907); G.C.M.G. (1911): K.C.V.O. (1909).—B. 1874; ed. at Sandhurst; lieut., reserve of offs.; late lieut., Royal Scots, and major, Middlesex L.Y.; served in S. Africa war (wounded, Queen's medal, 3 clasps); lord-in-waiting in ordinary, 1905-7; capt. Lon. corps of gentlemen-at-arms, 1907-11; J.P., Sussex; gov. gen. and commdr.-in-chief, Commonwealth of Australia, 1911-1914.
DENNETT, JOHN HORACE, B Sc.—B. 1899; ed. King Edwd. VI Schl. and E'ham Univ.; asst. agric. chem., F.M.S., May, 1921; asst. chem., soils divn., Feb., 1927; soils chem., Jau., 1935.

DENNIS, OWEN GREGORY.—B. 1894; ent. Army, King's Own Scottish Borderers, 1915; war. serv. in Egypt and France, 1915-18; seconded serv., W.A.F.F., 1919; asst. comsnr., pol., Nigeria, 1922; reagd. Army comsnn., 1923; invalided from col. serv., Nov., 1923; re-appnt. as local commdt., pol. and asst. comnrr., Cyprus, Mar., 1928; comnrr. and local commdt., pol., Dec., 1929; comsnnr., 1934; admstive offr.; comsnnr., Jan., 1935; asst. comsnnr., Apr., 1936.
DENNY, WILLIAM JOSEPH, M.C.—M.P., S. Australia, since 1900; atty.-gen. and min. for Northern Territory, 1910-12; capt., A.I.F., European War, 1915-19; atty.-gen., 1924-27 and since Apr., 1930.

DE NOBRIGA, John.—B. 1884; 3rd clk., P.W.D., Trinidad, Apr., 1901; asst. olk., water and sewerage dept., Aug., 1903; storekeeper and clk., engr.'s office, Apr., 1905; clk., mines dept., Sept., 1908; ch. clk., ditto, Dec., 1910; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1921; ch. clk. and clk., leg. coun., Feb., 1925; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1930; ag. 1st asst. col. sec., Feb., 1931; ag. dep. col. sec., May-Aug., 1934; 1st asst. col. sec., Mar.,

DE NORMANN, ALBERT WILFRED NOEL.—B. 1891; ed. United Services Coll. and Chateau du Rosey, Switzerland; Canadian Expdy. Force, 1914; lieut., Royal Fusiliers, 1915; West African Forces, 1916-20; survr., Nigeria survey, 1921; asst. survr.-gen., 1930; ag. survr.-gen., Apr.-Sept., 1933; ag. commr., lands and survr.-gen., Mar.-Sept., 1935 and Mar.-May, 1936; commr., land and survr.-gen., 1936; M.L.C.

DE PINTO, CYRIL ERNEST.—B. 1892; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Sept., 1914; attd., Galle Kachcheri, Sept., 1914; office asst., Batticaloa Kach., Nov., 1917; pol. mag., Avissawella, Nov., 1919; office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, July, 1921; pol. mag., Jaffna, Oct., 1921; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, Aug., 1923; pol. mag., Matara, Apr., 1925; ditto, Galle, Mar., 1927; dist. judge, Kegalle, June, 1933; do., Matara, May, 1934.

DE ROME, F. J., B.Sc. (Lond.), M.B.E.—B. 1886; B.Sc. Lond. Univ., 1908; Jena Univ. (Germany), 1908-1909; asst. mast., Queen's Coll. Hong Kong, 1910; asst. postal censor, 1914-19; asst. mast., cls. II., 1919; asst. mast., cls. I., 1920; ag. headmr., Ellis Kadodoo schl., 1923; ag. headmr., Wantaai schl., 1923; headmr., Ellis Kadodoo schl., 1925; headmr., Queen's Coll., Mar., 1930.
DERRIMAN, FRANCIS PHILIP LOUIS.—B. 1901; ed. Ratcliffe Coll.; col. audit dept., Oct., 1924; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925; do., Nigeria, June, 1934.
DE SANTOS, EBROL LIONEL.—B. 1890; ent. Trinidad civ. serv., Apr., 1912; asst. treas., Jan., 1929; treas. and chmn., agric. bank, 1933.
DESILVA, CHARLES PERCIVAL, B.A. (cantab.), B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1910; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1933.
DESILVA, JOSEPH MARTIN.—B. 1880; apptd. to Ceylon civ. serv., Apr., 1926; extra off. asst., Ratnapura kach., Apr., 1926; ditto, Galle kach., Oct., 1929; ag. office asst., Colombo kach., Feb., 1932.

DESILVA, WALWIN ARNOLD, B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1905; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; attd., Galle kach., Jan. and Sept., 1928; attd., Jaffna kach., June, 1928; attd., Kegalle kach., Sept., 1928; attd., Matara kach., Nov., 1928; office asst., Galle kach., Oct., 1932; asst. govt. agt., Badulla, Dec., 1934.

DE SOYZA, GUNASENA, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1926; attd., Matara kach., Sept., 1926; attd. to Jaffna kach., June, 1927; office asst., Kegalle kach., Mar., 1928; 2nd landing survr., cust., Nov., 1928; office asst., Badulla kach., Oct., 1931; asst. govt. agt., Badulla, Aug., 1932; do., Mullaitivu, June, 1933; dep. registr., co-op. socs., Oct., 1934; addnl. dep. registr., do., Mar., 1935.

DESPICHT, STANLEY MONK.—B. 1890; ed. Boston Gram. Schl.; passed lower stand. exam. in Med.; war service, E. Africa, 1917-18; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1915; ag. asst. col. sec., 1919; dist. commr., July, 1923.
DESROCHERS, FERIX.—B. 1886; ed. St. Hyacinthe Seminary, Quebec; B.A. (1905); LL.L. (1908); called to bar, 1908; ch. librn., liby., Montreal, 1930; gen. librn., liby. of parlmt., Canada, 1933.
DE VERTEUIL, ARTHUR.—B. 1878; ent. Trinidad civ. serv., July, 1899; warden, county of Coroni, July, 1929; do., eastern counties, Mar., 1933; do., Victoria, St. Patrick, Apr., 1936.

DE VERTEUIL, ERIC J., M.D., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., D.P.H. (Lond.)—B. 1881; asst. med. inspr. of health, Trinidad, Oct., 1930; ag. dep. surg.-gen., May, 1933; secr. med. inspr. of health, Aug., 1935; M.O.H., N.W. dist., Jan., 1936.

DE VERTEUIL, ST. YVES DANIEL, M.B.E. (1932).—B. 1875; clk. of works, waterworks, Trinidad, Dec., 1900; head overseer, Arima, Jan., 1903; dist. engr., 2nd grade, Aug., 1916; dist. engr., 1st grade, Sept., 1916; warden, counties Nariva-Mayaro, Dec., 1925; warden, St. George, prot. of immigrts. and dir., lab. exchange, Mar., 1933; chmn., cent. water bd., Apr., 1936.

DE VILLIERS, ISAAC PIERRE, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. S. African Coll.; atty. and notary served S.W. Africa, in 1st Eastern Rifles, 1914-15; France and Palestine, in R.F.A., 1916-19; dep. commr. (headqrs.) S.A. pol., Feb., 1928; commr., S. African pol., Dec., 1928.

DE VILLIERS, SIR JEAN ETIENNE REKEN, M.A., LL.D., Kt.Bach. (1923).—B. 1875; ed. S. African Coll. and Cambridge; B.A., 1894; LL.B., 1897; LL.D., 1902; fellow of St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1901; judge of water ots. from 1913; ag. judge, sup. ct., Cape Town, 1915, 1916, 1918, 1919; judge pres., O.F.S., 1920; judge of appl., 1933.

DEVINE, A. SIDNEY.—B. 1892; ed. Fran. Coll., Oxon; enlisted Middlesex Regt., Aug., 1914; 2nd lieut., R. Warwickshire Regt., Oct., 1915; lieut., 1917; served in France and Ita', wounded; served with disposal bd. in Ital., 1919-20; disposal and liquidation commn., 1921-23; cler. offr., C.O., 19th Mar., 1923.

DEVONSHIRE, 9TH DUKE OF (creat. 1604), VICTOR CHRISTIAN WILLIAM CAVENDISH, K.C., 1916; P.C.; G.C.M.G.; G.C.V.O.; LL.D.; BARON CAVENDISH, 1605; EARL OF DEVONSHIRE, 1618; MARQUESS OF HARTINGTON, 1634; EARL OF BURLINGTON, 1831; BARON CAVENDISH (U.K.), 1831.—B. 1868; ed. Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.P. (L.U.) Derbyshire W., 1891-1908; treas. of H.M. Household, 1900-1903; financial sec. to treasury, 1903-1905; lord-lieut. of Derbyshire; president territorial forces, Derby; chancellor of Leeds Univ.; civil lord of the Admiralty, 1915; gov.-gen. of Canada, 1916-21; S. of S. for the Colonies, Oct., 1922 to Jan., 1924.

DE WAAL, HON DANIEL, D.S.O., K.C.—B. 1873; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; B.A., LL.B. (Cantab. and Cape); admitted, advoc., Cape and Transvaal, 1897; practised, Pretoria; German S. W. Africs campaign, 1915; K.C., 1919; ag. judge, Griqua land West Local Divn., Oct., 1919; judge, sup. ct., T.P.D., Aug., 1920; judge pres., T.P.D., 1927.

DE WAAL, HON. JAN HENDRIK HOFMEE, M.P.—B. 1871; ed. Normal and S.A. Colls., Cape Town; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1897; advoc., Cape bar, 1897; mem., Union H. of A. for Piquetherg since 1915; whip to Nationalist Party, 1915-24; chmn. of comtees and dep. speaker, 1924-29; speaker, 1929-33; el. pres., Afrikaans Taal Vereeniging, 1910; formerly chmn. Regs Kommissie and chmn. head comtee, Nationalist Party, Cape; author of "Afrikaner Grammar," and of various works of fiction in Afrikaans; edr. "De Goede Hoop," 1903-14.

DE WAAL, Col. Pieter.—B. 1899; ed. Zeerust Pub. Schl. and Transvaal Univ. Coll. (now Univ. of Pretoria); lieut., perm. force, Union Defence Forces, 1922; S.A. Permt. Garrison Arty., 1923; attd. Coast Arty. Schl. Shoeburyness, Aug. Dec., 1923; attd. R.A., S. Area, England, 1924-25; capt., S.A.P.G.A., 1925; seconded to S.A. Staff Corps as staff off., gunnery, Cape Peninsula, 1925; ag. A.D.C. to gov. genl., 1925-28; grad. Staff Coll., Camberley, 1930; A.D.C. to C.G.S., U.D.F., Impl. Confe., London, 1930; attd., W.O., 1930-31; O.C. S.A.P.G.A. and C.G.A., Cape Peninsula, and promoted major, 1931; O.C., No. 1 mil. dist., Capetown, with temp. rank of lieut. col., 1932; maj. (temp. lieut.-col.), S.A. Staff Corps, 1933; lieut. col., S.A. Staff Corps, 1933; O.C. Sp. Serv. Batt., 1933-34; O.C. Roberts' Heights commnd. and comdt., S.A. Mily. Coll., 1933; dir. mily. operatns. and training, defence hqrs., Pretoria, 1934, and temp. coll., 1934.
DEWAR, H. J.—B. 1878; elk., repatriation dept., O.R.C., 1902; elk., Br. refugee aid, 1903; elk., treasy., O.F.S., 1904; elk., treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1910; acctnt., 1916; ch. acctnt., 1928.

DEWAR, John.—B. 1883; ed. Otago Univ. Coll.; cadet, lands survey dept., New Zealand, 1901; survr., F.M.S., 1906; asst. supt., surveys, F.M.S., 1911; supt. of surveys, Kelah, July, 1920; supt., surveys, F.M.S., Oct., 1924; asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Oct., 1927; dep. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Dec., 1931; survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1933.
DE WET, Johannes Hendrik, B.A.—B. 1890; ed. Univ. of Pretoria; educ. dept., 1911-18; Union pub. serv. from 1918; ag. under sec., dept. of interior, 1931; under sec. do., 1932.
DE WET, the Hon. N. J., K.C.—Min. of just., Union of S. Africa, 1913-24; puisne judge, Transvaal prov. divn., 1932.
DIAS, Neil Quintas, B.A., (Lond.), Barrister-at-Law, (Middle Temple)—B. 1911; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1935.

DICKENS, K. J., B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Christ's Hosp.; schol. and exhib.; open classical scholarship, Keble Coll., Oxford, 1925; final honour schl., English language and lit., 1929; inspr., schl., Gold Coast, 1929.

DICKENS, William Samuel, M.B.E. (1919).—B.1891; Indian pol., 1906-16; Indian army R., of Offrs., 1916-21; Iraq pol. force, 1921-30; inspr., pol. comdt., local forces and supt., fire brig., Barbados, 1932.

DICKINSON, Arthur Harold.—B. 1892; ed. Bromsgrove Schl.; pol. probr., S.S., Nov., 1912; asst. supt., pol., Jly., 1916; adit., S.S. pol., May 1920; supt., pol., Jan., 1928; King's Pol. Med., Jan., 1928; ag. dir., crim. intell., S.S. and in addn. ag. dir., pol. intell. bureau, Dec., 1928; ch. pol. offr., Malacca, Dec., 1929; dir. crim. intell., S.S., Jan., 1932; ch. pol. offr., Penang, Nov., 1933; senr. supt. and senr. dep. comsr., Malayan pol. serv., 1936.

DICKINSON, John Robert, M.C.—Ed. Liverpool Coll.; sol., 1920; 2nd lieut., The King's Own (R. Lancaster Regt.), 1914; lieut., July, 1915; capt., Aug., 1916; France, 1915-18 (M.C. and Bar); asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1921.

DICKSON, William Murray, B.A.—B. 1875; ed. St. Mary's Schl. and Coll. Inst. and Toronto Univ.; B.A., 1897; priv. sec. to several solr. gens. and mins. of just. of Canada, 1914-25; asst. cht., remission br., dept. of just., 1925-30; priv. sec., Hon. G. D. Robertson, and later, Hon. W. A. Gordon, mins. of lab., 1930-33; dep. min. of lab., 1934.

DIEMONT, J. F. W.—B. 1884; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; asst., secretarial branch, posts and tels., O.R.C., 1903; elk., prov. sec., office, O.F.S., 1910; admstve. inspr., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1922-29; prin. elk., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1929; ch. do., 1935.

DILLON, Albert Barrow, I.S.O. (1932).—B. 1867; ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad; ch. to inspr. of schl., B. Hond., 1893; ag. elk. to audr. and in treasy. and cust., 1893; elk. to col. engnr., 1895; ag.-insp. of schl., 1895-8; 2nd elk., treasy. and cust., 1897; inspr. of schl., June, 1898; ag. supt. bot. station, Sept. to Dec., 1904; ag. ch. elk., C.S.O., and elk. of couns., June, 1905 to Jan., 1906; lieut., B. Hond. Territorial Force, Apr., 1918; ag. audr., May to Aug., 1918 and from Oct., 1919 to Oct., 1920.

DIMES, Capt. Douglas Henry, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1886; ed. Kingsbridge Gram. Schl. and Schl. of Agr., S.Devon; R. Vety. Coll., London; lieut., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1913; war serv. in France and Belgium, 1914-19; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1923.

DINGLE, Percival Alfred, C.B.E. (1937), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.S.M., certif. T.M.H., L.S.T.M.—B. 1881; dist. surg., N. Borneo, Dec., 1911; dep. health offr., July, 1915; civil res. surg., No. 1 London genl. base hosp., Jan., 1917 to May, 1918; P.M.O. and offl. mem., leg. coun., Apr., 1918; deleg. to 5th cong., Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Sept., 1923; deleg. to L. of N. Health Conf., S'pore, Feb., 1925; deleg. to meeting of advisory coun., eastern bureau, L. of N. health organisation, S'pore, Jan., 1926.

DIVE, Major Hubert Roy, M.C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), D.O.M.S. (Lond.), graduate Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med., certif. Royal Lond. Opthalmic Hosp. (Moorfields).—B. 1887; great war, Gallipoli; Egypt, Palestine and France, 1915-19; 1st med. offr./i war pensioners., St. Barth. Hosp., Lond., 1919-20; student and house surg., L.S.T.M., and Albert Dock Hosp. Feb.-Aug., 1920; med. offr., F.M.S., Dec., 1920; sr. med. offr., Pahang, Nov., 1927; assisting in office of prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Aug., 1930; ch. med. offr., Penang, June, 1932; ag. ch. med. offr., S'pore, Dec., 1933.

DIX, Charles Carey, C.M.G., D.S.O., R.N. (ret.)—Ed. R.N. Schl., Eltham and H.M.S. "Britannia"; R.N., 1898-1919; harbrmr., Barbados, 1926; harbrmr., Kingston, Jamaica, 1927; dep. mast. attdnt., S.S., Sept., 1931; pol. mag., S'pore, Oct., 1931; ag. mast. attdnt., S.S., Nov., 1933-Aug., 1934; mast. attdnt., 1936.
DIXEY, Frank, O.B.E. (1929), D.S., F.G.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1892; govt. geologist, Sierra Leone, Aug., 1918 to Dec., 1921; dir., geological surveys, Nyasaland, Dec., 1921.

DIXON, Charles William, C.M.G. (1932), O.B.E. (1924), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1888; ed. at Clifton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 1st cl. mods., 1909; 1st cl. Lit.Hum., 1911; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. elk., C.O., 11th Oct., 1911; priv. sec. to Sir G. Fiddes, 8th July, 1917; 1st cl.

DIXON, Capt. Harold Parson.—B. 1888; ed. Dover Coll. and Merchant Taylors Sch.; mily. serv., 1914-20; served in Salonika, Caucasus and Anatolia; mentd. in desps.; capt., 5th Durham L.I., T.A. (Res.), 1921; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1921.

DOBBS, Cecil Moore, C.M.G. (1931), O.B.E. (1928).—Ed. St. Columba's Coll., Rathfarnham, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A. Classics (honours); asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 25th Oct., 1906; dist. commr., 1910; senr. commr. (2nd cls.), Jan., 1925; ditto, 1st cls., Sept., 1927; ag. ch. native commr., Nov. 1928.
DOBSON, Kenneth Blair Austin.—B. 1907; ed. Clifton and Oriel Coll., Oxford; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, July, 1931; asst. dist. offr., Aug., 1933.

DODDS, E.W.—B. 1881; clk., treasy., Natal, 1899; clk., treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., treasy., 1920; acctnt., treasy., 1928; acct., dept. of posts and tels., Dec., 1931; ch. acctnt., 1936.

DOLAN, Robert Henry.—B. 1903; ed. Manchester Commercial Coll.; H.M. Forces, 1925-28; transf'd. to Army Reserve and subsequently command., Trinidad local forces; dep. supt. prisons, Trinidad, 1931; ag. supt. on several occasions: supt., prisons, Sierra Leone, 1936.

DOMMEN, Michel-Edouard, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1910; ed. Oakfield Sch., Rugby College Classique, Morges, Switzerland, Derby Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; post grad. course, Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford; asst. conserv., forests, 1st gr., Cyprus, Aug., 1931.

DONOVAN, William, M.Sc. (N.Z.), F.I.C.—B. 1879; ed. Thames (N.Z.) High Schil. and Schil. of Mines, Otago Univ. and Auckland Univ. Coll.; joined N.Z. govt. serv., 1905; asst. dom. analyst, 1919; dom. analyst, 1930.

DOOLEY, Edgar (Capt. R. of O.), A.M.I.Mech.E., M.I.A.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1886; A.S.C. (mech. transp't.), 1914; engrnr. offr., Tank Corps, 1917; R. of O., 1921; asst. mech. engrnr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, Nov., 1921; transf'd., motor transp't. dept., Aug., 1922; engrnr. transp't. offr., Nov., 1922.

DOORLY, A. N., B.A. (Oxon), Barrister at Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1887; asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1913; res. mag., Kenya, 1919; senr. mag., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1923; ag. judge, high ct., in 1923, 1924 and 1925; puisne judge, Zanzibar, 1925; ag. ch. just. in 1926, 1928 and 1929; atty.-gen., 1929; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1935.

DOORLY, Rev. Charles Stokley, B.A. Selwyn Coll. Camb.—B. 1882; 4th astt. mast., Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, July, 1911; 3rd astt. mast., July, 1914; 2nd astt. mast., July, 1920; 2nd mast., 1927; ag. prin., 1931 and 1936.

DOORLY, Sir Charles W., Kt. Bach (1935), C.B.E. (1930).—B. 1875; 3rd cls. clk., imigrn. dept., Jamaica, 1892; 2nd cls. clk., 1894; Hindustani interpreter, 1897; inspr. of imigrts., 1903; protector of imigrts., 1909; govt. emigrn. agt., Madras, 1913; asst. col. sec., Jamaica, 1922; admstr., St. Lucia, Feb., 1928; ag. gov., Windward Is. in 1930, 1931 and 1933; ret., 1935.

DORAN, Charles Frederick Garfield, B.A., B.L.—Ed. Royal Belfast Academical Inst. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd Lieut., R.A.F., 1916-19; served in France, Nov., 1916 till wounded in 1917; called to Irish Bar (King's Inn) 1913; crown counsel, Kenya, Dec., 1925; ag. solr-gen. and M.L.C., Mar.-June, 1930 and Dec., 1931-July, 1932; ag. solr-gen., Sept., 1933-Jan., 1934, res. mag., 1934.

DORION, Hon. Charles Edward.—B. 1874; ed. Quebec Seminary; Laval Univ. (L.L.L., 1884); admitted to bar of Prov. of Queb., 1884; practised law in Queb. City until 1911; LL.D., Laval Univ., 1894; prof. of laws, Laval Univ., 1898; and since, prof. of civ. code; judge, superior ct., Prov. of Queb., 1911; judge, ct. of king's bench, 1921.

DORMER, Basil Anthony, M.D., M.B., B.S. (Durham), D.P.H., B.Hy. (Durham).—B. 1905; dem. in pathology, Univ. Durham and clin. pathologist, R. Victory Infirmary, Newcastle-on-Tyne, 1926-27; res. med. offr., King George V sanatorium, 1927-28; house phys. and sen. house surg., Royal Vic. Infir., 1928-29; res. med. offr., 1930; res. and asst. tuberculosis med. offr., city hosp., Newcastle, 1930-33; asst. med. offr. of health, Croydon, 1933-35; med. supt., Nelspoort sanatorium, Union of S.A., 1935.
D'ORNEILLAS, Gustave.—B. 1885; 6th cls. clk., reg. office, Br. Guiana, 1905; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1906; 5th ditto, 1910; 4th ditto, 1915; 3rd ditto, 1923; 2nd ditto, 1925; asst. surv., cust., 1927.

DOUGHTY, Sir Arthur George, K.B.E. (1935), C.M.G. (1905), M.A., Litt.D.—B. 1869; priv. sec. to min. of pub. wks., Quebec, Canada, 1896; librarian, Quebec, 1900; Dominion archivist, Canada, 1904; mem. of comm. on War Records and Trophies, Dec., 1918; joint del. to internat. congress of the history of America at Rio-de Janeiro, Sept., 1922.

DOUGHTY, Leslie Roy, B.Sc. (Agric.), N.D.A.—B. 1902; ed. Derby Munic. Secondary Schil., Midland Agr. Coll. and Schil. of Agr., Cambridge; geneticist, East African agr. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1929.
DOUGLAS, Charles Homer, M.M.—B. 1884; ed. Wellingtonborough Grammar Schil.; war serv., R.E., 1915-19; 2nd cls. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921; land survr., 1933.

DOUGLAS, Cuthbert Scholto.—B. 1877; temp. survr., irrigtn. dept., Union of S.A., May, 1917; temp. ast. engrnr., Dec., 1919; 2nd ast. prof. ast., Dec., 1927; prof. ast., Nov., 1929; survr.-gen., Bloemfontein, May, 1934.

DOUGLAS-JONES, Sir Crawford Douglas Kt. Bach (1934), C.M.G. (1920).—Ed. Harrow; priv. sec. to res. commr., S. Rhodesia, Apr., 1898; lieut., S. Rhodesia volrs., July, 1908; capt., May, 1910; musketry instr.'certif., Hythe, 1908; sec. to res. commr., Rhodesia, Apr., 1910; ag. res. commr., S. and N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1918; res. commr., July, 1918 to Sept., 1923; res. commr., N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1923; col. sec., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1924; admstd. govt., Sept., 1924 to April, 1925 and from Apr. to July, 1926; col. sec., Br. Guiana, 1926; O.A.G. on various occasions, 1927-28.

DOVEY, John Edward, M.B., Ch.B., D.T.M. & H. (Edin.).—Med. offr., Hong Kong; do., Kowloon, 1927-28; med. offr. in ch., subord. staff and families, med. offr. in ch. venereal disease clinic and astt., Victoria Hosp., 1927; M.O. in ch., Victoria Hosp., May-Sept., 1928 and 1934; M.O. in ch., civil and ment. hosp., 1933; do., Victoria hosp., Feb., 1934.

DOWBIGGIN, Sir Herbert Layard Kt. Bach., C.M.G. (1926).—B. 1880; inspr. of pol., Ceylon, Jan., 1901; ast. supt. of pol., Apr., 1901; ast. of pol., Feb., 1905; inspr.-gen. of pol., Nov., 1913; on sp. duty, Cyprus, Nov., 1926; resumed duties as inspr.-gen., pol., Ceylon, Nov., 1926.

DOWLAND, Charles Edwin James.—B. 1880; ed. Macandrew Road Sch., normal schl. and Otago Boys' High Sch., Dunedin, N.Z.; ent. N.Z. land and income tax dept., 1897; dep. comsrr., taxes, 1928; comsrr., taxes, 1932.

DOWNIE, H. F., O.B.E. (1934), B.A. (1912), M.A. (1914).—B. 1889; ed. at Christ's Hospital and Oxford; Lodge exhibt., (class.) Univ. Coll., 1908-1912; 1st class mods., 1910; 2nd class Lit. Hum., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Dec., 1912; on mil. serv. from 5th Aug. to 19th Oct., 1914, and from 10th June, 1915, to 7th Apr., 1918; ag. 1st cls. clk., 3rd Jan., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; visited Sudan, Uganda, Kenya, Tanganyika, Nyasaland, N. and S. Rhodesia and S. Africa as sec., East Africa comsrr., on closer union, Dec., 1927-May, 1928; ast. sec., 21st Oct., 1935.

DOWSE, William Robert, A.I.E.E.—B. 1891; Br. P.O. engrn. dept., G. Britain, Jan., 1912; Dub. Univ. ex-graduates, O.T.C., Aug., 1914; comsmd., R.E., Jan., 1915; Gallipoli and France, May, 1915; ast. teleg. engrn., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., June, 1919; demob. reg. army R. of O., June, 1919; engrn., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., Oct., 1926; senr. engrn., ditto, May, 1928; senr. engrn., P. and T. Selangor and Pahang, Mar., 1932; ast. engrn-in-ch., posts and tels., Malaya, Dec., 1932; ag. dir., tels. and telephone and engrn.-in-ch., posts and tels. dept., S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1934.
DOWSETT, Francis Dudley.—B. 1908; ed. Bristol Grammar Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1931; ast. dist. offr., Aug., 1933.

DOYLE, Thomas Francis.—B. 1891; ed. St. Patrick's Acad., Dungannon; offr. in Imp. cust. and excise serv., Aug., 1912; inspr., cust., Palestine, July, 1926; ag. dep. dir., cust., excise and trade, periods in 1929 and 1930; dep. collr., cust. and excise comsrr., Mauritius, Feb., 1932; ag. collr., cust. and harbrmst., June, 1932; collr., cust. and harbr.-mast., Aug., 1936.

DRAKE, Harold William.—B. 1889; ed. City of London Schl.; entd. Col. Audit Dept., July, 1904; seconded to W.A.F.F., Oct., 1914 (Nigeria and Cameroons); paymr., Overseas Contingent, W.A.F.F. (G.E.A.); Col. Audit Dept., Aug., 1918; ast. audr., Nyasaland, Mar., 1919; senr. ast. audr., Dec., 1921; ag. audr., Apr. to Oct., 1922 and Apr. to Oct., 1925; audr., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1927; ast. dir., col. audit dept., Dec., 1931; audr., Nigeria, 1933.

DRAYTON, Bernard Morgan.—B. 1886; service in post office, pub. wks. and treasy. depts., Grenada, 1902-14; treasy. ast., 3rd grade, Nigeria, 1914; ast. treasy., civ. adminstr., German E. Africa (Occupied Territory), 1917; ast. treasy., Nigeria, 1920; senr. ast. treasy., Nigeria, 1928.

DRAYTON, Hon. Sir Henry L., Kt. Bach. (1915), K.C.—B. 1869; ed. in England and Canada; called to the bar, Ontario, 1891; counsel, railway comttee., Ontario legislature, 1902; counsel for City of Toronto, 1910; Ontario govt. comsrr., on Toronto Power Comsrr., 1911; ch. comsrr., bd. of railway comsrrs., Canada, 1912; resig., to assume portfolio of min. of finance in Union Govt., Aug., 1919; elec. by acclamation for Kingston, Oct., 1919; re-ele. for York West, g.e., Dec., 1921; resig., portfolio on defeat of admin., Dec., 1921; chmn., bd. of liquor comsrrs., Ontario, 1928.

DRAYTON, Robert Harry, LL.B. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1892; solr. (Eng.); lieut., Devonshire Regt. and Machine Gun Corps, 1914-1918; serv. in France, Jan., 1916 to Sept., 1917; miny. of reconstrn. and Lord Chancellor's dept., 1919; treas. solr.'s office, 1919-20; ast. legal sec., govt. of Palestine (continuing as ast. atty.-gen.), Nov., 1920; ag. legal sec., Aug., 1921 to Oct., 1921; mem. of advisory and exec. couns., Apr. to July, 1923 and July to Sept., 1924; title changed to solr.-gen., 1925; rep. Palestine at internat. arbitrn. on partition of Ottoman pub. debt, Geneva, 1923; mem., finan. comsrr., orthodox patriarchate of Jerusalem, 1924-28; one of counsel rep. govt. in partly. comsrr. re disturbances of 1929; ag. atty.-gen. on several occasions, 1922-31; legal draftsmen, Jan., 1932; called to bar, 1934; comsrr. for revision of laws of Palestine, Sept., 1934; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1934.

DRENNAN, John Matthew, D.C.M. (1818).—B. 1891; ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin.; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., 2nd div., civ. serv. and assigned to B. of T., 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., 3rd ola., Crown Agents' office, Feb., 1912; on mil. serv., 1914-19 (France, Salonica, Palestine); ast. head, appts. dept., 1919; stores dept., 1922; shipping dept., 1930; dep. head, gen. dept., Jan., 1933; shipping dept., May 1933.
DREW, Desmond, B.A., M.B., B.Ch. (T.C.D.).—Med. offr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1921.

DREW, Ringrose Charles Willington, B.A., B.A.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. Monkton Combe Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; ast. engrn., P.W.D., F.M.S., Dec., 1912; exec. engrn., Dec., 1921; ag. state engrn., Kelantan, Apr., 1928; senr. exec. engrn., Apr., 1930; state engrn., Kelantan, June, 1931; senr. exec. engrn., Muar, Nov., 1934; do., waterwks., Johore, Aug., 1935.

DROWER, E. M., C.B.E. (1923).—B. 1880; admitted solr., 1904; practised, consular ct., Cairo, 1904-6; Sudan bar, 1906-14; served European War, 1914-18; pres. of first instance, Hillah, 1918; Mosul, 1918-19; Baarah, 1919; ast. judicial adviser, Iraq, 1921; tempy. judicial adviser, 1921; govt. counsellor, 1921; adviser to miny. of just., 1922.

DRUMMOND, David R.—B. 1898; ed. King Edward's Schl., Birmingham and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; M.A., 1923; ast. dist. comsrr., Sierra Leone, July, 1923; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1925; ag. dist. comsrr., on five occasions, 1924-30; pte. sec. to gov. and ag. gov. on five occasions, 1924-29; ag. senr. ast. col. sec., Aug.-Sept., 1930; ag. ch. ast. col. sec. on several occasions; edir. "Sierra Leone Studies".

DRUMMOND, Capt. The Hon. Edmund Rupert, M.V.O. (1922), R.N.—B. 1884; ed. H.M.S. "Britannia"; lieut., 1906; commdr., 1918; capt., 1926; ch.of-staff to commdr.-in-ch., Portsmouth, 1930-32; commanded H.M.S. "Delhi," 1932 and H.M.S. "Despatch," 1933-35; commodore commdg. N.Z. station and naval advr. to N.Z. govt., 1935.

DRYBURGH, ALEXANDER MITCHELL, M.A. (Edin.).—B. 1893; ed. George Horiot Schl., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., 1919; govt. rice agt., Tapah, Mar., 1920; 1st ast. dist. offr., B. Padang, Sept., 1920; attd. Fedl. sect., Nov., 1920; supernmy. offr., cls. V., 1922; cls. IV., 1926; dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, 1926; dist. offr., Raub, 1928; sec. rea., Negri Sembilan, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. III., 1931; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, June, 1933; ast. col. sec., Mauritius, 1934; ag. col. sec., Dec., 1934 to Nov., 1935 and Apr. 1936; gov's. dep., Sept., 1935.

DU BOULAY, GEORGE CORNIBERT, C.B.E. (1930).—B. 1883; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. audit clk., St. Leoa, Apr., 1899; 3rd clk., govt. office, Aug., 1899; 4th clk., treas. and customs, Mar., 1903; ag. 2nd clk., treasy., from Mar. to Nov., 1903; chief clk., post office, Jan., 1906; ag. harbr. master, St. Lucia, Sept., 1909; priv. sec. and clk. to gov. of Seychelles, clk. to councils and supt. of printing, Nov., 1912; ag. auditor, May, 1916; ag. treas. and mem. exec. and legis. couns., Mar. to May, 1919; ast. sec., col. sec.'s off., Gibraltar, Dec., 1919; senr. ast. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1922; ag. col. sec. and gov's. dep. on various occasions, 1924-25; ch. ast. col. sec., Gold Coast, Jan., 1926; ag. col. sec., on various occasions; gov's. dep. on various occasions; ag. gov., Apr. to Sept., 1930 and Dec., 1931.

DU CANE, GEN. SIR JOHN, G.C.B. (1928), K.C.B. (1916), C.B. (1910).—B. 1865; lieut., R.A., 1884; capt., 1893; major, 1900; lieut.-col., 1902; col., 1908; major-gen., 1915; lieut.-gen., 1919; gen., 1926; D.A.A.G., Staff Coll., 1905-07; G.S.O., 1st grade, helpers of Army, 1908-10; C.R.A., 3rd div., 1911-12; S.O. to I.G., Home Forces, 1913-14; served S. Africa, 1899-02 (desp.), brevet lieut.-col., Queen's med., 5 clasps, King's med., 2 clasps); European war, brig.-gen., III. Corps, 1914; maj.-gen., R.A., G.H.Q., 1915; sp. appt., min. of munitions, 1916; commanded XV. Corps; Br. rep. with Marshal Foch, 1918 (7 times ment. in desp.); master-gen., ordnance, 1920-23; G.O.C.-in-ch., Western command, 1923-24; G.O.C.-in-ch., Br. Army of Rhine, 1924-27; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Malta, 1927; resigned, 1931.

DUCKER, RONALD SIDNEY.—B. 1908; ed. Pembroke Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1929); M.A. (1935); spec. course in educn. at London Univ. for C.O. probationers, 1929; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, 1930; 2nd inspr., schls., Br. Guiana, 1932; supt., educn., Nigeria, 1936.

DUCKWORTH, EDWARD HARLAND.—B. 1893; ed. Cheltenham Coll., City and Guilds Engnr. Coll., Finsbury, and R. Coll. of Science; B.Sc. Lond., A.R.C.Se.; war serv. with London Elec. Engnr., R.E.
