(T.), 1915-18; France, 1916-18; inspr., educn., S. Prov., Nigeria, Oct., 1930; author of "The Laboratory Workshop" and various articles in the "School Science Review."

DUFF, DAVID, C.M.G. (1935), M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Univ. Dubl.), D.P.H. (Irel.).—B. 1883; ed. Portora, Enniskillen and Trin. Coll., Dubl.; sch. in classics; B.A. (nat. sci.) with 1st cls. hon. and gold med.; med. offr., W.A.M.S. and posted to Gold Coast, June, 1910; senr. med. offr., 1929; ast. dir., med. serv., Jan., 1927; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1927; mem. bd. of exams. in native languages, Apr., 1928; dep. dir., med. serv., Aug., 1931; dir., med. and sanry. serv., Feb., 1932.

DUFF, SIR H. L., K.B.E. (1918); C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1872; apptd. by F.O., ast. res. B.C. Africa, 1897; resig. from serv., 1903; re-apptd. Apr., 1904; res., 1906; seconded, 1909-10; admitted mem. of Inner Temple, Jan., 1909; 1st cls. hon. constitutional law, 1909; ag. dep.-gov., July, 1910; and Apr. to July, 1911; 1st grade res., July, 1911; sec. to the admstr., 1912; ch. sec. to govt. and mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1914; served with Nyassaland Field Force, Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1915, ment. in despatches; served against rebels in Shire Highlands, 1915 (medal and clasp); ch. polit. offr. to Brig.-Gen. Northey, C.B., in operations against G.E. Africa, July, 1916; ag. gov., Nyassaland Prot., 1st Oct., 1918; res., 1920; author of "Nyassaland under the F.O.," 2nd ed.; and a history of Nyassaland in the native dialect published by the Prot. govt.

DUFF, Rt. HON. SIR LYMAN POORE, G.C.M.G. (1934), P.C. (1919), B.A., LL.B. Toronto Univ., B. 1865; apptd. judge, sup. ct., British Columbia, 1904; judge, sup. ct. of Canada, 1906; cont. appeal judge, Mil. Serv. Act, 1917; dep. of gen., 1926; ch. just., Canada, 1933.

DUFF, STUART MURRAY, B.A.—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1928; ast. kach., Kandy, Jan., 1929; ast. Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1930; office ast., Nuwara Eliya kach., Mar., 1930; pol. mag., Gampola, Oct., 1931; ast. ast., Jaffna kach., Feb., 1934; coinsmr., revenue and pol. mag., Puttalam, and office ast., Puttalam kach., Mar., 1935.

DUGAN, MAJ.-GEN. SIR WINSTON, K.C.M.G., C.B., D.S.O.—B. 1877; 2nd lieut., Lincolnshire Regt., 1900; served, S. Africa, 1899-1902 (Querns' med., 3 clasps, King's med., 2 clasps); ast., 1901-4; garrison adjt., Irish commd., 1904-10; D.A.A. and Q.M.G., E. Anglian divn., 1914; ast. prov. marshal, 1914; commd. 2nd R. Inf. Regt., France, 1915-16; ast. inspr.-gen., trumps, B.E.F., 1918; brig.-commdr. (temp. brig. gen.), 1916-18; commlr., 10th Infy. Brig., 1919-23; A.A.G., Southern Comm., 1926-30; A.D.C. to King, 1928-30; commdr., 56th (1st Lond.) div., T.A., 1931; gov., S. Australia, 1934.

DUKE, CATHERINE HILL, M.A., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.P.H. (Glas. and Edin.).—Ed. Queen Margaret Coll. and Glas. Univ.; lady med. offr. out-door dispensary, women and children, Shrewsbury and offr., Ice Girls Schls. in addn., June, 1925; lady health offr., Ice Girls' Schls., Spore, Jan., 1929; joint S.S. and F.M.S. rep. at 6th Int. Soc. Hyg. Cong. and English-speaking Conf. on Maternity and Ch. Welfare, July, 1933.

DUKE, EDGAR MORTIMER, LL.B.—B. 1885; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; Br. Guiana open school, in maths., 1911; Andrews' entrance schol. in science, 1913 and Joseph Hume schol. in jurisprudence, 1914. Univ. Coll., London; Campbell Foster Prizeman of Middle Temple; prizeman in crim. law and procedure, 1915; LL.B. (Lond.), 1916; Barstow law schol., 1916; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1917; admitted to prac. Br. Guiana, 1917 and in Trinidad and Tobago, 1921; ag. registr., deeds, sup. ct., etc., Br. Guiana in 1929, 1930 and 1932; dep. registr., deeds, sup. ct., etc., Jan., 1930; stip. mag. and mem. dist. licensing bds., Demerara and Essequibo, Sept., 1931; registr., deeds, 1933; author, "Law of Immovable Property in British Guiana." —DUKE, OKRIK ST. A., M.B.E. (1933), M.M.-B. 1896; ent. govt. serv., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1913; serv. European War, 1915-19; inspr. Edward Is. pol., Sept., 1927; ch. inspr., pol., July, 1935.
DUMANOIRE, JOSEPH WENCESLAS.—Ast., trigonometrical survey, Trinidad, 1902; 2nd ast., survey office, 1903; 1st ast., ditto, 1913; dep. irvr-gen., 1930; ditto and sub. intendant, 333.

DUMBRELL, HENRY JAMES EDWARD, O.B.E. (1886).—B.A.—B. 1886; ast. mast., Heaton Lodge chl., nr. Huddersfield; mast., St. Bedes Coll., Pietermaritzburg, Natal; headmnr., Ompumulo Native training Coll.; inspr., schls., Natal, 1921; inspr., educn., Bechuanaland Prot. and Basutoland, 1928; dir., educn., Bech. Prot., 335.

DUNCAN, HAROLD HANDASYDE.—B.1885; d. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; hon. schl. med. hist.; M.A.; qualified in compet. exam. for ppt. as junr. clk., 1st cls., F.O. (placed 4th in 308 and 5th in 1909); conf. sec. to London comte., mp. Ottoman Bank for work relating to Turkish ov't. finance and indu. enterprise in Near East from Dec., 1909; on sp. mission to Constantinople, 912; served in army, Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1919; France and Italy; liaison off. (intelligence) between Br. G.H.Q., Italy and Br. Mily. Mission, Comando Supremo from Aug., 1918; capt., Norfolk Yeomanry; called to bar, Inner Temple, May, 1919; S. Eastern circuit; temp. legal asst., Ministry of Health, 25th Apr., 1927; est., 15th Mar., 1928; 2nd ast. legal adviser, D.O. and C.O., 31st lar., 1930; asts. legal adviser, (do., 1st Apr., 1931; ag. legal adviser, Dec., 1931 to Mar., 1932 and Feb., 1933 to July, 1933.

DUNCAN, SIR PATRICK, G.C.M.G. (1937), M.G. (1904), K.C., LL.D. (Edinburgh).—B. 1870; ed. at Oxford, schol. Ball. Coll.; 1st cls. class. mod., 1891; 1st cls. final class. school, 1893; Raven scholarship, 1890; clk., after compet. exam., int. rev., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir A. Milner; rln. elk., 1898; treas., Transvaal, 1901; col. sec., st. Dec., 1903; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; mem. I.C.C.; ag. lieut.-gov., Oct., 1906; barrister, Middle Temple and advoc., sup. ct., S. Africa; f.l.a., for Yeoville; min. of interior, pub. health and educn., 1921-24; min. of mines, Apr., 1933; ov. gen., Union of S. Africa, 1937.
DUNCAN, WILLIAM McFARLANE.—B. 1896; d. Craigie Pub. Schol. and Acad.; certifa. in building constr., mechanics, mathematics, etc.;aledonian rly., Perth, 1911; ast. engr., Tanganyika rly., Feb., 1921.

DUNCOMBE, F. A. C.—B. 1880; 4th clk., O., Bahamas, 1903; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1904; clk., record office, 1909; consmr., 4th div., 1912; consmr., grade III., June, 1920; clk., grade III., egistr.'s office, July, 1927; ag. ast. regiar-gen., 1928 and from June, 1929.

DUNCOMBE, WALTER KELSALL.—Bahamas, 1895-1900; Out Island commr., 1900-5; transf. Lagos Col. as supervisor, cust., Jan., 1905; collr., cust., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1909; senr. collr. cust., Nigeria, Jan., 1920; dep. contr. cust., Dec., 1922; contr., Dec., 1929; mem. ex. and leg. couns.

DUNDAS, HON. C. C. F., C.M.G. (1934), J.B.E. (1923).—Ast. dist. commr., E.A.P., Sept., 1908; dist. commr., Aug., 1914; dist. polit. offr. with hon. rank of major), German E. Africa, provisional admtn., Jan., 1916; senr. commr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; ag. ast. ch. sec., June, 1924; ag. sec. for native affrs., 1926; col. sec., Bahamas, Apr., 1929; admtn., May to Nov., 1929, June to Aug., 1930 and from June, 1931; dep. gov., in 1931 and 1932; O.A.G., Aug. to Sept., 1932; ch. sec., N. Rhodesia, July, 1934; ag. gov., July-Oct., 1934.
DUNN, C. de S.—Served in South Africa, 1901-1902 (medal and five clasps); S.A.C., 1903-1906; camel constab., Somaliland, 1912.

DUNN, J. S., B.Sc.—B. 1902; ed. Secondary Schl., Blyth, Northumberland and Univ. of Reading; B.Sc. (Hons.), London and Reading; inspr., schla., Gold Coast, Sept., 1926; engng. chem., P.W.D., G. Coast, 1936.

DUNNING, HON. CHARLES AVERY.—B. 1886; came to Canada, 1902; ed. pub. schls.; apptd. R. comsnr. by Saskatchewan govt. to investigate question of agrl. credit and grain marketing, Europ., 1913; mem., Canadian coun. of agr., 1911-16; chmn., Saskatchewan comte., victory loan, 1917-18-19; mem., Canada food bd. as dir., food production, Canada, 1918; prov. treas., Sask., 1916; min. of rlys., 1917; do., telephones, 1918, and min., agr., 1919; premier, Sask., 1922; pres., coun., prov., treas. and min. of rlys., 1929; min., rlys. and canals in Dom. govt., 1926; el. for Regina by acclmn. after assuming office; resigned, 1926; re-el. to H. of C., g.e., 1926; min., rlys. and canals, 1926; re-el. by acclmn., 1926; Canadian del. to L. of N., 1928; min. of finance, 1929; resigned with lib. govt., 1930; re-apptd. min. of finance, 1935.
DUNSTAN, HON. ALBERT ARTHUR, M.L.A.—B. 1882; ent. Victorian parl't., 1920; min., lands and forests, 1932-35; premier and treas., Victoria, 1935.

DUNSTAN, SIR WYNDHAM ROWLAND, K.C.M.G. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—M.A. (Oxon.); LL.D. (Aberdeen); F.R.S.; ed. Bedford schl.; lec. and demonstr. of chem. Oxford, 1884-86; prof. of chem., Pharm. Soc., 1886-96; lec. on chem., St. Thomas' Hosp., 1892-1900; sec., chem. soc., 1903-5; vice-pres., 1903-6; pres. section of chem. and agric. science, Brit. Assoc., 1906; mem. of coun., Royal Soc., 1904-7; mem. of advy. comte. on trop. agric., C.O., 1908, vice-pres., Internat. assoc. of trop. agric., 1907; pres., 1910; pres., Internat. Congress trop. agric., London, 1914; mem. comm'l. intell. comte., Bd. of T., 1900-6; dir., scientific and tech. dept., Impl. Inst., 1896-1903; dir., Impl. Inst., from 1903 to 1923; reported on agric. resources of Cyprus for C.O. in 1904, and of Asia Minor in 1907 (reports presented to parl'mt.); visited Ceylon at request of colonial govt., 1910, and again in 1913; visited Newfoundland at request of colonial govt., 1914; arranged and supervised govt. min. surveys in Ceylon, N. and S. Nigeria and Nasawland (reports presented to parl'mt.); author of numerous scientific papers in the Phil. Trans. and Proceedings of Royal Soc., chem. soc., etc., and of tech. reports relating to the utilisation of the resources of the colonies, including "Cotton Cultivation in the Brit. Empire and in Egypt," 1904, "Brit. Cotton Cultivation," 1908; edited "Tech. Reports and Scientific Papers," Impl. Inst., 1903; "Selected Reports," Impl. Inst., 1909 (presented to Parl'mt.); "Reports on work of Impl. Inst., 1906-14" (presented to Parl'mt.); "Report on present position of Cotton Cultivation," 1910; editor, Impl. Inst. Handbooks on Commercial Resources of Tropics; author of article, "Rubber," in last edition "Ency. Brit."

DUPLESSIS, STEPHANEUS HENDRIX.—B. 1885; mast., high ct., Windhoek (S.W.A.), Feb., 1920; ast. mast., sup. ct., Bloemfontein (O.F.S.), July, 1926; ditto, Capetown (C.P.), Dec., 1926; mast., sup. ct., Bloemfontein (O.F.S.), Oct., 1931.

DURANLEAU, HON. ALFRED, P.C. (Can.). LL.L., K.C., M.P.—B. 1871; ed. at Ste. Marie de Monnier; Laval Univ.; mem., Laurier divn., Montreal, 1923-27; lsc. el. H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1930; minister of marine in Bennett cabinet, Aug., 1930; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office, Aug., 1930; judge, sup. ct., prov. of Quebec, 1935.

DURUTY, CHARLES ERIC.—B. 1892; war serv. with 23rd London Regt., (Artists Rifles) and The Royal Warwickshire Regt.; awarded M.C.; served audit dept. and pol., Trinidad, 1911-21; ast. consnr., pol., Gold Coast, 1921; commr., pol., 1927.

DUSSEK, ORMONDE THEODORE, B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and Univ. of London; B.A., London, 1909; mem. of convocation; senr. ast. mast., Alleyne's Grammar Schl., Uxbridge, 1909-11; senr. ast., mast., High Schl., Malacca, 1912-14; prin., Malay Training Coll., Malacca, 1914-19; prin., Sultan Idris Training Coll., F.M.S., 1920; in addn., ast. dir. of educn. (Malay), and examr. in Malay, Mar., 1924.

DU TOIT, PETRUS JOHANN, Ph.D. (Zurich), Dr. Med. Vet. (Berlin), B.A. (Cape), F.R.S. (S. Africa).—Senr. vety. offr., vety. research divn., Vryburg, Dec., 1919; sub-dir., vety. educn. and research, Pretoria, July, 1920; dep. dir., vety. educn. and research, Pretoria, July, 1921; dir., vety. services, Pretoria, Apr., 1927; dean of faculty and prof. trop. diseases and protozoology, Univ. of S. Africa, 1927; visited Nigeria at request of C.O. to rept. on stock problems, 1927; del. to Imp. Agr. Research Confce., London, 1927; chmn., 6th Pan-African Vety. Confce., Pretoria, 1929; dir., vety. services and animal industry, 1929; del., internat. vety. cong., New York, 1934.
DU TOIT, STEPHANUS FRANCOIS, LL.B.—B. 1897; ed. Victoria Coll. Stellenbosch and Univ. of C.T.; translator, Union senate, 1920; gent. usher of black rod, 1926; clk. ast., 1930.

DUTTON, ERIC ALDHelm TORLIGH, O.B.E. (1930), M.A.—Ed., Hurstpierpoint, and Keble Coll., Oxford; major, W. Yorks Regt.; served in Gallipoli (ment. in des.). temp. app't., Basutoland adminstr., 1918-19; priv. sec. to gov. of Uganda, 1920-22; priv. sec. to gov., Kenya, 1922-30; sec., high consnr., Zanzibar, 1922-25; priv. sec. to high consnr., transport, Kenya and Uganda, 1925; prin. ast. ch. sec., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1930; ast. ch. sec. in 1931, 1932 and 1934; gov't. dep. on various occasions, 1932-35; chmn., finance com-n., 1932.

DUTTON, ERNEST GODFREY.—B. 1886; clk., Impl. mil. rly., Apr., 1902; clk., high consnr., for S. Africa, Apr., 1903 to May, 1916; Swaziland service, Apr., 1907; clk. to ast. consnr., Maseru, Basutoland, June, 1916; ch. clk., master of ct. and registr., July, 1919; ast. gov't. sec., June to Dec., 1923 and Feb. to Oct., 1924; ast. consnr., 1924; dist. consnr., 1936.
DUTTON, STEPHEN BERNARD.—B. 1881; served in stores dept., C.S.A.K., 1902-3; served in Zululand rebellion, 1906; comptroller of stores, Basutoland, Oct., 1906.

DYE, W. H., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.D.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), cert., Lond. S.H. and T.M. (Distine).—B. 1889; R.A.M.C., 1914-25; surg. specialist, 1918-20; N. Persian forces memorial med., for research, 1924 and 1927; seconded, Nyasaland Aug., 1921-Apr., 1925; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

DYER, ALEXANDER WEEK, M.A.—B. 1910; ed. Dumfries Acad., Andrews Univ.; 1st cl. hon., c. 1914; Samuel Coll., Cambridge; cadet, prov. adminstr. Inq. Territory, June, 1934; admistr. sec., Cyprus, Apr., 1935; ast. ens., 1935; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1935.

DYER-MELVILLE, STONE ORCHARD M.I.E.E., S.A.—B. 1885; ed. Cin. schl.; ast. dir., elec. and wireless, Dec., 1911; ag. dir., Apr. to Nov., 1915 to Apr., 1916 and from Dec., 1925; immigr. offr., Sept. to Dec., 1925; director, Jan. to Dec., 1919; ag. dir., and wireless tel. depa., for surv., 1925-32; ag. D.P.W. and electricity, 1933; temp. mem., leg. com., Jan., 1934.

DYKE, HAMILTON WILLIAM, C.B., M.B., Ch.B., Glasgow, 1896—B. 1886; ed. African Coll., Cape Town and Glasgow; housephys., Glasgow Royal Infirmary; Glasgow Western Infirmary, Glasgow Inst. and Glasgow Royal Hosp. for succ. med., offr., Maseru, Basutoland, 1915; capt., R.A.M.C., 1915-19; serv., med., 1915-19; S.M.O., S.A. Native Le Rouen area, 1919; surg. spe., Jerusalem and gov't. of Palestine; med. offr., Bechuana Land Prot., Basutoland, 1935; author of "An Scurvy among South African Natives," Lancet, 1917; "A case of Madura Fever," S. African Med. Jnl.
DYKE, KENNETH H., M.B., Ch.B., B. 1892; med. offr., Basutoland, 1924.

DYSON, EDWARD TREVOR.—B. 1892; Ruthin Gram. Schl., U.C.W., Aberystwyth and Jesus Coll., Oxford (B.A.); ast. civ. ser., Nov., 1910; office ast., N.W. Prov., Sept., 1912; pol. mag., N.W. Prov., Sept., 1913; addnl. ast. col. sec., May, 1918; pd. ast., 3rd ast. col. sec., May, 1918; pd. ast., N.E., Sept., 1920; ast. gov't. ast., N.E., 1921; ditto, Jaffna, Dec., 1924; dist. Matara, Mar., 1926; ast. gov't. ast., May, 1926; ast. gov't. ast., Anuradhapura, 1928; do., Jaffna, Nov., 1930; do., Colombo, Mar., 1934; chmn., munici. com., Colombo, addn.; do., N.E., Jan., 1935.

EAGLESOME, SIR JOHN EGAN, C.B.E. (1916), C.M.G. (1905).—M. Inst. C.E., P.W.D., rlya., India, 1890; recd. Indian gov't., Kohjak tunnel, 1892; bridge, 1900; services lent to C.O., 1900; as D.P.W., Northern Nigeria; Baro-Kano rly. from 1907; dir. of works, Nigeria, 1912; ret., 1919.

EALES, SHIRLEY, C.M.G. (1933); (1928), O.B.E. (1922).—B. 1883; served out S. African war, 1899-1902; 2nd cls. gov't. 's office, Transvaal and O.R.C. July, 1904; transid. to Bechuana Land Prot., Basutoland and Swaziland, 1921-22; ag. ast. imp. ed., Nov., 1921 and Mar.-Sept., 1922; ast. imp., 1923; ag. imp. sec., in 1925, 1927, 1929; title changed to admistr. sec. to high comm., 1933; mem. for Boch. Prot. on rly. com., Earle, Hugh J. M.—B. 1896; ed., Essex Regt., 1918; ent. dept. of landau., B. Guiana, 2nd Aug., 1921; 6th secretariat, 1st Mar., 1922; sec. for sp. connexion with Georgetown disturbance. D.C. to O.A.G., May-Nov., 1925; cadet, c. serv., Nigeria, 1927.

LE, Sir Lionel, K.C.B. (1916); C.B., C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1866; ed. at Marl., of Göttingen and Paris, and Merton Coll., 1; ast. sec. to royal comm. on Paris, 1898-1900; ag. 2nd sec. of embassy, priv. sec. to lord pres. of the coun. (Earl we), 1907; priv. sec. to S. of S. for the (Earl of Crewe), Apr., 1908; priv. sec. to S. for the colonies (Mr. Lewis Harcourt), 1910; sec. to H.M. Office of Works, ret.

STER, B. H. M., C.B.E. (1936), M.B.E., (Hons.) Lond.—B. 1893; ed. Christ's Coll., Ley; served R.N. Dn., 1917; Scot's Guards, 1918; Ampleforth Coll., O.T.C., 1919-23; ar., St. Mary's Coll., St. Lucia, 1923; dir., headmnr., Grenada, Dec., 1930-Aug., 1931; adviser, educn. comttee, 1931; dir., educn., ica, Apr., 1932.

STWOOD, CHRISTOPHER GILBERT.—B. scholar of Eton, 1917 and of Trinity Coll., rd., 1923; apptd. to C.O. as ast. prin. after et. exam., 2nd Nov., 1927; seconded for serv. te. sec. to high commr., Palestine, 11th Feb., prin., 18th Mar., 1935.

DEN, WILLIAM SYDENHAM.—B. 1887; ed. on and Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb., tripos, 1909; cadet, S.S., 1911; 2nd lieut., 1915; lieut., Salonika Front; wounded and w., Bulgaria; dist. offr., F.M.S., 1920; dep., rubber expts., mag., Johore, and recr. goff concession, in addn., July, 1924; regis., ct., Johore and 1st mag., Johore Bharu, 1925; ast. adviser, Segamat, Jan., 1926; cls., II., Nov., 1928; ast. treas., Penang, 1928; dist. offr., Upper Perak, Nov., offr., cls. I.B., Apr., 1931; commr., lands, Mar., 1932; cls. I.A., 1935.

CCLES, LAUNCELOT WILLIAM GREGORY.—B. 1890; ed. Michaelhouse Sch., Natal and oester Coll., Oxford; ast. survr., N. Rhos., Oct., 1912; on war serv., 1914-20; ast. r., diagrams, Apr., 1921; ag. ch. survr. from, 1922; dep. dir., surveys and exam., dir., and surveys, Oct., 1924; ag. dir., surveys, 1925, 1928 and 1931; commr., lands, mines local govt. and M.L.C., 1933 and 1936.

CCLES, WILLIAM HUNTER.—Ed. St. Paul's, London; clk., educn. office, Trinidad, 1st, 1907; elk. to commrs. of currency, e., 1908; customs, Jan., 1911; temp. sub. ofp., Nov., 1914 to Feb., 1915; on mily. serv., afterwards capt., 1915-19; returned to cust. nidad, July, 1919; on sp. mil. duty to B. iana and Trinidad, Aug. to Oct.; ast. recvr., Gambia, Oct., 1919; relinquished comman., h rank of capt., Sept., 1921; A.D.C. and sec. to Mr. G. Gwyn, Sept., 1921 and to C. R. M. Workman, Sept., 1921 to Jan., 22; ag. clk., exec. coun., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 22; offr.-in-charge, posts and tels. dept., Feb., July, 1924; offr.-in-charge, cust. on various occasions between 1923 and 1928; ast. comptr., st., Sierra Leone, 1928; ag. comptr., cust., various occasions, 1929-32; comptr., cust., 32.
ECKBO, NILS BONNIVIE (M.F. Yale).—Offr. ch., timber seasoning investigations, Pretoria, 19; apptd. to perm. estab. as timber investiga- ons offr., 1924; ch., Forest Products Inst., 32.

ECKEL, CAPT. PERCY.—B. 1893; audit dept., Trinidad, Jan., 1912; seconded, W. O., 1915-20; wounded, France, 1917; sub-inpr. Trinidad constab., 1920; ast. commr., pol., Gold Coast, 1921; commr., pol., 1927.

EDGINTON, EDWIN.—B. 1886; sorting clk. and telegraphist, Imp. serv., 1908; resigned, Jan., 1912; re-apptd., Dec., 1912; dist. survr., posts and tels., G. Coast, Oct., 1913; senr. dist. survr., Feb., 1917; served as offr. in ch. posts and tels., Br. Zone, Togoland, Nov., 1916 to Aug., 1917; postmnr., Cyprus, Feb., 1920; P.M.G., Trinidad, 1922; divnl. survr., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, 1927; contr., ditto, 1930.

EDMONDS, B. D.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., 7th Aug., 1914 to 5th Dec., 1919; awarded Mily. Med.; temp. clk., C.O., 2nd Feb., 1920; apppd. after compet.exam., cler.offr., C.O., 5th Nov., 1921; clerical offr., higher grade, 3rd Feb., 1928.

EDMONDS, EDWARD REGINALD.—B. 1901; matric., London Univ., June, 1921; entered C.O., 1917; apppd. after exam., ast. clk., 23rd Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; clk., P.W.D., Uganda, 1926; higher grade cler. offr., C.O., 31st July, 1929.

EDMUNDS, HERBERT GLYN.—B.1900; ed. Swansea and Univ. Coll., London; med. offr., Falkland Is., June, 1931; lieut., Falkland Is. Def. Force, July, 1931; ag. prin. med. offr., Mar. to Oct., 1932; M.S.A. (Lond.), 1932; ag. senr. med. offr., Dec., 1934 to Feb., 1935; med. offr., Nigeria, 1936.
EDMUNDSON, K., M.B., Ch.B. (Liv.).—B. 1903; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1928.

EDWARD, DOUGLAS SANDEMAN, A.M.I.Struct.E., A.I.A.A., M.Inst.R.A., Chart.Struc.Engnr.—B. 1897; ed. Grove Acad., Broughty Ferry, Tech. Coll. Dundee (Armitstead med.), and St. Andrews Univ.; mily. serv., R.E.s, 1914-19; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

EDWARDS, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR ALFRED HAMILTON MACKENZIE, C.B.—Served in 1st Dragoon Guards, Jan., 1883, to Apr., 1892; adjutant, Behar Light Horse Volunteers, India, Apr., 1892 to Apr., 1897; 1st and 5th Dragoon Guards and Imperial Light Horse, Apr., 1897 to Oct., 1900; Hazara (N.-W. Frontier, India), 1888; ast. prov. marshal (ment. in des., medal and clasp); commanded Imperial Light Horse, S. African war, Nov., 1899 to June, 1900; dist. commr., Potchefstroom and Krugersdorp dists., June to Oct., 1900; commanded "A" division, S.A.C., Oct., 1900, to end of war (ment. in des., Queen's medal, 4 clasps, King's medal, 2 clasps) substantive lt.-col. in the army, dated 29th Nov., 1900; commdt., Transvaal volunteers; left the service, 1906; A.A.G., Northern com., India, 1906-7; chief constable, metrop. police, 1907-12; commt.-gen., Rhodesian forces, 1912; ret. 1st Apr., 1923.

EDWARDS, CAPT. DAVID, M.A. (1912), L.I.R. (Aberdeen), 1914.—Temp. offr., R. Artv., 1915-20; served at home, N.W. Frontier of India, German E. Africa and Mesopotamia; Scottish solr., 1921; ast. dist. commr., Kenya, 1921; ag. regisr. sup. ct., Kenya, 1921; regisr., sup. ct., Kenya, 1924; called to Scottish bar, 1924; res. mag., Kenya, 1928; relieving pres., dist. ct., Palestine, 1935; pres., dist. ct., 1936.
EDWARDS, ERNEST JAMES.—B. 1886; astt. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Nov., 1912; head mast., Ellis Kadooria schl., 1935; senr. mast., educn. dept., 1934; J.P., 1935.

EDWARDS, THOMAS.—B. 1879; clk., dept. native affrs., Pretoria, 1901; sub. N.C., 1907; junr. S.N.C., 1912; mag., 1923; nat. comsnr., S.W.A., 1926; ch. N.C. for S.W.A., 1930; senr. gr. mag., Middelburg (Tvl.), 1931; spec. "C" mag., Pietersburg, 1935; under-sec., lands, 1935.

EDWARDS, WILFRID HENRY, D.I.C., F.E.S.—B. 1897; lect., zoology and entomology, Coll. of Agr., Mauritius, 1924; ag. entomologist, botanist and mycologist, dept. of agr., Mauritius, 1928; govt. entomologist, Jamaica, May, 1929.

EDWARDS, WILLIAM STUART, C.M.G. (1935), K.C.—B. 1880; called to the bar, Ontario, Sept., 1909; legal offr., dept. of justice, Canada, Nov., 1910; sec. of dept. of justice, 1913; ast. dep. min., of justice, 1914; dep. min. of justice, 1924.

EVERTON, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1838; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; cadet, S. Stltltns., Oct., 1850; astt. to the Chinese prot., Jan., 1881; mag., Singapore, Jan., 1881; passed final exam., Malay, May, 1882; collr. of land rev., Penang and P.W., Jan., 1883; 2nd mag. of pol., Penang, May, 1883; also official assignee under the Bankruptcy Ordinance (Sept., 1882); a J.P. and coroner for S. Stltltns., and a visiting justice for Penang; offr. in char., Sungei Ujong, July, 1893; off. assignee and registr. of deeds, Singapore, June, 1896; inspr. of prisons, S. Stltltns., May, 1897; dep.-pres. of mun. comsnr., Singapore, Mar., 1897; 1st mag. Penang, Apr., 1897; 1st mag., Singapore, Oct., 1898, but continued to act as res. coun.; res., Neg. Semb., Aug., 1902; high comsnr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1903; and gov., Lagos, July, 1904; gov. and comdr.-in-chief of the amalgamated colony and prot. of S. Nigeria, 1st Mar., 1906; represented W. African Colonies and Prots. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; gov., B. Guiana, Mar., 1912; ret., Mar., 1917.

EGG, RICHARD TYRER.—B. 1886; called to bar, Inner Temple, June, 1913; admitted to practice before sup. ct., Br. Guiana, 1913; ag. mag., Oct., 1919; stip. mag., 2nd grade, 1921; ditto, 1st grade, 1924; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., for various periods, 1925-35; pol. mag., Gold Coast, May, 1929; ag. puisne judge, various periods, 1933-36.

ELDER, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, O.B.E. (1925).—B. 1881; ed. Reading and Edinburgh; graduated M.R.C.V.S., 1902; F.R.C.V.S., 1914; served in S. African war as civ. vet. surg. with army vet. serv., 1902-3; vet. offr., Swaziland admin., May 1903; prin. vet. offr., 1919; agrl. offr., 1928.

ELIAS, DAVID HENRY, C.M.G. (1936), M.C.—B. 1882; G.W.R., Eng. May, 1899-May, 1902; S. African Rlys., May, 1902-Sept., 1914; S. African Mounted Rifles, German S.W. Africa, S. African Inty. Brig., France, M.C., Sept., 1914-July, 1919; S. African Rlys., July, 1919; sup't. line, Kenya and Uganda Rly., Sept., 1922; ditto, Nigerian Rly., Sept., 1925; dep. gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., Sept., 1931; gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., May, 1933.

ELKINS, MAJOR WALTER HENRY, O.B.E. (Mily.).—B. 1883; ed. Cirencester Grammar Schl.; S. African and Rhodesia Rlys.; Mesopotamia Mil. Rlys.; reorganisation rlys. Trans Caucasus; dep. dir., rly. transport, S. Russia, 1919; dep. traffic man., F.M.S. Rly., 1928; ag. traffic man., Dec. 1928 to Aug. 1929; divnl. supt., F.M.S. rlys., Sept., 1932; ag. transpnt. man., Apr., 1934.
ELLENBERGER, VIVIAN FREDERIC.—Ck. to res. comsnr., Bech. Prot., 1915; passed 2nd grade Seewana exam., 1919; sub-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1920; 1st grade ck., Apr., 1928; res. mag., May, 1934.

ELLIOTT, COLIN FRASER (Fraser), K.C., B.I. Sc., C.E.—Solr., income tax br., dept. of finance, Canada, July, 1919; ast. comsnr., income tax dept. of national rev., May, 1926; comsnr., income tax, July, 1932.

ELLIOTT, FRANK FIELD.—Temps. reg. registry, survr., O.F.S., Mar., 1903; ast. registry, survr., May, 1903; chief draughtsmn and comptlr., Apr., 1912; add. prof. ast., Apr., 1914; prof. ast., Sept., 1917; survr.-gen., O.F.S., Nov., 1930; do., Cape Prov., Dec., 1931.

ELLIOTT, HON. JOHN CAMERELL, R.C.L., D.C.L.K.C., (Middlesex West).—B. 1872; Ed. Keifrid pub. schl., Strathroy C.I., Glencoe Schl. and Trinity Univ.; B.C.L., Glasgow Ed., 1898; D.C.L., Toronto Univ., 1903; barrister-at-law; elec. Ontario legis., g.e., 1908; re-elec. 1911 and 1914; first elec. H. of C., Canada, Oct., 1905; mem., Privy Coun. and app't. asl. of lab., 1926; re-elec. by-el., Mar., 1926; min. health and soldiers' civ. re-estab., Apr., 1926; resigned, June, 1926; re-elec., g.e., Sept., 1926; min., pub. wks., King Cabinet, Sept., 1926; re-elec. by accl., Nov., 1926; re-elec., g.e., July, 1928; resigned portfolio after defeat of liberal admin. at polls, Aug., 1930.
ELLIOTT, PHILIP JOHN.—B. 1892; ast. crown agents' office, Dec., 1911; ast. head dept., July, 1921; dep. head, stores dept., Nov., 1921.

ELLIS, CAPT. FREDERICK ROBBINS, M.C.—Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1924.—B. 1872; ed. Blundell's Schl., Tiverton; 2nd lieut. 4th batt., Duke of Cornwall's Light Inf. M.C.; served in Mesopotamia with 2nd Bore Regt., 1915-16; prisoner of war on capitulation of Kut-el-Amara, 1916; escaped from capt., 1918; W.O. staff, 1919; Siberia, 1919; pass. solrs. final exam.; junr. admin. offr., S. Laos, 1920; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Jan. to Apr., 1921; J.P., 1921; ag. astt. col. sec., 1921; ast. comsnr., pol., 1921; ag. dist. comsnr., hearth dist., 1921-23; dist. comsnr., Aug., 1928.

ELLIS, JOHN ALFRED.—B. 1900; ed. "Micah house," Natal, tech. coll., Durban; on war sec. E. African campaign, 1917-18; clearance sec. E. African waracts., Kenya and Jubaland, Jan. 1920 to Apr., 1924; examr., acts. auditors, Kenya, Apr., 1924; ag. astt. audtr., Nov., 1924; June and Mar. to Nov., 1928; astt. acts. P.W.D., Apr., 1929; ag. dep. ch. acctnt., 1929 and 1935; acctnt., P.W.D., Cyprus, Apr., 1935.

ELLIS, JOHN MEDLICOTT, C.B.E. (1935).—B. 1895; ed., Haileybury and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schl.); served Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1914-19; ast. sec., ch. sec's. office, Cyprus, Jan., 1919; passed exam. in mod. Greek, March, 1920; Turkish, July, 1921; ch. astt. sec., July, 1921; ag. ch. sec. on various occasions, 1923-25; ast. sec., Falkland Is., Sept., 1926 to Aug., 1927; ast. sec., Aug., 1927; ag. gov., in 1927, 1928, 1929 and 1931; local audr. from May, 1930 to Feb., 1931; regisr.-gen. and mag. from July, 1931; Jan., 1931; col. sec. and regisr.-gen., Gambia, Oct., 1934; astt. ch. sec., Nyasaland Prov., 1935; ag. ch. sec., May-June, 1936; ag. prf. comsnr., July, 1936.
ELLISON, EDWARD WILFRID.—B. 1906; ed. Shrewsbury and Queen's Coll., Cambrian cadet, Gold Coast, 1930; ast. dist. comsnr., N. Elphinstone, Sir H. G., Bart.—ast. dist. comsnr., Kenya, June, 1921.

ELTON, MAJOR HUMPHREY DE CARTERET, D.—B. 1883; ass't supt., chandu monop., slangor, and superver. cust., Kuala Lumpur, ov., 1920; asst. supt., chandu monop., F.M.S., m., 1921; ar. ass't supt., F.M.S., May, 1923; pt., chandu monop., Perak, May, 1925; supt., cise., Ipoh, Aug., 1930; ar. supt., trade and ist., N. Sembilan, June, 1931; do., Selangor E., ar., 1931; dep. chmn., stdg. advisory comtee., use of opium in Malaya, Feb., 1933; ar. supt., ade and cust., Ipoh, Apr., 1934; ag. dep. mntr., cust. and excise, Perak, Feb.-Mar., 1936.

EMBURY, BRIG.-GEN. HON. JOHN FLETCHER COPOD, C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1916), K.C.—B. 75; ed. Toronto Univ. and Osgoode Hall, Toronto; served European War, 28th Can. Inf. Bde., 2nd Inf. Bde., Can. Contingent, r. G.H.Q., France (Canadian sec.), 1914-19; ent. in desps., three times, C.B., C.M.G.; judge King's Bench, Saskatchewan, 1918.
EMBURY, W. E.—B. 1898; on mil. serv., et., 1916 to Dec., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., ec., 1919; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., Nov., 1921.
EMERSON, HON. CHARLES H., K.C.—B. 1864; H.A., Newfoundland; elected for Burgeo and Poile, 1900, Fortune Bay, 1908 and 1909; mem. exec. coun. without portfolio, 1909; regis., supreme ct., 1917.

EMMENS, JOHN HOWARD.—B. 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the v. ser., and assigned to C.O., 11th Mar., 1901; inf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918; deptl. aast., 26th Jan., 1922; promoted ag. aast. prim., under clause 51, 9th Sept., 1929.

EMPSON, CHARLES.—B. 1898; ed. Harrow and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; R.F.A. (S.K.); joined Iraq civil admin., Aug., 1930; admnl. under sec. to high commr., Sept., 1921; under sec. (consular), July, 1922; H.M.'s consul, Baghdad, Dec., 1924; consul sec., July, 1927.
ENGLISH, JOSEPH SANDYS, B.A., M.D., L.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), M.L. (Rotunda Hosp., Dub.), F.C.O.G.—B. 1890; prof. of midwifery and gynaecology, King Edward VII. Coll. of medicine, Singapore, Jan., 1922.

ENGLISH, PATRICK THOMAS.—B. 1902; 5th lk., treasy., Montserrat, 1921; clk. to mag. and egr., 1924; ag. offr. in ch., prison, 1924; 3rd lk., treasy., 1925; ag. regr. sup. ct., 1925; jnr. lk. col., sec. 'office, Antigua, 1927; ag. clk. of couns., 1928 and 1930; ag. clk. to admnstr. and lk. of couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1930; ag. aast. col sec., Leeward Is., and clk., fed. couns., 1930; lk. and cust. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., 1931; ag. act., 1931-33; ag. sec. to govt., June-Aug., 1933.
ENGLISH, ROBERT.—B. 1888; aast. printer, Tanganyika Territory, 1919; senr. aast. do., 1925; jnt. printer, Sierra Leone, 1930; do., Nigeria, 1932.

EPSTEIN, ALEXANDER, M.B.E. (1937).—B. 1886; ed. Hebrew Inst., Liverpool; Jews Hosp., W. Norwood; inspr., dept. of commerce and industr., Palestine, 1920; dist. offr., Jerusalem-Jaffa dist., 1923; admnstr. offr., S. dist., 1925.

ERNST, NOEL EDWARD.—B. 1891; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1912; office aast. Jaffa kach, July, 1913; pol. mag., Jaffna and Kayta, Nov., 1914; pol. mag., Avisawella, June, 1915; aast. comsnr., excise, Nov., 1916; pol. mag., Gampola, Mar., 1918; office aast. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1918; pol. mag., Galle, Oct., 1924; dep. comsnr., stampa, Feb., 1928; comsnr., do., July, 1931; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Mar., 1933; aast. govt. agt., Kurunegala, May, 1934; dist. judge, Badulla, Dec., 1934; do., Kalutara, July, 1935.

ESPEUT, CLAUDE VIVIAN ARMIT, M.Inst., C.E.—B. 1875; ed. St. Paul's Sch., Lond. and Crystal Palace Sch. of Engng.; served, P.W.D., Jamaica; aast. engr., Lagos, govt. rly., 1900-01; dist. engr., Gold Coast Govt. rly., 1901; exec. engr., P.W.D. E. Africa Prot., June, 1905; ag. aast. D.P.W., 1908 and 1909; D.P.W., Uganda Prot., 1911; Uganda Volunteers, 1914-19; hon. lt.-col., 1916; miny of munitions while on leave, 1917; D.P.W., Jamaica, Aug., 1921; mem., leg. coun., D.P.W., F.M.S., Mar., 1923; mem., fed. coun.; chm. electrical hd. and others; mem., rlv. bd., immig. comm. and others; ret. July 1928; re-employed as eng. in ch., colonial development fund wks., St. Lucia, Oct., 1930.

ESPITALIER-NOEL, JOSEPH GEORGES.—B. 1894; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, May, 1916; ag. dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, 1919; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Sept., 1921; addnl. subat. procureur and advoc. gen., Dec., 1922; ag. subat. procureur and advoc. gen. on various occasions, 1924-29; ag. procureur and advoc. gen., Mar., 1929; ag. mast. and regis., supt. ot., Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1932 and Sept., 1932; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct. in 1932 and 1933.

KULER, HON. WILLIAM D. (Waterloo N.)—B. 1875; ed. at pub. schla., Berlin (Kitchener), High Schl., Toronto Normal Schl.; mayor of Berlin (Kitchener), 1913-14; pres., Kitchener bd. of trade, 1917; first elec. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1917; re-ele., g.e., 1921, 1925 and 1926; mem. privy coun. and apptd. min. of national rev. in King cabinet 1926; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office; re-elec., g.e., 1930; resigned portfolio with Mr. King's cabinet, 1930; re-elec. at g.e., 1935; min. of trade and commerce in King cabinet, Oct., 1935.

EVANS, ARTHUR ANTHONY, O.B.E., M.C., M.I.E.E., Lieut., Col., R. Corps of Signals (Res.)—Ed. Brodribb Acad., Carmarthen and Univ. of Wales, Cardiff; served in S. African War (Queen's med., 4 clasps); engr., branch, G.F.O. 1898-1914; R. E., signals, Gallipoli, Egypt; asst. dir., Army signals and ch. signal offr., Palestine and Syria, 1914-20; four ment. in desps., Order of White Eagle, 5th cls. with swords, O.B.R., M.C., Br. and Allies war meds., "1914-15" Star; ch. engr., Palestine posts and tels., 1920.

EVANS, BENJAMIN DAVIES, F.R.A.S.—B.1887; computer, Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1903; junior asst., Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1909; latast., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1912; on military service, 1917 to 1919; ag. ch. asst. for various periods in 1915, 1916, 1920, 1921, 1925, 1926 and 1929; mast. dir., 1932; ag. dir. for various periods in 1934 and 1935.

EVANS, EDWARD WALTER, R.A. (Oxon.), C.M.G. (1931).—B. 1890; ed. Marlborough and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; 1st class mods., 1st class lit. hum.; asst. dist. comsnr., Zanzibar Prot., Feb., 1915; seconded for mil. ser., June, 1917; temp. comsnr. in K.A.R.; ag. 1st asst. sec.; Zanzibar, 1919-20; asst. sec., Uganda, Jan., 1921; senr. asst. sec., June, 1923; ag. asst. ch. sec., July, 1924; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, Apr., 1925; ag. col. sec., Jan. to Mar., 1927; col. sec., Br. Honduras, June, 1927; admstd. govt., on two occasions; col. sec., Bermuda, 1929; col. sec., Mauritius, Apr., 1932; admstd. govt., Aug., 1932 to Apr., 1933 and Apr., 1936.

EVANS, FRANK DUDLEY, M.Inst., C.E.—B. 1883; ass't engr., P.W.D., F.M.S. 1907; malaria adv. bd., 1911; exec. engr., grade III, 1913; ag. regt. sergt. maj., M.S.V.R., 1917; 2nd lieut., do., 1917; engr., malaria adv. bd., 1919; ment. in despe., 1919; lieut., M.S.V.R., 1919; anti-malaria engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1921; awarded Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. med., 1921; ag. asst. D.P.W., 1924; ag. state engr., Negri Sembilan, 1924; ag. capt., M.S.V.R., 1924; visited anti-malaria wks., Palestine and Italy, 1926; D.P.W., Nigeria, 1926; ag. D.P.W., and M.L.C., Nigeria on various occasions 1926-35; malarial coman., health sect., L. of N., Geneva, 1928; mem. comtee. road v. rail transp., 1933; chmn., do., 1935; D.P.W. and M.L.C., 1935.

EVANS, SIR FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.V.O. (1912), C.M.G. (1881), C.V.O. (1903), C.B.E. (1918); Comdr. of Order of Isabel la Católica (Spanish), 1912; Knight Comdr., ditto, 1914.—B. 1849; entered the civ. ser., admny., Somerset house, Dec., 1867; transf. to sec.'s dept. at Whitehall, Jan., 1872; asst. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, May, 1878; priv. sec. to Sir S. Rowe, K.C.M.G., Feb., 1877, to Feb., 1881; ag. clk. of legis. coun., Sept., 1879; ag. col. sec., Dec., 1879 to Feb., 1880; ch. sec. and ch. of the staff to Sir S. Rowe during threatened hostilities with Ashanti, 1881; asst. col. sec., G. Coast, 1882; ag. collr., cust. and treas., Oct., 1882, to Jan., 1883; ag. col. sec., Aug. to Dec., 1883 and on other occasions; clk. legis. coun., Feb., 1882 to Apr., 1884; dep. gov., Lagos, July to Aug., 1883 and May, 1885 to Jan., 1886; dep. gov., G. Coast, Sept., 1883 and Apr., 1885; col. sec., Lagos, Jan., 1886; ag. adminstr., July, 1886; col. sec., G. Coast, 1887; Leeward Isl., 1888; Jamaica, 1895; ag. 1st cl. clk., C.O., Feb., 1900; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1901; ag. gov. on seven occasions; ret. 1914; ag. col. sec., Gibraltar, in 1915 and 1917.

EVANS, HON. SIR JOHN WM., KT. BACH. (1926), C.M.G. (1906)—B. 1855; M.L.A. for Kingborough, Tasmania, since 1890; warden and master warden of marine bd. of Hobart for many years; premier and min. of educn., Tasmania, July, 1904 to Oct., 1905; premier and treas., Oct., 1905, to May, 1906; premier and ch. sec., May, 1906 to June, 1909; speaker.

EVANS, LEONARD WYNNE, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., B.Sc., certif. L.S.T.M. (with distinc.).—B. 1891; med. offr., gen. hosp., S'pore, Jan., 1922; med. and health offr., P.W., north and central, June, 1923; ch. med. offr., Kelantan, Jan., 1929; do., S'pore, Sept., 1935.

EVANS, SAMUEL ALBAN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1899; ed. Mountjoy Schol. and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; war serv., R.A.F., cadet, 1918; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1921; asst. vety. pathologist, May, 1926.

EVELYN, EDWARD ARTHUR,(jr.)—B. 1904; ed. St. Kitts Grammar Schol.; supernumy. rev. offr., St. Kitts, 1922; ch. clk., post office, 1927; dep. harmbr. and cashier, treasy., 1928; gov't. offr., Eastern dist. and clk. to mag., dist. F, Dominica, 1930; ag. appt. in treasy. and post office, Tortola, Virgin Isl., 1932; ag. mag., dist. F, also adml. mag., dist. E, 1933; J.P., 1934; prin., adminstr. office, clk. of couns. and mem., pub. treas. comtee., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1935.

EVESON, STEPHEN WILFRED, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (R.C.P. & S., F.R.S.S., Certif., Schl. of Trop., Med. and Hyg., B. 1900; clin. asst., ven. dis. clin. (L.C.C.), Lond. Hosp., 1924-26; med. offr., S.S., July, 1926; health offr., Malacca, Feb., 1926 and Dec., 1927; do., Kedah, Sept., 1927; do., S'pore, Dec., 1931; health offr., B. Padang and L. Perak, Sept., 1934.

EWART, F. K.—B. 1902; ed. Boteler Schol., Warrington and Rossall Schol., Williams Mem. Exhn.; schol., Exeter Coll., Oxford, 1920; prn. O.U.S.C., 1923; B.A., 1923 (chem. pt. I); F.C.S., 1923; 2nd chem. pt., 1924; mem. Inner Temple, 1925; asst. mast., King's Coll., Hongkong, 1926; M.A., 1927.

EYBERS, GEORG VON WELFLING.—B. 1878; ed. Boy's High Schol., Beaufort West, Victora Coll., Stellenbosch, Univ. of Amsterdam; King's Coll., London; B.A. (Cape); M.A. Litt. (Lond.); prn., Graaf Reiniet Coll. and Roet Volkskool, 1919-26; mem., Cape prov. educn. comsn., 1926-27; inspr., schls., 1927; under sec. educn., Aug., 1927; prof. asst., Union educn. dept., July, 1934; author, various works on African hist. and travel.

EYRE, JOHN COLES, B. Sc. (Agric.), D.I.C.—B. 1903; ed. Diocesan Coll., S. Africa; B.N. (Agr.), plant pathology, Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1928; D.I.C., plant pathology, Royal Coll., 1930; Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., 1931; tech. offr., Empire Marketing Bd., 1929; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1932.

EYRE-SMITH, CAPT. ST. JOHN ROBERT, M.C.—B. 1888; ed. St. Edmunds Coll., Ware; wrangler with R.A.S.C., France and Germany, 1915-16; Turkey, 1919-20; mem. in despe.; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. comsnr., 1922; asst. sec., native affrs., 1931.

EZECHIEL, SIR PEGGY HUBERT, K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1875; B.A. Exon., 1889 (pt. 14); B.A., London, 1894; major sec., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1896; 4th wrangler and B.A., Camb., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd clk. C.O., 11th Oct., 1898; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1903; sec. to crown agts. for the colonies, May, 1905; mem. of Fair Wages Advancement Comte., 1909-1920; sec. to Colonial Economic Development Comte., 1919-20; sec. and mem. of African Currency bd., 1919 (resigned secretarieship, 1921); crown agt. for the cols., Dec., 1920; sec. of colonial scholars, 1921; rep. of C.O. on educn. of Br. Empire Exhibtn., 1921-25; chm. Palestine currency bd., 1926; mem., Hong Kong currency comsn., 1931.

FAGAN, HON. MARK, M.L.C.—B. 1873; ed. Waratah public schl., Tasmania; appd. mem. N.Z. leg. encl., 1930; represented N.Z. at Empire Parly. Conf., London, 1935; leader leg. encl. and mem., exec. encl., 1935.

FAHEY, FRANCIS JOSEPH.—B. 1878; ed. St. Michael's Coll., Toronto, Canada; printer; arrived S. Africa, 1898; mem., prov. coun., Natal, 1920-23; prov. exec., 1924; joined bd. of trade and industries, Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1924; re-apptd., Apr., 1930; chmn., Mar., 1932.
FAIR, HON. ARTHUR, LL.B. (N.Z.), K.C.—B. 1889; ed. state schls., Nelson Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; crown solr., 1921; prin. lab. offr., 1923; solr.-gen., 1925; judge, sup. ct., N.Z., 1934.

FAIR, Lieut.-Col. J. G., D.S.O.—B. 1864; aid. Army (21st Hussars—now Lancers) 1885; capt., 1892; major, 1900; staff off. to Gen. Gatacre during Atbara campaign, 1898 (desp., Khedive's medal and clasp); with 21st Lancers at Omdurman (Queen's medal, clasp, 4th cls. Medjidieh); adjut., cavalry depot, Canterbury, 1899-1900; S. African war, 1901-2 (desp., D.S.O., Queen's medal, 5 clasps); commanded div. S.A.C., Eastern Transvaal, 1901-4; ditto, O.R.C., 1904-8; nom. mem. of I.C.C., 1906; res. comnr., and somt.-gen., Southern Rhodesia, 1908-1911; dep. asst. dir. of remounts since 1911.

FAIRBAIRN, Sir George, K.T. Bach. (1926).—B. 1855; ed. Geelong Grammar Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; M.L.A. for Toorak, Victoria, Oct., 1903 to Sept., 1906; M.H.R. for Fawkner, 1906-13; Commonwealth Senator for Victoria, May, 1917; agt.-gen. for Victoria,
FAIRBAIRN, H., M.D., Ch.B. ( Glas.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1898; milly. serv. 1917- 19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925; sleeping sickness offr., 1935.

FAIRCLOUGH, F.R.—B. 1895; apptd., after compt. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil serv. and assigned to C.O., Aug., 1914; on military service from 13th Dec., 1915 to 9th March, 1919; priv. sec. to Sir James Stevenson, 12th Aug., 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 7th July, 1923; staff offr., 16th July, 1933.

FAIRLEY, WILLIAM, B.A., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1891; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.) B.A., (mech. science tripes 1913; 6th Bn. E. Surrey Rgt., 1914; 1st K.T., 1917-19; asst. engr., P.W.D., Kenya, Dec., 1919; ag. exec. engr., Mar. to Dec., 1920; dist. engr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, Feb., 1922; ag. ass't, D.P.W. in 1923 and 1924; exec. engr., P.W.D., Kenya, Aug., 1925; ch. engr., N. Rhodesia, 1935; temp. mem., leg. coun., 1936.

FAIRWEATHER, Eugene Ernest, M.A., LL.B.—B. 1884; ed. at King's and Acadia Unv. and Dalhousie Law Schl., N.S.; asst. solr., dept. of rlys. and canals, 1912; solr., 1914; counsel, 1919; asst. gen. counsel, Can. Nat. rlys., 1922.

FAIRWEATHER, James, N.D.A.U.D.A.— B. 1896; ed. Montrose Acad., Aberdeen Univ., N. of Scot. Coll. of Agric.; A.F.O., Malacca, Apr., 1921; agr. mun. sec., Malacca in addn., May, 1927- Feb., 1928; agrl. offr., Johore, Dec., 1931; offc'tg. prin. agrl. offr., Apr., 1933; agrl. offr., Johore Cent., Feb., 1935.

FAIRWEATHER, William Gemmel, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1886; ed. at Univ. of St. Andrews (B.Sc.), A.M.I.C.E.; asst. surr., N.W. Rhodesia, Sept., 1910; ch. asst. surr. and examiner of diagrams, Nov., 1917; ag. ch. surr. on various occasions, 1916 to 1920; ch. surr., 1920; title altered to dir. surveys, Oct., 1924; mem., leg. coun., 1928-32, 1933 and 1934; ag. comnr., lands and mines, 1936.

FALCONER, John.—B. 1898; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll. and Aberdeen Univ.; M.A. Aberdeen (1st cls. hons.); cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1922; asst. collr., land rev., K. Lumpur, June, 1924; dep. regisr., titles, Selangor, June, 1924; second ast. advr., Muar, Feb. 1926; offr., cls. IV., Apr., 1928; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Aug., 1930; do., Raub, July, 1931; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; ast. advr., B. Pahat, June,

FALK, Edward Morris, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Bradford Grammar Schl., on the Continent and at Victoria Univ.; ast. dist. comnr., S. Nigeria, 1907; West African G.S. Medal, 1909; dist. comnr., 2nd grade, 1913; interned as prisoner of war in Germany, 1914; escaped and ret'd. to England, 1915; resumed duty, Nigeria, 1915; res., 1923; staff grade, 1929; ag. admstr. of col., 1931.

FANNIN, C. G., M.C., B.A. Oxon., Assoo. Inst. C. E.—B. 1890; ed. Hilton Coll., Natal, Rhodes schol., Exeter Coll., Oxford in 1908-11; houe. engnr., sci., 1911; G. E. C. London, 1911- 14; 1st Lt. Lond. Heavy Bty. R.G.A., 1915-16, adjt., 7th H. A. Group, 1917; (ment. in desps., M.C.); ast. inspr., guns, Woolwich, 1918-19; Kenya survey dept., 1920; dist. surr., ast. land offr.; author of paper "Townplanning at Mombasa."

FARMER, Frank.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Dec., 1914; ast. clk., Apr., 1917; on milly. serv., Mar., 1917 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted, under order in council of 1916, clk. of the 2nd div., Feb., 1920; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Dec., 1924; cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925; staff offr., 20th Apr., 1934.
FARQUHAR, Edward Allan.—Comnr., S. Aust. harbours bd., 1914; chmn., 1924.

FARQUHAR, George Neil, M.C.—B. 1896; on milly. serv., Dec., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Mar., 1920; attd. to secretariat, Apr., 1920; ditto, Puttalam Kach., June, 1921; office ast. to gov't. agt., S. Prov., Mar., 1922; ditto to gov't. agt., N.W. Prov., 1925; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1926; sec., land comnr., Aug., 1928; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1930; clk. to leg. coun., Mar., 1930; clk. to state coun., July, 1931; contr., establishments, July, 1932; col. treas., Sierra Leone, 1934.

FARQUHARSON, Robert Alexander, M.A. (Oxon), M.Sc., F.G.S., A.O.S.M.—B. 1883; ed. Boys' High Schl., Otago Univ., N.Z. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; senr. schol., N.Z. Univ., 1905; dipl. in mining engr., 1905; 1st cls. hons., geology and 2nd cls., physical science, N.Z., 1906; Rhodes schol., N.Z., 1906; B.A., Oxford, hons. in geology and mineralogy, 1908; M.A., 1912; gov't. petrologist, mines dept., W. Australia, 1911-22; lent at request of C.O. to Somaliland govt. to furnish rept. on geological and mineral resources of Prot., 1923; gov't. geologist, Somaliland, 1923-26; recd. thanks of S. of S. for geol. map of Somaliland; dir., agr. and geologist, Somaliland, 1926; geol. advr., Br. Somaliland-Ethiopia bndry. comnr., 1932-33; geologist in ch., water boring scheme under Col. Development Act, 1932-34.
FARR, Frank James, M.B., Ch.B., L.D.S., D.M.R. & E.—B. 1900; radiologist, med. dept., Hong Kong, 1928.

FARRELL, Capt. Wilfrid Jerome, O.B.E. (1936), M.C., M.A., R. of O.—B. 1882; ed. Ushaw; Hymers; Jesus Coll., Cambridge; fellow of Jesus Coll., 1906; student, Br. Schl. of Archaeology, Athens, 1906; Munich Univ., 1907; on staff of Rugby Schl. and Haileybury Coll., 1911- 19; served in European war, 1914-19; civ. educn. offr., Iraq, Sept., 1919; officiating dir. and adviser, miny. of educn., Iraq, 1921; senr. educn. offr., Palestine, Apr., 1923; ast. dir., educn., Apr., 1927; dep. dir., Oct., 1929; ag. dir. on various occasions, 1927-36; Br. mag. for riot cases, Sept. to Oct., 1929; dir., educn., 1936.

FAULKNER, Odin Tom, C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1890; ed. St. Albans and Cains Coll., Cambridge (Schol.), B.A. (1st cls., nat. sci. tripes, 1911); dipl., agr., 1912; mycologist and agrl. expert to Rubber Growers' Assocn., Malaya, 1912-14; dep. dir., agr. to govt. of Punjab 1914-21; dir., agr. Nigeria, 1921; do., S.S. and F.M.S., 1936.
FAULKNER, S.N., O.B.E. (1818)—Astm. auditor R.A.P., Jan., 1909; senr. astm. auditor, Apr., 1915; dep. ch. accts., Uganda Rly., 1917.

FAWCETT, CAPT. ARTHUR EDWARD, B.A., B.A.I. (Dublin), A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ. Engrn.—B., 1890; war serv., 1916-19, R.E., France, Persia, Afghanistan, ment in desp.; astm. engrn., F.M.S. Rlys., Apr., 1920; dist. engrn., Apr., 1925; do., grade I, Apr., 1929.

FEARON, FRANK ST. CLAIR.—Ed. Charterhouse (schol.) and Exeter Coll., Oxford (exhib.), B.A. (litt. hum.), 1902; chtd. law dept., Cape Colony, 1903; astm. regisr., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1908; last astm. regisr. and taxing offr., 1923; regisr. and taxing offr., sup. ct., Johannesburg, 1927; ditto, Grahamstown, 1930.

FRASEY, GILBERT GEORGE.—B. 1891; ed. Westminster; served with H.A.C. and Nigeria Regt., W.A.F.F.; active serv., Cameroons; astm. dist. offr., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; dep. res., Apr., 1933; res., July, 1934.

FREETHAM, RICHARD, C.M.G.—B. 1874; ed. Marlborough and New Coll., Oxford; mem., Inner Temple; called to bar, 1899; deputy town elk., Johannesburg, 1902-3; town elk., Johannesburg, 1903-5; admitted advoc., Transvaal, 1903; legal adviser to high comsrr. for S. Africa, 1907-10 and 1912-13; K.C., 1919; mem., leg. coun., Transvaal, 1907-10; mem., H. of A., Union of S. Africa, 1915-23; lieut., 1st battn. Cape Corps, 1916-19; mem., Southborough comtee. on Indian Reform and chmn. functions comtee., 1918-19; mem., India Office comtee. on Govt. of India Bill, 1919; ag. pres., sup. ct. of Swaziland, 1922-23; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal Prov. Divn., Aug., 1923; judge pres., sup. ct., Natal prov. divn., May, 1930.

FEHILLY, JOSEPH PATRICK, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. N.U.I., F.A.C.S., D.P.H. Queen's Univ., Belfast.—B. 1892; ed. Blackrock Coll. and Univ. Colls., Cork and Dublin; late temp. surg., R.N. and capt., R.A.M.C.; res. med. offr., Br. Hosp., Buenos Aires, 1919; med. offr., Hong Kong govt. med. serv., 1924; M.O. in ch. Kowloon and new territories; med. health offr., port, 1926; M.O. in ch., Kowloon insp., 1927; port health offr. and inspnr., immigrts., 1931.
FELIX-JONES, IVOR, B.A.—B. 1899; ed. Westminster and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, Mar., 1922.
FELTHAM, STANLEY CRATHERN, A.R.I.B.A., chtd. architect.—B. 1896; ed. Northern Polytechnic, London; architect, P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

FENDALL, FRANCIS ALAN, L.S., N.Z. King's Coll. Auck. Univ.—B. 1891; lic. survr., N. Z., Mar., 1916; survr., F.M.S., Feb., 1920; do., Kedah, May, 1920; astm. supt., surveys, Sept., 1923; do., F.M.S., Mar., 1928; senr. astm. supt., surveys, Spore, Dec., 1933.

FENN, EGERTON DUDLEY.—B. 1893; ed. Haileybury Coll. and Diocesan and St. Andrew's Colls., S. Africa; elk., staff of gov.-gen., Union of S. Africa, 1912-28; served, S. A. Arty. and lieut., R.A.F., 1915-19; sub-inspnr., Swaziland pol., 1928; inspnr., 1931; ch., pol., 1933.

FENTON, HON. JAMES EDWARD.—M.H.R. C'wealth of Australia since 1910; mem., standing comtee., pub. accts., 1917-25; opposition wh., 1922-29; min. for trade and cust., 1929; Ausm. del. to Disarmament Comm., London, 1930; P.M.G., Lyons' Ministry, 1932; resigned, Oct., 1932.

FENTON, JAMES STEVENSON, O.B.E. (1855—B. 1891; ed. Dundee High Schl. and Edinburgh Univ., M.A. (Edin.); war service, Mesopotamia, 1917-19; passed progress exam. in Mendi; dist. comsrr., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1915; dist. comsrr., Apr., 1925; senr. dist. comsrr., 1935; ag. prov. comsrr. on several occasions.
FERGUSON, SIR DAVID GILBERT, KT. Bar. (1934)—B. 1861; called to the bar, 1880; judge of sup. ct., New S. Wales, Mar., 1912.

FERGUSSON, GEN. SIR CHARLES, BAR. G.C.B. (1932); G.C.M.G. (1924); K.C.B. (1935); K.C.M.G. (1918); C.B. (1911); M.V.O. (1944); D.S.O. (1988), D.L., J.P.—B. 1865; ed. Sandhurst; joined Grenadier Guards, adjutant, 1890-94; joined Egyptian Army, 1895; Sudanese campaigns, 1896-98; severely wound Rosaries (med. with eight clasps, med. five clasps in deaps.); commanded 15th Sudanese, 1898; Medjidiye; garrison and dist. Omdurman, 1900; adjt.-gen., Egyptian Army, 1901-3; commanded 3rd Batt. Grenadier Guards, brig.-gen., gen. staff, Irish commd., 1907-8; infy., 1909-13; served European War, 1914-18; commd., 6th divn. and subsequently of 2nd and 17th Army Corps; mily. gov., occupied German territory, 1918-19 (seven times ment. in desg. gov. and commd.in-ch., New Zealand, 1924; assumed govt., Dec., 1924; ret., 1st chmn., W. Indian Closer Union Comm., 1925.

FERNANDO, PETER OLIVER.—B. 1888; ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd. to Kurugalla kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Feb., 1922; ditto, Jaffna kach., Mar., 1925; mag., Puttalam, Apr., 1924; attd. Ramapura kach., Sept., 1925; office asst., ditto, Nov., 1926; pol. mag., Panadura, Dec., 1926; asst. Mannar kach., Oct., 1927; pol. mag., Mata Jan., 1929; astt. attit. offr., Jan., 1930; May, 1934; astt. attit. offr. in addn., May, 1934.

FERNANDO, SELLAPERUMAGAN SHRI CLARENCE, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1907; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1931; astt., ch. sec.'s off., Dec., 1931; attd., Ratnapura kach., Sept., 1932; office asst., Matale kach., Mar., 1933.

FFORDE, SIR CECIL ROBERT, KT. BAR. (1930).—Ed. Bedford Schl.; called to Irish bar 1903; crown coun., Ireland, 1912-21; mem. coun. of bar of Ireland and of standing counsel, K.C., 1919; coun. to mily. during Sinn Fein rebellion, 1916; coun. to atty.-gen., 1919; coun. to crown, 1920; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1922; judge, high ct., Punjab, 1922-31; pres., spea. cts., Swaziland and Bechuanaland Prot., comsrr., Basutoland, and legal advr. to his comsrr. for S. Africa, July, 1934.

FIELD, EDGAR JOHN.—B. 1874; ed. pol. schls. and Christian Brothers' Coll., Adelaide; survey dept., S. Australia, Jan., 1891; ed. office of comsrr. of crown lands, 1892; ch. clk. acct., 1909; ch. inqmr. offr., 1911; sec. for land chmn. of advances to settlers bd. and rec. of rents, 2nd Apr., 1917; chmn., irrig. coms., Aust., 1923; dir., lands, 1931.
FIELD, HENRY TREVOR CROMWELL.—B. 1897; ed. St. John's Schl., Leatherhead and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); on war serv., 1914-18; demob. (capt.) 1919; inspnr. and schoolmast., S.P.
GERIA, 1922; supt., educn., 1926; prin., govt., Ibadan, 1932.
FIELD, JOHN WILLIAM, M.D. (Hons. B'ham).—B. 1899; med. offr., F.M.S., July, 1925; malaria reach, offr. II, inst. for med. resch., May, 1931; do., grade I, Aug., 1934.

FIELDGATE, ALAN FREDERIC EDMOND, A. (Oxon).—B. 1889; ed. Worcester Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast Colony, 1915; ag. dist. commnr., on various occasions, 1918-21; dist. commnr., Aug., 1922; pol. mag., Mar. to June, 1925; prov. commnr., 1924.

FIENNES, Lieut.-Col. THE HON. SIR STEPHEN EDWARD TWIBLETON-WYKEHAM, BART.—B. 1864; Lieut.-Col., Oxford's Inf. Yeo.; served in rebel rebellion, 1886 (medal with clasp); Egypt, 1887 and 1888 (medal with clasp, bronze star); pioneer expdn. to Mashonaland, 1890; South Africa, 1900 (medal with three clasps; twice ment. in desps.); European war, 1914 (France, Belgium, Flanders and Home); M.P. (L.) Banbury Is., Oxfordshire, 1906-1910, and 1910-1918; gov. and comdr-in-chief, Seychelles, 6th July, 1918; assumed govt., 8th Oct., 1918; gov., Leeward Is., 27th Oct., 1921; assumed govt., 4th Dec., 1921; t., 1929.

FINDLAY, ALEXANDER JOHN.—B. 1886; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schl., Aberdeen Univ. and North of Scotland Coll. of Agr.; M.A., B.Sc. (agric.), N.D.A., N.D.D., sup't., agr., Nigeria, 1912; senr. sup't., 1922; asst. dir., 1927; dep. dir., 1930; dir., agr., Zanzibar, 1931; M.L.C., 1932.

FINDLAY, GEORGE HUGO, C.M.G. (37).—A. (Oxon).—B. 1888; ed. Repton and Oriel Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. commnr., S. Nigeria, 1911; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1919; asst. sec., S. rova., 1922; cls. I, grade I, admstr. serv., 1928; ag. admstr. of colony, Lagos, 1930; ag. min., ass't sec., 1930; ag. sec., S. Provs., 1931; aff. grade, 1932; ag. commnr., col. and lands, 1934; ag. ch. sec., 1934; ag. ch. commnr., S. rova., 1936.
FINLAYSON, D. A.—B. 1892; home cust., excise serv., 1912-24; war serv. in R.N., 1918; offr., cust., Sierra Leone, 1924; dep. dir., dept. f cust., excise and trade, Palestine, 1928.

FINLAYSON, GEORGE DANIEL.—B. 1882; d. Picton Acad. and Dalhousie Univ.; B.A., 1907; apptd. to dept. of insurance, Canada, 1907; associate of Inst. of Actuaries of Great Britain, 1911; supt. of insurance, Canada, 1914.

FIRTH, OWEN WATTS.—B. 1884; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; asst. dist. commnr., Nigeria, 1911; solt. offr., Udi-Okiwigi Patrol, 1915; 2nd cls. list offr., 1919; cls. I, grade I, admstr. serv., 1927; ag. prin. ass't sec., S. Provs., 1928; staff grade, 1932; ag. lieut.-gov., S. Provs., 1934.
FISCHER, PERCY U., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1878; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, S.A. Coll., Cape Town, Trinity Hall, Camb.; judge, sup. ct., South Africa, 1929.

FISET, MAJOR-GEN. SIR EUGENE, KT. BACH. (1917), C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O., G.G.H.S., B.A., M.D.—B. 1874; staff, adjt. med. corps., Canada, 1902; dir.-gen., med. services, 1903; dep. min., militia and defence, vice-pres., the militia coun. since 1906; rec'd. D.S.O. for service in S. Africa; mentioned in despatches; ret., 1924; mem. for county of Rimouski.

FISHER, BERTIE ST. JULIAN.—B. 1885; Fiji surv., 1909; dist. commnr., 1918; legal dept., 1920; ag. dep. regtr.-genl. and regir., sup. ct., 1921; ag. regir., sup. ct., curator of intestate and vacant estates, pub. trustee and offr. recr. in bankruptcy, 1923-27; regir.-gen. and regir., titles, 1929; regir., sup. ct., pub. trustee, offr. recr. in addn., Jan., 1931.

FISHER, JOHN CAMPBELL, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1880; ed. at Oakham Schl. and Sid. Suss. Coll., Cambridge; olk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., July, 1902; asst. auditor, Lagos, Sept., 1903; detached for duty in N. Nigeria, Nov., 1903; asst. aud., N. Nigeria, Apl., 1906; local audr., July, 1909; examnr., col. audit branch, K. and A. dept., Feb., 1910; 1st div. olk. (2nd cls.), central office, May, 1910; aud., Malta, Moh., 1913; mem. of control board and hd. of food and commerce control office, Malta, 1917; senr. clk., centl. office, May, 1918; asst. dir., col. audit, Apr., 1928; deputy dir., Oct., 1929.
FISHER, HAROLD MAYSTON, L.D.S., R.C.S. (Eng.).—B. 1898; mil. serv., 1914-19; dental surg., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; senr. dental surg., 1930.

FISHER, REV. ROBERT, M.A.—B. 1887; ed. Cambridge; asst. mast., educn. dept., Gold Coast, 1921; house mast., 1922; mast., Adhimots Coll., 1926; supt., educn., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1928; prin., govt. coll., Umuhia, 1928.

FISHER, SIR STANLEY, KT. BACH. (1922).—Ed. at Westminster and Brasenose Coll., Oxford, M.A., 1891; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple and Lincoln's Inn (called 1890); apptd. pres. of dist. ct. of Kyrena, Cyprus, May, 1902; puisne judge, Cyprus, 1911; ch. just., Cyprus, 1919; on sp. mission to Cairo under F.O. 24th Jan. to 14th Mar., 1924; ch. just., Trinidad, 1924; ch. just., Ceylon, 1926; ret., Aug., 1930; passed exam. in modern Greek, Dec. 1903 and in Turkish, 1918; joint commnr. for compiling revised edition of Statute Laws of Cyprus (published 1907), for which rec'd. thanks of legis. coun. and S. of S.; joint commnr. under reprint of statutes law, 1913; edition published, Feb., 1914; commnr. for laws, under reprint of statutes law, 1922; edition published, 1924; compiler of "The Mukhtar's Handbook," 1911; published "Ottoman Land Laws," 1919.

FITZGERALD, RICHARD DESMOND, B.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dublin), T.C.D., D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.), certif. (hons.) L.S.T.M., M.C.—B. 1885; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; M.O., Penang, July, 1912; do., Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1912; health offr., Malacca, Sept., 1914; med. offr., gen. hosp., S'pore, Sept., 1914; served in European war, 1914-19 (ment. in desps., M.C.); med. offr., Tan Took Seng hosp. and surg., native hosp., S'pore, Dec., 1919; M.O., gen. hosp., S'pore, Apr., 1920; med. offr. in ch., officials, pol. surg. and prison surg., Jan., 1921; prin. med. offr., Johore, June, 1923; offr. del., Far Eastern Asn. Trop. Med., Calcutta, Dec., 1927; dep. dir. med. and health services, S.S., Feb., in 1932 and 1933; dir. med. and health services, S.S. and advr., med. and health services, M.S., Dec., 1933; dir., med. servs., S.S. and advr., med. servs., M.S., Mar., 1934.

FITZGERALD, WILLIAM JAMES, K.C.—ed. Blackrock Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; M.C. and Croix de Guerre (French); admstr. serv., Nigeria, 1919; crown coun., Nigeria, 1924; solr.-gen., N. Rhodesia, 1932; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1932; atty.-gen., July, 1933; ag. judge, 1934; atty.-gen., Palestine, 1936.

FITZ-GIBBON, WILLIAM GUERRIN.—B.A., Royal Univ. of Ireland; B. 1885; rly. dept., Hong Kong, 1913; 1st olk., col. secretariat, Hong Kong, 1917; ag. ch. olk., col. secretariat, 1921 and 1924; J.P., 1924; gen. sec., P.W.I., 1927.

FITZ-HENRY, WILLIAM COOPER, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; ed. Dulwich Coll.; asst. engrnr., Nigerian rly., 1908; lieut. R.E., 1915; capt. R.E., 1916; served with miny. of munitions and admry., 1918; in India on rly. work, 1920-27; road engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1927; ch. road engrnr., N. Rhodesia, July, 1928; ag. D.P.W., Aug., 1930; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., 1935.
FITZMAURICE, ALEXANDER LINDSAY—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (1909); M.B., B.S. (Lond.), 1913; late out-patient offr., asst. house surg. and house physician, Guy's Hosp.; med. offr. Somaliland Prot., 1914.

FITZPATRICK, RIGHT HON. SIR CHARLES, P.C. (1908); G.C.M.G. (1911); K.C.M.G. (1907).—Born in Quebec, 1853; Dufferin medallist, law faculty, Laval Univ., 1876; one of the counsel for the defence of Louis Riel at Regina in 1885; elected to Quebec legislature, 1890; to Dominion parl., 1896, again in 1900 and 1904; solr.-gen., Canada, 1896; min. of just., 1902; ch. just. of Canada, 1906-1918; mem. of Hague tribunal of arbitration, 1908; lieut.-gov. of Quebec, 1918 to 1923.

FITZPATRICK, JAMES PATRICK, L.R.C.P. and S. (Ireland).—B.1885; med. offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1921; ag. med. supt., Central Mental Hosp., T. Rambutan, May-Dec., 1929; ag. dep. state med. and health offr., Perak, Feb., 1935.
FLANAGAN, MAURICE JOSEPH, B.A. (Cantab.).—Asst. audr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1912; audr., Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1916; audr., Windward Is., June, 1919; audr., Nyasaland, Oct., 1925; do., Uganda, 1935.

FLECKER, ORIEL JOYCE, B.Sc.—Ed. Cheltenham Ladies' Coll.; assoc. of Cheltenham Ladies' Coll., 1908; gold med., Société Nationale des Professeurs de Français en Angleterre, 1909; B. Sc., Lond., 1912; Somerville Coll., Oxford, 1924-5; headmistress, girls' schl., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925; author of "A School Chemistry," 1923.

FLEMING, ALAN MCKINSTRY, M.C., M.B., B.S. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.)—Served with 8th Bedfordshire Regt., B.E.F., 1915-18, ment. in desps.; Middlesex Hosp. Med. Sch.; house physician and house surg., Middlesex Hosp.; house physician, Brompton Hosp. for consumption; res. physician, Ruthin Castle; E. African med. serv., 1929; physician and lect. in medicine, Uganda Med. Sch., 1930-32; invalided, 1933; med. offr., Gibraltar, 1936.

FLEMING, EDWARD DONOVAN.—B. 1898; ed. Bradford Gram. Schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb., 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Sept., 1917; Salonika, Dec., 1917-Feb., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921, attd. Chinese prot., K. Lumpur; offr., cls. IV, V, Apr., 1924; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; asst. prot., Chinese, Taiping, May, 1930 and supt., convict estabmt., in addn., Nov., 1930; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; prot., Chinese, Johore, July, 1934; do., Perak, Apr., 1936.

FLEMING, ERNEST.—B. 1891; 5th class clk., crown agts. office, Oct., 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd division of the civ. serv. and temporarily employed in the Bd. of Trade, Jan., 1912; assigned to C.O., April, 1912; on military service from 26th May, 1915 to 9th Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; 2nd ass't. acctnt., 22nd Oct., 1930.

FLEMING, COL. JOHN GIBSON, C.B.E. (1919), D.S.O. (1916), late R.E.—B. 1880; ed. Haileybury Coll. and R. Milly Acad., Woolwich; joined R.E.'s Coll. and R. Milly Acad., Woolwich; served R.E.'s Coll., 1892; served S. African War, 1901-2 (Queen's 1899; served S. African War, 1901-2 (Queen's med. 5 clasps); Euro. med. 1914-18 (desps. med. D.S.O., bt. lt.-col., col., 1924; apptd. fourth Crown Agent and engrnr.-in-ch., 1st June, 1932.
FLEMING, W. P.—B. 1887; apptd. after compet. exam., S. and T. elk., G.P.O.; lett. C.O., 9th May, 1921; cler. offr., C.O., 1st Sept., 1921; on mil. serv., 4th Apr., 1915 to 4th May, 1919.

FLETCHER, SIR ARTHUR GEORGE MUNCHER, K.C.M.G. (1930), KT. BACH (1929), C.M.G. (1922), C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford (exhib.); cadet, Hong Kong, 1901; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si, June, 1903; ag. ass't. reg'ar., gen., 1903; J.P., 1896; ag. ass't. col. sec. and elk. of couns., 1905-9; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1907; dep. offr. revtr., 1909; sec. to atty-gen., 1909; reg'ar., trade marks and letters patent, 1910; ag. assessor of rates, 1911; ag. dep. revtr., 1911; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1912; offr. revtr., 1912; ag. ass't. col. sec. and elk. of couns., 1913; ag. treasur., 1913; ass't. col. elk. of couns., Aug., 1913; priv. sec. to O.A.G. and to gov., Aug.-Dec., 1913; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. and leg. couns., in 1916, 1917, 1919, 1920 and 1922; chmn., shipping com. comtee., 1917-19; ag. col. sec. and mem. exec. leg. couns., 1923 and 1929; col. sec., Caylon, 1923; O.A.G., May-Aug., 1927; gov., Fiji and high comm'r., W. Pacific, 1929; gov. and comm'rs-in-ch., Trinidad and Tobago, 1936.

FLETCHER COOKE, JOHN.—B. 1911; Batham school, Malvern Coll., 1929; sent. elkh., St. Edmund Hall, Oxford, 1929; Ritchie schol.; 1st cls. philosophy, politics, econ., 1932; apptd., after compet. exam., asst pr. C.O., 1934.
FLETCHER, REGINALD H.—Joined Jamacian civ. serv., post office dept., by compet. exam., 1890; 1st cls. clk., 1908; ob. clk., 1915; postmstr., 1921; postmstr., Jamaica, 1923.

FLEURET, ARTHUR ISADORE.—B. 1899; mem. C.S.O., Falkland Is., Dec., 1912; ag. jun. clk., 1914 to Sept., 1919; clk., treas. and cust., Oct.-Sept., 1919; C.S.O., Jan., 1924; clk. and elk. of couns., Jan., 1928; offr.-in-ch., secretariat, Oct., 1928; Jan., 1929 and Feb. to June, 1931; 2nd lieu. Falkland Is. Defence Force, Jan., 1931; lieu. Mar., 1934; aide-de-camp to gov., Feb., 1935; ag. offr. commdg. F. I. Defence Force, May, 1935; adjt. do., May-Dec., 1935; ag. col. pos. mr., July, 1935 to Feb., 1936.

FLINN, MAJOR WILLIAM HENRY, O.B.I. (1919).—B. 1895; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Trinity Coll., Dublin; Royal Irish Regt.; staff, 1914-19; admin. offr., Cyprus, 1919; ass't. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, 1921; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to H. Coman., Apr., 1921; ag. elk., exec. coun., Feb., Mar., 1922; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Apr., 1922; ass't. sec., July, 1922; ag. comanr., Sept., 1922; ag. local commdt., police, Sept., 1922; ass't. sec., June to July, 1923; ag. reg'ar., soc. societies, June, 1923; reg'ar., co-op. credit soc., July, 1923; ag. reg'ar., co-op. credit soc., June, 1923 to Apr., 1924 and from Oct., 1924; 2nd cls. elk., 1926; ag. ch. ass't. sec. for various pos., 1924-27; ag. col. sec. in 1926 and 1927; comanr. for Cyprus in London, July, 1927; Cyprus, 1935.

FLOOD, J. E. W., C.M.G. (1883)—B. 1883; ed. at Portora Royal Sch., Enniskillen, Trin. Coll., Dublin, B.A. (scholar univ.), 1900; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cls. elk., 10th Oct., 1910; sec., edible nuts comte., 1st cls. elk., 15th Oct., 1918; on milit. from 16th Apr., 1917 to 7th Dec., 1919.

FORBES, Leslie.—B. 1889; B.A., Oxon. (lit. hum.), 1913; cadet, F.M.S., 1913; lent to Kedah govt., 1914; sec. to adviser, Kedah, 1916; dist. offr., Dec., 1920-1922; offg. dist. offr., Bentong, Feb., 1925; offr., ols. III., Nov., 1926; ch. ast. dist. offr., Kinta, Feb., 1927; offr., ols. II., Nov., 1930; coll., land rev., Seremban, Feb., 1931; dist. offr., Klang, June, 1933; ag. advr., lands, Kedah, Feb., 1935.
FORBES, Paul Henry.—B. 1892; admiralty (war staff), Aug.-Oct., 1914; B.E.F., 1914-19; ast. ch. acct., Palestine rly., Aug., 1921; ch. acct., Aug., 1923; ch. acct., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1926.

FORD, George James.—B. 1896; Harvey Gram. Schl.; P.O., U.K., 1913; war serv. with R.E. in Egypt, Palestine, Turkey and Russia, 1915-19; passed supplementary clerksp. compet. exam. G.P.O. secy's office, 1920; supt., P.O., S'pore, Feb., 1921; supt., P. and T., Perak, Feb., 1930; supt. i/c., N. Sembilan, Nov., 1933.

FORDE, John Reid (late Capt., R.A.M.C.), M.B., B.Ch.—On mily. serv., 1915-18; apptd. to W. African med. staff (Gold Coast), 1918; senr. med. offr., 1932; transf., Gambia, 1934; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; senr. med. offr., G. Coast, 1935.

FORRER, Henry Augustus, M.A. Cantab., Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1886; cadet S. S. Oct., 1909; passed cadet, Aug., 1913; supernm. offr. of cls. V., Mar., 1914; addl. to censor's office, May 1915; services lent to the F.M.S., Jan., 1917; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, July, 1921; offr., ols. III., Nov., 1921; 2nd mag. and coroner, Penang, Dec., 1923; dep. regis., and ast. offr. assignee, Penang, Sept., 1924; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1926; regis., sup. ct., Penang, May, 1927; regis., sup. ct., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1929; offr., cls. IB, Apr., 1930; dist. judge and lat. mag., S'pore, Jan., 1933.

FORREST, Arthur Eric.—B. 1892; ast. audr., E. Africa Prot., July, 1915; on active service, Oct., 1915 to Dec., 1916; ast. audr. in charge, Somaliland, 1917; ast. senr. ast. audr., E. Africa Prot., in 1917, 1919 and 1920; ast. audr. in charge Zanzibar, Feb., 1920; senr. ast. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1922; senr. ast. treas., Nov., 1925; treas. acct., Jan., 1926; mem. and sec., Kenya cost of living comm., 1926; title of subst. post changed to prin. ast. treas., 1928; dep. treas., Uganda, Apr., 1930; ast. treas. in 1930, 1932 and 1934; treas., Oct., 1934; currency offr., mem., rly. advy. coun.

FORREST, Robert Andrew Dermot—B. 1893; ed. Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., 1914; cadet, Hong Kong, Oct. 1919; passed final exam. in Cantonese, Jan., 1922; addl. educn. dept., Jan., 1922; ast. head, sany. dept., Nov., 1925; ch. ast. sec. for Chinese affr., 1928; ast. treas. and dep. estate duty commr., 1931 and 1935; head, sany. dept., 1934; senr. ast. col. treas., Nov., 1934; supt., impts. and expts., Jan., 1935; 2nd ast. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., Dec., 1935; prin. ast. col. sec. and clk., couns., Nov., 1935; ast. col. sec., Sept., 1935 and Aug.-Sept., 1936.
FORSYTH, L. E. W.—B. 1877; ast. consmr., Mangrove Cay, Bahamas, 1922; Out Island consmr., 4th grade, 1927; 2nd grade, July, 1927.
FOSBROOKE, Henry Albert, M.A.—B.1908; ed. Pocklington Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1931; ast. dist. offr., Aug., 1933.

FOSTER, Lionel Hubert Lisle.—B. 1883; ed. Bradford and Christ Ch. Oxford (B.A. 1912); min. of educ., Egypt, Jan. 1913; asst. res., Nyasaland, Jan., 1915; res., 1st grade, Apr., 1925; ag. asst. ch. sec., May-Nov., 1932; ag. prov. commnr., Nov.-Dec., 1933; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Jan.-Sept., 1934; dep. prov. commnr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1935.

FOSTER, Richard Shepherd—B. 1894, apptd. after compet. examin. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil service; temp. employed at Nat. Hlth. Insurance Commn., Mar., 1913 to June, 1914; assigned to C.O., June, 1914; on military service from Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1919; col. audt. dept., 1919; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; do., Sierra Leone, Feb., 1923; do., Gold Coast, Nov., 1931; ag. dep. audr., various occasions, 1931-33; senr. asst. audr., Nigeria, 1934.

FOSTER, Robert Spence, M.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1891; ed. Magdalen Coll. Schl. and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; 2nd lieut., K.S.L.I., 1915; K.A.R., 1917-19; head mast., educ., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; ag. dir., educ., Aug. to Oct., 1924; Swahili examr.; dep. dir., educ., Uganda, 1930; ag. dir., educ., in 1931, 1934 and 1935.

FOSTER, William, O.B.E. (1935), M.B.E.—B. 1880; served in S. African War, 1900-02; Queen's Med., 3 clasps; King's Med., 2 clasps; Br. post office, 1897-1920; serv. in Gt. War, 1914-20; ment. in desps.; major, 1920; dep. postmtr.-gen., Palestine, July, 1920; ag. postmtr.-gen., on several occasions.

FOULGER, Harry Cecil, M.A. (1928)—B. 1891; ed. Malvern Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; capt., R. Fusiliers; asst. res., Nyasaland Prot., Aug., 1914; attd., K.A.R., ag. pvte. sec. and A.D.C. to gov., 1919; res., 1st grade, Jan., 1924; ag. prov. commnr., Jan.-Sept., 1935.

FOUND, William Ambrose, D.Sc.—B. 1873; ed pub. schls. and Prince of Wales Coll., Prince Ed. Island; dept. marine and fisheries, Canada, 1898; supt., fisheries in combined dept., 1911; advr. to Br. plenipotentiaries when pelagic sealing treaty negotiated, 1911; mem., Canadian-American Fisheries Commn., 1918; dep. min., fisheries, 1928.

FOURIE, The Hon. A. P. J.—Formerly mem., Cape prov. coun. and subsequently mem., H. of A. for Somerset; M.H.A., Gordonia; admstr., Prov. of the Cape of Good Hope, Jan., 1926; min. of mines and industries, Aug., 1929; del., Imp. Confec., London, 1930 and del., Ottawa Econ. Confec., 1932; min., lab., commerce and industries, Apr., 1933.

FOURIE, Louis, M.B.E.—B. 1878; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Edin. Univ.; Berlin; M.B., Ch.B. (Hons) (Conan Doyle Prizeman); D.P.H., F.Z.S., F.R.A.I., Fell. Soc. M.O.H., M.R. San. Inst., mem., Amer. Anthropol. Assocn., res. med. offr., R. Infirmary, 1905; city fever hosp., 1906; roy. hosp. sick. child., Edin., 1906-7; h.-surgeon, Paddington Green Child. hosp., Lond., 1907; act. serv., Boer Forces, 1899-1900; D.S. Taunsg, 1913-15; capt., S.A.M.C., 1916-19; M.O. to admstr., S.W.A., 1920-29; asst. health offr., Union Health Dep., 1930.
FOWLIS, Henry George—Entd. judl. dept., Gambia, 1890; gov't. off., 1893-1901; col. sec.'s off., 1902-06; ch. clk. and acctnt., P.W.D., 1906.

FOX, Harold Robert Leslie, B.Sc. (Mass.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1889; Institute of Technology; served in Great War, 1915-19; ment. in desps., demob. with rank of capt., 1919; ch. engrnr., Jamaica govt. rly., 1926; ag. dir., June-Sept., 1931.

FOXLEE, Richard William, M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1885; ed. Westminster Schl.; served in rail. dept., G.N.R., 1903-09; G.C.R., 1909-15; F.L.R., 1915-21; dep. head, engrnr. design dept., 1921; dep. ch. engrnr. (civ.), 1928.

FRAMPTON, Algernon deKewer, C.D.A.—B. 1904; ed. Christ's Hosp., Oxford Schl.; Economics and at Wye Agrl. Coll.; Imp. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1926; ag. offr. agr., Nigeria, 1927; agrl. supt., Br. Guiana, 1929.

FRANCE, Henry Dunlop.—Rev. of Anguilla, 1896; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1917; rev. offr., Nevis, 1899; tariff clk., rev. of St. Kitts, 1901; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts, 1902; supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Sept., 1917; clk. supervisor of cust., Aug., 1919; ag. cust. preven. serv., Mar.—Oct., 1911; ag. maritime cust., Oct., 1916 to Jan., 1917; collr. cust., Jan., 1921.

FRANCIS, Clifford Claude, Barr-at-Law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1889; ed. at Keble Schl., Rochester, and Lincoln Coll.; on service in Fiji, 1908-12; cadet W. Pacific (Br. Solomon Is. Prot.), June, 1912; passed May, 1914; dep. commnr., high constab., Dec., 1914; lieut., E. Surrey Regt., May; capt., Apr., 1916; served in France and Fland., 1917-18; dist. offr., Gizo, Br. Solomon Is., 1919; ag. res. commnr. of Prot., Oct., 1920; Mar., 1923; rep. natives of Gizo dist. before lands commn., 1923; judl. commn. for Pacific in New Hebrides and Br. judge, for New Hebrides condominium, Aug., 1925; mag., Nigeria, 1930; puisne judge, Uga., and mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1934; ag. ch. just., Aug.-Oct., 1934.

FRANCIS, Cyril Gerard Brooke (1934)—Apptd. Fiji civ. serv., 1898; lieu. nat. constab., 1901; certif., musketeer, machine gun, Hythe, 1905; called to the bar, Temple, 1907; inspr., Fiji constab., barrister and solr., sup. ct., Fiji, 1908; ag. to ag. gov., for various periods, 1908-10; grade dist. commnr., May, 1912; 2nd in local forces with rank of major, Aug., 1914; by Army Council for services in 1914 in connection with defence of Fiji; capt., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; ovv. commr., K.A.R., Africa, 1916-19; 1st grade dist. commnr., Jan., 1916; regir.-gen., regir., sup. ct., ex-stamps, and pub. trustee, Fiji, Jan., 1920; enemy property and contr. L.C.O., Fiji-Pacific, Feb., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1921; commr. for the revision of the laws of Fiji, 1920 (not completed); atty.-gen., Honduras, July, 1921; ag. ch. just., 1922 and 1923; chmn., commn. to ensure outbreak of yellow fever, 1921; chmn., government commn., 1922; chmn., franchise commn., 1923; revd. and consol. law, B. Hond., atty.-gen., Zanzibar, Nov., 1924; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; judge, high court, N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1934.
FRANCIS, Josiah.—Served European war with A.I.F., 1916-19; M.H.R., (wealth Australia since 1922; hon. min., Lyons' min., 1932.
FRANKS, John William, O.B.E. (1881)-1879; prison commn., H.O., June, 1889; asst. supt., Victoria Gaol, Hong Kong, Nov., 1897; asst. supt. pol. res., Feb., 1929; supt. pres., Dec., 1920.
FRASER, ALASTAIR GARDEN, B.A.—B. 1902; Rugby and Queen's Coll., Oxford; B.A., '4; 3rd cls. hons., phil., polit., and econ.; c.t. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

FRASER, REV. ALEXANDER GARDEN, C.B.E. (1830), M.A.—B. 1873; ed. Merchiston Castle, in., Edin. Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; assn., 1913; priest, 1915; C.M.S., Uganda, 1903; prin., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, '4; chaplain with 3rd divn., France, 1917-18; aplain to the forces; prin., Prince of Wales ll., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

FRASER, D. M.—B.1890; Br. post office, 1906; st. clk. and telegraphist, Kenya and Uganda, 13; postmr., Tanganyika, 1917; ch. acct., postal d'tels., 1919; ast. P.M.G., Keyna, 1933; ret., 34; ast. P.M.G., Trinidad, 1936.
FRASER, H.—Ed. Grammar Sch., Inveraray; Jorge Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; B.Sc., 1925; ast. conservator of forests, anganyika Territory, 1925.

FRASER, HUGH., B.A.—B. 1890; ed. Wellington and Exeter Coll., Oxford(schol.); cadet, F.M.S., 13; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., Apr., 1914-Feb., 15; ag. supervisor of cust., Port Dickson, May, 15; ditto, Port Swettenham, Jan., 1916; ast. cadet, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1916; d ast. sec., Fed. sect., Aug., 1917, priv. sec. to sec. in admn., Sept., 1917; dist. offr., Jelebu, sh., 1918; ast. dist. offr., Ipoh, Sept., 1920; fr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; 3rd ast. sec. to govt., M.S., Dec., 1920; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 24; offr., class III., Nov., 1925; ag. ast. advr., edah, Mar., 1927; ag. temp. legal adviser, edah in admn., Apr. to Dec., 1927; ag. ast. eases., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, June, 129; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; dist. offr., Larut ad regis., titles, Perak, Nov., 1930; offg. mag., Lumbur, Nov., 1932; ag. sec. to res., Perak, lar., 1933; offr. cls. I.B., 1934; Malayan estab., ftr., Mar., 1936; sec., high commr. in admn., Aug., 336.

FRASER, JOHN ALEXANDER, M.C., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1896; ed. Trinity Academy, with, and Edin. Univ.; on mil. serv., 1915-19; adet., Hong Kong, 1919; addl. to sany. dept., 921; 3rd ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1921; nd pol. mag., Apr., 1922; dist. offr.
