(North), June, 1922; passed in Cantonese, 1922; ast. cad., sany. dept. and sec. to sany. bd., 1922; ist. offr., South, 1924; ast. supt., impts. and xpts., 1925; dist. offr., North, Feb., 1926; dist. ffr., North and South Sept., 1928; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1931; pol. mag., Kowloon, July, 1931; ag. ast. atty.-gen., Oct., 1932; ag. crown solr., Aug., 1936.

FRASER, SIR JOHN GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1884; cadet Ceylon iv. ser., Oct., 1887; ag. dist. judge, &c., Badulla, Feb., 1894; ast. govt. agt., Chilaw, Dec., 1899; and settm. offr., Aug., 1901; ag. govt. agt., W. Prov., 4th Jan., 1912; ag. controller of revenue, in 1911, 1912, 1913, 1915 and 1916; govt. agt., W. Prov., July, 1914; M.L.C.; ret., 1924.

FRASER, HON. PETER.—B. 1884; ed. Board Schl. and Workers' Educnl. Assn.; mem. of N.Z. H. of R. since 1918; represented N.Z. at Empire Parly. Confce., London, 1935; min. of educ., min. of health and min. of marine, 1935.
FRASER, WINFORD BLACHE.—B. 1868; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1892; mag., St. Andrew, Trinidad, Jan., 1923; mag., Caroni, Nov., 1923; mag., St. George East, Dec., 1924.

FRASER, HON. ST FRANCIS VERNON, KT. BACH. (1935), M.A., LL.B.—B. 1880; ed. Nelson Coll., Otago Univ. Coll., and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; barrister and solr., stip. mag., 1911; chmn., N.Z. pub. serv. appeal bd., 1918; ast. pub. serv. comsrr., 1920; judge, ct. of arbit., 1921-35; chmn., dairy industry comsrr., 1934; dep. chmn., exec. comsrr. of agr., 1935 also ast. dir., marketing, 1936.

FREEMAN, BENJAMIN JOHN.—B. 1888; ed. Christ's Hosp., London, and Horsham; br. P.O., Oct., 1903—Dec., 1913; jnr. postal clk. and telegraphist, Kenya, Jan., 1913; 2nd cls. postmr., Apr., 1919; ast. ch. acctnt., Apr., 1918; 1st cls. postmr., Apr., 1920; dep. ch. acctnt., June, 1924; ast. P.M.G., July, 1931; snr. acctnt., S.S., Aug., 1932; contrlr., P. and T., Selangor and Pahang, Agr., 1933.

FREEMAN, GROFFREY JAMES.—B. 1889; ed. privately and in Clongoweswood Coll., Ireland; served in Royal Dublin Fusiliers and Royal Munster Fusiliers in the Great War, 1914-21; colls., cust., Gold Coast, 1922; senr. do., Feb., 1932.

FREESTON, LESLIE BRIAN, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1892; ed. at Willaston Schi., Nantwich and Oxford; scholar New Coll., 1911-1919; 2nd cls., hons. mod., 1913; B.A. (war), 1919; on mil. ser., 17th Sept., 1914 to 8th Mar., 1919; temp. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 10th Mar. to 15th July, 1919; appt. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 16th July, 1919; ast. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. under-sec., 18th June, 1920; sent to Ceylon for temp. serv. as sec., salaries comsrr., 1921; ast. prin., 6th Dec., 1922; prin., 1st April, 1924; visited Bahamas and Br. Hond. on duty, 1927; seconded for serv. with Col. Development Advisory Comtee., 1st Feb., 1930; sec., E. African govts. confce. and to the high comsrr. for transp., Kenya and Uganda, Jan., 1936.

FREETH, ARTHUR CAUSTON.—B.1890; ed. Lancang Coll. (exhib.), St. Mary's Hosp. and Coll. of Med., Newcastle-on-Tyne, M.B., Dunelm, certif., London Schi. of Trop. Med.; temp. capt., R.A.M.C., 1915-20; med. offr., Grenada, 1921; do., Trinidad, 1921; do., Uganda, 1924; ag. senr. health offr., Uganda, 1929; med. offr., Zanzibar, 1930; tuberculosis offr., 1933.

FRENCH, Lewis, I.C.S.(Ret.).—Ed. Merchant Taylors' Schi. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; served as colonisation offr., Chenal Canal, Punjab, afterwards as dep. comsrr. and joint ch. sec. to govt., Punjab, 1896-1921; dir., development, Palestine, 1931.

FRERE, SIR BARTLE HENRY TEMPLE, KT. BACH. (1918), LL.B.—Ed. at Charterhouse schl. and Trin. Coll., Cam.; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1887; pres. dist. court, Cyprus, 1897; transf'd to Gibraltar as pol. mag. and coroner, 1902; atty.-gen., 1911; ch. just., Apl., 1914; judge, prize ct., Aug., 1914; cable censor during European War; ret., Aug., 1922.

FRERE, NOEL GRAY, C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1885; ed. Wanganui Collegiate Schi., N.Z., and Cranleigh Schi.; ast. dist. comsrr., S. Leone, July, 1909; dist. comsrr., Jan., 1915; ag. prov. comsrr., N. Prov., in 1921, 1923, 1926 and 1928; prov. comsrr., Jan., 1929.

FRETZ, WILMOT THEODORE STUART.—B.1888; ed. Framlingham Coll., Suffolk and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; Rhodesian admistr'n., B.S.A.P., N. and S. Rhodesia, 1913-19; Transvaal Border Patrols, 1918; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1921; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 1923; chmn., agrl. comsrr., 1924; ag. admstr., St. Lucia, Dec., 1924; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, July, 1925; res. mag., Jamaica, Apr., 1927; asst. judge, Zanzibar, Aug., 1934; ag. ch. just., 1935.

FRITH, GEOFFREY HAMMOND.—Served in the Great War; just. of peace, Turks and Caicos Is., 1919; ch. clk. to commr., olk. to leg. bd., sec. to bd. of educn., 1920; sub-insptr., pol., 1922; asst. treas., collr. of cust., postmr., gen. man. of savings bank, regist. of shipping, 1923; ag. asst. comsnr., mag., etc., 1927; ag. comsnr. and judge, grand ct., Cayman Islands, 1929; sub. treas., etc., Tobago, 1931; treas., St. Lucia, 1933; ag. col. sec. from Oct., 1933.

FUAD, MUSTAFA.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1922; ed. Turkish Schls., St. Paul's Schl., and English Schl., Nicosia, Cyprus; olk., treas., Cyprus, 1905; inspkr., pol. and dep. coroner, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Limassol, 1913; judge, dist. ct., Nicosia, 1916; mem., legal bd., 1916; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1927; pres., sheri. ct. of appeal, 1927.
FUGLES, NORMAN RALPH.—B. 1907; ed. Sir Roger Manwood's Sch.; B.S.C., Wye (Lond. Univ.), dipl. agr., Cantab and A.I.C.T.A., Trinidad; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1930.

FULLER, HON. SIR GEORGE WARBURTON, K.C.M.G. (1919).—Elected to first H. of R., C. of A., 1901; re-elected, 1903 and 1906; min. of home affairs, C. of A., June, 1909 to Apr., 1910; col. sec., N. S. Wales, Nov., 1916-20; premier, 1922-25; agt.-gen., N.S.W., 1926-31.

FULLERTON, HON. C. P., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. Dalhousie Univ., Dalhousie, Nova Scotia, Canada; admitted to bar, 1896; judge, ct. of appl., Manitoba, 1917-31; ch. comsnr., bd. of rly. comsnr. for Canada, 1931; chmn., Canadian National Rly. trustees, 1933.
FULTON, ADAM, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1897; lieut., May, 1915; capt., 1916; on active serv., Gallipoli, Egypt and Suez, France and Italy, 1915-18; demob., Jan., 1919; vety. offr., Gold Coast, May, 1926.

FURNESS, SIR ROBERT HOWARD, Kt. Bach. (1919).—B. 1880; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man; solr. (hons.), 1902; practised at bar of B. Honduras, 1906-13; regnr.-gen., B. Honduras, 1913; served with B. W. I. Regt., 1915-19; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1920; senr. mag., Apr., 1921; solr. gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1923; ch. just., Barbados, Oct., 1926; do., Jamaica, 1936.

FURNNESS-SMITH, CECIL, Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1890; ed. Birkenhead Schl. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1912; LL.B., 1913; asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, Apr., 1914; dist. comsnr., 1921; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1922; crown coun., Mar., 1925; ag. solr.-gen., May-Oct., 1927; ag. circuit judge, 1929; solr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1932; ag. atty.-gen. in 1933; atty.-gen., Zanzibar, Mar., 1936.

PURSE, MAJOR R. D., C.M.G. (1935), D.S.O.—B. 1887; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford, B.A.; asst. priv. sec. to sec. of state (Mr. Lewis now Lord Harcourt), Dec., 1910 to Aug., 1914; on inactive service in 1st King Edward's Horse, K.O. D.R., 5th Aug., 1914 to 26th Nov., 1918; served in France and Italy, Apr., 1915 to 26th Nov., 1918; promoted capt., 1914; major, 1916; wounded, 1917; D.S.O., July, 1918; Bar to D.S.O., gazetted, Mar., 1919; twice ment. in despatches, asst. priv. sec. (apptmts.) to sec. of state (Lord Milner), May, 1919; Mr. Winston Churchill, Feb., 1921; Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; priv. sec. (apptmts.) to sec. of state, Mr. Thomas, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Nov., 1924; Lord Passfield, 8th June, 1929; visited Can. on C.O. business, 1922, and again in 1923; at Imp. Forestry Confce; mem., bd. govrs., Imp. Forestry Inst.; asst. sec., Int. 1930.

GALEA, ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1930)—B. 1872; Malta Lyceum; ent. Malta civ. serv., 1891; olk., 1st Jan., 1896; 2nd cls. clk., July, asst. registr. cts. of judicial pol., Dec., 1900; registr., Jan., 1914; transf., secretariat, 1918; ch. clk., lieut.-gov.'s office, Sept., 1919; ch. clk. to legislature, Dec., 1921; char. charitable institutions, Apl., 1923; off. sec. head of ministry, Mar., 1927; asst. sec. to Apr., 1934.

GALIA, PROF. ROBERT V., O.B.E. (1883)—L.S. & A.—B. 1882; matric. (with hon.) M. Univ., 1900; graduated as land surv. and architect, 1903; lect. on engnr. and architecture, M. Univ., 1905; prof., ditto, 1915; ag. rector of 1923; el. for lat. div. in leg. assem., June; re-el., Aug., 1927; min. for pub. health, charitable institutions, Aug., 1927 and 1932, also min. for treas., Aug., 1929; leader constitutional party, 1932; leader constitutional party in L.A., 1932-33; rector, Malta Univ., 1934.

GALIZIA, HON. MAJOR JAMES A. C. (1933), O.B.E. (1924).—B. 1876; clk., Malta ser., 1893; ch. clk., P.W.D., 1905; man. wks. and elect. light dept., 1913; aspt. pub. and man., water wks. and elect. light dept., seat in exec. coun. and coun. of gort., M. treas., 1930.

GALLOWAY, WILLIAM CHARLES GOMBEY.—B. 1892; Lond. Institution of City and Guilds bronze medalist, telephony, A.I.E.E.; Br. P. engnr. dept., Jan., 1912; on active serv. (Signals), Nov., 1915; asst. telegr. engnr. P. dept., F.M.S., June, 1920; engr., Oct., 1923; sr. engr., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., May-1928, and Feb-May, 1929; sr. engr., Dec., 1934; ag. asst. engrnr.-in-ch., Nov., 1934.

GALWAY, SIR VISCOUNT (cr. 1727) GREVRE ABUNDILL MONCKTON ARNOLD C.M.G. (1935), D.S.O. (1917), O.B.E. (1918), B. 1882; ed. Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; Sherwood Rangers Yeomanry, 1900-04; Int. Guards, 1904; adjt., 1908; capt., 1911; capt., 4th Cavalry Brig., 1914; D.A.A. Q.M.G., 2nd Cavalry Divn., 1915; A.A. Q.M.G., 1917; brevet lieut.-col., 1919; Lieut. Life Guards, 1922-29; col. commdr., Hon. 4th Coy., 1933; gov.-gen. and commdr.-in-ch., New Zealand, 1935.

GALWAY, LT.-COL. SIR HENRY LANE K.C.M.G. (1910), C.M.G. (1899), D.S.O. (1888), B. 1859; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; entered army, May, 1878; promoted brg., Mar., 1881; capt., Oct., 1887; brevet-maj., May, 1897; maj., Mar., 1899; and lieutenant, Apr., 1901; apptd. dep. comsnr. and vice-coms. Oil Rivers prot., Mar., 1891; dep. coms. coms. Niger Coast prot., Jan., 1897; and dep. coms. Niger Coast prot. (now South Nigeria), Apr., 1899; ag. consul-gen. Niger Coast prot., to Nov., 1896; Jan., 1897; Feb., 1898, to Jan., 1900; ag. high-coms. South Nigeria, Mar. to Dec., 1900; Brass expdn., 1895 (desp., medal with clasps); D.S.O.; Benin expdn., 1897 (desp., clasps, bar majority); Aro expdn., as chief political officer, 1901-2 (desp., medal with clasps); govt., Helena, 1902; govt., Gambia, 30th Sept., 1914; govt., S. Australia, 27th Feb., 1914; govt., 18th Apr., 1914, retired, 1920.

HALWAY, JOHN PATRICK FRANCIS.—Ed. St. Anics Xavier's Coll., Liverpool; clk., vety. biological lab., Kabete, Aug., 1910; seconded, ly. serv., 1915; corp., E. African Vty. Corps., June, 1916; lieut., ditto, 17th Oct., 1917; ut., 3rd K.A.R.; "114-15" Star, Gen. Serv. d Victory med.; labour inspnr., offr., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919; office supt., P.W.D., c., 1922.

GAMBLE, FREDERICK CHARLES—B. 1891; Felsted and Wadham Coll., Oxford, B.A. (ons. jurisprudence) 1914; asst. dist. commr., A.P., Feb., 1915; transp. offr., N. Frontier it., 1916; ag. dist. commr., 1918; res. mag., ly., 1923; off. arbitr., Mombasa town planning beme, 1929; judge, high ct., Uganda and mem., of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1936.

GAMBLE, JOSEPH SAMUEL—Ag. sub-inspnr., B. Guiana, 1905; sub-inspnr. pol., 1906; vte musketry certif., 1908; dist. inspnr. gen., 12; county inspnr., 1920; ag. dep. inspnr. gen. nd sup't, fire brigade for various periods in 1920, 22, 1924 and 1926; inspnr.-gen., pol., Fiji, and mmdt., Fiji defence force, Oct., 1927; nom. em., leg. coun., 1928; title changed to commr., sl., 1936.

GAMBLES, ROBERT MOYLAN, B.A. (Cantab.), R.C.V.S.—B. 1910; ed. Westminster Schl. King's schol.) and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (cl. nat. sci. triplos, pt. I in 1931); R. (Dick) Vety. coll., Edin. (C.O. scholarship) and Univ. of din.; vety. offr., Cyprus, Jan., 1935; ag. ch. sty. offr., June-Oct., 1935.
GAME, FREDERICK HAROLD ROBERTS, A.C.G.I. (1921), A.M.I.C.E. (1925).—B. 1900; ed. Taunton chl. and Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Technology, S. Kensington; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., Uganda, sept., 1925.

GAME, AIR VICE-MARSHALL SIR PHILIP WOOLCOCK, G.B.E.(1929), K.C.B.(1924), K.C.M.G. (1935), C.B. (1918), D.S.O. (1915).—B. 1876; ed. charterhouse; ent. R.A., 1893; capt., 1901; adjt., 902; major, 1912; air vice-marshal, 1922; G.S.O., 1st and 2nd grade, W.O., 1910-14; served, S. Africa, 1901-02 (despa., Queen's Med. five clasps); European war, 1914-18 (despa. six times); dir., training and organization, Air Ministry, 1919-22; commanded R.A.F., India, 1922-23; air. mem. for personnel on air coun., 1923-28; ret. list, 1929; gov., New South Wales, 1930-35.
GANADO, PROF. EDGAR, LL.D.—B. 1884; matric., Malta Univ., 1901; LL.D., 1910; prof. of law, 1921; ag. pub. pros. and treasy. coun., 1928; judge, 1929; ex. in law, 1930.

GANADO, ROBERTO F., LL.D.—B. 1875; ed. Malta Lycceum; matric., Malta Univ., 1891; LL.D., Malta, 1898; bar, Malta, 1899; advoc. for poor, 1905; mag., judl. pol., 1918; examr. laws, Malta Univ., 1919; chmn., licensing bd., 1920; visitor, notarial acts Nov., 1921; ag. crown advoc., June, 1922; ag. pub. pros. and treas. coun., July to Oct., 1922; chmn., bd. of prison visitors, 1924; ag. judge, supr. cta., June to Sept., 1924; mem., gen. coun. of Univ. of Malta, 1924; judge, supr. cts., Sept., 1925.

GANE, PERCIVAL CARLETON, M.A. (Oxon.), LL.B. (Cape).—B. 1874; ed. Kingswood Schl., Bath and Jesus Coll., Oxford; admitted advocr., S. Africa, 1903; K.C., 1919; ag. judge many occasions, 1920-34; puisne judge, eastern dists. local divn., sup. ct., S. Africa, 1924.

GARDINER, HON. JAMES GARFIELD, B.A., LL.D., (Assimboia, S.).—B. 1883; ed. pub. schls.; el., Sask. legislature at by-elec., 1914; re-elec., 1917, and at g.e., 1921 by acclm.; min., highways and min. in ch., bureau of lab. and industries, 1922; re-elec. without contest after assuming office at by-elec., 1923; re-elec., g.e., 1925; premier, Sask., 1926; prov. treas., 1926-27; min. of educn., 1928-29; re-elec., g.e., 1929; resigned as premier, 1929; re-elect., g.e., 1934; premier and prov. treas., Sask., 1934; resigned as premier when apptd. min., agr. in Rt. Hon. W. L. Mackenzie King's cabinet, 1935; first elec., H. of C., Canada at a by-elec., Jan., 1936.
GARDNER, H. M.—Ast. conservator of forests, E.A.P., May, 1914; senr. ast. conservator, forests, Kenya, Dec., 1923; ag. conservator, 1925 and 1927; conservator, 1928.
GARDNER, JOHN HYSLOP, B.So.—B. 1883; ed. Kelvinside Acad. and Univ., Glasgow; ast. engrnr., Basutoland, 1909; govt. engrnr., Swaziland, 1919; served with R.E., Flanders, 1916-18.

GARDNER, JOSEPH HENRY.—B. 1882; active serv. with Imp. Yeomanry, S. African War; mil. serv., E. African Exped. Force attd. Rly. Corps., 1916-18; ast. loco. supt., Tanganyika Rlys., Mar., 1918; dist. loco. supt., 1930; wks. man., 1933; ag. ch. mech. engrnr., Nov., 1935.
GARLAND, T. V., B.A., B.A.I. (T.C.D.).—B. 1906; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1930; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1934.

GARLICK, GEORGE HERBERT, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.M.R.E. (Camb.).—B. 1886; med. offr., Johore, 1917; Malayan med. serv., 1923; ag. prin. med. offr., Johore, 1924, 1927 and 1931; physician and radiologist, Johore, 1927; prin. med. offr., 1934; Johore rep. at Far Eastern Cong., trop. med., Nanking, Sept., 1934.

GARNER, JOSEPH JOHN SAVILLE.—B. 1908; ed. Highgate Schl. (senr. foundation schol., 1923-26) and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (mod. languages triplos 1926-29); mod. and med. languages triplos Pt. I, cls. 2, div. 1; ditto, Pt. II, cls. 1; B.A., 1930; ast. mod. lang. mast., Haileybury Coll., 1929-30; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. prin., D.O., Oct., 1930.

GARNETT, CHRISTOPHER BRADSHAW, M.A. (Agr.).—B. 1903; ed. Clayesmore Schl., Winchester and Peterhouse, Cambridge; B.A. (Agr.), 1924; M.A. (Cantab.), 1928; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1926.

GARNETT, W. J., B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1889; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Jan., 1907; seconded for service in Oversea Stltm. Office, Jan., 1919; promoted ast. prin. under clause 51, 1924; ast. to the rep. in the C'wealth of Australia of H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., 1931.

GARRAN, SIR ROBERT RANDOLPH, K.C.M.G. (1920), KT, BACH. (1917), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1867; barrister-at-law; sec. to drafting comte. of Australian Fed. Convention, 1897-8; sec. to atty.-gen.'s dept., C. of A., Jan., 1901, and partly, draftsman; solr.-gen., 1916-32; chmn., Canberra univ.; author of "The Coming Commonwealth," 1897, and joint author (with the Hon. Sir John Quick) of "The Annotated Constitution of the Austn. Commonwealth," 1901.

GARROOD, HARRY ST. CLAIR.—B. 1882; ed. St. Polycarp's Schl., Farnham; articled architect; Sudan civ. serv., 1906; 2nd lieut., gen. list, 1917; capt., 1918; served with Hedjaz Expdy. Force; Br. agt., Jeddah, 1919 with rank of major; M.C., 3rd cls. Order of Nahla from King Hussein; ment. in desps.; demob., 1920; gen. headqrs., Egypt to 1928; crown survr. and engrnr., Gibraltar, 1929.

GARRY, ARTHUR NICHOLAS MELVILLE, B.A., (Cantab.).—B. 1890; cadet, N. Borneo, Feb., 1915; ag. ast. dist. offr., Apr., 1916; ag. mag., 1st cls., Sept., 1919; ast. commnr., cust., Oct., 1920; ast. dist. offr., Apr., 1922; mag., 1st cls., Sept., 1922; astd., ast., Oct., 1923; ast. commnr., Chinese affrs. in addn., June, 1924; ast. commnr., cust. and excise, Mar., 1927; dist. offr., Mar., 1928; dep. prot., lab. and ast. sec., Chinese affrs., supt. census in addn., Mar., 1931; dist. offr., in addn., June, 1932; ag. commnr., lands, Dec., 1932; ag. prot., lab. and sec. for Chinese affrs. in addn., Feb., 1933; commnr., lands, prot. lab. and sec. for Chinese affrs. in addn., Feb., 1935; addl. sess., judge, July, 1935; govt. sec., Dec., 1935.

GARSON, ALEXANDER DENIS.—B. 1904; ed. Epsom Coll. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. hon., hist. tripods; M.A.; cadet, Nigeria, 1928; ast. dist. offr., 1931; seconded to C.O. as ast. prot., Oct., 1933.

GARTON, HARRY.—B. 1889; served in R.F.A., France, Salonica, Egypt and Palestine, 4th Aug., 1914 to Apr., 1919; clk. to court martials offr., R.A.F., May to Sept., 1919; Admiralty, 21st Oct., 1919 to Oct., 1922; served on Naval Inter-Allied Coman. of Control in Germany, May, 1921 to Oct., 1922; cler. offr.; C.O., 21st Oct., 1922.

GARVEY, FRANCIS WILCOCKS.—B. 1882; served in S. Africa, first in the ranks and afterwards as transport offr.; served Transvaal town pol., 1904-08; ast. commnr., pol., S. Nigeria, 1909; commnr., pol., 1918; recd. thanks of govt. for investigation and suppression of human sacrifices to Agbara Juju at Awka, 1921; commnr., pol., Lagos, 1922; ag. dir., prisons, S. Provs. and Colony, Feb., 1925 to Mar., 1925; dir., prisons, S. Provs. and Colony, Mar., 1925.

GARVEY, RONALD HERBERT, M.A. (Cantab.), F.R.A.I.—B. 1903; ed. Trent Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Fiji and Western Pacific services, Nov., 1926; ast. dist. offr., Mala, Apr., 1929; dist. offr., Oct., 1930; ast. sec., W. Pacific high comm., Aug., 1932; ag. sec. in 1932, 1933 and 1934.

GATER, BOWENLEY ALAN REX, M.A., Dip. Agric. (Cantab.), D.I.C., F.R.M.S., F.E.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1896; war serv., 1914-19; ast. entomologist, dept. of agric., F.M.S., Nov., 1922; malaria resch. offr., Nov., 1926; entomologist, Inst. Med. Resch., 1927; prof., biology, King Edward VII Coll. of Medicine, S'pore, Aug., 1930; del. to 8th cong. of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Dec., 1930.

GATFIELD, WILLIAM HUBERT, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Streatham Gram.Schl. and St. George's Coll., London; apptd. to the 2nd divn. of the Imp. civ. serv., 1914; joined Queen's Westminster Rifles, 1915; 2nd lieut., 1916; served in France, and demob. with rank of lieut., 1919; cadet, S. Sttns., Nov., 1920; proceeded to China to study Chinese, July, 1921; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1922; 2nd ast. prot., Chinese, S'pore, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. IV, Dec., 1926; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1931; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, May, 1934.
GAUNT, A. E.—B. 1897; entd. C.O., Aug., 1913; apptd. ast. clk., Oct., 1915; on mil. ser. from Dec., 1915 to Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 28th Dec., 1928.

GEAR, HARRY SUTHERLAND.—B. 1903; B.Sc. (is cls. hon.) 1925, M.B., B.Ch. Witwatersrand Univ., S. Afr., 1928; D.P.H., Univ. London, 1931; D.T.M. & H., R.C.P.S. Eng., 1932; med. offr., N. Rhodesia med. serv., Aug., 1929; med. patho., S. Afr. Inst. Med. Res., Mar., 1930; s. Afr. govt. post grad. schol. in pub. health, London Sch. of Hy. and Trop. Med., Sept., 1930; hd., dept. of prev. med. and med. staunce Henry Lester Inst., Shanghai, July, 1932; med. health offr., Union of S. Afr., May., 1935; published several articles on anthropology and pub. health in various med. jnl.

GEBERT, SYLVESTRE.—B. 1893; ed. Durban High Sch., Royal College, Mauritius and sec. of Agr., Mauritius; dip. agr., 1st class hon. (Maur.) 1917; scientific ast., div. of biol. dept. of agr., Mauritius, Aug., 1917; zoology and entomology do., Sept., 1917; entd. ast., Bact. Inst. Mauritius, July, 1921; ast. dir., C.O. anti-malaria survey, Mauritius, Jul., 1923; scientific ast., Bact. Inst., Mauritius, Jul., 1923; ast., entomologist, sp., malaria serv., Feb., 1927; ast., entomologist, hookworm and malariab., July, 1932; entomologist, med. and heat. dept., 1934; author of various papers on economic and medical entomology.
GEERING, SIDNEY.—Temp. ast. to engr., P.W.D., B. Guiana, Aug., 1916; Oct., 1918; ag. dist. engr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; 1st cls. overseer, July, 1919; clk. of war, Gambia, Aug., 1920.

GELDENHUYS, FRANS EDUARD.—B. 1898; B.A., 1910, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S.A.; studied in U.S.A. at Unive. of Cornell, West. Illinios, and California; B.Sc., Cornell Univ., 1913 and Ph.D., 1917; teacher of study and agric. science, Grey Coll. Schi., 1917; dir., "Die Landbou-weekblad," Bloemfontein, S.A., 1919; first ch., divn. of agr. econ., and marketing, dept. of agr., S.A., 1925; ast. sec. for agr., 1926; dir., forestry, 1931; coun. coun., Rome, Oct., 1934.

GENEVE, FEDERICO PIERRE, K.C. (1929)—B. 1888; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; called bar, Middle Temple, Nov., 1909; ag. dist. and mag. for varying periods; dist. and mag. Mauritius and Rodrigues, July, 1921; ast. substitute procureur and advoc. gen.; ast. substitute procureur and advoc.-gen., 1923; ditto, Aug., 1924 to Feb., 1925 and Apr., 1925; Jan., 1927; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1927; ag. col. sec., Apr.-Sept., 1928, Mar.-Dec., 1929 and Apr.-Sept., 1930; ag. ch. just., Mar., 1930.

GENT, GERARD EDWARD JAMES, O.B.E. (1935), D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1895; ed. King's Canterbury and Trinity Coll., Oxford; served with Duke of Cornwall's Light Inf., 1914-19; prnt., C.O., 1920; sec., rubber restriction comm., 1922; act. prnt., 1923; pvt. sec. to party, 1924; prnt., 1927; dep. sec., C.O. comm., 1927; sec., col. services comtes., 1929; seconded India Office as a sec. of Indian Round Table Conf., 1930; accompd. Permt. Under-Sec. State to Ceylon, Malaya and Hongkong, 1932.

GENTLE, GEORGE EDWIN PALMER, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1890; ed. Denstone Coll.; clk. office, Grenada, 1898; warden, P.W.D., S. Vincent, 1901; road survr., Grenada, 1905; ast. sup't., pub. wks., 1914, 1916, 1918, 1921, 1922; ast. sup't., pub. wks., 1916; ast. sup't., pub. wks., 1922.

GEORGE, HENRY BERNAUD.—B. 1880; clk. Imp. P.O., 1897; clk., G.P.O., Cape, 1900; clk., P.W.D., 1903; clk., C. and A.G.'s office, 1912; clk., audit office, Cape admnstr., 1912; Great War, France, with S.A.O.E.F. and R.A.I.; offr. in ch., local govt. audt., 1920; prov. ass. Natal Prov., 1932; prov. audr., Transvaal Prov., 1932.

GORDON-HALL, WILLIAM ALEXANDER.—B. 1894; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; lieut., M.V.I., Mar., 1923; dist. offr. Kuala Langat, May, 1926; offr., cls. III., May, 1931; dist. offr., Krian, May, 1932; ag. sec., res., Selangor, July, 1933; offr., cls. II., May, 1936.
GORDON-SMITH, F.—see Smith, Frederick Gordon.

GORING, GEORGE INGHAM, A.R.T.C. (Glasgow), A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1890; ed. Allan Glen Schl.; asst. mech. engrnr., col. transport dept., Br. Guiana; ag. ch. mech. engrnr., transport and harbors dept., Nov., 1932-Aug., 1933; ch. mech. engrnr., Sept., 1933.
GORMLEY, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1904; ed. Dover County Schl. and Reading Univ.; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1927; asst. dist. offr., May, 1929; do., Somaliland Prot., May, 1936.

GORSUCH, LESLIE HAROLD.—B. 1897; ed. Ipswich Schl. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; B.A. (1920); war serv., May, 1916-Jan., 1919; lieut., Sept., 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1920; attached to dist. office, Pekan; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1923; 4th asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1925; asst. sec. to res., Perak, July, 1926; offr., cls. IV., dist. offr., Jelebu, Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1932; asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Feb., 1933.

GOSDEN, MINNIE, M.B., B.Sc. (Lond.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—War serv. as V.A.D. nurse, 1914-17; chem. pathology asst., Hale chem. lab., London hosp., 1925; pathology asst., 1926; asst., bacteriological dept., 1927; asst., med. offr., Lingfield Epileptic Colony, 1928; bacteriologist, Cyprus, Feb., 1929; pathologist, Trinidad, Mar., 1936.

GOSLING, WILLIAM RICHARD, R.A.—B. 1891; ed. Sutton Valence and Cambridge Univ.; served in France, the Balkans and Turkey, 1915-1919; demob. with rank of major, 1919; asst. dist. consmr., Gold Coast, May, 1920.

GOSTELOW, CECIL, F.I.A. (Lond.).—B. 1891; ed. Fort St. Model High Schl., S. W.; actuary, N.Z. nat. provident: seek re-election at gen.-elee., 1917; re-el., g.a., 1921; min. of militia and defence, and of naval service in King admtn., Dec., 1921; min. rly., and canals, 1922; senator, Dec., 1926.

GRAHAM, JAMES MACKAY.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Gram. Schl. and S. African Coll.; ent. Transvaal civ. ser., 1903; asst. mag., Johannesburg, 1916; mag., Heidelberg, 1927; do., Lichtenburg, 1930; do., Durban, 1933; do., Cape Town, 1934.

GRAHAM, RICHARD, F.L.A.A., F.R.Econ. S.B. 1896; U.K. postoffice, 1911-20; supt., P.O., Port Elizabeth, 1920; acctnt., Penang, 1923; supt., mails, Penang, Apr., 1925; senr. supt., mails, Port Elizabeth, June, 1927; acctnt., P. & T., F.M.S., Sept., 1928; ag. senr. acctnt. various occasions 1929-32; acct-gen., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1935.

GRAHAM, SIR THOMAS LINDEOCH, K.T. (ACH), (1920), K.C.—B. 1860; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown, and Clare Coll., Camb. (A.), barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1885; adv. sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope; M.L.C., 1898; atty.-gen., May to Oct., 1898; col. sec., 1900-1902; atty.-gen., 1902; ag. prime min., June-Aug., 1902; judge of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope, July, 1904; judge pres., Eastern Districts civil div., Apr., 1913; chrmn. pub. serv. inquiry comm., June, 1918.

GRANNUM, SIR EDWARD ALLAN, KT. BACH, (1926), C.M.G., 1915.—Passed Barbados, W.I., ver. exam., 1896; 3rd clk. regis.'s off., Jan. to Oct., 1885; 2nd clk., Oct., 1885 to Nov., 1887; clk. master-in-chancery off., Nov., 1887, to June, 1888; honours graduate, diploma, Ontario Com. Coll., Canada, affiliated institute chartered accountant, Canada, Dec., 1889; practised public accountant, Barbados, W.I., to Aug., 1897; asst. auditor, Sierra Leone and Gambia, Sept., 1897, to Jan., 1900; acted auditor May to Nov., 1898; asst. auditor, Gold Coast and Lagos, Jan. to Mar., 1900; auditor, March, 1900; introduced system double entry accounts Gold Coast P.O., July, 1901; prepared scheme for introduction of double entry system of accounts for Accra town council, Aug., 1901; local auditor, Cyprus, 1902; auditor-gen., Mauritius, Apr., 1909; revr.-gen., Mauritius, 1912; ag. col. sec., on various occasions, 1919-20 and 1922-23; col. sec., Aug., 1923; admtn. govt. on various occasions, 1924-30; ret., 1931.
GRANT, D. K. S.—Asst. conservator of forests, Tananika Territory, Dec., 1920.

GRANT, EZEKIEL AUGUSTUS.—Ent. British Honduras civ. serv. after compet. exam., as copyist to survey dept., Nov., 1899; 3rd cls. clk. (asst. keeper), King's warehouse, Feb., 1906; 2nd cls. clk., P.W.D., Apr., 1907; on active serv. with B.W.I. Regt. during war, Apr., 1916 to July, 1919; ag. 1st cls. clk., P.W.D., Aug., 1919 to Mar., 1920; acct., P.W.D., Apr., 1920; ag. ch. clk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., Apr., 1921; ag. asst. inspr., schls., during 1921 to 1923; asst. inspr., schls., provisionally, Apr., 1923; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1928; dist. commsnr., Orange Walk, Nov., 1928; ag. atty.-gen., Apr. to May, 1931; ag. dist. commsnr., Belize and offl. admtn. and offl. recr. from Apr., 1931.
GRANT, PETER.—B. 1883; Hong Kong pol., Sept., 1906; inspr., ditto, Dec., 1923; ch., ditto, Mar., 1928; King's pol. med., 1933; asst. supt., pol., Feb., 1938.

GRANT, RICHARD WILLIAM, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Jones' Schl., Mon. and Royal Mil. Acad., Woolwich; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; attd., col. sec's. office, Dec., 1920; offr., cls. V, dist offr., N. Tobal, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1927; dist. offr., Bentong, Dec., 1929; asst. advr., K. Tinggi, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1932; asst. advr., Segamat, Oct., 1933; ag. 1st asst. sec. (A), S.S., Feb., 1935.

GRANTHAM, ALEXANDER WILLIAM GEORGE HAMER, M.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1899; cadet, Hong-Kong, Nov., 1922; attd., C.S.O., 1925; 2nd pol. mag., Sept., 1929; sec. retrenchment comm., July, 1930; asst. to P.M.G. in addn., July, 1930; dist. offr., South, in addn., Dec., 1930; extra asst. col. sec., and sec. to harbr. advisory comtee., 1931; 1st pol. mag. in addn., 1932; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk. of couns., June, 1933; Imp. Def. Coll., 1934; extra asst. col. sec., Apr., 1935; col. sec., Bermuda, 1935; ag. gov., Apr.-May, 1936.

GRANTHAM, WILLIAM, M.A.—B. 1898; tempy. asst. engnr., P.W.D., Perak, F.M.S., Feb., 1924; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Feb., 1924; asst. drnge. and irrign. engnr., Jan., 1932; drnge. and irrign. engnr., Krian, Feb., 1933; sr. drnge. and irrign. engnr., Malay States, May, 1936.
GRAVER, GEORGE STANLEY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1898; ed. Devonport High Schl.; war serv., 1917-19; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923; ag. exec. engnr., May-Dec., 1933.

GRAY, CHAR. EDGAR.—B. 1904; cler. asst., offr. recr.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1919; 6th cls. offr., col. sec's. office, 1921; 5th cls. offr., 1923; 4th cls. offr., 1926; sec. to prisons comtee., 1928; 2nd cls. elk., 1933; ag. prin. elk. and elk., exec. and leg. couns. in 1932 and 1933; cls. I. elk., 1934; sec., floods investigation comtee., 1934; ag. pvt. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., 1934 and 1935; ag. asst. dist. commsnr., 1936.

GRAY, JOHN, B.A., M.D., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), L.M., Rot. Hosp. (Dub.), certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1882; med. offr., gen. hosp., Singapore, Apr., 1909; grade II, F.M.S., Apr., 1910; sr. surgn., class III, Oct., 1913; ch. med. offr., Malacca, Jan., 1919; ch. med. offr., Singapore, Nov., 1920; ag. prin. civil med. offr., S.S., and tempy. M.L.C., for various periods, 1929-31; state surgn., Kelah, Oct., 1931; senr. med. offr., St. Helena, 1936.
GRAY, JOHN MACFARLANE, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (I), L.M. (Rot.), D.P.H. (Univ. Dub.)—B. 1898; health offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1933; ag. M.O.H., 1934; ag. venereal diseases offr., 1935.

GRAY, JOHN MILNER.—B. 1889; ed. Perse Schl. and King's Coll., Cambridge, M.A.; admitted solr., sup. ct., 1923; called to bar, Gray's Inn, Apr., 1932; war serv. with B.E.F., France (twice wounded); asst. dist. commsnr., Uganda Prot., 1920; mag., 1924; ag. solr.-gen., 1929; ag. police judge, 1933; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1934; O.A.G., May, 1935.

GRAY, R.—B. 1880; clk., treasy., 1902; clk., rev. dept., 1904; dep. recr., rev., Lichtenburg, 1905; clk., rev. dept., Jo'burg, 1908; Pretoria, 1912; prin. elk., 1921; survr., 1928; acctnt., 1929; recr., E. London, 1931; do., Pretoria, 1932; do., Durban, 1933.

GRAY, SIR REGINALD, KT. BACH, (1920), K.C. (1908).—B. 1851; called to the Inner Temp., bar, 1875; compiled, in 1884, Bermuda laws from 1690-1883; revising offr., Bermuda, 1889-93; compiled, in 1903, Bermuda laws from 1690-1902; counsel to statute law consolidation comtee. since 1900; atty.-gen., Bermuda, 28th May, 1900; ag. chief just. in Oct. and Nov., 1911; ex-officio mem. of exec. coun.; M.L.A., 1895-1897, 1900-1906, and from 1907; ret.

GRAY, THEODORE GRANT, M.B., Ch.B. (Aberdeen), M.P.C.—B. 1884; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schl. and Aberdeen Univ.; asst. med. offr., 1911; med. supt., Nelson (N.Z.), mental hosp., 1920; med. supt., Auckland mental hosp., 1924; dep. inappr.gen. mental hosps., N.Z., 1925; inappr-gen., 1927.

GRAY, MAJOR WILLIAM BAIN, C.B.E. (1935).—B. 1886; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh; Univ. of Edinburgh; M.A., 1st cl. hons., Gladstone Prize and Ph.D., and New Coll., Oxford, B.Litt.; some time Carnegie Research Fellow; lect., Tech. Coll., Brighton; examnr., Univ. of Edinburgh, Oxford and Cambridge joint bd. and Oxford local exams.; 2nd lieut., 6th Batt., the Royal Scots, Feb., 1912; mob., Aug., 1914; adjt., Jan.-Dec., 1915; capt., Aug., 1916; gen staff, Aldershot commd., Dec., 1918-Apr., 1921; major, Army Educncl. Corps, Nov., 1920; major, R.A.R.O., Mar., 1923; commr., educn., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1924; (appt. altered to dir., Nov., 1927); mem., educn. comsn., Nov., 1924; chmn., Georgetown public free liby., May, 1926; mem., ct. of policy and combined ct., June, 1927; chmn. diamond and gold industries comsn., Sept., 1927; mem. first exec. and leg. couns. under reformed constitution, July, 1928; ag. col. sec. and gov't dep. on various occasions.

GREAVES, ARTHUR WILLIAM.—B. 1888; ed. King Edward's High Schl., B'ham; insp., pol., K. Lumpur, July, 1919; 1st inspnr., cent. stn., K. Lumpur, June, 1923; asst. supvr., cust., F.M.S., July, 1923; supt., cust., prev. br., F.M.S., Nov., 1923; supt., trade and cust., Nov., 1931; supt., cust. and excise, Ipoh, Nov., 1933.

GREAVES, AUBREY VERNON, M.B. (Tor.), M.C.P. & S. (Ont.), D.T.M. (L'pool).—B. 1886; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Univ. of Toronto; Milne med. in trop. med. Univ. of L'pool; on active serv. as capt. Canadian A.M.C., 1918-19; govt. bacteriologist and asst. med. offr. of health and med. offr. in charge V.D. clinic and infant welfare clinic in addn., Bahamas, 1926; sent to Miami, Florida, to investigate epidemic of small-pox, 1926; ag. ch. med. offr., Apr.-Dec., 1927; bacteriologist, Bahamas, 1928; asst. bacteriologist, Hong Kong, 1929; bacteriologist, 1931; lect. in forensic med. and toxicology, Univ. of Hong Kong.

GREAVES, SIR WILLIAM HERBERT, Kt., B.A. (1904), K.C. —B. 1857; B.A., Oxon., honours, juris., 1879; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., 1880; ag. solr.-gen., Barbados, Aug., 1884, to July, 1885 and July, 1886, to Jan., 1887, when confirmed; J.P., Sept., 1885; escheator-gen., 1887; Q.C. for Barbados, 1890; mem. exec. coun., 1890; acted as atty. gen., July to Dec., 1887, and June to Dec., 1891; mem. of house of assem. and of bd. of educn., 1888-1902; atty.-gen., Mar., 1896; ch. just., Apr., 1902; ret.

GREEN, FRANKLIN WALTER VICTOR.—B. 1891; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; 6th clk. offr., 1914; asst. commr., 1917; sec. admin. bd., July, 1923; 3rd gde. commr., July, 1925; 2nd gr. offr., dist. adminstr., Jan., 1932; asst. commissary and dist. commr., Georgetown and E. Bank, Demerara, May, 1934; dist. commr., W. Demerara, Jan., 1935; do., Roseau, Apr., 1936.

GREEN, GEORGE.—B. 1894; ed. Westminster City Schl.; on mil. serv., Oct., 1914 to Mar., 1919; 2nd lieut., The Buffs; wounded in France; temp. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919 to Feb., 1920; maj. of lab., Sept., 1920 to Dec., 1920; C.O. Dec., 1920 to June, 1922; appld. cler. offr., C.O., June, 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, Jan., 1928.
GREEN, GEORGE C.—B. 1882; appld. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the cv. ser., and assigned to C.O., 26th Aug., 1901; maj. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.

GREEN, GEORGE C., M.B.E. (1926)—B. 1887; clerical asst., col. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1916; 6th clk. clk., 1913; 3rd cls. clk., 1918; sec. comte., for considering disposal of colony land, 1917; 2nd cls. clk., 1920; sec. to cv. ser. commr., 1923; sec. to Br. Guiana section, Wembley, 1924; prin. clk., 1924; sec., rly. comm., 1925; liaison offr., partly delegation to Br. Guiana, 1926; sec., Br. Guiana delegation to W. India confce., London, 1926; sec., civ. serv. comm., 1926-27; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec. and clk. etc., leg. coun., various occasions, 1926-30; sec. to Br. Guiana constitution comn., 1927; O.A.G. in 1928; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1930; asst. col. sec., 1931; prin. asst. col. sec., 1934.
GREEN, HAROLD.—B. 1887; ed. at Que.

GREEN, HUGH ABOTT, M.C. and Bar.—1888; ed. Marlborough Coll.; trooper, Na pol., 1906; served in Zulu rebellion, 1906; maj. and clasp; war serv. with 4th (R. Irish) Dragoons, and Royal Inniskilling Fusiliers; maj. rank of temp. capt.; ment. in desp., 1912; N. Rhodesia, 1911; asst. nat. comrn., 1912; nat. comrn., 1916; asst. mag., 1926; dist. dist. nat. comrn., 1929; grade I., 1935; ag. prov. comn., grade II., 1935; ag. prov. comn., July, 1935 to Apr., 1936.

GREEN, JOSEPH.—B. 1887; U.K. post off., Aug., 1914; on mil. serv., lieut., Indian Ar. Res., 1915-19; supt., mails, Penang, Feb., 1916; sen. tr. acctnt., Sept., 1924 and Nov., 1926; sen. tr. mails, Spore, Dec., 1926; supt., N. Semb. Mar., 1932; ag. contrlr., Penang and P. Wall., Dec., 1933; do., Perak, Dec., 1935.

GREEN, CAPT. MAURICE JOHN, R.N.—V.D.—B. 1878; ed. Sea Point pub., ad. S.A. Coll.; ast. clk. of papers, Cape A., 1896; clk. of papers, 1897; com. Cape leg. comrn., 1904, and Union senate gent. ush., black rod, 1916; clk. ast. clk. of senate, 1930; sworn att. clerk (Dutch); sec. and shorthand writer (govt. comms.); ast. sec. speaker's conf. const. senate; war serv., Anglo-Boer, in Great War, in Ger., S.W. Afr., as a cabinet secretariat; O.C., S.A. divm., 1929-34.

GREEN, RICHARD THOMAS BONG B.S. (Melb.), D.T.M. (Liv.).—B. 18 Gurne Church of Eng. Gram. Schl. and Trinity Coll., Univ. of Melbourne; med. offr., F.M.S., June, 1925; pathologist II, inst. med. resch., M.S., Mar., 1926; malaria resch. offr., I, inst. med. resch., Jan., 1929; Milne Medal, Liv. S. of M., Mar., 1930; rep. S.S. and F.M.S. at 2nd Inter. Cong. on Malaria, Algiers, May, 1930; mem. of malaria comsn., health organ., L. of N., Dec., 1930; ag. bacteriologist, Inst. Med. Resch., Apr., 1932-Feb., 1933; bacteriologist, F.M.S., and malaria resch. offr., I in addtn., Aug., 1934.

GREENBERG, LEOPOLD.—B. 1885; ed. at Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, and S. African Coll., Cape Town; B.A., LL.B.; called to Transvaal bar, 1911; practised at Johannesburg, 1911-24; puisne judge, Transvaal Prov. divn. sup. ct., Nov., 1924.

GREENE, HON. COL. EDWARD MACKENZIE, C.M.G. (1909), K.C.V.D.—Hon. colonel in army; served in Zulu war, 1878-1879 (medal, with clasp); S. African war, 1899-1902, in command of Natal Carbineers (Queen's medal, with three clasps; twice mentioned in despatches); mem. of Natal legis. assem., 1893 to date of Union; min. for rly. and harbours, 13th July, 1908; Natal deleg. to S. African National Convention, Oct., 1908, to May, 1909; represented Natal at Impl. Conf. on defence, 1909; mem., rly. and harbours bd., Union of S. Africa, 1910 to 1918.
GREENE, CAPT. GORDON STRATTON.—B. 1887; ed. Keble Coll., Oxford; dip. forestry (Oxon); war serv., 1914-19; asst. cons., forests, Gold Coast, 1920; prov. forest offr., 1930.

GREENE, MAURICE CHERRY.—B. 1881; ed. at Rathmines schl. and St. Stephen's Green schl., Dublin, and Dublin Univ.; B.A. 1905; student, King's Inns, Dublin, 1910-11; dist. offr., N. Nigeria, July, 1912; served with W.A.F.F., 1914-15; station mag., 1918; pol. mag., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Mar., 1928; ag. puisne judge, Sept.-Dec., 1935.

GREENFIELD, LIEUT.-COL. HERBERT, O.B.E., R. of O.—B. 1880; ed. privately; served in S. African War, 1901-02; joined R.A.V.C.; lieut., May, 1903; served in India, 1903-08; capt., May, 1908; attd. staff coll., 1910-12; sp. serv. and research work, N. and S. China, 1912-14; served in Great war, 1914-18 Tsingtao operns.; major, July, 1915; D.A.D.V.S., 10th divn., Gallipoli; Greater Macedonia, Serbia, Bulgaria, 1916-16; temp. lieut.-col., 1916; D.A.D.V.S., France and Belgium, 1917-18; ment. in desps.; A.D.V.S. (lieut.-col. temp.) Rhine Army, 1919; served in Waziristan operns., 1920 as D.A.D.V.S. and D.D.V.S. (temp. lieut.-col.) Wazir Force, 1920-21; ment. in desps.; D.A.D.V.S., India and in Burma, 1921-24; exec. duty, Aldershott, 1925-28; vety. offr., Barbados, Oct., 1928.

GREENHALGH, N., B.Sc., Hons. (Vic.).—B. 1888; agric. and science master, Antigua gram. schl., 1910; lecturer in agric. science to students of training coll., Antigua; asst. inspr. of schl., Barbados, 1912; lecturer in educ. and master of method, training inst., Codrington Coll.; ag. chief inspr. of schl., Trinidad, June to Oct., 1915; inspr., schl., Barbados, 1934.

GREENHILL, MARCUS ARTHUR.—B. 1889; entd. C.O., Sep., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on mily. serv., May, 1917 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st June, 1923; staff offr., July, 1934.

GREENIDGE, CHARLES WILTON WOOD.—B. 1889; educ. Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Downing Coll., Camb.; B.A. (Nat. Sci. Tripos) 1910; LL.B., 1911; called to bar, Mid. Temp., 1912; 1st cls. hon. criminal law; admitted to bar, Barbados, 1912; parochial treas., St. James', Barbados, 1915; mag., dist. D. St. Kitts, 1919; mag., dist. D. Barbados, 1923; judge, ct. of appl., Barbados, 1925; mag., Port of Spain, Trinidad, 1927; ag. solr.-gen., Oct., 1928 to June, 1929; ag. puisne judge in 1929; ag. atty.-gen., Oct. to Nov., 1929; ag. regurl., Oct., Nov., 1930; ag. solr.-gen., Jan., 1931; ch. just., Br. Honduras, 1932; solr.-gen., Nigeria, 1936.

GREENING, CAPTAIN LESLIE STUART, M.C. (1917), BAR. (1918).—B. 1895; served European War, 1914-18; asst. inspr., pol., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920; cadet, June, 1925; asst. dist. offr., June, 1927; ag. dist. offr., and ag. res. mag. on various occasions, 1928-35; admstive. offr., Cyprus; commr., Nov., 1935.

GREENLAND, STANLEY ARTHUR.—Served with Natal Field Force, Rebellion, 1906; clk., govt., sec. dept., Papua, Mar., 1912; J.P., Sept., 1912; ag. asst. res. mag., Ioma, May, 1913; ag. asst. res. mag., Kairuku, Mar., 1915; inspr., dept. native affairs, Mar., 1917; ag. curator, intestate estates, regist.-gen., etc., Apr., 1917; ag. ch. inspr., native affairs, 1918; ag. Papua govt. agt., Sydney, N.S.W., Dec., 1919; travelling inspr., native affairs, July, 1922; J.P. for N.S.W., Oct., 1923; ag. mem., land bd., Papua, Nov., 1924; ch. inspr., native affairs, Dec., 1924.
GREENSHIELDS, HON. ROBERT A. E., LL.D., D.C.L.—B. 1861; ed. Danville Acad. and McGill Univ., Canada; ag. ch. just., superior ct., Quebec, 1929; ch. just., superior ct., Quebec, 1933.

GREENWAY, PERCY JAMES, F.L.S.—B. 1897; ed. East Anglian Inst. of Agr., Chelmsford, R. Horticultural Soc., Wisley, Ripley, and R. Botanic Gardens, Kew; mily. serv., France and Italy, 1916-19; herbarium asst., R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1922-25; do., and ag. demonstr. in systematic botany, Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford, 1925-28; systematic botanist, E. African agrl. research stn., Armani, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

GREENWOOD, GEOFFREY MARTIN, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1908; ed. Rugby and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; tropical African serv. course, 1930-31; cadet, Uganda, 1931; asst. dist. offr., 1933; asst. dist. commr., Palestine, 1935; admstive. offr. (asst. sec.), Cyprus, July, 1935; ag. commr., Sept., 1935; ag. ch. asst. sec., July-Aug., 1936; clk., exec. conn., July, 1936.

GREGG, JAMES REALI (R.A., Cantab.)—B. 1899; ed. Royal Belfast Acadl. Inst. and Harvard and Cambridge Unvrs.; called to N. Ireland bar, 1923; mem., Gray's Inn (Lond.), and King's Inn (Dublin); chmn., ct. of referees under Min. of Lab., N. Ireland, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., St. Vincent, May, 1929; atty.-gen., Oct., 1930; ag. ch. just. on several occasions; govt. rep. on St. Vincent arrowroot bd.; attd. to C.O. for short period, Nov., 1931; pol. mag., Gold Coast, Jan., 1933; crown coun., Nyassaland, 1936.
GRELL, JESSIE MITCHINSON POTTER, M.B., B.S. Cantab., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1880; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, June, 1908; dist. med. offr., Aug., 1912.
GRENFELL-HICKS, J. B.—B. 1891; Rhodesian pol., 1911-16; lieut., Rhodesia Native Regt., 1916-19; Rhodesian adminstr., 1919-20; asst. susp., Kenya pol., Nov., 1920; staff offr., 1923; sup., 1926.

GRETON, RONALD HENRY.—B. 1907; B.A. (Br. Col. Canada); trop. African serv. course, Oxford, 1928; cadet, Nigeria, 1929; seconded, Gambia, 1931; pvt. sec. to ag. gov., 1931 and 1933; asst. col. sec., 1933; commr., 1935; ag. col. sec., Apr.-May and June-Oct., 1936.

GRICE, NORMAN, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1893; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; attd., Chinese sect., Perak, Feb., 1923; attd., cust. dept., Mar., 1923; attd., Chinese sect., Jan., 1925; attd., prot., Chinese, Selangor, Jan., 1925; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, Penang, Feb., 1927; asst. contr., rubber, S'pore, Mar., 1927; asst. dist. judge, S'pore, in addn., Aug., 1927; cl. III, Oct., 1929; 2nd mag., S'pore, Sept., 1933; offr. cls. II., Oct., 1934.

GRIER, Sir SELWYN MACGREGOR, K.C.M.G. (1936), Kt. Bach. (1934), C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1878; ed. at Marlboro' Coll. (classical scholar) and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (classical scholar); 2nd class tripus, 1900; 1st class French and English special, 1901; B.A. 1900; called to the bar, Nov., 1910; asst. res., Northern Nigeria, 1906; passed in Hausa, 1907; 3rd class res., 1st Oct., 1908; in charge of Zaria prov., April, 1910; and from May to Nov., 1911; sec., native affrs., Nigeria, Jan., 1921; col. sec., Trinidad, Aug., 1929; ag. gov. for various periods 1929-1934; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Windward Is., 1935; ret., 1937.
GRIERSON, JOHN DARBYSHIRE, M.B., Ch.B., L. Edin., F.R.C.S., Edin., F.R.C.S., Eng.—B. 1898; med. ser., B. Guiana, 1927; res. surg. and surg. spec., pub. hosp., 1931.

GRIFFIN, SIR CHARLES JAMES, KT. BACH. (1923), K.C., Ireland (1920), K.C., Gibraltar, (1914).—B. 1875; B.A. (hons.), Royal Univ., Ireland; 1st scholar in modern literature, exhibitioner; Chancellor's gold medallist; called to the Irish Bar, June, 1898; went Connaught circuit; apptd. Crown prosecutor, B.C.A. Protectorate (now Nyasaland), July, 1901; ag. chief judicial offr. and H.B.M. vice-consul, Nov., 1901 to June, 1902; ag. judge of High Court, Feb. to Oct., 1904 and Nov., 1905 to Feb., 1906; atty.-gen., Nov., 1905; judge of High Court, Feb., 1906, and member of H.B.M. Court of Appeal for Eastern Africa; chairman of comsn. on trading and licensing, May to Sept., 1909 (recd. thanks of Prot. govt.); sole comsnr. for revision of Nyasaland laws, 1913, and recd. thanks of Prot. govt.; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, Oct., 1914; King's proctor for prize ct. proceedings; K.C. for the colony, Dec., 1914; chief justice, Leeward Is., 1919; sole comsnr. for revision of laws of Antigua and Montserrat, 1921; ch. just., high ct., Uganda, 1921-32; sole comsnr. for revision of laws of Uganda, 1923, and recd. thanks of govt. in coun.; chmn., Bataka land comsn., 1924.

GRIFFIN, JOHN BOWES.—B. 1903, ed. Clongowes & Trinity Coll., Dublin; M.A., LL.D., 1st cls., moderatorship, gold medallist; prizeman; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1926; cadet, prov. admtn., Uganda, 1927; seconded, legal and judl. depts. and secrts. various occasions, 1927-29; asst. dist. offr., 1929; regar., high ct., Uganda, 1929; ag. crown comsn., 1931-33; sec., E.A. law offrs. comte., 1933; sec., comsn. of inquiry, adm. of just., E.A., 1933; crown coun., Uganda, 1933; ag. solr.-gen. on various occasions, 1934-35; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1934-Feb., 1935; comsnr. for revn. of laws, Uganda, 1935; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1936; ag. ch. just., 1936.

GRIFFIN, NEVILL ALFRED MALCOLM.—B. 1888; ed. Blundell's; pol. probr., S. Stllntns, Feb., 1911; asst. supt. pol., Penang, Mar., 1912; asst. supt. pol., Malacca and assst. prot., Chinese, July, 1912; seconded, Kedah, Oct., 1914; adjt., S.S. pol., Oct., 1916; supt. pol. (C.P.O.), Penang, Mar., 1923; dep. comsnr. pol., Selangor, 1932; ag. comsnr., pol., F.M.S., Feb., 1933; supt. pol., S'pore, Oct., 1935.

GRIFFIN, WALTER ALBERT.—B. 1898; European statn. mast., F.M.S. Rly., Apr., 1919; traffic inspr., Apr., 1917; asst. trafic inspr., July, 1921; asst. trafic man., 1925; senr. trafic inspr., Aug., 1932; ag. divnsl. supt., July, 1933; Apr., 1934.

GRIFFITH, C.—B. 1884; clk., Br. post. tel. serv., 1901; clk., Natal post and tal. serv., 1911; clk., engrg. headrs., Union of S. Africa, 1914; trafic inspr., 1922; asst. engrg., 1923; ag., 1927; ch. engrg., 1933.

GRIFFITH, GERALD VICT ALEXANDER, B.T. M.D., (Dublin).—B. 1903; med. offr., med. insp., Hong Kong, 1930; med. offr., in ch. of scot. families, Victoria Gaol, infectious disease and pol. surg., Hong Kong, 1931; asst. M.O., Kowloon hosp., Jan., 1935; med. offr. in ch., May, 1936.

GRIFFITH, SIR WM. BRANDFORD, KT. BACH. (1898).—B. 1858; Gilchrist scholar, 1878; M.A. (Lond.), 1880; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., 1881; ag. Queen's advoc., G. Coast Col., Apr., Oct., 1884; dist. comsnr., 1885; ag. puissant, Lagos, Jan., and Cape Coast, May, 1886; Queen's advoc., 1886 and 1887; compiled ordinances of G. Coast Col., 1887; res. mag., Jan., 1888; R.M., St. Catherine, Feb., 1893; ag. gen., May to Dec., 1893; ch. just., G. Coast Col., May, 1896; admstd. govt. of Lagos, Jan., Aug., 1896; dep. gov. for G. Coast Col., Aug. to 1897; revised ordinances of G. Coast Col., 1897 and 1903; retired, 1911.
GRIFFITHS, J. A., F.R.C.V.S., F.L.S., Vety. offr., Nyasaland, 1913; ch. vety. at Nyasaland, 1919; dep. ch. vety. offr., Nigeria.

GRIFFITHS, JEFFREY ARTHUR, F.R.C.V.S., B. 1884; Ed. Univ. of Liverpool; veterinary, Nyasaland, 7th June, 1913; attached Intell. Off. Nyasaland Field Force, Aug., 1914 to July, 1917; seconded to rinderpest comsn., German E.A., July, 1917 to May, 1919; ag. rinderpest comsn., Aug., 1918 to May, 1919; chief vet. offr., June, 1919; ment. in desps. (gallantry in act.) South Africa, 1901; ment. in desps., services to Field Force, Nyasaland, 1916 and 1917; ag. dist. agr., Oct. to Dec., 1920.

GRIFFITHS, JOHN HABOLD CROFT, O.F. (1935).—B. 1895; ed. Newtown County Sch., Univ. Coll. of Wales; served in France, Mesopotamia, 1915-17; prob., N. Rhodesia, 1918; asst. native comsnr., Sept., 1919; nat. comsnr., Jan., 1925; asst. mag., Jan., 1925; asst. offr., grade II, Apr., 1929; asst. sec., Apr., 1930; ag. prin. asst. ch. sec. for various periods, 1930-35; dep. treas., 1935; ag. treas., Apr.-Nov., 1935.
GRIFFITHS, Lieut. NOEL STEWART, R.N. B. 1896; ed., Leatherhead Sch.; supt., educ. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, 5th Apr., 1922.

GRIFFITHS, W., A.M.I.E.E.—Imp. P. Office, 1899; R.N.A.S., 1915; twice ment. in desps.; transfd., unemployed list, 1919; seconded to Kenya and Uganda as engnr. ast., 1920; tel. engnr., 1922; ag. tel. engnr., 1923.

GRIGG, SIR EDWARD WILLIAM MACL. K.C.M.G. (1928), K.C.V.O. (1920), C.M.G. (1919), C.V.O. (1919), D.S.O. (1918), M.C.—B. 1878; ed. Winchester (schol.) and New Coll., Oxford (schol.); 2nd cls. mod., 1900, 3rd cls. lit., 1902; Gaisford Greek Verse Prize, 1902; editorial staff "Times," 1903; ast. "Outlook," 1905-6; rejoined editorial staff "Times," 1908; joined Grenadier Guards, 1914; became G.S.O.I., Guards divn.; mil. sec., Prince of Wales, Canada, Australia, N.Z.; relinquished coman. with rank of lieut.-col., 1918. Mr. Lloyd George, 1921-22; sec., hodes trustees, 1923; M.P., Oldham, 1923-25; jv. and comdr.-in-ch., and high commr. for transport, Kenya, 1925-31.

GRIMBLE, ARTHUR FRANCIS, C.M.G., M.A., R.A.I.—B. 1888; ed. Magdalene Coll., Cambridge, France, Germany; cadet, Gilbert and Ilice Is., Mar., 1914; passed cadet, Mar., 1915; sp. commr. for W. Pacific, Aug., 1915; ag. dist. fr., Tarawa, Mar., 1916; dist. offr., Aug., 1916; int. Gilberta, Feb., 1917; S. Gilberta, Oct., 1918; sp. commr. to settle native land disputes throughout col., and organise records, Jan., 1920; z. res. commr., Apr., to July, 1920 and Jan. to eb., 1922; 1st dist. offr., 1924; ag. res. commr., far. to Sept., 1924; res. commr., Gilbert and Ilice Is., 1926; admstr., St. Vincent, July, 1933; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Seychelles, 1936.

GRIMMER, WARD CHIPMAN HAZEN, K.C., L.D.—B. 1858; admitted atty., 1880; barrister, 1881; K.C., 1902; elected to prov. leg., N. Bruns., 1903; re-elected, 1908, 1912; surv. gen., N. Bruns., 1908; atty. gen., 1911; puisne judge, appeal ct. of N. Bruns., Jan., 1914.

GRINDLEY-FERRIS, IVON, K.C., B.A. (Cape), B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.).—B. 1876; ed. Diocesan coll., Rondebosch; admitted barrister, Inner Temple, June, 1900; advoc., sup. ct., Transvaal, Nov., 1902; K.C., 1922; ag. judge, high ct., W. Africa in 1924 and 1928, and as judge, Transvaal prov. divn. during 1927; judge pres., native high ct., Natal, Mar., 1930; seconded to Natal prov. divn., sup. ct., May, 1930; judge, up ct. Transvaal prov. divn., 1933.

GRIST, DONALD HONEY, Camb. Univ. Dip. of Agric.—B. 1891; ed. Huntingdon Grammar Schl., Camb. County Sch. and Camb. Univ. Schl. of Agr.; agric. instr., agric. dept., F.M.S., June, 1914; on duty in Ceylon, Feb., 1921; ag. agric. instr., Sultan Idris Coll., Tanjong Malim, n.addn., Dec., 1922; agric. economist, 1924; dep. regar.-gen., statistics, S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., June, 1931; agric. economist and edr., S.S. and F.M.S., Sept., 1931.

GRIST, MAJOR H. G., R. of O., Fusiliers.—B. 1881; ed. Aldenham Schl., Herts.; 2nd lieut., 7th bn., R. Fu-lliers, 1899; lieut., 1900; capt., 1903; R. of O., 1907; S. African War, Queen's med.; capt., 14th (S.) bn., Northumberland Fusiliers, Nov., 1914; B.E.F., France, 1915-19; major, Oct., 1915; ag. lieut.-col., July-Oct., 1916; staff capt., Abbaville area, 1918; ment. in desig.; ch. of pol. and offr. commg. local forces, St. Vincent, Jan., 1924.
GROBLER, HON. P. G. W., M.P.—Rep. Rustenburg elec. divn. in Union of S. Africa parlmt. since 1915; min. of lands, 30th June, 1924; del., Ottawa Econ. Conf., 1932; min. for native affrs., Mar., 1933.

GROOM, HON. SIR LITTLETON ERNEST, K.C.M.G. (1924), K.C., M.A., LL.M.—B. 1867; M.P. for Darling Downs, Queensland; elected to 1st hse. of rep., C. of Aust., 1901; re-elected, Dec., 1903; mem. of royal comm., on Iron Bonus, 1902, and Navigation Bills, 1904-5; several times Crown prosecutor, and twice ag. judge of dist. ct., Queensland; min. for home affairs, C. of Aust., July, 1905; atty.-gen., Oct., 1906; min. of external affairs, June, 1909, to Apr., 1910; min. of trade and customs, June, 1913-Sept., 1914; hon. min., Feb., 1917; vice-pres., exec. coun., Nov., 1917 to Mar., 1918; min. for works and rlyas, Mar., 1918; ag. atty.-gen. from Apr., 1918; atty.-gen., Dec., 1921; atty.-gen., Bruce ministry, 1923; speaker, house of reps., Jan., 1926-29; re-el., M.H.R., Dec., 1931.

GROSSKOPF, JOHANNES FRIEDRICH WILHELM.—B. 1886; ed. Bloemfontein and Stellenbosch; M.A. (Stellenb.), 1906; studied Univ. of Berlin, Vienna, Leiden, Zurich; Jurr. Dr. (Leiden), 1913; prof., Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1920-34; mem., Carnegie poor-white investgn., 1929-30; mem., co-opern. and agr. credit comm., 1933; chm., div. of economics and markets, dept. of agr. and forestry, Pretoria, 1935.

GROSSMITH, C. A.—B. 1895; 2nd lieut., E. Lancs. Regt., 1916; lieut., 1918; served, Mesopotamia and N. Persia, 1917-19; ment. in desig.; demob., 1919; attd., Br. mission in Czechoslovakia, 1919; temp. employed, clearing office, enemy debts, Berlin, 1921-23; enemy debts dept., London, 1924-26; cler. offr., C.O., 12th July, 1926; asst. prin., 1st Oct., 1934.

GUILLEMARD, SIR LAURENCE NUNNS, G.C.M.G. (1927), K.C.B. (1910), K.C.M.G. (1923), C.B. (1905).—B. 1862; ed. Charterhouse, and Trin. Coll. Camb.; entered Treasury, 1888; priv. sec. to Sir W. Harcourt and Sir M. Hicks-Beach, 1892-1902; deputy chrmn., board of inland rev., 1902; chmn., bd. of customs and excise, 1909; gov., S. Sttlmts., 7th Feb., 1920; ret., 1927.

GULLETT, HON. SIR HENRY SOMER, K.C.M.G. (1933).—B. 1878; served European War, 1915-18; dir., Australian War Museum and offl. historian, A.I.F. in Palestine; dir., Australian immigrn. bureau, Oct., 1920; M.H.R., C'wealth of Australia from 1925; min. for trade and cust., 1928-29; dep. leader of opposition, Nov., 1929; min. for trade and cust., Lyons' ministry, 1932; Ottawa Conf., 1932; min. without portfolio for negotiating trade treaties, 1934.
GULLIVER, E. G.—Cust., asst., Kenya, July, 1916; collr., cust., Jan., 1926.

GULLOCH, WILLIAM SUTHERLAND.—B. 1893; 2nd lieut., Scottish Rifles, 1914; lieut., Scottish Rifles, 1916; lieut., Gold Coast Regt., 1918; capt., Gold Coast Regt., 1919; served France and Togoland; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, 1919; ag. commr., pol., Eastern prov., 1924-5; commr., pol., Cent. prov., 1926; ch. pol., Gibraltar, Nov., 1927.

GUNARATNA, WALTER DANDRIS, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; attd., Galle kach., Jan., 1928; attd., Kandy kach., May, 1928; ag. pol. mag., Dambulla, Dec., 1928 to Jan., 1929; extra office asst., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1929; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1930; pol. mag., Puttalam, Oct., 1931; office asst., Badulla kach., Aug., 1932; asst. govt. agt., Galle, Feb., 1935.

GUNASEKARA, SEPTIMUS THEODOSIOS, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.P.H. (Lond.), L.M.S. (Ceylon).—B. 1881; 1st house surg., gen. hosp., Colombo, Ceylon, Sept., 1904; med. offr., Kandy, Jan., 1907; ag. 3rd surg., gen. hosp., Colombo, Oct., 1908; dir., ankylostomiasis campaign, Dikoya and Amella, Oct., 1917 to Mar., 1920; sany. offr., Apr., 1920; ag. sany. commr., Oct., 1922; asst. dir., sany. services, Oct., 1925; dep. dir., med. and sany. services, 1935.

GUNAWARDANA, DON CHARLES RAJAKARUNA, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1924; attd., Matara kach., Jan., 1925; ditto, Jaffna kach., Apr., 1926; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, June, 1927; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Sept., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Ratnapura, Jan., 1930; do., Colombo, Jan., 1931; do., Batticaloa, Mar., 1931; office asst., Kandy kach., Dec., 1931; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Feb., 1934; do., Mannar, Aug., 1935.

GUNDRY, JOHN ARNOLD, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1900; ed. St. Bees Schol. and Faraday House; engrnr.-in-ch.'s office, G.P.O., Lond., 1920; asst. engrnr., posts and tels., Nigeria, Mar., 1924; engrnr., Mar., 1929; divnl. engrnr., Apr., 1933; engrnr.-in-ch., posts and tels., 1936.
GUNN, HON. JOHN.—B. 1885; M.P. for Adelaide, 1915; leader, party. Labour Party, 1918; premier and treas., S. Australia, Apr., 1924-26; mem., migration and development comsn. of Australia, 1926.

GURNEY, HENRY LOVELL GOLDSWORTHY.—B. 1898; asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, June, 1921; dist. offr., 1923; ag. sec., secretariat, 1927; asst. col. sec., Jamaica, Feb., 1935; resigned, Aug., 1935; temp. attd., C.O. as asst. prin., Oct., 1935; dist. offr. and asst. sec., secr., Kenya, 1936.

GURUSINHA, KANKANAN TANATURU SUMATRIPALA.—B. 1877; apptd. cls. V Ceylon div. serv., Mar., 1925; office asst., Kalatura kach., Mar., 1925; addnl. asst. commr., stamps, May, 1930; attd., Galle kach., Oct., 1933; ag. office asst., do., Mar., 1935.

GUTHRIE, HON. HUGH, P.C. (Can.), K.C.—B. 1866; ed. Coll. Inst. Guelph and Osgoode Hall, Toronto; el. to H. of C., Canada at g.e., 1900; re-el. at g.e., 1904, 1908, 1911, 1917, 1921, 1925 and 1926; solr. gen., Canada, 1917; min. of militia and defence, 1920; min. of national defence in Meighen adminstr., 1926; min. of just. and atty.-gen. in Bennett adminstr., 1930; ch. comsnr., rly. comsnr., 1935.

GUTSCHE, HON. CLEMENS, B.A., LL.B., C.B.E., V.D.—B. 1877; ed. Dale Coll., King Williams Town and S. African Coll., Cape Town (gold med., lit. and philos.); on active serv., Anglo-Boer War (2 meds. and clasps); admitted to Cape bar, 1904; edr., Cape Town rept., 1908-10; and joint edr., Repts. of Cape Prov. Divn.; served in Great War, 1914-18; offr. in commd., Table Bay defences; Gen. Serv. and Victor. meds., twice ment. in desps., C.B.E. and V.D.; resumed professorship, law, S.A. Coll., 1919; first judge, high ct., S. West Africa, 1919; judge, eastern dists. local divn., sup. ct., Grahamstown, 1930.

GYBBON-MONYPENNY, RICHARD MONTAGU, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1905; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; attd., Badulla kach., Jan., 1928; office asst., Kurunegala kach., May, 1930; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1933; attd., rubber contrlr.'s office, July, 1934; dist. judge, N. Eliya, Aug., 1934; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Aug., 1935.
HAARER, ALRC ERNEST, F.L.S.—B. 1894; ed. Birkenhead Inst., Battersea Gram. Schol. and Royal Horticultural Coll. (dipl. hort.); dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1921; agrl. lect., 1930.

HACKING, RT. HON. DOUGLAS HEWITT, P.C. (1929), O.B.E., M.P.—B. 1884; ed. Giggleswick Schol. and Manchester Univ.; comsnr., E. Lanes. Regt., 1914; served two years France (ment. in desps.); M.P., Chorley Divn. of Lanes since 1918; parly. sec. to Sir James Craig (now Lord Craigavon) at miny. of pensions, 1920, and Admy., 1920-21; parly. pte. sec. to Sir L. Worthington-Evans, Bart., M.P., S. of S. for War, 1921-22; vice-Chamberlain, royal household, 1922-24, and 1924-25; conserv. whip, 1922-25; parly. under sec. of state for home dept. and rep. of office of warden of c., 1925-27; sec. D.O.T., parly. under sec. of state for foreign affrs., 1927-29; parly. under sec. H.O., 1933-34; finan. sec., W.A., June, 1934; parly. under sec. of state for dom. affrs., 29th Nov., 1935.

HADDILL, CYRIL F.—B. 1902; apptd. the compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 29th Apr., 1928; HAIG, RUPERT ALEXANDER.—B. 1894; Glenalmond and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. hons. schol., jurisprudence, 1925; called to bar, 1927; asst. dist. comsnr., Nyasaland Prot., 1928; ag. asst. atty. gen., various periods, 1929-34; ag. town mag., 1931; ag. atty., Oct., 1933; res. mag., Zanzibar, 1934.

HALES, EVERETT OLIVE.—B. 1876; ed. N.Z. pub. trust office, 1894; offr. in charge of branch, 1914; inspr., 1919; local dep. trustee, 1920; asst. pub. trustee, 1922; ed. N.Z. state advances office, 1931; pub. trust, 1934.
HALFYARD, HON. W.W.—Elected M.H.A., Fogo, Newfoundland, 1913; min. of agric. mines, 1917; M.E.C., 1917; col. sec., 1918.

HALIFAX, 3RD VISCOUNT (c. 1866), Hon. EDWARD FREDERICK LINDLEY WOOD.—B. 1878; ed. Eton and Christ Church and All Souls' Order (M.A. and fellow); major, Yorkshire Dragoons M.P., Ripon divn., Yorks, 1910-25; under sec. state for the cols., Apr., 1921; min. of educ., Oct., 1922; created 1st baron Irwin, viceroys of India, 1926; pres. bd. of educ., 1926.
HALL, GUTTIE.—B. 1897; 2nd batt., Cheshire Regt., 1915; lieut., 1917; served in Waziristan Field Force, 1919; ast. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. comsnr., 1925.

HALL, J. HATHORN, C.M.G. (1935), O.B.E. (1931), D.S.O., M.C., Croix-de-Guerre (Belg.)—B.1894; ed. St. Paul's Schol. and Lincoln's Oxford (1913-14); B.A.; on mil. sec., Trans. and Belgium, Dec., 1915 to Dec., Egyptian civ. serv., miny. of finance, 1919 to Feb., 1921; apptd. under reconstr. scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 17th Feb., 1922; ag. prin., 11th July, 1923; prin., 11th Jul., 1927; on various occasions accredited as perm't. Mandates Comsn., 1926-32 and more. Br. delegn. to coun. of L. of N., 1929; seconded, F.O., Oct., 1932 to June, 1933; sec., Palestine, June, 1933; Br. res., Zanzibar, 1937.

HALL, JULIAN DUDLEY, B.A. (Oxon).—B. S.S., Nov., 1910; passed cadet, July, 1912; supernym. offr., cls. V; asst. adviser, Pahat, Johore, 1914; supernym. offr., IV., Dec., 1919; 3rd asst. col. sec., Apr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; pte. sec. to gov. and comsnr., Nov., 1925; cls. II., Nov., 1927; viser, land office, Kedah, 1930; offr., 3rd Apr., 1930; sec. to high comsnr., Aug., Bradivr., Kedah, Mar., 1932.

HALL, KENNETH LAMBERT.—B. 1894; Brasenose Coll., Oxford; asst. res., N. 1912; ag. 2nd ast. sec., N. P., 1919; Nigerian secretariat, 1924; clk., exec. couns., 1925; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1925; ch. sec., in 1927 and 1928; ch. sec., Prot., 1930; ag. gov., May-Nov., 1932, 1933 and Mar.-Sept., 1934.

HALL, PERCY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1894; engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1913; do., Gold Coast, 1915; prov. engrnr., 1921 as res. engrnr., Achimota Coll. constr. senr. pub. health engrnr., 1927; asp., 1928; dep. D.P.W., 1932.
HALL, ROBERT.—B. 1880; asst. survr., Hong Kong, 1912.
HALL, ROBERT DE ZOUCHER.—B. 1904; ed. Villaston Sch., Liverpool Univ. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Nov., 1928; ag. dist. offr., June, 1932.

HALL, SIR ROGER EVANS, Kt. Bach. (1937)—3. 1883; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; 1st cl. hona. mods.; 2nd cl. hona. final jurisprudence; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1908; asst. list. commnr., Gold Coast, 1910; crown counsel, 1914; ag. senr. crown counsel, 1914; senr. crown counsel, 1915; ag. solr.-gen., 1916, 1917 and 1918; circuit judge, Ashanti (temporary appt.), 1918; ag. atty.-gen., 1919; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, G. Coast, Jan., 1920; ag. puisne judge, 1920; puisne judge, 1921; ag. ch. just. on various occasions, 1925-31; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, 1931; ag. gov., Feb.-Mar. and June-Aug., 1934; ch. just., Uganda, 1935.

HALL, THOMAS DONALD HORN, LL.B. (N. Z.)—B. 1885; ed. Wanganui Collegiate Schl., Wellington Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; N. Z. dept. of agric., 1901; asst. law draftsmen, 1921; clk. of house of reps., 1930.

HALLIER, CAPT. FRANK COLLINGS, M.C., Reg. Army, R. of O.—B. 1888; U.D.F. and Cape Corps, 1913-18; S. African Rebellion and German S.W. Africa, 1914-15; G.E.A., 1916-18; seconded as asst. pol. offr., civ. admin., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1918; admin. offr., 1st gde., Feb., 1922; dep. prov. commnr., Jan., 1933; prov. commnr., Nov., 1935.
HALIFAX, JOHN GORDON, C.S.L.L.—B. 1883; clk. law dept. (Cape), 1903; clk., pub. serv. commn., 1913; ch. clk., ditto, 1927; pub. serv. inspr., 1930; sec., pub. serv. commn., Oct., 1935.

HALLINAN, MAJOR THOMAS JOHN, C.B.E. (1932), O.B.E. (1924), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Dublin Univ.)—B. 1886; ed. Archbishop Benson's Schl. and Charing Cross Hosp. (exhib. and Pereira prize); commnd., R.A.M.C., 1910-25; served, B.E.F., Egypt, 1914-16; Mesopotamia, 1916-20; D.A.D.M.S. (San.), Amarah, 1918; D.A.D.M.S. (San.), Basrah, 1918-20; thirce ment in despa.; seconded, govt. of Iraq, health offr., 1920; inspr.-gen., civ. and health services, 1922; ret., R. of O., 1925; suptg. med. offr., chmn., cent. bd. of health, chmn., quarantine bd., Jamaica, 1933; M.L.C., 1934.

HALLOCK, ANANDA RAJAH.—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1927; attd. Puttalam kach., Dec., 1927; attd., Kalutara kach., Nov., 1928; office asst., ditto, Oct., 1929; ditto, Jaffna kach., Feb., 1931; pol. mag., Puttalam, July, 1932; office atts., Mannar and Mullaitivu kach., Oct., 1934; do., Ratnapura kach., Mar., 1935.

HALLOWEES, BRABAZON HENRY COLLIS, B.A., B.A.I.—B. 1884; ed. Corrig Sch., Kingstown and Trinity Coll., Dublin; survr., valuation and landrv. survey of Ireland, 1910; 2nd cls. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; capt., R.F.A., 1914-19; land survr., 1921.

HAM, GORDON LUPTON.—B. 1885; ed. King Edward's Schl. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; lent to Kelantan, Oct., 1913; cls. IV., S. S., Jan., 1918; clk. of couns., Sept., 1919; 1st asst. col. sec., S. S. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1920; asst. offr., assignee, Dec., 1920; ag. sec. to high commnr., Feb., 1921; ag. asst. adviser to govt. of Kelantan, Apr., 1921; ag. 1st asst. sec. B. S. S., Apr., 1922; ag. dep. treas. (B), S. S., Dee., 1924; ag. dep. treas. (A), Mar., 1925; cls. II., Nov., 1925; sec. to res., Perak, Nov., 1925; cls. IB., Jan., 1927; pres., munic. commn., Penang, May, 1927; Br. advr., Trengganu, Mar., 1931; offr., cls. I.A., dep. pres., munic. commn., S'pore, Nov., 1932; offr. cls. IA, under sec., S.S., June, 1933; ag. col. sec., S.S., on various occasions, 1933-36; dep. P.M.G., S'pore, Mar., 1936.

HAM, HENRY SOMERSET McKENZIE.—B. 1878; clk., rec. of rev., O.F.S., 1900; clk., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1901; ch. clk. and examr., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1904; asst. regis., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1912; ditto, Pretoria, 1928; ditto, Bloemfontein, Oct., 1928; regis., deeds, Pretoria, 1933; do., Cape Town, Feb., 1934.

HAM, LAURENCE PETRUS VAN ZYL.—Clk., rly. and cent. tel. office, 1898; clk., res. mag., Hoopstad, June, 1902; A.R.M., Hoopstad, Aug., 1902; Jacobadal, Nov., 1903; Thaba 'Nchu, Feb., 1907; Winburg, Feb., 1910; D.A.R.M., Fouriesburg, Aug., 1911; Trompsburg, May, 1915; mag., Phillipolos, Aug., 1919; Winburg, 1920; Wakkerstroom, May, 1920; ag. mag., Harrismith, Apr., 1925; inspr., pub. serv. comm., Feb., 1926; sec., ditto, Jan., 1928; sec., dept. of mines and industries, Sept., 1928.
HAMBER, HON. ERIC WEBB, B.A.—B. 1880; ed. Univ. of Manitoba (B.A.); ent. Dominion Bank, 1899; dir., Dominion Bank, 1912; gov., Univ., Br. Columbia; lieut.-gov., Br. Columbia, May, 1936.

HAMBLIN, R. A.—B. 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser. and temporarily employed in the science and art dept. from Mar. 21st to May 26th, 1899; assigned to C.O. July, 1899; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918; deptl. asst., 1st Apr., 1925.

HAMER, SAMUEL.—B. 1832; foreman, works, Port Sudan, 1906; 1st cls. overseer, P. W. D., Hong Kong, 1910; supervr. and acct. impts. and expts. office, 1917; survr., blgds., 1920; senr. inspr., works, 1924; permit offr., crown lands office, 1931; attached, assessor's office, treas., 1931; 2nd asst. assessor, rates, 1933.

HAMILTON, ARCHIBALD JOHNSTON.—B. 1892; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; ent. service of govt. of Trinidad, Apr., 1911; mag., county of St. Patrick, Jan., 1927; do., St. George East, Jan., 1933; asst. mag., St. George West, Mar., 1934.

HAMILTON, ERIC WILLIAM, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Blundell's; exhibtnr., Sid. Suss. Coll., Camb., 1906; scholar, 1907; B.A., 1909; cadet, Hong Kong civ. ser., 1911; J.F., 1914; attd. sany. dept., 1915; attd. impts. and expts. office in admn., 1916; attd. to sany. dept. for sp. serv., 1918; dist. offr., 1918; dist. offr., South, 1920 and 1922; 2nd pol. mag., 1922 and 1923; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1924, 1928 and 1931; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1926; dep. clk. of couns. in admn., 1927; ag. 1st pol. mag., 1929; extra assist. col. sec., 1930; 1st pol. mag., 1933 and 1934; supt., impts. and expts., 1935.

HAMILTON, MAJOR FREDRICK LINDSAY, M.C.—B. 1891; B.E.F., France, 1914-18; N. Russia, 1919-20; dist. inspr., R.I.C., 1921; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, June, 1921; commr., 1927; ag. senr. commr., pol., 1933-34.

HAMILTON, PERCIVAL JOHN ALEXANDER.—Col. audit dept., Aug., 1915; asst. audr., Nyassaland, Sept., 1915; audr., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1922; audr., Leeward Is., Dec., 1923; audr., Fiji, Br. Solomons Is. Prot., Gilbert & Ellice Is. Col., May, 1928; seconded to audit accounts of condominium govt., New Hebrides, Apr.-July, 1929; asst. audr., Hong Kong, 1932.

HAMILTON, SIR ROBERT WILLIAM, K.T., B.A.C.H. (1918).—Ed. at St. Paul's schol. and Trin. Hall, Camb.; class. scholar, B.A., 1889 (honours); M.A., 1892; student, Inner Temple, 1893; called to the bar, 1895; dist. commr., Lagos, 1895 to 1897; regis., E. Africa Prot., 1897; town mag., 1899; ass't judge and admstr.-gen., 1900; judge of high court of E. Africa, Aug., 1902; apptd. to serve on land comm., Oct., 1904; prin. judge, E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1906; chief justice, 1911; ret., 1920; partly under sec. of state for the cols., 3rd Sept., 1931; reapptd., 9th Nov., 1931; resigned, 1932.

HAMILTON, HON. ROBERT ALEXANDER BENJAMIN, (Master of Belhaven)—B. 1903; ed. Eton and R.M.O., Sandhurst; lieut., R. Scots Fusiliers; seconded (local capt.), Aden Prot. Levies, 1930; seconded, polit. offr., Aden, Sept., 1934.

HAMMOND, STANLEY ALFRED ANDREW, M.A.—B. 1898; ed. Banforo's Schol. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; supt., educ., N. Provs., Nigeria, Apr., 1922; dir. educ., Jamaica, 1928; senr. educ., coms., W. Indies, 1936.

HAMZAH, BIN ABDULLAH.—B. 1890; ed. Malay Coll., K. Kangsar; cler. serv., F.M.S., June, 1908; sttlmt. offr., Selangor, May, 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., K. Selangor, Apr., 1916; passed cadets' law exam., Dec., 1917; Malay offr., grade I, Jan., 1918; ditto, grade I, Jan., 1919; ditto, spl. cls., Jan., 1920; offr., cls. V, Oct., 1921; asst. offr., assignee, Perak, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. IV, dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1925; 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, June, 1929; dist. offr., K. Langat, Feb., 1930; offr., cls. III, mag., Seremban, Oct., 1930; dist. offr., U. Selangor, May, 1934; offr., cls. II, Oct., 1935.
HANCOCK, CYRIL GEORGE.—B. 1902; ed. Kingland Grange, Shrewsbury, Bromsgrove Schol. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. coms., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1925; dist. coms., Jan., 1931.

HANCOCK, Capt. Otho Lewis, O.B.E. (1925)—B. 1893; ed. King's Coll., Taunton and Exeter Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., Aug., 1914; lieut., 1914; capt., 1915; wounded, 1915; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Leeward Is., 1916; staff offr., local forces and adjutant, volunteers, Barbados, 1917; gov., H.M. prison, Barbados, 1920; ag. senr. pol. mag., Aug. to Sept., 1922; commr., Virgin Is., 1923; dir., prisons, Gold Coast, Aug., 1926; inspr., prisons, S.S. Stilims. and asst., Singapore prison, 1930; inspr., prisons, S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1932.

HANDS, ANDREW CHARLES.—B. 1893; ed. St. Paul's Schol. and Christ's Schol., Camb.; on war serv., Aug. 1914-Nov., 1919; col. audit dept., Nov., 1921; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1922; senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Oct., 1932; dep. audr., 1935.

HANDS, ARTHUR L., M.B.E.—B. 1880; cl. in P.O., St. Helena, Sept., 1896; 2nd cl., col. sec.'s off., Apr., 1898; regis. of births and deaths, 1904-07; oh. cl., and cl., exec. coun., Jan., 1907; J.P., 1910; ag. crown prosecutor, cl. of the peace and pol. mag. during 1923.

HANDYSIDE, WILLIAM LUCAS, M.A. ( Glas.), B.Sc. Econ. (Lond.), F.R.G.S., F.R. Econ. Soc., B. 1891; prizeman, hon. English, Glasgow Univ., 1913; 1st place language, English hon. schol., Glasgow Univ., 1914; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, July, 1915; mast. of method, Hong Kong Univ. (in addn.), 1918-20; lect. tech., II, Jan., 1920; lect. teach., inst., 1917-20 and 1922-2.

HANITSCH, CAPT. KARL VERNON, M.I. (Cantab.).—B. 1893; ed. Blundell's Schol.; Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; war R.F.A., 1914-19; adminstr. offr., cl. 4, Niger, 1920; asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1926; res., 1932, 1934, and 1935; adminstr. serv., cl. 1934; ag. sec., S. Provs., 1936; adminstr. cls. I, 1936.

HANKEY, LIEUT.-COL. SIR, M.P.A., C.B. (1919); K.C.B. (1916); C.B. (1921)—B. 1877; ed. Rugby; joined R.M.A., 1896; served H.M.S. "Ramillies," flagship and 2nd flag, Mediterranean, 1899-1901; naval intell. dept., 1902-6; granted temporary rank of Major while serving on a special comittee, 1906; intell. of Mediterranean, 1907; asst. sec., comittee of Imp. defence, 1908; apptd. sec., 1912; sec. w cabinet, from 1916; British sec., Peace Comm., 1919; comdr., Legion of Honour, 1917; commodatore, Crown of Italy, 1918.

HANKINSON, WALTER CROSSFIELD, O.B.E. (1936), M.C. (1918).—B. 1894; ed. Manches. Gram. Schol. and Jesus Coll., Oxford (exhibitions); private, Royal Fusiliers, Sept., 1914; 2nd lieut. Yorks and Lancs. Regt., 9th Jan., 1915; lieut. 1st Apl., 1915; capt., 13th May, 1915; served Egypt and France; demobilised, 29th Feb., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cl. c.O., Feb., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. priv. sec. to Duke of Devonshire, 1st Mar., 1925; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas, 22nd Jan., 1924 and to Mr. Amery, 20th Nov., 1924; asst. prin., 2nd Nov., 1926; rep. in Commonwealth Australia of H.M.'s govt. in U.K., 1931.

HANNAN, GERALD HUGH CRAWFORD.—B. 1879; ed. Mercers' Schol., London; served in S. Afric War, 1900-02 (2 meda., 5 clasps); 2nd cl. exec. and leg. coun., Transvaal, Oct., 1902; 1st cl. elk., 1903; gentleman usher of black reg. leg. coun., Transvaal, 1907; elk., asst. prov. coun., Transvaal, 1910; elk. of prov. coun. and exec. comittee, 1917.

HARDING, SIR ALFRED JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1930), C.B.E. (1927), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1878; ed. Christ's Coll., Brec. and St. John's, Camb.; foundtn. schlr., 1898; 1st class nat. sci. trip part I. 1900; B. 1900; M.A., 1920; ent. C.O. after compet. ex. as 2nd cl. elk., Oct., 1901; sec. to comittee inquire into organisation of Crown agents' o. 1908; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Crewe, 1st 1909; sec. to Royal comit. on Mauritius, 1910; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, Nov., ag. 1st cls. elk., Sept., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and 1st Apr., 1912; 1st cls. elk., 12th Oct., sec. colonial blue-book comittee., 1917; 1st elk., 1st Jan., 1920; asst. sec., 1st apr., mem., W. African currency bd., 1926-1928; mem., Palestine currency bd., 1926-1928; col. audit, 1st Oct., 1928.

HARDING, SIR EDWARD JOHN, K.C.B., K.C.M.G. (1928), C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1917) (1907).—B. 1880; ed. Dulwich Coll. and H. Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1899); 1st cls. nods, 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1903; B.A., 903; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., marine dept. of trade, 30th Nov., 1903; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 31st May, 1904; sec. to distressed colonial seamen comttee., 1909; ass't priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 14th Oct., 1912; sec. to Dominions Office Royal Coman., Dec., 1912; ag. 1st cls. clk., 10th Apr., 1916; 1st cls. clk., 4th June, 1916; on military service from 20th Nov., 1915, to 9th Apr., 1916; sec. to comttee. for attlt. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; ag. prin. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ass't sec., 1st Dec., 1921; ass't under-sec. of state for dominions affairs, 30th July, 1925; perm. under-sec. of state for dom. affrs., 24th Jan., 1930.

HARDING, SIDNEY WALTER.—B. 1896; L.D.S. (R.C.S.Ire.); house surg., Inc. Dent. Hosp., Dublin, 1925-29; demonstr., 1929-31; house surg., Middlesex Hosp., Lond., Mar. to Sept., 1931; dental surg., Falkland Is., Nov., 1931; served in Great War, 1914-19; R. Dublin Fusiliers, Leinster Regt. and R.A.F.; lieut., Falkland Is. Defence Force, 1932; dent. offr., Malayan med. serv., 1936.
HARDING, WILLIAM, B. LITT., LL.D.—B. 1898; matric., Malta Univ., 1912; B. Litt., 1915; LL.D., 1919; legal adviser to army, navy and air forces, 1929; judge, 1934.

HARDY, THOMAS JAMES.—B. 1900; ent. C.O., 3rd Mar., 1916; apptd. after compet. exam., ast.clk., 24th Apr., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; acctnt., "Discovery" comttee., Dec., 1926-Dec., 1928; cler. offr., higher grade, 5th Nov., 1928.

HAREVEN-STONEHILL, DAVID, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1896; ed. privately and Univ. of Nancy (France); mil. serv., B.E.F. (R.E. Signals), 1918-19; served in occupied enemy territory admintr., 1919; tele. inspr., Palestine, July, 1920; ag. ast. engnr., Aug., 1933 to Apr., 1934; ag. engnr. in 1934 and 1935; ch. storekeeper, Oct., 1935.

HARFORD, JAMES DUNBAR.—B. 1899; ed. Repton and Balliol Coll., Oxford (hon. schol.); M.A.; mil. serv., 1917-19; ast. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1926; ast. sec., 1930; clk. of couns., 1931-33; ast. C.O., Sept., 1934 to Sept., 1936; admtr., Antigua and Fed. sec., Leeward Is., Sept., 1936.

HARGREAVES, GEORGE MCNEILL, M.B., Ch.B., M.R.C.P. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Lond.), D.O.M.S. (Eng.)—B. 1901; Univ. of Edin. and Univ. Coll., Lond.; ast. dist. med. offr., 1925; ag. govt. bact. and pathologist, Jamaica, 1926; med., offr., Kenya, 1928; health offr., schls. and med. offr., ophthalmic br., Hong Kong, 1934.

HARINGTON, GEN. SIR CHARLES, G.C.B. (1933), G.B.E. (1922), K.C.B. (1919), C.B. (1917), D.S.O. (1900).—B. 1872; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and R.M. Coll., Sandhurst; ent. 2nd Batt., King's Liverpool Regt., 1892; served on staff, S. Africa, 1899-1900 (despa. twice, D.S.O.); European War, 1914-18 (despa., bt. lieut.-col. and col., comdr., Legion of Honour, maj. gen.); instr., R.M.
