Coll., Sandhurst, gen. staff, W.O.; bde. maj., 6th Infantry Brig.; G.S.O.L., B.G.G.S., Canadian Corps.; M.G.G.S. 2nd Army; dep. ch. Imp. gen. staff, 1918-20; G.O.C. Army of Black Sea, 1920-21; G.O.C. allied forces of occupation in Turkey, 1921-23; Northern command, 1923-27; gen., 1927; G.O.C. in C., Western command, India, 1927-31; G.O.C. in C. Aldershot command, 1931; A.D.C. gen. to King since 1930; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Gibraltar, 1933.
HARKNESS, EDWARD BURNS, C.M.G. (1922).—Entered treasy., New S. Wales, 1891; sec. to Premier's office, 1901-16; under sec., depts. of ch. sec. and public health, since 1916.
HARKNESS, J., L.R.C.P. & L.R.C.S. & L.R.F.P.S. (Edin.& Glas.), L.D.S. (Ed.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (Liv.)—B. 1896; mily. serv., 1914-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1927.
HARLEY, JOHN A.—M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), certif. Lond. S.T.M. (with distinction), July, 1909; apptd. to W.A.M.S., Gambia, Apl., 1910; ag. prot. med. offr., Jan. to May, 1911.

HARMAN, WM. HENRY, M.B.E. (1935).—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., olk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to science and art dept., 10th Apr., 1900; to bd. of agric., 6th June, 1901; transf. to C.O., 27th Jan., 1902; staff offr., 8th Apr., 1930.

HARMER, JOHN DANIEL.—B. 1876; ed. Linlithgow and Trinity Old Stratford Schls., Edinburgh Univ., M.B., Ch.B., Edin., F.R.C.S., Edin.; war serv., 1914-17; med. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1917; surg. specialist, Apr., 1928; ag. D.D.M.S., 1934 and 1935.

HARNETTY, LIEUT. EDWARD.—B. 1887; ed. Brompton Oratory schl.; capt., Lincolnshire Yeomanry; lieut., Sierra Leone Batt., W.A.F.F., 1918-21; passed lower stand. exam. in Mendi and Mandingo; ast. dist. comanr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1922; passed higher standard exam. in Mende; dist. comanr., May, 1928.

HARNEY, EGBERT EWART.—B. 1898; clk. to regis-gen., Antigua, 1916; 3rd audit clk., 1921; 2nd do., 1922; ch. clk., regis.'s office, 1927; comanr. for oaths, 1927; ag. regis. and prov.-marshal, Dominica, 1931; prin., treasy. dept., Mar., 1933; passed bar final exam. (Inner Temple) Dec., 1934; ag. mag., dist. F., May, 1935 to Feb., 1936; ag. treas., Mar. Oct., 1936; temp. mem., exec. and leg. couns., Mar.-Oct., 1936; ch. regis., Leeward Is. and regis. and provost marshal, Antigua, 1936.
HARP, AUSTIN BYERS.—B. 1908; ed. Blundell's; agrl. dipl., Cedara, Natal, S. Africa; agrl. dept., Nigeria, 1930; Jamaica constab., 1933.

HARPER, SIR CHARLES HENRY, K.B.E. (1930), C.M.G. (1921), O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. at Blundell's schl., Devon, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1899; called to bar, Inner Temple, May, 1909; cadet, G. Coast, Jan., 1900; astd., Ashanti field force, June to Sept., 1900; astd. dist. comanr., Sept., 1901; ag. ast. col. sec., Oct., 1904; ast. col. sec., Feb., 1906; ag. ch. ast. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Apl. to June, 1908; cantonment mag., Coomassie, Oct., 1908; prov. comanr., July, 1909; census offr., 1911; ag. ch. ast. and clk. of couns., May to June, 1912; ag. col. sec., June to Oct., 1912 and from May to Aug., 1914; astd., Togoland field force, Aug., 1914; sen. polit. offr., Togoland, Sept. to Dec., 1914; ch. ast. col. sec., Feb., 1915; ag. col. sec. in 1915, 1916 and 1917; ch. comanr., Ashanti, May, 1920; gov., St. Helens, Dec., 1924; assumed govt., Feb., 1925; ret., 1932.

HARPER, FRANCIS HENRY, M.B.E. (1920).—B. 1877; transferred from the service of the Royal Niger Co. as supplementary clk. to C.O., 1st Jan., 1900; conf. clk., Nov., 1912; deptl. ast., 1st Apr., 1922; sec., "Discovery" comttee., 1st June, 1928; ret., 1937.

HARPUR, ISAAC, A.M.I.C.E., Chtd. Civ Engnr.—B. 1885; P.W.D., Nyasaland, 1917-20; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Dec., 1920; ag. exec. engrnr., various occasions, 1923-29; exec. engrnr., div. pub. wks. office, Dec., 1929.

HARRAGIN, MAJOR ALFRED ERNEST ALBERT, D.S.O., V.D.—B. 1877; sub.-inspr., constab., Trinidad, Feb., 1905; asst. supt., fire brigade, Port-of-Spain, Sept., 1910; sub. inspr., Tobago, divn., May, 1911; inspr., constab., 1912; senr. inspr., constab., Apr., 1934; dep. major-gen., 1936.

HARRAGIN, WALTER, K.C.(1935).—B.1890; barrister-at-law; elk. of the peace and sub-registr., San Fernando and Victoria, Trinidad, Mar., 1914; served with the army as temporary major; mag., Victoria, Sept., 1919; crown counsel, 1924; ag. solr.-gen., Apr. to Oct., 1924 and from Jan., 1925 to Aug., 1926; atty.-gen., Nyasaland, Apr., 1927; ag. judge in 1927, 1929, 1930 and 1932; atty.-gen., Kenya, Oct., 1933.

HARRIES, CLIFFORD LOUIS REEVES.—B. 1879; C.S.L.L.; entd. Transvaal serv., 1900; inspr., native affrs. dept., 1903; native commr., 1904; seconded to Tanganyika admstrn., 1916; dist. polit. offr., 1917; returned to Union of S.A. serv., June, 1919; mag., Potgietersrust, Apr., 1923; do., Ladysmith, Aug., 1930; do., Kimberley, Sept., 1935.

HARRIS, SIR CHARLES ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1917); C.B. (1904); C.M.G. (1900); C.V.O. (1917); M.V.O. (1911), B.A.—B. 1855; scholar, prizeman, and Porteous medallist of Christ's Coll., Camb.; Tancered student in com. law, Linc.'s Inn, 1877; 12th classic, 1878; apptd. 2nd cls. elk., C.O., after open competn., 1st July, 1879; sec. to W. Indies Finance Comms., Dec., 1882; asst. sec. Sugar Bounties Confe., 1889; priv. sec. to Mr. Buxton, M.P., Jan., 1894, to June, 1895; and to the Earl of Selborne, June, 1895; 1st class elk., 1896; specially engaged in work connected with Venezuelan bdry. arbitn., 1896-8; prin. elk., Nov., 1898; sent to Paris, 1899, in connection with the Venez. bdry. arbitn.; conducted British case in Brazilian-B. Guiana boundary arbitn., 1901-4; mem. of comte. to inquire into organisation of Crown agents' office, 1908; ch. elk., 1st Feb., 1909; represented C.O. on advisory comte. of comm. intell., B. of T., 1900-17; mem. of managing comte. of Imp. Inst. 1909-16; gov. and c. in c., Newfoundland, Nov., 1917; assumed govt., Dec., 1917; ret., 1922; author of numerous articles in Dict. of Pol. Econ. on economic and statistical subjects, and of lives of various colonial govts. and statesmen in Dict. of Nat. Biog.

HARRIS, DAVID JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1897; apptd. after compet. exam., boy elk., G.P.O., 1912; on milly. serv. (R.E. and R.A.F.), 8th Oct., 1915 to 27th Feb., 1919; temp. employment elk., Minv. of Labour, 1st Oct., 1919 to 20th June, 1920; permanent ditto, 21st June, 1920 to 30th Sept., 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 1st Oct., 1922.

HARRIS, HENRY GLADSTONE BOLTON, A.C.S.M.—B. 1885; ed. Exeter Sch. and Cambourne Schl.; mines; inspr. mines, F.M.S., 1910; ag. asst. warden, mines, Perak, 1914; ag. warden mines, Selangor, 1920; ag. warden mines, Perak, 1923; warden mines, N. Sembilan, 1927; warden mines, Selangor, 1929; do., Perak, 1933; senr. warden, mines, and offg. tin restriction contr., F.M.S. in addn., 1934; tin control advr., Johore in addn., June, 1934.

HARRIS, HERBERT JOHN.—B. 1884; entd. serv. of Imp. Post Office, Jan., 1900; transf. to dept. of posts and tels., Cape of Good Hope, Nov., 1902; resig., Dec., 1907, and entd. S. Rhodesia tel. serv., Jan., 1908; resig., Jan., 1910; prov. postmr., S. Nigeria, Feb., 1910; asst. supt. posts and tels., F.M.S., June, 1913; seconded for mil. serv., Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., Indian army r.n.r. of offra., unattached, Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut. Punjabis, July, 1918; lieut., Mar., 1919; released from war list, Oct., 1919; resumed duty, Kuala Lumpur, 1920; relinquished comen., L.R.O., Sep., 1920; contr. posts and tels., F.M.S., Oct., 1925; P. and T., S.S., Jan., 1929; do., Sport., 1931; asst. sec. for postal affrs., S.S., dep., Nov., 1932; dir., posts, P. and T. dep., and F.M.S., Feb., 1933.

HARRIS, PRECTY GRAHAM.—B. 1886; Bees Schl.; Law Soc.'s studentship, 1911; ser. with King's (Lpool) Regt. and 1st Bn. Regt., 1914-19; France, 1915-16; admin. cls. IV, Nigeria, Jan., 1919; anthropologist, Lond., 1932; dep. rea., Apr., 1933; na., 1934; officiating sec., N. Prov., Apr., 1934.

HARRIS, REGINALD HARRISON.—B. 1886; Lord Williams Grammar Schl., Thame; Coll., Lond.; war serv., France 1915-16; R.I. ster Fusiliers; lieut., E. Africs, 4th K.A.; M.C. ment. in desps.; tax duties, Taps., 1934.

HARRIS, REGINALD ROBERT.—B. 1886; Queen Mary's Schl., Basingstoke; elk., gov't. office, Nyassaland Prot., June, 1915; govt. gaz., since 1914; elk., 1st cycl., 1920; ag. ch. elk., secretariat, Nov., 1921; asst. treas., Apr., 1925; ag. sec. treas., in 1930 and 1931; ag. dep. treas., 1935 to Mar., 1936.

HARRIS, WILLIAM VICTOR, M.Sc., A.I.C.F.E.S.—B. 1904; B.Sc. (Agr.), Armstrong Durham Univ., 1925; Cambridge, 1925; Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1927; taxonomist, dept. of agr., Tanganyika Terr., 1927.

HARRISON, AUSTEN ST. BARBE, R.I. (chtd. archt.), mem. of Town Planning inst., 1892; ed. Monkton Combe Schl. and McGill Univ., Montreal; archt. and town planner of communications, Greece, 1920-22; P.W.D., Palestine, 1922.

HARRISON, ERNEST, C.M.G. (1933), M.Iwa., B.Sc. (Agric.), N.D.A. (Hons.), lect. in agr., schl. of agr., Grootefontein, Cap., prin., schl. of agr., Cedara, Natal, 1920; dir., agr., Kenya, 1921; ag. dir., agr., Nov., 1922, and Mar.-Oct., 1924; dir. agr., ganvika Territory, 1930.

HARRISON, CAPT. HORACE DERNON, 1884; war serv., with D.L.I., 1914-19; France, 1918; twice ment. in desps.; headmast., grm. schls., Gold Coast, June, 1920; mast. tr. coll., Jan., 1921; inspr., schls., Apr., 1924; asst. dir., educn., for various periods.

HARRISON-JONES, CHARLES.—B. 1886; Reading and St. John's Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1907; asst. gov't. Colombo and Negombodists., July, 1909; Matara, May, 1912; on military duty, dist. judge Tangalla, Dec., 1919; asst. g. Matale, June, 1920; do., Nuwara Eliya, 1924; gov't. agt., E.P. Jan., 1929; gov't. & P. Nov., 1932; do., S.P., Nov., 1935.

HART, BRIG.-GEN. SIR HENRY HART (1935), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1918), Croix-de-Guerre French (1915),—Served in S. Afr. from W. lieut., N. Z. volunteers, 1907; cap. territorial force, 1911; major, 1912; N.Z. exp. force (main body), 1914; 1915; col., 1916; temp. brig.-gen. commd. territorial brig., 1920; ret. list, 1930; admstr., Western Sam was A.D.C. to gov.-gen., N. Z., times men in desps.

HART, Henry George.—B. 1894; served in African Mounted Riflemen, 1913-16; R.F.A., 1916-19; war serv., German S.W. Africa, 1914-16; constable, N. Rhodesia pol., 1919; sergt., 1921; lieut., 1927; asst. comnr., 1930; comnr., 1936; ag. comnr., pol. and prisons, on several occasions.

HART, H. W.—B. 1897; on mily. service in France and Flanders, Mar., 1915 to Jan., 1919; service in corps of mily. accnts., 1919-21; temp. C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; apptd. after compet. am. cler. offr., C.O., 9th July, 1926.

HART, Lawrence Hereford.—B. 1891; ed. incoming Coll., pol. probtn., F.M.S., Nov., 1912; st. comnr., pol., June, 1918; ditto, Selangor, Oct., 1920; ch. pol. offr., J. Bahru, Dec., 1922; asst. comnr., pol., Perak, Dec., 1924; ch. pol. offr., Malacca, Apr., 1928; 1st asst. comnr., pol., Perak, Mar., 1929; asst. comnr., pol., M.S. Riys., June, 1930; 1st asst. comnr., pol., Perak, June, 1932.

HART, Rupert Lancelot Leon, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1885; ed. Reading Schl. and R. Veterinary coll., London; govt. vety. surg., N. Rhodesia, 1910-14; vety. offr., Uganda, 1914; served with African Forces in Great War, 1914-17 and Western Front, 1917-19; senr. vety. offr., Uganda, 1920; ag. D.D.V.S. for several periods; D.V.S., June, 1932.

HAWKEN, H. E. A.—B. 1879; ed. pub. schls., Ottawa Colleg. Inst.; ent. civ. serv. of Canada as jun. clk., dept. marine and fisheries, 1901; 2nd cls. clk., 1908; 1st cls. clk., 1910; ch. clk., 1917; ast. dep. min., Apr., 1920.
HAWKER, CHARLES ALLAN SYMOUR.—Served European War 1914-17 (wounded); M.H.R., C'wealth of Australia, 1929; min. for markets and repatriation, Lyons' ministry, 1931-2; resigned, Oct., 1932.

HAWKINS, BRIAN CHARLES KEITH.—B. 1900; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.), hon. mod. hist., 1923; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1924; 2nd ast. collr., treas., June, 1927; ast. supt., impts. and expts., Nov., 1927; ast. head, san'y dept., Oct., 1930; dist. off., South, May, 1932; 3rd ast. col. sec., May, 1933; sec., economic cons., July, 1934; ch. ast. to sec., Chinese affs., Oct., 1935.

HAWKINS, GERALD.—B. 1891; B.A., London Univ.; on mil'y serv., 1914-20 (capt.); cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; att'd. to land office, Penang, Dec., 1920; dep. collr. of land rev., Penang, Jan., 1921; offr., cls. V, May, 1919; cls. IV, May, 1923; sec., res., Pahang, Apr., 1926; dist. offr., Mar., 1928; cls. III, May, 1928; assessor, wild life comm., Aug., 1930; ast. advr., Segamat, May, 1931; offr., cls. II, May, 1933; att'd., co-op. socs. dept., Sept., 1934.

HAY, ALISTAIR WILLIAM.—B. 1899; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1921; offr. cls. V, ag. inspr. Chinese schls., Nov., 1924; passed cadet, June, 1925; 2nd ast. dir., educn. (Chinese), Sept., 1925; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1925; 3rd mag., S'pore, Jan., 1932; offr. cls. III, ast. prot. Chinese, S'pore, Nov., 1932; do., Malacca, Jan., 1936.
HAY, CLIFFORD H., C.M.G., C.B.E. (1923), M.V.O.—Under sec., and perm. head, premier's dept., New S. Wales since Mar., 1916.

HAY, MORTIMER CECIL.—B. 1891; ed. The Schl., Bishop Stortford and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge (exhbr.); M.A., Cantab.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; passed cadet, July, 1916; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1920; dep. contrlr. of lab., Klang, Dec., 1920; dep. contr., lab., Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; ast. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Jan., 1930; cls. II, Nov., 1930; Br. advr., Perlis, Dec., 1930; ag. legal advr., Johore, Mar., 1933; cls. B. July, 1934; dist. judge and 1st. mag., Penang, June, 1936.

HAYCRAFT, SIR THOS. WAGSTAFFE, K.T. BACH. (1919).—B.A., St. John's Coll., Oxon: called to the bar, In. Tem., 1885; practised in S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; examr. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lond. chain of arbitn., 1897; pres. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1899; pol. mag., Gibraltar, 1911; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1913; ch. just., Grenada, 1916; ch. just., Palestine, 1921; ret., Mar., 1927; author of "Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder."

HAYDEN, JAMES J.—B. 1883; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Dublin; clasped in feudal and English law, Dublin Univ. (dual gold medallist); admitted solr., sup. ct. of judicature, Ireland, 1906; called to Irish bar, 1926; regist., high ct., Uganda, Jan., 1921; also addnl. dist. mag., 1st cls. admstr.-gen., rev. authority, regist. of companies, patents, designs, trade-marks, documents and business names, custodian of enemy property and contr. of local clearing house; mag., Uganda, Mar., 1927; offr. recr. and regist., trade marks and patents, Hong Kong, Apr., 1934; puisne judge for sup. Nov., 1934; puisne judge, Dec., 1935; ag. adj. just., May-June, 1936.

HAYDOCK-WILSON, HUGH.—B. 1897; ed. Bedford Modern Schl.; on war serv., Artists' Rifles (London R.) and Manchester Regt., 1915-19; cts. civ. serv., Br. Guiana as 4th cls. clk., C.S.O., Apr., 1921; 3rd cls. clk., July, 1924; capt. G. Militia (Art.), 1922-25; 2nd cls. clk., July, 1928; pvte. sec. to O.A.G., Oct. to Nov., 1925; hon. A.D.C. to gov. Feb., 1929; sec., colonial oomtee., Jan., 1929 to Mar., 1930; ag. prin. d. C.S.O., Nov., 1930 to Mar., 1931; ast. comm. pol., Nigeria, May, 1931.

HAYES-SADLER, CECIL, WILLIAM, M.A. (Oxon)—B. 1892; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; served in France with R. Engnr., (Signals), 1914-18; French Croix de Guerre, 1917; ast. inspr. comntr., Kenya Col., 1920; ag. senr. ast., 1925; ag. prin. ast. col. sec., 1933; ag. senr. ast. sec., 1935.

HAYWARD, ARTHUR EDWIN.—B. 1878; joined Cape civ. serv., Feb., 1900; clk., post-office stores, Cape Town, 1903; clk., audit br., 1906; clk., office of contr. and audr.-gen., 1910; inspr. clk., do., 1912; senr. clk., do., 1922; prin. clk., do., 1931; ch. clk., do. (Windhoek), 1932; inspr. (rev.), do., 1935.

HAZEN, HON. SIR JOHN DOUGLAS, K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C., LL.D.—B. 1860; ed. at Colleran Schl., Fredericton, and Univ. of New Brunswick; called to the bar, 1883; K.C., 1894; elec. to H. C., Canada, for St. John City and County, 1891; defeated, 1896; elec. to New Brunswick legis., in Sunbury, 1899, 1903, 1908; leader of the oppos. in the New Brunswick legis. during that period; prem. and atty.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1908-1912; has been pres. of St. John law soc. and barristers' soc. of New Brunswick; mem. of the P.C. in Canada, and min. of marine and fisheries in M. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; chief just. of New Brunswick, Nov., 1917.

HAZLERIGG, GRAY, O.B.E. (1920).—Ed. St. John's Coll., Camb., B.A., LL.B. (class trip., 1900, law trip., 1905); dist. comntr., G. Ceylon, Dec., 1908; regist., sup. ct., Ceylon, Oct., 1909; called to Ceylon bar, 1914; sec., Ceylon Cour. of legal educn., 1914; served in Belgium and France with Rifle Brig., 1915; served in C.O. from Mar., 1916; sec. to gov.-gen., Union of S. Africa, 1916-21; employed in C.O. since 1924; temp. ag. prin. 1st Oct., 1930.

HAZLERIGG, THOMAS MAYNARD, O.B.E. (1937), M.C.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1906; lieut., R.A.S.C., 1914-1915; capt., R.A.S.C., 1915 to 1920; ast. crown solr., Hong Kong, 1920; ag. ast. land offr. in addn., 1921 to 1922; from 1922 to 1923; ag. crown solr., Mar. to Nov., 1924; treasy. solr. in addn., 1926-30; ag. ast. regir. in addn., Apr., 1928 to Feb., 1929; pa. mag. in addn., July to Sept., 1929; regir., sup. ct., offr. admstr. and regist., companies, in 1929 and 1932-33; ag. crown solr., Mar. to Nov., 1932; crown solr., 1935.

HEAD, W. G.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., asts. clk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on mil. ser. from July, 1917, to Oct., 1919; cl. er. offr., Irel., 1920; higher cler. offr., 19th May, 1925; seconded for service with Br. economic mission to Austria, 10th May, 1928 to 3rd Mar., 1929; staff offr., 18th Dec., 1934.
HEAPE, WILLIAM LESLIE.—B. 1896; ast. sec., Somaliland Prot., 1919; ast. td. for duty in C.O., 1926; resumed subst. appt., Somaliland.
HEDLEY, RIDDLE, M.I.C.E.—B. 1878; irrig. engrnr., Ceylon, 1907; ast dir. P.W. and surveys, Mauritius, 1914; dist. engrnr., P.W., Palestine, 1922; ast. D.P.W., 1928.

HEENAN, JOSEPH WILLIAM ALLEN, C.B.E. (1937), LL.B.—B. 1888; ed. Wellington Coll., and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. col. secy's office, 1906; ast. law draftsman, 1920; first ast. law draftsman, 1930; under-sec. for internal affrs. and clk. of writs, 1935.

HELPS, ERNEST HERBERT.—B. 1888; joined Lord Milner's staff in Transvaal, 1905, and served with Inter-Col. coun. of the Transvaal and O.F.S. until July, 1908; clk., gov't's office, Transvaal, July 1908, and gov-gen.'s office, May, 1910; admstr.'s office, Cape Prov., Mar., 1913; senr. clk., 1920; prin. clk., 1928; munic. sec., Dar-es-Salaam, Dec., 1930; pres., Dar-es-Salaam township authority and admtrive. offr. in ch. of town, Aug., 1934.

HELPS, GEORGE SYDNEY.—B. 1886; bldg. inspr., P.W.D., S.S. Mar., 1914; war serv., Oct., 1914-Nov., 1917; ast. engrnr., F.M.S. Rlys., Feb., 1918; ast. way and wks. depot, K.Lumpur, Dec., 1921; dist. engrnr., grade II, F.M.S. Rlys., Feb., 1923; dist. engrnr., F.M.S. Rlys., Feb., 1927; senr. dist. engrnr., do., 1936.

HEMANS, CAPT. CECIL RUPERT, R.N. (Ret.).—B. 1883; ed. H.M.S. "Britannia"; Boxer Rebellion, 1900; med. and clasps; Gen. Serv. Med. with Persian Gulf bar; war serv., 1914-18; ment. in desps.; placed on ret. list, 1922; pvt. sec. to Sir Gordon Guggisberg, 1923; harbmr., Takoradi, Sept., 1927.
HEMMINGS, EDWARD JOHN.—B. 1906; late R.N.R.; cargo supt. and relieving offr., coastal serv., Trinidad, Jan., 1931; ast. harbmr., Jan., 1932.

HENDERSON, FREDERICK LOUIS.—Ed. at Camb. Univ.; B.A., 1897; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., (Lon.); med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., Oct., 1904; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1920; dist. surg., May, 1923.

HENDERSON, GUY WILMOT McLINTOCK, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1897; ed. at Bundells, Wangannu Collegiate Schl., New Zealand and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; served in Great War with R.F.A., for 3½ years; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1923; prof. clk., prosecuting staff, G.F.O., London, 1929; stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas, 1932; ag. atty-gen. and temp. mem., exec. coun., in 1933, 1934, 1935 and 1936.

HENDERSON, JAMES ABBEY, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), B.Sc. (Pub. Health), D.T.M. and H. (Edin.), Offr., Order St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1889; lieut., R.A.M.C., T.F., 1914; sany. offr., E.E.F., D.A.D.M.S., Jerusalem, 1919; ret. with rank of major, 1921; senr. med. offr., Jerusalem, 1920; do., Palestine Rlys. and Haifa, 1928; senr. sany. med. offr., Jamaica, 1930; ag. supt. med. offr. and mem., leg. coun., on two occasions; surg. gen., Br. Guiana, 1933; mem., leg. coun.

HENDERSON, JOHN, M.A., D.Sc. (N.Z.), B.Sc. (Eng.), A.O.S.M., F.R.S.N.Z.—B. 1880; ed. Otago Boys' High Schl. and Otago Univ.; instr., Reefton schl. of mines, 1903; mining geologist, geolog. survey, 1911; dir., 1928.

HENDERSON, RICHARD MCNEILL, M.I.C.F., M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1886; ed. Allen Glen's Inst. and R. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; senr. astt. engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1912; exec. engrnr., 1920; astt. D.P.W., Hong Kong, Jan., 1928; J.P., 1929; mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1932; D.P.W., 1932.
HENDERSON, W. W., M.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M. (Vic.), capt., R.A.V.C. (R. of O.), Vety. offr., E. Africa Prot., 1913; vety. offr., Nigeria, 1924; ch. vety. offr., Nigeria, 1927.

HENDRIE, CAPT. HARRY GEORGE, M.C.—Foreman of Works, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 26th June, 1921; head mast., jun. trade schl., educn. dept., Dec., 1921; on mil. serv., Sept., 1914; 2nd lieut., Sept., 1916; coy. comdr. and ag. capt., Sept., 1916; demob. with rank of capt., Feb., 1920; M.C. and Bar; seconded to Gambia as prin., new vocational schl. to organise tech. educn., 1929.

HENRY, ROBERT NOEL, M.A., higher educn., T.C.D.—B. 1898; ed. Coleraine Trinity Coll., Dublin; on staff of St. Columba Coll., Dublin, 1924-28; war serv., 1916-19; dir., educn., Cyprus, 1930; ag. dir., educn., various periods, 1929-35.

HENWOOD, SYDNEY WILLIAM.—B. 1898; war serv., 1917-18 (France); serv., in India, 1919-21; R.M. Schl. of Music, 1921-22; bandmaster, The Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1923; ser., India, 1923-31; Sudan, 1931-32; bandmaster, Guiana Militia Band, May, 1935; lieut., 1935.

HERBERT, PERCY FREDERICK.—B. 1879; Latymer, Liverpool Inst. High Schl. and Jv. Coll., Oxford, B.A.; supt., schl., S. Afr., Nigeria, 1915; inspr. and schmst., 1919-20; ast. dir., S. Provs., 1927 and 1928; ag. dir., S. Provs., 1929; ch. inspr., S. Provs., 1929; ast. dir., educn., S. Provs., 1934.

HERDMAN, SIR ALEXANDER LAWRENCE KT. BACH.—B. 1869; ed. at Otago, Oamaru high schl.; barrister-at-law, 1895; entd. New Zealand parl., 1902; atty.-gen., n. of justice, 1912; atty.-gen. in National mar., 1915-18; judge of N.Z. sup. ct., 1918-35.
HERRING, HERBERT RALPH.—B. 1895; Ipswich and Oriel Coll., Oxford, B.A. (hons.), B.A. (forestry); ast. conservator, Tanganyika Territory, July, 1928.

HERTZOG, HON. J. B., M.B., B.A., LL.M.P.—B. 1866; ed. at Victoria Coll., Cape Town, Bosch, and Amsterdam Univ.; judge of O.F.S., 1895 to 1900; comm. Boer forces of S.W.A., 1899 to 1902; one of the signatories of the treaty of Vereeniging; atty.-gen., O.R.C.; min. just., Union of S. Africa, 1910-1912; leader opposition in Union parl., 1913-24; prime min., nat. party govt., June, 1924 to Mar., 1933; coalition govt., Mar., 1933.

HEWLETT, JOHN BERTRAM.—B.1880; ag. auditor, G. Coast, Feb., 1914; ag. ast. auditor, Jan. to Mar., 1919; ag. ast. sec., Aug. to Sept., 1919; jun. cls. cl., Col. Ave Dept., July, 1920; 2nd cls. cl., Apr., 1921; audr., Gibraltar, Nov., 1922; audr., Transvaal, Sept., 1926; audr., N. Rhodesia, 1930.

HEWSON, ROBERT WARREN DALE.—B. 1880; ed. Repton Schl., Edinburgh Univ., R. Colls., Edinburgh; L.R.C.P. & S. Edinb.; L.R.F.P. & S. Glasgow; served, S. Afr. Queen's med.; served, European war, as R.A.M.C.; med. supt., Jamaica lunatic asylum, Nov., 1926; joint author with Capt. E. Stewart "Case of Acute Febrile Pneumonia," B.M.J., 1919.

HEYMANS, DR. G. M. A.—B. 1870; M.D. (Brussels) 1895; personal physician to late Pres. Kruger, 1899-1904; consul for Belg. in Transvaal, 1908-29; vice-consul for Belg. in Transvaal, 1907-29; chmn., med. bd., Pretoria, 1912-29; S. African trade commr., Milan, 1929; 1st sec., S. African legn., Rome, 1929-34; min. plenip. rep., Rome, 1933-34; min. plenip. rep., Sept., 1934.

EYWOOD, GRAHAM SCUDAMORE PERCIVAL.—B. 1903; ed. Winchester Coll. and New Coll., ord.; B.A. and B.Sc., 1925; meteorological arch asst., war dept., Leasfield, 1928; profess. asst., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1932.

EYWOOD-WADDINGTON, ALFRED.—B. 3; ed. Devonport High Sch. and Oriel Coll., ord.; B.A., 1920; lieut., 3rd Somerset L.I., y, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; offr. cls., Mar., 1924; fedl. examr. in Tamil, Sept., 4 and July 1926; offr., cls. IV, Mar., 1928; emigrn. comsnr. for Malaya; asst. dir., opern., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; senr. contr. lab., lang., Jan., 1931; offr., cls. III, Mar., 1933; r., estate duty, F.M.S., off. assignee and ag. ist., companies, Apr., 1934; ag. emigrn. snsr., Malaya, Nov., 1935.

HIBBERT, JOHN GEOFFREY, M.C.—B. 1890; Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 1st hon. schl. mod. languages (French), 1912; 28th County of London Regt., Aug., 1914; lt., R.A.O.C., Nov., 1915; capt., May, 1916; jor., May, 1918; D.A.D.O.S. 15th (Scottish) vn.; ment. in desps.; attd. mission to Lithuania, t., 1919; junr. examr., clearing office (enemy ote) B. of T., Sept., 1920; senr. examr., Jan., 21; asst. claims clk., Jan., 1922; attd., Br. vt. agt., Mixed Arbitral Tribunal, July, 1928; np. admvte. offr., D.O., Nov., 1929; C.O., b., 1930; asst. pvt. sec. to S. of S. for the Js., Oct., 1930; asst. prin., 1st Oct., 1934; pvt. to partly. under sec. of state for cols. (Lord ymouth), 23rd Sept., 1935.

HICKS, ARTHUR HAXWELL.—B. 1894; ed. Cardiff High Schl.; entd. C.A. office after mpet. exam., 1913; mil. serv., 1914-19; conded to G. Coast as sec., harbr. dept., 1925; asst. head, pay dept., Feb., 1930; appts. pt., Oct., 1930; finance dept., 1932; sec. to colonial savings banks comttee., 1934; dep. head, ores dept., 1935.

HICKS, ERNEST CRANFORD, M.A. (Oxon.)—1898; Eurpn. mast., S.S., Sept., 1921; do., edah, Jan., 1923; ag. supt., eduon., Nov., 1929; admast., High Schl., Klang, Apr., 1930; instr., hls., Johore, Aug., 1931; do., Pahang, June, 1935.

HICKSON-MAHONY, R.S.B.M.—Civ. ser., Cape Colony and Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1903 to Mar., 1917; war ser., Boer War, 1899-1900, Great War, 9th S. African Inf., 1915-1917; set. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1917; 1st grade admvte. offr., Apr., 1921; secnded as dep. lab. comsnr., 1930-31; dep. prov. omanr. Sept., 1934; ag. prov. comsnr., Aug., 1935; do., N. Prov., June, 1936.

HIDE, EDGAR WALTER—B. 1893; ed. Muncl. secndy. Schl., Eastbourne; P.O., Gr. Britain, 910; R.E. Sp. Res., France, 1914-19; supt., grade II, P.O., Penang, Nov., 1919; do., Spore, Nov., 1920; do., Malacca, Dec., 1924; ag. otnr., P. & T., Selangor and Pahang, Nov., 1927; do., N. Sembilan and Malacca, July, 1930; do., Perak and Dindings, Feb., 1932; otnr., Nov., 1932.

HIGGINS, MAJOR ROBERT HENRY CONSTABLE, M.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; war serv., France, Mesopotamia and Archangel, 1914-19; ment. in desps.; vetv. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1923.

HILLYARD, LT.-GEN. SIR REGINALD JOHN THOROTON, K.C.B. (1936), C.B. (1928), C.M.G. (1917), D.S.O. (1915).—B. 1876; entd. army, 1896; capt., 1904; maj., 1915; col., 1919; maj.-gen., 1929; lt.-gen., 1934; employed with S.A. constab., 1900-3; A.D.C. to lt.-gen., S. Africa, 1904-5; to G.O.C., Africm, 1905-8; gen. staff offr., 3rd grade, W.O., 1911-13; brig.-maj., S. commd., 1913-14; P.S.C.; served S. Africa, 1899-1902 (Queen's med. 4 claps, King's med. 2 claps); European War, 1914-18 (desp. seven times, D.S.O., bt.-lt.-col. C.M.G., bt.-col.); brig. comdr., 2nd Rhine Brig., 1928-29; comdr. 43rd (Wessex) divn., T.A., 1930-34; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Bermuda, Apr., 1936.

HILL, ALEXANDER GEORGE GLENONDON, B.A., Agr.B., A.R.C.Soc.I.—B. 1900; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; supt., agr., Nigeria, 1923; botanist, Nigeria, 1926; senr. geneticist, sugarcan research stn., Mauritius, 1929; sent to Java to study cane breeding methods, 1930; ag. dir., agr., Mauritius, Oct., 1930 to Mar., 1932.

HILL, GILBERT.—B. 1889; war serv., 1914-19; offr. in ch., wireless stations, Scapa Flow, 1919-20; wireless engnr. operator, Gambia, Feb., 1923; tel. asst. dir., Somaliland, Oct., 1926; ag. dir., posts and tels. in 1931, 1932 and 1933.

HILL, HON. LIONEL LAUGHTON, M.P. (S. Australia)—B. 1881; comsnr. of pub. wks., min. of educn. and min. of industry, S. Australia, 1924-26; premier, treas. and min. of educn., 1926-27 and since Apr., 1930.

HILL, REGINALD DYKERS RICHARDSON.—B. 1902; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1924; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1926; ag. prin. clk. and clk. to the ct. of policy and combined ct., Sept. to Apr., 1928; ag. prin. clk. and 2nd A.C.S. on various occasions, 1928-29; mag., 1933.
HILL, STANLEY OLIVER, L.R.I.B.A., A.M.T.P.I., A.R.San.l.—B. 1893; ed. Royal Grammar Schl., Guildford and Liverpool univ.; mily. serv., R.F.A., 1915-19; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924.

HILLARY, DENIS.—B. 1886; pol. probr., S.S., Dec., 1908; inspr., pol., Malacca, Mar., 1912; ch. inspr., pol., K. Star, Kedah, July, 1915; ag. asst. comsnr., pol., N. Kedah, Sept., 1919; do., S. Patani, Apr., 1926; do., S. Kedah, Dec., 1927; asst. supt., pol., S'pore, July, 1928; J.P., S'pore, Oct., 1928; ag. comsnr., pol., Kelantan, Sept., 1934.

HILLIER, SIDNEY CHARLES.—B. 1888; London Schl. of Economics (Brunel med.); G.W.R., 1902; mily. serv., 1915-1921; ment. in desp. (twice); G.W.R., 1921; asst. acct., Tanganyika rly., 1922; ag. dep. ch. acct., Tanganyika rly., various occasions, 1925-29; ag. ch. acct., 1928; dep. ch. acct., July, 1930; oh. acct., 1930.

HIMSWORTH, ERIC.—B. 1905; ed. Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A.; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Feb., 1929; passed cadet, Feb., 1931; dep. regisr., Feb., 1931; attd. treasy., 1932; asst. head sanry. dept., 1932 and 1933; 2nd pol. mag. and 1st clk. Kowloon, Apr., 1936.

HINCKS, MAURICE THORP.—B. 1885; ed. Stonegate Schl., Leicester, and Blairlodge Schl., Polmont, N.B.; solr., sup. ct., 1908; comsnr. for oaths, 1914; on mily. serv., 1915 to 1920; junr. administrative offr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; pol. mag., Accra, Apr., 1920; ag. ch. regisr., Gold Coast, on various occasions; ag. solr.gen., Jan. to May, 1922; ch. regisr., sup. ct., Nigeria, Aug., 1927; offr. admstr. and pub. trustee in aldtn., 1932-33; regisr., high ct., Zanzibar, June, 1934; ch. regisr., Gold Coast, 1937.

HINGLEY, ANTHONY CAPPER MOORE, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1908; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1931; attd., finan. sec's office, Jan., 1932; do., Kandy kach., Aug., 1932; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, June, 1933; pte. sec. to gov., Apr., 1934.

HIPWELL, WILLIAM HUMPHREY.—B. 1888; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., N. Nigeria, July, 1914; lieut., R.E., Oct., 1918; asst. engrnr., F.M.S., July, 1919; Johore, Oct., 1919-Feb., 1924; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., July, 1928; ag. sr. exec. engrnr., S'pore, Nov., 1932, and July, 1934.

HISLOP, CAPT. FRANCIS DANIEL, M.A. (hons. hist.), Edin. Univ.—B. 1891; served Territorial Force, 1909-21; on active serv., 1914-19; served Gallipoli, Egypt, France; seconded to Machine Gun Corps, 1917; instr. in machine gunnery, 1918-19; cadet, S. Stltms., Oct., 1919; attd. treas., Singapore, Dec., 1919; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, Singapore, June, 1920; resign. on med. grounds, June, 1921; cadet, Kenya, Dec., 1923.

HITCHENS, ALFRED EDEN.—B. 1880; asst. engrnr., water wks., Trinidad, Dec., 1900; 1st grade dist. offr., P.W.D., Trinidad, Sept., 1906; divisional engrnr., Apr., 1925; ag. asst. D.P.W., various periods, 1926-36.

HOAL, WILLIAM GARNER.—B. 1886; ed. Normal Coll. Schil. and S. African Coll., Cape Town; B.A., 1904; LL.B., 1908; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1909; clk. to mag., Woodstock, 1906; sup. ct., Cape Town, 1908; asst. regisr., prize ct., 1914; asst. regisr. and taxing offr., sup. ct., Kimberley, 1917; prof. asst. to atty-gen., Pretoria, 1920; govt. atty., 1924; atty-gen., O.F.S., 1929; law advr., Pretoria, 1935.

HOARE, ALBERT RAWLINSON, M.B.E. (1935).—B. 1884; govt. schilmr., Falkland Is., Aug., 1915; local audr., Apr., 1928; supt., educn. and headmnr., govt. schl., Stanley, Sept., 1928; ag. mag. and regisr.-gen., Mar.—May, 1929 and Aug., 1933—Dec., 1935.

HOARE, HENRY SAMUEL MALORTIE, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1928; attd., Kurunegala kach., Feb., 1928; attd., Anuradhapura kach., April, 1928; office asst., Jaffna kach., Feb., 1929; pte. sec. to gov., Feb., 1931; land survey., cust., Nov., 1932; ag. dep. collr. cust., July-Sept., 1933.

HOARE, COL. FRANCIS RICHARD GURNEY, C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1879; ed. Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 9th Middlesex Regt. and 4th Suffolk Regt. (Volunteers), 1894-1900; served S.A. War, 1899-1900; Queen's Med. and two clasps; ent. Transvaal civ. serv., 1903; 2nd lieut., Transvaal Horse Arty., 1912; seconded to Army Ordnance Dept. as temp. ord. offr., 4th cls. with rank of capt., 1914; do., 3rd cls. with rank of maj., A.O.D., 1915; depot commdr., 1915; temp. lt.-col., R.F.C., 1917; staff offr., air equipment to R.A.F. training brig., Canada and U.S.A.; maj. (temp. lt.-col.) (tech.), R.A.F., 1918; inspgr. ord. offr. (lt.-col., Active Citizen Force), Union of S. Africa, 1919; lt.-col., staff offr., 1st grade. Perint. Force (staff), U.D.F., 1919; staff offr., air equipment, in addn. to duties of inspgr. ord. offr., 1920; lt.-col., S.A. Ordnance Corps, 1933; dir., tech. services, U.D.F., May, 1933; col., S.A. Staff Corps, Sept., 1933.

HOBBES, JOSEPH.—B. 1886; R.I.C., 1906-13; inspgr. pol., F.M.S., May, 1913; supvr. cust., F.M.S. (Kuala Lumpur), Sept., 1921; ch. supt. prev. br., Oct., 1923; ag. dep. commnr., trade and cust., F.M.S., Oct., 1923-May, 1924 and in May, 1927; dep. commnr., trade and cust. (excise), F.M.S., Sept., 1927; ditto, prev. br., June, 1930; dep. commnr., trade and cust., Perak, Jan., 1931; senr. dep. commnr., do., F.M.S., Nov., 1935.

HOBDAY, WILFRED ERNEST.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; asst. dist. duty, 1914; land survey., cust., May, 1914; 4th asst. col. sec., Jan., 1922; 3rd assi. col. sec., Feb., 1922; 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1923; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Mar., 1925; asst. pa. agt., Kegalle, Feb., 1927; ditto, N'Elia, Mar., 1928; dep. collr., cust., Apr., 1932; ag. estab., July, 1932; contr., lab., Dec., 1933.
HOBSON, JOHN BASIL.—B. 1905; ed. borne; admitted solr., 1929; prim. offr. and dep. marshal, sup. ct., Trinidad, 1935; dep. reg. and dep. marshal, Jan., 1936.

HODGE, HENRY PARNELL, CAPT., R.A.M.C. B.A. (Oxon.), L.M.S.S.A. (London), L.S.T.M.—B. 1887; med. offr., S.S., May, 1917; med. offr., S'pore, June, 1923; do., K. Lumpur, July, 1923; do., Coast, Selangor, Aug., 1923; Pahang, May, 1925; do., gen. hosp., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1928; med. offr., Coast, Selangor, Aug., 1931.
HODGE, JOHN ERNEST.—B. 1911; Taunton Schil.; sub-inspr., Jamaica const., Nov., 1931; asst. commnr., pol., Nigeria, 1931.
HODGE, S.O.V.—Asst. dist. commnr., E.I., Apr., 1914; ag. dist. commnr., Kenya, 1926; dist. commnr., 1935.

HODGES, ARTHUR HARRIS.—B. 1884; int. civ. ser., acnt.-gen.'s dept., G.P.O., London, 1901; ch. acnt.'s office, G.P.O., O.R.C., Australia, 1908-9; asst. treas., Nigeria, 1920; senr. asst. treas., Nigeria, 1920; dep. treas., Nigeria, 1924; ag. treas., in 1925, 1927, 1928, 1933; treas., Jamaica, 1934.
HODGES, ALFRED WALTER, A.K.I.E. Chartered & Reg. Archt.—B. 1893; ed. arch. training, Exeter; war service, R.E. & R.A.I., 1914; archt. a.s.t., England, 1919; archt., P.W.I., Hong Kong, 1925.

HODGES, WILLIAM CARLTON, A.C.A.—B. 1892; ed. King's Schil., Grantham; 54th (E.A.) Divul. Cyclist Co., July, 1917; resigned commnr., Dec., 1921; ch. acct. ens., monops., S'pore, Dec., 1921; ch. acct. ens., dept., S.S. Apr., 1935.

HODGKINS, GILBERT GRAHAM.—B. 1875; N.Z. pub. schls. and Otago High Sch.; entd. N.Z. regisr.-gen.'s dept., 1901; regisr., births, 1907; dep. ch. electoral offr., 1913; ch. elect. offr., 1928; also dep. regisr.-gen., 1932.

HODGSON, ANTHONY GEORGE OWEN.—1890; ed. Rugby; asst. res., Nyassaland, 2nd lieut., 1st London Bde., R.G.A. (T.F.), 1912; lieut., 1914; capt., 1916; R. of O., 1922; lat. Bn., K.A.R., N.F.F., A.G.A. camp., 1915-17; ag. A-de-C to gov'r., 1919; asst. pd. offr., Tanganyika, 1920-22; 1st lieut. admin., Nyassaland, 1923; ag. asst. ch. sec., 1929-31; in 1933; ag. ch. sec., June to Oct., 1933; July, 1936; prov. commnr., May, 1934; ag. prov. commnr., Feb.—July, 1935; gov'r. at various occasions, 1936.

HODGSON, DUDLEY HENRY, B.A. (Oxon.), Dip. in Forestry.—B. 1899; asst. conserv. forest, F.M.S., Sept., 1921; conserv. forest, Oct., 1930; ag. dep. dir., forestry, S.S. and F.M.S., in 1931, 1932 and 1933.

HODKIN, CAPT. THOMAS WILLIAM, F.R. Econ. Soc., Fellow R. Statistical Soc., Fellow Inst. of Commerce, Assoc., Chartered Inst. Seca., Assoc., Inst. of Bankers.—B. 1885; Manchester Grammar Schil., Manchester Uni. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; dipl. in econ. Oxford; served in France with E. Lance, Regt., 1914-18; attd. to gen. staff; educn. offr., divn., 1918-19; instr., schl. of econ. in

HODSON, SIR ARNOLD WIEHNOLT, K.C.M.G. (1932), C.M.G. (1922).—Ed. Italy, Mulgrave, East Riding, and Felsted; in Central Queensland, 1900-1902; joined Australian Commonwealth Horse for service in S. Africa, 1902; in Transvaal, 1902-4; sub-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1904; specially employed in connection with amara war (med. and clasp); mem. of Ngamiland Commission, 1906; ag. astm. comsrr., Mar. to May, 1910, and Mar. to Dec., 1911; dist. comsrr. and dist. cls. mag., Somaliland, 1912; H.B.M. consul gen. Southern Abyssinia, 1914; ditto, S.W. Abyssinia, 1923; served part time in European War; present at coronation of Empress Zoditow, 1917; dist. cls. Star of Ethiopia; African Gen. Ser. Med. and Clasp, Jubaland, 1917-18; attchd., Abyssinian army in expedition against Tigre, 1919 (med. and clasp); late capt., Royal Sussex Regt. (Territories); passed Cape Univ. mag. law exam.; Somali language, lower standard; Galla language, higher standard; gov. and commr.-in-chief, Falkland Islands and their dependencies, Dec., 1926; ditto, Sierra Leone, May, 1931; ditto, Gold Coast, 1934; author "Trekking the Great Thirst," "Seven Years in southern Abyssinia," "Where Lion Reign," joint author of "A Practical Galla Grammar."

HODSON, BERNARD.—B. 1876; ent. Natal iv. serv., 1894; clk., mag.'s office, Dundee, 1894; clk., min., native affairs, 1895; capt. and mily. mag., Volksrust, Transvaal, 1900; prin. clk., Durban, 1905; addnl. mag., Durban, 1910; mag., Jongoma, Zululand, 1916; mag., Mpfotana, 1917; mag., New Hanover, 1921; mag., Pinetown, 1921; mag., Dundee, 1926; ch. mag., Maritzburg, 1929.

HODSON, THOMAS ARTHUR.—B. 1882; ed. Heltham and Wadham Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; seconded asst. sup. of pol., Galle, Dec., 1906; asst. sup. of pol., Galle, July, 1909; ditto, Tangalla, May, 1909; ditto, Matara, Dec., 1909; office asst. to gov't. agt., S. Prov., Jan., 1910; pol. mag., Kandy, Feb., 1911; astt. gov't. agt., Trincomalee, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, May, 1914; astt. settlmt. offr. and spec. offr. under Waste Lands Orde., June, 1916; on mil. duty, 1917; addtl. astt. col. sec., Apr., 1919; astt. gov't. agt., Kalutara, May, 1919; addnl. astt. dir., educn. Mar., 1922; astt. gov't. agt., Matale, Mar., 1924; gov't. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1925; ditto, N.W. Prov., Oct., 1926; do., Cent. Prov., Jan., 1931; do., Prov. of Uva, Nov., 1931; gov't. agt., Cent. Prov., Oct., 1932.

LOFMEYR, JAN HENDRICK, M.A., D.Sc., M.P.—B. 1894; ed. S.A. Coll. High Schl., S.A. Coll., Cape Town, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; prof. of classics, Univ. Coll., Johannesburg, 1917; prin., univ. of Witwatersrand, 1919; admstr., Transvaal Prov., 1924; M.P., Johannesburg (North), 1929; min. of interior, educn. and pub. health, 1933.

HOGAN, HON. EDMOND JOHN, M.L.A. (Victoria)—B. 1884; M.L.A., W. Australia, 1913; leader, Victorian party, lab. party, Apr., 1926; premier and treas., Victoria, May, 1927 to Nov., 1928 and from Dec., 1929 to May, 1932; joined country party, 1935; min., agr. and forests, 1935.

HOGAN, GERALD GEORGE.—B. 1886; barrister, 1910 (Victoria); practised, Victoria until 1915; served Australian Field Arty., Egypt and France, 1915-19; rank of major on retirement; ment. in desps.; stip. sp. mag., N. Territory, Australia, 1918; dep. judge, sup. ct., N. Territory, 1920; crown law offr., mandated Territory of New Guinea, 1922; M.E.C. and M.L.C., 1933.
HOGGEN, SIDNEY JOHN, B.A.—B. 1898; ed. Highgate Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Prova., Nigeria, Aug., 1921; senr. educn. offr., Palestine, 1933.

HOGG, WILLIAM EDWARD, A.R.C.S., A.M. Inst. C.E., Whitworth Schl.—B. 1880; ed. City of London Schl. and Imp. Coll. of Science; eng. inspr., C.A., 1910; astt. engrnr., 1919; dep. head, engrng. inspnt. dept., 1920; ch. insp. engrnr. and head, engrng. inspnt. dept., 1924.

HOLDER, FRANK WILFRED, B.A. (Durham)—B. 1897; ed. Harrison Coll. and Codrington Coll., Barbados; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; mem., H. of A. and mem., exec. comtes., Barbados; solr.-gen., Oct., 1936.
HOLDING, RICHARD HENRY.—B. 1898; on mily. serv., Apr. 1917 to Mar., 1920; temp. clk., O.S.O., Apr. 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., 6th Oct., 1921.

HOLE, COMMDR. GEORGE FRANCIS, R.N.—B. 1887; cadet, H.M.S. "Britannia," 1902; midshipman, 1904; sub-lieut., 1907; lieut., 1909; commanded H.M.S. "Peterel," 1914-15; invalided and pensioned, 1916; astt. master attendant, Colombo, Ceylon, Mar., 1916; offr. in charge, mine-sweeping Colombo, 1917-19; lieut.-commdr. 1917; priv. sec. and ag. A.D.C. to gov., Apr., 1919; master attendant, Colombo and Galle, July, 1922; harbrmr., Hong Kong, 1924; commr., 1927; offr. mem., leg. coun., 1929; dir., air services, 1929; head, Hong Kong delegn. to Shipping Confe., Simla, 1931.

HOLGATE, MAXWELL ROSSELL, M.A. (N.Z.).—B. 1896; astt. mast., Raffles Schl., July, 1920; sr. mast., Sultan Idris Training Coll., Perak, Nov., 1922; ag. prtn. do., Sept., 1926 and Apr., 1930; astt. inspr. schl., Singapore and Labuan, Nov., 1930; headmast., King Edward VII Schl., Taiping, Dec., 1930; inspr., schl., Malacca, Nov., 1934.

HOLLAND, CAPT. FRANCIS GEORGE LEOPOLD, M.B.E., (1931).—B. 1884; N.Z. educn. dept., 1902-11; Germany, 1912-14; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1915; served in Macedonia, 1915-19; capt., 1917; ment. in desps.; headmnr., govt. educn. scheme, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1920; dep. commr. (temp. and provln.), 1929; supt., educn., 1931; ag. dist. offr. at various times, 1928-33.
HOLLAND, JOHN JAMES, B.Sc., B.K., M.Inst.C.E., M.I.M. & Cy.E.—B. 1893; ed. Univ. Coll., Galway; astt. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, Aug., 1928; divl. engrnr., P.W.D., Cyprus, Mar., 1930.

HOLLIDAY, ARTHUR ROBERT.—B. 1884; atty. and notary, sup. ct. of S. Africa, 1906; astt. dist. comntr., Gold Coast Colony, Jan., 1915; seconded for service in Togoland; dist. polit. offr., Misaihoe, Oct., 1917; dist. polit. offr., Lome, 1918; ag. dist. comntr., Bole, N. Territories, Gold Coast, Oct., 1918; astt. admstr.-gen., Zanzibar, Jan., 1925; ag. admstr.-gen., pub. trustee. regiar. of doc. and land offr. on various occasions, 1925-29; admstr.-gen. and offl. recr., Uganda, 1930; admstr.-gen., offl. recr., pub. trustee, regiar.-gen., births, deaths and marriages, regiar., companies, trade marks, business names, patents, designs and trade unions, Tanganyika Territory, 1936.

HOLLINGSWORTH, LAWRENCE WILLIAM, M.B.E., B.A. (Lond.), F.R.Hist.S., M.R.S.T.—B. 1896; ed. Sir Walter St. John's Sch., London Univ., and London Day Training Coll.; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-19; headm. teacher's training schl., Zanzibar educn. dept., 1922; prin., govt., secondary schl., 1935; author "Milango ya History" (Vols. I, II and III); "Jigrafia ya Unguja na Pemba," "A Short History of the East Coast of Africa," "Njia za Kufundisha," part author "A School History of Zanzibar."

HOLLIS, SIR ALFRED CLAUD, G.C.M.G., (1934), K.C.M.G. (1927), C.M.G. (1911), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1874; asst. collr., E.A.P., Mar., 1897; employed on transport duty during Uganda Mutiny, 1898; collr., June, 1900; ag. Brit. vice-consul for German East Africa, Apr., 1900 to Feb., 1901; ag. sec. to admtn. and priv. sec. to H.M. commr., Feb., 1901 to Apr., 1903; sec. to the admtn., 1st Apr., 1903; Jubaland expdn., 1900-1, and Nandi expdn., 1905-6 (medal and two clasps); sec. for native affairs and M.L.C., June, 1907; on sp. serv. to Uganda, Oct.-Nov., 1909; ag. ch. sec. to govt., Apr., to Dec., 1911, and Feb. to Oct., 1912; Swahili examiner, 1901 to 1912; col. sec., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1913; ag. govr., Oct., 1913 to Feb., 1914, June to Oct., 1915 and Jan. to Mar., 1916; sec. to provisional admtn., German E. Africa (Tanganika Territory), Nov., 1916; ag. admtn., Feb. to Nov., 1919; ch. sec., Tanganika Territory, 1919; ag. govr., Feb. to Oct., 1922; Br. res., Zanzibar Jan., 1924; 1st cls., Order of Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1929; gov. and commr.-in-ch., Trinidad and Tobago, 6th Jan., 1930; assumed govt., 22nd Mar., 1930; ret., 1936; author of works on the Masai, Nandi and other E.A. tribes.

HOLLOWAY, JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1890; B.A. (hons. hist.), 1910, Univ. C. of G.H. (Victoria Coll.); res. mast., Grey Coll. Schl., Bloemfontein, 1911-13; mem., coun. of examrs., O.F.S., 1912-13; D.Sc. (Econ.) Lond. (Schl. of Economics), 1917; Hutchinson research medallist; lect., Grey Univ. Coll., 1917-19; Transvaal Univ. Coll., 1919-21; prof., economics, T.U.C., 1921-25; dir., census and statistics, Union of S. Africa, 1925; Union rep. at Internat. Conven. on Statistics, Geneva, 1928; adviser, Union govt., Ottawa Econ. Conf., 1932 and World Econ. Conf., London, 1933; economic advr., treas., Jan., 1934.

HOLMES, STEPHEN LEWIS, M.C. (1918).—B. 1896; King's scholar of Westminster, 1909; 2nd lieut., Royal Garrison Art'y., July, 1915; served in France and Belgium, 1916-19; adjt.; ag. major; twice ment. in desps.; scholar of Christ Church, Oxford, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 7th Feb., 1921; B.A. (lit. hum.) July, 1921; M.A., June, 1930; pte. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir S. Wilson), Feb., 1926; asst. pte. sec. to S. of S. for dom. affs. and for cols. (Mr. L. S. Amery), Feb., 1928; accom. Sir S. Wilson on visit to W. African cols., Aug. to Nov., 1928; ag. prin., D.O., 10th Dec., 1928; prin., 24th Jan., 1930; U.K. delegation to Monetary and Econ. Conf., London, 1933; Imp. Defence Coll., 1934.

HOLMES, WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B. (Dublin).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attld. to Badulla kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, N. Eliva kach., Aug., 1924; ditto, secretariat, June, 1925; pol. mag., Gampola, Dec., 1926; asst. pte. sec. to govt., Mar., 1927; office asst., Kurunegala kach., Sept., 1927; landing survr., cust., Mar., 1929; pte. sec. to O.A.G., Apr. to Sept., 1930; landing survr., cust., Feb., 1931; dist. sept., Sept., 1932; ast. comm., max. dep. reg. dist., co-op. socs., Jaffna; ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Oct., 1933; cust., June, 1935; dist. judge, N.W. Dist., N.W. Dist., 1935; education.

HOLMES, WILLIAM FREDERIC, B.A., Assoc., R. Coll. of Science (Lon.), Mem., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy, Basra (1900)—Demonstr. mine surve., Basra Mines, 1901; mining encnr., Basra Mines, Gold Coast, 1914; sec. to mines, Gold Coast, 1914; see sec. to mines.
HOIT, CAPT. VIVIAN, B.E., B.S., sex Regt. (T.A.R.O.), joined loc. div., asst. polit. offr., Sulaimani, 1925; to high commr., Iraq, Oct., 1925; ditto, Nov., 1923; oriental sect. telg., Aug., 1926.
HOLLTON, RICHARD ESQ., M.A., F.L.S.—B. 1895; ed. Booth Hall, John's Coll., Camb.; ast. dir., 1922; ag. dir., Feb., -Oct., 1925; dir., May, 1925.
HOME, BRUCE FORBES, L.B.E., L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Edin.).—B. 1896; alienist med. officer, Oct., 1927; asst. med. supt., mental hospital, Oct., 1931; do., med. supt., Aug., 1933.

HONE, CAPT. HERBERT RALPH, LL.B. (Hons.)—B. 1896; ed. Vaclavou and London Univ.; enlisted in O.T.C., 1915; 2nd lieut., 1st Lond. 1915; lieut., June, 1916; capt., Jan., H.Q. 60th Divn., Sept., 1915; adj. Maj., Feb., 1916; served twice B.E.F. and 1917-18; capt. R. of O.T.A. treas., Uganda, Sept., 1920; called to Temple, 1924; went S. Eastern Circuit in bankey. and registr., H.E.M., Zanzibar, 1925; ag. res. mag., Zanzibar, 1926 to Feb., 1927; res. mag., Zanzibar, 1928; crown coun., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; ag. sol. gen., Jan., 1930; asst. legal advr., D.O. and C.O., Jan., 1930; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, Aug., 1933; several occasions; K.C. (Lib.), revised laws, 1933; atty.-gen., Uganda.

HONEY, SIR GEORGE SIMONS, M.B., Kt. Bach. (1932), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1893; in B.S.A. Co.'s expdt. to Mashona, 1893; with B.C. Africa admtn., 1894; asst. dept., Transvaal, Apr., 1901; asst. dept., 1902; sec. for Swaziland affairs, 1903; offr., Oct., 1904; govt. sec., Swaziland, Mar., 1907; rec. commr., Swaziland govt., Seychelles, 1928; ret., 1933.

HOOD, FREDERICK JAMES, M.R.C.S., 1893; ed. George Heriot's Sch. and Med. Coll., Edinburgh; war serv., France; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1914; J.P. for col., 1916; ag. Queen Victoria schl., 1918; ag. sup. prov. mem. leg. coun., Nov., 1921; educn., 1927; tutor, Cent. Med. Sch., prin., do., med. supt., cent. lunatic visiting M.O., Suva gaol, 1936.

HOOGENHOUT, F. IMKER.—Stellenbosch and Cape Town, R. sachers' certifs. 1904 and 1906; headmr., Jeppe ducnl. Inst., Johannesburg, 1908, and Hoogend. High Schl., Bethal, 1910; inspr., educn., W. Transvala, 1921; ch. examr. to Trans. iuen. dept., 1927; sec. for interior, 1929.

HOOPER, C. A.—Ch. legal draftsman, min. fjust., Baghdad, 1926-28; pres., ct. of first inst., Israh, 1928-30; pres., land ct., Jaffa, Palestine, 1931; seconded as judl. advr., Trans.-Jordan, 1931-36; procureur and advoc-gen., Mauritius, 1936.

HOPE, WILLIAM MANSFIELD SANDERSON.—B. 1884; asst. supt., refugee camps, O.F.S., 1901; supt., do., 1902; sec. and acct., repatriation dept., O.F.S., 1903; inspr. of acct., do. nd govt. relief dept., 1903; examr. of acts. udit dept., 1904; 2nd cls. clk., do., 1908; 1st ls. clk., 1909; senr. clk., dept. of contr. and udr.-gen., 1912; prin. clk., do., 1921; senr. aspr., do., 1930; ch. inspr. (expended), do., 1933; sst. contr., and audr.-gen., Nov., 1935.

HOPE-JONES, ERNEST.—B. 1877; clk., col. ecn. dept., Cape, 1892; dept. of agr., 1899; ontir. and audr.-gen. office, 1905; acct., dept. f lab., Union of S. Africa, 1925; sec., pub. (ebt.) comsnrs., 1932.
HOPKINS, FRANK ARTHUR.—B. 1886; home prison dept., 1910; Cyprus prison dept., 1919; upt., prisons, 1921; asst. supt., prisons, Hong-Kong, Jan., 1931; ag. supt., prisons in 1931, 1933 and 1936.

HOPKINS, HERBERT OXLEY, M.A. (Oxon.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), grad. S.T.M.—B. 1895; mily. seriv. with 7th Bnnt. The King's Liverpool Reg., 1915-18; assr. bact. gt., Middlesex Hosp., 1926; Malaria resch., offr., nat. med. resch., M.F.S., Sept., 1927; bact. gt., S'pore, July, 1929; med. offr., Tan Tock Seng Hosp., S'pore, Jan., 1932; gov't. bact.gt., S'pore, Jan., 1933.
HOPKINS, ISAAC MORELAND, B.Sc., Glasgow.—B. 1902; ed. Bellahouston Acad., Univ. of Glasgow, Jordan Hill Coll. for Teach- ers; prin., ov't. training coll. for teachers, Trinidad, 1935.
HOPKINS, J.G.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Mar., 1917.

HOPKINSON, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1894; cler. asst., post office, Br. Guiana, 1911; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1912; 5th cls. ditto., 1914; 4th cls. clk., treasy., 1920; 3rd cls. ditto., 1923; 2nd cls. clk., col. secretariat, 1925; 2nd cls. clk., ditto, 1926; sec., comsnrs. of currency, 1928; 1st cls. clk., treasy., 1933.

HORN, ARTHUR EDWIN, C.M.G. (1922). M.D., B.Sc. (Lond.); M.R.C.P. (Lond.); D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.); certif., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med. (with distinctions).—Ed., at St. Mary's Hosp. and London Univ.; civil surg., 19th brigade field hosp., S. African field force, 1900-01; W. African med. staff, 1904 and served in G. Coast, N. and S. Nigeria and Gambia; awarded Cragg's re-search prize for original research in trop. med. (London S.T.M.), 1908; recd. thanks of S. of S. for the Colonies for rept. on cerebro-synaptic fever in N. Territories of the G. Coast, 1908, and for rent. on sleeping sickness in Volta River dist. of G. Coast, 1910; seconded for sp. serv. at C.O., 1910-12; personal asst. to the P.M.O., S. Nigeria, 1913; senr. med. offr. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Gambia, 1914-16; prin. civ. med. offr., German E. Africa, 1917-19; attd. Rockefeller comsnr. for investigation of yellow fever in W. Africa, 1920; dir., med. and san. services, Malaya, 1921-22; pres. elect for 5th Congress of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., 1923; med. sec. to Col. Advisory Med. and Sanit. Comtee. at C.O., 1923-24; junr. med. adviser to C.O., 1924; consulting physician to C.O., 1928; mem., exec. comtee. of Br. Empire Leprosy Relief Assoc.; fellow R. Soc'y. of Med.; R. Soc'y. Trop. Med.; author of "Health of Europeans in W. Africa," "Health of Europeans in E. Africa."

HORNBYS, ARTHUR JOHN WARD, M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1893; ed. King Edward's High Schl., Birmingham Univ. (B.Sc. 1914) and Armstrong Coll., Durham Univ.; R.F.A., Sept., 1914 to Oct., 1919; agrl. chemist, Nyasaland, Sept., 1921; asst. dir., agr. and agrl. chemist, Apr., 1931; ag. dir., agr., June, 1933 to Jan., 1934.

HORNBYS, CAPT. HENRY EPTON, O.B.E., F.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M.—B. 1890; ed. King's Coll. Schl. and R. Vety., London; lect. in bacteriology to S.E. Agrl. Coll. (Univ. of London), 1912-13; vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1913-20; war serv., E. Africa, N. Rhodesia Rifles, 1914-17; S.A.V.C., 1917-19; ment. in desps.; studied physiology and pathology, Univ. Coll. Schl. of Trop. Med., London, Ecole de Medicine, Paris, and Vety. Research Lab., Pretoria; vety. pathologist, Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922; dep. dir., vety. services and vety. pathologist, 1930; ag. dir., vety. serv., Apr., 1931 to June, 1932; dir., vety. serv., Jan., 1933.
HORNE, EDWIN NOEL.—B. 1902; aptt. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 14th Apr., 1930.

HORNE, W. K.—B. 1883; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1913; war serv., 1915-19; ch. just., Tonga, 1925; compiler, revd. edn., "Laws of Tonga," 1928; ag. ch. just., Fiji and ag. ch. judicial comsnr., W. Pacific, Feb.-Sept., 1928; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1930; puisne judge, Kenya, 1933.

HORSBURGH, HARRY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1876; ed. Aske's Haberdasher's Company Schl.; C.A., Jan., 1908; asst. dir., min. of munitions, 1915; dep. contr., munitions disposal bd., 1919; dep. head, engrng. contracts, 1920; tech. offr., "Discovery Comte," 1923.
HORSFIELD, WILLIAM, B.A.—B. 1896; ed. Denstone and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cl. hons. mech. sci. tripos, 1921; staff survr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1921.

HORSLEY, BERNARD HILL, O.B.E. (1931), D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1890; European War, 1914-18; major, 8th (service) batt., K.O.Y.L.I.; D.S.O., M.C., despa.; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, 1919; dist. comsnr., Somaliland, 1922; polit. offr. with Anglo-Italian (Somaliland) bndy. coman., Aug., 1929-Sept., 1930; head, Anglo-Italian claims sub-comsnr., Mar.-Sept., 1930; O.A.G., Somaliland for various periods, 1932-33.

HORSLEY, RICHARD.—B. 1893; ed. Cowley Gram. Schl., St. Helens; gen. fitter, P.W.D., Gold Coast, Aug., 1919; instr. of metalwks., Accra tech. schl., Apr., 1920; ag. prin., Dec., 1920 to Mar., 1921; house mast., Sept., 1922; head mast., junr. trade schl., Apr., 1926.
HORWOOD, THOMAS BERRIDGE, K.C., LL.B.—B. 1888; ed. Maritzburg Coll. and Ch. Ch. Oxford; I.C.S. India, 1911-19; advoc., Natal, 1923-36; judge, native high ct. of Natal, 1936.

HORWOOD, HON. SIR WM. HENRY, KT. BACH. (1904).—B. 1862; ed. at Bishop Feild Coll., St. John's; called to the bar, 1886; bencher of the law society, Newfoundland, 1891; Q.C., 1895; elected to the legislature, 1894; acted as col. sec. 1894-6; delegate from the Newfoundland govt. to the Ottawa conf., Apr., 1895, on the subject of confedn. with Canada; mem. of ex. coun., 1894-97; ag. atty.-gen., 1897; elected to legislature to represent Harbour Grace, 1897; apptd. by ch. just., with minister of just. to act in conjunction with judges as committee to amend rules and procedure, sup. court, 1899; minister of just. and atty.-gen., 1900; ex-officio mem. of ex. coun., and of treasy. board; acted as leader of govt. during sess. of 1901; and also in 1902; apptd. ch. just., July, 1902; administered govt., 1902, 1904, 1909, 1912, 1913, 1914; deputy gov., 1907, 1910; chrmn. of royal com. on Sealing Industry, 1913; pres., Newfield, centre of St. John Ambulance Assoc.; chrmn. of comttee. for selection of non-combatants for service abroad; chrmn. of Newfield, fund for hospital cots for soldiers and sailors; representative on Peace Comn., established under treaty between Great Britain and U.S.A., May, 1916.

HOSEGOOD, THOMAS WILLIAM HAROLD.—B. 1886; younger brother of Trinity House, master mariner; ed. St. Paul's Cathedral Choir Schl.; harbmr. and naval agt., St. Lucia, 1918; ag. ch. of pol. on various occasions; asst. harbmr., marine mag., etc., Hong Kong, 1927.

HOSKING, ETHELBERT BERNARD, O.B.E., (1932).—B. 1890; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and Wadham Coll., Oxford (class schl.); asst. dist. comnrr. E.A.P., Dec., 1913; dist. comnrr., Kenya, 1925; ag. prin. asst. to commr., local govt., etc., 1932; ag. comnrr., mines, 1933; comnrr., mines, 1934; senr. dist. comnrr., 1935; ag. comnrr., local govt. lands and altit., 1936.

HOUGHTON, H. E., F.R.A.S.—B. 1892; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1912; attached to office of high commr. for S. Africa, Nov., 1920; transf. to Basutoland serv., Nov., 1923.

HOW, DOUGLAS WALSHAM.—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll., London Matric.; Un. of S. Africa civ. serv. lower law exam.; Basutoland 2nd grade Sesuto exam.; clerical asst., Basutoland, Oct., 1910; sub-inspr., Basutoland mtd., pol., July, 1912; chief clk., master of ct. and regar., July, 1917; served as 2nd lieut. with Royal Welsh Fusiliers in France, 1918; inspr. and staff offr., Basutoland mtd. pol., July, 1919; dep. asst. comnrr., 1923; asst. comnrr., 1927; ag. govt. sec., Bech. Prot., Mar., 1932 to Apr., 1933; dist. comnrr., Basutoland, 1936.

HOWARD, HON. ERATUS EDWIN.—B. 1868; ed. Iroquois High Schl., Inverness Acad., and McGill Univ.; B.A., 1895; gold medallist in maths, and nat. philos.; B.C.L. (gold medallist) 1898; barrister; practised in Montreal, 1899-1919; called to the bar, Quebec, July, 1899; K.C., Sept., 1913; judge, superior ct., prov. of Quebec, 26th Sept., 1919; judge of ct. of King's Bench, 9th Aug., 1920; prof., civ. law, McGill Univ. and elective fellow of law faculty.

HOWARD, FREDERICK JAMES, O.B.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Bedford; 2nd divn. clk., W.O., June, 1901; 2nd cls. asst. acctnt., army accts. dept., May, 1905; selected for advancement to higher grade of scale, 1st Nov., 1911; ag. 1st cls. asst. acctnt., Oct., 1914; ag. acctnt., Nov., 1916; 1st cls. asst. acctnt., 15th Dec., 1919; personal supernny. appt. as acctnt., 1st Jan., 1921; serv. with min. of munitions as dir. from, 1915, and with disposal and liquidation coman. treasy., as ast. contr. of deptl. finance until 1921; O.B.E., 1st Jan., 1918; ag. prin., old style, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. prin. C.O., 15th Sept., 1921; prin., 15th Aug., 1928; attended Imp. Defence Coll. ext., 1933.

HOWARD, JOHN CUETOIS, K.C.—B. 1867; ed. Uppingham and Clare Coll., Camb. term, honours, nat. sci.; called to the bar, Inner Temp., Jan., 1913; 2nd lieut., 4th Lincoln Regt., May, 1915; served in France, Jan., 1917 to July, 1918; legal adviser, G.H.Q., France, May, 1919 to Feb., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Aug., 1920; ag. advoc., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1923 and Aug., 1924; king's advoc., July, 1924; title altered to ag. gen., May, 1925; joint compilers, 2nd ed. statute laws of Cyprus, 1923; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1926; ag. atty.-gen. on various occas., 1927-30; temp. asst., legal adviser staff, Nov., 1931; atty.-gen., Gold Coast, Mar., 1932; atty.-gen., Ceylon, 1936.

HOWARD-FLANDERS, WILLIAM ENGLAND.—B. 1894; ed. Mill Hill Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1920; M.A., 1927; served in H.M. Forces, 1914-18; called to bar, M.T., 1922; mem., south-eastern circ. and cent. crim. mag., St. Vincent, June, 1927; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1928; income tax commr., 1928; educ. bd., 1928; ag. col. sec., July, 1928; adm. gen., Sept., 1928; ag. ch. just., May-Dec., 1929; adm. govt., Feb., 1930; asst. regar., Rhodesia, Sept., 1930; ag.regar., 1935; adm. gen. and offr. recr., 1936.

HOWAT, CLARENCE HUGH, M.B., B.S. (Edin.). D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1908; ed. Edin. Acad. and Univ. of Edin.; medallist in med. in clinical med.; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, 1928; med. offr., Nyassaland Prot., 1933.

HOWE, HON. CLARENCE DECATUR, B.S.—1886; ed. Public schls. and Massachusetts Inst. of Technology; prof., civ. engng., Dalhousie Univ., Halifax, N.S., 1908; ch., engng., dist. grain commrs. for Canada, 1913; el. to H. of Canada, at g.e., 1935; mem. of rly. & can. and min., marine in Mackenzie King cabinet, 1935.

HOWE, GERARD LEWIS.—B. 1890; ed. Armagh Royal Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; served, R. Inniskilling Fusiliers, 1916-17; lined R. Irish Rifles, 1917-20; ment. in desps., Fame 1918; called to bar, Kings Inn, Ireland, 1920; called to bar, N. Ireland, 1924; practised in Ireland and L.F.S., 1923-30; res. mag., Kent June, 1930; arbitrator, Mombassa munic. rat. action appls., Jan. to July, 1934; crown coun. Gold Coast, Aug., 1934; ag. solr.-gen., Mar., 1936; crown coun., S.S., 1937.

HOWE, JOHN WALLACE, Dp. Ag. B.S.A. M.Sc.—B. 1901; ed. Westward Ho! and Univ. Alberta; in ch. grain experimental plots, prof. dept. of agr., Vermilion (Alta.), 1921; grader, dairy branch, Alberta dept. of agr., 1922; instr., dairying, schl. of agr., Alberta, 1923; instr., animal husbandry, Univ. of Alberta, 1924; headmnr., govt. farm schl., and supt., govt. stock farm, Hope, Jamaica, July, 1931.

HOWELL, CHARLES GOUGH.—B. 1894; ed. Mill Hill Schl. and Caius Coll., Camb.; B.A., Cantab.; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914; served in France and Flanders, May to Dec., 1915 and ret. on account of wounds in Aug., 1916; called to bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1921; mem. S. Wales circuit; crown coun., Kent, Nov., 1921; ag. solr. gen., June to Oct., 1930; mem. crown coun.; atty.-gen., Fiji, July, 1931; ag. ch. just. and ch. jud. comnrr., W. Pacific, Apr., 1932 to Jan., 1933; legal advr., July, 1933; atty.-gen., S.S., 1936.

HOWELL, E. H., M.B.E.(1927).—B. 1879; ptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O. Mar., 98; promoted, with gratuity, Dec., 1907; conf. c., 1911; staff clk. 1st grade, 8th Sept., 1917; pld. ass't, 16th Jan., 1923; clk. for legal instruments, 26th Feb., 1923; staff-offr., gen. dept., h Apr., 1933.

HOWELL, FREDERICK ROUSE.—B. 1886; p. clk., P.O., Barbados, Feb., 1907; jun. clk., O., Nov., 1907; clk. to master in chancery, sc., 1907; 4th clk., C.S.O., May, 1909; 3rd c., C.S.O., 1919; 3rd grade offr., clerk, exec. un. and exec. comtee., 1925.
HOWELL, WILFRED IRVING.—Cadet botanical d experiment statn., St. Kitts, July, 1907; reman., Mar., 1909; agri. instr., Nevis, Apr., 12.

HOWES, ARTHUR BURNABY.—B. 1879; ed. at ulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Middlesimple, June, 1906; Western circuit; conveyor, Uganda Prot., May, 1916; custodian enemy property, Mar., 1917; dist. mag., st. judge, and admstr.-gen., Entebbe, Uganda, pr., 1907; dist. mag., dist. judge, and offi. rec. bankruptcy, Kampala, Uganda, July, 1917; judge of high ct., Jan.-June, 1920; ag. attym., Feb. to Dec., 1921; senr. mag., Jan., 22; ag. judge of high ct., Apr. to Oct., 1922 nd June, 1923; puisne judge, Gold Coast, Dec., 24; ag. ch. just., Feb., 1927 and Sept. to Oct., 28; puisne judge, Straits Stltmts. and judge, M.S., Jan., 1934.

HOWITT, CHARLES ROBERTS.—B. 1894; ed. arisle Grammar Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; det., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; seconded to Johore, eo., 1920; 1st cls. mag., 1922-1924; lieut., V.R., ug., 1924; 3rd mag., S'pore, Oct., 1925; ag. 2nd ast. sec. (B.), S'pore, May, 1926; offr., cls. IV., une, 1926; dist. offr., Jasim, May, 1927 and May, 328; ag. lat ast. sec. (B.) S'pore, July, 1930; frr., cls. III., June, 1931; ag. lat ast. sec. (B) nd clk. of couns., Aug., 1932; addl. Malayan stabmt. office, Aug., 1934; ag. Br. advr., erlis, Apr., 1935; offr. cls. II, June, 1936.

HUBBALL, JOHN GORST.—Ck., Cape, 1895; examr., 1895; astt. ctntt., 1903; astt. acctnt., contrlr. and auditoren's office, Union of S. Africa, 1910; priu. clk., 912; chief acctnt., treasry., Union of S. Africa, 916; sec., office of high commr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1928; sec. for finance, Nov., 1931.

HUBBARD, PERCIVAL CYRIL.—B. 1902; ed. dill Hill and Calais Coll., Cantab.(mod. languages scholarship; B.A. hona., 1923; LL.B. hona., 924; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1925; cadet, Jr. Solomon Is. Prot., May, 1928: ag. astt. dist. frr., Malaita, Oct., 1928; ag. dist. offr., Ysabel, an., 1929; ch. mag. and legal advr., Br. Sol. Is. prot., Feb., 1929; ag. Br. judge, joint ct. and udl. commr. for New Hebrides, 1930-32; ch. nag., Palestine, 1934.

HUCKS, GEOFFREY WILLIAM YOUNG.—B. 906; ed. Highgate Schl. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. hist. tripes, pt. I, 1927; 2nd cls. anthropol. tripes, 1928; B.A., 1928, M.A., 1932; cadet, Taunganya Territory, July, 1929; astt. dist. offr., 1931.

HUDSON, HON. ALBERT BLELOCK.—B.1875; ed. Portage la Prairie, Manitoba, LL.B., (Man.); called to Manitobah bar, 1899; bencher, Law Soc., Manitoba, 1913; K.C., 1914; mem., Manitoba legislature, 1914-20; atty.-gen. (Man.), 1915; resigned, 1917; mem., Ho. of Co. Canada, 1921-25; just., sup. ct. of Canada, 1936.

HUDSON, PHILIP JAMES.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., June, 1916; cadet, Ceylon, Mar., 1920; attd. to Colombo kach., Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Balapitiya, Mar., 1921; astt. settlmt. offr., 1922; office astt., Jaffna kach., Dec., 1924; astt. settlmt. offr., May, 1925; astt. govt. astt., Galle, Dec., 1929; do., Matara in addn., Sept., Dec., 1932; astt. settlmt. offr., Dec., 1934; astt. govt. astt., Hambantota, Feb., 1935.

HUDSON, LIEUT.-COL.WILLIAM,C.B.E.(1932), O.B.E., M.C.—B. 1880; ed. privatel.; and King's Coll., London; S. African War, 1900-02; Queen's Med., with 2 clasps; King's Med., 2 clasps; contr.'s staff, cent. telegraphs, London, 1902; dept., P.M.G., N. Nigeria, 1912; ag. P.M.G., Apr. to Aug., 1912; R.E. (signals), 1914-18; offr. in charge, civ. tels., occupied enemy territory adminstr., Palestine, 1919; P.M.G., Palestine, July, 1920.

HUETT, FRANCIS JOHN.—B. 1886; ed. Anerley Coll.; ag. supvr., cust., P. Swettenham, July, 1921; do., P. Buntar, Aug., 1921; do. T. Anson, Apr., 1923; astt. cust., P. Swettenham, Mar., 1924; do., P. Dickson and harmr. in addn., Jan., 1927; astt., chandu monop., N. Sembilan and astt., cust., in addn., Nov., 1930; sr. astt., trade and cust. dept., F.M.S., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1931; dep. commr., do., Apr., 1933; do., Perak, Mar., 1935.

HUGGARD, SIR WALTER CLARENCE, Kt.Bach.(1933), K.C., B.A., LL.D.—B. 1884; ed. at Dublin Univ.; 1st honouram. and senr. mod. in legal and polit. science; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dublin, 1907; station mag., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; ag. puisne judge, July to Sept., 1914; pol. mag., Jan., 1917; solr.-gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1920; ag. puisne judge, June-Oct., 1921; K.C., 1921; ag. atty.-gen., Jan.-Oct., 1922; atty.-gen., lat Nov., 1922; prepared revd. edn., Laws of Trinidad and Tobago, 1925; atty.-gen., Kenya, Feb., 1926; ag. col. sec. and gov't. dep., Aug., 1927; attygen., S. Stltmts., June, 1929; ch. just., 1933 ret., 1936.

HUGGINS, HASTINGS DUDLEY, M.Sc., Dip. Agr., I.C.T.A.—B. 1905; ed. Antigua Gram. Schl. and Cornell Univ.; astt. mast. Antigua Gram. Schl., 1923; Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1923; astt. field man., sugar expt. sta., Br. Guiana, 1926; astt. agrl. supt., dept. agr., 1928; agrl. supt., 1934.

HUGGINS, CAPT. JOHN, M.C.(1916).—B.1891; ed. Bridlington Gram. Schl. and Leeds Univ., B.A.; 2nd lieut., Yorks Regt., Nov.1914; Gallipoli, 1915; Egypt, 1916; France, July, 1916; capt., Sept., 1916; ment. in deep.; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; govt. rice agt., Klang, May, 1920; agt. to food contr., Selangor and Pahang, Oct., 1920; 4th astt. sec. to govt., Mar., 1921; passed cadet Malay, June, 1921; dist. offr., Aug., 1921; offr., cls. IV., Aug., 1923; recvr., Alsagoff concession, Johore, Feb., 1925; pyte. sec. to high commr., May, 1926; offr. cls. III., Aug., 1928; sec., res., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1929; dist. offr., Kuantan, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. II, Aug., 1933; astt. advr., Kedah, July, 1933; ag. Malayan estabmt. offr., May, 1934.

HUGHES, CLIFFORD BRYNOR.—B. 1894; war serv., 1914-19; Machine Gun Corps, Salonica, 1916-19; astt. acctnt., Gold Coast rly., Sept., 1919; acctnt., do., Nov., 1924; senr. acctnt., do., July, 1931; ch. acctnt., do., Sept., 1934.

HUGHES, EDWARD HENRY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1879; ed. Oswestry High Schl.; artd. borough engr., Oswestry; survr. and astt. engr., Capetown, 1900; Elands River Survey, 1908; res. engr. Le Chasseur and Goree bd., 1911; asst. engr., Cape, 1911; circle engr., irrig. dep., 1918; suptd. eng., 1934; asst. dir. irrig., 1936.
HUGHES, FREDERICK EDWARD, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1906; ed. Dean Close Sch., 1924 and Worcester Coll., Oxford, 1928; asst. consnr., forests, Gold Coast, July, 1928.
HUGHES, GEORGE SPENCER, B.A. (Dub.), LL.B. (Dub.)—B. 1887; Barrister-at-law; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1911; dist. offr., cls. II, 1919; ag. res., 1923; cls I, admstve. serv., 1930.

HUGHES, HERBERT.—B. 1888; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, Pembroke Coll., Oxford, (schol.), and Ridley Hall, Cambridge; B.A. (1911), M.A. (1914); ordained, 1915-16; appd., educn. dept., Jamaica, 1925; examn., 1925; secy., bd. of educn.; ag. asst. dir., educn., various periods; supervising instr., secondary schls., sec., Jamaica schls. commn., and Rhodes scholarship selection comttee., 1933.

HUGHES, JOHN WHITEHOUSE WARD.—B. 1883; ed. Oakham and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1905; M.A., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., 1906; mag., Sept., 1911; offr., cls. II, Deo., 1925; 1st mag., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1926; offr., cls. IB, commr., lands, F.M.S., continues ag. as above July, 1928; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Oct., 1930; commr., lands, F.M.S., May, 1931; ag. Br. res., Negri Sembilan, July, 1931; Br. res., Mar., 1932.

HUGHES, Rt. Hon. WILLIAM MORRIS, P.C. (1916).—B. 1864; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1894-1901; elec. to Ist H. of R., C. of A., 1901, and member since; min. for external affairs 1904; representative of C. of A. at Navigation Conf., London, 1907 atty.-gen., C. of A., Apr., 1910-1913, and from Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1915; prime min., and atty.-gen., Oct., 1915; Canadian privy councillor, Feb., 1916; prime min. and atty.-gen., Nov., 1916; ditto, Feb., 1917; ditto, Jan., 1918; resigned Feb., 1923; min. for health and repatriation, Lyons min., 1934 and vice pres. federal exec. coun.; resigned, 1935.
HUGO, FRANCOIS DANIEL, B.A.—B. 1883; instr. schls., Natal, Apr., 1916; ch. instr., schls., Jan., 1931; supt., educn., Aug., 1931.

HUME, RICHARD HENRY.—B. 1897; Imp. post office, 1912-1919 (war serv. R.E. Signals, G.E.A., 1915-19); postmtr., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; senr. postmtr., do., 1923; dist. survr., posts and tels., Gold Coast, 1930.
HUMFREY, ERIC BERTRAM.—B. 1903; ed. Cranleigh; constable, Kenya pol., Jan. 1926; asst. instr., 1929; ag. asst. supt., 1931-32; local commt., pol., Cyprus, June, 1933; ag. commr., July-Nov., 1935.
HUMPHERSON, LESLIE HERBERT.—B. 1902; ed. King Edward's Schl., Birmingham and Worcester Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1929; dist. commr., Jan., 1931.

HUMPHREYS, LIEUT.-COL., Sir FRANCIS HKNY, G.C.M.G. (1932), G.C.V.O. (1928), K.C.M.G. (1929), K.B.E. (1924), C.I.E. (1920).—B. 1879; ed. Shrewsbury and Christ Church, Oxford; 2nd lieut., Worcestershire Regt., 1900; served S. Afric-n War, 1900-01 (Queen's med. 3 clasps); ent. polit. dept., govt. of India, 1903; pvte. sec. to ch. commr., N.W.F.P., 1904; asst. commr., Hazara, 1907; dep. commr., Jammu, 1910; Kohat, 1914; polit. agent, N. Waziristan, 1915; polit. offr. with troops, N.W. Frontier, India, 1917 (desp.); served European War in Europe as pilot R.A.F., 1918; polit. agt., Khyber, 1919; dep. foreign sec. to govt. of India, 1921; last envoy extraordinary and min. plenipot. at the court of H.M. the King of Afghanistan, Jan., 1922; high commr., Iraq, 1929.

HUMPHREYS, H. L.—Ed. at gam., Antigua; senior Camb. certif., 3rd div.; cashier, treas., Antigua, Jan., 1882; 2nd offr., 1896; ag. 1st indoor offr. on several occasions; ag. ch. audit clk., Leeward Is., Dec. to Jan., 1898; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts, Dec., 1903, to Mar., 1904; 1st indoor offr. treas., Antigua, Apr., 1904; ag. ch. and federal acctnt., Leeward Is., May, 1909, Aug., 1910, and from Apr., 1911 to 1912; ag. treas. of Antigua, and federal acctnt., from Dec., 1912 to Apr., 1913.

HUNT, WILLIAM EDGAR, B.A. (1934), C.B.E.—B. 1883; ed. Warwick and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. commr., Nigeria, 1908; asst. dist. commr., Lagos, 1910; 2nd cls., 1917; res., 1924; sudo., 1929; mem., comm. of inquiry into disturbs. in S.E. Provs., 1930; ag. lieut.-gov., S. Provs., 1930-33; commr. of col. and lands, 1934; lieut.-gov., S. Provs., 1934; ch. commr., S. Provs., 1936.

HUNTER, LIEUT.-GEN., SIE ABRAHAM K.C.B. (1898), D.S.O. (1886).—Ed. at Royal Acad. and Sandhurst; 4th King's Own Lancaster, 1874; capt., 1882; lieut.-col., 1894; maj.-gen., 1896; served in Egypt twice wounded; gov. of Dongola, commdt. Frontier Field Force, 1893-95; of Omdurman, 1899; Egyptian medal; orders; in command of 1st cls. dist. at Lieut.-gen. on staff, commanded 10th div., 1900-01 (twice ment. in desps.); commr., Scotland, 1901-3; Western Army Corp., 1904-7; commanded Southern Army, Army, 1907-9; gov. and commr.-in-chief, 1910-1913.
HUNTER, HON. JAMES AITCHISON STON, M.P.—El. to H. of Rs., C'wealth of Australia for Maranoa (Queensland), since 1921; min., Lyons min., 1934.

HUNTER, JAMES BLAKE, B.A.—R. 1878; public schls. and Colleg. Inst., Woolwich; Toronto Univ. (B.A. 1889); ent. Dominions serv., 1889; pvte. sec. to successive mins. crown; 2nd cls. clk., 1900; 1st cls. clk., ch. clk., Apr., 1907; asst. dep. min. of pub. 1908; dep. do., 1908.

HUNTER, JAMES STANLEY.—B. 1879; Hawera High Schl.; entd. N.Z. govt. serv., post and tel. dept., 1905; pub. serv. cont. office, 1914; pvte. sec. to various mins., 1916-24; offr., sec., N.Z. rly., 1924; commr., N.Z. rly., 1928; commr., transp., 1929-35; sec. of lab. and commr., unemployed, 1935; pvte. sec. to Sir Francis Bell at National gen. assm. and also at Imp. Conf., 1935.
HUNTER, JOHN.—B. 1878; appld. compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. and appt. to the C.O., Apr., 1899; clk. to paroch. comtce., Nov., 1907; staff-offr., 1st aprt., librarian, 6th July, 1933.

HUNTER, JOHN ADAMS.—B. 1891; Royal Grammar Schl., Newcastle-on-Tyne; John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1913 (his cls. 2, L.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; sec., Sept., 1918; asst. dist. offr., Oct., 1918; to chief sec.'s office, Jan.-Mar., 1919; sec. for serv. in food control dept., May, 1919; ag. 3rd asst. sec. to Govt., F.M.S., Oct., 1919; 4th asst. sec. to Govt., Oct., 1921; offr. dep. contr., lab., Seremban, Nov., 1921. K. LUMPUR, July, 1922; on spl. duty, census, July, 1923; offr. in ch., govt. shops in addn., July, 1923; 3rd ast. sec., F.M.S., Nov., 1923; dist. offr., Ulu at., Oct., 1926; Class III, Nov., 1926; ag. res., Selangor, July, 1927; ag. Br. res., gor in addn., Nov., 1929; dist. offr., Ulu gor, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1931; sec. to govt., F.M.S., June, 1933; ag. under S.S., Mar., 1935.
INTER, K. L.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. a Prot., Apr., 1919.

INTER, LOUIS LUCIEN.—B. 1889; cadet, civ. ser., Sept., 1914; addl. comsnr. requests and pol. mag., Ratnapura, Oct., 1915; anadura, Sept., 1916; offr. asst. Anurahapura, Aug., 1918; pol. mag., Chilaw, Sept.; addnl. astt. govt. agt., Colombo, Feb.; astt. sttlmt. offr., Jan., 1923; addnl. astt. educn., July, 1928; astt. govt. agt., Kalutara, 1930; astt. govt. agt., Colombo, Jan.; do., Matara, Mar., 1934; ag. govt.-agt., P., Sept., 1934.
UNITINGFIELD, 5TH BARON (Cr. 1796) LIAM CHARLES ACRDORCHNE VANNEK, M.G. (1934).—B. (Queensland) 1873; M.P., divn., Suffolk, 1923-29; partly. prte. secy., B. of T., 1927-28; gov., Victoria.
URST, RAYMOND GEORGE.—B. 1877; Indian rly., 1900; E. African rly., 1917; astt. io supt., Tanganyika rly., July, 1925.

USBAND, CHARLES THOMAS MAIN, F.S.I., G.S.—B. 1891; ed. Box Hill Grammar School, Melbourne; 1st grade survr., F.M.S., 1913; ed France, 1915-18; astt. supt., surveys, S., Jan., 1919; ag. supt., Selangor, July, 1920; Perak, Feb., 1920; Johore, Feb.; supt., surveys, F.M.S. & S.S., July, 1929; Kedah, Apr., 1930 and Jan., 1932; do., acca and N. Sembilan, May, 1933; do., Mal., Jan., 1935.

USBAND, THOMAS GIBSON, B.Sc. (Edin.), I.I.C.E., Chartered Civil Engrn.—B. 1890; Heriot Watt Coll. and Edin. Univ.; engr., Jasir, S.S., July, 1919; ag. exec. nr., Malacca, May, 1923; astt. engr., Penang, 1924; ditto. P. Wellesley, Jan., 1927; engr., South P. Wellesley, Apr., 1929; exec. engr., P.W.D., June, 1931; ditto. adl. and marine. Spore, July, 1931; senr. exec. mr., wks. and blggs., K. Lumpur, Oct., 1932; Town, Apr., 1934.

USSEY, JOHN DREW.—B. 1900; 2nd lieut., vonshire Regt. & Tank Corps, Apr., 1918-May, 9; pol. probr., F.M.S., Mar., 1921; ag. astt. nevr. pol., various occasions, 1922-24; adjt., M.S. pol., Dec., 1925; A.C.P., various occasions, 1925-31; dep. comsnr., pol., F.M.S., Jan., 33; comd., F.M.S. pol. depot, Aug., 1935.
HUSSEY, THOMAS MACDONALD.—B. 1888; Long Ashton Coll., Somerset; offl. censor, cinematograph films, S.S., F.M.S. and Johore, 1919.

HUTCHINS, ERNEST ELWYN.—B. 1886; ed. tester Schl.; forestry course, Edinburgh Univ., 03-04; forest dept., S. Africa, 1904; forest deo., Kenya, 1909; astt. conservator, forests, enya, 1911; E. Africa Mounted Rifles, 1915-16; st. polit. offr., German E. Africa, 1916-21; imstve. offr., 1st grade (now dist. offr.), Tanganyika Territory, 1921.

HUTCHINSON, GEORGE STUART MINTETH.—B. 1883; ed. United Services Coll., Westward Ho! and Royal Milv. Coll., Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., unattd. list, 1902; Indian Army, 1903; capt., 1911; major, 1917; Burma Milv. Pol., Southern Shan States, 1910-14; Indian Expedy. Force, France, 1915; ment. in desps., 1915; invalided, 1917; examining offr., Min. of Pensions, 1917; regional wounds offr. for Scotland, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 1st Apr., 1924; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

HUTCHISON, PATRICK HAMILTON.—B. 1902; ed. Edinburgh Acad. and Edin. Univ.; M.A., 1924; LL.B., 1925; admitted solr., Feb., 1927; astt. admstr. gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; ag. admstr. gen., offl. recor., pub. trustee, regis., companies, trade marks, patents, designs, trade unions, regis. gen., births, deaths, marriages, house tax and estate duty comsnt., 1933-34.

HUTT, ALEXANDER MACDONALD BRUCK.—B. 1904; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown, S.A., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (English lit.); cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1926; astt. dist. offr., Jan., 1928; ag. dist. offr., May, 1932; seconded, secre., Mar., 1935.
HUTTON, THEODORE RALPH, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1889; astt. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Oct., 1919; ag. exec. engr., various occasions, 1922-27; exec. engr., 1928-32; ag. sr. exec. engr., Coast, Palang, July, 1933.

HUTTON-MILLS, ALEXANDER, B.A.—B.1900; ed. Dulwich and Keble Coll., Oxford, hons. in jurisprudence; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; practised in Gold Coast, 1924-30; pol. mag., Gold Const., 1930; ag. crown coun., Jan., 1935.
