HUXHAM, HAROLD JAMES, C.M.G. (1936).—B. 1889; income tax advr., Ceylon govt., Aug., 1929-Apr., 1930; comsnr., income tax, Ceylon, Apr., 1932; finan. secy., July, 1934.

HYAMSON, ALBERT MONTEFIORE, O.B.E. (1931), F.R.Hist.S.—B.1873; ed. Oxford High Schl. and Swansea Gram. Schl.; savings bank dept., G.P.O., London, 1896-98; supplementary estab., secy.'s dept., G.P.O., 1898 to Jan., 1921; dept. of infrn., F.O., Dec., 1917 to Mar., 1918; in charge, of sec., miny. of infrn., Apr., to Nov., 1918; Br. war mission, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; astt. dir., immigrn. and travel, Palestine, Jan., 1921; contrlr., lab. Dec., 1921; in charge, dept. of immigrn. and travel, Dec., 1923 to Mar., 1924; contrlr., permits, Apr., 1924; ch. immigrn. offr., Oct., 1926; sec., cent. town planning comsnt., Sept., 1922; mem., economy comitce., chmn., comitce. on rates of wages for govt. employees; dep. chmn., lab. standing comitce.; dir., immigrn., Jan., 1932; author, "A History of the Jews in England," "Palestine—the Rebirth of an Ancient People," "Palestine, Old and New," "British Projects for the Restoration of the Jews," etc.

HYDE, ANTHONY, M.A., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1900; ed. Westminster Schl. and Christ Church, Oxford (exhbr.); called to bar, Inner Temple, 1925; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1924; attld., dist. office, Alor Gajah, Malacca, 1925; prte. sec. to Gov., 1929; seconded to C.O. as astt. prin., Oct., 1931; ag. prte. sec. to parly. U.S. of S. for the Colts., Aug.-Sept., 1933; ag. 2nd astt. sec. (B.) S.S., Nov., 1933.

ILSLEY, THE HON. JAMES LORIMER, K.C., B.A., LL.B. (Digby-Annapolis-Kings).—B. 1894; ed. Somerset pub. schl., Berwick High Schl., Acadia Univ., Wolfville (Arts), and Dalhousie Law Schl., Halifax; degrees of B.A. (Acadia) and LL.B. (Dalhousie); barrister-at-law; elec. to H. of C., Canada, at g.e., 1926, 1930 and 1935; min. of national rev. in King's cabinet, 1935.
IMBERT, A. E.—Labour inspection offr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1919; ch. registr., natives, Kenya, Jan., 1925.

INGLEDOW, CEDRIC FRANCIS.—B. 1894; on mily. serv., Mar., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Aug., 1920; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Oct., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Chilaw, Feb., 1922; office asst., Katnapura kach., Mar., 1924; ditto, Jaffna kach., 1925; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1926; dist. judge, N'Eliva Dist., Dec., 1927; asst. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Oct., 1930; asst. stimt. offr., Mar., 1931.

INGRAMS, WILLIAM HAROLD, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1897; ed. Shrewsbury; served K.S.L.I., Sept., 1914 to May, 1919; wounded, Belgium, May, 1916; employed, directorate of recruiting, W.O., Dec., 1916; min. of nat. serv., Dec., 1917; min. of lab., demob. and resettlement dept., Nov., 1918; F.Z.S., May, 1919; asst. dist. commnr., Zanzibar, July, 1919; F.R.A.I., 1921; F.R.G.S., 1922; M.R.A.S., 1923; mem., Société d'Anthropologie de Paris, 1923; certif., acad. of internat. law, Hague, 1924; higher standard, Swahili, 1923; 2nd asst. sec., Jan., 1925; prte. sec. to ag. high commr., Apr. to June, 1925; ditto to Br. rea., Nov., 1925 to Aug., 1926 and from Oct. to Nov., 1926; ag. pvtc. sec. to H.H. the Sultan, Nov., 1925 to June, 1926; clk., prot. comm., Feb. to June, 1925; edr., Off. Gaz., in 1924, 1925 and 1927; olk., exec. and leg. couns., Aug. to Oct., 1926 and Mar. to July, 1927; mem. Pemba educn. comtee, and teaching of Koran coman., 1924; mem. museum comtee., 1924 and ag. chmn., Aug., 1926; cls. IV., Order of Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1927; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, 1927; chmn., museum reorganisation comtee., 1928; chmn., coman. of enquiry, med. and health dept., 1928; mem. schls. comtee., 1928-29; vice pres. bd. of dirs., Mauritius Inst., 1929; mem., R. Coll., comtee., 1929-31; ag. asst. prin. C.O., 1929-30; ag. recr.-gen., June-July, 1932; ag. col. sec., Jan.-May, and from Aug., 1932; polit. advr., Aden, 1934; ag. polit. secy., Sept., 1934; part author, "Zanzibar; an account of its People, Industries and History" (1924), "Schl. History of Zanzibar" (1925), and "Ava Za Kuraani" (1926); author "Dialects of Zanzibar Sultanate" (1924), "Chronology and Genealogies of Zanzibar Rulers" (1926), "Guide to Swahili Examinations" (1927), "Zanzibar, Its History and People" (1931), "School History of Mauritius" (1931), "School Geography of Mauritius" (1932), and various pamphlets and articles.

INNES, CAMERON STAR.—B. 1892; Imp. serv., male learner, post office, 1908; seconded col. serv., 1921; war serv., 1914-19; ent. Nigeria serv., as asst. survr., posts and tels., Mar., 1921; divnl. survr., Apr., 1933.

INNES, Rt. Hon., Sir James Rose, P.C. (1915), K.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.—B. 1855; called to the bar of sup. ct., Cape Colony, Feb., 1878; mem. for Victoria East house of assem., Cape, 1884; subsequently reptd. Cape div.; atty.-gen., 1st Rhodes ministry, July, 1890, to May, 1893; selected by Imp. govt. to watch proceedings in connection with trial of reform comtee. prisoners at Pretoria, 1896; atty.-gen. 4th Sprigg ministry, 1900-02; deleg. for Cape Colony to attend conf. in Lond. on final ct. of appeal, 1901; ch. just., sup. ct., Transvaal, Mar., 1902; ordinary judge of appeal, sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1910; ch. just. of S. Africa, Oct., 1914; ret., Mar., 1927.

INNESS, WILLIAM JAMES DEACON, C.M.G. (1929), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Ireland).—B. 1877; ed. Exeter Schl. and St. Mary's Hosp., Lond.; house physician, Brit. Royal Hosp.; med. offr., N. Nigeria, 190; asst. med. offr., Nigeria, 1914; asst. dir. of med. Nigeria, 1921; dir., med. and san. serv., Sierra Leone, 1924; dir., med. and san. serv., Gold Coast, Mar., 1927; mem., exec. couns.
INNISS, KNOLLYS ULRIC ALEXANDER.—Ch. B. Edin.—B. 1882; ent. med. Trinidad, Aug., 1904; dist. med. offr., 1912; ag. surg.-gen., Aug.-Dec., 1934.
INSKIP, WALTER.—B. 1887; 14th Hussars, 1906-13; home prison serv., 1914; esq., serv., 1914-18; ment. in desps., 1917; sent to prison serv., Zanzibar, 1924; sup't. transit., 1926.

INSKIPP, PERCY SIDNEY, O.B.E.—Clerk, admnstr. offr., S. Rhodesia, 1891; B.S.A. Co., 1892; sec. to admnstr., 1893; under sec. joined London staff, B.S.A. Co., 1900; B.S.A. Co., Apr., 1919; gen. man., B.S.A. S. Africa, June, 1922; mem. of pioneer corps (occupation of Mashonaland), 1890; also with the Mashonaland horse, Matabele reg., 1896 (medal), and Rhodesia field force (medal and 2 clasps); joined R.A.S.C., O.C., R.A.S.C., Harwich dist., 1914-17; 48th divn., France, Flanders, Italy, 1917-18; di Guerra, Italy; ment. in desps.

IRBY, G. N.—B. 1889; ed. Eton and Magd. Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., Oxford and N.I., May, 1915; invalided with rank of maj., Jan., 1918; asst. priv. sec. (apptmnt), to Milner, July, 1920; to Mr. Churchill, 1921; the Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1921; J. H. Thomas, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. Amery, Nov., 1924; prin., 1st Oct., 1926.
IRELAND, FREDERICK JOSEPH.—B. 1889; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., Sept., 1920.

IRVINE, FREDERICK ROBERT, M.S.—Fellow, Linnean Soc. (1927).—B. 1888; ed. Grammar Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne; w/o Gordon Highlanders, France and Belgium, 1919; 2nd lieut., R.A.F., 1918; R.S.E. at Armstrong Coll., Durham Univ., 1920; prof. of agr. and biology, Achimota, Gold Coast.

IRVINE, ROBERT, M.C. (1917).—B. 1894; Dunfermline High Schl.; bd. of agr. Soc., 1913; served in France, 1915-18; capt., L. Fusiliers, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; sec. to govt., Mar., 1921; cls. V., July, 1921; asst. dist. offr., Tampin, Dec., 1922; ag. 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1923; dist. offr., Jelebu, Nov., 1924; asst. offr., Lower Perak, Jan., 1925; offr., July, 1925; ag. 2nd asst. sec. (A) S.S., 1926; ag. infast. sec. (B) S.S. and clk. of corp., Mar., 1928; dist. offr., Tampin, Dec., 1929; cls. III., July, 1930; ag. 1st asst. sec. (A) S.S., Jan., 1934; offr. cls. II., July, 1935.

IRVINE, Hon. Sir WM. HILL, G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.M.G. (1914), LL.D., K.C.—B. 1855; ed. the bar, Victoria, 1884; premier of Victoria, 1904; mem. H. of R., C. of A., since 1906; atty.-gen. C. of A., June, 1913 to Sept., 1914; chief justice, Victoria, 1918-35; lieut.-gov., Victoria, 1918; ag. gov. from June, 1931 to May, 1935.

IRVING, FRANCIS ERNEST.—B. 1883; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and Marist Bros., S. Africa; served in Boer War, 1901-2; S.W. Africa, with Imp. Light Horse, 1904; German E. Africa, 1916; seconded to admin. Mafia Is., as finan. offr., Nov., 1916. asst., cust. dept., Zanzibar, Oct., 1919; ag. compt., cust. for various periods, 1920-22; compt., cust., Dec., 1922; ag. compt., cust. various periods, 1924-36.
VING, HUGH CRAUFORD.—Asst. dist. ir., Somaliland Prot., June, 1914.

VING, HENRY EDWARD, M.B.E.—Ed. at warehouse, and Handelslehranstalt, Leipzig; 9th Lancers, 1899-1902; served in S.A. (and four oases); oh. clk. to rent. riation bd., O.R.C., Mar., 1902; olk. to ec., O.R.C., Oct., 1902; prov. sec. dept.; lieut., 16th M.R., 1913; seconded for duty snsr., O.F.S., 1914-19; ment. in desps.; M.B.E., 1918; ch. clk. to prov. sec., 1924; prov. sec., O.F.S. prov. admstrn., June; prov. sec., O.F.S., Nov., 1935.

WIN, HENRY BAKER, B.A.—B. 1885; ed. at eybury and Selwyn Coll.; Cambridge exhib.; Haileybury exhibitioner; hon. class. m., 1908; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1908; shed to secretariat, July to Dec., 1911; priv. to ag. gov., C. L. Temple, Esq., C.M.G., e., 1912.
WIN, ROBERT EDWARD.—B. 1899; ed. at ist'a Hosp.; col. audit dept., Oct., 1925; asst. r., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1925; asst. r., Gold Coast, Dec., 1928.

SAACS, RT. HON. SIR ISAAC ALFRED, (1921), G.C.M.G. (1932), K.C.M.G. (1928)—1855; called to the bar, Victoria, 1880; Q.C.; M.L.A., Victoria, 1892-1901; solr.-gen.; atty.-gen., 1894-9; and again in 1900; mem. Aust. Fed. Convention, 1897-8; and of first leral parlmt.; atty.-gen. in Deakin ministry, 5-6; judge of High Ct. of Australia, Oct., 6; ag. ch. just., 1925 and 1927; ch. just., r., 1930; gov. gen. of the C'wealth of australia, Jan., 1931.

SHERWOOD, ALBERT ARTHUR MANGNALL, B.E. (1926), M.A. (Oxon) (hons.).—B. 1889; St. John's schl., Leatherhead, and University II., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, 1913; Nigerian Land Contingent, 1914; polit. dept., N. Nigeria, 1915-17; polit. r., Tanganyika admstrn., 8th Oct., 1917; ag. nr. comsnr., Feb., 1923 to Jan., 1924; dep. dir., ucn., Sept., 1924; ag. dir., educn., Oct., 1924 Mar., 1925; ditto and mem., exec. and leg. uns., in 1927, 1929, and 1931; Swahili examr., r. educn., 1931.
IVES, HON. WILLIAM CARLOS, B.C.L.—B. 73; ed. pub. schl., Pincher Creek, Alta.; oaticock, Queb., and McGill Univ.; puianeidge, sup. ct., Alta., 1914.

IVEY, LILKUT. COMMDR. A. V. P., R.D., N.R.—B. 1894; Royal Navy, 1915; sub. asst., R.N.R., 1917; lieut. commdr., 1927; commdr. (ret. list), 1935: Cameroons expedy. force, 1915-16; Harwich force destroyers, 1916-17; commanded Torpedo boats, 1918; commanded I.M. river gunboat "Robin" on West River, China, 1919; marine offr., Nigeria, 1920; harb. nast., Port Harcourt, 1926; prim. marine offr., 1930; ag. dir., marine, Oct., 1934 to May, 1935; dir., marine, 1935.

IZARD, GODFREY WALLACE.—B. 1891; ed. Uppingham; B.S.A. Co., 1909-14; called to bar Middle Temple, June, 1914; enlisted, Aug., 1914; invalided; admstve. offr., Nigeria, Jan., 1915; admstve. serv., Apr., 1930; dep. comsnr., lands, 1930; prim. asst. sec., N. Prova., 1931; sec., N. Prova., May, 1936.
IZARD, H.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915.

JACK, HENRY WALTER, M.B.E. (1930), D.S.c., B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Univ. Coll., Cork (scholar); B.A. (econ.), 1911; B.Sc. (biol.), 1912; agr. inspr., F.M.S., 1914; agr. instr., 1915; co. botanist, S.S. & F.M.S., 1919; mem., comttee. on rubber research, 1923; D.Sc. by research, 1926; rep. of Malaya, Pac. Sc. Cong., Japan, 1926 and in Java, 1929; economic botanist, Mar., 1929; mem., coconut and oil palm research comttee, 1929; hon. sec., rice cult. comttee, Malaya, 1930; dir. agr., Fiji, 1934; nom. M.L.C.; publns. on rice, coconuts, oil palm, cotton, rubber, etc.

JACK, MURRAY M.—B. 1893; ed. Peterhead Acad. and Aberdeen Univ.; solr., sup. ct. Scotland, 1920; H.B.M. consular ct., Egypt, 1922-25; war serv., Gordon Highlands and I.A.R.O., 1914-19 (capt., ment. in desps.); dep. registr., sup. ct., Kenya, 1925; asst. admstr. gen. and offic. recor., Tanganyika Territory 1926; registr., sup. ct., Kenya and E.A. ct. of appl., July, 1929; rep. and coll., E. A. Law Repts., 1930-32; ch. registr., sup. ct., Palestine, 1935.

JACKLIN, S., M.C.—Acctnt., repat. dept., Christiana, Aug., 1902; rev. clk., law dept., Nov., 1902; clk., civ. comsnn. off., Pretoria, Nov., 1903; clk., rev. dept., Mar., 1906; prin. clk., Oct., 1906; prin. clk., inland rev. dept., Apr., 1912; inspr., inland rev. dept., Nov., 1916; survr., inland rev. dept., Apr., 1918; ag. dep. comanr., Johannesburg, Sept., 1919; ch. clk., estabtns., treas., Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1920; sec., pub. ser. comsnn., Oct., 1921; ret., Oct., 1926; treas., League of Nations, Oct., 1926.

JACKSON, DONALD EDWARD, LL.B. (Lond.).—B. 1892; called to bar (Middle Temple), 1927; practised at bar, Br. Guiana; stip. mag., 1931; Br. Guiana sp. comanr. to Trinidad to investigate working of workmen's compensation ordn., 1933; ag. asst. atty.-gen., 1933 and 1936; ag. regtr., deeds, sup. ct. and West Indian ct. of appl., 1934 and 1935; senr., stip. mag., 1936.

JACKSON, SIR EDWARD ST. JOHN, KT. BACH. (1933), O.B.E. (1918), K.C.—Ed. Stonyhurst and Beaumont Colleges and Brazenose College, Oxford; B.A., 1908; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1910; legal adviser, Gambia, Oct., 1912; atty.-gen., Nyasaaland, Oct., 1918; judge of high ct., July, 1920; pres. of lands comanr., 1920; pres., finance comanr., 1923; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1924; atty.-gen., Ceylon, May, 1929; ret., 1936.

JACKSON, GEORGE MOORE ROSS, M.N.Z.I.S., L.S.—B. 1889; lands and survey dept., N.Z., Feb., 1906-June, 1919; survr., Johore, Dec., 1920; do., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1925; asst. supt., rev. surveys, do., Jan., 1926; do., Salangor, May, 1929; do., N. Kedah, Aug., 1930; do., Muar, Jan., 1933; ag. supt., rev. surveys, Selangor and N. Sembilan, Jan., 1935.
JACKSON, GILDART.—B. 1901; ed. Edgeborough Schl., Guildford and Westminster; asst. dist. comanr., Sierra Leone, Nov., 1924.

JACKSON, JOHN.—Ed. St. Mark's Coll. Schl., Chelsea, and King's Coll.; called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1934; on serv. 1914-19 (U.K., France and N. Russia); admstve. serv., Nigeria, Dec., 1920; transf'd to col. legal serv. as ast. judge of high ct., Nigeria, Apr., 1935.

JACKSON, ROBERT BEST, M.B., D.P.H., B.A.O., M.D., T.C.D., L.M. Rotunda Hospital, Dublin, certif., London Schl. Trop. Med.—B.1879; R.A.M.C., 1914-17; med. offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; M.O.H., Negri Sembilan, 1921; do., Pahang West, 1926; malariologist, med. dept. Hong Kong, 1930.

JACOBSON, CHARLES ERNEST HARVEY.—B.Sc. (Chem.), B.Sc. (Bot.).—St. Peter's College, Cambridge; M.A., Christ's College, Cambridge; ed. London School Board; B.Sc. (Bot.), B.Sc. (Chem.); R.C.S.; R.A.C.S.; M.I.M.E.; F.R.C.S.; R.F.C.; M.F.H.; F.F.I.; F.F.R.; F.F.R.; F.F.F.; F.F.F.; F.F.F.; F.F.F.; F.F.F.; F.F.F.; F.F.F.; F.F.F.; F.F.F.; F.F.F.; F.F.F.; F.F.F.; F.F.F.; F.F.F.; F.F.F.; ch. sec., Jan., 1929; gov's dep. on several occasions since 1927; ag. gov., in 1929, 1931 and 1933; gov., North Borneo, 1933; accredited rep. to Permut. Mandates Com., 1929, 1930 and 1931; joint-editor "Hand-book of Cyprus," 1913 and 1919, and author of "Mad Mullah of Somaliland," 1923.

JARDINE, ROBERT FRIER, C.M.G. (1928), O.B.E. (1926).—B. 1894; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge; served European War in Gallipoli, Egypt and Mesopotamia; polit. offr., Mesopotamia, 1917; asst. divnl. adviser, Mosul prov., under Iraq govt., 1921; admntr. msptr., Mosul prov., 1923; on sp. duty in Europe in connec. with Mosul question, 1925; admntr. inspr., Sulaimani, 1925; do., Mosul prov., 1925; ditto, Basra, 1928; ch. inspr., land regist., sec., dept. lands and surveys, Palestine, 1936; author "Grammar of Baghdad Kurmanji Kurdish," 1922.

JARRETT, JAMES HENRY, K.C.—B. 1895; ed. Lancing Coll.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; H.A.C., 1914; served European War, 1914-19; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, 1919; seconded, judl. dept. as mag., 1922; mag., 1924; asst. atty.-gen., 1926; ag. solr. gen., May, 1927 to Jan., 1928; crown coun., 1927; atty.-gen., Grenada, 1929; ag. admntr. and ag. ch. just., St. Vincent in 1930 and 1931; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1933; ag. ch. just. and O.A.G., 1934; dep. gov., 1935 and 1936; col. sec., Aug., 1935; O.A.G., 1936.

JARRETT, NORMAN ROWSTONE.—B. 1889; ed. Highgate and Exeter Coll., Oxford, 3rd cls. class. mods., 1910, 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1912; B.A., 1912; cadet, F.M.S., 1913; passed cadet, July, 1914; offr., cls. V, Feb., 1916; cls. IV, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Mar., 1921; offr., cls. II, Jan., 1925; offr., cls. II, 1930; asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Apr., 1930; ag. sec. to res., Perak, May, 1931; ag. Br. advr., Trungguanu, June, 1933; offr., cls. I I, 1934; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Feb., 1934.

JARVIN, CYRIL CHESTER.—B. 1884; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; Rhodes schol., 1904; Wadham Coll., Oxford, B.A. (hon. juris.); LL.B., Cape Univ.; sec., ch. just., Cape Colony, 1907; admitted Cape bar, 1910; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., O.F.S.; prof. ass't. to atty.-gen., Cape Prov., 1921; crown pros., Johannesburg, 1926; atty.-gen., Natal, 1933.

JAYATNE, MERENNA FRANCIS DE SILVA, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; attd. Colombo kach., Mar., 1927; office asst., Matale kach., Jan., 1928; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1930; fice asst., Batticaloa kach., Mar., 1933; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Oct., 1934.
JAYASUNDARA, LAYARD, B.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1910; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1935.

JEFF, CAPT. JOHN.—B. 1896; ed. Glasgow Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., attd Chinese prot., Ipoh, Apr., 1920; passed cadet, Dec., 1922; asst. prot., Chinese Seribum, Dec., 1923; attd., cust. dept., Jan., 1925; offr., cls. IV., Apr., 1927; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, Apr., 1928; ditto, N Sembilan, Jan., 1930; ditto, Selangor and Pahang, May, 1931; offr., cls. III., Apr., 1932; ag. ast. sec., Chinese affrs., S.S., Aug., 1933; do., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1934.

JEFF, RICHARD HUDLEY ALLISON.—B. 1889; jr. asst. engnr., F.M.S. Rlys., June, 1913; dist. engnr., grade III, head office, July, 1915; supervmr. dist. engnr., July, 1919; on sp. duty in S. India, Aug., 1919; dist. engnr., grade II, K. Lumpur, Jan., 1920; dist. engnr., grade I, Jan., 1924; ag. sec. to gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., June, 1928-Aug., 1929; sr. dist. engnr., head office, June, 1929; ditto, Johore Bahru, Feb., 1931; divnl. engnr., F.M.S. rlys., Aug., 1932.

JEFFERY, CECIL ALBERT, C.M.G. (1936).—B. 1888; ed. Clyde schl., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. post and tel. dept., 1908; cik., govt. house, 1913; pvt. sec. to various ministers, 1915-20; ch. pvt. sec. to prime min., 1926-35; elk., exec. coun. and sec. to cabinet, 1935; served with N.Z. expd. force.

JEFFERY, GEORGE, O.B.E. (1924), F.S.A.—Scholarship, R. Coll. of Art and Science, 1872; scholarship in architecture, R. Academy of London (1874); F.R.I.B.A., 1892; architect to Rt. Rev. Bishop in Jerusalem and the East, 1892; hon. corres. mem. of Imperial Institute of Arch. of Russia, 1897; local sec. for Cyprus Society of Antiquaries; inspr. of pub. wks., Cyprus, 1898; curator of ancient monuments, Cyprus, 1903; relinquished appt. July, 1919; reappointed curator of ancient monuments, Cyprus, Feb., 1921; author of "Summary of Architectural Monuments of Cyprus," "Cyprus under an English King," "Historic Monuments of Cyprus," and of several papers on the medieval archaeology of Cyprus and Jerusalem, edr., new edition (1929) of "An attempt at a Bibliography of Cyprus."

JEFFREY, PAT COMMIR, A., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1892; ed. Glas. Univ.; R.N., 1914-19; probationer, N. Rhodesia, 1919; J.P., 1920; clk. of ct. and under-sheriff, Batoka dist., 1920; asst. nat. commr., 1921; nat. commr., 1926; passed higher Chinyanga; asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory 1927; ast. sec'y., Jan., 1930; ag. ast. ch. sec., 1934-35; clk., exec. and leg. couns., 1935; admntr. offr., 1st gr., Zanzibar, Aug., 1935; ast. sec'y., clk., leg. coun., edr., gazette and treas. liaison offr., Aug., 1935; ag. ast. ch. sec., Mar.-Oct., 1936.

JEFFREYS, ROBERT SYDNEY.—B. 1888; ed. Cape Town (S. Africa), Bedford Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A., LL.B. (1909), Barrister-at-Law (Gray's Inn, 1910); joined admntr. of N. Rhodesia, June, 1914; ast. native commr., and J.P., July, 1916; resigned to proceed on active serv., Feb., 1918; rejoined N. Rhodesia admntr., May, 1919; native commr., July 1921; seconded to secretariat, Nov., 1921-Dec., 1922; ast. mag., Apr., 1926; dist. offr. (grade II) with powers of mag., Apr., 1928; seconded as ast. to sec. for native affrs., Mar., 1927-Oct., 1928; ag. crown coun., Nov., 1928-Mar., 1929; ag. pol. mag., May, 1930; res. mag., Apr., 1936.

JEFFRIES, CHARLES J., O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1896; ed. Malvern Coll.; class demy., Magdalen Coll., Oxford, 1914; 2nd lieut., 3rd Wiltsire Regt., Sept., 1915; served in France, 1916; lieut., 1917; invalided from army, Nov., 1917; tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 27th Dec., 1917; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., 16th July, 1919; sec., E. Africa land stintln. selection board, 1919; ast. sec., E. Africa currency board, prmn., 1st Apr., 1920; hon. sec., Corona Club, since 1921; sec., comteee. on governors' pensions, 1927-28; ast. sec., 1st Oct., 1930.

JEFFRIES, CHARLES WILLIAM, F.R.A.S.—B. 1882; Computer, Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1897; junr. ast., Royal Observatory, Cape of Good Hope, 1903; 1st ast., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1907; chief ast., ditto, 1912; ag. dir. in 1915, 1920, 1924, 1925 and 1929; dir., 1932.

JEKYLL, Lieut.-Col. Sir Herbert, K.C.M.G. (1901), R.E., C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1846; lieut., R.E., Apr., 1866; employed under War Office and Trinity House in 1868 to 1870, and destruction of sunken ships, from submarine mining, and under G.P.O. in the telegraph dept., from 1870 to 1873, and 1874 to 1876; served in the campaign of 1873-74 on the G. Coast; priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, Jan., 1877, to Feb., 1878; sec. to the col. defence comte., 1878; sec. to the roy. comm. on the defence of Br. possessions and commerce abroad, 1879-82; specially employed to visit and report on the defences of Singapore and Colombo, 1883-4; sec. col. defence comte., 1885; priv. sec. to Lord Carnarvon, viceroy of Ireland, June, 1885, to Jan., 1886; sec. to roy. comm. for Melbourne centennial exhib., 1888; priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-5; sec. to the roy. comm. for the Paris exhibition, 1900.

JELF, Sir Arthur Selborne, Kt. Bach. (1932), C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1876; ed. Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxford (extr.), 2nd cls., class. mods., 1897; 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1899; held various admin. and exec. post in Malay States, 1900-10; asst. gen. adviser to Johore govt. Jan., 1911; offr. of cls. IV., Jan., 1912; chmn., san. bd., Kinta, Pernak, Apr., 1914 to Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., Sep., 1914; lieut., M.S.V.R., Jan., 1915; seconded for mil. serv. (lieut., general list), Apr., 1917; offr. of cls. III., Dec., 1917; tempy. capt., gen. staff, S. Stlttns command; attchd. gen. staff, W.O. (milit. intell.), Jan., 1918; H.M. Petroleum Exec. (sec. to Lord Harcourt's comte.), June, 1918; offr., cls. II., June, 1919; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., June, 1920; dir., Malayan polit. intell. bureau, cls. I.B., Dec., 1921; mem., Br. Malaya opium comte., Dec., 1923; pres., estate health comn., Apr., 1924 (recd. thanks of S. of S.); sec. to, Jamaica, Oct., 1925; ag. gov., for various periods 1925-32; lieut.-col., local forces, Oct., 1927; ret. 1935.

JENKINS, Edward Enoch.—B. 1895; ed. Peterhouse, Cambridge, B.A., LL.B., 1922; M.A., 1927; called to bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1924; joined South Wales circuit; war serv. (France) 1914-19; lieut., R.F.A.; cadet, Nyasaland, Sept., 1925; ag. atty-gen., Apr., July, 1927; ag. asst. atty-gen., July-Oct., 1927; asst. regtr., high ct., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1927; crown coun., Apr., 1930; ag. atty-gen., various occasions, 1931-36; ag. judge, high ct., 1934; solr-gen., Jan., 1936.

JENKINSON, Thomas Richard.—B. 1893; Cape Col. postal serv., Mar., 1910; postal asst., N. Rhodesia, Mar., 1914; postmr. various centres, 1915-20; ag. acct., G.P.O., 1925-28; survr., 1928; ag. depy. P.M.G., several occasions, 1927-33; asst. P.M.G., Apr., 1933; ag. P.M.G. and chmn., elec. control bd., in 1934 and 1936.

JENKS, J. C. A.—Ed. Bristol Gram. Schl. and Angers, France; awarded Croix-de-Guerre (Palm) 1917; Order of Saint Stanislaus with Swords (2nd cls.), and ment. in desps., 1919; colonial audit serv., 1920; asst. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, Mar., 1923; senr. asst. audr. in charge, Jan., 1926; mem., retrenchment and rev. comte., Zanzibar, 1927; Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar (5th cls.), 1928; senr. asst. audr. in charge, Kenya and Uganda rly. and harbrs., 1929; audr., Mauritius, 1933.
JENNER, Frank.—B. 1878; served in Cape Mounted Rifles, Bechuanaland campaign 1896-97; Aug., 1928.
JENNINGS, F. G.—Asst. dist. comm. E.A.F., Apr., 1919.
JENNINGS, Priscilla Henry.—B. 1896; Christ's Hosp.; col. audt. dept., 1926; asst. audt., N. Rhodesia, 1927; asst. audt., Mauritius, 1928; ag. audt., 1932.

JEPSON, Frank Price.—B. 1886; M.A., Pembroke Coll., Camb.; exhib., medalion sch. dip. in agric. (hons.) in entomology, Wraysb. Coll., Kent, 1906; trop. disease reserch student in med. entomology, Camb. Univ., 1910; assisted in investig. by L.G.B. on flies of infection, 1908 and 1909; offr. entomologist, Fiji, 1909; offr. visits to Hawaii, 1911; Sec., 1912, Java, 1913; asst. entomologist, (civ.), 1919; ag. entomologist in addn., July, 1920; Mar., 1924 and Oct. to Nov., 1925; offr., U.S.A. (termite investig.), 1929; dep. dir., 1936.

JERMYN, Lancelot Ambrose Scott.—B.A. (Oxon.), M.R.S.T.—B. 1886; ed. Trin. (greenwood and Keble Coll., Oxford; held Malay Coll., K. Kangsar, F.M.S., Oct., 1900; do., Northam rd. Eng. Schl., Penang, Jan., 1901; do., Govt. Eng. Schl., Johore, Feb., 1901; do., Anderson Schl., Ipoh, Feb., 1902; do., High Schl., Malacca, Sept., 1930; do., High Schl., Auckland, Sept., 1931; Thames High Schl. and Auckland Grad., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. state fire insurance offr., acct., 1906; dep. gen. man., 1908; gen. treas., July, 1936.

JESSE, William.—B. 1895; ed. Magd. Coll.; served Indian Army R. of O. in Afghan campaign, 1919; Calcutta Leg., 1921-22; apptd. treas., Kenya, 1931; treas., Uganda, Jan., 1923; asst. currency treas., Uganda, 1927-28 and 1933; asst. treas., Uganda, 1935; asst. currency offr., Jan., 1936.

JEWELL, Morton, M.B.K. (1931)—B. 1899; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the treasury, 1919; asst. clk. of the treasury, 1920; conf. clk., 1911; deptl. asst., 1922; staff offr., establishments, 1924.
JINADASA, Hettiarachchi, R.S.—Lond.—B. 1910; cadet, Ceylon civ. service, 1934.

JOBLING, Geoffrey Lionel—B. 1895; Bowen Schl., Brisbane; called to bar, Gray's Inn; served German S.W. Africa, 1915; R.A.F., 1918-19; law dept., B.S.A., Rhodesia, Nov., 1915; asst. regtr., Rhodesia; asst. offr. recr. in bankery, 1920; gen., asst. regtr., lands and deeds, births, deaths and marriages, asst. companies, asst. custodian enemy property, local clearing office, asst. regtr., Uganda, June, 1927; crown coun., Uganda Territory, Aug., 1929; ag. solr.-gen., 1934.
JOHN, Samuel Spedding.—B. 1895; at-law, Four Courts, Dublin, 1914-20; admstive serv., Nigeria; asst. judge, high ct., 1934.
JOHNS, Brian Maurice (N.Z.), F.R.C.S. (Ed.), D.T.M., 1901; med. offr., S.S., Mar., surgery., King Edward VII Coll.

INS, Robert.—B. 1903; ed. Monmouth Agr. Inst., Usk, and Univ. Coll. of Wales, Aberystwyth; Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad; A.C.D.A., C.D.D., A.I.C.T.A.; stock, Tanganyika, 1926; C.O. agric. research, Trinidad, and Univ. of Reading, 1929-30; offr., Zanzibar, 1931.
JOHNSTONE, JAMES MONTAGU CHOLMELRY.—Ent. prison dept. (England and Wales), Aug., 1921; dep. asst. supt., prisons, Trinidad, 1924; asst. supt., 1927.

JOMARON, ADOLPHE CHARLES—B. 1893; ed. at University Coll. Schol. and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb. (class. schol.)—B.A., 1920; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914; lieut., 1915; capt., 1917; ag. major, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920, attd. to asst. advisor's office, Muar, Johore; 2nd asst. adviser, Muar, Johore, Jan., 1922; offr., cls. V., June, 1922; asst. state audr., chim., conservancy bd., Khotia Bharu and coroner, June, 1924; offr., cls. IV., June, 1925; 3rd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1925; sec. to rivers commrs. in addn., Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III., June, 1930; hon. 2nd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; hon. A.D.C. to high commr., Mar., 1931; offr., assignee and regisr., companies in addn., July, 1936; offr., cls. II., June, 1935.

JONES, CHARLES ERNEST.—B. 1892; B.A., B.Sc., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attd. to Kegalla kach., Dec., 1914; Ratnapura kach., Apr., 1915; Kegalla kach., Jan., 1916; censor's off., Apr., 1916; on mil. duty, 1918; returned to censor's off., June, 1919; pol. mag., Matara, July, 1919; sec., retrenchment comm., Jan., 1922; admnl. asst. dir., educ., Dec., 1923; dist. judge, N'Elia, Apr., 1925; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Nov., 1925; asst. col. treas., Sept., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Matale, June, 1929; dep. collr., cust., Sept., 1933; ag. govt. asst., Prov. of Uva, Apr., 1935; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Nov., 1935.
JONES, HON. FREDERICK.—B. 1884; ed. Albany Strt. schl., Dunedin and Normal schol., Christchurch; entd. N.Z. H. of R., 1931; postmtr. gen., min. of tels. and min. of defence, 1935.

JONES, GEORGE ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1889; app't., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Dec., 1907; asst. sec. to Oversea Prize Disposal Comtee., Mar., 1917; ag. sec. to ditto, Jan., 1918; promoted under O-in-C. of 1910, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 14th Mar., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

JONES, GWILYMD ARTHUR, Agric. diploma, Univ. Coll., Bangor, N. Wales, with 1st cls. in agric.; awarded Wm. Griffith prize, 1906-8; holder of certif. in forestry; ag. agric. and science master, Antigua gram. schl., 1909; asst. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, Aug., 1909; in charge of agric. schl., Dominica, Aug., 1909, to Jan., 1910; asst. sec. to permanent exhibtn. comtee., 1909; ag. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, Apr., 1913; chemist, agric. dept., Dominica, Apr., 1915, and a director of the Victoria museum, 1915.

JONES, CART. NELSON, M.C. and Bar.—B. 1895; ed. Glasgow Univ.; commr., Cameron Highlands, 1915; discharged, on account of wounds, Apr., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; asst. contr., Lab., Penang, Mar., 1920; ditto, K. Lumpur, May, 1921; passed cadet, Dec., 1921; attd. dist. office, Klang to act as 1st cls. mag., 1923; 3rd mag., S'pore, July, 1924; offr., cls. IV., Oct., 1926; asst. advr., Kukup, May, 1929; ag. emigrn. commr., Malaya, Madras, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. III., Oct., 1931; mag., J. Bahrui, Jan., 1933; offr., admstr., Johore, Nov., 1934; ag. regisr.-gen., statistics S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1936.

JONES, PERCY SYDNEY TWENTYMAN.—B. 1876; ed. at Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; White prize for classics; Archbishop's Greek testament prize; B.A., Cape, 1896; LL.B., Cap., 1898; called to bar, Aug., 1898; took silk, 1921; "Liquor Laws of Cape," and jointly w/ E. Buckle "Magistrates' Court Practice of S. Africa," judge, Aug., 1926.
JONES, RHYS CARADOC, M.B., B.S. (L'pool), M.R.C.S., (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Ed.), D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), B.1901; Med. Nigeria, Aug., 1929; health offr., Hong Kong, Dec., 1935.

JONES, ROBERT NOBLE, C.B.E. (1864); barrister and sol., NZ, 1895; native land ct. and dist. land reg., 1901; judge, native land ct., since 1918; wrt. sec., native dept., 1922-33 and native dept., 1933.

JONES, SAMUEL BENJAMIN, M.B., B.S. (Durh.), B.A. (Lond.), Inter. LL.B. (Lond.), L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (Edin.), M.R.C.S. (Glasg.).—B. 1874; ed. Antigua Gram. med., offr., and mag., Anguilla, 1918; by exec. coun. of St. Kitts for checking cases of smallpox in Anguilla, 1921; med. offr., No. 2, St. Kitts, 1924.

JONES, STANLEY BOSSCAWEN.—Chaplain, Beira and Mashonaland and Rhodesia, Bulawayo, 1912-18; 2nd Rhodesia Regt., asst. native commr., and native commr., Rhodesia, 1918-28; asst. dist. offr., Transvaal Territory, Apr., 1928; dist. offr., Oct., 1930.

JONES, STANLEY WILSON.—B. 1868 (Manchester), 1st cls. hons. hist., 1908; B.A., law (Middle Temple); cadet F.M.S., 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., Pekan, passed cadet, Dec., 1913; offr., cls. IV., 1915; asst. dist. offr., Pekan, Aug., 1917; Kangsar, June, 1918; offr., cls. IV., 1919; dist. offr., Jelebu; Jan., 1920; do., R.L., 1920; local examr. in law and Malay, Nov., 1920; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1923; attd. to mil. duty, Aug., 1924; local examr. in Malay, Sept., 1924; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1925; IB, May, 1931; commr., lands dept., Nov., 1933; under sec. to govt., F.M.S., cls. I.A., Feb., 1936.

JONES, WILLIAM JOHN ANGUS.—(1937), B.A. (Oxford).—B. 1889; ed. Oxford; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1913; dist. commr., Dec., 1918; commr., Jan., 1924; prov. commr., sec., native affrs., 1930; ag. commr., sec., 1932; gov't. dep., Sept., 1932; N. Territories, 1933.

JORDAN, ARTHUR BENJAMIN.—B. 1893; Nottingham High Schol., and Jesus Coll., (class. exhib.)—B.A., Oxon., F.M.S., Nov., 1913; passed final exam., 1914; E.S.E., Apr., 1916; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., cadet, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. V., 1917; contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, Dist. Commr., M.V.I., Dec., 1918; asst. dist. commr., (Bentong), Feb., 1919; offr., cls. V., 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, N. Sembilan, offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; prot., 1926; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1926; prot., Selangor and Pahang, Dec., 1926; prot., L. of N. Opium Confe., Patani, prot., Chinese, Selangor and Perak, prot., Chinese affrs., Malaya, 1933.
JORDAN, WILLIAM JOSEPH.—B. 1893; St. Luke's Parochial Schol., Liverpool; H. of R., 1919-36; high commr., U.K., 1936; represented N. Opium Confe., Canada, 1928.
JORDAN, A. H.—B. 1903; ent. C.O., 17th Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 14th Oct., 1921.

JOUBERT, M. J. A., B.S.A. (Toronto).—B. 1882; pupil teacher, O.F.S., 1897; asst. to prov. agronomist, O.F.S., 1905; itinerant schol., 1910; offr.-in-ch. and prin., Glen schl. of gr., 1913; prin., schl. of agr., Glen., 1917; prin., rootfontein schl. of agr. (Cape), 1927; asst. ch. gr., plant industry, dept., agr., Pretoria, 1929; i., divn. of agr. educn. and extension, Pretoria, 1932; under-secr., agr., 1933.

JOWITT, HAROLD.—B. 1893; ed. Univ. Coll., Southampton, B.A., M.Ed., Dip. Ed. (S.A.); admr., Edendale Training Coll., Natal, 1913; aspr., schls., Natal, 1918; dir., nat. educn., S. Rhodesia, Dec., 1927; dir., nat. development, pr., 1929; dir., educn., Uganda, Dec., 1934; author of "Principles of Education for African teachers", "Suggested Methods for African schools".

JOY, GEORGE ANDREW.—B. 1896; ed. St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Bruges; ent. Army, 1914; served in Belgium and France, 1915 and 1916; Palestine and Syria, 1917-18; staff gen. headqrs., Egypt and Palestine, 1919-20; S.W. Arabia, 1921-23; ment. in desps., lieut., R.A.R.O., Feb., 919; asst. to res. commr., New Hebrides, Aug., 924; dep. commr., W. Pacific, July, 1926; ag. tr. res. commr., Aug., 1927; res. commr., New Hebrides, 1928; temp. attd., staff of col. development advisory comte., C.O., Jan., 1931.
JOYCE, G. F. W. C.—B. 1897; ent. E.I.O., June, 1912; asst. clk., Jan., 1915; on mil. serv., sept., 1914 to Apr., 1919; cler. offr., O.S.O., st. Jam., 1920.

JOYCE, BREVET LIEUT.-COL. PIERCE CHARLES, J.B.E. (1920), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1878; ed. Beaumont Coll., Old Windsor; joined 1st Batt. The Sonnaghetti Rangers, 1900; serv. S. African War, 1900-02 (wounded, Queen's Med. with 3 clasps, King's Med. with 2 clasps); attd., Egyptian Army, 1907-16 (Egyptian Med. with clasp, 4th cls. Order of the Mejidieh, 4th cls. Order of the Nile); European War, 1915 (Gallipoli); Arab campaign against the Turks, 1916-18; mily. advr. to Amir Faisal, 1917 (ment. in desps. five times, brevet of lieut.-col., C.B.E., D.S.O., Croix d'Officier, Legion of Honour, Arabian Order of the Naehdad, 2nd cls.) gov., E. and S. Desert Provs., Egypt, 1920; mil. adviser to Iraq Govt., Mar., 1921.

JOYNT, HENRY RAYMOND, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Bradford and Balliol Coll., Oxon. (math. schol.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; passed cadet, July, 1914; dist. offr., K. Langat, Nov., 1919; dep. contr. of lab., Seremban, Mar., 1921; agt. to food contr., N. Sembilan in addn., Apr. to July, 1921; on sp. duty, rubber census office, Kuala Lumpur, June, 1922; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1923; second asst. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Nov., 1923; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928; attd. lab. dept., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1929; commr., lands and mines, Johore, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. I.B., May, 1931; agt. finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S., May, 1933 and Mar., 1936; contr., rubber, Malaya, Apr., 1935.

JUDSON, HARRY.—B. 1874; ed. Mount Radford Schl., Exeter; inspr., Nat. Bank of S. Africa, 1903; ch. inspr., 1912; gen. man., Barclays Bank (Dom., Col. and Overseas) Pretoria; ret., 1932; vice chmn., S. African bd. of Barclays Bank (Dom., Col. and Overseas); pub. dept. commr., Union of S. Africa, 1935.

JUNER, MAJOR NORMAN ROSS, O.B.E. (1936), M.C., D.Sc., D.I.C., M.Inst.M.M.—B. 1891; ed. Melbourne Univ. and Imp. Coll. of Sc. and Technology; 1851 exhibn. schol. for Mel bourne Univ., 1913; lect. in geology, schol. of Mines, Ballarat, 1919-20; war serv. in France, Italy and Malta, 1914-18; geologist, Gold Coast geol. surv., 1920; dir., Sierra Leone geol. surv., 1927; dir., Gold Coast geol. surv., Oct., 1930.
KANAGASUNDARAM, KATHIRVALOOG, B.A. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1912; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1935; office asst., Hambantota kach., Nov., 1935.

KANNANGARA, CHARLES HENRY WILKOKON.—B. 1889; Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1928; office asst., Matara kach., Oct., 1928; extra office asst., Ratnapura kach., Jan., 1934; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Apr., 1934; ag. office asst., Kurunegala kach., Oct., 1934; addnl. office asst., Kurunegala kach., Feb., 1935; office asst., do., June., 1935.

KANNANGARA, EDWARD WILMOT.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1919; attd. to Kandy kach., Aug., 1919; attd. Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1920; ag. addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo, Avissawella, May, 1921; pol. mag., Avissawella, Mar., 1923; ditto, Jaffna, Nov., 1924; ditto, Balapitiya, June, 1926; asst. commr., stamps, Jan., 1929; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Mar., 1930; sec. to min., agr. and lands, Nov., 1931; clk., state coun., and sec. bd. of ministers, June, 1933.

KANTA WALA, MOHAN HARGOVINDAS, M.A., Cantab., barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn;—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1915; att. Anuradhapura kach., Feb., 1915; addtl. pol. mag., Anuradhapura, in add., July, 1915; addtl. pol. mag. and addtl. mun. mag., Colombo, July, 1917; pol. mag., Avissawella, Mar., 1918; office asst. to govt. agt., E. Prov., Oct., 1919; pol. mag., Negombo, Jan., 1921; ditto, Panadura, June, 1923; ditto, Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1924; addnl. dist. judge, Jaffna, June, 1927; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Mar., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Sept., 1929; dist. judge, Negombo, Mar., 1930; pol. mag., Galle, June, 1933; dist. judge, Matara, Nov., 1933; sec., min. for lab., industry and commerce, May, 1934.

KAUFMANN, HILARY PILKINGTON, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1892; on. mil. serv., Aug., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Apr., 1920; attd. to Jaffna kach., June, 1920; ag. office asst. to govt. agt. N. Prov., May, 1921; pol. mag., Balapitiya, Nov., 1922; dep. rubber contr., Nov., 1923; pol. imag., Kandy, Feb., 1925; pol. mag., Colombo, Dec., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Sept., 1929; chmn., munic. coun., and addnl. govt. agt., Kandy, Oct., 1931; asst. to distress relief commr., Jan., 1935; chmn., munic. coun., Colombo, Mar., 1935; ag. commr., local govt., pres., local govt., bd. and sec., min. for local adminstr., Nov., 1935.

KAUFMANN, JOHN ERNEST.—Forester, Umtata, 1900; clk., conservator of forest's office, Umtata, 1901; senr. clk., ditto, 1902; asst. dist. forest offr., Umtata, 1903; transf. to Kokstad, 1912; dist. forest offr., Kokstad, 1912; transf. to Knyssna, 1925; 2nd grade conserv. of forests, eastern conservancy, 1926; conserv. of forests, Transkeian conservancy, 1927; ditto, Midland conservancy, 1929; ditto, Eastern conservancy, 1931; forestry inspr., E. inspectorate, 1932.

KAUNTZE, WILLIAM HENRY, C.M.G., (1937), M.B., B.A., M.D., Ch.B. (Vic. Univ.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Cert. London S.H. and T.M.; (1st place and med.), D.P.H. (Vic. Univ.)—W. African med. staff, Jan., 1914 to Jan., 1919: served with W. African Forces, Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1916; seconded for serv., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1916 to Jan., 1919; ment. in desps.; pathologist and asst. bacteriologist, Kenya, Jan., 1919; dir., laboratory services, Kenya, Apr., 1919; dir., med. and sanry. services, Uganda, Nov., 1932.
KAY, WILLIAM, M.A. (Edin.).—B. 1887; Hong Kong educn. dept., 1913; head, Queen's Coll., 1933; headm., King's Coll., 1934.

KEAST, MAJOR SYDNEY BANKS, O.B.E. (1937), R.E. (R. of O.), M.C. and Bar, French Croix de Guerre.—B. 1884; ed. City of London Sch.; Sudan P.W.D., 1906-13; aspt. suptg. sanry. engr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1913; Cameroons E.F., 1915-16; B.E.F., 1916-19; senr. pub. health engr., 1924; asst., D.P.W., 1926; dep. D.P.W., 1928; D.P.W., 1932; lt.-col. commdg. Gold Coast Defence Force, 1931.
KEATES, CAPT. E. H. O., M.C., F.R.G.S.—B. 1896; ed. St. Peter's Sch., York; on mily. serv. France and Belgium, 1915-18; Germany, 1919; admnstr. off., N. Nigeria, 1921; asst. treas., Nigeria, 1925.

KEATINGE, WILLIAM MATBURY, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1879; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin (Jellett prizeman); B.A., LL.B. (1900); L.L.D. (1903); solr., 1904; admnstr. gen., regir. of deeds, and sec., Wafk comm., Zanzibar, Mar., 1915; joint custodian of enemy property and liquidator of enemy firms, Nov., 1915; off., trustee, 1919; contr., local clearing office, Nov., 1920; regir.-gen., pub. trustee and off., recr., Kenya, June, 1924.

KEEGAN, R. J.—B. 1901; ed. R.M.C., Sandhurst; lieut., Indian Army; served, N.W. Frontier and Iraq; asst. to commdt., pol. (Br. divn.), New Hebrides, Sept., 1927; ag. acct., Jan. to June, 1928 and Mar. to Nov., 1930; ag. dist. agt., Efate and commdt., constab., Nov., 1929 to June, 1930; ag. commdt., constab., Jan.-June, 1933; ag. asst. res. commr., Dec., 1934-June, 1935.

KEEN, KENNETH.—B. 1907; ed. King's Sch., Rochester, Guy's Hosp. and King's Coll., London; cadet, Hong Kong, 1930; asst. sec., Chinese affrs., 1933; asst. head sanry. dept., Nov., 1934; mag., Kewloon, Nov., 1935; dist. offr., N. dist., New Territories, May, 1936.

KEET, MAJOR HUBERT GORDON, D.S.O., M.C. (Reg. Army, R. of O.)—B. 1894; supt. excise, S.S., 1909; served Great War, France and Belgium, 1914-18 (twice ment in desps.); asst. contr., govt. monops., S.S., June 1928; ag. asst. supt., do., 1930; contr., govt. monops., June, 1931; senr. contr., May, 1933; contr., govt. monops., Malacca, May, 1934; dep. contr., rubber, June, 1934; J.P., Malacca, Oct., 1934; supt., excise, Spore, Mar., 1936; supt., cust. and excise, Taiping, Perak, Dec., 1936.

KEET, JOHAN DIRKEMIR MOHR.—Asst. dist. forest offr., working plans, eastern conservancy, Jan., 1908; asst. dist. forest offr., Keiskama Hoek, Jan., 1909; ag. dist. forest offr., Keiskama Hoek, Jan., 1914; ag. dist. forest offr., Knyesa, Nov., 1915; dist. forest offr., Knyesa, Apr., 1917; conserv. of forests, Transvaal, Mar., 1925; ch. forest offr. (ch. divn. of forest management), 1932.

KEEVIL, L. G. M., M.I.R.S.E., A.M.I.R.E.—B. 1896; ed. pvtly.; served with R.A.S.C. (TF) and R.E. Stgs. (TF) in France, German East Africa and Turkey, 1914-20; R.A.R. of O. and capt., Gold Coast Defence Force; engnr. asst., Posts and tels. dept., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; asst. tel. engnr., Gold Coast, 1925; tel. exp., 1929; ag. asst. suptdg. tel. engnr. in 1933 and 1934.

KEIR, ALEXANDER, M.A., U.D.A.—B. 1896; ed. Fordyce Acad. and Aberdeen Univ., M.A. (Aberdeen), 1905; diploma, agr., Camb. Univ., 1913; educ. dept., S.S. and F.M.S., inspr., schls., Selangor, 1911; head, Malay coll., Matang, 1913; inspr., schla., Penang, 1920; repd. British Malaya at Imp. conf., educn., 1923; ag. ch. inspr., English schla., and F.M.S., in 1923, 1924, 1928 and 1932; inspr., schla., Perak, Sept., 1924; del. for Imp. Malaya to Imp. Educn. Conf., June to July, 1927; inspr., schla., Perak, Apr., 1929; sec., Perak state coun., Oct., 1935.

KEITH-ROACH, EDWARD, C.B.E. O.B.E. (1927), Commdr. of Order of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1885; ed. privately; 2nd Lieut., 7th Lancs. Fusil., Aug., 1914; sec., Nov., 1914; major, July, 1915; sec., gen. staff, G.H.Q., R.E.F., July, 1915; bashi, Egyptian Army, May, 1916; intell. Sudan govt., May, 1916; inspr., Port Said, Aug., 1916; Darfur Exped., and first intell. Umkedada, Darfur, Oct., 1916 (Sudan Med. Bar); 2nd cls. mag., 1916; dist. commr., Darfur, Jan., 1917; passed Sudan govt. intell. exam., 1917; 1st cls. mag., June, 1917; cust., enemy property in Palestine, occupied enemy territory adminstr., Dist. 1st asst. sec., Palestine govt., 11th Oct., lent to C.O. by Palestine govt., and ag. pur. 1924 to Feb., 1925; asst. dist. commr., 1st Oct., 1925; dist. dist. commr., Jer., divn., 12th Nov., 1926; publication "Palestine, a Province of the Anglo-Egyptian Sudan," Pageant of Jerusalem," joint editor, "History of Palestine, 1922"; ed., "Gen. Reg. of Govt. of Palestine."

KELLAGHER, GEORGE BANNEMAN—1889; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; cadet, S.S., 1913; attd. reg. councilors' Penang, Dec., 1913; attd. G.P.O., Singapore, 1914; passed cadet, Feb., 1917; supernumerary cls. V., Feb., 1917; on mil. serv., Apr., 2nd Lieut., R.E., Dec., 1917; B.E.F., France, 1918; wounded Oct., 1918; lieut., June, 1919; demob., Feb., 1920; supernumerary, offr., cl. IV., 1920; offr., cls. III., 1925; supernumerary, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; judge, Kedah, July, 1933.

KELLIE, GEORGE GORDON.—Second London postal serv. and apptd. 2nd cls. posts and tels., Somaliland, Jan., 1934; asst. dir., Somaliland, Apr., 1934; June-Oct., 1937; surrv., posts and tels., Nyika Territory, 1931; asst. P.M., Uganda and Tanganyika postal serv., 1932.
KELLY, EDWARD JAMES GLENN—B. 1894; admnstr. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1933; ag. Br. consul, Boma, 1929; admnstr. 1933; ag. res., 1934; admnstr. 1934; sec., S. Prove., 1936.

KELLY, HON. HUGH THOMAS.—St. Michael's Coll., Toronto, and Toronto called to bar, 1886; K.C.; Co. Law Assoc., 1910 and 1911; Lib. pub. lib., bd. for 3 years; a student, Univ., 1906-1912; jud., high court, 1911.
KELLY, JOSEPH ANGELO.—O'Brien Coll., Marino, Dublin; Belgium and Egypt, 1915-19; R.A.F., ment. in desp., 1919.
KENNEDY, JOHN SINCLAIR, M.C., M.A., B.Sc. (Glas.), M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1891; dir. irrig., Ceylon, Oct., 1935.
KENNEDY, NORMAN MALACHY, A.C.A.—B. 1900; acctnt., govt., opium shops, Penang, June, 1927; sr. acctnt. acctnt. and offr. i/c. govt., opium shops, Penang, June, 1932.

KENNEDY, HON. ROBERT, M.A., LL.M. (N.Z.)—B. 1887; ed. Southland Boys' High Schol. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; admitted barrister and sol., 1909; examr. in law to N.Z. Univ.; pres., N.Z. law socy., 1924-25; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1929.

KENNEDY-SKIPTON, GEORGE STACY.—B. 1898; ed. Sherborne and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 1st class schl., 1919; triple sem. mod., 1920-21; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1921; asst. head, army dept., and sec., army bd., 1924; asst. sec., Chinese affrs., 1926; asst. supt., impts. and exports., 1926; asst. treas., 1927; dep. estate duty commr., 1931; senr. ass't. treas., 1933; dist. offr., S., 1935.

KENNIFE, VICTOR, B.E. (Syd.), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E. (Aust.), M.I.M. AND. C.F. E.—B. 1896; ed. Sydney High Schol. and Sydney Univ.; asst. eng., P.W.D., N.S. Wales, 1916; dist. eng., P.W.D., Fiji, 1921; ag. asst. commr. of wks., 1927; ag. commr. of wks., chinm., cent. road bd., Apl. to Dec., 1927; mem., leg. coun., Fiji, May to Dec., 1927; asst. D.P.W., Cyprus, Mar., 1930; ag. D.P.W. on various occasions, 1930-33; asst. D.P.W., Palestine, 1935.

KENT, HENRY TUFFLEY MONKY, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1886; ed. Elstow schl. City and Guilds Coll.; instr., tech. schl., F.M.S., Sept., 1914; ag. timber suptd., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1914; contr., timber supplies, F.M.S. rly., Jan., 1919; ditto, forest dept., S. Stiltsme. and F.M.S., Jan., 1923; sent to report on timber utilization problems in India and Burma, May, 1924; on spl. duty, Madison, U.S.A., Nov. to Dec., 1926; prin., tech. schl., F.M.S., Dec., 1930.
KENT, HON. JAS. M.—K.C., B.A.; M.H.A. Newfoundland, 1904; mem. of exec. coun. and min. of just., 1907; judge, supreme ct., 1916.
KERR, ALLAN FRERE, B.A. (Hons.)—B. 1910; ed. Stowe and Hertford Coll., Oxford; cadet, Gold Coast, 1933; asst. dist. commr., 1934.

KERR, ROBERT ASHFORD.—B.1870; ed. pub. sch., Swellendam; served Matabele war and rebellion and seriously wounded in latter; formerly mem., dep. ch., and ch. of com. prov. coun., Transvaal and pres. senate; mem., Union senate since 1921.

KERR-PEARSE, MAJOR B.A.T., C.M.G. (1929) (formerly Rifle Brigade).—B.1871; A.D.C. to gov. gen. of Australia (Lord Northcote), 1905-07; priv. sec. to gov. of West Australis (Sir G. Strickland), 1909-12; priv. sec. to gov. of Tasmania (Rt. Hon. Sir W. Ellison-Macartney), 1913-15; priv. sec. to gov. gen. of Australia (Rt. Hon. Sir R. Munro Ferguson), 1915-16; mil. sec., 1916.

KERSHAW, ARTHUR EDWARD POMEROY, M.C., A.C.S.M., M.I.M.M.—B. 1890; inspr., mines, F.M.S., 1912; sec. for mil. serv., Sept., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.F.A. (S.R.), Feb., 1918; demob., Feb., 1919; inspr., mines, F.M.S., 1919; ast. warden, mines, Sept., 1921; senr. inspr., mines, B. Gajah, Feb., 1931; ast. warden, mines, Perak, Apr., 1931; warden, mines, Palang, 1934.

KERWIN, HON. PATRICK.—B. 1889; ed. Sarnia Separate Schol., Sarnia Collegiate and Osogood Hall; called to bar of Ontario, 1911; K.C., 1928; mem., Ontario parole bd., 1932; just., sup. ct. of Ontario, Sept., 1932; just., sup. ct., Canada, July, 1935.
KEY, WALTER JOHN SMITH, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1898; ed. Dundee High Sch., Dundee Tech. Coll. and St. Andrews Univ.; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

KIDD, MAJOR GEORGE MONTGOMERY, B.A., Dublin, M.C.—B. 1889; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; seconded for mily. serv. 1915 to 1919; sec. to advr., Kedah, Jan., 1920; asst. agt., food contr., S. Kedah, Feb., 1920; agt., food contr., Kedah, Sept., 1920; dep. supt., census, Kedah, Oct., 1920; sec. sany. bd., Alor Star, Sept., 1922; sec., rubber restn. comtee., Kedah, Oct., 1922; attld., rubber restn. off., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1923; chmn., assess. comtee., F.M.S., Mar., 1925; dist. offr., May, 1927; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Dec., 1929; ag. asst. advr., Muar, Sept., 1932; offr., cls. I B, 1934.

KIDSON, ERNEST O.B.E. (1919), M.A., D.Sc., F.Inst.P., F.R.S. N.Z.—B. 1882; ed. Nelson Coll. and Canterbury Coll., N.Z.; on staff, Australian C'wealth meteorological bureau, 1921-27; asst. dir., 1923-27; dir., meteorological services, N.Z., 1927; N.Z. rep. at Empire and internat. meteor. conferees., 1935.

KIDSON, NORMAN S. B.—B. 1885; served in Connaught Rangers, 1914-19; 2nd asst. mast., Boys' Gram. Sch., Fiji, Mar., 1920; clk. of peace, Rewa, Sept., 1920; ag. dist. commr., Rewa and ag. prov. commr., Naitasiri, Mar., 1921; ag. dist. commr. and prov. commr., Colo East, Apr., 1921; govt. sec., Br. Solomon Is., Prot., Nov., 1922; on sp. duty as judl. commr. for W. Pacific, Apr., 1924; has acted as offr.-in-charge at headqu. on several occasions; ag. res. commr. on various occasions, 1925-1936; offl. mem., advisory coun., 1927; judl. commr. (temp. and provisional), 1928-36.
KILLICK, ANTHONY BERNARD.—B.Sc. Agr. (Lon.), Dip. Agr. (Wye)—B. 1901; agrl. offr., Uganda, 1924; senr. agr. offr., 1930; ag. dep. dir., agr., 1931 and 1934; asst. dir., agr., Trinidad, Apr., 1936.

KILPIN, RALPH.—B. 1887; ed. Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch, S. Africa; clk. of papers, Cape H. of A., 1903; clk. of papers, Union H. of A., 1910; 2nd clk.-asst., 1917; clk.-asst. and acctnt., 1920; admitted sworn translator (Eng. and Dutch), sup. ct., of S. Africa, 1912; assessor apptd. by admstr., Cape exec. comtee. elections and elec. of senators since 1910; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-17; recd. vote of thanks of leg. assembly, S.W. Africa, for preparing standing rules and orders and assisting in inauguration of assembly, 1926; author of "The Old Cape House" and "The Romance of a Colonial Parliament."

KIMBER, GUTHL.—B. 1906; ed. Perse Schl. and Cambridge; nat. sci. schl., Clare Coll., 1924-27; 1st cls. nat. sci. tripus, pt. I, 1926; 2nd cls., pt. II, 1927; B.A., 1927; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 19th Oct., 1928; temp. agt. prin., 18th Mar., 1932; pvt. sec. to Parl'y U.S. of S. for Dom. affrs. (Mr. Malcolm MacDonald), 1st, Feb., 1933 and accompanied him on tour to Australia, Sept.-Dec., 1934; ag. asst. to rep. in C'wealth of Australia of H.M.G. in the U.K., 3rd, Dec. 1934 to 19th Oct., 1935; prin., 1st Jan., 1936.

KINCAID, PERCY FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. Wynberg High Schl.; Cape govt. rly. serv., 1902; transfd., coll. sec.'s dept. (Cape), 1909; ch. elk., dept. of interior, 1925; commr. for immigr. and Asiatic affrs., 1933.

KING, CLEMENT HAMPDEN.—B. 1876; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; ag. 5th cls. elk., police dept., B. Guiana, June, 1893; gradual prom. to prin. elk.; dist. inspr. of polices, 1901; county inspr., Dec., 1908; ag. adjt., July, 1907 to Mar., 1908; Sept., 1908 to Feb., 1909, and from Oct., 1911 to Apr., 1912; adjt. and staff offr., local forces, Oct., 1914 to Aug., 1919; capt., B. Guiana Militia, Oct., 1914; major, Aug., 1919; ag. deputy inspr.-gen. and asst. of Georgetown regt. brig., July to Oct., 1914; ag. deputy inspr.-gen. on various occasions; dep. inspr.-gen. pol. and supt. fire brigade, Aug., 1919; commr. pol., Sierra Leone, June, 1921.

KING, SIR FREDERICK TRUBY, Kt. Bach. (1925), C.M.G. (1917), M.B., B.Sc.—Founder of New Zealand Plunket Soc'y, and famous for his work in connection with infant-life protec.; dir. of divn. of child-welfare, N.Z., health dept., 1921; inspr.-gen., mental hosps., 1925-27.
KING, GEDNEY BIRFHAM.—B. 1882; ent. cir. serv., Barbados, 1901; acct., post office, 1901; ag. col. postr., 1934; ag. audr. gen., 1936; audr.-gen., July, 1936.

KING, GEORGE HOWARD, I.S.O. (1932).—Ag. clk., treas., St. Kitts, 1894; ag. rev. offr., Nevis, 1895; clk., P.O., St. Kitts, 1895; ag. clk. regis.'s office, Nevis, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, Jan., 1898; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1899; rev. offr., Nevis, June, 1905; 1st clk., P.O., and sub-assy. of schla., St. Kitts, 1906; ag. postmstr., St. Kitts, 1914; postmstr., May, 1915.

KING, JOHN EDMUND.—B. 1900; entered C.O., Sept., 1916; served with R.N.V.R. from Sep., Sept. to 19th Dec., 1918; uppd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apr., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 4th July, 1920.
KING, JOHN GEORGE MATTHEWS.—B. 1906; oondle Schl. and S. Eastern Agrl. Coll., Wye, Kent; dipl. agr., C.O. Agrl. schol., Cambridge, 1929-30; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territ., Dec., 1931.
KING, LOUIS HERBERT, M.I.E.E.—B. 1895; chf. engr. and manr., elec. wks., Dar-es-Salam, 1919; chf. elec. engr., P.W.D., Hong Kng, 1923.

KING, PERCY WILLIAM.—B. 1891; ed. Seminary, Quebec and Mungrct Coll., Ireland; passed final exam., Law Soc.; ag. offr., recr. pub. trustee and crown solt., Br. Guiana, in 1920, 1921 and 1922; offr. recr., pub. trustee and crown solt., 1923; ag. asst. col. sec. in 1932; ag. col. sec., 1934 and 1935.

KING, SYDNEY NOEL.—B. 1897; ed. Do Coll. and Bedford Schl.; served in R.F. 1916-1920 (coman., Aug., 1916); cadet, 8 Mar., 1920; passed cadet, Mar., 1922; cls. V., Mar., 1923; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1923; asst. advr., Kukub, July, 1928; ag. 1st Johore Bharu, Sept., 1928; dep. pres. bil. in addn., Oct., 1928; dep. pub. pros. Jv Nov., 1929; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1932; asst. sec. (A) S.S., Aug., 1933; ag. 1st Jv S.S., Mar., 1935.

KING, STUART EDGAR.—B. 1898; ed. chant Taylor's Schl. and St. John's Colford; cadet, S.S., July, 1921; attcd. China Spore, Aug., 1921; offr., cls. V., July, 1922; asst. prot., Chinese and asst. contr. Spore, Apr., 1926; offr., cls. IV, as Chinese, Perak, July, 1928; ditto, Malacca, 1931; offr., cls. III., July, 1933; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, Sept., 1933; dep. immi. Spore, Mar., 1935.

KING, THOS. HENRY.—B. 1883; ed. Gram. Schl.; pol. probationer, Hong Kng, passed in Cantonese, 1907; attcd. to passed in Hindustani, 1907; J.P., 1908; emigr. offr. in addition to pol. duties, 1910; ed in Punjab, 1910; mem., bd. of exams.; ast. supt., pol., 1912; dep. supt., pol.; ag. capt. supt., pol., various occasions, 1929; dir., crim. intell., 1923; dep. inspr., pol., Sept., 1930; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., Mar., 1932 and 1934; inspr.-gen., pol., Apr., just., Gibraltar, 1927; ret., 1931; author of "Some Gold Coast Judgments, 1915-17"; "The Massai Case, 1913"; "The Law Affecting a Company Prospectus"; parly. candidate, Sheffield (Attercliffe), 1909-10; S. Hackney, 1910.

KING-HARMAN, Sir CHARLES ANTHONY, K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1893),—B. 1851; ed. Chelt. Coll.; B.A., of Trin. Coll., Camb., 1872; M.A., 1873; priv. sec. to the gov. of the Bahamas, Nov., 1874, to Nov., 1879; priv. sec. to high comsrr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; ast. comsrr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; ast. to the ch. sec., Cyprus, Mar., 1881; ag. ch. sec. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., June to Sept., 1882; auditor-gen., Barbados, 1883; elected mem. of house of assm., 1884-93; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. coun., 1884, 1885, and 1886; col. sec., Mauritius, 1893; ag. gov., Jan. to Aug., 1894, and Mar. to Sept., 1896; admnstr., St. Lucia, 1897; gov. S. Leone, Oct., 1900; high comsrr., Cyprus, 15th Apr., 1904; represented Mediterranean Colonies at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

KINGSBURY, ALLAN NEAVE, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.D., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Eng.)—Asst. pathologist, Middlesex Hosp. and bacteriologist, Middlesex Hosp., ven. diseases clinic; pathologist, City of Lond. Mat. Hosp.; lieut., R.F.A.; ag. govt. pathologist, Penang, 1923; pathologist, Inst. for Med. Research, F.M.S., 1924; on sp. duty in India and Ceylon, 1924; ag. bacteriologist, Inst. for Med. Research and ag. dir., govt. laboratories, F.M.S., 1925; professor of bacteriology, King Ed. VII. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, 1926; bacteriologist, Inst. Med. Research, F.M.S., Apr., 1927; ag. dir., ditto, Aug., 1927; dir., ditto, Oct., 1927; awarded N. Persian Forces memorial med. for research, Oct., 1927; del. L. of N. health organization laboratory confce., Copenhagen, May 1928; rep. C.O. at Internat. Cancer Confce., London, July, 1928; rep. F.M.S. at Leonard Wood memorial leprosy confce., Manila, Dec., 1930; author of many medical and scientific publications.
KINLOCH, JOHN BAXTER.—B. 1906; ed. Kirkcaldy High Schl. and Edin. and Oxford Univ.; B.Sc., forestry, Edin., 1927; asst. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1927.

KINNEAR, GEORGE, M.D. (Univ. of Manitoba), M.V.P.S. (Sask.), Certif. L.S.T.M. and H.—B.1894; ed. Regina Collegiate Inst., 1st cls. certif., Regina Normal Schl., 1912; prin., Earl Grey and Govan. Pub. Schls. (Sask.), 1913-17; Leeward Is. med. serv., 1926; D.M.O., dist. D., Dominica; M.O., Bahama gen. hosp., 1930; ag. M.O. and bacteriologist, Bahamas gen. hosp., 1931; ag. ch. med. offr., 1932 and 1935-36; S.M.O., Falkland Is., 1937.
KINSMAN, CYRIL WILLIAM, O.B.E. (1936)—B.1887; offl. sec. to gov., Victoria and cks., exec. coun., 1930.
KIPPING, RUPERT HARRY, M.B., Ch.B. (Leeds).—B. 1904; late jun. house surg., Darlington Genl. Hosp.; locum house surg., Sheffield Roy. Hosp.; med. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1929.
KIRBY, A. C.—Ast. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; 2nd grade res., Nyasaland, Jan., 1925; 1st grade res., Mar., 1925; ag. prov. comsrr., June, 1933.

KIRBY, ARTHUR FRANK, A.M. Inst.T.—B., 1899; ed. Sir William Borlase's Schlr., Marlown and London Univ. (Schl. of Economics); served European War (France), 1918-19; office ast., Gold Coast rly., 1928; traffic man., 1936.

KIRK, JAMES BALFOUR.—B. 1893; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edin Univ., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Oxford), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), F.Z.S. (Lond. and Scot.), Vans Dunlop scholarship bot. and zool., Edin. Univ., 1911, certif. (with distinc.), L.S.T.M., 1922; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914-16; lieut., R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1917; capt., 1918; asst. pol. offr., Hai., 1918-19; civ. surg., Arabistan, 1919; asst. M.O., Baghdad, 1919-20; bacteriologist, R. Inst. Pub. Health, 1920-21; demonstr., L.S.T.M., 1921; M.O.H., Mauritius, 1922; dir., med. and health dept., 1927; author of "Pub. Health Practice in the Tropics" (1931), "Hints on Equipment and Health for intending Residents in the Tropics," and several articles on med. and hygienic subjects.
KIRKBRIDE, ALAN LOGAN, O.B.E.—B. 1901; admstv. offr., Palestine, 1921; seconded to Trans-Jordan as 2nd asst. ch. Br. rep., 1921; finan. advr., 1928; asst. sec. on reversion to Palestine, 1934.

KIRKBRIDE, ALEC SRATH, O.B.E. (1932), M.C.—B. 1897; mily. serv., Egypt, Palestine, Hejaz, Trans-Jordan, 1916-21; Br. rep., Es Salt, Apr., 1921; junr. asst. sec., Palestine, Apr., 1922; asst. sec., Nov., 1926; clk. to exec. and advy. couns., Nov., 1924-Nov., 1927; asst. Br. rep., Trans-Jordan, Nov., 1927.

KIRKHAM, G. H.—B. 1881; S. African constab., 1901; served S. African War, 1901-02 (wounded); Transvaal pol., 1908; adjt., 4th S. African Mounted Riflemen, 1914; S. African Rebellion, 1914; capt., 1st S. African Infy. (Overseas Brig.) 1916-18; German W. Africa campaign, 1916-17 (twice wounded); M.C., Apr., 1917; King's pol. med.; dep. commnr., pol. and prisons, Tanganyika Territory, 1918; commnr., 1929.

KIRKPATRICK, THOMAS WINFRID, M.A. (Cantab.), Dipl. Agr. (Cantab.), F.E.S.—B. 1896; ed. Marlborough and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; mily. serv., 1915-19; entomologist, miny. of agr., Egypt, 1921; do., Kenya Colony, 1925; entomologist, Welcome Trop. Research Labs., Sudan, 1928; entomologist, E. African agrl. research stn., Amari, Tanganyika Territory, 1931.
KIRWAN, SIR JOHN WATERS, Kt. Bach. (1930).—B. 1869; M.L.C., S. Prov., W. Australia since 1908; comm. of comtees., 1923-26; pres., leg. coun., 1926.

KITCHING, A. E.—Ed. Oundle Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. commnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1919; dist. offr., June, 1926; ag. asst. sec., native affrs., Apr., 1929; asst. sec., native affrs., 1930; ag. sec., native affairs, Apr. to Dec., 1931; dep. prov. commnr., Apr., 1932; prov. commnr., 1934.

KITCHING, GEOFFREY CHARLES, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1892; ed. Oundle Schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; on mily. serv., 1914-19; asst. polit. offr., Iraq, 1920; miny. of int., Iraq, 1920-30; dep. dir., development, Palestine, 1931; polit. secy., St. Helena, 1932.

KITCHING, THOMAS, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1890; ed. Lancaster and Peterhouse, Camb.; survey prob., F.M.S., May, 1913; asst. supt., rev. surveys, N. Sembilan, 1919; Perak, 1921; Selangor, 1924; supt., survey dept., F.M.S. and S.S., 1930; seconded to Trengganu as supt., surveys, 1928-31; officy. asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Dec., 1931 and Sept., 1932; ag. dep. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., June and Dec., 1932; supt., surveys, Johore, Mar., 1933; do., Kedah, Mar., 1935.

KITTERMMASTER, SIR HAROLD K.C.M.G. (1936), K.B.E. (1928), C.M.G. O.B.E. (1918).—Ed. Shrewsbury and Oriel, Oxford; Transvaal educn. dept., 1920-26; dist. commnr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1926; commnr., 1915; offr. in charge, N.F.D.; secy. to admstrn., Somaliland, Aug., 1917; and commdr-in-chief, Somaliland Prov. gov., and commdr-in-ch., Br. Hodumdo, do., Nyasaland Prov., 1934.

KNIBBS, STANLEY GEORGE (F.R.G.S.—B. 1886; ed. Sydney Gran. New South Wales; dipl. in all subjects, Hurbury Agrl. Coll., 1908; land survr., Fland survr., Victoria and N.S.W., 1912; land survr., Br. Solomon Is. Prov., 1913; pub. wks. in admin., 1918; ag. dist. offr., lands during 1919; regar. of land titles, 1919; dep. commnr. for W. Pacific, 1919; natives in lands commnr., 1920 and 1921; commnr. during 1920; commnr. of currency in 1920-24; qualified as civ. engnr. to three municipalities of N.S.W., 1923; commnr. and crown survr., Br. Solomon Is. Prov., chmn., mining bd., 1927; attended Imp. Conf. and Internat. Geog. Conf., 1927.
KNIGHT, CLIFFORD.—B. 1909; ed. Bras Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Inner Temp., req. mag., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1935.

KNIGHT, JOHN MARCUS, M.B.E. (1878-1894; ed. Grenada Grammar Sch.; treasy., Grenada, 1912; 2nd clk., G.P.O. served in the Great War, 1914-19; Egypt, Palestine, 1917; twice ment. in desp., 1919; cust. offr., Grenada, 1919; 2nd clk., secy. and clk., leg. coun., 1921; ch. clk., gov't., Windward Is. and clk., ex. coun., Grenada; sec. to govt. of Windward Is., 1928.

KNIGHT, WALTER AUGUSTUS.—G.P.O., Grenada, May, 1903; 2nd clk., June, 1904; clk., supt. of wks., Jan., 1906; pol. mag., S. Dist., May, 1907; req. of road survr., W. Dist., Jan., 1909; coroner, St. Mark's parish, Jan., 1913; req. N. Dist., Dominica, Dec., 1914; comm., (i) iacou, Dec., 1937.

KNIGHT-BRUCE, G. K.—B. 1891; ed. P. Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; B.A.; cls. hona., schl. of jurisprudence, 1918; dist. commnr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ment. desp., 1919; ag. dist. commnr., 1920; req. mag., Mombasa, 1923; req. mag., Uganda, res. mag., Zanzibar, 1925; ag. judge, Zanzibar, 1926; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1927; judge, Zanzibar, for various periods, 1927-30; atty.-gen., 1930 and 1933; puisne judge, Tanganyika Territory, 1936; edited Zanzibar Law Rev. Vol. IV.

KNOWLES, CHARLES HENRY, B.Sc. (Lond.), B. 1878; ed. Burton-on-Trent gram. sch., Mason Univ. Coll., Birmingham; req. mag. agric. schl., St. Vincent, Jan., 1902; req. agric., Fiji, 1905; M.L.C., 1912; chmn. experiments comtee., 1916; chmn. food comtee., 1917; ag. govt. entomologist (over 1917 and 1919-21; J.P., 1917; M.E.C., June, 1920; prov. supt., agric., G. Coast, 1921; dep. dir., agr., Aug., 1924; dir., Apr., 1924.
KNOWLES, JOHN WILLIAM FERGUSON, M.B.E. (1934).—B. 1877; joined Trincom. surv., 1898; warden, counties Nanu and June, 1922; warden, St. Andrew and S.

OLLER, Thurstan William, A.S.A.A., C.A., ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; clk., O.F.S. serv., July, 1900; senr. inspr., Union audit Feb., 1929; chf. inspr., rev., do., Sept.; provi. audr., Cape Prov., Dec., 1932.

OTZE, HON. SIR JOHN GILBERT, Kt., (1917), K.C., LL.B. (LOND.).—B. 1849; South African Coll. and Land Univ.; to the bar, Inner Tem., 1874; sole judge high court of the Transvaal, 1877 to 1880; one of the commrs. under the convention Pretoria to settle claims for losses and injuries suffered during the Boer War of 1880-81; justice of the Transvaal, 1881-1896; chmn. of examrs. in literature and science, Transvaal, 1890-1898; recd. the Portuguese honour of Grand Cross of the Order of the Conception, 1896; atty.-gen. and member of exec. legis. couns., S. Rhodesia, 1900; ag. admtr. Rhodesia, 1902; judge of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope, 1903, and judge pres., Eastern dists. et al.; chmn. of the coun. of Rhodes Univ. Coll.; nr. in law and jurisprudence to the Univ. of G. H., 1903-1908; puisne judge, C. of G.H., vl. Div., 1st Apr., 1913; judge, president, 14th c., 1920; judge of appeals, appellate divn., 1922; ret., 1932.

OTZE, PETER CANZIUS, B.A. (Cape), Dip. (Oxon.).—Ast. forest offr., King William's Town, 1911; ditto, Port Elizabeth, 1912; ditto, Port Elizabeth, 1914; forest offr., Storms River, 1918; to, Eshowe, 1920; ditto, Kokstad, 1927; ditto, Bloemfontein, 1929; conservator, forests, Midrand conservancy, 1931; forestry inspr., Millrand pectorate, 1932.

OTZE, SIR ROBT. NELSON, KT. (BACH.), L.—B. 1870; ed. at S. Africa Coll., and R. Ill. of Mines, Clausthal, Germany; gold med., Ill. Coll. and of Chem. Met. and Mining Soc. of Ill.; ast. engrnr. and consulting engrnr. to Transvaal Gold Fields Ltd., and subsidiary coys., 1896-97; govt. mining engrnr., Transvaal, Jan., 1908; to, Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn., Univ. Witwatersrand; ret., Nov., 1928.

TRAUSE, FREDERICK EDWARD TRAUGOTT.—1868; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, Victoria Ill., Stellenbosch, Amsterdam, London, Cambridge Univ.; B.A. (Hons.), Cape, 1888; LL.D., Amsterdam, 1893; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1893; LL.D., Cape, 1905; advoc., sup. S. Africa and high ct., Rhodesia; state executor and ag. atty.-gen., Witwatersrand, 1906-1900; sp. commdt. and mil. govt., Witwatersrand, 1900; chmn., mining regs. coman., 1907-9; mem., Johannesburg town coun., 1905-9; L.A., Vrededorp, 1907-10; chmn., hosp. indry coman., Johannesburg; mem., prop. representation coman.; mem., Witwatersrand cent. sh. bd.; K.C. (Union of S. Africa), 1912; ag. dge., Transvaal prov. divn., 1914; judge, sup. Transvaal prov. divn., Apr., 1923; judge-pres., F.S. prov. divn., 1933.

KREFT, HERMANN HEINRICH GERHARD, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Normal Coll., Cape Town and S.A. Coll., Capetown; inspr., schls., Cape Town, 1916; dir., educn., S.W. Africa, 1923; tech. advr., Transvaal educn. dept., 1935; sec., transvaal educn. dept., 1936.
KROGH, ANDRE LOUIS, M.D., Ch.B. (Edin.).—B. 1887; govt. med. offr., Dec., 1920; dist. med. officer, Tobago, 1922.

KRUGER, JOHANNES JACOB.—B. 1888; on active serv., E. Africa and overseas, 1915-19; govt. printer, 1927; Union R. of O. with rank of major; mem., exec. comtee., nat. industrial coun. of the printing and newspaper industry of S. Africa.

KUIT, ALBERT.—S. Africa Repub. govt. and Z.A.S.M. rly. serv. till Br. annexation; served Jameson raid and Boer war on Boer side; finan. and estate agt., Amersfoort (T'vaal) town coun., J.P. and practically all public bodies, 1902-25; rly. comanr. since 1925; pub. debt. comanr. since 1928; a comanr. of oaths for Union.

KUSCHKE, GEORG ADOLF CARL, (M.A.).—B. 1892; ed. Normal Coll., Johannesburg, Transvaal Univ. Coll. and Univ. of S. Africa (B.A.), Stellenbosch Univ. (M.A.); teacher, Transvaal, 1912; prin., Transvaal, 1921; inspr., schls., Transvaal, 1927; tech. advr., Transvaal educn. dept., 1933; dir., educn., Transvaal, 1934.

KUSCHKE, HERMANN F. G., B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Johannesburg and Pretoria; head, normal sec., Stofoberg Gedenk Schu., 1916-24; organising inspr., native educn., O.F.S., 1924; ch. inspr., native educn., O.F.S., 1926.

LABORDE, EDWARD DANIEL, C.B.E. (1922), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1863; 1st clk., audit office, Tobago, Jan., 1882; dep. regisr., ag. confidential clk. to administrator, and clk. of couns., June, 1882; 2nd clk., administrator's office, St. Lucia, Feb., 1883; ch. clk., Sept., 1883; ag. 2nd clk., gov.in-chief's office, Barbados, 1884; ch. clk. to gov., Windward Is., Grenada, 1885; priv. sec. to Gov. Sendall, 1886; ch. of pol. and excise, St. Vincent, 1889; inspr. of prisons, 1889; supervisor of cust. and port offr., 1897; in charge of relief work after hurricane of 1898; specially mentioned in despatches and thanked by S. of S. for services; chmn. town bd., 1899-1901; mem. ex. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to May, 1901; ch. of pol. and inspr. of prisons, St. Lucia, 1902; has served on several comens. of enquiry in different colonies; ag. admtr., St. Vincent, June to Dec., 1904; ag. admtr., and col. sec., St. Lucia, July to Nov., 1905; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1906, to Jan., 1907; ag. administrator and col. sec., May to Nov., 1907; treas., St. Lucia, 1912; col. treas., Grenada, 1915; ag. col. sec., Grenada, for various periods, 1916-20; admtrd., govt., Sept., Oct., 1915, and Sept. to Oct., 1920.

LACEY, MAJOR ALFRED TRAVERS, O.B.E. (1936).—B. 1892; ed. King Edward VI Gram. Schl., Norwich, and King's Coll., Cambridge; class. tripods; hons. degree, M.A.; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; dir., educn., Nyasa Land Prot., 1930.

LAFERLA, CAPT. ALBERT V., O.B.E. (1935), LL.D.—B. 1887; ed. Flores's Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1903; grad. in law, 1910; lieut., King's Own Malta Regt. of Militia, 1903; capt., 1911; demob., 1920; dir., elem. schl., Malta, Aug., 1920; dir., educn., Nov., 1933.

LA FONTAINE, SYDNEY HUBERT, O.B.E. (1930), D.S.O., M.C. (1916).—Ast. dist. comanr., E.A.P., 1910; district comanr., Kenya Oct., 1918; 2nd grade prov. comanr., 1931; ag. ch. native comanr., 1933; prov. comanr., grade I, 1934.

LAFLECHE, Lieut.-Col. LEO RICHER, D.S.O.—B. 1888; lieut., 1914; lieut.-col., 1918; examr., bd. of commerce, 1919; mem., purchasing coman., Canada, 1920; mem., pension appl. ct., 1930; dep. min., national defence (Can.), 1932.

LAING, GORDON DACOMB, B.Sc., M.B. Ch.B. (St. Andrews), D.P.H. (Johannesburg)—B. 1899; ed. Merchiston Schl., Pietermaritzburg, Madras Coll., St. Andrews, and St. Andrews Univ.; senr. lec. in anatomy, Univ. of Witwatersrand, 1924; asst. pathologist, S. African Inst. for Med. Research, 1927; asst. health offr., for Union of S. Africa, 1928; med. offr., health, Pretoria munic., Nov., 1929; asst. med. offr., health, Johannesburg, 1934; maj., sp. R. of O., Union Defence Force; published some papers on anatomical and anthropological subjects in the Journal of the S. African Assocn. for the Advancement of Science.

LAING, MALCOLM BUCHANAN.—B. 1890; ed. Forest Schil., Essex; asst. commissary, B. Guiana, Oct., 1909; seconded as ch. clk., immigrn. dept., Apr., 1913; mem., govt. standing bd. of examrs. in Hindi, etc., Nov., 1913; immigrn. agt., Aug., 1920; capt. B. Guiana Arty. (Militia), Jan., 1921; senr. clk., col. sec's office, Aug., 1925; clk. to exec. coun. and leg coun., July, 1928; pte. sec. to gov., Apr., 1929 to June, 1930; dist. comsrr., Jan., 1932; temp. M.L.C., June, 1933; M.L.C., Oct., 1935.

LAKE, FREDERICK JAMES.—B. 1894; 2nd divn. clk., G.P.O., 1912; B.E.F., France, 1915; K.A.R., German E. Africa, 1917; exec. offr., G.P.O., 1920; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika, 1921; asst. dist. offr., 1923; dist. offr., 1932.

LAKE, LIEUT.-COL., MORICE CHALLONER, C.M.G. (1936)—B. 1885; ed. Wellington Coll. and Sandhurst; comsmd., unattd. list (attd. Ist Battn. Somerset Light Infy.), Jan., 1904; T.A., 1905; regt. duty, 1905-15; Aden, 1913; served, Aden Field Force, 1915-19; offr. in ch., signals, 1915-16; offr. commdg. signal schl., Lucknow, 1916-17; raised and commanded Aden (Arab) Labour Corps, 1917-18; raised and commanded Ist Yemen Infy., 1918-25; sp. duty, W.O. revising mly. rept., Aden Prot., 1926-27; reverted, mly. duty, India, 1927-28; adviser to C.O. and Air Miny. on raising levies, etc., in Aden, 1928; polit. offr., Aden, Nov., 1928; ag. 2nd asst. res. and Prot. sec., July to Nov., 1930; polit. sec'y, May, 1934; ag. ch. comsrr., Apr.-Sept., 1935 and May-Oct., 1936.
LAMACQ, FRANK CONSTANT.—B. 1903; apptd. cler. offr., London telephone service, May, 1921; transf'd to C.O., July, 1923.

LAMBERT, C. E.—B. 1900; on mil. serv., May, 1918 to Nov., 1919; temp. clk., cust., 1919 to 1920; R.I.C., 1920 to 1921; temp. clk., Min. of Lab., Apr., 1921 to July, 1923; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 9th July, 1923; seconded as reg'ar, on staff of high comsrr. in Canada for H.M.'s govt. in Great Britain, 1928-32; returned to C.O., 12th Sept., 1932; asst. prin., 1st Feb., 1937.

LAMBERT, EDGAR BUCHANAN, F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. Dulwich Coll., R. Mily. Coll., Sandhurst and Crystal Palace Engng. Sch.; 2nd lieut., The King's (Liverpool) Regt., 1909-11; land surv., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1913; 1st cls. do., 1920; asst. supt., surveys, 1934.
LAMBERT, WILLIAM OSBORNE, M.I.N.A., M.I.Mar.E.—B. 1887; asst. govt. marine survr., Hong Kong, 1917; Hong Kong del. of safety of life at sea confee., Simla, 1931.

LAMBORN, WILLIAM ALFRED STEDWELL, O.B.E. (1930), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1877; ed. Oxford Preparatory Schil., King's Schil., Canterbury, and Middlesex Hosp.; asst. med. offr., St. Mary's Infirmary, London, 1899 to 1905; asst. med. offr., British Guiana, 1905; asst. med. offr., Oxford County Asylum, 1906; med. offr., Nigeria Bitumen Co., 1908; govt. entomologist, S. Nigeria, 1913; entomologist, Imp. Bureau, 1914; temp. med. offr., Tanganyika, 1917; med. offr., Nyasaland, 1919; asst. med. entomologist, F.M.S., 1920; med. entomt., Nyasaland, 1921.
LAMBOURNE, NELSON THOMAS, M.A. N.Z.—B. 1877; ed. Mangere Schil. and Auckland Coll.; many years' teaching ser., N.Z. misc. schls.; ch. impr., primary schls., 1931; eduen., 1933.

LAMINGTON, LORD (2nd Baron, crat.)—SIR CHARLES WALLACE ALEXANDER NEIL COCHRANE-BAILLIE, G.C.I.E. (1903), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G. (1895).—Only son of late Sir Charles, born, 1860; dep. lieut. Lanarkshire; lieut. Lanarkshire yeomanry, 1883; M.P. for N. St. Patrick, 1886-90; gov. Queensland, 1895-1901; g. o. Bombay, 1903-7.

LAMONT, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1888; prtn., Schil. of Agr., Grootfontein, Middelburg (Cape), dep. of agr., South Africa; prtn. of agr., Elsenburg, Mulder's Vlei, 1920; ass't divn. of agr. educn. and extension, 1925; divn. of economics and markets, 1929; agr. educn. comsrr., 1921 and fruit export comttee, 1924; chmn., wine expt. comsrr., ag. under-seo. for agr., 1927; seconded for duty, expt. of Union tobacco, 1928; chmn. comsrr., 1931; seconded for sp. duty as gen. co-op. maize agency, 1932; under-seo. agr. sec., dept. of commerce and industries, 1933.

LANCLOT, CHARLES, K.C.—B. 1848; ed. Mary's Coll., Montreal, and Laval Univ.; tutor; called to the bar 1888; K.C., 1890; law offr., Quebec, 1891; dep. atty.-gen., Quebec, 1905; mem. of coman. for revision of code of proceed., 1893-97; coman. for revision of code of Quebec, 1909-10; published "Traité théorique et pratique du droit criminel"; "Manuel de paix"; "Annotated Criminal Code."

LANE, CHARLTON ADELBERT, C.G.T.—B. 1890; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Hons.), 1911; asst. dist. comsrr., E.A., 1913; ag. dist. comsrr., 1918; attd., secret., 1918; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1919; senr. asst., 1920; dist. comsrr., Zanzibar, 1923; mag. in Tanganyika Territory, 1925; res. mag., Kericho, 1929; ag. judge, supt., ct., 1935.

LANG, EARNEST PHILIP HENRY.—B. 1854; Monmouth; admitted solr., sup. ct., Eng., 1903; pte. prac., Hong Kong, 1903-19; comttee., Aug., 1912 to June, 1913; spe., from June, 1913 to 1922; M.L.C., 1924-31.
LANG, HON. JOHN THOMAS.—M.L.A., South Wales; treas. in Storey and Dooley premier, N.S.W., 1925-27 and 1930-32.

LANGLEY, CARLETON GEORGE, K.C.—B. 1885; ed. City of London Schil.; call. bar, Middle Temple, 1913; on mil'y. serv. in France and Ireland, 1914-21; pte. prac., Lincolns Inn and Bahama Is., 1921-30; pte. prac., Leeward Islands, 1931; comsrr., J.P.; ag. puisne judge, 1931-3; dual app., gen. and ag. col. sec., 1934-36; admtr. App., 1936-38; puisne judge, B. Guiana, 1936.
LANGSTONE, HON. FRANK.—B. 1881; N.Z. H. of R., 1922-25 and since 1928; pte. lands and comsrr. of state forests, 1935.

LANGWORTHY, Henry Bruce.—B. 1896; l. probr., F.M.S., July, 1920; ast. commr., July, 1923; 1st ast. commr., Selangor, July, 1925; adjt., Fed. hdqrs., Oct., 1925 and Feb., 1930; supt., convict estab., Taiping and spr. prisons, F.M.S., Sept., 1926; ch. pol. offr., Pahat, Oct. 1931; ast. supt., pol. S.S., Dec., 1933; dy. commr., pol., F.M.S., Feb., 1935; dy. commr., pol. and oficfg. commr., pol., Johore, July, 1935.

LANIER, Paul Edouard Maxim, M.D., R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Ed. Middlesex Hosp., London, Paris and Lausanne; ast. med. offr., Seychelles and visiting mag., outlying islands, 1924; signed; re-ent. col. serv., as res. surg., Seychelles and visiting mag., outlying islands, 1929; n. med. offr., 1935.

LANKTREE, Charles Joseph Dane, Barrister-at-Law, Kings Inn, Dublin.—B. 1895; on mil. rv., 1914-19; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1921; attd. Colombo kach., Nov., 1921; office st. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Mar., 1924; lstvte. sec. to dir. of med. and sanitary serv., Oct., 1925; addnl. asts. dir., educn., Nov., 1926; lstvte. sec. to dir., elec. undertakings, July, 1928; asts. stttm. offr., Jan., 1932; ag. man., elec. undertakings, Apr., 1933; addnl. man., do., July, 1934; ast. govt. agt., Matara, Sept., 1934.
