LANSDOWN, Charles William Henry, I.A., LL.B.—Cllk., C.G.R., Mar., 1889; clk., S.O., Capetown, Oct., 1893; atty.-gen.'s off., Nov., 1894; mag.'s clk., Aliwal N., Feb., 1898; te. sec. to atty.-gen., Aug., 1898; ag. chf. clk. o sol.-gen., Grahamstown, May, 1901; ag. ast. mag., Port Elizabeth, Sept., 1901; ch. clk. and te. sec. to atty.-gen., Oct., 1901; addnl. legal dvr., July, 1907; prof. asts., atty-gen.'s off., apetown, Apr., 1912; law advr., dep. of justice, Union of S. Africa, Aug., 1918; atty-gen., Cape prov., Dec., 1926; senr. law adviser, 1927; puisne judge, Natal prov. divn., Oct., 1931.

LAPOINTE, Hon. Ernest, B.A., LL.B., C.C., LL.D. (Quebec East).—B. 1876; ed. Rimouski Coll. and Laval Univ., Quebec; called to bar, 1898; K.C., 1908; Canadian del. to L. of N., Geneva, 1922; rep. Canada with Rt. Hon. Mackenzie King at Imp. Confce., 1926; negotiated and signed, with Hon. Mr. Fielding, treaty with France, 1922; negotiated and signed, on behalf of Canada, treaty with U.S.A. regarding Pacific fisheries; signed four other treaties with U.S.; leader of govt. in H. of C. session of 1926; Can. plenipotentiary at Naval confce., Geneva, June-July, 1927; head of Can. deleg., to Imp. Confce., on Imp. and Dominion legis., 1929; barrister-at-law; elec. by accl. to H. of C. Canada, 1944; re-el. at g.e., 1904, 1908, 1911 and 1917 for same const.; resigned, 1919 to contest Quebec East; elec., 1919; re-el., g.e., 1921; min. of marine and fisheries, King cabinet, 1921; min. of just., Jan., 1924, and held position in 2nd King adminstr.; re-el. at g.e., 1925, 1926 and 1930; resigned portfolio with liberal admn., Aug., 1930; re-el. at g.e., 1935; min. of just., Oct., 1935.

LARTER, Leslie N. H., B.Sc., A.R.C.S., A.I.C.T.A.—Ed. Imp. Coll. (Lond.), schol. and medallist, graduated (1st cls. honors), 1929; col. agrl. schol., Fitzwilliam House, Camb., and Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1930-32; geneticist, Jamaica, Nov., 1932.

LASCELLES, Sir Alfred George, Kt., Bach. (1913), B.A.—Univ. Coll., Oxon; 2nd class mod. hist.; b. 1857; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1885; acted on several occasions as pres. of dist. cts. in Cyprus and as puisne judge: pres. of dist. cts., Papho, 1892; ditto, Larnaca, 1893; ag. puisne judge, June, 1893; ag. Queen's advoc., Feb., 1896; pres. dist. cts., Nicosia, 1896; Queen's advoc., 1898; atty.-gen. of Ceylon, 1902; ag. ch. just., Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., Oct., 1906; chief just., May, 1911; retired, 1914.

LATCHFORD, Hon. Francis Robert, B.A., K.C.—B. 1856; ed. Ottawa Univ.; called to the bar, 1886; K.C., 1898; elec. to Ontario legis., 1899; re-elect., 1902; min. of pub. wks., Ontario, 1899; atty.-gen., 1904-5; judge, chancery divn., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1908; ch. just., 2nd divn. ct., sup. ct., 1923.

LATHAM, Charles Kingsley, B.Sc. (Agr.).—B. 1901; ed. S. African Coll. Schol., Cape Town; B.Sc. (agric.) Univ. of S.A., 1922; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1924; senr. agrl. offr., 1930.
LATHAM, D. V., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., R.A.O. (Dub.)—B. 1897; war serv., R.N.V.R., 1917-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925.

LATHAM, Rt. Hon. Sir John Greig, P.C., G.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G., K.C., M.A., LL.B., M.P.—B. 1877; ed. State Schls., Scotch Coll., Melbourne and Melbourne Univ., called to bar, 1904; formerly lecturer, Melbourne Univ.; during European War was lient.-commdr., Intelligence Branch; attended, with min. for navy, Imp. Confce., 1918; assist. sec., Br. Empire delegation, Peace Confce., Paris, 1919; Br. sec. to inter-allied comm. on Czechoslovak affrs. at confce.; M.H.R. (C'wealth) for Kooyong (Vic.); atty.-gen., C. of A., Dec., 1925; a rep. of C'wealth at Imp. Confce., 1926; leader of opposition H. of R., 1929; dep. leader, 1930; atty.-gen. and min. of external affrs. and industry in Lyons' ministry, 1932; leader, goodwill mission to Japan, 1934; resigned from parl't., 1934; ch. just., high ct., 1935.
LAURENCE, C. G. W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 30th Nov., 1936.
LAURIE, William, M.Inst. M., M.Cy.E.—B. 1890; asts. engnr., Kedah, Apr., 1920; exec. engnr., F.M.S., Apr., 1929; do., Kedah, June, 1933.

LAVILLE, Louis Victor Joseph.—B. 1888; ed. at Bedford Schol., and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb. (schol.), 2nd cls. class. and hist. tripos, B.A., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; cadet, S. Sttltns., Dec., 1912; attd. to col. sec'y's office, Jan., 1913; passed cadet; supv. officer, cls. V., Jan., 1916; lent to govt. of Kelantan, June, 1917; offg. dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, Jan., 1918; supv. offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1919; offg. dist. offr., Bukit Mentajam, July, 1920; offg. collr., income tax, Singapore, Apr., 1922; mag. and asts. regsr., sup. ct., Seremban, Dec., 1923; D.P.P., Perak, July, 1925; sheriff and dep. regsr., sup. ct., S'pore, Nov., 1928; 1st mag. and pres. town hd., Johore Bahru, Mar. and Oct., 1929; cls. II., Dec., 1929; offg. regsr., sup. ct., Penang, Aug., 1931; ag. legal advr. and judl. commr., Kelantan, June, 1932; offr., cls. IB., Nov., 1933; dist. judge, S'pore, Feb., 1935; legal advr., Kedah, May, 1936.
LAW, Alfred Noel, M.C.—B. 1895; mil. ser., 1915-18; inspr., dist. adminstr., Palestine, 1920; ag. ast. dist. commr. on various occasions, 1925-30, and once as dist. commr.; ast. sec., 1932.

LAW, Charles Ewan.—B. 1884; ed. High Schol., Croydon and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; Campbell-Foster prizeman, criminal law, Middle Temple, 1903; called to bar, 1905; Burma bar, 1905; staff capt. (tempy. Brit. serv.), Burma divn., Mar., 1917; Volunteer Long Service med. (India), Feb., 1919; maj. (temp. Ind. army res., of offrs.) comdg. 15th Mech. Transport Coy., July, 1919; released from serv., rank capt., I.A.R.O., Feb., 1920; crown counsel, Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. solr.-gen., Mar., 1923 to Oct., 1924; mem., legis. coun., May, 1923 to Oct., 1924; ag. atty.-gen. and mem., exec. coun., Apr., 1924; Swahili (L.S.), Sept., 1923; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1925; ag. judge, Kingston ct., on various occasions, 1925 to 1927; judge, Kingston ct., Feb., 1927; ag. puisne judge, on various occasions 1925 and 1927 to 1929; mem., standing comm., of res. mags., June, 1926; mem., comm. of enquiry into admstr. of prisons, Sept., 1926; puisne judge, Uganda, Oct., 1930; ag. ch. just., from May, 1932 to Mar., 1933; chan. of diocese, Jan., 1933; mem., comm. of enquiry into admstr. of just. in crim. matters in E. Africa, Jan., 1933; ch. just., Zanzibar, Mar., 1934; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa; chmn., agrl. indebtedness comm., Aug., 1934.

LAW, Y. P., B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1888 asst. mast., educ. dept., Hong Kong, 1915; ag. inspr. of vernac. schls., 1915 and 1917; mem., bd. of exams., 1919-22; inspr., vernac. schls., 1920 and 1921; hon. sec., bd. of educ., in addn., 1920-22; inspr., vernac. schls., New Territories, 1922; re-apptd. mem., bd. of exams., 1924; ag. sen. inspr., vernac. schls. for urban districts in admin., 1925; inspr., vernac. schls., 1927.

LAWLEY, HON. SIR ARTHUR, G.C.I.E. (1911); G.C.I.E. (1906); K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1860; sec. to adminstr., S. Rhodesia, May, 1896; dep. adminstr., Matabeleland, Nov., 1896; adminstr., Mashonaland, Dec., 1898; ag. adminstr., Mashonaland, July, 1899; gov. of W. Aust., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., Transvaal, 15th Aug., 1902; ag. gov. of Transvaal and O.R.C. and high commnr., 1903; chmn. of ex. coun., pres. of leg. coun., mem. I.C.C.; gov. of Madras, 1905.
LAWLOR, JOHN HENRY, B.A.—B. 1880; asst. mast., Pietermaritzburg Coll., Feb., 1908; asst. inspr., schls., Natal, May, 1910; inspr. of schls., Apr., 1912; ch. inspr., schls., Aug., 1931.

LAWRANCE, MAJOR SIR ARTHUR SALISBURY, K.B.E. (1934), C.M.G. (1931), D.S.O.—Major, late reserve of officers; served with I.V. in S. Africa, 1900-1902 (Queen's medal with 3 clasps, King's medal with 2 clasps, ment. in desps.); served with 6th Batt., K.A.R., Somaliland, 1905-1911 (African General Serv. medal with clasp "Somaliland 1908-1910," ment. in desps.); asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1911; dist. commnr., Somaliland, Sept., 1914; comdt., Somaliland Camel Corps, Oct., 1913; operns. against Mullah, 1913; ment. in desps.; A.G.S. med. with clasp; ag. consul., Harar, 1915 and 1919; operns. against Mullah, 1920; ment. in desps., D.S.O.; admstd. govt. on various occasions, 1921, 1925-32; sec. to gov't., 1926; commnr. and commdr.-in-ch., June, 1932; gov't. and commdr.-in-ch., May, 1935.

LAWRENCE, MAJOR CHARLES TREVOR, O.B.E. (1926)—B. 1879; ed. St. David's, Reigate and at Shrewsbury Schl.; N. Nigeria Regt., 1900-03; A.D.C. to Gov., S. Nigeria, 1906; junr. asst. sec., S. Nigeria, 1910; ag. asst. prov. commnr., Cent. Prov., 1912; pvt. sec. to gov.gen., 1914 and 1915; commanded Nigeria Land Cont., 1914-15; 1st asst. sec., S. Prov., 1916; addl., Nigeria Regt., 1917-19; sec., petroleum pool bd., 1918-19; ag. sec., S. Prov., 1919, 1920 and 1921; prin. asst. sec., 1921; commnr., Br. Empire Exhibn., 1923; ag. lieut.-gov'r., S. Prov., 1927; staff grade, 1928; admstr. of col., 1930.

LAWSON, HON. SIR HARRY SUTHERLAND WIGHTMAN, K.C.M.G. (1933).—B. 1875; M.L.A. for Castlemaine, Victoria, 1899; sat continuously in State Parlmt. from 1899; commnr. of crown lands and survey, Dec., 1913 to Nov., 1915; afterwards atty.-gen., solr. gen. and min. of public instrm., premier and atty.-gen. 1918; premier and min. of agr., 1920-24; now C'wealth senator for Victoria.
LAZARD, RONALD ANTHONY JOSEPH.—B. 1913; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Univ. Coll., Oxford, B.A., 1935; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1936.

LEA, LESLIE.—Ed. privately and Univ. Coll. Schl., Gray's Inn, 1922; clk. acct., fencing dept., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1908; conf. clk. to commr. for lands, 1909; conveyancing clk., land dept., 1910; ag. conveyancer, 1913; asst. regitr., crown lands, 1916; musketry instr., K.A.R., 1916 (red. thanks G.O.C.L. of C.); regitr. of titles, 1919; dep. recorder of titles, 1919; ag. prin. regitr., crown lands, 1920; dep. regitr., gen. titles and ch. controlling rev. authority, 1920-21; law offr., legal adviser, etc., St. Helena, Aug., 1923; col. audir., mem., bd. of commrs., crown property, Sept., 1923; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, 1925; commnr. for preparation of new edn. of laws, 1925.
LEA, WALTER ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Carpenters' Company's Coll. and West Ham Tech. Inst.; dist. engrnr., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1914; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.

LEACH, FRANK, M.C., B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1889; on mil'y. serv., July, 1917; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd., secretary, June, 1921; office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Nov., 1922; asst. attnt. offr., Oct., 1923; offic. asst. to dir., med. and sanry. services, Apr., 1929; asst. attnt. offr., Jan., 1930; asst. govt. agt., Badulla, Feb., 1931; do., Hambantota, Apr., 1932; asst. ch. sec. and sec., pub. services comm., Dec., 1935.
LEADBETTER, SIDNEY.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 3rd Mar., 1937.

LEAVITT, CLYDE.—B. 1878; ed., High Schl., Bellaire, Mich., Univ. of Mich. (B.A., 1901; M.Sc. For., 1904); ent. U.S. forest serv., assistant asst., 1903; apptd. forest asst., 1904, with successive appts. as ch. of boundaries, asst. ch. of organization, ch., office of organization, dist. forester, and asst. forester; ch. forester, comm. of conservn. and bd. of rly. commrs., Canada 1912; ch. fire inspr., bd. of rly. commrs., 1921; mem., soc. of American Foresters; successively pres. and sec., Can. Soc. of Forest Engnr.
LECK, W. J., M.I.M.E.—B. 1889; ed. St. Bees Schl., Cumberland; colliery man., Nigeria, 1914.

LECKIE, CAPT. PETER MARTIN, L.S. (Vic.).—B. 1890; survr., dept. lands and surveys, Dec., 1907; staff survr., May, 1913; war survr., Sept., 1915-18; survr., Perak, F.M.S., Oct., 1920; do., Kedah, Apr., 1923; asst. supt., surveys, Jan., 1926; do., Spore, Mar., 1929; do., Kelantan, May, 1932; do., Pahang, Sept., 1936.

LE CONTE, LOUIS.—B. 1881; mag., lesser dependencies, Mauritius, 1909; do., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Oct., 1913; ag. mast. and regitr., sup. ct., 1913-14; ag. prot. of immigrts. and poor law commnr., 1914; war survr. in France and Macedonia, 1916-19; food contr., May 1920; puiane judge, July, 1932; ag. ch. judge, sup. ct., Oct., 1933.

LEDEBORG, CLAUD MARIUS.—Served in S. African Constab., 1901-7 (S.A. war med.); Bech. Prot. police, from 1907; sub-insp. Bech. Prot. police, 1st Apr., 1914; asst. res. mag.; passed Cape civ. serv. lower law exam. and 2nd grade Sechuan exam.; 2nd lieut. gen. list, 1919; ag. res. mag., 1921; res. mag., 1921; passed Cape civ. serv. higher law exam., 1923; ag. asst. res. commnr., 1930.

LEE, FRANK G.—B. 1903; ed. Brentwood Schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls. English tripus, 1923; 1st cls. historical tripus, part II, 1924; foundation schl., 1924; apptd. to C.O. after compet. exam., 8th Nov., 1926; temp. agr. prin., 26th Sept., 1930; seconded to Nyasaland adminstr., 2nd Apr., 1931; asst. pvtc. sec. to S. of S. for the Cola., 4th Aug., 1933.

LEE, HARRIE BERTIE, D.S.O., M.C., M.B., B.S. (hons.), Melbourne, D.T.M. and H., Eng.; certif. London S.T.M. (with distinct.).—B. 1888; ed. Sydney and Melbourne Univ.; four years serv. with A.I.F. in field as R.M.O.; field ambulance offr. and D.A.D.M.S., 1st Australian Divn., 1914-18; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Nigeria, Jan., 1924; specialist Nigeria, Aug., 1933.

LEE, JOHN ALEXANDER, D.C.M. (1917).—B. 1891; ed. Albany Street Public Schl., Dunedin; war serv. with N.Z.E.F.; mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1922-28 and since 1931; partly under sec. to min. of finance, 1936; author of "Children of the Poor" and "The Hunted."

LEE, JOHN HENRY BURKILL.—B. 1905; ed. Univ. Coll., Leicester; cadet, Hong Kong, civ. serv., Dec., 1928; asst. treas., Apr., 1931; asst. P.M.G., 1932; 1st clk. and pol. mag., Kowloon, Feb., 1934; asst. dist. offr., North, Jan., 1935; pvtc. sec. to O.A.G., May, 1935; do., Dec., 1935; extra asst. col. sec., Mar., 1936; dep. clk., comms., Aug.-Sept., 1936.
LEE, LIONEL JAMES, A.M.I.M.E.—Ed. R.N.E. Coll., Keyham; asst. engrnr., marine dept., Nigeria, 1913; prin. engrnr., 1927; asupt. engrnr., June, 1933.

LEE, SYDNEY WILLIAM TIMPSON, M.D., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., D.P.H. (Belf.), D.T.M. (L'pool).—B. 1898; surg. sub. lt., R.N.V.R., 1917-18; M.O.S., Rhodesia, 1921-22, M.O., Uganda, 1922; S.H.O., Uganda, 1930; ag. D.D.S.S., 1930; ag. D.D.M.S., 1934; S.M.O., Zanzibar, 1934; M.O.H., port health offr. and offr. i/c eye clinic, 1934.
LEE, Hon. Sir W. H., K.C.M.G. (1922), Kt. BACH (1920).—M.H.A., Tasmania; premier, chief sec., and min. for educn., 1916-22.

LEEMBRUGGEN, EDWARD LESLIE.—B. 1893; regisry. clk., Western Pacific high commr., 1917; corres. clk., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., 1920; sec. to res. commr., 1922; sec. to gov't., 1931; ag. admntr. offr., Fanning Is., June 1936.

LEES, AUBREY TREVOR OSWALD.—B. 1899; ed. Repton and Royal Mil'y. Academy, Woolwich; ent. R. Arty., Jan., 1919; served with 2nd R.W. Fus., Ireland, June, 1920; and with Arab and Kurdish Levies, Iraq and Kurdistan, June, 1922; attd. air hd. qrs., "Iraq (air staff intell., spec. serv.) June, 1924; adminstr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1926; pvtc. sec. to Br. res. and ag. asst. sec., Nov., 1926; clk. of exec. and leg. couns. and edr. of gazette, Sept., 1927; temp. attd., C.O., 1928; admntr. offr., Palestine, 1929.

LEGGE, CHARLES ARTHUR LIONEL.—B. 1879; clerical asst., B. Guiana, May, 1897; 6th class offr., cust., June, 1899; 5th class clk., pol. dept., Dec., 1900; lieut., B. G. Artillery, 1901; attached to No. 32 Coy. R.G.A., Portsmouth, July, 1904; cert. in depression range finding, July, 1904; Hythe musk, cert., Aug., 1904; sub-inspr. of pol. and excise, Grenada, Oct., 1904; promoted capt., B. Guiana Artillery, and seconded, Dec., 1904; mem. local health authority, St. Andrew's parish, Grenada, Jan., 1905; ag. ch. of pol., Grenada, from June, 1907.

LEGGE, CYRIL HENRY ETHELWOOD.—B. 1883; joined B. Guiana civ. serv., June, 1901; clk. in surg. gens. office, cent. bd. of health, cust. and atty.-gens. office; commissary, May, 1906; ag. stip. mag., Mar., 1914 to Dec., 1916; stip. mag., 2nd grade, Dec., 1916; stip. mag., 1st grade, Mar., 1921; passed final bar exams., 1912; called to bar, Lincolns Inn, July, 1922; passed govt. Hindi exam.

LEIGH-CLARE, HARRY JAMES LEIGH.—B. 1891; on mil'y. serv., Jan., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Feb., 1920; attd., Colombo kach., Mar., 1920; attd. office of food contrlr., Apr., 1920; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Mar., 1921; office asst. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Apr. and Sept., 1922; addnl. landing survr., cust., Colombo, Oct., 1924; 2nd landing survr., Nov., 1924; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Dec., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Dec., 1927; landing survr., cust., Mar., 1928; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Matara, Dec., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Matara, Feb., 1930; admntr. secy. to dir. med. and san. services and sec. to min. of health, Sept., 1932; ag. tea ext. contr., Dec., 1934; dep. collr., cust., Apr., 1935.

LEMAIRE, ERNEST JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1874; ed. Sherbrooke (Que.), Coll.; ent. privy coun. office for Canada, Jan., 1894; priv. sec., prime min., 1904-11; post office dept., 1912-23; clk. of privy coun. for Canada, Aug., 1923.

LEMBERGER, MAURICE.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., 23rd Apr., 1918; on mil'y. serv., 26th Aug., 1918 to 27th Feb., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; trans. to P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; trans. to C.O., 1st Sept., 1923.

LEMPRIERE, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1898; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; enlisted K.R.C., 1916; 2nd lieut., Cheshire Regt., 1917; ag. capt., Sohl. of Instrn., E.E.F., 1919-21; demob., 1921; col.; audit dept., Oct., 1921; asst. audr., Gold Coast; Nov., 1921; senr. asst. audr., Kenya, 1933; audr., B. Guiana, 1936.

LENDRUM, SAMUEL JOHN.—B. 1876; ed. St. Luke, Cork, and pte. tuition; clk., Imp. mil'y. rly., S. Africa, 1900; conf. clk., govt. comm'r's office, 1901; clk., Transvaal pol., 1902; ag. clk., do. pol., 1903-8; asupt. clk., do. pol., 1908; sub-inspr., S.A. pol., 1913; inspr., do. pol., 1918; senr. inspr., 1928; dep. comm'r, 1929; ch. dep. comm'r, 1933.

LENTON, HENRY JOHN.—Cape teleg. serv., Sept., 1898; clk., staff branch, Jan., 1902; 1st cls. clk., ditto, Apr., 1912; senr. clk., ch. clk.'s office, Apr., 1918; prin. clk., ditto, Dec., 1919; 2nd grade, ch. clk., Apr., 1921; asst. sec., Nov., 1923; ag. P.M.G., May to Oct., 1924 and from July-Dec., 1925; P.M.G., Apr., 1926.

LERMIT, Lieut.-Col. ARTHUR ALFRED, O.B.E., V.D.—B. 1881; asst. supt. rev. surveys, Malacca, Feb., 1921; 2nd lieut. S.S.V.F., Jan., 1922; maj. and offr. commdg., Malacca V.C., Apr., 1922; asst. supt., surveys, Malacca, Jan., 1926 and Mar., 1934; asst. supt., rev. surveys, Apr., 1930; do., Kinta, Jan., 1934.

LE ROUX, JONATHAN JOHAN DU PRÉ.—B. 1900; ed. B.H.S., Paarl, Univ. Cape Town, Royal Coll., Edin. and Univ., Witwatersrand; L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), D.P.H. (Wit.), 1933; med. offr., leper inst., Pretoria, Nov., 1927; med. supt., Jan., 1933.
LESSELS, RAYMOND.—B. 1897; asst. engnr., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, Mar., 1924; divnl. engnr., Apr., 1933; ag. engnr.-in-ch., 1932 and 1934.

LESTER, A. R., M.B., B.S. (Bomb.), F.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), D.P.H., D.T.M. and H. (Edin.)—B. 1890; Indian med. serv., 1917-22; mily. serv., India and Persia, 1918-22; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1924; med. offr. in ch., sp. sociological investgtn., Kahama, 1927-31; senr. med. offr., Uganda, 1934.

LETHAM, CHARLES KINGSLEY, B.Sc. (agri.)—B. 1901; ed. S. African Coll. Schl., Cape Town; B.Sc. (agri.), Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria, Univ. of S. Africa, 1922; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924.

LETHEM, SIR GORDON JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1936), C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1886; ed. at Mill Hill Schl., Middlesex, and Edin. and Grenoble Univ.; M.A. and L.L.B. (with distinction), Edin.; mem. of Lincoln's Inn; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Mar., 1911; res., Aug., 1924; ag. sec., N. Provs., Apr. to Nov., 1927; ag. sec., native affrs., Nov., 1927 to Sept., 1928; staff grade, Mar., 1928; sec., N. Provs., Apr., 1929; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Seychelles, 1933; do., Leeward Is., 1935.
LEVINKIND, LEOPOLD.—B. 1892; ed. S. African Coll., Cape Town; B.Sc. (engng.); corp., C.P. Rifles, S.W. Africa campaign; ent. dept., irrig., Union of S. Africa, 1917; hydrographic surv., 1934.

LEWEY, MAJOR A. W., M.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-Law (Inner Temple).—B. 1894; ed. St. Paul's and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; Middlesex Regt., European War, 1914-18; Gallipoli, Egypt, Flanders, Palestine, severely wounded; pol. mag., Gambia, Dec., 1929; aide-de-camp and pte. sec., Gambia, Jan.-June, 1930; ag. legal adviser, and prov. mem., exec. and leg. couns. on several occasions; crown counsel, Kenya, Oct., 1932; attld., legal advy. staff., C.O. May-June, 1936; solr-gen., Uganda, Aug., 1936; ag. atty-gen., Sept.-Dec., 1936.

LEWIN, CLAUD JOHN, M.C., B.Sc.—B. 1898; ed. Sexey's Sch., Univ. Coll., Reading and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; on war serv., 1915-17; supt., agr., Nigeria, Dec., 1922; junr. botanist, 1923; senr. botanist, 1925; ch. agriculturist, N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1930; ag. sec., agr., Dec., 1932; dir., agr., July, 1933; temp. M.L.C., 1935 and 1936.

LEWIS, ALFRED DALE, R.A. (Cape), M.A. (Cantab.), M.I.M.E., M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1881; P.W.D., India, 1905-07; circle engnr., irrig. dept., Cape and Union of S. Africa, 1907-15; on mily. serv. as major, R.E., in France, Egypt, Mesopotamia, 1915-19; ag. dir. of irrig., 1920; dir. of irrigation, 1921; author of "Irrigation in America."

LEWIS, BERTRAM.—B. 1887; Br. P.O., sect's office, C. and A.G.'s dept. and London & prov. audit, 1903-17; asst. acctnt., dep. ch. acctnt., Kenya and Uganda, P. and T., 1917-24; ch. acctnt., Palestine, 1924; do., Kenya, Uganda and Tanganyika post. serv., 1936.
LEWIS, CHARLES HENRY, B. 1890; linotype operator, govt. printing press, Zanzibar, Nov., 1921; ag. govt. printer, Oct., 1922 to May, 1923; asst. govt. printer, Nov., 1923; govt. printer, Feb., 1933.

LEWIS, ERNEST HARRY, C.B.E., C.A.—Ed. King's Coll., Lond.; on war serv., 1899-1901; 1st grade clk., audit dept., Transvaal, 1902; acctnt., educn. dept., 1907; ch. acctnt., Transvaal prov. admnstr., 1910; ch. acctnt. and acctnt., S. African pol., 1910; ass't sec., defence dept., 1916; finan. undersec., finan. advr., S. African pol., 1929.
LEWIS, JOHN VINCENT.—B. 1907; ed. William's Coll., Isle of Man and Keble, Oxford (B.A.); asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930.

LEWIS, JOHN WILLIAMSON, A.M.C.L.—1887; ed. King's schl., Canterbury; Cruston's schl. of practical engnr.; jr. asst. engnr. ord. dept., F.M.S., rly., 1909; asst. engnr., grade III, 1913; senr. dist. engnr., No. 2 ditto, Bahru, May, 1930; do., head office, 1931; maintenance and new wks. engnr., F.M.S., rly., Aug., 1932; ag. ch. engnr., wayside, Oct., 1934.
LEWIS, MALCOLM STUART.—B. 1889; H.A. 1914-16; R.G.A., 1917-19; treas. agt., N.W. 1919; senr. asst. treas., 1934.

LEWTHWAITE, RAYMOND, M.A., B.Ch. (Oxon.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Edin.)—B. 1894; resch. student, Inst. Med. Ed., F.M.S., Dec., 1926; ag. pathologist I, Mar., June, 1929, and May, 1931; pathologist II, 1929; pathologist I, Dec., 1931.

LEY, MAJOR CUTHBERT HILLYAR, O.B.E., R.E., F.R.Met.Soc.—B. 1872; comm. Engrs., 1892-1907; ret. from army, 1917; serv. in Jamaica, 1896-99; S. African war, 1900 (Tugela Heights and relief of Ladysmith); A.D.C. to insp'r. gen. of fortifications, 1900-03; geodetic survey of S. Africa, 1904-07; topographical secn., gen. staff, 1907-09; of Fiji, 1908; ordnance survey of Gt. Brit., 1912; served during war, at Southampton, depts. of fortifications, min't. of munitions, directorate of arty.; dir., Palestine survey, 1920.

LEY, HENRY COATES.—B. 1898; clk. to Leeward Is., Nov., 1920; 1st clk., treas. and shipping master, Falkland Is., Apr., 1921; also postmtr., Apr., 1928; ag. treas. and cust., man., savings bank, recr. of wreck regisr., shipping, June 1928; ch. acctnt., Cyprus, Sept., 1930; ag. dep. treas., in 1934; dep. treas., Nov., 1934; ag. treas., Nov., 1936.

LEYH, STANLEY GEORGE HOARE, Tech. R.N.R., O.B.E. (Milv.)—B. 1893; ed. East Tech. Coll., A.S.C. (T.F.), 1908-12; Royal Naval College, 1913-20; staff of comdr.-in-chief, Grand Fleet, 1914-17; admity. war staff (allied naval), 1918-19; staff, comdr.-in-chief, China, 1919-20; "1914-15 Star," Brit. War Med. Med.; librarian, col. sec.'s office, Suez, 1920; office asst., col. sec.'s office, Suez, 1922; offr.-in-ch., records revision, Feb., 1924; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, S'pore, Nov., 1929; ag. senr. ast., do., June, 1933-Jan., 1934; seconded dep. cust. and excise, F.M.S., Feb., 1935-Mar., ag. senr., dep. comptn., excise, S'pore, 1936.

LIAS, EDMUND THOMAS MORTLOCK, R.A.—1892; ed. Haileybury and Peterhouse; war serv. Egypt and Palestine, 1915-19; mast., S.S., Oct., 1921; headmst., F.M.S., Schl., Penang, Jan., 1927; do., Francis Schl., Jan., 1930; do., High Schl., R.M.C., Nov., 1934.

ESCHING, PERCIVALE, C.M.G. (1932) — B. 1895; ed. Bedford Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1920; pvt., 1st batt., C.C., 1914; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brig., Feb., 1915; June, 1915; capt., Tank Corps, Oct.; served in France, 1914, 1917-18; E. Africa, 1918; apptd. under instruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 26th 1920; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec. for union affrs., 6th Aug., 1925; temp. ag. prin. Jan., 1927; prin. 1st Apr., 1929; accom. U.K. delegation to L. of N. Assembly, to signature of l'act of Paris, 1928 and to Economic Conf., Ottawa, 1932; seconded to H. Cr. for the U.K. in Canada, 1928; nd as polit. sec. to H. Cr. for the U.K. in the con. of S. Africa, 1933; ag. high commr. for S. Africa, Aug.-Dec., 1933.

IGHT, JOSEPH.—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. Nov., 1921; attd., Galle kach., Jan., 1922; o., Kegalla kach., Feb., 1923; office astt. to govt. agt., Kegalla, Aug., 1923; pol. mag., Malam, Apr., 1925; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1927; o., Jaffna, Apr., 1928; astt. govt. agt., Kotaloos, Jan., 1929; astt. attltn. offr., Jan., 9; astt. govt. agt., Puttalam, June, 1933; do., tutura, Apr., 1934; addnl. astt. govt. agt., tara, Nov., 1935.

IGHTHOURN, J. L.—B. 1889; cadet, Out. ands, Bahamas, 1910; ag. 3rd and 2nd clk. col. 'office, 1910-12; sec., bd. of educn., Mar., 1914; pol. comsn., 1921-22; ag. audr., pub. accts., y-Nov., 1924 and from Jan. to Apr., 1926; ag. assnr., Out Islands, Nov., 1926 to May, 1927; grade I., audit dept., July, 1927; ag. audr., various occasions, 1929-36.
LILLEY, CYRIL CHARLES, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; ot., Worcestershire Regt.; astt. dist. comsnr., old Coast, Aug., 1920.
LILLEY, RONALD, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Bedford and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; col. audit dept., 1930; astt. audr., Tanganyika, July, 1930; S.S. and F.M.S., 1936.

LINDOP, REGINALD ALEXANDER ESKERINE.—B. 1897; war serv., 1915-19; Leeward Is. pol. rec., 1919-24; dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Barbados, 24; ag. inspr.-gen., pol. and supt., fire brig., various periods, 1925-34; commit. pol., Bahamas, 1936.

LINDOW, ERIC DELAFIELD, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), R.C.P., Lond., certf. L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. Christ's Coll., Blackheath, Lond., Univ., King's Coll., and King's Coll. Hosp.; med. offr., gen. esp., Spore, Apr., 1923; do., T.T.S. hosp., Dec., 23, Oct., 1924 and Apr., 1932; ag. ch. med. fr., Malacca, Feb., 1928 and Apr., 1929; ag. apmny. ch. med. offr., A. Spore, June, 1933, ab-scale offr., gr. B., Jan., 1935.

LINDSAY, JAMES.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; M.B., h.B., 1906; late res. physician, Grampian anatorium for consumption; late clinical astt., royal Victoria Dispensary for Consumption and ises of the chest, Edin.; joined W.A.M.S., Apr., 1910.
LINDSAY, K. G.—Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, June, 1921.

LINDSELL, HAROLD OFFLEY.—B. 1884; ed. at Bradford Coll. (exhibitioner) and All Souls' Coll., Oxford, B.A. (Litt. Hum.), 1907; astt. es., N. Nigeria, Apl., 1909; dist. offr., 1917; es., 1925; res., Kano, 1929.

LINDSELL, ROGER EDWARD.—B. 1885; ed. at Uppingham schl. (scholar) and King's Coll., Camb. (scholar); class. tripes, part I, Cls. I, div. 2; called to bar, 1930; cadet, Hong Kong, 1909; passed cadet, 1911; ag. 2nd astt. regisr.-gen., 1911; J.P., 1912; 3rd astt. to sec. for Chinese affrs., but continued to act as 2nd ditto, 1914; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1915; ag. chief astt. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1915 to 1916; ag. astt. P.G., 1916; ag. astt. supt., imports and exports dept., 1918; ag. dep. regisr., sup. ct., 1918; ag. P.M.G., 1923; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1919, 1921, 1922 and 1923; ag. 1st pol. mag., on various occasions, 1922-30; ag. puisne judge, 1931 and 1933; ag. astt. atty.-gen., Feb., 1932; puisne judge, 1934; ag. ch. just., Dec., 1935—May, 1936.

LINEHAN, WILLIAM.—B. 1892; ed. Christian Brother's Coll. and Univ. Coll., Cork (class. schol. and exhib.), 1910-13; B.A. (1st cls. class., 1913); post grad. scholarship, 1913-14; M.A. hons., class., 1916; cadet, F.M.S., 1916; 2nd astt. dist. offr., Jan.-April, 1919; astt. dist. offr., Apr., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1923; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1928; ag. astt. Br. advr., supt., lands and regisr., titles, Kelantan, Feb., 1931; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1933; dist. offr., Seremban, Jan., 1934; ag. astt. advr., Muar, Mar., 1935.

LINFORD, GEOFFREY BINDER.—B. 1891; pol. prob., F.M.S., Nov., 1912; astt. comsnr., pol., June, 1918; 1st astt. comsnr., pol., Ipoh, Jan., 1923; paymaster, Jan., 1925; regisr., criminals, S.S. and F.M.S., Mar., 1925; adjt., F.M.S., pol., Feb., 1934.

LINTON, REGINALD DUKE, B.Agr., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1904; ed. Mountjoy Sch., Dublin, Belfast Univ. (B. Agr.) (distinct.), 1927), Oxford Univ. and Imp. Coll., Trop. Agr. (A.I.C.T.A.), 1929; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1929.

LISSAMAN, ARTHUR EVELYN, F.S.I., M.Inst. M. & Cy. E.—B. 1897; ed. Luton Modern Schl. and Coll., Estate Management, Lond.; mil. serv., R.E.'s, 1915-19; survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921; engrnr., 1923; ag. engrnr., Feb., Nov., 1935.
LITTLEJOHN, NORMAN KNIP, A.M.Inst. C.E., A.M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1900; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll. and Robert Gordon's Tech. Coll., Aberdeen; engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1926.

LIVERPOOL, EARL OF, RT. HON. ARTHUR FOLJAMBE, P.C. (1917); G.C.B.; G.C.M.G. (1914); G.B.E. (1918); K.C.M.G. (1912); M.V.O. (1900); Viscount Hawkesbury of Kirkham; Baron Hawkesbury of Haselbich.—B. 1870; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brigade, 1891; lieut., 1893; capt., 1894; staff capt., Dublin district, July, 1900 to Dec., 1901; served in S. Africa, 1901-2; major, 1907; state steward and chamberlain to Earl of Aberdeen, K.T., lord lieutenant of Ireland, 1906-08; gov. of New Zealand, 9th Sept., 1912; assumed govt., 19th Dec., 1912; governor-general of New Zealand, 1917-20.

LIVINGSTON, WILLIAM, A.M.Inst.C.E., P.A.S.Inst.—B. 1890; ed. Sch. of Science & Art, Kirkcaldy and Heriot-Watt Coll., Edinburgh; war ser., Gallipoli, Egypt and France, 1914-19; astt. dist. engrnr., Trinidad, 1919; dist. engrnr., 1920; dist. engrnr., Br. Guiana, 1924; seconded for duty on reconnaissance survey to interior, Br. Guiana, Mar.-Aug., 1927; D.P.W., Bermuda, Sept., 1929.

LLIWELLYN, JOHN LIONEL BRETHERTON LLWELLYN, M.A. Oxon. (hons. hist.), F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; astt. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 22nd Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., N. Frontier, Sept., 1915. June, 1920; A.G.S. med., "Jubaland, 1917-18"; ag. dist. comsnr., Jubaland, Nov., 1922-May, 1924.
LLOYD, EDGAR BRINEY.—B. 1894; ed. Aberavon; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1919; ag. land asst., 1920; ag. regis., crown lands, documents and titles, 1921.

LLOYD, T. I. K.—B. 1896; ed. Rossall Schol. and Caius Coll., Cambridge (schol., 1914); cadet, Royal Mil. Acad., 1915; 2nd lieut., R.E., 1916; served in Egypt and Palestine, ment. in desps., 1917; app'td. under re-construction scheme, asst. prin., miny. of health, July, 1920; att. to C.O. as asst. prin., Dec., 1921; ag. prin., D.O. 4th Sept., 1929; seconded for serv. as sec. to Palestine coms., 1929.

LLOYD, WILLIAM BUTLER.—B. 1880; ed. Shrewsbury, and Christ Church, Oxford; M.A.; called to the bar, 1905; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1913; mag., Rodriguez, 1916; mag. for occupied territories of German East Africa, 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1919; pol. mag., S. Leone, June, 1922; puisne judge, Mar., 1924; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1924 to May, 1925, and from July, 1928; puisne judge, sup. ot., Nigeria, 1930; ag. ch. just., July-Oct., 1933.

LLOYD, WILLIAM HENRY, O.B.E. (1937)—B. 1882; ed. Hampden Gunnery Schol.; asst. acctnt., open lines Baro-Kano rly., N. Nigeria, July, 1912; transf'd. to admistr. ser., Apr., 1917; admistr. offr., cl. I, Dec., 1932.

LLOYD, HON. SIR WM. FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1864; ed. Owen's Coll., Manchester and Durham Coll. of Science, Newcastle-on-Tyne; bachelor of law, London Univ., 1894; D.C.L., Victoria Univ., Toronto, 1901; vice-principal, Bishop Field Coll., St. John's, Newfild., 1891; editor, "Evening Telegram," 1904; M.H.A., Trinity dist., 1904; leader of the opposition, H. of A., 1916; with Sir Edward (now Lord) Morris formed a National Govt., becoming attorney-general, 1917; ag. prime minister, 1917; prime minister, 1918.

LLOYD-BLOOD, CAPT. L.I.N., M.C., B.A., Barrister-at-law.—B. 1896; ed. Tonbridge and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd lieut., 5th R. Dublin Fusiliers, Jan., 1916; France, 1916-18; ment. in desps.; dep. regist., sup. ct., Kenya, Oct., 1920; regist., July, 1921; asst. atty.-gen., Nyasaland, Aug., 1924; ag. atty.-gen., in 1925, 1926, 1927, 1929 and 1932; solr.-gen., Palestine, 1932; ag. atty.-gen., in 1933 and 1934; atty.-gen., Cyprus, Sept., 1936.

LOCK, FREDERICK JOHN.—B. 1888; ed. King's Schol., Peterboro and Quernmore Coll., Upper Norwood; temp. acct., Nyasaland, July, 1915; acct., July, 1918; asst. treas., Feb., 1923; senr. asst. treas., Apr., 1925; ag. dep. treas. in 1922, 1923, 1924, 1932-33; ag. treas., Somaliland, Apr., to Sept., 1932; dep. treas., Nyasaland, Apr., 1933; chmn., nat. civ. ser. bd., 1933-34; ag. treas., Oct., 1936.

LOCK, GEORGE ROBERT.—B. 1876; ed. Weymouth Coll. and Sidney-Sussex Coll., Camb., 1895 (schol.); senior optime, maths, tripes, 1898; 1st cls. mech. sciences tripes, 1899; ent. C.A. as tech. asst., 1905; dep. head, wks. dept., 1913.

LOCK, GEORGE WINNLOW, N.D.A., N.D.D.: Dip. Agr., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1902; ed. Latymer Schol., London; Mid Agril. Coll.; econ. div. M.A., I.C.T.A., 1929; dist. agril. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1930.

LOCKHART, CHARLES RAMSDALE, C.B.E. (1935).—B. 1892; mil. serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919 (Gallipoli, Egyt, and with K.A.R. in E. Africa); twice ment. in desps.; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; ag. senr. treas., May, 1921; senr. asst. treas., Apr., 1922; dep. treas., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1931; ret., Apr., 1933.
LOCKHART, F. R., M.B., Ch.B. (Mar.), D.T.M. & H. (London).—B. 1897; milit. med., 1916-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924.

LOCKHART, KENNETH SPENCER.—R. M. copyist, admistr. office, Dominica, 1908; clk., regist. office, 1910; 2nd rev. offr., harbmr., Nevis, 1913; ag. acctnt., treas., 1913; also sec. to quarantine bd., and clk. to treas. bd. of health; govt. trade agt., to ensure rep. on conditions re W.I. labourers at Domingo, Nov., 1918; rev. offr., St. Kitts, 1920; ag. audit. clk., Sept., 1920; 2nd clk., dept., July, 1921; capt., St. Kitts-Nevis-Dominica Force; ag. 1st clk., treas., May-Sep., 1921; audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1922; treas. tax commr., 1924; prin., Oct., 1929.

LOCKHART-SMITH, WILLIAM JAMES.—1902; ed. Uppingham; 1st clk., Kowloon Mag., Hong Kong, July, 1924; attd. land office, 1926; admitted solr., sup. ck., Eng., Nov., 1933; dep. O.R. and reg. tr. marks in aids., Aug., 1933; O.R. and reg. tr. marks, Sept., 1934-35; asst. crown solr., 1934; asst. offr., Aug., 1935; ag. land offr., Apr., 1936.
LOCKIE, JAMES REACH.—B. 1904; ed. Coldbeltown and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (forestry, cls. hon.); asst. conservtr., forests, Br. India, Nov., 1926; do., Nigeria, 1936.

LOGAN, SIR EWEN REGINALD, Kt., F.R.S. (1928), J.P.—B. 1868; ed. at Charterhouse and Exeter Coll., B.A., 1891; M.A., 1895; stud. Inner Temp., Jan., 1894; called to bar, Nov., 1894; joined Middlex. and N. London sess. and criminal, 1900; serv. in Boer war as 2nd Lieut. 65th squad., I.Y., 1901; Queen's medal and clasps; mines dept., Transvaal govt., Aug., 1902 to Apr., 1902; asst. res. mag., Transvaal, May 1902 to May 1904; mag. E. Africa Prot., 1905; chief just., Seychelles, 1914; admin. govt. from July, 1916 to Mar., 1917 and May to Oct., 1918; drafted in conjunction with Alfred Gellé Esq., barrister-at-law, code of procedure for Seychelles; recd. thanks of state; puisne judge, G. Coast, June, 1920; senr. seniory from Mar., 1920; senr. puisne judge, Dec., 1922; ag. ch. just., Mar. to Nov., 1925; Feb. to Mar., 1925; ch. just., Bahamas, Feb., 1925; chmn., bd. of educn., Aug., 1925; high ct., N. Rhodesia, May, 1927; ret., Jan., 1931; J.P., County of London, 1935.

OGAN, WILLIAM MARSTON, O.B.E. (1933).—Ed. Bristol and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; M.A.; ed. Bridgeman prize man, B.A.; asst. commr., East Africa Prot., Mar., 1913; sec., committee of supplies, 1914-15; hon. lieut., attached list, 1915-17; ag. dist. commr., 1918 Dec. to July, 1921; ag. senr. asst. sec., Sept. Nov., 1920 and July, 1921 to June, 1924; 1st adm. off., June, 1924; sen. asst. sec., 1924 to Mar., 1925; ag. asst. col. sec., to Oct., 1925 and in 1926; sec., local govt. comm., 1926; ag. asst. col. sec., 1927; prin. commr. for local govt., lands and attm., 1928; commr., local govt., lands, attm. and mines, 1933.
OLTGEN, W. G.—B. 1894; on mily. serv., May, 1916 to 18th Feb., 1920; temp. clk., O.S.O., Mar. 1920 to 27th June, 1922; apptd. for compet. exam., cler. off., O.S.O., 27th June, 1922.

ONDON, GEORGE ERNEST, C.M.G. (1936).—B. 1889; ed. at Warwick and Downing Coll., Cambridge (schol.); ist nat. sci. tripods, 1910; hist. pos., 1911; B.A., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; ols., V., 1914; on mily. serv., 1916-19; ols., Glouc. Regt.; offr., cls. IV, 1919; sec. to Pahang, 1920; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1923; p. contr., rubber expts., S.S. and F.M.S. and p. supervisor, rubber F.M.S., Nov., 1924; cls. II, Nov., 1928; ag. finan. commr. and audr. gen., Jan., 1930; sec. to res., Perak, Sept., 1930; cls. IIB, Apr., 1931; a. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1932; ag. Br. res., N. Semban, June, 1933; under sec. to govt., F.M.S., 1934; lsec., Gold Coast, 1934.
LONEY, GEORGE HENRY.—B. 1871; ed. Timaru Pub. Schl.; ent. N.Z. printing and stationery dept., 1895; supt., 1922; govt. printer, 1933.

LONG, CAPT. HERBERT EDWARD.—B. 1887; Kendrick Schl., Reading; B.E.F., Egypt; Gallipoli, Salonika, Mar., 1915 to Feb., 1918; India and Aden, Mar., 1918 to April, 1921; dist. offr., Somaliland, July, 1921; H.B.M.'s vice-consul, Jigjiga, Sept., 1927; consul, Oct., 1930; cons. consul, Harar, Nov., 1931; dist. offr., Somaliland Prot., Mar., 1935.
LONGHURST, ALBERT HENRY.—B. 1877; geological commr., Ceylon, Oct., 1935.
LOOS, W. T.—B. 1885; apptd. cls. III, Ceylon v. serv., Mar., 1934; office asst., Trincomalee ach., Mar., 1934.

LORD, EDWARD ERNEST.—B. 1898; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Schl., Clifton; mily. serv. with L.A.C. and R.A.F., 1916-18; offr. Imp. cust. and excise, Jan., 1920; asst. to commr. of cust., Kenya and Uganda, Oct., 1924; senr. coll. of cust., Jan., 1935; ag. dep. commr., cust., on various occasions since 1930.
LORD, GEORGE ERIC LUDWIG, R.A.—B. 1907; ed. Felstead Schl. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; col. and dist. dept., June, 1930; asst. audr., Gold Coast, July, 1930; do., Palestine, 1935.

LORD, LIUT.-COL. J. E. C., C.M.G., D.S.O., V.D.—Ent. Tasmanian civ. ser., 1886; commanded 40th Batt. A.L.F. in European War from Feb., 1915 to Aug., 1919; commands 12th Inf. Brig., Australian Mil. Forces; A.D.C. to the gov.-gen.; commr. of pol., Tasmania.
LOUDOUN, WILSON ANDERSON.—B. 1874; pub. schl., Chatham, New Brunswick; asst. audr.-gen., 1902; audr.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1907; now comptroller-general.

LOUW, ERIC H.—B. 1891; ed. Beaufort West High Schl. and Univ. of Stellenbosch, S. Africa; A. and LL.B. (Cape); called to bar, 1916 and practised at sup. ct., Grahamstown; el. M.P. for Beaufort West, 1924; first S. African commr. to U.S.A. and Canada, 1925; high commr. for Union of S. Africa, London, Mar., 1929; min. plenipotentiary, Washington, Oct., 1929; do., Rome, Nov., 1933; do., Paris, 1934.
LOUW, JAN DANIEL, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Victoria Coll. and Univ., Stellenbosch; educ. serv., Cape Prov., 1912-1930; ch. translator, Pretoria, 1930.
LOWE, CLARENCE VAN RIET, B.Sc., Assoc. M.I.C.K., Cape Town, F.R.S., S. Africa, etc.—B. 1894; ed. Grey Coll. Sch., Bloemfontein and Univ. of Cape Town; dir., archaeological bureau, Johannesburg.

LOWE, CAPT. HERBERT JOHN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and R. Vety Coll., Dublin; lieut., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1914; capt., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1915; war serv., France, 1914-18; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1918; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1923; ag. dep. ch. vety. offr., May to Dec., 1926; ag. dir., vety. serv., in 1932 and 1933; ag. dep. dir., do., in 1933.

LOWICK, HENRY CAPELL, P.A.S.I.—B. 1882; ed. Northampton County Sch.; land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong 1910; asst. engnr., 1913; engr. in ch. archt., 1923; exec. engnr., do., 1930; ag. A.D.P.W., 1934, 1935 and 1936; J.P., 1935.
LUCAS, HON. SIR EDWARD, KT. BACH (1921).—M.L.C., S. Australia 1900-1918; agt. gen. for S. Australia in London, 1918-24.

LUCETTE, EDWARD HENRY, B.A. (Oxon.), M.C.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Apr., 1915; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd., Anuradhapura kach., June, 1921; attd., Trincomalee kach., Mar., 1922; attd., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1922; pvt. sec. and ag. A.D.C. to the gov., July, 1924 to Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Campbell, Feb., 1926; ditto, Kandy, Dec., 1926; dist. judge, Badulla, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., Colombo, Feb., 1929; addnl. dist. judge, Kandy, July, 1931; asst. regist., co-op. socs., Nov., 1931; asst. gov't. agt., Kegalle, Oct., 1932; regist., co-op. socs., Oct., 1934; dep. regist., do., Mar., 1935.

LUCIE-SMITH, SIR ALFRED VAN W., KT. BACH (1911).—Ed. at Rugby; solr., Br. Guiana, 1877; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1881; ag. solr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1887; pres. dist. ct., Famagusta, Cyprus, 1887; Limasol, 1892; ag. Queen's advoc., 1893; ag. judge, cons. ct., Constantinople, 1896; res. mag., Kingston, Jamaica, Nov., 1896; puisne judge, B.G., Jan., 1898; ag. ch. just., 1900-01; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1900; ch. just., Trinidad and Tobago, 1908; pres., W. Indian ct. of appeal, May, 1920; ret., 1924.

LUCIE-SMITH, JOHN ALFRED, O.B.E., V.D.—B. 1888; ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; col. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1905-06; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; advoc. and solr., S. Stiltns, 1910-14; on mily. serv., 1914; twice ment. in despe.; ag. pres., dist. ct., Trinidad, 1920; asst. mag., Port of Spain, 1920-23; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1924; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Dec., 1925; ag. puisne judge for various periods, 1926-27; ag. crown pros., Nov., 1926; puisne judge, Oct., 1927; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1927; puisne judge, Trinidad, 1929; chmn., oil and water bd., 1931; puisne judge, Kenya, 1931; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa.

LUCIE-SMITH, JOHN DUDLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Berkhamstead schl.; entd. civ. serv. of Jamaica, 1909; 2nd cls. clk., 1914; 1st cls. clk., 1920; sec., Westmoreland hurricane relief commn., 1912; sec. foot and mouth disease commn., 1922; ag. priv. sec. to gov. of Jamaica (Sir Sydney Olivier) 1912; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov. of Jamaica (Sir Leslie Probyn), 1920-23; prin. clk., col. secretariat, 1929; war serv., R.F.A. (France and Flanders), 1915-18; A.D.C. to C.R.A., 68th Div., 1918; W.O. staff, 1919; ed. of "The School Garden in Jamaica" and "Elementary Hygiene."

LUCKMAN, CYRIL HERBERT MORTON.—B. 1881; clk., cust., Johannesburg, 1903; 1st grade clk., Capetown, 1912; senr. clk., 1915; 2nd grade prin. clk., Pretoria, 1922; 1st grade ditto, 1926; acctnt., Pretoria, 1930; oh. clk., 1933; ch. inspr., 1933.

LUDDINGTON, NORMAN JOHN, M. Sc., Durham Univ.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 17th Nov., 1911; attd., Jaffna kach., Dec., 1911; pol. mag., Negombo, May, 1915; Panadura, June, 1915; Negombo, Aug., 1915; Galle, Apr., 1916; Colombo, July, 1921; astt. consnr., excise, July, 1923; astt. sttlm. offr., Nov., 1926; addnl. consnr., lab., Sept., 1931; gov. agt., Sabaragamuwa, July, 1933; gov. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, July, 1935.

LUGARD, 1ST BARON (cr. 1928), Rt. Hon. Sir Frederick John D'ALTRY LUGARD, P.C. (1920), G.C.M.G. (1911), K.C.M.G. (1911), C.B. (1895), D.S.O. (1887).—B. 1858; ed. Rossall and Sandhurst; 1st comnr., 9th foot, May, 1878; captain, Aug., 1880; major, Aug., 1896; lieut.-col., July, 1899; col., July, 1905; temp. brig.-gen., 1900-07; to India, Sept., 1878; active service, Afghanistan, Sept., 1879, to Sept., 1880; medal; Soudan, Feb. to Nov., 1885; medal clasp, "Tofrek" Khedive's Star, despatches Burmah, Oct., 1886, to Aug., 1887; mentioned in despatches, D.S.O. and medal. To Africa, Feb., 1888; commanding expdn. against slave traders; very severely wounded. To East Africa, Nov., 1889, to Oct., 1892; exploration of Sabakhi; adminstr. Uganda; went to Borgu in comd. expdn., July, 1894, and made treaties on behalf of Royal Niger Company, returned, Apr., 1895, Feb., 1896, led expedition across Kalahari for Brd Charterland Co.; apptd. comanr. and comdt., West African frontier force, Aug., 1897; high comanr. for Northern Nigeria, Dec., 1899; resigned Sept., 1906; gov. of Hong Kong, May, 1907; gov., N. and S. Nigeria, Mar., 1912; gov.-gen., Nigeria, Dec., 1913; assumed govt., Jan., 1914; retired, 1919; Br. mem., Permanent Mandates comnr. since 1922; author of "The Dual Mandate in Br. Tropical Africa" and other works.

LUGG, HARRY CAMP.—B. 1882; ed. Greytown; elk. and Zulu interp., mag.'s office, Bulwer, Dec., 1899; Msinga, Jan., 1901; Greytown, Feb., 1902; elk., civ. comm., Zululand, Aug., 1903; native affrs., Natal, Nov., 1903; Zulu interp. and astt. regisr., native high ct., July, 1908; sworn Zulu interp., sup. ct., Aug., 1909; sen. clk. and astt. mag., Durban, Dec., 1916; mag., Ndewede, Dec., 1918; Ingwavuma, May, 1920; Mapumulo, Aug., 1921; 2nd grade mag., Richmond, Apr., 1923; 1st grade mag., Verulam July, 1928; senr. mag., Verulam, Aug., 1930; pres., native appl. ct., Natal and Transvaal, Oct., 1932; ch. native comanr., Natal, May, 1933.

LUKE, Sir HARRY CHARLES, Kt. Bach. C.M.G. (1926), Knight of Justice of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1884; ed. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mod. hist., B.A., 1906; M.A., 1910; J. Litt., 1919; priv. sec. to the govt. of Sierra Leone 1908, and A.D.C., 1909; agt. astt. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Mar. to July, 1909; priv. sec. to the govt. of Barbados, Feb., 1911; attached to C.O., May to July, 1911 (Coronation medal); priv. sec. to the high comanr. of Cyprus (Sir H. Gooch-Archer, G.C.M.G.), and clk. of exec. coun., Oct., 1912; astt. sec. to govt., Cyprus, 1912; astt. sec., Paphos, 1917; comanr., Famagusta, 1918; Cyprus defence comte., Aug., 1914; H.M.S. Doris on Syrian Coast, 1914-1915; polit. offr. to Admirals Lord Wester Wemyss and Sir Ian and govt. sec., Mudros, Feb., 1915 to Feb., 1916; (lieut.-comdr., and ag. comdr., R.N.V.R.) recd. thanks of admry.; Italian med. for valour; political offr. to Admiral of the Fleet Sir J. de Robeck, Constantinople and Black Sea, Aug., 1919; Br. ch. comsnr. in Geor. Armenia and Azerbaijan, Apr. to Sept., 1920; astt. gov. of Jerusalem, Oct., 1920; comanr. on the Orthodox Patriarchate of Jerusalem, and on the Jaffa disturbances, 1921; finan. comanr. of the Patriarchate, 1921-24; sec., Sierra Leone, May, 1924; ag. sec. on occasions; ch. sec., Palestine, July, 1928; gov., Malta, Aug., 1930; passed in Turkish modern Greek (lang.); author of "A Bibliography of Sierra Leone," 1910 and 1925; "Cyprus and the Turks," 1921; joint editor, "Handbook of Cyprus," and "Handbook of Palestine and Transjordan."

LUKE, S. E. MARTIN, FREDERICK JOHN, M.A., Ph.D. (Cantab.), F.I.C.—B. 1891; nat. sci. tripes pt. 1 and agril. dipl.; war serv., 1914-19; active serv., France, 1918-17; demob., rank of capt. (flight commdr.), R.A.F., 1919; genr. asst. gov't. chem., Sudan govt., 1919-24; agril. chem., Sierra Leone, 1924; asst. dir., agr., 1930; dir., agr., 1934.

MARTIN, JOHN MILLER, B.A.—B. 1804; ed. Edinburgh Acad. and Oxford; class. schol., Corpus Christi Coll., 1923-27; 1st cl. class. moda., 1924; B.A., 1927; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., Doms. Office, 2nd Nov., 1927; seconded to Malayan civ. serv., 1st Dec., 1931; pvt. sec. to perm. under-sec., D.O. (Sir Edward Harding), 21st Nov., 1934; prin., C.O., 21st Oct., 1935; sec., royal comm. on disturbances in Palestine, 1936.

MARTIN, KENNETH SEFTON, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1888; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1911; supervisor, cust., 2nd grade, 1911; 1st grade, 1919; senr. collr., 1929; asst. compt., 1933; ag. compt. on various occasions, 1931-35.
MARTIN, LEWIS VERNON.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 17th Mar., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Jan., 1936.
MARTIN, HON. WILLIAM LEE.—B. 1870; ed. Waimea High Schl.; mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1927-31 and re-elected, 1933; min. of agr., 1935.

MARTINDALE, MILES HAY, Barrister-at-Law (Middle Temple).—B. 1893; asst. dist. offr., 1919; pvt. sec. to gov't., 1920; asst. sec., S.P. secret., 1923; ag. dist. offr., 1924-27; dist. offr., 1926; ag. crown coun. and ag. ch. registr., 1933; asst. judge, high ct., Prot., 1934.

MARTYN, CHARLES DARK.—B. 1889; cadet, N. Borneo, Sept., 1912, asst. prot., lab., Feb., 1916; asst. dist. offr., Mar., 1916; dist. offr., June, 1921; asst. gov't. sec., Mar., 1923; prot. dept. and sect. Chinese affrs., also lands dept., for various periods, Oct., 1923 to Oct., 1932; prot. lab. and sec. for chinese affrs., Oct., 1932; commr., lands, in addn. until Dec., 1932; mag., 1st cls., Jan., 1933; ag. res. and offl. mem., leg. coun., Feb., 1933; commr., lands, prot., lab. and sec. for Chinese affrs. in addn., May, 1934; ag. res. and offl. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1934; res. and ag. judge, high ct., Sept., 1936.

MARTYN, ELDER BRIDGEMAN, B.A., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1903; ed. Marlborough Coll., Oriel Coll., Oxford and Imp. Col. Sci. & Tech.; C.O. scholarship courses in mycology, Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, and Imp. Col. Trop. Agr., Trinidad; botanist mycologist, dept. agr., Br. Guiana, 1928.

MARWICK, ALLAN GRAHAM, O.B.E. (1925).—B. 1877; served in Natal div. serv., 1895 to 1899; served Anglo-Boer war, 1899-1901; sub. native comsnr., Mbabane, Swaziland, Jan., 1903 to Mar., 1907; asst. comsnr., ditto, Mar., 1907; asst. comsnr., Hlatikulu, Swaziland, Aug., 1916; mem. Swaziland-Portuguese bdry. coman., 1920; asst. comsnr., Mbabane and ag. dep. res. coman., Oct., 1932; dep. res. comsnr., 1933; res. comsnr., 1935.

MASON, GERALD BOYELL.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; matric., London Univ., 1890; joined London hosp., 1890; passed prelim. scientific, 1892; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1896, certif., London Schi. of Trop. Med., 1909; D.P.H., Cambridge, 1917; med. offr., No. 4 dist., Antigua, 1903; ag. comsnr., Virgin Is., 1904; surg. lieut., Antigua def. force, 1904; mag. and med. offr., Anguilla and dep. judge of sup. et., Leeward Is., Jan., 1905 to Sept., 1906; med. offr., No. 6 dist., Nevis, 1906; med. offr., dist. “D”, and mag. dist. “F”, Dominica, 1907; med. offr., St. Vincent, 1909-12; temp. aspt., R.A.M.C.; med. offr. in charge of troops, Cambridge, 1916-19; founder and hon. sec., W. Indian Club, 1897-8; founder and hon. sec., West India Red Cross Socy., 1921-1924; dir., med. and health dept., Bermuda, Aug., 1924.

MASON, HON. HENRY GREATHEAD REX, M.A., LL.B. (N.Z.).—B. 1885; ed. Clyde Quay Schi., Wellington Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; mem., N.Z. H. of R. since 1926; atty.—gen. and min. of just., 1935.

MASSIE, HON. VINCENT.—B. 1887; ed. St. Andrew’s Coll., Toronto, Univ. of Toronto and Balliol Coll., Oxford, (M.A.); lect., mod. hist., Univ. of Toronto, 1913-15; in ch. musketry, mil. dist. No. 2 (Canada), 1915-18 (ment.); temp. lieut. col., 1916; assoc. sec., war comittee of cabinet (Ottawa), Jan.-Dec., 1918; sec., govt. repatriation comittee, and later dir., 1918-19; minr. without portfolio in Dominion cabinet, 1925; attended Imp. Conf., London, 1926; H.M. envoy extraordinary and min. plenipotentiary for Canada to U.S.A., 1926-30; high comsnr. for Canada in London, 1930.

MASSON, GERALD GUSTAVE—B. 1888; dipl. of agr., Roseworthy Agr. Coll., S. Australia; served in Great War with 9th Australian Light Horse Regt., 1915-19; Gallipoli, Sinai, Palestine and Syria; ment. in desps.; inspr., agr., occupied enemy territory adminstr., 1919; inspr., agr., N. Palestine, 1920; inspr., agr. and forests, 1924; ch. agr. offr., 1930; sec., comittee of agronomy, gen. agr. coun., Palestine.

MASTERTON-SMITH, SIR JAMES EDWARD, K.O.B. (1919), C.B. (1915).—B. 1878; ed. Harrow (schol.) and Hartford Coll., Oxford (schol.); ent. home civ. serv. (cls. L.), admity., 1901; priv. sec. to 2nd sea. lord, 1904-8; priv. sec. to perm. sec., 1908-10; priv. sec. to successive 1st lords of the admity., 1910-17; asst. sec., min. of munitions, Sep., 1917 to Jan., 1919; asst. sec. (adm.), W.O. and Air Min., 1919-20; joint perm. sec., miny. of labour, 1920; perm. under-sea. of state for the colonies, 30th Aug., 1921; sec. to the Order of St. Michael and St. George; mem. of the offi. side of the National Whitley Coun.; ret., 1925; Offr. of the Belgian Order of the Crown.

MASTERTON-SMITH, PERCY FRANK, M.C.—B. 1896; ed. Christ’s Hosp. and Wadham Coll., Oxford (hons. med. hist.); B.A., 1921; R. Fusiliers, 1914-16; 60th Rifles, 1916-19; M.C., 1917; capt., res. of Offrs., 60th Rifles; admin. offr., Nigeria, 25th Jan., 1922.
MATEER, EDWARD JOHN.—Ed. Foyle Coll. and Taunton Schi.; asst. treas., Uganda, July 1920; senr. asmt. treas., 1931; prim. asmt. treas., 1935.

MATHER, NORMAN FREDERICK HUGH, B.A., (Oxon).—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylor’s Schi., and Hartford Coll., Oxford (schol.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; served in Rifle Brig. Dec., 1915-Sept., 1919; asst. sec. to res. Selangor, Nov., 1919; asst. dist. offr., May, 1921; registr., war serv. land grant scheme, S. Feb., 1922; sec. to res., Pahang, Dec., 1923; mag., Seremban, July, 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; chmn., sany. bd., Seremban, Aug., 1926; cls. II, Nov., 1930; asst. advr., Segamat, May, 1928; dist. offr., Larut and regitr., teles., Perak, Mar., 1932; chmn., govt. rice mill in addn., May, 1933; ag. comsnr., trade and cust., Johore, Sept., 1935.

MATHERS, FREDERICK FRANCIS, K.C., LL.B.—B. 1871; ed. schla. in Halifax, Nova Scotia, Dalhousie law schl. and Harvard law schl.; called to the bar, 1892; dep. prov. sec. and clk. of exec. coun., N.S., 1902; also regitr. of joint stock companies, 1909; now deputy atty.-gen.

MATHESON, CAPT. C. L. T., M.C.—Commissioned in Royal Engineers, 1909; asst. dir. of surv., G. Coast, 1914; Togoland campaign, 1914; Cameroon, 1914-15; France with Field Coy. R.E., 1916-17; W.O., 1918; dep. dir. topographical surveys, G. Coast, 1920.

MATHEW, CHARLES.—B. 1903; ed. Downside schl. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Lincoln’s Inn, 1927; cadet, admin. Nigeria, 1929; asst. dist. offr., 1932; ag. pol. mag., crown coun., and offi. admtr. on various occasions, 1930-33; mag., Uganda, 1933; ag. mag., Palestine, but did not proceed; crown coun., Uganda, 1936; ag. solr.-gen., Nov., 1935-Dec., 1936.

MATHEWS, ARNOLD BURNETT.—B. 1889; ed. Shrewsbury (schl.) and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (cls. schl.); sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1917; 2nd cls., Pt. I, classical tripes, 1920; M. 2nd cls., anthropological tripes, 1922; asst. d. offr., N.P. Nigeria, Jan., 1923; asst. sec., N. secretariat, Feb., 1925; asst. sec., Nig. secretariat, Aug., 1927; clk. to exec. and couns., Apr.-Sept., 1928; stdt., C.O., July, 1929; asst. sec., Nigerian secret., Sept., 1929; dist. offr., N. Prov., Aug., 1930; dist. offr., 1931; sp. duty reorganization pagan admtr.

MATHIESON, Hon. John Alexander, K.C.—B. 1863; ed. Harrington Gram. Sch., Prince of Wales' Coll., Taught Sch., Desable Gram. Sch., P.E.I., read law with McLean and Macdonald; called to the P.E.I. bar, 1893; K.C., 1903; el. to legislature for King's county, 1900; re-el., 1904, 1908 and 1912; leader of the opposition, 1903; premier and atty.-gen., 2nd Dec., 1911; ch. just., P.E.I., 13th June, 1917.

MATHIESON, Joseph George, B.L. (Glasgow)—B. 1907; ast. admstr. gen., Zanzibar, 1930; ag. munici. and land offr., Apr., 1934; munici. and land offr., Sept., 1934; ag. admstr. gen., pub. trustee, regis. docs. in addn., Apr., 1935; ast. admstr. gen. and dep. offr. rec., Uganda, 1936.

MATTHEWS, Ernest Lewis, K.C., C.M.G. (1914).—Called to the bar, Inner Temple; law adviser to Transvaal govt., June, 1902; law adviser to govt. of Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1910; puisne judge, Natal prov. divn., 1926.

MATTHEWS, Leslie Gadd.—Ed. Plymouth. Oxford Univ. O.T.C., 1915; O.A.S., France, 1916 and 1917, Lanc. Fusiliers; Mesopotamia, 1917-21, attd., Indian Army and Iraq Levies; Arab rebellion, Iraq, 1920 (med. and clasp); astt. treas., Somaliland, June, 1921; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1923; ast. commr., pol., Nigeria, 1926.

MAUDE, Henry Evans.—B. 1906; ed. Highgate Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1929; M.A., 1933; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1929; dep. commr., 1931; ag. native lands commr., 1931; admstr. offr., 1932; ag. sec. to govt., Mar.-June, 1933; nat. lands commr., Gilbert Is., 1933; rep. Gilbert & Ellice Is. at confce. and seminar on educn. in Pacific countries, Honolulu, July-Aug., 1936; ast. dist. offr., Zanzibar, Sept., 1936.

MAULE, John Patrick.—B. 1906; ed. Berkhamsted schl. and Sidney Sussex coll., Cambridge; M.A., 1932; dipl., agr., 1929; A.I.C.T.A. (Trinidad), 1930; astt., agr., Nigeria, 1930; man., govt. stock farm, Cyprus, 1934.

MAYROGORDATO, Col. Arthur Stephen, O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1886; ed. at Oakham Schl., Rutland; clk., pol. headqrs., Cyprus, Nov., 1903; inspr., mil. pol., Cyprus, May, 1905; in ch. pol. detachment, Kyrena, 1907-1909; gov't., prison and dep. coroner; in command of Papho dist. several occasions; detailed for sp. duty in Larnaca dist. in connection with Limassol riots, May-June, 1912; ast. commr., pol., Sierra Leone, June, 1913; J.P. and dep. coroner, 1913; ag. commr., pol., also sheriff, prison visiting just. and gov't. nominee on city coun. of Freetown, and performing duties of marshal of the Adny. prize ct., on various occasions, 1916-18; ag. harbmr. and detaining offr. under mil. authorities, in 1916, 1917, 1918 and 1919; ag. dist. commr. in headqrs. dist., S. Leone, Feb.-Mar., 1919; dep. inspr.-gen., pol. and prisons, Palestine, Dec., 1922; ag. inspr.-gen., pol. and prisons, Apr., 1924; commit., ditto, Apr., 1926; rep. Palestine govt. at internat. pol. confce., Antwerp, Sept., 1930; inspr.-gen., constab., Trinidad, July, 1931; mem., leg. coun.; awarded King's Pol. med., 1934.

MAXWELL, Sir Wm. George, K.B.E. (1924), C.M.G. (1915); Royal Humane Society's med. (1882).—B. 1871; ed. at Clifton Coll.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temp.; jun. offr., Perak, F.M.S., Mar., 1891 ast. mag., Kinta, Apr., 1892; registr. of cts., Kinta, Perak, Feb., 1899; dist. offr., Dindings, Aug., 1900; dep. pub. pros., Singapore, 1906; sol.-gen., S. Settmts., Aug., 1906; British adviser to govt. of Kedah, July, 1909; ag. col. sec., S. Stiltns., 1914; ag. sec. to high commr., Malay States and Brunei, 1915; ag. British res., Perak, Dec., 1916 to May, 1917; ag. sec. to high commr., Malay States and Brunei, June, 1917; vice-chmn., food control comtees., S.S. and F.M.S.; vice-chmn., shipping control comtees., S.S.; pres., rubber commr., 1918; pres., Singapore housing commr.; pres., Singapore centenary comte.; general adviser to govt., Johore, June, 1918; ag. col. sec., S. Stiltns., Dec., 1918; Br. res., Perak, Dec., 1919; ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Sept., 1920; ret., 1926; publications, "Perak Land Laws (past and present)," "The Laws of Perak, 1877-1903;" "The Laws of the S. Stiltns., 1827-1907;" "In Malay Forests."

MAXWELL-ANDERSON, Sir Maxwell Hendry.—Kt. Bach. (1934), C.B.E. (1919); O.B.E. (1918); K.C., Aug., 1919; capt., R.N. (ret.).—Ent. Royal Navy, 1893; ret. at own request, 1912; recd. thanks of Admy. for series of magnetic observations carried out in the Pacific Ocean; inst., R.N. Coll., Portsmouth, 1908-10; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1909; younger brother of Trinity House, 1910; rejoined Navy on outbreak of war; promoted capt., Apr., 1919; govt. mem., central (unemployed) body for London, 1913 to 1919; astt.-gen., Gibraltar, May, 1919; ag. chief just., and col. sec. on several occasions; ch. just., Fiji and judl. commr., W. Pacific, 1929; ag. high commr., W. Pacific, June to Aug., 1930; ret., 1936.

MAY, Henry O'Hara, M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Univ. Dubl.), D.P.H. (Irel.).—B. 1878; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Apr., 1907; med. offr., health, Nov., 1911; junr. sanry. offr., Aug., 1916; dep. dir., sanry. serv., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1922; dep. dir., health serv., Gold Coast, June, 1926.

MAY, Thomas William Vivian, A.R.I.B.A., M.I.Struct.E., archt.—B. 1889; ast. in prim. archt.'s dept. of H.M.O.W., 1909-14; war serv., Aug., 1914; Aug., 1919; ast. archt., Spore, Harbr. Bd., Nov., 1919; ast. archt., P.W.D., May, 1924; ast. archt., Spore, July, 1933; state archt., Johore, Mar., 1934.

MAYBIN, John Alexander, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1889; M.A. (Edin.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1914; attached to the Badulla kach, Jan., 1914; ag. office astt. to govt. agt., July, 1914, and Jan., 1915; addtl. dist. judge and pol. mag., Badulla in addn. June, 1915; pol. mag., Panadura, Mar., 1916; on mil'y. duty, 1917; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, July, 1919; 2nd finan. astt. to chmn., mun. coun., Colombo, Mar., 1920; finan. astt., Colombo Municipality, Nov., 1920; addnl. ast. col. sec., Jan., 1922; landing survr., Colombo cust., Mar., 1923; 3rd ast. col. sec., Feb., 1924; 2nd ast. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun., Mar., 1925; ast. regiar., co-op. socs., Apr., 1928; dep. regiar., co-op. socs., Sept., 1932; ch. sec., Nigeria, 1934; O.A.G., Dec., 1934 and June-Oct., 1935.

MAYHEW, Arthur Innes, C.M.G. (1936), C.I.E.—B. 1878; ed. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford (schl.); B.A., 1901; Berlin and Jena Univs. ent. Indian educn. serv., 1903; educn. adviser, Hyderbad State, 1910; dir., pub. instrn., Cent. Prove, 1913; ag. educn. commr. to govt. of India, 1921; mem., Eton Coll. staff, 1922-29; joint sec. to advisory comte. on educn. in the C.O., 1929; author of "The Education of India," "Christianity and the Government of India."

MAYLE, NORMAN LESLIE.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Oct., 1917; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted to clk. of the 2nd div., Feb., 1920; on mil serv. from Sept., 1917 to Sept., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 7th July, 1923; ast. prin., 16th Nov., 1928; ag. pvt. sec. to parl. U.S. of S. for the Cola., 2nd Feb., 1932; pvt. sec., do., 18th Apr., 1932; ag. prin., 1st Jan., 1936.
MAYNARD, PRYCE.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 15th Oct., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 3rd Mar., 1937.

MEAD, JOHN PHILLIPS.—B. 1886; dip. forestry (Oxon); ed. Charterhouse Sch.; Royal Indian Engrg. Coll. (Coopers Hill) and Exeter Coll., Oxford; ast. cons. forests and ag. dep. cons. forests, Aug., 1907-May, 1916; on mil serv. 1916-19; inspr., forests Kordofan prov., Soudan, Aug., 1918; conserv., forests, Sarawak, 1919; temp. forestry advr. to govt., Fiji, Nov., 1926; ast. cons., forests, F.M.S., Jan., 1928; dep. cons. forests, F.M.S., March, 1928; dir., forestry, F.M.S. and S.S., Jan., 1930.

MEADE, CHARLES RICHARD.—Copyist, treasy. and post office dept., Montserrat, Oct., 1907; 5th clk., Apr., 1912; 4th clk., Nov., 1912; gov't. offr., Sandy Point, St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1915; clk., treasy., Virgin Is., May, 1917; clk., exec. council; prin., Apr., 1927; prin., treasy., Dominica, Dec., 1928; ag. treasy., mem. exec. and leg. couns. and chmn., commrs. of income tax in 1931 and 1932; ast. treasy. and postmtr., Montserrat, Dec., 1933.

MEADE, CAPT. JOSEPH MICHAEL, B.A. (T.C.D.). Barrister-at-Law (King's Inns, Dublin).—B. 1889; European mast., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1920; ast. mast., King Edward VII schl., Taiping, Feb., 1921; ast. inspr., schls., Perak, Jan., 1923, Sept., 1925 and Apr., 1929; inspr., schls., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1926; ast. inspr., schls., Spore, Oct., 1930; ag. inspr., schls., Malacca, Jan. 1931; senr. educn. offr. (E), Nov., 1931; ag. supt., educn., Johore, Jan., 1934.
MEADEN, HENRY, M.B.E. (1931).—B. 1882; ast. warden, Tobago, 1919; ast. warden, St. Patrick, 1922; mag. warden, Tobago, 1st Apr., 1926.
MEARNS, JOHN WILLIAM, B.Sc. (Engng.), Glasgow, A.M.I.Struct.E., A.M.I.C.E.—B.1894; ast. engr., sea defences, Br. Guiana, 1920; do., pub. wks. dept., 1922; dist. engr., 1926.

MEDWAY, F. W.—Imp. serv. (acctnt-gen.'s dept., G.P.O.), 1901-2; clk., G.P.O., Bloemfontein, Nov., 1902; clk., treasy., O.F.S., May, 1908; senr. clk., treasy., Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; senr. clk., inland rev. dept., Dec., 1916; prin. clk., Apr., 1917; oh. clk., April, 1928; recr., Pietermaritzburg, Apr., 1933.
MEEK, A. I., L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., D.P.H. (Edin.), L.R.F.P. & S. (Glas.).—B. 1889; R.A.M.C., Aug., 1918 to May, 1920; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1922; senr. health offr., Apr., 1930.

MEGARRY, THOMAS, B.A.—B. 1898; ed. Royal Academical Inst., Belfast and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (1st cl. hons., classics and mental and moral philosophy); cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1922; passed cadet, Dec., 1924; ast. to sec., Chinese affrs., 1925; ast. head, sany. dept., Mar., 1928; 3rd ast. col. sec., June, 1928; sec., currency comttee, in addn., 1930; mag. and dist. offr., N. district, New Territories, Mar., 1931; head sany. dept. and pres., sany. bd., Jan., 1933; dist. N. dist., New Territories, Jan., 1934.

MEGAW, ARTHUR HUBERT STANLEY, M. (Cantab.).—B. 1910; ed. Campbell coll., Puke and Peterhouse, Cambridge; Walstoned, 1913; ast. dir., Br. schl. of archaeology, Athens, Oct., 1935; ag. dir., May, 1936; dir. antiquities, Cyprus, July, 1936.

MEGSON, JOHN.—B. 1895; apptd. dir. compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil service temporarily assigned to Board of Agric., Jul., 1919; Home Office, Aug., 1914; on military serv., Nov., 1916 to Mar., 1919; Home Office, May-Apr., 1919; assigned to C.O., Apl., 1919; elec. higher grade, 9th Jan., 1923; staff offr., Oct., 1930.

MEIGHEN, Rt. Hon. ARTHUR P.C., K.C.B. 1874; ed. St. Mary's Coll. Inst. and Torr. Univ.; B.A., 1896; Ist. at H. of C., Can., g.e., 1908; re-el., 1911, 1913 and 1917; defe., 1921; el. by-el., 1922; re-el., 1926; sol., 1913; re-el. acl., 1913; p.c. (Can.), 1915; min. of state and min. of mines (Can.), 1917; last name year, min. of interior and sup. gen. affrs.; prime min. of Canada, 1920; p.c. (E.), 1920; prime min., Can., 1928; del., 1927; min. senate, 1932; min. without portfolio in Ben cabinet and govt. leader in senate, 1933; less of oppsn., senate, 1935.

MELLISH, HON. HUMPHREY.—B. 1882; Mount Mellick Schl., Prince of Wales Rd., Charlottetown, 1876-79 (holder of Govt. scholarship for County of Queen's P.E.I., 1878; medallist, classics and maths, 1879); Dalhousie Univ., A.B., 1882, LL.B.; matriculant, Univ. Lond., hons. div., 1883; called to N.S. bar, K.C., 1904; practised law, 1891-1918; N.S. Barristers' Soc., 1912-13; agt. comm. min. of just. in N.S., 1903-04; judge, N.S., 1918, also surrogate, and dep. loco. adm., for N.S. dist., 1920; local polit. adm., 1921.

MELVILLE, EUGENE.—B. 1911; ed. Queen Park Schl., Glasgow and United Coll., St. Andrews; Harkness res. schol., 1929-33; ast. hons. classics, 1933; 1st cl. hons. econ. and moral philosophy, 1935; ast. inspr., treasy., apptd., after compet. exam., ast. prin., C., Jan., 1936.

MENZIES, Rt. Hon. ROBERT G. P.C. (1937), K.C., LL.M., M.P., M.I.C. V., 1928-32; min. without portfolio, 1928-29; min. for Nunawading, Victoria, 1932-34; ast. min. for rly. and dep. premier; sl. M.H. C'wealth of Australia for Kooyong (Vic.), atty.-gen. and min. for industry, Lyons, 1934.

MERCIECA, PROF. SIR STEPHEN ARTHUR Bach, (1926), M.A., L.L.D.—B. 1878; Malta Univ., 1894; M.A., 1897; LL.D., 1915; ast. of coun. of govt., 1907-10; ast. crown adv., 1915; prof. of law, 1915; crown advocate, judge, 1921; ch. just., 1924; ex. in law, 1926.

MEREWETHER, SIR EDWARD K.C.M.G.(1916); K.C.V.O.(1907); C.V.O. (1902); B.1858; ed. Harrow; ca., Oct., 1880; confirmed, Jan., 1883; Malta, with H.M.S. "Pegasus" to Tenom in c., with the "Nisero" case, Tenom in c., Dindings, 1886; 2nd ast. col. sec., jun., 1891; ast. col. sec., jun., 1891; ast. coll. of land rev., and in charge of Malacca, July, 1892; inspr. of prisons, ast. coll. sec. and clk. of couns., May, 1892; res., Selangor, Dec., 1901; lieut.-gov.,

MIDDEBROOK, STANLEY MUSGRAVE.—B. 1898; ed. Bradford Gram. Sch., and St. Catharine's Coll., Camb. (open exhibn., mod. history); war serv., 1917-19; cadet, S.S. Apr., 1921; offr., cls. V., Apr., 1924; asst. audr., Chinese, Nov., 1924; admst., gov't. monops. dept., S'pore, Nov., 1927; offr., cls. IV., Apr., 1928; asst. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang; do., Muar, Apr., 1931; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., F.M.S., July, 1932; sec., uniform licences and fees commt., (road and rail transport, Malaya), Aug., 1932; offr., cls. III., Apr., 1933, asst. prot., Chinese, S'pore; asst. dir., educn., Chinese schls., F.M.S., Aug., 1934; prot., Chinese, Johore, Oct., 1936.
MIDDLETON, I. C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (Liv.)—B. 1899; mily. serv., 1917-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1929.

MIDDLETON, SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1931), K.B.E. (1924). O.M.G. (1916).—B. 1870; ed. Sedburgh and Univ. of Edin.; M.A.; asst. dist. comntr., S.
