MORICE, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1887; Ampleforth Coll., York; Br. P.O., Jan., war serv., 1914-19; lieut., R.E., Sept.; asst. teleg. engrnr., P. & T., F.M.S. Nov.; teleg. engrnr., Johore, June, 1921; engrnr., F.M.S., Oct., 1926; do., Penang, Mar., 1927; engrnr., P. & T., S.S. & F.M.S., Dec., 1927.

MORLEY, ARTHUR HAROLD, M.B. (Leeds), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Ed.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (Liverpool), D.P.H.—B. 1887; ed. Leeds Gen. Infirmary; house surg., Gen. Infirmary, 1926; ditto, Northampton Infirmary, 1927; med. offr., Somaliland.

MORPHY, HENRY, B.A. (lst cls. hon.), M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., F.S.I.—B. 1889; ed. Coll., Dublin (senr. moderator), gold med. men's sci., etc.; 2nd grade survr., Nigeria, M. senr. survr., 1921; passed lower Hausa Arabic; asst. survr.-gen. (superannuated) added to Kano native admntr., 1928.

MORRIS, EDWARD GILBERT, O.B.E., B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1884; ed. Bedford and Hertford Coll., Oxford (exhibitor); asst. comnr., Uganda, 1908; dist. comm., lieut., unattached E.A. Forces, 1914; Uganda T.C., 1916; dist. pol. offr., 1919; resigned, 1920; re-app., 2nd cls. offr., Uganda, 1924; labour comm., 1925; schls., 1925; ag. dir., educ., 1927.

MORTON, STANLEY PARK.—B. 1894; ed. Allen's Endowed Schl. and Armstrong Coll., Newcastle-on-Tyne; war serv., 1914-19; U.K., G.P.O., Feb., 1914; ast. divanl. engr., S.S., Mar., 1921; engrnr., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., Oct., 1926; engr., S'pore, Nov., 1927; sr. engr., Aug., 1936.

MOSELEY, FRANCIS ARNOLD.—B. 1883; ed. Perth, W. Australia and Brasenose Coll., Oxon.; B.A. 1904; called to bar, Middle Term., May, 1905; judge's associate and clk. of arraigns, W. Aust., Oct., 1905; mily. serv., Australia, 1915-16; France, 1916-19; Ireland, 1920-22; M.C., 1919; ast. regisr., titles, Tanganyika Territory, 1922; ag. regisr., titles, in 1922, 1923, 1924 and 1925; ag. solr-gen. for various periods in 1922, 1923 and 1927-32; dep. land offr., Mar., 1926; crown counr., 1926; atty.-gen., Nyasaland Prot., 1933; ag. judge, Nov., 1933 to Dec., 1934; puisne just., Ceylon, 1936.
MOSS, ALBERT JAMES ROBERT, FT. LINTOT, R.A.F.O.—B. 1898; supt., aerodrome, Hong Kong, 1930.
MOTHERSILL, LAWRENCE JOHN.—B. 1899; ed. Bedford Schl. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; regular army, The Royal Scots, 1918-28; cadet, Gold Coast, 1929; ast. dist. commr., 1930.

MOTTISTONE, 1st Baron (cr. 1933), MAJOR-GEN. RT. HON. JOHN EDWARD BERNARD SEELY, P.C. (1909), C.B. (1916), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1900).—Ed. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; lieut.-col. commdg. Hampshire Carabiniers; served with Imperial Yeomanry in S. Africa, 1900-1901 (desig., Queen's medal, five clasps, D.S.O.); M.P. for Isle of Wight, 1900-06; M.P. for Aberconwy div., Liverpool, 1906-1910; M.P., Ilkeston div., Derbyshire, 1910; recd. gold medal (French gov.) for saving life at sea, 1891; parly. under-sec. of state for the colonies, 1905; chmn. of comtee. to inquire into organisation of crown agents office, 1908; under-sec. of state for war, 1911; sec. of state for war, 1912-14; joint parly. sec. to ministry of munitions, 1918; under-sec. of state for air, Jan., 1919.

MOUBRAY, GEORGE ALEXANDER DE CHAZAL DE.—B. 1888; ed. Loretto Schl., Manchester Univ. (civil engnr.), Zurich Polytechnikum and Univ. (pure science); cadet, F.M.S., 1912; offr. cls. V, Jan., 1916; do., cls. IV, Dec., 1919; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., F.M.S., Aug., 1920; regisr., titles and town planning admstr., Selangor, in addn., July, 1923; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1924; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929; 1st mag., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1930; offr., cls. I B, dist. offr., L. Perak, Oct., 1933; do., Kinta, Feb., 1934; do., Lower Perak, Nov., 1934.

MOUNTAIN, HAROLD GRIFFITHS, M. Inst., M.M.—B. 1887; ed. at King's Schl., Worcester, and Schl. of Mines, Camborne (1st cls. dip.); served R.E., tunnelling coys. and eng. stores dept., 1916-19; inspr., mines, Cyprus, Oct., 1925; ag. water engnr., July-Oct., 1930; inspr., lab. and regisr., trade unions, Jan., 1933; sec. for mines, Gold Coast, 1934.

MUCHMORE, ALFRED, A.L.A.A.—B. 1893; Br. postal serv., 1912-1920; Civ. Serv. Rifles and R.A.F., 1914-19; acot., post and tels., Tanganyika territory, 1920; ast. treas., 1923; senr. ast. treas., 1931; ag. dep. treas., for various periods, 1933-36; finance offr., Aden, 1936.

MUIR, JOHN COCHRAN.—B. 1902; ed. Kilbarchan, Campbell & Allan Glens schls., Glasgow Univ. and West of Scotland Agrl. Coll.; B.Sc., (Agr.) N.D.A., N.D.D., post-grad. course, Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agr., Trinidad; ast. supt., agr., Gold Coast, 1925; senr. agrl. offr., Zanzibar, 1935.

MULHALL, JOHN ARCHIBALD.—B. 1899; on mily. serv., 1916; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd. Kalutara kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Puttalam kach., Nov., 1922; extra office ast., Badulla kach., Oct., 1923; office ast., ditto, Jan., 1924; office ast., Galle kach., June, 1925; ditto, Anuradhapura kach., May, 1927; addnl. ast. col. sec., Jan., 1928; ast. col. sec., May, 1928; sec. retrenchment commn., Apr., 1932; ast. ch. sec., Oct., 1933.

MULLER, JAN FREDERIK.—B. 1891; 3rd grade clk., dept. of agr., Union of S. Africa, 1909; 2nd grade clk., 1919; 1st grade clk., 1921; senr. clk., 1926; prin. clk., dept. of mines, 1928; 2nd grade ch. clk., do., 1932; 1st grade ch. clk., do., 1934; under sec., do., 1934.

MULLIN, JOHN, O.B.E. (1935), F.S.I.—B. 1878; ed. England and Wales; assayer, surveyor and mining engr.; mem., instit. of mining and metallurgy, England; cert. sworn land surv., B. Guiana, 1906; 3rd cls. offr. and surv., B. Guiana, 1908; warden, mining dist. and J.P., 1910; 1st cls. clk., dept. of lands and mines, 1911; ag. chief clk., 1911-12, 1913-14; 1st lieut., B. Guiana local forces, 1915; capt., 1918; seconded to govt. secretariat as conf. clk., 1916; chmn., canals polder, 1917-25; ch. clk., lands and mines dept., 1918; ch. clk. and ag. ast. D.P.W., 1919-20; ast. commr., lands and mines, 1922; mem. and sec., bd. of colonisation and development, 1926; ag. commr., lands and mines, chmn., local govt. bd. and mem., town council on various occasions; commr., land and mines, 1927; mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1929.

MULOCK, Rt. Hon. Sir WILLIAM, P.C. (1925), K.C.M.G. (1902), K.C., LL.D., M.A.—B. 1843; graduated at Toronto Univ., 1863; gold medal in modern languages; called to the Ontario bar, 1868; vice-chanc. of Toronto Univ. in 1881; ast. in parl. 1882 to 1905, representing N. York; postmtr.-gen. in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896; ch. just., exchequer div., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1905; ch. just., Ontario, 1923; ret., 1936.
MUNRO, THOMAS AMBROSE VESPER.—B. 1883; harbmr., Bahamas, 1st Sept., 1913; ag. marshal, admv. side, sup. ct., 1919.
MURDOCH, JAMES WILSON, M.B., Ch.B. (Aberdeen).—B. 1900; 2nd ast. med. supt., ment. hosp., T. Rambutan, F.M.S., Jan., 1928; med. supt., do., July, 1931.

MURISON, Sir James William, KT, B.A. (1919), B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1872; ed. privately and at Trin. Hall, Camb.; senr. class. scholr., 1891; 1st div., 2nd cls. class. tripes, 1894; 3rd cls. law tripes, Part II., 1895; Latham English prize, 1895; B.A., LL.B., 1895; called to Bar, Middle Temple, Apr., 1896; devilled and practised at the Chancery Bar, 1896-1902; regisr., E.A.P., Oct., 1902; town mag., Mombasa, Jan., 1904; 2nd ast. judge, H.B.M. ct. for Zanzibar, Apr., 1904; sen. ast. judge, Mar., 1905; ch. judge, Zanzibar, 20th Sept., 1914; mem., of ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1905-1910, and from 1914 upon transfer of Zanzibar to C.O.; judge, sup. ct. of H.H. Sultan of Zanzibar from 1908; arbitrator in dispute between Zanzibar and E.A.P. govts. as to H.H. mainland properties, 1911; recd. thanks of S. of S. for conversion of applied Indian Acts into Zanzibar Decrees, 1917; judge prize et. Zanzibar from 1914; passed govt. exam. in Hindustani, 1903, and in Swahili, 1904; exam. in Swahili to the Zanzibar Govt.; joint edr., Zanzibar Law Repta., 1868-1918; attr.-gen., Sttimta, 1919; ag. col. sec., June, 1920 to May, 1921; ch. just., S. Sttimta, July, 1925; rec., 1933; commr. to Palestine to inquire into Jewish-Arab riots, 1934; edited the laws of the S. Sttimta, 1926; author of "Swahili Translations," 1911.
MURPHY, Hon. Denis, B.A.—B. 1870; Ottawa Univ.; B.A.; 1892; called to the bar, Ottawa; 1896; elec. to provincial lega., British Columbia, 1900; judge of sup. ct., B. Columbia, 1909.

MURPHY, JOHN HOWARD BLACKWOOD.—B. 1895; Schl. of Physic, Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1913; R.A.M.C., Jan., 1915; India Office, Apr., 1919; A.D.C., Jubaland, Dec., 1919; dist. commr., Gosa, 1920; ditto, Choref, 1922; ditto, Ukambani, 1923; resigned, private affairs, Feb., re-apptd., commr., Somaliland, 1927; dist. offr., Kenya, 1930.

MURPHY, JOHN PELLY.—B. 1905; Mt. St. Mary's Coll., Chesterfield, E.I., LL.B., (T.C.D.); solr., high ct. of just., June, 1932; junr. legal offr., Hong Kong, Apr., 1934; ag. dep. regnr., sup. ct., Apr., 1936.
MURPHY, JOSEPH.—B. 1892; ed. Scoil, Sligo; R.L.C., 1913-22; Jamaica counc., Apr., 1922; sub-inspr., Apr., 1926; sec. inspr., Apr., 1931; King's pol. med., 1926.
MURPHY, PHILIP PAINE, B.A., M.B., B.C., B.A.O. (Dublin)—B. 1910; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; Royal City of Dublin Hosp.; dist. offr., Somaliland, Jan., 1935.
MURPHY, TIMOTHY.—B. 1882; joined Hong Kong pol., Dec., 1903; passed final exam., Cantonese, 1912; inspr., Aug., 1913; med., 1924; C.D.I., Nov., 1926; ast. sup. ct., July, 1931.

MURPHY, WILLIAM LINDSAY.—B.A., Coll., Dublin; B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1910; attd. Kandy kach., Dec., 1910; pol. mag., Colombo, Aug., 1911; ag. offic. in govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Jan., 1912; ast. to govt., Prov. of Uva, May, 1913; ast. settmtn. offr., July, 1913; ag. ast. govt., Kandy, Feb., 1918; addtl. pol. mag. and mun. mag., Colombo, Apr., 1915; ag. ast. agt., Mullaitivu, Oct., 1915; ditto, Hanwella, Nov., 1917; ditto, Matare, Oct., 1920; dist. judge, Galle, Jan., 1921; ag. ast. agt., Kegalla, May, 1921; ast. govt. agt., Sept., 1922; ast. govt. ast., Trincomalee, 1923; temp. employed, C.O., 1926; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1927; 2nd ast. col. sec., leg. coun., Feb., 1927; prtn. ast. col. clk., exec. coun., May, 1928; comptr., 1931; chmn., munie. coun. and mayor, Mar., 1932.

MURRAY, ALEXANDER ROSS, A.B.—B. 1896; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., F.W.D.K., 1923; ast. engrnr., hydrds., P.W.D.K., 1928; ast. drnge. and irrig. engrnr., drnge. and irrig. engrnr., Selangor, 1930; ast. drnge. and irrig. engrnr., Nov., 1935.

MURRAY, ARTHUR EVELYN FRANCIS (Oxon.), Barrister.—B. 1888; ed. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; ast. dist. judge, Nigeria, 1912; cls. I, grade I, admin., 1928; sec. to comm. of inquiry into mining in S.E. Provs., 1930; ag. sec. for 1930; staff grade, 1936.
MURRAY, FREDERICK.—B. 1879; town Acad., Wick, Scotland; C. 1897; prov. acct., O.F.S., Jun., Transvaal, Feb., 1935.
MURRAY, Hon. Sir George John Robert, C.M.G. (1917), B.A., LL.M.—B. 1863; judge sup. ct., S. Australia, 1912; chief just., Jan., 1916; and lieutenant-gov., Apr., 1916.

MURRAY, Sir J. H. P., K.C.M.G. (1925), M.G. (1914).—M.A., Oxon.; ed. at Sydney Univ.; schol. and won Knox prize, Fairfax prize, Univ. prize, and John West medal; went to Brighton Coll., Germany, and Univ. Coll., London; entd. Oxford Univ. and gained foundation scholarship to Magdalen Coll., 1880; 1st class mods., 1882; 1st cl. Lit. Hum., 1884; B.A., 1885; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1886; practised as a barrister in Australia until appointed crown prosecutor, N.S. Wales; acted as ass't. at judge on several occasions; commanded N.S.W. Irish Lancers, 1898; served in S. African war, 1900-1901; rec'd. comm. of major in Imperial army in recognition of his services, 1902; chief official off. and mem. of exec. and legis. councils, Cape, Sept., 1904; ag. administrator, 8th Apr., 1907; and lieutenant-gov., in addition to duties of judge, 1908.

MURRAY, Menzies.—B. 1890; ed. Stellenbosch and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (hons.); LL.B. (T.C.D.), 1914; L.B. (S.A.), 1920; war serv., 1914-19; capt., T.W. Fus., M.C.; advoc., 1921; ch. clk., patent office, Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1930; register patents and registers, companies, June 1932.
MURRAY, Robert Everitt King.—B. 1902; llk., grade II, Basutoland, 1924; sub-instr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1930.

MURTLAND, Charles Campbell.—B. 1884; d. Harrogate, York and Dublin; sub-instr. of police, B. Guiana, 1908; dist.-instr., 1912; ag. county instr., 1910-1911, 1914-1916; capt., B. Guiana militia, 1915; county instr., 1920; detective instr., 1927; ag. dep. instr.-gen., various periods, 1928-33.

MYCOCK, Charles.—B. 1890; asst. mast., ducan dept., Hong Kong, Jan., 1915; asst. able censor, 1914-16; on mil'y serv., 1917-19; adm'r. Ellis Kadodoo schl., 1919; do., Yaumati chl., 1928-30 and 1932-34; 2nd mast., Queen's coll., 1930.

MYERS, Rt. Hon. Sir Michael, P.C. (1931), K.C.M.G. (1930), K.C. (1922), LL.B. (1897)—B. 1873; ed. Thorndon schl., Wellington Coll., and Canterbury Univ. Coll. (N.Z.); in legal practice many years; ch. just., N.Z., 1929.

NAIRAC, George Edouard, K.C.—B. 1876; d. R. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, 1898; ag. dudl. subst. procureur and advoc.-gen., 1903-06; mem. for Port Louis in coun. of govt., 1911-23; nominated mem., 1926; K.C., 1926; procureur and advoc.-gen., July, 1927; mem., exec. coun. and coun. of govt.; ag. col. sec., July, 1928 to Mar., 1929; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1929 to Oct., 1930; ch. judge, 1935; edr. Mauritius Law Repts. since 1906 and of the Law Repts. Digest, 1902-25.

NAISH, Richard Bryant, B.A. (Oxon.), M.R. San. I.—B. 1891; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attached to Galle kach., Dec., 1914; addl. pol. mag., Galle in addn., Apr., 1915; extra office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Oct., 1916; pol. mag., Gampola, Dec., 1918; asst. contr. of estate supplies, May, 1919; office asst. to govt. agt., C. Prov., Feb., 1920; addl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avissawella, Sept., 1920; asst. finan. asst. to chm., mun. coun., Colombo, Jan., 1921; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1921; asst. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Mar., 1923; ditto, Karunegala, Sept., 1925; asst. contr., rev., Mar., 1928; sec., univ. comsn. in addn., July, 1928; addl. pol. mag., Colombo, Sept., 1931; dep. contr., lab., Oct., 1931; attd., atty.-gen.'s dept. for sp. duty, May, 1934.

NANKIVELL, Howard.—B. 1893; ed. Christ's Hosp.; clk., col. secretariat, Jamaica, 1911; asst., 1912; 2nd cls. air mech., R.F.C., 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.C., 1916; lieut., tech. R.A.F., 1917-1920; 2nd cls. clk., col. secretariat, Jamaica, 1917; 1st ditto, 1920; sec. salaries regrading comsn., 1922; sec., disturbances comsn., 1922; sec. advisory finan. comtee., 1924; sec. civ. serv. comtee., 1925; prin. clk., secretariat, 1925; sec. prelim. W. I. conf., 1926; attd. for temp. duty, C.O., 1926; sec., salaries comsn., Jamaica, 1928; ag. clk., privy coun., 1928; asst. col. sec., Trinidad Apr., 1929; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1929-36; dep. col. sec. Jan., 1930; sec., intercol. conference trade relations with Canada, 1930; sec., Trinidad air bd., 1930; ag. gov.'s dep., Jan., 1932 and Aug., 1934; chmn., unemployment bd., 1935; do., nutrition comtee., 1936.
NAPIER, Sir Walter John, K.T. Bach. (1909), D.C.L. (Oxon.).—Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; atty.-gen., S. Stirlings., 1907-1909; mem., W. African lands comtee., 1912.

NASH, Herbert Frederick William.—B. 1901; entered C.O., 26th Nov., 1917; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk. and assigned to C.O., 29th Dec., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher cler. offr., 17th Sept., 1929.

NASH, Hon. Walter.—B. 1882; ed. St. John's Sch.; mem., N.Z. H. of R. since 1929; min. of finance, min. of cust., min. of stamp duties, 1935, also min. of marketing, 1936; mem., national induc. conf., 1927; has represented N.Z. at various conf., overseas, including 2nd internat. lab. conf., Geneva, 1920, conf. of inst., of Pacific relations, 1927 and 1933, and conf. of Br. commonwealth relations, 1933.
NASH, Walter.—B. 1890; ed. King's Coll., London; conf. clk. to gov.-gen., Nigeria, July, 1913; adm'v. offr., 1918; res., 1932.

NASON, Wilfred Paul.—B. 1894; ed. Christ's Hosp.; served with forces, France and Russia, 1915-19; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory; do., Nigeria, 1928; audr., Leeward Is., 1932; audr., Trinidad, 1935.

NATHAN, Lt.-Col. Sir Matthew, G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1899).—B. 1862; entd. R.E., 1880; capt., 1889; maj., 1898; li.-col., 1907; served in Nile expedit., 1885; Lushai expedit. (medal with clasp), 1889; sec. col. def. comtee., 1890; adminstr. gov. of S. Leone in 1899; gov., Gold Coast, Oct., 1900; gov., Hong Kong, 21st Sept., 1903; gov., Natal, 1st May, 1907; sec. to G.P.O., London, 1910; chrmn. of bd. of inland revenue, 1911; under sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1914; permanent sec. to ministry of pensions, 1916; gov., Queensland, 1920-25.

NATTRASS, Roland Marshall, B.Sc. (Agr.), Lond., Ph.D., Lond., D.I.C., Dip. Agr., Wye.—B.1895; ed. Nottingham High Schl.; asst. to cotton specialist, Tanganyika Territory, 1922; advisory mycologist, Western Prov.; Bristol Univ., Long Ashton Research Statn., 1924; senr. mycologist, miny. of agr., Egypt, 1928; mycologist (now plant pathologist), Cyprus, 1931.

NATTRISS, Ernest Alfred, O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1882; ent. C.A. shipping agents, 1898; apptd. to C.A. office, 1912; dep. head, shipping dept., 1922; apptd. shipping offr. to the "Discovery Committee," 1927; head, shipping dept., 1933; do., gen. stores dept., 1935.

NAVARATNAM, SARAVANAMUTTU SAMUEL, B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1894; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Matara Kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Ratnapura Kach., May, 1922; extra office ast. to gvt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Sept., 1923; pol. mag., Matale, May, 1924; ditto, Chilaw, Feb., 1926; ast. comsrr., excise, Aug., 1927; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1930; ast. attlmt. offr., July, 1931; sec. to min. of lab., industry and commerce, Oct., 1931; dep. contr., lab., Aug., 1933; ast. land comsrr., Dec., 1933; ast. settlmt. offr., June, 1934.

NEAVE, JOHN RICHARD, M.C.—B. 1893; ed. King's Coll., Lond.; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; attd., res. office, Malacca, Nov., 1919; ast. advr., Kota Tinggi, Feb., 1925; res., Labuan, Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1930; ag. comsrr., lands, Trengganu, July, 1931; 2nd astt. sec. (A), S.S., Dec., 1932; ag. astt. treas. and collr., land rev., Malacca, Aug., 1933; offr. cls. II, Jan., 1935.

NEFF, ERROLL AUBREY, M.D., L.C.P. & S. (Alberta), D.P.H.—B. 1887; ed. High Schl., Ingersoll, Ont.; Toronto Univ. and Western Univ.; on war serv. with rank of major, 1914-19; dist. med. offr., Fiji, and med. supt., Cent. Leper Hosp., Makogai, Fiji, 1921; two Rockefeller foundation fellowships, 1926 and 1931; rep. S. Pacific cols. at Internat. Leprosy Confce., Manila, 1931; senr. med. offr., (now dir., med. services), Cyprus, Nov., 1934.
NEILL, J. H.—Med. offr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919; ditto, Uganda, July, 1920.

NEIL, WILLIAM HARDLEY EDWIN, F.R.G.S.—B. 1892; ed. Cathedral Sch., Winchester, Taunton's, Southampton and Hartley Univ. Coll., Extn., Southampton; war ser., Sept., 1914-Sept., 1919; survey probtrn., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; astt. supt., trig. surveys, Dec., 1923; ditto, topo. br., Dec., 1925; ditto, rev. surveys, Pahang, Jan., 1931; astt. supt., surveys, N. Kedah, Aug., 1932; senr. astt. supt., surveys, Malaya, 1935.

NEILL, JAMES SCOTT, B.A., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1889; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; clas. schol., 1911; 1st sen. mod. hist. and pol. sci., 1912; Victoria prizeman, Kings Inns., Dublin; cadet, Fiji, 1914; clk., leg. and exec. coun., Oct., 1915; dist. comsrr., 1916; regr. sup. ct. and off. recr., 1922; ag. atty. gen. and mem. exec. and leg. coun., July, 1922 to July, 1923; ag. prin. astt. col. sec. and ast. gen., imigr., Jan., 1924 and Sept., 1924; mem., bd. examrs. Fijian language and ag. editor, Indian newspaper "Radjut"; seconded sp. duty under Tongan govt. and apptd. mem., privy coun. of Tonga, Aug.-Sept., 1924; comsrr. of assize, circuit ct., June, 1925; ct. of review, income tax, Nov., 1925; Br. agt. and cons. gen., Tonga, Jan., 1927; is also a dep. comsrr. for W. Pacific and a judl. comsrr. for matrimonial causes; seconded for sp. duty, Ocean Is., Dec., 1930 to Mar., 1931.

NEILSON, HARRY ROSS, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Mod. Sch. and Aberdeen Univ.; med. offr., Uganda, 1912; temp. capt., Uganda med. staff, 1914; health offr., 1921; senr. do., 1924; ag. D.D.S.S. for various periods, 1926-32.

NEILSON, MAJOR JOHN BARRIE, M.C. and 2 Bars, M.A. (Oxon.), M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1896; ast. insur. sbls., Perak, July, 1921; ast. mast. gov't. Eng. schl., Alor Star, Aug., 1921; ar. ast. mast., Raffles Inst., Apr., 1926; inspr., schls., N. Sembilan, Sept., 1928; do., Pahang, Dec., 1930 and Jan., 1935; ag. headmr., Penang Free Schl., June, 1935.
NEISH, DONALD GORDON.—B. 1906; Bedford Schl.; 2nd lt., Jamaica loco, 1928; lt., 1930; machine gun offr., 1931-33; inspr., Jamaica constab., 1933; 3rd cl. ast., 1936.

NETTTELTON, GERALD ENRAGHT.—Ch. comsrr.'s office, 1914; sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot., pol., 1917; sp. J.R., 1917; ast. nat. tr. 1920; passed 1st gr. Seeawa exam., 1920; civ. serv. lower law exam., 1922; ag. nat. tr. res. mag. and hom. insp. Bech. Prot., 1924.

NEWBOLT, CAPT. A. F.—B. 1893; Chester and New Coll., Oxford; B.A.; civ. serv., Oxfordshire Light Infy., Aug., 1914; May, 1919; served in Flanders, wounded, 1915; capt., 1915; adjt., 1919; ast. sec. (appts.) to S. of S., Lord Milner, 1919; Mr. Winston Churchill, Feb., 1921; Lord of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; Mr. J. H. P., Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Nov., 1924; Lord Passfield, June, 1929; prim., Ist, 1930.

NEWBOULT, ALEXANDER THOMAS, M.B. 1896; ed. Kingswood and Exeter Col., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; ast. secr., Dec., 1920; offr., ola V, Aug., 1921; offr., Jelebu, Nov., 1925; offr., cl. IV, 1927; sec. to res., N. Sembilan, Dec., 1927; cls. III, Aug., 1932; ag. comsrr., lands and Trengganu, Oct., 1933.

OLIVER, WALTER REGINALD BROOKS.—F.R. Soc. N.Z.—B. 1883; ed. Taumarunui schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; cust. dept., 1900; Dom. museum nat. dir., 1928.

OLIVIER, 1ST BARON (created 1929).—Sir SYDNEY OLIVIER, P.C. (Knighted 1907), C.M.G.(1898), B.A.—B. 1859; Cambridge schl.; open exhib., Corpus Ch. Oxford; 2nd class classical mods., 1879; 3rd class in final classical schl., 1881; after compet. exam., clk. in the office S. of S. for cols., 17th Apr., 1882; notably mentioned for Cobden prize essay, 1883; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1895; 1891; ag. auditor-gen., Leeward Is., 1895 to Feb., 1896; priv. sec. to the Trimborne, Sept., 1896; sec. to the Rover to inquire into condition of W. India in 1896; 1st class clk., June, 1897; sent to W. toon on sp. serv. in connect with W. reciprocity negotiations, 1898; col. sec., 1900-1904; ag. gov. ,1900, 1902, and 1904; ed. to C.O. as a prin. clk., Oct., 1904; of Jamaica, Apr., 1907; assumed govt., 1907; represented West Indian Colonies at Coronation of H.M. King George V perm. sec., bd. of agriculture and trade, 1913; mem. of comtte. for settm. of men within the Empire, 1917; ass. treas. and audr., exchequer and audit dept., S. of S. for India, 23rd Jan. to 7th July, 1917; provost marshal's dept., 1917; pol. offr., Zanzibar, Jan., 1924; 1924-25; supt., pol., Feb., 1926; C.I.D., Nov., 1925 to May, 1926; ag. asst. commdt., 1930; A.D.C. to hon. E. Stuart-Wortley and camp commdt., 1917-18; provost marshal's dept., 1920.

ONRAET, RENÉ HENRY DE S——B. 1857; pol. prob., S. Sttltnge, Dec., 1919; final exam. in Hokkien, Aug., 1920; exam. in Malay, Aug., 1921; asst. to July, 1912; ag. asst. of pol., Singapore; asst. asst. of pol., Singapore; M.S. pol. dir. crim. intell., S.S., Nov., 1930; pol. offr., Spore, Dec., 1925; asst. dir., crim. intell., S.S., Oct., 1930. Perak, F.M.S., Oct., 1931; ag. inspr. gen., S.S., Mar., 1932; ch. pol. off., Perak, Oct.; inspr.-gen., pol., S.S., Oct., 1935.
ORLOFF, CRICIC JAMES, B.A. (Lond.)—1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1930; attd., Kalutara July, 1931; office ass't, Hambantota kach., 1932; do., N. Eliya kach., Apr., 1935.
REGAN, JOHN WILLIAM HAMILTON, B.A. (on)—B. 1913; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1930.
REILLY, HARRY ALLAN OSWALD.—B. 1893; ad to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1924 (cert. of hon.); registr. gen., Trinidad, 1934; atty.-gen. and sup. ct., St. Vincent, 1936.

REILLY, JAMES GALLUS.—B. 1883; ed. 'ges' Acad., Belfast; R.I.C., May, 1902; pr., pol., F.M.S., Dec., 1910; inspr., wghts, mars., Aug., 1911-Dec., 1912; supvr., cuat., I.S., Sept., 1921; supt., cuat. prev. br., F.M.S., Oct., 1923; supvr., ext. duties, F.M.S., i and S', ore., Nov., 1925 and Jan., 1927; oh., prev. br., cust. dept., F.M.S., Sept., 1927; supvr., govt. monops., S'pore, June, 1930; commr., trade and cust., N. Sembilan, Feb., 2; ditto, Perak, Nov., 1932; do., Selangor Pahang, Oct., 1933; ast. supt., govt. nops., S.S., Penang, Nov., 1934.

RMSBY-GORE, RT. HON. WILLIAM GEORGE THUR, P.C. (1927).—B. 1885; ed. Eton New Coll., Oxford (B.A.); late capt., Worcestershire Yeomanry; intell. officer Arab eau, 1916-17; astt. sec., War Cabinet, 1917; astt. polit. offr., Palestine, 1918; British m. Permanent Mandates Comn., L. of N., 1-22; M.P., Stafford since Jan., 1910; accompanied C.O. mission to British West Indies, 1921-22; partly under sec. of state Colonies, 1st Nov., 1922 to 23rd Jan., 4 and since Nov., 1924; chmn., partly. comn. E. Africa, 1924; apptd. by S. of S. to visit and t. on W. African dependencies, Jan., 1926; of S. for the Coms., 1936.

RR, SIR CHARLES WILLIAM JAMES, K.C.M.G. (28), C.M.G. (1921).—B. 1870; ed. Bath Coll., Woolwich Acad.; 1st comn., Royal Artill. reg., Feb., 1889; capt., Sept., 1899; maj., 14; ret., Sept., 1908, served with Chitral lef force, 1895 (medal with clasp); Indian ntier, 1897 (clasp); Tirah expdn. force, 1898 (clasp); China war, 1900-01 (medal); S. african war, 1900-02 (medal, 2 clasps); 3rd cl., N Nigeria, Feb., 1903; 2nd cl., rea., 1904; ol. res., 1908; ag. sec. to admstr., N. Nigeria, by Sept.; ch. sec., Cyprus, Feb., 1911; mstnd. govt., July to Oct., 1911, and Apr. to ig., 1913; ret. on pension on account of ill alth., Feb., 1917; astt. W.O., Int. branch, 1918; col. sec., Gibraltar, June, 1919; ag. gov., several occasions; gov. and commodr.-in-ch. of the hamsas, 1926-1931; ret.

ORR, LIONEL ALEXANDER WILLIAM.—B. 1906; St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin and Trinity Coll., Dublin, B.A., LL.B.; solr., sup. ct., I.F.S., st. coman., lands, Gold Coast, Aug., 1929 Aug., 1932; astt. admstr.-gen., N. Rhodesia, ig., 1932; ag. admstr.-gen., ag. offr. recvr., ag. gsr., high ct., ag. regist'r. of companies, ag. gsr. of patents, trade marks and designs, ag. gsr. of deeds, Mar.-June, 1934; dep. regist'r. gh.ct., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1934; regist'r., 1936.

OSBORNE, MAJOR D. R.—B. 1879; ed. since Alfred Coll., Adelaide, S. Australia; Adelaide Univ. (mental and moral phil.) and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., law; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1909; practiced at bar until 1914; on mily. serv., 1914-19; st. martial offr., Nov., 1918 to May, 1920; in Ireland as A.L.O. until Apr., 1922; stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas, June, 1923; ag. ch. just., Aug. to Sept., 1923 and from July to Nov., 1924; ag. reg-gen. in addn. June to July, 1924.

OSTLER, HON. HENRY HUBERT, K.C. (1925); LL.B. (1904).—B. 1876; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; assoc. to New Zealand ch. just., 1903-06; edr., N.Z. Law Repts., 1906-10; lst astt., crown law office and crown proc., 1910; ag. solr.-gen., 1913; in private practice, 1915-25; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1926; exam. in law for N.Z. Univ. for many years; fellow N.Z. Univ. senate, 1916-20.

O'SULLIVAN, TIMOTHY ADOLPHUS, M.A., M.R.S.T.—B. 1894; ed. King's Hosp. and Trinity coll., Dublin; astt. mast., col. for blind, Wores ter, St. Christopher's Eastbourne, Ranelagh sch., Athlone, 1913-15; Inns Ct. O.T.C. and R.A., 1915-19; astt. mast., Raffles inst., Singapore, 1919; inspr., schla., Pahang, Feb., 1922; do., Malacca, Nov., 1924; do., Selangor, Mar., 1927; senr. educon. offr. (A.), S.S., 1931; inspr., schls., Penang, Aug., 1931.

OSWELL, HENRY THEODORE WARREN.—B. 1896; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; war serv., 1915-18; cadet, F.M.S., April, 1920; passed cadet, June, 1922; offr., class V, Apr., 1923; astt. dist. offr., T. Malim, Feb., 1925; astt. contrlr., lab., Seremban, Aug., 1925; offr., ols. IV, Apr., 1927; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Nov., 1929; prot., lab., Kedah, Mar., 1930; offr., ols. III, Apr., 1932.

OTWAY, J. H., O.B.E. (1935).—Clk., treasy., Grenada, 1897; 2nd audit clk., 1899; audit clk., St. Vincent, 1901; audit clk., St. Lucia, Jan., 1916; sub-clk. and dist. offr., Soufriere; J.P. for the col.: cable censor, St. Vincent, 1918; ch. clk., govt. office, and clk. of couns., St. Vincent, 1918; ag. ch. clk. and pvt. sec. to gov., Grenada, 1923; col. treas., contr., cuat. and ch. of excise, St. Vincent, 1923; ag. admstr. in 1932 and 1933.

OWEN, ALBERT EDWARD.—Asst. acot. N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1915; astt. lands dept., Oct., 1915 to May, 1922; treasy., May, 1922; ch. clk., dept., agr., Jan., 1927; treasy., Apr., 1930; ag. dep. treas., in 1928, 1929 and 1931; contr., stores and transport, Dec., 1932.

OWEN, A. H., B.A. (Camb.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. (Camb.), Cert. of S.T.M. (Lond.)—Med. offr., Uganda, Oct., 1912; sp. serv. (venereal diseases), Uganda, 1912; active serv., E.A. Campaign, and temp. capt., U.M.S., Jan., 1915 to May, 1918; ment. in deeps.; senr. med. offr., Uganda, Jan., 1921; sen. sany. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; dep. dir., sany. serv., 1928; dir., med. and sany. services, 1932.

OWEN, GEORGE DOUGLAS, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1887; ed. Highgate Schl. (Eng.); entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, 16th Jan., 1907; 3rd clk., col.-sec.'s office, Aug., 1916; astt. sec. local defence com- tee., 1914-1915; ag. sec., ditto, 1915-1916; sec. to educn. comn., June, 1914 to Nov., 1916; sec., West Indian Court of Appeal Conf., 1916; priv. sec. to Sir S. W. Knaggs in 1910, 1912 and 1916; to Sir G. R. Le Hunte, 1913-1915; to Sir J. R. Chancellor, 1916, 1920 and 1921; served with H.M. Forces, 1916-19; ch. and conf. clk., col. secretariat, and clk. of legis. coun., May, 1920; priv. sec. to Sir T. A. V. Best, 1921 and to Sir S. Wilson, 1922-24; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec. for several periods since 1921; asst. col. sec., clk. to exec. coun., combined ct. and ct. of policy, Br. Guiana, Feb., 1925; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1925-30; col. sec., Barbados, Sept., 1931; ag. gov. in 1932, 1933 and 1935.

OWEN, HAROLD ALBERT.—B. 1892; entd. crown agt.'s office, Dec., 1911; seconded as 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, July, 1916; sec. to commn. of enquiry into condition of St. John's fire brigade, Nov., 1916; returned to crown agt.'s office, Sept., 1919.

OWEN, JOHN HENRY, D.S.C. (1917).—Younger brother, Trinity House, London; master mariner; ed. Univ. Coll. Sch., London and H.M.S. "Worcester"; served in war, 1914-18; lieut., naval reserve; port mine-sweeping offr., Jamaica, Oct., 1918; harbour and naval agt., Kingston, Jamaica, Jan., 1919; harbour, Malacca, 1927.
OWEN, WILFRID HERBERT, B.Sc.Tech., A.R.I.B.A., Chart. Arch.—B. 1896; ed. St. John's, Cheetham, Manchester, Manchester Grammar Sch., and Manchester Univ.; archt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925.
PAGE, HON. EDWARD.—B. 1877; ed. Wellington Coll., N.Z.; pvt. legal practice some years; mayor of Eketahuna, 1909-12; stip. mag., 1912-35; chmn., many inquiry commns.; judge, N.Z. ct. of arbit., 1935.

PAGE, RT. HON. EARLE CHRISTMAS GRAFTON, P.C., M.B., Ch. M. (Syd.).—B. 1880; ed. Sydney High Sch., and Sydney Univ. (George Allen Mathis Scholarship and John Harris Scholarship for anatomy and physiology); served in European War with A.I.F. as surg. specialist to No. 3 Australian casualty clearing station, France, and No. 3 Australian gen. hosp., Egypt; mayor of St. Grafton, N.S.W., 1918; pres., N. Coast Development League, 1918; cl. mem., H. of R., C. of A., for Cowper, Dec., 1919; treas., C. of A., 1923-29; leader of country party in Fedl. parlt., 1930; min. for commerce and dep. leader, Lyons miny., 1934.

PAGE, SIDNEY MAYNARD.—Transvaal constab., Oct., 1900; clk., mgte. off., July, 1902; asst. mgte., Mar., 1913; mgte., Mar., 1918; pub. serv. inspr., Nov., 1924; addnl. mag., Pretoria, May, 1928; mag., Pietersburg, Sept., 1928; ditto, Durban, Feb., 1930; do., Johannesburg, Mar., 1933.

PALETHORPE, RONALD FLINT.—B. 1891; ed. Hereford Sch., and Keble Coll., Oxford; asst. mst., St. George's Sch., Windsor Castle, 1913; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 5th Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Kenya, 1926; res. mag., 1929.

PALMER, HAROLD.—B. 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser.; apptd. to national health insurance audit dept., 13th Jan., 1913; on military service, 2nd May, 1918, to 26th Feb., 1920; assigned to C.O., 5th Mar., 1920; second asst. acctnt., 5th Nov., 1928.

PALMER, SIR HERBERT RICHMOND, K.C.M.G. (1933), C.M.G., C.B.E.—Ed. Oundle Sch. and Trinity Hall, Camb. (class. schol.), M.A., LL.B.; Farrar-at-law, Middle Temple, 1904; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1904; comsnr., native rev., N. to M.C.'s, 1911; rev. mission to Anglo-Egyptian to adminstr.; supervisor, native rev., Nigeria, 1914; May-Aug. and Prov., Nigeria, 1915-16; res., Bornu 1934; senr. ass't., 1917; visited Anglo-Egyptian 1934; ag. gov., Ru via Wadai and Darfur in con. proposals, 1918; ag. lieut., 1936.
OLDFIELD, H. geria, May-Dec., 1921; ag. res., Prot., June, 1919, to May, 1922; lieut.-gov., N.
OLIPHANT, GREY, 1925; gov., Gambia, 1930; ed. Morgan Acad., ch., Cyprus, 1933.

PALMOUR, FREDERICK JOSEPH, F.S.I.—B. 1884; ed. Daventry Sch., Queen's Coll., Oxon.; surv., Qa. Prov., 1911; valuer, valn. dept., I.R., N.H.R., ord. surv. office, Southampton; land agent, lands branch, W.O., 1928; land dept., Nigeria, 1928; land officer, Gambia, 1934.

PANES, JOHN NELSON.—B. 1888; Close Sch., and Hartford Coll., Orton; honors.; mst., King Edward VII Sch., 1914-22; asst. mst., King's Coll. Lagos, 1922; asst. educn., S.P., Nigeria, 1929; inspr., educn., 1930-31; prin., King's Coll., 1931.

PANET, LIEUT.-COL. CHARLES LOCH.—B. 1870; ed. Ottawa Univ.; ent. ser., 1889; sec., Canadian defence comm., 1914; sec. to late Sir F. W. Borden, min. of defence, 1904-12; sec., dept. of militia from 1907; pres., bd. of enquiry, applicants for the fenian raid volunteer, 1912-16; pres. of pensions and claims comm., in desp. for serv. rendered in Can., 1914-16.
PAPAFIO, M.K.Q.—Ed. Aldenham Sch., and in England; 6th grade teacher, 6th grade do., 1912; 2nd cls. do., 1919; int. headmistress, 1920; headmistress, 1921.

PAPATETROU, ANTONI, M.B.E.—B. 1878; clk., land regist. and survey dept., Cyprus, Oct., 1903; inspr., land regist. and survey dept., Apr., 1919; asst. dir. and reg. surveys, July, 1927; ag. dir., land regist. surveys for various periods, 1928-35.

PARK, ALEXANDER DALLAS, C.M.G.—B. 1882; ed. Waitaki Pub. Sch., and privately; served with army in S. Africa; N.Z. pub. wks. dept., 1919; dept., 1919; inspr., pub. serv. comm., 1920; sec., marine, 1922; asst. pub. sec., 1923; asst. sec., treas., 1925; sec., finan. adv., 1931; joint managing dir., mortgage corp., 1938; joint managing dir., advances corp., 1936.
PARK, CAPT. EDWARD NICKL, O.M.C.—B. 1889; ed. Aldenham Sch., Regt., M.C.; desps.; Somaliland Corps, 1917-20; opera. against M.C. dist. police offr., Somaliland, 1920; Somaliland, Feb., 1922.

PARKE, JOSEPH.—B. 1888; 1908-May, 1913; inspr., pol. F.M.S., ag. asst. comsnr., pol., Nov., 1923; detect. br., Perak, Nov., 1924; dist. br., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1927; do., 1932; dep. comsnr., pol., F.M.S., orig. intell., F.M.S., Oct., 1934.

PARKE, CAPT. L. S.—Coms. Hampshire Regt., 1909; Coldstream, 1911; on war ser., Uganda and selected by W.O. for serv. with Aden, 1919-20; cadet, Zanzibar, asst. finan. advr. and comdt., pol. rein, Persian Gulf, 1926-32 (twice to govt.); 1st grade inspr., 1933.

PARKER, CECIL JOSEPH, M.C.—B. 1888; asst. engr., Peru, 1919; P.W.D., F.M.S., Jan., 1919 (Field Co.), 1915-19; served in ment. in desps., 1917; M.C.; victory meda.; ex. engr., P.W.D., 1920; sen. ex. engr., Apr., 192

PARKER, JAMES T.—Cert. teacher, Leeward dist., postmr., Bethesda, 1901; schlr., govt. reformatory schl., 1903; clk. and store-er, P.W.D., and clk. to road bd., Antigua; 1st govt. offr., Virgin Is., 1915; inspr. of hts and measures, Virgin Is., 1917.

ARKER, LANGLELOT—B. 1879; clk., cust., burg., 1902; survr., Cape Town, 1915; served A. in Great War; cust. investigation offr., Union in New York, 1926; collr. cust. and ping mast., Port Elizabeth, 1930; do., Dur., 1933.

ARKER, PELHAM.—B. 1888; Mediterranean Egyptian Expedy. Forces, 1915-20 (Gallipoli, Suda, Palestine and Egypt); 2nd lieut. (Gen.), 1917; lieut., 1918; miny. of P.W., Egyptian irrign. serv., 1921-22; astt. accont., June 1920; astt. accont., 1st cls., Jan., 1932; ch. accont., Nov., 1934; ag. regiar., co-op. and co-credit socs., June-Oct., 1936; ag. dep. treas., y-Oct., 1936.

ARKES, EDWARD, M.V.O., I.S.O.—Ent. manian pub. serv., 1906; clk. of exec. coun., 1915; sec. to premier, 1925; offl. sec., Tasman.-agt.-gen.'s office, London, 1927-28; under sec. ch. electoral offr., 1930; state organizer, it of H.R.H. the Duke of Gloucester, 1931; sm. salmon and freshwater fisheries comm., 25-35.
PARKHILL, SIR ROBERT ARCHADE, K.C.M.G. (186).—El. to H. of R., C'wealth of Australia, 27; min. for home affrs. and transport, Lyons' ministry, 1932; P.M.G., 1932-34; min. for defence, 1934.

PARKINSON, SIR ARTHUR CHARLES COSMO, C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1931), O.B.E., M.A. (1910).—B. 1884; ed. at Epsom and Oxford; ass. demy., Magdalen, 1903-7; 1st cls. moda., 1905; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1907; apptd. after impt. exam., 1st cls. clk., accont.-gen.'s dept., admiralty, Feb., 1908; transf. as 2nd cls. clk., 21st Apr., 1909; sec. man. comte. of nat. bureau of entomology; sec. to comte. to investigate relationship between wild and trypanosomiasis, 1913-14; astt. Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 5th Oct., 1914, to May, 27th May, 1915, to Mr. Walter Smith, 1916; 1st cls. clk., 8th Dec., 1916; service from 16th May, 1915 to prin., last Apr., 1920; priv. 1st Oct., 1920; astt. sec. of the Brilliant Star of India; astt. under-sea. of state, 1923.

GRANNIS, O.B.E.—Steel bounties, Sydney, 1902; trade and commerce, Canada, 1902; 1st cls., Apr., 1911; on mily. serv., astt. in desps.; head clk., Apr., Jan., 1921; astt. dep. min. and trade, 23rd; on sp. duty to U.K., 1928; dep. min. trade and commerce, Nov., 1931.

PAKNALL, JOHN.—Solr., astt. aud., E. Africa Prot., 1915; astt. aud. in charge, Zanzibar, Nov., 1915; ag. treas., Zanzibar, Sept. 1919 to July, 1920; regiar. and offl. assignee, H.B.M. ct., Zanzibar; regiar. of patents, designs, and trade marks, Feb., 1920; ag. admistr.-gen. and regiar. of docs., Apr. to Oct., 1922; astt. admistr.-gen., Oct., 1923 to Jan., 1924; admistr.-gen., pub. trustees, regiar. of docs. and land offr., Zanzibar, May, 1924; ag. atty.-gen., June-Dec., 1929; also offl. assignee, regiar., patents, designs and trade marks, Jan., 1934; ag. treas., Nov., 1934.

PARR, HON. SIR CHRISTOPHER JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1930), K.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1869; ed. Auckland Gram. Schl. and Auckland Univ. Coll.; barrister-at-law; mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1914-26; min. of educ., 1920-26; min. of health, 1920-23; min. of just., 1923-26; P.M.G. and min. of tele., 1925-26; high commr. for N.Z. in England, 1926-29 and 1934-36; M.L.C. and leader of leg. coun., 1931-33.
PARRY, J. H., B.A. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—Tempy. comm. in Indian med. serv., Mar., 1915 to Mar. 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr., 1928.
PARRY, REGINALD FRANCIS SCARLETT.—Ed., St. John's Schl., Leatherhead, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; jnr. aupt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. inspr., S. Prov., 1934.
PARRY, HON. WILLIAM EDWARD.—B. 1878; ed. pub. schls., N.S.W.; mem., N.Z. royal comm. on mines, 1911; mem., N.Z. H. of R. since 1919; min. of internal affrs., 1935.
PARSONS, HON. SIR HERBERT ANGAS, KT. BACH. (1936), LL.B.—Judge of sup. ct., S. Australia, 1921.

PARTIDGE, GEORGE JAMES, O.B.E. (1937), B.A. (1915), M.A. (1921), Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple (1927).—B. 1893; ed. Christ's Hospital and Cambridge; open exhib. (maths.), and Essex County Scholar, Queens' Coll., 1912-14; 2nd cls. maths. tripes, part I., 1913, B.A. (War), 1915; temp. comm., 1914-18; on active serv. in France and German E. Africa; 1914-15 Star; ment. in desp.; astt. polit. offr., 1st grade, provisional admtn., German E. Africa, Dec. 1918; priv. sec. to ag. admistr., Aug. to Dec., 1919; astt. sec., Oct., 1919; ag. senr. astt. sec., on various occasions, 1921-23; clk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1922 to Sept., 1923; senr.-astt. sec., Aug., 1925; ag. astt. ch. sec., in 1927, 1928 and 1929; astt. ch. sec., Apr., 1930; ag. dep. ch. sec. for various periods, 1930-33; 1st astt. ch. sec., 1934.
PASCHALIS, NEOPTOLEMOS, K.C. (1931).—B. 1880; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1902; solr.-gen., Cyprus, Mar., 1927; ag. atty.-gen. for various periods, 1927-36.

PASKIN, JESSE JOHN, B.A., M.C.—B. 1892; ed., King Edward's Schl., Stourbridge and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls., maths. tripes, pt. I., 1913; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919 (Worcestershire Regt., T.F. and Machine Gun Corps); Croix de Guerre, 1918; transf. to T.F. res. with rank of major, Jan., 1920; apptd. to reconstruction scheme, junr. higher divn. clk., miny. of transport, Jan., 1920; astt. prin., C.O., 29th Mar., 1921; astt. pvtc. sec. to S. of S., 10th Dec., 1928; prin., 4th Sept., 1929.

PASSFIELD, 1ST BARON (Cr. 1929) Rt. Hon. SIDNEY JAMES WEBB, P.C., 1924, LL.B. (Lond.), Hon. Doc., Polit. Economy (Munich), etc.—B. 1859; ed. London and abroad; ant. W.O. by open compet., 1878; survr. of taxes, 1879; C.O., cls. I by open compet., 1881; resigned, 1891; sometime hon. lect. on polit. economy at City of London Coll. and Working Men's Coll. and subsequently hon. prof., public admtn., London School of Economics, 1912-27; M.P. (Seaham Divn.), 1922-29; pres., Bd. of Trade, Jan.-Nov., 1924; sec. of state for dom. affrs. and the cols., June, 1929; has served on numerous royal comms. and deptl. comtees; author of many works on politics and economics.

PASSINGHAM, FRANK SELWOOD, M.B.E. (1937).—B. 1883; ed. Goldsmiths' and Morley Colls., Lond.; hons. medallist in typography, City and Guilds of Lond. Inst.; govt. printer, Mauritius, Feb., 1914; also supt., bookbinding works and stat. stores, Apr., 1914; supt. of printing, Jamaica, Mar., 1924; supt., govt. printing office, Cyprius, Oct., 1930.

PATENAUME, JOSEPH OSCAR, I.S.O.—Ed. Longueil Coll., Quebec; clk., Dom. govt. stationery office, 1889; asst. supt., Dom. govt. stationery, Ottawa, 1899; supt., stationery, 1911; contr., purchases dept., pub. printing and stationery, 1920; King's printer of Canada, 1933.

PATERNON, CAPT. GAVIN, M.M.—B. 1891; cust. dept., Gold Coast, Feb., 1920; senr. collr., cust., Apr., 1927; prov. collr., cust., June, 1928; title changed to asst. comptlr., cust., Jan., 1932; dep. comptr., 1936.

PATRSON, HENRY SIRRALD.—B. 1890; ed. Winchester Coll. (schol.) and Magdalen Coll., Oxford (exhib.), B.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; seconded for war serv., Mar., 1915; active serv. in France and England, 1915-19; ment. in desp.; demob. with rank of major, Aug., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Aug., 1919; seconded as asst. adviser, Trengganu, July, 1920; supernumy. offr., cl. IV., Nov., 1920; dist. offr., Dec., 1923; offr., cl. III., Nov., 1925; offr., cl. II., Nov., 1930; asst. advr., Kedah, Apr., 1932; offr. cl. IB, Sept., 1934; P.M.C., Penang, Nov., 1935.

PATRSON, RALPH STANLEY WATSON, M.C.—B. 1884; ed. Perth Acad. and Tech. Coll., Dundee; val. dept., inland rev., 1913; R.E., 1915; county survr., Zetland, 1919; engrn., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1924; tech. secy., D.P.W., 1932.

PAUL, ROBERT JAMES.—B. 1888; sub.-inspr., constab., Trinidad, Sept., 1912; 2nd lieut., Trinidad local forces, Jan., 1913; pay and qr-mr., constab., and senr. supply and transport offr., Trinidad local forces, Nov., 1914, to Oct., 1916, and from Apr. to May, 1917; ag. inspr. of constab., and capt. local forces, Aug., 1915; asst. supt., fire brigade, Nov., 1914, to Nov., 1915, and from Feb. to Mar., 1919; ag. supt., fire brigade, Nov., 1915, to Sep., 1916; J.P. for the colony; ag. chief of police, comdt. local forces, supt. of prison, St. Vincent, Aug., 1919; J.P. for the colony; supt. of pol., Nyasaland, Mar., 1923; ag. contr., pol. and prisons, May, 1925 to Sept., 1926; ag. ch. commr., pol. and ch. inspr., prisons, in 1926, 1929 and 1932.

PAULIN, GEORGE.—B. 1885; ed. Canterbury Coll., Univ. of New Zealand, B.Sc., 1906; B.Eng., 1908; pupilage with govt. rly., New Zealand, with city engrn., Christchurch, and with Selwyn county engrn. and engrn.-in-chief, city of Dunedin drainage; engrn.-survr., P.W.D., N. S. Wales, 1910; dist. engrn., Fiji, 1912; res. engr., Suva harbour, wks., Fiji, 1915.
PAVLIDES, STIKLOS.—B. 1892; barrister-law, Gray's Inn, 1916; crown coun., Cyprus, Dec., 1927; ag. solr.-gen., for various pers., 1928-36.

(N.P. secretariat), 1915; on active serv., 1919; resumed duty, 1919; ag. prim. assist. Nigerian secretariat, Apr., 1930 to Aug., 1931; dep. res., July, 1934; admstive offr., cl. I, 1935.

PEACH, LEONARD WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M. & Cy. E.—B. 1888; dist. engnr., attd. to prov. engrn., N.W.P., Ceylon, 1914; on war serv., 1914-19; dist. engrn., Ceylon, 1919; exec. engrn., Nigeria, P.W.D., 1921; senr. exec. engrn., 1930.

PEARCE, ALEXANDER HUGH BLAKE, L.R.C.P. & S., Edin., L.R.F.P.S., Glas., 1924; L.M., Dublin (1966); D.P.H., Dublin (1919); certif., L.S.T.M., F.R.I., pub. health F.R.S., Trop. Med. and Hygiene, F.R. San. Ins.—1882; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edin. Univ. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; junr. med. offr., Maccarat, 1907; D.M.O., St. Kitts, 1908; res. med. supt., charitable inst., Antigua and M.O.H. Pacific, 1936; dir., cent. med. schl., Suva; lect. in obstetrics, do.

PEARCE, Rt. Hon. Sir GEORGE FORST, K.C.V.O. (1927), P.C. (1921).—Senator for Western Australia in commonwealth parlt., sec. of trades coun., at Perth, 1896; pres., last returned to first Commonwealth Senate, 1901; re-elected, 1906, and elected chmn. of comm. min. of defence, C. of A., Apr., 1910 to Feb., 1913; at. Imp. Conf., 1911; min. of defen., Sept., 1914; rep. Australia at the Wash-ington Disarmament Conf., 1921; min. for def. and territories in Bruce ministry, 1923; pres., exec. coun., June, 1926; min. of defen. and govt. leader in senate (Lyons' ministry); resigned mem. of defence and apptd. mem. of external affrs. and min. in ch. territories, 1934.

PECK, EDWARD FINCH, M.R.C.V.S., B.V.Sc., or.), D.T.V.M.—B. 1901; ed. Sedbergh, Univ. Toronto, Liverpool and Edinburgh; farming, Canada, 1920; inspr., health of animals br., Canada, 1929; vety. offr., Nigeria, 1931; vety. d. agr. offr., Somaliland, 1935.

PEDLOW, JOSEPH HOWARD, B.A. (N.U.I.), arrister-at-Law (Middle Temple).—B. 1889; ed. PENNINGTON, ARTHUR LAWRENCE, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Newbury Gram. Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1925.

PENTZ, EDWARD.—B. 1882; clk., deeds, Cape Town, 1900; exam., 1908; 1st grade exam., Bloemfontein, 1919; oh. exam., Vryburg, 1921; asst. regir., Vryburg, 1921; do., Pietermaritzburg, 1932; do., Cape Town, 1934; regir., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1935.

PEPPYS, WALTER EVELYN.—B. 1885; ed. at Malvern and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1907; M.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., 1908; passed cadet, June 1911; cls. PERCY, H. Honduras, Oct., 1892; 5th clk., reasy., customs and P.O. depts., June, 1893; 4th clk., Feb., 1894; 3rd clk., treasy. and cust., slept., May, 1897; 2nd clk., June, 1898; 2nd clk., C.S.O., Sept., 1900; ch. clk. and clk. of cours., Jan., 1906; priv. sec. to gov. Sir E. Swayne, 1909-10; to gov. Collett, 1911-13; to ag. gov., Walter, 1910; ag. comsnr. of currency, Feb., 1911; ag. treas., collr. of cust., 1911-14 and 1917-20; ag. col. sec., 1920; Br. Honduras del to Canada-W. Indies Confce., Ottawa, June, 1920; asst. pool. sec., Apr., 1921; comsnr. and judge, Turks and Caicos Is., June, 1923.

PHILLIPS, SIR JOHN RANDAL, Kt., (1930), O.B.E., (1927), B.A. (Durham), M.B., C.M. (Edin.)—B. 1867; ed. Lodge Schi. and Codrington Coll., Barbados and Edinburgh Univ.; M.L.C., Bar- bados, 1909; pres., leg. coun., 1928; mem. exec. coun., 1935.

PHILLIPS, WILLIAM JAMES ELLERY, M.C., M.B., B.S., D.T.M. & H., Lond.—B. 1891; ed. Prince Alfred Coll., Adelaide and Adelaide Univ.; on war serv., major A.A.M.C., 1915-19; med. offr., Malayan med. serv., 1923; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, Feb., 1935; ag. bacteriologist, Feb., Apr., 1936.

PHILLIPSON, SYDNEY, M.A. (Manchester).— R.1922; on milly. serv., Oct., 1915; cadet, Ceylon civil serv., Nov., 1919; attched. to Kandy kach., Dec., 1919; pol. mag., Matara, 1922; office asst., Kurunegala kach., July, 1924; asst. contr., rev., Jan., 1925; addnl. asst. col. treas., Nov., 1929; addnl. asst. col. treas., June, 1930; contr., estab., July, 1931; contr., supplies and finance, July, 1932; contr., estab., Feb., 1934; ag. dep. finan. sec., May-Nov., 1935.
PHILIP, DAVID.—B. 1893; asst. acct., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1919; ag. dep. acct., in 1930, 1932, 1934 and 1935; ag. asst. sec. to gen. man., June, 1931; expend. acct., Aug., 1935.
PICKERING, SIR GEORGE HUNTER, Kt. Bach. (1932).—Town mag., E. A. P., 1910; ag. 2nd puisne judge, 1915; puisne judge, 1917; ag. ch. just., Apr. to Nov., 1927; ch. just., Zanzibar, 1929; ret., 1932.

PICKWORTH, CECIL HENRY.—B. 1890; war serv., in France, 1914-19; attd., experimental dept. of tanks, miny. of munitions, 1917-19; res. engrn., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1921; res. engrn., Singapore municipality, 1924; elec. engrn.-in-ch., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1927.
PIDDUCK, H. B.—Ed. Harper Adams agril. coll., Univ. of Wales; Shropshire Yeomanny, 1914-19; science mast., Dominica Grammar Schi., 1922; headmast., 1923; agr. offr., 1929.
PIEGROME, JOSEPH CHARLES, M.C.—B.1898; lieut., Northumberland Fusiliers, 1915; France, 1916-18; wounded, 1916; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, Sept., 1921; commr., 1927.

PIENAAR, ANDRIES GUSTAF ERLANE.—Ck. of ct., Lichtenburg, July, 1902; ag. pub. prosec. Feb., 1903; pub. prosecutor, June, 1903; ch. clk. and ag. asst. res. mag., Nov., 1905; addnl. asst. res. mag., Krugersdorp, Sept., 1906; res. mag. and native comsnr., Waterberg, Nov., 1910; res. mag., Emelio, Apr., 1912; senr. mag., Germiston, May, 1920; pub. serv. inspr., Sept., 1921; mag., Louis Trochhardt dept. of just., Aug., 1924; sec. for int., Dec., 1924; chmn., pub. serv.commn., Aug., 1926; chmn., Union tender and supplies bd., June, 1934.

PIENAAR, ANDRIES JACOBUS, K.C., B.A. LL.B.—Ed. Muir Coll., Uitenage (Cape) and S.A. Coll., Cape Town; advoc., sup. et. of S. Africa; addl. legal advr., Cape Prov. admintrn., 1929; war serv., S.W. Africa and E. Africa, 1914-19; partly, draughtsmen, partit. of Union of S. Africa, 1936.

PIENAAR, LIEUT-COL. FILIPPUS FOURIEZ, LL.B., O.B.E., R. of O., Union Defence Forces—Civ. serv., S. A. Republic; ment. in desps., Great War; admitted sworn translator, sup. et.; accredited rep. of Union of S.Africa to League of Nations; H.M. cons.-gen. in Mozambique for the Union of S. Africa, 1934.
PIERS, FRANCIS A.—B. 1882; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1910; inspr., 1923.

PIERS, Harold Henry Ross.—Cler. asst., Peddie, Cape Prov., 1895; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1895; R.M. clk., Peddie, 1898; on active serv., served Boer War, 1899-1900; senr. clk. comsnr., Kimberley, 1902; first clk., Calvina, 1903; Albany, 1907; asst. mag., Albany, 1907; 3rd grade mag., Keiskama Hoek, 1914; ditto, Hopefield, 1919; 2nd grade mag., Molteno, 1920; 1st grade mag., Greytown, 1927; senr. grade mag., Windhoek, S.W.A., June, 1929; sp. "B" mag., E. London Mar., 1933; sp. "A" mag., Pretoria, 1935; mag., Durban, Mar., 1936.

PIGGOTT, Sir Francis Taylor, Kt. Bach. (1905).—Barrister-at-law; employed by F.O. on special service in 1887; legal adv. to prim. min., Japan, Nov., 1887; sec. to att. gen. Sir C. Russell (afterwards Lord Russell of Killowen) during Behring Sea arbitrn., 1893-4; proc.-gen., Mauritius, 1894; ag. ch. just., 1895 to 1897; ch. just., Hong Kong, Apr., 1905; ret., 1912; recd. thanks of coun. of govt. for ser. in connection with planters' loans, 1902; author of "Nationality and Nationalization," "Exterritoriality," "Extradition," "Foreign Judgments and Jurisdiction," "Law of Torts," and "Imperial Statutes applicable to the Colonies."

PILBEAM, S. A.—B 1899; entd. C.O., Jan., 1915; passed asst. clk.'s exam., July, 1916; on military service from 18th July, 1917, to 10th Mar., 1919; assigned to CO as asst. clk., Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.
PILKINGTON, Charles William MacDonald.—B. 1877; clk., cust., Cape Town, 1895; oh. clk., Port Elizabeth, 1930; do., Pretoria, 1933; collr., cust., Johannesburg, 1934.

PILLING, Henry Guy, C.M.G. (1932).—B. 1886; ed. King's Sch., Ely, and Keble Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1911; cadet, Fiji, 1907; passed cadet, 1910; 2nd grade dist. comsnr., 1914; mem., Indian Franchise Comsn., 1920; ag. asst. col. sec., 1920; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1921; ag. sec., W. Pacific high comsnr., Mar., 1924; sec., W. Pacific high comsnr., 1926; asst. to high comsnr., W. Pacific, 1929; col. sec. Br. Honduras, Dec., 1929; ag. gov. in 1930 and 1931; dep. col. sec., Kenya, 1933.

PINDER, Richard Francis, (R. of O.).—B. 1898; ed. St. Bees, Cumberland; served, European War, Flanders, Germany, 1916-19; asst. audr., Sierra Leone, May, 1920; ag. audr., Nov.-Dec., 1920; audr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1929; ag. audr., Feb.-May, 1930; senr. audr., Uganda, Aug., 1931; ag. audr., July-Dec., 1932; audr., Fiji, Solomon Is. Prot., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., Aug., 1934.

PINKERTON, William James Dunlop, A.M.I.C.E., Chartered Civil Engrn.—B. 1890; asst. engrn., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1914; exec. engrn., Ulu Selangor, July, 1923; sr. exec. engrn., in addn., Jan., 1930; offic. as sr. exec. engrn., Kinta, in addn., Nov.-Dec., 1930; senr. drainage and irrign. engrn., Malay States, Jan., 1932; irrign. engrn., Kelantan, Jan., 1932; asst. audr., drainage and irrign., Malay States, Nov., 1935.

PINNEY, Capt. Chas. Robert, M.C. (1917).—B. 1883; ed. Essendon Grammar Sch.; draughtsmen lands dept., Papua, 1906; on war serv., A.I.F., 1914-20; wounded; transfd. R. of O., C.M.F., 1920; ch. draughtsmen, Papua, 1920; staff survr. in addn., 1924; ag. comsnr., lands, etc., 1927; comsnr., lands, dir., mines, dir., agr., dep. chmn., land bd., mem. exec. and leg. couns., July, 1930; admstr., Norfolk Is., 1932.

PIRIE, D. A.—B. 1886; clk. Barberton Transvaal, 1903; treasvr., 1903; sec. min. Union of S. Africa, 1920; sec. min.; treasury, 1920; ch. clk., grade II, 1921; grade I, 1932; dep. comnr., pensions, 1932.

PIROW, Hans, D.Sc., M.Inst.M.M.T.—vice-pres., Geol. Soc. of S.A.; mem., chem. Met. and Min. Soc., and S.A. Assoc. of Science.—B. 1892; ed. S. Africa at continent; grad., S.A. and London; various capacities on Witwatersrand gold mine 1919; govt. research schol., 1919-20; min. G.M.R., Dec., 1926; is also mem., min. bd. and elec. control bd., assessor min. grnt bd. and statistical coun.; ag. sec., mining industries, 1927.

PIROW, Oswald.—B. 1890; ed. Africa, German and England; barrister, 1913; K.C., 1924; ent. part. Union of S. Africa, 1924; min. of jusr., 1929; min., rly. and harbors and mar. Mar., 1933; World Econ. Conf., Locar.

PITBLADO, David Bruce, M.A.—Lilstrand Schol. and Emmanuel Coll., Casa 1930-34); hist. tripors part 1 and tripors 2; B.A., 1933; hist. tripors part 2; supervisor in hist., Emm. Coll., 1933-35; after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O. (burgh Coll. of Agr.; on war serv., France, Mesopotamia and Palestine; R.F.A.; ment. in despe.; dist. agr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1921; agr., Cyprus, Mar., 1929; agr. dir. at various occasions, 1930-35; agr. dir. husbandry, June to Sept., 1930, and June to Sept., 1932.

PITCAIRN, Andrew.—B. 1894; Edinb. Coll. of Agr.; on war serv., France, Mesopotamia and Palestine; R.F.A.; ment. in despe.; dist. agr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1921; agr., Cyprus, Mar., 1929; agr. dir. at various occasions, 1930-35; agr. dir. husbandry, June to Sept., 1930, and June to Sept., 1932.

PITMAN, Charles Robert, Sec. D.S.O. (1917), M.C. (1916).—B. 1869; Naval Schol., Blundell's and R.M.C.; att'd. R. Warwickshire Regt.; 27th Foot, capt., 1915; war serv., Egypt, 1914-15; Fru 1915; Mesopotamia, 1916-18 (wounded twice, M.C., D.S.O.); Palestine, gen. staff, 1919-21; game warden, 1925; seconded to N. Rhodesia for sp., 1931-33.

PITT, Walter James, B.A.—B. 1873; Downing Coll., Cambridge (modern and language tripors); ent. Middle Temple, 1920; passed Bar final exam., Dec., 1920; head of Coast Govt. Schol., 1910; inspr. of schol., prov. inspr. of schol., 1914; att. to police, Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; att. dist. exc. July, 1917; dist. comnr., Jan., 1927; prov. comsnr., W. Prov., Ashanti, Feb.-Nov., 1927.

PITTMAN, William.—B. 1878; African Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxon. (Univ. S.A.), M.A. (Oxon); called to bar, V. Temple, 1903; admitted to Cape (Transvaal) 1905 bars; dean of faculty of Univ. S.A., 1918-25; K.C., 1924; judge of S. Africa eastern dists. local divn., 1925; delimitation coman., July, 1927 and Mar., 1928.

PLACE, Alan Whitley.—B. 1888; affrs. dept., S. Rhodesia, 1910-19; mili. sec., 2nd Rhodesia Regt., 1915-16, 4th K. 1916-24; cadet, Uganda, 1924; asst. sec. temp. serving in D.O. and C.O., 1928-29; asst. sec., Uganda, 1934.
PLANT, George Frederic, C.B.E. (1877); ed. Manchester Gram. Schol. and Bar.

ATTES, SHELLEY ARDEN.—B. 1899; ed. Oxford Schl.; passed army entrance exam.; Engg.'s India cadetship," 1917; gazetted 2/9th ca Rifles; 3rd Afghan War, 1919; attd. 2/11th ca Rifles and 4/70th Burma Rifles, Burma, cadet, admstive service, Tanganyika Terr., May, 1926; ast. dist. offr., July, 1928.

OWMAN, CLIFFORD HENRY FITZHERBERT, G.(1937), O.B.E (1928).—B.1889; ed. at King's Ely and Trin.Coll., Camb.; B.A.(class, tripods); dist. comnstr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912; offr., Turkana Expedtn., 1915 (A.G.S. Med. clasps); ag. offr. in charge, Northern Frontier, 1919-20; seconded to F.O. as H.B.M.-Consul, Harar, Abyssinia, 1920; H.B.M.-Consul transfer Harar Consulate to C.O., 1922; ifd., Somaliland, 1922; H.B.M. chargé, Addis Ababa, Dec., 1925 to Jan., mem., mission to Abyssinia for coronation of emperor, 1930; 2nd cls., Star of Ethiopia; comnstr. and pol. offr., Br. Somaliland-opia bdy., comnstr., 1931-33; sec., govt. of ailand, 1933; ag. comnstr., May-Oct., 1934; gov', May-Oct., 1936; has passed E. Africa law exam. and in Swahili, Boran Galla and Arabic languages.

UNKETT, JOHN OLIVER, M.I.C.E., Chtd. Engrn.—B. 1888; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Dec.; demob. with rank of capt., Sept. 1919; engrn., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1920; do., Sept., 1922; exec. engrn., Nov., 1929; engrn., F.M.S., Oct., 1933; ag. senr. exec., Muar, Aug., 1935.

YMOUPTH, 2nd Earl of (cr. 1905) and 16th Windsor (cr. 1529) IVOR MILES WINDSOR.—B. 1889; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; D.L., Worcestershire; lord-lieut., morganshire, 1923; capt., hon. corps of yeomen-at-arms, 1925; M.P., Ludlow divn., Tiphare, 1922-23; partly, under-sec. of state Dominion affrs., Jan., 1929; kt. of grace of John of Jerusalem; partly, sec., min. of sport, Sept., 1931; partly, under sec. of state the cols., 30th Sept., 1932; do., F.O., Ist, 1936.

OLE-EVANS, ILLTD BULLER, C.M.G. (1921), (Cantab.), D.Sc. (Wales), F.L.S.—B. 1879; Cambridge Gram. Schol. and Univ. of Wales Cambridge; mycologist, Transvaal, 1905; Transvaal govt. at 4th Internat. Botanical Congress, Brussels, 1910; ch., divn. of mycology plant pathology, 1911; ch., divn. of botany plant pathology, 1912; ap. mission to P.E. disease, coconut palm disease, 1913; dir., botanical survey of S. Africa, 1918; pres., S. African socn. for Advancement of Science, 1920; ch., divn. of botany, horticulture and entomology, Union eastern prov., 1927; ch., divn. of plant industry, 1929; author many scientific papers.

OLLARD, ARNOLD.—B.1893; ed. Heles Schl.; ster.; active serv., R.N.A.S. and R.N.R., 5-19; col. audit dept., Feb., 1920; ast. tr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; ast. tr., Kenya, May, 1924; ag. senr. ast. audr. on several occasions in Tanganyika and Kenya; audr., Windward Is., Jan., 1926; senr. ast. audr., Uganda, Jan., 1929; ag. audr., Apr., 1929; audr., Trinidad, June, 1931; audr., N. Rhodesia, 1935.

POLLOCK, CAPT. JAMES HUEY HILL (R. of O.).—B. 1893; ed. Royal Schl., Armagh; war serv., 1914-20; Gallipoli, Macedonia, Egypt and Palestine; men. in desps.; staff capt., O.E.T.A.; inspr., grade 3, Palestine, July, 1920; dep. gov', grade 3, Ramallah, Oct., 1920; admstve. offr., Nigeria, Sept., 1923; ast. sec., acort., Lagos, Dec., 1927; admstve. offr., Palestine, Jan., 1930; ast. dist. comnstr., 1932.

PONGIA, JOHN.—B. 1898; ed. Oratory Schl. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; 2nd lt., R. Warwick Regt., Sept., 1917; served in France, Italy and India, 1917-21; seconded, 6th K.A.R., T.T., 1921-27; admstve. offr., Zanzibar Mar., 1928.

POOLE, EDWARD HUMPHREY LANE.—Ed. Rugby and Balliol Coll. Oxford, B.A.; ent. B. S.A. Co'a admnstr., N. Rhodesia, 1913; ast. nat. comm., 1915; nat. comm., 1919; ast. mag., 1926; title changed to dist. offr., 1928; ag. dir., nat. educn., 1934-1935; prov. comnstr., 1936; mem. and sec., nativereserves commn., E. prov., 1924-25; various publications on native history and customs.
POOLEY, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 22nd Oct., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, Aug., 1933.

POPE, GEORGE WILLIAM, L.L.M.R.C.P., and S.I. (Ireland), D.P.H., R.C.P. and S.I.—B. 1891; med. offr., Kenya, 1920; 2nd health offr., port med. dept., Hong Kong, 1924; ag. ast. bacteriologist, 1924; ast. M.O.H., 1925; M.O.H., 1926; health offr., 1936.

POPE, LAWRENCE HENRY.—B. 1896; ed. Uptingham; served European War, 1915-19 (France and N. Russia); ast. P.S. to govr., S. Australia, Sir W. E. E. A. Weigall, K.C.M.G., 1920; P.S. to govr., Tasmania, Sir W. L. Allardyce, G.C.M.G., 1921; col. audit dept., 1923; ast. audr., Fiji, 1923; ag. audr., 1924-28; ast. audr., Tanganyika Territory, 1928; do. Nigeria, Apr., 1934.

POPE, STEAD.—Br. post office, Feb., 1903; dist. survr., post and tels., Gold Coast, Dec., 1913; on active serv., Togoland Field Force, Aug., 1914; and later in charge posts and tels., Togoland to May, 1915; Imp. army serv., Sept., 1915-19; lieut., R.E. and R.A.F.; dist. survr., Gold Coast, June, 1919; dist. survr., Jan., 1927; P.M.G., Nyasaland, Mar., 1930; do., Gold Coast, 1936.

POPHAM, HENRY BRAIDSHAW, C.M.G. (1935), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1881; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; comnstr., 13th Light Infy., 1900; served, S. African War(2 medis.); seconded, G.Costreagt., W.A.F.F., 1906; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to ag. govr., G. Coast, Mar. to Aug., 1909; col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1910; priv. sec. to ag. govr., Feb. to June, 1911; ag. ch. ast. col. sec.and olk. of couns., July to Aug., 1913; ag. senr. ast. col. sec., Oct., 1913; dist. comnstr., Jan., 1914; seconded to Togoland polit. admtn., Nov., 1914; senr. ast. col. sec. and olk. of couns., Jan., 1920; ag. ch. ast. col. sec., Sept., 1920 to Feb., 1921; dep. prov. comnstr. and ag. comnstr., W.Prov., Feb., 1921; prov. comnstr., Apr., 1922; retd. (invalided) May, 1923; comnstr., Cyprus, Oct., 1925; admstr., Dominica, 1933; mem., exec. coun., Leeward Is., 1933; gov. and commdr.in-ch., Windward Is., 1937.

POPHAM, AIR CHIEF MARSHALL SIR HENRY ROBERT MOORE BROOKE, G.C.V.O. (1935), K.C.B. (1927), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1915)—B. 1878; ed Haileybury, Sandhurst and Staff Coll.; ent. Army, 1898; capt., 1904; R.F.C., 1912; bt. maj., 1913; maj., 1915; R.A.F., 1919; served European War, 1914-19; commdt., R.A.F. Staff Coll., 1921-26; air offr., commdg. fighting area air defences, Gt. Britain, 1926-28; air offr., commdg. Iraq, commdt., 1928-30; commdt., Imp. Defence Coll., 1931-33; air offr., commdg.in-ch., air defence, Gt. Britain, 1933-35; prin. air A.D.C. to the King since 1933; I.G., R.A.F., 1935-36; gov. and commdr.in-ch., Kenya, and high commr. for transport, 1936.
