REAY, Capt. George Henry, A.M.I.E.E., D.F.H.—B.1888; ast. sec., F.M.S., Jan., 1914; war serv., 1914-18; imprt., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; elec. engnr., Singapore, July, 1922.
REDELINGHUYS, Johannes Nicolaas, Cape post and tel. serv., 1899; sec. to Town, 1909; senr. clk., 1920; ch. clk., ast. under-sec., 1926; under sec., 1930; P.M.G., 1927; under sec., posta., 1930.
REDFEARN, Edward Scott.—B. 1879; St. Paul's Coll., Cheltenham; mas., Inst., K. Lumpur, July, 1921; do., Malacca, Jan., 1929; headmr., govt. K. Pilah, Dec., 1930; do., govt. Penang, Jan., 1935.
REDFEARN, Percy William.—ast. acctnt., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1919; Jan., 1925; fincl. offr., July, 1929; ast. acctnt., F.M.S. rly., Aug., 1930.

REDMAN, Robert Carne.—B. 1879; Bromsgrove; admitted as solvr., admtr-genl., Tanganyika Territory; admtr-genl., offl. recr., pub. tr. companies, trade marks, patents, unions, regar. genl., births, death house tax and estate duty comm.

REECE, C. Mansel.—B.1891; ed. Magdalene Coll., Cambridge, 1914-18 (ment. in despa.); called Temple, 1921; pvtv. practice, Shire mag., Zanzibar, 1927; ag. puir., July, 1929; crown coun., Uganda gen. in 1930, 1931, 1932 and 1933; Grenada, Nov., 1933; oomsmr., Grenada; K.C., 1934; regar., s. 1935; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, 1935.
REECE, Courtenay Walter.—B.1899; ed. Harrison Coll. and Barbados and Jesus Coll., Barb., Middle Temple, 1925; pol. Barbados, 1926; do., att. "B" ag. judge, ast. ct. of appl., 1927-36.
REECE, Gerald, O.B.E. (C.B.), Rugby Schl.; served in Fre Foresters, 1915-18; solvr., sup admtve. cadet, Kenya, 1921.

REED, CLINTON AUSTIN, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1876; ent., civ. serv., Barbados, 1898; war serv., 1916-19; inspr., pol. and ast. supt., fire brig., 1919; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., various periods 1920-32; gov., Glendary prison, 1923; compt., cust., 1932; ag. col. sec., various periods, 1934-36.

REED, E. A.—B. 1894; on mily. serv., Aug., 314-Jan., 1918; temp. paper keeper, C.O. lib., 2nd Nov., 1920-15th Aug., 1924; estab. paper keeper (library attendant) after qual. exam., 6th Aug., 1924; cler. offr., 31st Oct., 1928.

REED, EDWARD BOYS, P.A.S.I., F.R.G.S.—B. 1881; ed. S. Savior's, Eastbourne, Hurst and Bounty Tech. Coll., Sussex; land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1906; 1st grade survr., 1913; on mily. serv., capt. R.G.A., 1915-19; prin. land survr., 1922; supt. surveys, 1926; supt., crown lands and surveys, 1932; J.P., 1932.

REED, HON. SIR JOHN RANKEN, Kt. Bach. (1936), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1864; ed. Gram. Schl., Auckland, N.Z.; Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1921; pres., priors bd., 1928; judge advog.-gen., N.Z. Defence Forces, with rank of colonel, 1911-33.

REED, THOMAS BOUCHER.—B. 1892; ed. St. Stanislaus Coll. and Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; joined lands and mines dept., Br. Guiana, 1908; successively 5th, 4th, 3rd and 2nd cls. offr., P.W.D., 1910-24; senr. clk., P.W.D., 1929; sec., standing unemployment relief comte., 1933; sec., food relief finance comte., 1934.

REEVE, GEORGE WALKER, B.A. hon. (Alta., Canada).—B. 1887; on mil. serv., 1914-19; admin. branch, A.I.P., Air Miny., 1918-22; ast. mast., aducn. dept., Hong Kong, Mar., 1922; lectr., Hong Kong Univ., 1925-37; 2nd mast., Queen's Coll., 1931 and 1933; headmtr., Wansai schl., 1934; do., Kadoorie schl., 1936.
REEVES, CHARLES JAMES.—B. 1875; entd. office consulting engnr. to W. African govt., 1895; apptd. to Crown Agents office, 1911; ast. head, appts. dept., 1919; dep. head, do., 1933.

REGNARD, MAJOR LOUIS FERDINAND.—B. 1888; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius, Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Technology and McGill Univ. (B.Sc.); 2nd lieut., R.E., Nov., 1914; war serv., France, Belgium and Palestine, 1914-20; major, R.E., 1918-20; regular army R. of O.; ast. D.P.W. and surveys, Mauritius, 1921; D.P.W., 1926; M.L.C.
REID, C. B. B., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.).—Capt., R.A.M.C., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1919.

REID, JAMES STUART WELLESLEY, M.A. (Edin.).—B. 1899; ed. George Watson's Coll. and Edin. Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1921; attchd. land office, Kajang, Nov., 1921; do., dist. office, L. Perak, Mar., 1922; do., U. Perak as financ. ast., Sept., 1922; passed cadet, July, 1923; priv. sec. to ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Jan., 1924; offr. cls. V, Oct., 1924; ast. dist. offr., Rembau, Apr., 1925; do., Sitiawan, Sept., 1926; offr. cls. IV, Oct., 1928; 2nd ast. advr., Muar, Apr., 1931; ast. dist. offr., K. Bahrud and P. Puteh, and treas., Kelantan, May, 1932; offr. cls. III, Oct., 1933; 2nd ast. sec. (A), S.S., Mar., 1935; ag. 1st ast. sec. (A), Dec., 1935.
REIDY, CAPT. MISCHEL JOHN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1888; ed. Royal Veterinary Coll., Dublin; Fiji, 1922; ast. col. vet. surg., Hong Kong, 1924.

REILLY, BERNARD JOSEPH.—B. 1898; forestry and colonists' dipls., Edin. Univ.; Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford; on war serv., 1915-19; ast. conserv., forests, lat gr., Cyprus, May, 1922; ast. ast. prin. forest offr., May to Dec., 1923 and 1924; ag. prin. forest offr., on various occasions, 1926-1931; ast. comsnr. in addn., 1928-32; ag. comsnr. on various occasions, 1930-34; ag. conserv., forests on various occasions, 1932-36.

REILLY, SIR BERNARD RAWDON, K.C.M.G. (1934), C.I.E. (1926), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1882; ed. Bedford; joined Indian Army, 1902; ent. Indian polit. dept., 1908; served in India and Aden in various appts.; officiated as polit. res., Aden, 1925 and 1926, and as res. and commdr.-in-ch., Aden, 1930 and 1931; res. and commdr.-in-ch., Mar., 1931; ch. commr., Aden, Apr., 1932; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Aden, Apr., 1937.

REILLY, JOSEPH OLIVER.—B. 1891; Imp. postal serv., 1911; war serv., R.E.s., 1914-19; postmr., Tanganyika, 1920; postmr., Nyasaland, 1924; ag. acct. tel. engrnr. and survr., P. and T., Nyasaland, 1932; ag. P.M.G., Nyasaland, 1935; P.M.G., Br. Guiana, July, 1936.

REILLY, ROBERT O'MALLEY, C.B.E. (1935).—Sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. police, 1940; ag. ast. res. mag., 1912; inspr., Apr., 1914; ag. res. mag., 1914; res. mag., 1921; passed 3rd grade Seeawana exam., 1922; ast. res. comsnr., Oct., 1931; ag. res. comsnr., April to Oct., 1932 and Mar.-Sept., 1935.

REINECKE, THEODORE GERALD WELLERBY, M.Sc. Agr.—B. 1888; lect. in chem., Schl. of Agr., Potchefstroom, 1910; prin., Sch. of Agr., Elsenberg, 1918; do., Potchefstroom, 1920; dir., Stellenbosch-Elsenberg Coll. of Agr., 1926; prin., Schl. of Agr., Glen, O.F.S., 1929; dir., native agr., Union native affrs. dept., Pretoria, 1934.

REITZ, HON. DENYS.—B. 1882; M.P. for Bloemfontein, 1920; M.P. for Port Elizabeth, 1920-27; M.P. for Barberton, 1929; min. of lands, Smuts cabinet, 1921-24; min. of lands, coalition cab., 1933; min., agr., irrign. and forestry, 1935.

RENDLE, HILARY CAMERON RUSSEL.—B. 1899; ed. Christs Coll., N. Zealand, Cantby Coll., Univ. of N.Z. and New Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1923; offr., cls. V, Nov., 1924; ast. ast. to res., Perak, Jan., 1925; priv. sec. to chief sec., July, 1925; ast. dist. offr., B. Pahang, Feb., 1927; offr. cls. IV, 2nd ast. advr., Muar, Nov., 1928; ast. dist. offr., L. Perak, Jan., 1932; offr. cls. III, 2nd ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1933; dist. offr., K. Lipis, Feb., 1936.
RENSON, PATRICK MUIR, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1911; C.O., Oct., 1932; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1935.

RENNIE, ALFRED BAILLIE, LL.B.—B. 1896; ed. King's Coll., Lond.; lieut., B.W.I.R.; war serv., 1916-19; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1922; clk. of sts., Jamaica, May, 1929; res. mag., Jan., 1933; sg. judge, Kingston ct., Jan.-Dec., 1933.

RENNIE, GILBERT MCCALL, M.C., M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1893; on mily. serv., July, 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Apr., 1920; attd. to Anuradhapura kachcheri, June, 1920; attd. to N'Ellya kachcheri, Nov., 1920; office ast. to govt. agt. E. Prov., Nov., 1921; pol. mag., Danadagamuwa, Dec., 1923; ditto, Chilaw, Mar., 1924; addnl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, Dec., 1925; office ast. to dir., med. and sanry. services, Apr., 1926; sec., min. for health, July, 1931; contr., finance and supply, Sept., 1933; sec. to gov., Dec., 1934.

RENWICK, HENRY LANGDON.—B. 1898; London Rifle Brig., Apr., 1915; attd. gen. staff, W.O., 1919; ch. clk. to H.M.T.C. in E. Africa, Dec., 1919; asst. immigr. and embarkation offr., Mombasa, July, 1920; asst. cust. dept., Zanzibar, Dec., 1920; immigr., offr., Aug., 1921; ag. asst. compt., cust., for various periods, 1923-34; ag. asst. treas., May, 1932-May, 1933; aviation control offr., Aug., 1932; ag. compt., cust., Feb.-Aug., 1934; ag. asst. treas., Aug., 1934 to Nov., 1935.
RETIEF, D. J., B.A.—B. 1882; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; prin., Boshof, 1913; prin., Winburg, 1921; inspr. of schls., O.F.S., 1927.

REX, MARCUS.—B. 1886; ed. Highgate and Trin. Coll., Camb. (exhbr.); B.A., 1908; cadet, F.M.S., 1910; asst. sec. to res., Perak, 1912 and 1915; asst. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, 1913; supk., chandu monopoly, Scl., N.S. and Pahang, 1917; dist. offr., Raub, 1919; mag., K. Lumpur, June, 1921, sec., retrenchment commn., in addn., Feb., 1922; dep. contr. of rubber expts., in addn., Oct., 1922; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1927; offtcg. commnr., trade and cust., Johore, offr., cls. IB, Aug., 1930; finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S., Jan., 1932; offr., cls. IA, June, 1932; contr., rubber, Malaya, May., 1934; ag. ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Apr., 1935; ag. Br. res., Perak, Feb., 1936.

REY, CHARLES FERNAND, C.M.G. (1932)—B. 1877; W. Africa, 1900; joined B. of T. 1900; sec. to China Tariff commn., 1902; to Imp. Inst. Advisory Comtee. and Imp. Inst. Trustees, 1903; to All Red Route Comtee., 1904; Roumanian Treaty Negotiations, 1905; mission to Constantinople and Sofia, 1905; Swiss Treaty negotiations, 1906; gen. man., lab. exchange and unemployment insurance, 1909; asst. gen. sec., miny. of munitions 1916; dir., employment dept., B. of T., 1917; dir.-gen., national lab. supply, miny. of national service, 1918; chmn. and Br. rep., Allied commn., Rotterdam, for food supply to Germany; seconded for serv. in Abyssinia, 1919; further journeys to Abyssinia, 1922-3, 1925-6, 1926-7; unemployment grants comtee., 1920-29; asst. res. commnr., Bechuanaland Prot., Oct., 1929; res. commnr., June, 1930; F.R.G.S., commdr., Order of the Star of Ethiopia; author of "Unconquered Abyssinia as it is to-day," 1923; "In the country of the Blue Nile," 1927; "The Romance of the Portuguese in Abyssinia," 1929; "The Lady or the Leopard," 1929; contributions to the Royal Geographical Jnl. and the Jnl. of the African Society.
REYNOLDS, A. E.—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., elk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1900; conf. elk., 1st Jan., 1918.

RHODES, ALEC LEOPOLD, M.B.E. (1936).—B. 1884; gen. foreman, govt. printing and stationery dept., Trinidad 1921; typography instr., bd. of indus. training, 1922; mem., bd. indus. training, 1923; govt. printer, 1925.

RHODES, HON. E. N., P.C. (Can.), K.C., B.A., LL.B., D.C.L.—B. 1877; ed. Amherst Acad., Acadia Univ. and Dalhousie Univ.; apptd. K.C., Nova Scotia, May, 1916; 1st el. to H. of C. Canada at g.e., 1908; el. dep. speaker, H. of C., Feb., 1916; speaker, 1917; re-el. Mar., 1918; el. to legis., N.S., 1925; prov. premier and prov. sec., 1925; re-el. at g.e., 1928; joined Dom. cabinet of Bennett admnstr., as min. of fisheries, Aug., 1930; min. of finance, 1932; mem., senate, 1935.
RHODES, BRIG.-GEN. SIR GODFREY DEAN, Kt., Bach. (1934), C.B.E., D.S.O., R.E. (ret.), M.E.I.C., M.Inst.T., A.M.I.C.E.—Ch. engrnr., Kenya and Uganda rly., 1920; gen. man., 1928.

RHODES, HON. SIR ROBERT H., K.C.V.O. (1927), K.B.E. (1924), L.B.A. Oxford; entd. New Zealand police, served in S. African war (Queen's sea claps); lt.-col., 1st Regt., Canterbury Yeomanry Cavalry; postm.-gen., m.n. graphs, min. of pub. health, 1920-35; defence, 1920-26; commn., state forces, leader of leg. coun. and mem., exec. over portfolio, 1926; dep. leader, leg. comn., lic. from office, 1928 and from leg. comn., lic. apptd., M.L.C., 1934.

RHODES, T. A. F., B.A., M.E. Transvaal Univ., Pretoria; elk., Cape tele. serv., 1899; sen. asst. asst., 1921-26; contr. of stores and transp., B. accent., 1930; asst. under-secr., 1932; (staff), 1936.
RHODES, WILLIAM FREDERICK R.A., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond., Oxon.)—B. 1881; ed. Bromsgrove Sci. bridge Univ. and St. Thomas' Hosp.; pathologist, and offr.-in-charge, gen. inst., Cape Town.
RICE, HARRY PATRICK, O.B.E. (1886; S.A. constab., 1906-15; K.A.R.; asst. supt.-pol., Kenya, Nov., 1919; sup. dep. commdt., pol. (C.I.D.), Palestine, inspr.-gen., 1936.
RICE, J. A., B.A., LL.B., (T.C.)—ed. Clongowes; admitted solr., sup. asst. commnr., lands, dept. govt., 1927; ag. commnr., lands, Apr., 1930.
RICH, HON. SIR GEO. EDWARD (1932).—Sen. puisne just. of the high court, Australia, Apr., 1913.
RICHARDS, ARTHUR COURTNEY.—2nd asst. govt. printer, Ceylon, Sep.; asst. govt. printer, Apr., 1923; govt. clerk, Apr., 1932.

RICHARDS, SIR ARTHUR EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1933), B.A., cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; offr., cls. I., 1913; mag., cent. ct., Kota Bharu, audr., Aug., 1915; adviser, land offr., and ag. supt. of mines, June, 1916; offr., cls. I., Jan., 1918; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1920; col. sec., S. Stiltna., and ag. ek. of addn., Apr., 1921; gen. sec., S.S. return comtee., in addn., Jan., 1922; on spe. duty, London, May, 1923; sec. opinion comtee. B. in addn., Nov., 1923; chmn., sec. commn., Malaya, Br. Empire Exhibition, in addn., cls. IA, under-secr. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1930; gen. adviser, Johore, Nov., 1929; gen. adviser, Hong Kong, Dec., 1921; ag. head offr., Br. Schl., 1922 and 1932; asst. m.n. Coll., 1934.

RICHARDS, EDMUND CHARLES.—agr. dept., B.A.P., 1909; secr., Nya asst. polit. offr., G.E.A., provlnl. ad. dist. polit. offr., T.T., 1922; dep. polit. offr., 1928; prov. commnr., 1931; dep. res. commnr., Basutoland, May, 1933.
RICHARDS, FRANCIS OTTENIE vol. 6th cls. elk., postal dept., Br.
RICHARDS, HON. FREDERICK WILLIAM.—D.—Crown solr., S. Australia, 1916; former parliamentary draftsman; ag. judge, sup. ct., 5; judge, ditto, 1927.
RICHARDS, P. E.—B.; 1901; apptd. after apet. exam., asst.clk., C.O., 24th Apr., 1919; r. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher ide., 20th Dec., 1928.

RICHARDSON, ALEXANDER STEWART, B.Sc. gr.).—B.; 1897; ed. Burgh and County High hl., Peebles; mily. serv., R. Arty., 1916-19; Sc., Edinburgh Univ., 1923; dist. agrl. offr., nganvika Territory, Apr., 1924; senr. agrl. r., 1930.

RICHARDSON, CECILY.—B.; 1889; ed. The ures, Rugby and Royal Holloway Coll.; B.A., ndon, 1912; asst. mistress, govt. girls' schl., nang, 1914; asst. mistress, Raffles girls' schl., napore, 1915; ag. prin., 1920 and 1924; prin., George's girls schl., Penang, Sept., 1928.

RICHARDSON, BRIG.-GEN. SIR GEORGE AFFORD, K.B.E. (1925), C.B. (1917), C.M.G. (1915), C.B.E. (1919).—B.; 1867; enlisted in Royal tillery, Aug., 1886; attached to N.Z. Forces instructor in gunnery with rank of master gun. r., Aug., 1891; capt., N.Z. militia and dir. of tillery services (N.Z.), Apr., 1907; transf. to Z. staff corps, Jan., 1911; major, 1912; lieut.-l., 1915; col., 1919, and brig.-gen. with seniority from Dec., 1919; N.Z. representative at War office, London, and attached Impl. gen. staff, ec., 1913; attached to Admity. staff, Aug., 1914; attached Royal Naval Divn. as asst. jutant and qtrmr., Nov., 1914; active service in Antwerp with Royal Naval Divn.; subse- quently served as A.A. and Q.M.G. on Gallipoli; elected as D.A. and Q.M.G. of an Army Corps Salonika with temporary rank of brig.-gen.; mdt., N.Z.E.F. in U.K., Mar., 1916; G.O. charge admtn., N.Z. Forces, May, 1919; instr., W. Samoa, 1923-28; also admtn., okelau Is., 1926-28; rep. N.Z. before mandates committee, L. of N., 1928; Legion of Honour Croix de Chevalier) conferred by Pres. of the French Republic, 1916; ment. in despatches (3).

RICHARDSON, ROBERT BOOTH.—B.; 1902; ed. anc. Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A.; nd cls. juris-prudence, 1923; called to bar, Inner emple, Jan., 1927; cadet, Tanganyika Territory dmstrn., May, 1924; asst. sec., Dec., 1924; attd. as asst. prin., C.O., Feb. to Aug., 1927; ag. crown coun. in 1929 and 1932; ag. asst. admstr. gen. and egir. companies, 1929; ag. res. mag., 1934; ag. ec., dept. lands and mines, 1935 and 1936; sec., Tanganyika soil erosion and gold mining industry comtes.

RICHES, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B.; 1885; on mily. serv., Jan., 1915 to Mar., 1919; 2nd lieut., London Scottish, Sept., 1917; apptd. to army audit staff, Eastern command, 1921; cler. offr., J.O., 17th July, 1922.

RICHMOND, ERNEST TATHAM, F.R.I.B.A.—B.; 1874; ed. High Croft Schl., Clifton Coll., Bristol, Architectural Schl., Westminster and R. Acad. Architectural Schl.; archt. to pub. wks., miny., Egypt, 1900; subsequently dir.-gen. state bldgs. dept.; attld., dept. of fortif., and wks., W.O., Dec., 1914—Oct., 1916; in ch., grenade sec., trench warfare dept., miny. of munitions, Mar., 1917; lieut., R.N.V.R., Mar., 1917; archt. attd. to war graves serv., France, 1918-19; asst. civ. sec., Palestine, 1920; dir., antiquities, 1927.

RIDDELL, WALTER ALEXANDER, M.A., PH.D.—Supt., trades and lab. dept. of pub. wks., Ontario govt., 1916; dep. min. of lab., Ontario, 1919; ch. of agrl. serv., Internat. Lab. Office, L. of N. Jan., 1920; Dominion of Canada advisory offr. to L. of N., Geneva, 1925.

RIDDELL, HON. WILLIAM RENWICK.—B.; 1852; ed. Cobourg, Ont., Coll. Inst. and Victoria Univ., Toronto; lecturer in maths., Normal Schl., Ottawa, 4 years; called to bar, 1883; Q.C., 1897; bencher, Law Soc., Upper Can., since 1892; judge, high ct., Ont., King's Bench dvn., 1906; judge, appellate dvn. (second div. ct.), 1913; hon. mem., American Bar Assoc., and of Bar Assocs. of several states; F.R. Hist. Soc., England; F.R.S.C., Can.; LL.D., Toronto, Yale, McMaster, Lafayette, Rochester, Wesleyan, North Western Univ.; L.H.D., Syracuse Univ.; J.U.D., Trinity Coll., (Toronto); author of several legal and hist. works; has contrib. extensively to hist., scientific and literary jnl.

RIDGEWAY, THE Rt. HON. SIR J. WEST, G.C.B. (1906), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.S.I., P.C. (Ireland).—B.; 1844; ret. from the army with the rank of col., 1889; under-sec., foreign dept., India, 1881-5; commr. for delimitation of Afghan frontier, 1885-7; under-sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1887; lieut.-gov. of the Isle of Man, 1893; gov. of Ceylon, Dec., 1895-1903; chmn. of comm. sent to S. Africa on Transvaal con- stitution, 1906; mem. of hon. man. comtes. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908; chmn., ct. of dts., Br. N. Borneo Co., 1910.
RIDGWAY, T. R. J.—Asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1922; supt., pol., 1935.

RIGDY, WILLIAM EDWARD, M.C.—B.; 1897; ed. Bury Gram. Schl.; served in France, Aug., 1916-Mar., 1918 and June, 1918-Nov., 1919; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; attchd., Chinese Prot., Penang, Dec., 1920; passed cadet, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. V, Nov., 1923; asst. supt., govt. monops., S'pore and asst. contrlr., rubber, Mar., 1926; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1927; 3rd mag., S'pore, June, 1930; inspr., prisons, in adtn., Sept., 1931; relief asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1932, offr., cls. III; asst. Malayan estabtmt. offr., Aug., 1933.

RIGNELL, JOHN HERBERT.—B.; 1886; Soots Guards, 1904-25; mily. prov., staff corps, 1911; warrant offr., cl. I., 1920-25; physical training instr., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1915-19; physical training instr., Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Sept., 1925.
RIMINGTON, CAPT. G.B., M.C.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, May, 1920.

RINFRET, HON. FERNAND, B.A., M.P.—B.; 1883; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Quebec (B.A.); el. St. James divn., Montreal bye-el., 1919; re-el., 1921, 1925, 1926 and 1930; sec. of state, Sept., 1926; re-el. by accl., Nov., 1926; mayor, Mont- real, 1932-34; re-el., H. of C., 1935; sec. of state, Oct., 1935.

RINFRET, HON. THIBAUDEAU.—B.; 1879; ed. St. Mary's Coll. (B.A., 1897), McGill Univ. (B.C.L., 1900); called to Quebec bar, 1901; K.C., 1912; min. of roads in Quebec Govt., 1910-22; prof., munic. law and law of public utilities, McGill Univ.; judge, sup. ct., Canada, Sept., 1924.
RING, JULIUS, B.Sc. (Eng.) (Birm.), F.S.I., Chart. Surveyor, A.M.I.Struc.E.—B.; 1892; engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925; asst. assessor, rates, 1928; 1st asst. do., 1933.

RIPPON, STEWART ROSS, M.R.C.V.S.—B., 1895; lieut., R.A.V.C., Jan., 1917; serv. in Salonica, Macedonia, and with Army of Black Sea; capt., Jan., 1918; demob., Jan., 1921; vety. offr., Gold Coast, May, 1921.

RISLEY, SIR JOHN SHUCKBURGH, K.C.M.G. (1922), K.C. (1921), C.B. (1912)—B., 1867; ed. at Marlborough and Magd. Coll., Oxford (classical exhibitioner); 2nd class classical mods., 1888; 2nd class jurisprudence, 1890; open studentship, Inns of Court, 1892; called to the bar, 1893; M.A. and B.C.L., 1894; author of "The Founding of St. Stephen's Golf Club," 1893; "The Law of War," 1897; "Notes and Echoes," 1902; editor of Dale's "Clergyman's Legal Handbook," 7th edition; joint editor of Waterlow's "Companies Acts Manual," 11th and 12th editions; legal assistant, C.O., March, 1901; legal adviser, 19th May, 1911.
RITCHIE, CAPT. A. T. A., O.B.E. (1936), M.C., B.A. (Oxon)—Game warden, Kenya, 1923; ch. game warden, F.M.S., 1936.

ROBERTS, GEORGE, F.I.H.E., J.P.—B., 1885; ent. Admvy., 1902; seconded as exec. engrn., Falkland Is., 1923; col. engrn., 1928; mem., leg. coun., 1929; title of office altered to dir., pub. wks., 1931; mem., exec. coun., 1933.

SCOTT, HENRY HAROLD, M.D., F.R.S. Edin., F.R.C.P., London; F.R.S. Edin., D.P.H.; M.R.C.S., Eng.; D.T.M. & H. (Cantab.), Fell. of Royal Soc. of Medicine; Fell. of Roy. SELBY, John Alexander Robertson, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.)—B. 1900; venereal diseases offr., med. dep., Hong Kong, 1930.

SELLS, Martin Perronet, O.B.E., A.M.I.M.E. M.I.Loco.—B. 1887; ed. Trent Coll. and Univ. Coll., London; junr. asst. loco. supt., Lancs and Yorks. SHEEHAN, JOHN JOSEPH.—B. 1896; ed. Christian Schla., Dublin Univ. and Univ. SHREWSBURY, HERBERT SUTCLIFFE, F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1878; ed. Newchurch Grammar Schl., Nottingham Univ.; asst. govt. analyst, Trinidad, 1910; prin. asst. govt. analyst, 1912; govt. analyst, 1920; author of various papers on orig. and analytical processes, and on agrl. and forensic chemistry.

SHUCKBURGH, Sir John Evelyn, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.B. (1818).—B. 1877; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1906; apptd. jnr. clk., store dept., I.O., Oct., 1900; transf. to judi. and pub. dept., Mar., 1901; joint editor, "India Office List," Oct., 1901; priv. sec. to permant. under-sec. of state, July, 1902; senr. clk., polit. dept., Mar., 1903; asst. sec., polit. dept., Oct., 1912; sec., polit. dept., Mar., 1917; ag. asst. under sec. of state, C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; asst. under sec. of state, 1st Apr., 1924; dep. under sec. of state, 15th Aug., 1931.

SHUTE, Geoffrey Gay, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Sch., Eastbourne, Uppingham and Clare Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; cl. I, admst. serv., 1930; prin. asst. sec., S. Prova., 1931; sec., S. Prova., 1933; staff grade, 1936.

SIDEBOTHAM, John Buddleth, B.A. (1914), M.A. (1920).—B. 1891; ed. King's Sch., Canterbury, and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge (Stanhope exhibr., open class exhibr., 1911, school, 1912); 1st cls., theolog. tripso, pt. I., 1914; 2nd lieut., Home Counties R.E. (T.F.), 1914; lieut., 13th June, 1916; serv. in France, 1914-15; wounded, Apr., 1915; demob., 1919; ent. inland rev., Somerset House, 7th Feb., 1920; transf. to C.O. as asst. prin. under reconstruction scheme, Dec., 1922; sec., managing comttee, bureau of hygiene and trop. diseases, 1925; sec., East African guaranteed loan advisory comttee, 1927; pvt. sec. to partly. under-sec. of state for dom. affrs., 16th Sept., 1928; pvt. sec., perm. under sec. for the cols., 8th July, 1929, prin., 24th Jan., 1930; accomp. perm. under S. of S. for the Cols. (Sir J. Maffey) to W. Indies, 1936.

SIDLEY, William Henry Francis, B.A. (Trinity Coll., Dublin).—Cadet, R.I.C., Aug., 1903; 3rd cls. dist. inspr., Oct., 1908; 2nd cls., Feb., 1910; 1st cls., Aug., 1919; county inspr., Nov., 1920; served in R.I.C. until disbandment of that force, July, 1922; depot inspr. (1st cls.), Jamaica constab., Dec., 1922; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1923; dep. inspr.-gen., Deco., 1923; ag. inspr.-gen., June to Oct., 1925.
SILVESTER, J. M.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. commr., Kenya, 1926.

SIMBY, Percy Arnold Tyndall.—B. 1892; ed. Sherborne and Queens' Coll., Cambridge; probationer, native dept., N. Rhodesia, July, 1914; asst. nat. commr., 1916; nat. commr., 1922; asst. mag., 1926; distr. offr., gr. I., 1935; ag. prov. commr., 1936.

SIMMONS, Benjamin Robert—Clk. in conservator of forest's office, E. conservancy, Transvaal, 1898; ag. dist. forest offr., Stutterheim, 1902; dist. forest offr., Stutterheim, 1906; ditto, George, 1914; 2nd grade conservator of forests, Transkeian conservancy, 1929; prin. forest offr. (forestry inspr.), Natal and Transkei, 1932; Western inspectorate, 1935.

SIMMONS, George Stanley, M.I.Mech.E., Chartered Mech. Engnr.—B. 1892; ed. Battersea Polytechnic; asst. loco. supt., Lancashire and Yorkshire's loco. supt., Togoland Milly. rly., Apr., 1917; asst. loco. supt., Gold Coast rly., May, 1920; dist. loco. supt., do., July, 1925; asst. ch. mech. engnr., do., Dec., 1926; ch. mech. engnr., do., Apr., 1934.

SIMMONS, Hon. Wm. Chas.—B. 1865; ed. at Collingwood Owen Sound High Schla.; B.A., Tor., called to the bar, N.W.T., 1900; elec. to legis. assm. of Alberta's bridge dist., 1905; resign., 1908; pres. sup. ct., Alberta, 12th Oct., 1910.

SIMMS, Henry, O.B.E. (1933), M.T., A.M.I.C.E. (1913).—B. 1881; nav. engr., Coast, 1908; navy, engr., Suez Lec., D.P.W., Cyprus, 1919; D.P.W., James Simpson, John—Imp. tel. ser., Ju., 1920; transf., post office, Cape of Good Hope, 1886; survr., posts and tele., May, 1929; survr. inspr., Aug., 1922; ch. pob. ser., July, 1934.

SIMS, George, M.A. (Oxon.), Edan.—B. 1908; ed. Chesterfield Grammar non-collegiate soc., Oxford; asst. nat. dept., Hong Kong, 1930; lect., tech. inst., 35; govt. exam., colloquial Cantonese headmast., govt. secondary sch., Cypr., sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Police, inspr., 1913; asst. commr., 1918; commr. and govt. sec., 1934.

SINCLAIR, Hon. John Ewen—K.C., local common schlr., mem. of swine coms., visited Europe in 1909; unacq. cand. P.E.I., 1908; el. to H.C., g.e., 1917; res. min. without portfolio in King admin., Dec., 1921; senator, June, 1930.
SINCLAIR, Hon. Sir J. R., Kt. Bach—B., 1851; ed. Dunedin High School barrister; M.L.C., N.Z., 1907 and rep. N.Z. on Dominion Royal Comm.

SINCLAIR-BURGESS, Major WILLIAM LIVINGSTONE HATCHWELL (1935), Kt. Bach. (1934), C.B., C.M.G., D.S.O. (1916)—Lieut., N.Z. Staff Gr., 1911; adjt., 16th Waikato Regt., staff group, Mar., 1911; capt., June, 1911; as exchange offr. with Australian forces, 1913; brig.-major, 6th mil. div., left Australia with A.I.F., O.C., 1914; major (A.I.F.), 1914; commr. Australian F.A., Bde., with rank lieu.-col., 1916; commr. 4th Australian Div., rank brig.-gen., Aug., 1917; m. corp., corps, 1919; lieut.-col., Aug., 1920; offr., cent. commd., Nov., 1919; G.H.Q., Apr., 1922; dir., mil. intell., Jan., 1924; ch. of gen. st., with temp. rank col. on staff; ag. mil. forces, Sep., 1925; ch. of mil. forces, Apr., 1931; Croix d'officier Honour; awarded American Distinguished Service Medal, in despo.

SISNETT, Sir Herbert Donnell, Kt. Bach. (1927)—Coll., Barbados; called to the bar, May, 1896; practised at bar, 1897 to Feb., 1898; ag. j. Bridgetown, Barbados, Sept., 1898; ag. res. mag. for St. Anna, J., ag. priv. sec. to Sir A. Hemans, 1902, and priv. sec. to Mr. Oliphant, June to Nov., 1902; clk., Jamaica, Apl., 1903; ag. re. and Trelawny, Sept., 1903, Mar., 1904, do., Manchester, do., St. James and Trelawny, 1906; regiar-gen., Br. Hon. of Belize, Apl., 1907; ag. ad. just., Br. Hon., on several Br. Hon., 1907; stip. mag., 1913 held inquiry into Rose and was highly commended.

CITTAMPALAM, CATHIRAVELU, B.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple).—1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1923; d., Kalutara kach., Mar., 1923; ditto, Kandy kach., Mar., 1925; office asst., Puttalam kach., 1926; pol. mag., Matara, Apr., 1928; ditto, Kottalam, Oct., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Nov., 1929; dist. judge, Kegalle, July, 1931; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, July, 1932; do., Kottalam, May, 1934.
SKAN, D. A., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M., T.H. (Liv.).—B.1899; war serv., R.N.V.R., 1918; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1926.

SKEETE, CECIL CARRINGTON, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1900; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and sua Coll., Cambridge; 3rd class hon. nat. sci. pos pt. I., 1922; dipl. course, Sch. of Agr., Cambridge, 1922-23; asst. dir., agr., Barbados, pt., 1923; ag. dir., agr., in 1924 and 1925; st. dir., sci. and agr., Sept., 1925; ag. dir. i. and agr. on various occasions, 1926-29.

SKELETON, OSCAR DOUGLAS, M.A., Ph.D.—B. 78; ed. Orangeville and Cornwall High Schls., Queen's Univ. and Univ. of Chicago; counsellor, pt. of external affrs., 13th July, 1924; under-c. of state for external affrs., 30th Mar., 1925.

SKENE, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1889; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Wren's; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1912; seconded for serv. in col. sec.'s Ace., 1912-13; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, Nov., 112; col. sec.'s office, 1912-15; priv. sec. to ag. pv., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Ashanti, 1916-18; st. comsnr., Oct., 1918; ag. pol. mag., Dec., 1922; dep. prov. comsnr., July, 1927; prov. comsnr., Sept., 1928; ag. sec., native affrs. Apr., 1930 and Apr. to Oct., 1932; ag. dep. col. so., June-Sept., 1930.

SKINNER, ARTHUR DONALD WILLIAM.—B. 390; ed. Bedford Schl.; 4th clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, Aug., 1914; sub-inspr. of schools, St. Kitts-Nevis, Apl., 1915; o/o cadet corps, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1917; ag. inspr. of schls., Leeward Is., Apr., 1918 to Feb., 1920; clk. o/o comsnr., clk. of couns. and educnl. dist. offr., Montserrat, Feb.-Oct., 1920; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, clk. of couns., Antigua and suplt., govt. printing office, Leeward Is., Nov., 1920; ag. asst. ol. sec. and ag. clk. of couns. Leeward Is. on various occasions 1921-24; bursar, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, 1927; ag. prin., Prince of Wales Coll., July to Sept., 1929; asst. col. sec., Sept., 1931; g. clk. couns., May to June, 1932.

SKINNER, HUGH WELBY.—B. 1894; war serv., 1914-19; served in France, India, Mesopotamia; apt., T.A. res., gen. list, R.A.; appt. col. audit lept., 1923; asst. audr., Nyasaland, 1923; asst. audr., Nigeria, 1926; col. audit, 1930; asst. audr., Gold Coast, 1935.

SKINNER, LESLIE ERNEST.—B.1890; Gloucestershire R.F.A., 1908 to 1910; B.S.A. Pol., 1910 to 1913; E.A.P. pol., Sept., 1913; asst. supt., 1915; staff offr. to inspr. gen. of pol. and prisons, Kenya Colony and Uganda Prot., 1920 to 1922; supt., pol., Nyasaland, 1922; ag. ch. comsnr. pol. and ch. inspr., prisons, 1923 to 1924; asst. commdt., pol., Zanzibar, 1924; ag. comsnr., pol. and gov. prisons on various occasions, 1925-35.

SKINNER, ROBERT BURNELL, M.B.E. (1933).—B.1893; ed. Bedford Modern Schl.; held various minor appts., Leeward Is., 1910-20; ch. elk. to admr., and clk., couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1920; ch. clk. to admr. and elk., couns., Dominica, Nov., 1924; sec., loan bd., May, 1926; mem., Roseau town bd., Apr., 1927; comsnt., income tax, May, 1927; ptye. sec. to Sir Eustace Fiennes, Nov., 1927 to June, 1929; ag. treas., Dominica, June to Sept., 1929; offl. mem., exec. and leg. couns., Dominica, June to Sept., 1929; O.A.G., Montserrat, Feb., 1930; ag. treas. and offl. mem., exec. and leg. couns., May to Sept., 1930; treas., Antigua and fedl. treas., Mar., 1931; offl. mem., fedl. exec. coun. and gen. leg. coun.; mem., exec. and leg. coun., Antigua; dep. treas. and comsnr., income tax, Trinidad, Mar., 1934; ag. treas., Aug.-Dec., 1936.

SLATER, SIR ALEXANDER RANSFORD, G.C.M.G. (1933), K.C.M.G. (1924); C.M.G. (1916); C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; ed. at King Ed. schl., Birmingham, and Emm. Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A. 1897 (30th wrangler); cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1898; extra offr. asst. govt. agt., W. P., Aug., 1899; 2nd asst., P.M.G., Mar., 1900; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1901; on spec. duty at camp for prisoners of war Diyatalawa, July, 1902; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1903; clk. legis. coun., May, 1904; dist. judge, Badulla, Apl., 1906; additional asst. col. sec. and elk., legis. couns., Apl., 1907; on special deptn. to Straits and F.M.S., Jan., 1908; 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1908; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., Dec., 1909; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Sept., 1910; joint comsnr. to report on salaries of pub. serv., Nov., 1911; prin. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1912, and clk. to exec. coun.; col. sec., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; on sp. duty in Togoland, 1st to 28th Sept., 1915; ag. govt., G. Coast on several occasions; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Sierra Leone, Apr., 1922; gov. and comdr-in-chief, Gold Coast, 1927; capt.-gen. and gov.-in-ch., Jamaica, 1932; resigned, 1934.

SLEEP, CAPT. ARTHUR, B.Sc. (Manchester).—B. 1894; cadet, F.M.S., Feb., 1920; attd. land office, Kelantan, Feb., 1920; passed cadet, Nov., 1921; asst. supt., lands, Kelantan, Mar., 1923; asst. dist. offr., Mar., 1925; dist. offr., Aug., 1926; offr., cls. III, Mar., 1931; 2nd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., May, 1931; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, May, 1934; offr. cls. II, Mar., 1936.

SLOLEY, SIR HERBERT CECIL, K.C.M.G. (1911); C.M.G. (1905).—Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griquas and Moirosi (medal with clasp, 1877-8-9); capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1880-1; mentioned in despatches; sub-inspr., Cape police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; inspr., 1886; asst. comsnt., Basutoland, 1889 ag. res. comsnr., July to Dec., 1893; govt. sec., 1898; ag. res. comsnr., Dec., 1900; res. comsnr., Sept., 1901; ret., 1926.

SMALL, ALEXANDER SYM., M.A., B.Sc. (Glas.).—B. 1887; ed. Glasgow Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1911; offr., cls. V, sec. to res., N. Sembilan, Mar., 1914; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1923; town planning adminstr., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1926; offr., cls. Ia, finan. comsnr. and audr.-gen., Johore, June, 1927; offr., cls. Ia, finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S., Dec., 1930; treas., S.S., June, 1932; col. sec., S.S., Dec., 1935.
SMALL, WILLIAM, M.B.E. (Mily.), ment. in despr.—B. 1885; ed. Univ. of St. Andrews; D.Sc.; botanist, Uganda, 1913; mycologist, 1920; mycologist, Ceylon, 1926; dir. of agr., Nyassaland, 1930.

SMALLEY, JAMES THORNTON, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1882; late clinical asst., asst. house surg., out-pat. offr. and house surg., Guy's hosp.; clinical asst., Evelina hosp., London; R.M.O., Colony hosp., Suva, Fiji; D.M.O., Ra; D.M.O. and M.O.H., and port med. offr., Levuka; stip. mag., Levuka, 1910-1913; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, Hong Kong, med. offr., Kowloon-Canton rly.; med. and med. offr. in charge, pub. mortuary, Kowloon, 1913; lecturer in Pharmacology and Therapeutics, and examn., physiology, Hong Kong Univ.; J.P., 1919; visiting med. offr., Kwong Wah hospital; med. offr. in charge, Lai Chi Kok branch prison; surg. lieut., Hong Kong Volunteer Corps; med. offr. gov't. civil hosp., 1923; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., Nov., 1927 to Jan., 1928; ag. dep. dir., med. and sanit. services, Feb., 1928 to Feb., 1929; senr. med. offr. and in ch., Kowloon hosp., Jan., 1931.

SMALLWOOD, MARGARET, M.B., B.S., Grad. L.S.T.M. and Hyg. Trin. Coll., Melbourne Univ.—B. 1898; lady med. offr., inf. wel. centre, Ipoh, May, 1924; do., hosp. Ipoh, Nov., 1927; do., women's hosp., K. Kangsar, Dec., 1933 and Nov., 1935; do., hosp. Ipoh, Sept., 1934.

SMALLWOOD, TROMAN NORMAN, J.P.—B. 1892; ed. Shrewsbury (England), Wellington (N.Z.), and privately; war serv., 1914-17; service with Auckland savings bank, 1908-33; joint managing dir., N.Z. mortgage corpn., 1935; joint managing dir., N.Z. state advances corpn., 1936.

SMART, CAPT. ARCHIBALD GULPILH HOLDSWORTH, M.B.E. (Mil.), M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H.—B. 1882; med. offr., i/c quarantine stn., Singapore, May, 1912; attd., govt. lab., Singapore, Jly., 1913; lent to Kedah for anti-cholera work, Jan.-Apr., 1914; asst. health offr., Singapore, June, 1914; on war serv., R.A.M.C., 1915-18; med. offr., S. Kedah, Oct., 1920; health offr., Kedah, Nov., 1921; ar. health offr., Penang, May, 1927; ditto, Perak, May, 1928; State med. and health offr., Selangor and registr.-gen., births and deaths, F.M.S., Jan., 1932; mem., S.S. and F.M.S. med. coun., Feb., 1932; ret.; senr. med. offr., St. Vincent, 1935.

SMART, HAROLD PHILIP.—B. 1899; ed. Michaelhouse Diocesan Sch., Natal, Glen Sch., of Agr., O.F.S. (Farmers' Weekly gold medallist), Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria, (Farmers' Weekly schol.), B. Sc. Agr., Balliol Coll., Oxford, Imp. Coll., Trop. Agr., Trinidad, A.I.C.T.A.; served with R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1918-20; lecturer in dairy ing., dept. agr., Union of S. Africa., 1926; agr. offr., Br. Honduras, 1928.

SMART, LESLIE MASON, C.B.E. (1936)—B. 1889; asst. supt., Kenya and Uganda Rly., Apr., 1919 to Mar., 1927; dep. traffic man., Tanganyika rlys., Mar., 1927; gen. man., Gold Coast rly. and hbr. authority, Takoradi hbr., Oct., 1933.

SMART, V. IRVING.—B. 1875; ed. Upper Canada Coll., Toronto; graduated from Queen's Univ.; engaged on surveys for the Dom. govt., 1897-98; ent. rly. serv., 1900; prof., rly. engnr. and oper., McGill Univ., Montreal, 1907-14; sp. engnr., Canadian National Rlys., 1923-28; gen. supt., trans., Western region, C.N.R., 1928-30; dep. min. of rlys. and canals, Dominion of Canada; also govt. dir., Canadian National Rlys., 1930.

SMARTT, FITZPATRICK FORBES PERCY.—Ed. Denstone Coll.; probationer, col. audit dept., July, 1921; asst. audr., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1921; asst. audr., N. Rhodesia, May, 1921; Tanganyika Territory, 1934.
SMIT, DOUGLAS LAING.—B. 1883; obs. serv., 1903; pub. prob., 1912; mag., 1913; sec. for just., Pretoria, 1933; sec. nat., 1934.

SMIT, HAROLD PRINGLE.—B. 1883; Cape civ. serv., Jan., 1901; office of intell., May-Aug., 1902; Transvaal ar., Sept., 1902; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., Transvaal, July, 1908; addl. mag., Beombi, Sept., 1910; war stores comm., 1915; mag., Bloemfontein, Dec., 1915; ch. clk., dept. of just., Dec., 1915; pub. serv., inap., Feb., 1922; sec. for intell., Africas, Apr., 1923; dep. adminstr., May, 1924 and July-Dec., 1928; sec. for land, 1934; contr. and audr.-gen., Nov., 1935.

SMIT, JACOBUS STEPHANUS.—B. 1878; (Dekoratie Trouwe Dienst), Anglo-East African, barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1905; sec. to Gen. Smute as col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; Gen. Botha as premier, Transvaal, 1910; Hon. J. de Villiers as atty.-gen., Transvaal, 1910; asst. mag., Transvaal, 1909; addl. mag., Transvaal, 1910; sec. civ. serv., 1910; resig., 1915; el. mem. Bloemfontein, Union of S. Africa, legis. assm., 1920 and 1924; high commr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1925; admstr., Transvaal, 1930; chmn., diamond control bd., 1934.

SMITH, LINUT. ARTHUR BERTRAM, F.R.C.S. B. 1889; ed. R. Masonic Sch., Bursary lieut., R.N.R., Oct., 1914; as. lieut., Jan., 1915; served in 8th submarine flotilla, 1914-15; 1st submarine flotilla, 1915-17; Russian Ostend, Anne, 3rd cls. (with swords), 1915; Russian St. Vladimir, 4th cls. (with swords); commanded submarine C 14 (N. Sea), Aug., 1918, and various surrendered submarines, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; St. Dar-es-Salam, Mar., 1919; harbour and lighthouses, Trinidad, Feb., 1926.

SMITH, ALLAN CHALMERS, B.A. (Eng.), B. 1893; ed. Warwick Acad., Bermuda; Schil., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; cadet, Gray's Inn, June, 1920; war serv., South Africa, 1915-18 (three times ment.); pol. mag., W. dist., Bermuda, 1920; M.L.A., 1920; pol. mag., cent. dist., licensing authorities, Oct., 1931; constab., Nigeria, Jan., 1935.
SMITH, A. H. W., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.M. (Univ. of London), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), St. Ann's lunatic asylum, Trinidad, 1924.

SMITH, B. O.—Cler., asst. postmaster, Guiana, Aug., 1898; sec., leave reg., 1900; sec., deptl. salaries gradually promoted to 1st cls. clk., currency comsrs., July, 1923; treas. secretariat, Apr., 1925; accnt., 1926-29; ch. acct., G.P.O., Dec., 1930; 1933-36.

SMITH, CHARLES ROBERT.—B. 1893; Borneo serv., Aug., 1913; asst. postmaster, 1916; mag., 1st cls., Jan., 1918; 2nd cls., 1919; asst. gov't. sec., Oct., 1920; July, 1929; ag. rec., May, 1930; sec., ag. gov't. sec., Mar., 1934; addl. mag., 1934; ag. judge, high ct., June, 1936; judge, high ct., Sept., 1937.
SMITH, CLIVE ALEXANDER.—Ed. Malton Grammar Schl.; R. N. Navy, inspr., Jamaica constab., Dec., 1929; inspr., May, 1930.
SMITH, CUTHBERT, Dip. in Forestry, B. 1890; asst. conserv., forest dept., 1930.

SMITH, HON. DAVID STANLEY, LL.M.—1888; ed. Southland Boys' High Sch., Wellington Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll. (N.Z.); serv. with N.Z. expd. force, 1917-18; rtrrister, solic., and notary pub. till apptd. judge, Z. sup. ct., 1928; examr. in law, univ. of Z., 1920-27; appointee of U.S.A. as non-tional mem., perm. comm. to settle disputes between U.S.A. and Peru; chmn., native affrs. comm. (N.Z.), 1934; author of "Adoption of children in N.Z." in Journal of Comparative Legislation, Oct., 1921.
SMITH, E. A.—B. 1896; ent. E.I.O., Dec., 1911; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., Dec., 1913; on milly. serv., Dec., 1915 to Jan., 1919; er. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

SMITH, EDWARD PAUL.—B. 1884; Cape mounted pol., June, 1901; clk. to regt. deeds, Cape Town, 1903; clk. to high sheriff, C.T., 1907; 2nd gr. clk., pub. ser. commn., 1913; on milly. serv.; Great War, 1915-18 (wounded); 1st r. clk., ditto, 1919; 1st gr. clk., treas., 1920; snr. clk., P.S.C., 1922; prin. clk., ditto, 1927; h. clk., ditto, 1930; pub. ser. inspr., 1931; under sec., dept. of commerce and industries, 1935.
SMITH, EMILE HAMEL, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., F.P. and S., Glas.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, Sept., 1909; dist. med. offr., Dec., 1912.
SMITH, ERNEST ALLEN.—B. 1886; war serv., 1914-19; asst. audr., Gold Coast, 1924; audr., Eeward Is., 1927; senr. asst. audr., N. Rhodesia, 1932; ag. audr., 1934; dep. audr., Gold Coast, 1936.

SMITH, ERNEST GARDINER, M.A., LL.B.—Advoc.; prizeman in constiit. law and list; ass't. list. commnr., S. Nigeria, Aug., 1908; ass't. pol. mag., Jan., 1911; 2nd grade mag., Nigeria, Jan., 1914; 1st grade mag., Jan., 1914; transfd. to legal dept., Sept., 1914; ag. solr. gen., Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921 and Aug., 1921 to Mar., 1922; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, G. Coast, Aug., 1922; puisne judge, G. Coast, June, 1925; ret., 1931; ch. just., Seychelles, 1935; compiled 1928 edition Laws of Gold Coast, Ashanti, etc.

SMITH, ERNEST JOHN.—B. 1888; ed. E. London Tech. Coll.; milly. serv., Gold Coast Volunteers, 1914-18; asst. acct., constrn., Gold Coast rly., 1920; ch. acct., 1922; dep. ch. constrn. acct., Nigerian rly., 1929; asst. ch. acct., 1930; ch. acct., 1933.

SMITH, FRANCIS EDWARD VINEY, B.Sc. (Hons.)—Ed. Colston's Schl. and Univ., Bristol, 1921; dept. sci. and indust. research, 1921; senr. asst. mycologist, min. of agr. and fisheries, Eng., 1924; gov't. microbiologist, Jamaica, 1927; Jamaica del., Impl. Mycological Confe., and Impl. Meteorological Confe., London, 1929; banana industry ad bd, 1932; ag. dir., agr., 1933; sec., W.I. Fruit and Veg. Confe., 1933; mission, Honduras and Florida on citrus industry, 1933.

SMITH, FREDERIC GORDON, K.C.—B. 1886; ed. Bromsgrove Schl.; solr., Jan., 1909; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, July, 1921; legal dept., N. Rhodesia, 1913; on active serv. in East Africa with N.R. Police, July, 1915 to July, 1918; held various appts. in legal dept., N. Rhodesia during B.S.A. Co.'s admn., including regt., high ct. etc., and asst. legal adviser and pub. prov.; asst. atty.-gen., Oct., 1924; solr.-gen., Kenya, Sept., 1926; atty.-gen., N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1927; ag. judge, high ct., Sept., 1927 to Mar., 1928 and in 1931; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; atty.-gen., Trinidad, Oct., 1932; puisne judge.

SMITH-FOLLETT, ROBERT REDVERS, B.Sc., A.R.C.S.—B. 1901; ed. Skinners Schl. and Impl. Coll. Sci. and Tech. (B.Sc. hons. chem.); Impl. Col. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1925; research asst. to prof., chem. and soil sci., 1927; chemist-ecologist attd. to col. comte., Br. Guiana, 1929; chemist-ecologist, dept. agr., Br. Guiana, 1930.

SMITH, SIR GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1914), C.M.G. (1911).—B. 1858; entd. war office, Apr., 1878; clk., oh. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Nov., 1879; ch. clk., ditto, 1881; asst. to oh. sec., Dec., 1883; clk., legis. coun., Mar., 1886 to Nov., 1891; commr., Papho, Nov., 1891; regar.-gen. and offl. mem. of legis. coun., June, 1895; as sec., Col. and Ind. Exhib. comte., 1886; sec. to Queen's Jubilee Memorial comte., 1887; pres. mun. commr., Papho, 1892; ag. Br. del. of Evcaf., under convn. with Turkey, Mar., to Nov., 1903; ag. prin. forest offr., 1903, and 1907; ag. dir. of agric., 1904 and 1905; Br. del. of Evcaf., July, 1905; ag. recr.-gen., and ch. collr. of cust., 1905, 1906, 1907 and 1909; col. sec., Mauritius, June, 1910; administered govt., Apr., to Nov., 1911; gov't., Nyassaland, May, 1913; assumed govt., Sept., 1913; ret., Deo., 1923.
SMITH, HAROLD DIGBY.—B. 1889; ed. Otago Boy's High Schl., Dunedin; war serv., 1915-19; joined N.Z. postal dept., 1906; regar., pensions, 1914; asst. commr., pensions, 1929; commr., 1936.
SMITH, HARRY ALSTON.—B. 1886; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1913; sub.-inspr. Basutoland mounted pol., 1919; war serv., 1914-18; inspr., 1930.

SMITH, HARRY LESTER, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1884; clk., G.P.O., O.R.C., May, 1903; clk., col. sec.'s office, O.R.C., Aug., 1903; clk., gov't. office, O.R.C., 1907; clk., gov'-genl.'s office, Union of S.A., May, 1910; prin. clk., July, 1924; ch. clk., Nov., 1926; ag. sec. to gov'-genl., Oct., 1925 to Jan., 1927; ch. clk., off. of high commr. for S.A., Jan., 1928; sec. Swaziland finan. and econ. comm., 1931-32; ditto, Basutoland, 1934-35; asst. admin. sec., 1934.
SMITH, HUGH MIDDLETON BRICE, M.A.—B. 1884; ed. at Pocklington Schl. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; asst. res., N. Nigeria Jan., 1909; 2nd cls. dist. offr., Oct., 1917; ag. mag. in 1921 and 1922.
SMITH, JAMES.—B. 1882; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1911; sub.-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1917; inspr., 1926.

SMITH, JAMES DAVID MAXWELL, M.A. (Aberdeen).—B. 1895; cadet, F.M.S., July, 1920; asst. collr., land rev., Lower Perak, Oct., 1920; passed cadet, July, 1922; offr., cls. V, Sept., 1922; asst. sec. to res., Perak, July, 1925; offr., cls. IV., Sept., 1926; offr., cls. III., Sept., 1931; retrenchment offr., F.M.S., May, 1932; ag. legal advr. and judl. commr., Kelantan, Apr., 1934; offr. cls. II., Sept., 1936.

SMITH, JAMES LANDRETH.—B. 1870; draftsman, P.W.D., St. Lucia, July, 1891; draftsman, survey offr., Sept., 1893; warden, and supt. of water-wks. under St. George's town bd., Apr., 1895 to Aug., 1901; chief overseer of roads and works, Grenada, Aug., 1901; asst. supt. of wks., Apr., 1902; commr. of crown lands, June, 1904; survr., crown lands, 1904; ag. supt., wks., June, 1906 to Jan., 1907; supervisor, gov't. cotton ginnery, Oct., 1912; col. engrnr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; supt., pub. wks., St. Kitts-Nevis, Sept., 1920.

SMITH, JOHN, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1883; ed. New Vety. Coll., Edinburgh (M.R.C.V.S.), and Liverpool Univ. (D.V.H.); vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, June, 1913; resigned, Apr., 1916; active serv., 1916-19; capt.; reappt., Oct., 1921; ag. ch. vety. offr., Nov., 1921-May, 1922; ch. vety. offr., May, 1922; mem., leg. coun.; ag. sec. for agr., Apr., 1925; J.P.; dir., animal health, Nov., 1929.

SMITH, JOHN NOEL.—B. 1886; ed. at Summer Fields, Oxford, Radley Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Rcs.) Batt., Oxford and Bucks. Light Infantry, 1909; seconded for serv. as asst. res., N. Nigeria, Nov., 1910; attd. Nigeria Regt., Cameroons, Mar., 1915; station mag., N. Provs., Mar., 1919; dist. offr., Jan., 1921; ag. sen. res., Sept., 1923.
SMITH, JOSEPH MARO, O.B.E. 1927, M.R.C.V.S.—Vet. inspr., min. of agric., Egypt, 1912-14; lieut., R.A.V.C., 1914; mily. service, 1914-19; major, 1919; ch. vety. offr., Palestine, July, 1920.
SMITH, L. J. D.—B. 1893; ed. Rastrick Grammar Schl.; U.K. cust., 1914-16; mily. serv., 1917-19; aspt. cust., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; senr. supervsr., cust., 1935.

SMITH, LAWRENCE DELPRÉ.—B. 1905; ed. Christ's Hosp., and Hertford Coll., Oxford (class. exhib.); B.A. 1928; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1929; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1929; asst. dist. offr., 1931; ag. crown coun., Jan., 1933-Apr., 1934; res. mag., Apr., 1934.

SMITH, LEO ANDREW, A.R.S.M., Assoc. Inst. M.M.—B. 1886; inspr., mines, Perak, June, 1916; asst. warden, mines, June, 1925; sr. inspr., mines, Larut, Feb., 1931; asst. warden, miner, Selangor, Aug., 1932; ditto, Perak, Mar., 1933 and Nov., 1934; ag. warden, mines, Perak, Sept., 1933.

SMITH, LESLIE STEPHEN.—B. 1898; entd. reglary. of friendly societies, 16th Jan., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., 27th Apr., 1916; on mily. serv., Jan., 1917 to Feb., 1919; trans. to savings bank dept., G.P.O., May, 1919; trans. to C.O., June, 1923; higher grade cler. offr., 1st Mar., 1930.

SMITH, LINDSAY LEE.—B. 1870; ag. clk. to commr., Turks Is., 1894, 1896, 1900, and 1903; confirmed 1st July, 1903; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of customs, postmr., cashier and regiir. of shipping) commr.'s office, June to Sept., 1904, and May to Aug., 1906; acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmr., cashier, and regiir. of shipping), Jan., 1907; gen. man., savings bank, 1st July, 1907; ag. asst. commr., Dec., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from Sept., 1912 to Apr., 1913.
SMITH, M.G., M.A.—B. 1904; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Dec., 1926.

SMITH, NORMAN LOCKHART.—B. 1887; ed. at Sedbergh and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Hastings scholar); B.A. 1910; cadet, Hong Kong, Oct., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., (north), 1912; ag. 2nd A.R.G., 1913; J.P. 1913; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1913; ag. 1st asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. asst. postmr.-gen., 1914; ag. postmr.-gen., 1915; seconded for military service 1916-19; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1919, 1920 and 1923; recd. Chinese order of Wen-hu, 5th cls., 1920; aspt., impts. and expts., 1921; attd. Geneva opium confce., 1923; head of saniy. dept., 1924; P.M.G., Oct., 1928; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk. of couns., 1930; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1931; dir., educn., 1933; sec. Chinese affrs. and mem., exec. and legis. couns., Mar., 1934; O.A.G., Sept., 1935; sec., Chinese affrs. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Nov., 1935; col. sec., 1936.

SMITH, P. C., M.I.Munic.R. (Lond.)—B. 1875; ed. at Wyggeston boys' schl., Eng.; pupil at Messrs. Keites and Fosbrooks, arks. and smtn. Leicester, for five years, and remained four years as asst.; joined staff of Messrs. Smith and Langley, contrs., Lond.; asst. to Mr. C. I. Mike, M.I.C.E., city engr., Sheffield; town engr., Rainy River, Ont., Can.; town engr., Souris, Manitoba, 1911; dist. engr Berbice, Br. Guiana, Aug., 1915.

SMITH, REGINALD HOPKINSON, O.B.E. (C.B.E.)—B. 1898; ed. Chatham House, Banbury; served in the Royal Naval Divn., Oct. to Dec., 1914; H.A.C. Oct., 1915 to Oct., 1917; Irish Army R. of O., Nov., 1917 to Aug., 1919; Field Force, July, 1918 to July, 1919; dist. offr., Somaliland, July, 1919.

SMITH, ROBERT KNOWLTON, LL.B., K.C.—B. 1887; ed. St. Francois Xavier Univ., Antwerp, N.S.; grad. Dalhouise Law Schl.; admitt. Nova Scotia bar, Mar., 1911; K.C., Jan., 1925; crown procs., Cumberland County, N.S. Aug., Oct., 1925; mayor, Amherst, 1923; el. to H.d. Canada, Oct., 1925, re-elec. at g.e., 1926; dep. min. of marine, Aug., 1935.
SMITH, SIDNEY W.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919; asst. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. el. 29th Oct., 1930.

SMITH, STEPHEN HENRY, C.B.E. (1929). F.A. Ins. H.S.—B. 1865; ed. Grafton Superior Fire Sch.; inspr., continuation schls., N. S. W., June, 1912; asst. under sec., educn., dep. N.S.W., June, 1920; dir., educn., N.S.W., 1925.
SMITH, STEPHEN JOHN.—B. 1887; N.Z. sec. and tel. dept., 1903; internal affrs. dept., 1903; sec., Samoa adminstr., 1915; ch. clk., exec. affrs. dept., 1920-30; ch. clk., Cook Is. 1920-28; sec., 1928.

SMITH, TRAFFORD, M.A.—B. 1912; ed. Boys Schl., Leicester and Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1930-34 (state schl., senr. schl., Jeston Exh.), B.A., 1933 (1st cls. pts. I and II mod. and languages trip); apptd., after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., 1935.

SMITH, WILLIAM FORGAN, M.L.A. (Queensland).—B. and ed. in Scotland; M.L.A. (Queensland) since 1915; successively min. without folio, sec. for pub. wks. and sec. for agr. affs.; stock; premier and treas., June, 1932.

SMITH, WILLIAM HARDEN, B.A., M.R.C.I., B.A.O. (T.C.D.), L.M. (Rot.), I.F.R. (R.C.P. and S.I.), D.T.M. (Calcutta).—ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; surg. sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1914; med. offr., Zanzibar, Nov., 1925; ag. D.D.S. on various occasions, 1930-33; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1935.

SMITH, WILLIAM HUMPHREY.—B. 1879; ed. Uppingham and Jesus Coll., Camb., Rustschuk, 1897; Abbott univ. schol., 1899; B.A., 1st class, tripso, pt. I, 1901; 2nd cls. pt. II, histor., 1902; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., Dec., 1903; asst. audr., Nyassaland, Oct., 1904; audr., N. Nigeria, Feb., 1910; asst. exchequer and audit dept., Aug., 1910; audr., Seychelles, Mar., 1911; 1st divn. clk. (2nd cls. col. audit dept., Oct., 1912; on mil. serv., 1914; capt., K.O.Y.L.I., Jan., 1915; wounded Mar., 1917; relinquished commn. owing to wounds and returned to col. audit dept., Sept., 1918; audr., Uganda, Dec., 1923; audr., Kenya, 1927.

SMITH, WILLIAM RAMSAY.—D.Sc., M.I.M.S., Edin.; chmn. cent. bd. of health, exec. vaccination offr., and inspr. of anatomy, stralia, 1899-1903; chmn., cent. bd. of health coroner, 1903-29; author of several medical scientific works.

MUTS, GEN. THE RIGHT HON. J. C., P.C. (7), C.H., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. at Victoria mill., Stellenbosch; B.A., Cape Univ., 1891; scholar at Christ's Coll., Camb.; double law trips, 1894; barrister, Cape Col.; attorney, S. African Republic, 1895; served Boer forces during S. African war, and was cmd. of Boer forces in Cape Col. during the later part of the campaign; mem. of head of Het Volk; col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; mem. of Imp. Conf. on Naval and Military defence, 1909; min. of finance and defence, of S. Africa, 1912; min. of defence, 1915; command of troops in E. Africa, 1916; mem. British war cabinet; represented S. Africa at conf. at Paris, 1919; prime min. and min. of native affrs., Union of S. Africa, 1919-24; P. for Standerton, 1924; leader, opposition, 1924-33; pres., Br. Assn. for Advancement of science, 1931; min. of just. and dep. prime min., allition govt., 1933; leader, S. African delegation, World Economic Conf., 1933.

SMYLY, SIR PHILIP CRAMPTON, KT. BACH. (905).—B. 1866; ed. at Trin. Coll., Dub., B.A., L.B.; called to the bar, King's Inn, Dub., 1888; L.D., 1891; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1892; queen's advoc., S. Leone, 1895; atty.-gen., 1896; just., S. Leone, 1901; ch. just., Gold Coast, 1911; ret., 1929.

SNELL, EDWARD WALTER.—B. 1879; asst. office, Kingwilliamstown, Mar., 1900; asst. P.O., Cape Town, Nov., 1900; clk., accts. ranch, July, 1903; 1st cls. clk., Apr., 1912; income tax office, Pretoria, Sept., 1914; senr. clk., income tax office, Cape Town, July, 1915; senior clk., rev. office, Johannesburg, Mar., 918; prin. clk., ditto, Jan., 1921; clk., ch. accts, income tax, head office, inland rev. dept., Pretoria, Aug., 1921; asst. compt. inland rev., Sept., 1929.

SNELLING, ARTHUR WENDELL.—B. 1914; d. Ackworth Schol. and Univ. Coll., London; Sc. (economics) lst. cls. hons., 1934; study group sec., Royal Inst. of Internat. Affairs., 1934- 16; appl. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1936.
SNYMAN, NICOLAAS.—Prof. asst. to survr., O.F.S., 1920; survr.-gen., O.F.S., Dec., 1931; ditto, Natal, May, 1934.

SOLLIS, CLIFFORD GEORGE, M.A.—B. 1895; ed. Bristol Grammar Schol. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; asst. mast., Bukit Zahrah Sch., Johore Bahru, Dec., 1920; ag. inspr., schls., Pahang, Oct., 1925; do., Penang, Mar., 1928; asst. educn., Kedah, Dec., 1930; inspr., schla., Selangor, Aug., 1931.
SOLOMON, SAUL.—B. 1875; ed. Bedford Grammar Schol. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; M.A.; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1900; K.C., Union of S. Africa, 1919; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., 1927.

SOMASUNTHARAM, KANDIAH.—B. 1897; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Kalutara kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Colomba Kach., Dec., 1922; office asst. to rubber contr., Mar., 1923; office asst., Galle kach., Aug., 1926; 5th asst. col. sec., Nov., 1926; 4th asst. col. sec., May, 1928; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Jan., 1929; addnl. asst. contr., rev., Nov., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, July, 1931; dist. judge, Kegalla, July, 1932; sec., min. for lab., industry and commerce, June, 1933; dep. contr., lab., May, 1934; contr., finance and supply, Jan., 1936.

HERBERT SOMERS COCKS, K.C.M.G. (1926), D.S.O. (1919), M.C. (1917).—B. 1887; ed. Charterhouse and New Coll., Oxford; serv. in Great War, 1914-19; capt., 1st Life Guards, 1914; lieut.-col., Tank Corps, 1918-19; ret., 1922; a lord in waiting in ordinary to the King, 1924; gov'r. of Victoria, 1926-31.

SOMERS, CECIL GEORGE.—B. 1896; served Dardanelles and E. Mediterranean with H.M. Transporta, 1914-18; gov't. pilot, Kenya, 1924; asst. port offr., Zanzibar, 1929; ag. port offr., on various occasions, 1929-35.

SOIFER, MAJOR EDWARD JOSEPH, (R. of O.). V.D.—B. 1889; engng. acct., F.M.S. Rly., Sept., 1923; asst. acct., Jan., 1925 and June, 1931; fincl. offr., engng. dept., July, 1928; do., gr. I., Sept., 1928; rev. acctnt., Aug., 1935.

SOPER, JOHN ROBERT PHILPOTT, B.A. (hons.), Cantab.—B. 1904; ed. Taunbridge Schol. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (schol.); headmaster, agrl. prim. schl., Zanzibar, Sept., 1926; seconded for duty as agrl. offr., 1926; agrl. offr., 1928; do., S.S. and F.M.S., 1936.

SOUTH, FRANCIS WILTON, M.A. (Cantab.)— B. 1886; ed. Marlborough Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; mycologist and lect. in agrl. sci., Imp. dept. of agrf. for W. Indies, Mar., 1909; ch. agrl. inspr., agrl. dept., F.M.S., May, 1913; ch. agrl. field offr., Nov., 1924; ag. sec. for agrl., S.S. and F.M.S., for various periods 1927-31; rep. of Malaya at mycological conf., in Lond., Sept., 1929; ag. dir., agrl., S.S. and F.M.S. for various periods, 1929 and 1931-32.
SOUTHBOURGH, 1ST BARON (creat. 1917), RT. HON. SIR FRANCIS JOHN STEPHENS

HOPWOOD, P.C. (1912), G.C.B. (1916), G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.B. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1906), C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1860; ed. at Louth by Canon W. W Hopwood; admitted a solr., 1882; asst. law clk., B. of T., 1885-1888; asst. solr., 1888-1892; priv. sec. to pres. of B. of T. 1892; sec., fly dept., 1892-1901; perm. sec., 1901; perm. under-sec. of S. for the colonies, 1907-1910; employed on different occasions upon offl. missions to U.S.A., Canada, and Newfoundland; Brit. deleg. to internat. rly. congress in London, 1895; and in Paris, 1900; hon. sec. to chmn. of select comte. of H. of C. on Jamaica raid, 1897; mem. of London traffic coman., 1903; visited S. Africa as mem. of Transvaal and O.R.C. constitutions coman., 1906; mem. of comans. on canals and waterways, 1906; and on ocean freights and shipping "rings," 1906; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to Quebec, 1908; mem. of Royal coman. on electoral reform, 1909; reg'ar. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1907-1909; sec. of the Order, 1909-1911; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Connaught on his visit to S. Africa in connection with the opening of the first parlmt. of the Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn. of the Development coman., 1910; specially apptd. to act as under-sec. of state for the colonies during the period of the Imperial Conf., 1911; additional civil lord of the Admiralty, Jan., 1912; sec. to the Irish Convention, 1917.

SOUTHORN, SIR (WILFRID) THOMAS, K.B.E. (1933), C.M.G.(1927).—B. 1879; ed. Warwick schol. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (M.A.); hon. LL.D. (Hong Kong Univ.) 1936; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1903; off. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Oct. 1904; N.C. Prov., May, 1905; dist. judge, Tangalla, Apr., 1907; landing survr., customa, Colombo, July, 1909; astt. govt. agt., Matale, Nov., 1911; priv. sec. to gov., Aug., 1914; dep. collr. of customs and landing survr., Colombo, Sept., 1915; 2nd astt. col. sec., Dec., 1919; prin. astt. col. sec., July, 1920; prin. collr., cust. and chmn., Colombo port commn., Dec., 1923; mem., leg. coun., 1923 and exec. coun., 1925; col. sec., Hong Kong, May, 1926; O.A.G., for various periods, 1927-35; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Gambia, Oct., 1936.
SPAFFORD, H. H., B.A.—B. 1899; mily. serv., France, 1917-18; demob., 1921; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Jan., 1928.

SPARROW, HERBERT ROBERT, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1889; astt. elec. engrnr., F.M.S., Mar., 1920; elec. engrnr., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1927; ag. dep. ch. elec. engrnr. and inspr. in addn., various periods, 1928-31; ag. ch. elec. engrnr. and inspr., F.M.S., June, 1934.

SPARROW, WILLIAM LABARTE.—B. 1904; pol. probationer, Nov., 1924; astt. supt., pol., Hong Kong, 1927; seconded to Weihaiwei as junr. dist. offr., Jan., 1928; passed in Mandarin and Cantonese; attd. Punjab pol., 1931-2; passed in Urdu, 1932; ag. supt., pol., 1936.

SPEED, SIR EDWIN ARNEY, KT. BACH. (1911).—M.A.; LL.B.; B. 1869; ed. at Rugby, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; major scholar and senior exhibtr., Rugby schl.; scholar and prizeman of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. clas. tripso, 1890; 2nd cls. law tripso, Part I., 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1893; dist. comnrr., G. Coast, 1899; atty.-gen., Lagos, 1900; edited a revised edition of Lagos laws, 1901; has acted on numerous occasions as ch. just. and also as col. sec., from Jan., 1905, to Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., S. Nigeria, May, 1906; edited revised edtn. of laws of S. Nigeria, 1907; ch. just., N. Nigeria, 1908; retired.
SPEIRS, R. C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.T.M. (Liv.)—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1925.

SPICER, ROY GODFREY BULLEN, C.M.G. (1934), M.C. (1918).—B. 1889; ed. Colet Court and St. Paul's; probationary astt. supt., pol., Ceylon, Nov., 1909; astt. supt., 1911; supt., 1915; on war serv., 1915-19; (liet., The Carabiniers); wounded, Oct., 1918; returned to Ceylon pol., 1919; supt., grade I, 1923; comnrr., pol., Kenya, June, 1925; inspr.-gen., pol. and prisons, Palestine, 1930.

SPILLER, JOHN WYATT, M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Wellington Schl. (Som.); served in engnr. dept., G.W.R., as pupil, survr., and draughtsman, new wks. astt. and divnl-engnr., 1896 to 1910; joined engnr. dept., Crown Agents, 1910; services lent to Minv. of Munitions, 1916; tech. adviser to dir., Munitions Overseas Transport, 1917; returned to C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engrnr., 1919; visited Ceylon, F.M.S., and S. Stlntma., 1920; ch. engrnr. (designs), 1922; visited Br. Guiana and Trinidad, 1922; acted for one year as gen. man., and ch. engrnr. rly., F.M.S., 1926.
SPOONER, ARTHUR CASWELL.—B. 1906; ed. Epsom Coll.; cadet, Gold Coast, 1929; astt. dist. comnrr., 1930.

SPOONER, MAJOR JOHN CHARLES GARTH, M.C.—B. 1883; ed. Marlborough Coll.; jr. astt. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., 1903-06; astt. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., 1906-12; dist. engrnr., grade III, 1912-14; dist. engrnr., grade II, 1914; on active serv., 1915-19; dist. engrnr., grade I, Ipoh, Jan., 1920; sen. dist. engrnr., Ipoh, Jan., 1921; senr. dist. engrnr., J. Bahru, Mar., 1927; K. Lumpur, Nov., 1928; engrnr. was av. June, 1929; ch. engrnr., do., Nov., 1934.

SPOWERS, JAMES HOWARD.—B. 1889; authorised survr., Queensland, Oct., 1911; serv. F.M.S., Nov., 1913; astt. supt. survrs. in 1919; do., trig. survrs., F.M.S., Nov., 1922; do., rev. survrs., Sept., 1924; ag. exp. survrs., Johore, Jan., 1931; do., Kedah, Oct., 1932; supt., survrs., F.M.S., and S.S., June, 1934; do., rev. survrs., Selangor and N. Seman., Nov., 1935.

SQUIBB, FRANCIS LEOPOLD.—B. 1886; Towcester Schl.; served Manchester Rgt. R. 19; Imp. war grws. comm., 1929-22; est. botanic gardens, Kew, 1922; astt. supt., bot. and forest dept., Hong Kong, 1924; curatur and agrl. offr., Dominica, 1926; astt. supt., Apr. to Nov., 1931; astt. dir., agr. sechelles, 1932; dir., Mar., 1934; mem., d. lez. couns., Apr., 1934.

STACPOOLLE, GEORGE WENTWORTH.—comnrr., lands survey dept., Gold Coast, transf. to lands dept., 1927; ag. dep. comm., July, 1928-Apr., 1929; ag. comnrr., Jan., 1932 and 1933; comnrr., lands, Oct., 1933.

STAINES, CAPT. EDMUND ALFRED.—B. 1886; P. and T. dept., G. Britain, 1899; Br. sec. offr., U.K.-U.S.A., 1907; astt. supt., P. and T., F.M.S., Sept., 1911; mil. service, 1915-18; acct., P. and T., F.M.S., 1921-1925; compt. and T., Perak and Dindings, June, 1927; in Selangor and Pahang, June, 1928; do., Penang and P.W., May, 1931.

STANHOPE, REGINALD ALFRED BROWNE, B.V. Sc. (Melb.), M.R.C.V.S., Melb., Univ. of Royal Vet. Coll., Lond.—B. 1891; vet. offr., F.M.S., May, 1920; do., Pahang, June, 1924; Aug., 1935; N. Sembilan, Feb., 1921; N. Selangor and Malacca, June, 1932; Selangor, Sep., 1934.

STANLEY, SIR HERBERT JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1930), K.C.M.G. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—R. ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford (M.A. priv. sec. to H.M. min. resident at Dresden, Coburg, and British vice-consul at Dresden, 1902; astt. priv. sec. to first Lord of the Admiralty, 1906-1908; priv. sec. to Lord Pres. of the Com., 1908-1910; priv. sec. to Viscount Gladstone, gen. of Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. to gen. gen., 1913; resident comnrr., Southern and Northern Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1915: Imperial sec. and acctnt. to high comnrr. for S. Africa, first gov. and commdr.-in-chief, N. Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1924; gov. of Ceylon, Aug., 1927; E. M. high comnrr. for S. Africa, and first high comnrr. for the U.K. in the Union of S. Afra., Apr., 1931; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., S. Rhodesia, 8th Jan., 1935.

STANLEY, ROBERT CHRISTOPHER STAFFORD.—B. 1899; ed. Westminster schl.; R.M.A., Woolwich; R. G. A., 1918-21; on war serv., Palestine, 1918; admstive offr., Nigeria, May, 1925; do., Cyprus: astt. sec., Dec., 1931; commr., Mar., 1936.

STANLEY, W. B., C.M.G. (1923), M.R.I. (1918), Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1917.—Served with 1st Border regt. occupation of Owa 1898; S. Africa, 1899-1900 (med. and 4 clas. 2nd liet., W. India regt., 1900; liet., 1901; ag. adjt., 3rd W. India regt., Gambia encl., 1901; (med. and clasps); ag. trav. comm., Gambia May and June, 1901; trav. comm., Gambia, Aug., 1901; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Gambia, Aug. to Oct., 1901; capt., 1901.

TANTON, SIR AMBROSE THOMAS, K.C.M.G., C.M.G. (1920), M.D., F.R.C.P., D.P.H., T.M. and H.—B. 1875; sen. house surg. (to Patrick Manson), hosp. for trop. diseases, 1926; author of publications on "Beri-beri," "Malayan Culicidae" and "Meloidiosis."
TAPLES, JAMES WEBSTER.—B. 1902; apptd. cler. exam., asst. clk., G.P.O., Oct., 1920; clerk, 1st Jan., 1920; transferred to C.O., July, 1923.
STAPLEY, FRANK R.—B. 1895; mily. serv., pt., 1914 to Dec., 1919; temp. clk., C.O., Apr., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 4th July, 1922; higher grade cler. offr., 17th Feb., 1930.
STATHAM, HON. SIR CHARLES ERNEST, K.T., M.C. (1926).—Mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1915-35; speaker, 1923-35; M.L.C., 1936.

STEAD, KINGSLEY WILLANS, C.B.E. (1933), B.E. (1928).—B. 1883; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham; apptd., after open compet. exam., st. in impl. cust., Cardiff, Mar., 1903; Harwich, st., 1906; asst. collr. of cust., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1908; collr. of excise, Apr., 1910; passed prelim. exam. in modern Greek, June, 1910; reg. of trade marks, June, 1911; passed lower standard exam. in modern Greek, June, 1912; asst. cable nr., Aug., 1914-Apr., 1919; cable nr., May-July, 1919; pub. cust. of enemy property, Jan.-Mar., 1920; refugee consnr., Larnaca, May, 1919 to Apr., 1920; reg. of patents, Aug., 1920; 1. collr. of cust. and excise, Oct., 1921; inspr. of sherries, 1921; Medaille de la Reconnaissance francaise, 3rd cls., 1922; ag. consnr., Larnaca for various periods in 1922, 1923 and 1924; reg. of companies, July, 1922; ch. supt., Port of Larnaca, June, 1922; reg. of companies, July, 1922; reg. of Cyprus ships, Jan., 1923; reg. of Br. ships, Jan., 1923; dir. cust., excise, and trade, Palestine, July, 1924; ag. reg. gen., Aug. to Dec., 1924; reg. of trade marks and patents, Apr., 1928; ag. reg. of companies and co-op. societies, Apr.-Dec., 1928.

STEAD, ROBERT J. C.—B. 1880; asst. gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1913; gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1917; dir. publicity, dept. of immigr. and colon. Ottawa, 1919; has written several books.
STEED, L. F.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., Feb., 1920, apptd. after compet. exam., cler., offr., O.S.O., July, 1922.

STEEL, HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1875; ed. Grenada Gram. Schl. and Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1899; admitted Grenada bar, July, 1899; mag., dist. F., Dominica, Jan., 1916; ag. mag. and registr.-gen., dist. E., Dominica, for various periods, 1917-20; ag. crown atty. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., St. Christopher and Nevis, Oct. to Dec., 1918; ag. crown atty. and mem. of the exec. and legis. coun., Dominica, Feb.-Mar., 1917, and July-Oct., 1920; pol. mag., Grenada, Oct., 1930; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1931.

STEPHENS, MAJOR FRANCIS TRANT, O.B.E. (1919), M.C., King's Police Med.—Served in S. African war, 1900-02; joined B.S.A. pol., Rhodesia, 1903; comand., 1907; served with Rhodesian column in S.W. Africa and E. Africa, 1914-16; seconded to 1st K.A.R. for service in E. Africa, 1916-20; 4 times ment. in desps.; ch. comand. pol., and ch. inspr. of prisons, Nyasaland Prov., July, 1920.

STEPHENSON, JOHN EVERARD, O.B.E. (1935).—B. 1893; ed. at Winchester Coll. and Oxford; passed intermediate exam. in Laws, London Univ., 1911; scholar, New Coll., Oxford, 1912; 1st cls., class. mods., 1914; called to the bar, 1919; B.A. (war), 1919; tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Sept., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. priv. sec. to S. of S., 18th June, 1920; ag. prin., 1st Mar., 1923; attd. secretariat, Imp. Economic Conf., 1923; prin., 12th Dec., 1924; U.K. delegation to L. of N. Assembly, 1925; attd., secretariat (economic sec.), Imp. Conf., 1926; jnt. sec., conf., on opern. of Dom. legislation, 1929; admvte. asst. sec., Imp. Conf., 1930; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1936.

STEVEN, GEORGE HENRY, M.B., Ch.B., U. Edin., 1901.—B. 1877; ag. res. surg., St. Lucia, 1919; bac. M.O.H. and port health offr., Grenada, 1920-21; C.M.O. (col. surg.) St. Vincent, 1921-25; med. ser., B. Guiana, 1925; G.M.O., pub. hosp., ag. asst. res. surg., pub. hosp., Georgetown, 1926; ag. bac. and path., 1925-26; govt. bac. and path., 1926.
STEVENS, BERTRAM SYDNEY BURNSIDE.—B. 1889; treas., N.S.W., 1929-30; premier and treas., 1932.

STEVENS, GEORGE ALEXANDER, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Bancroft's Schl., Woodford, Essex and Queen's Coll., Oxford; foundation schol. in mod. hist., 1919; 2nd dis. hon., mod. hist.; B.A., 1923; ast. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

STEVENS, WILLIAM OSWALD, M.A., Oxon.—B. 1891; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; attached to Jaffna kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in adln. May, 1915; att., Batticaloa Kacherhe, Oct., 1915; pol. mag., Matale, Aug., 1918; ditto, Avisawella, Jan., 1923; ditto, Kandy, Mar., 1923; ditto, Colombo, Feb., 1925; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Sept., 1927; ast. govt., agt., Matale, Nov., 1931; ag. govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Nov., 1934; dep. contr., lab., Dec., 1935.

STEVENSON, CECIL YOUNG, M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Mech.E., F.B.Met.Soc.—B. 1886; ed. Queen's Coll., Belfast; lieut., R.E., 1910; ast. engr., P.W.D., E. Africa, Jan., 1913; capt., R.E., 1915; adjt., 1916; served with B.E.F., France, Aug., 1914 to Sept., 1916; Indian Ex. Force and Aden Field Force, 1917-19; "Mons Star," ment. in desps.; exec. engr., German E. Africa, July, 1919; ag. D.P.W., Apl. to Dec., 1920; ag. dep. D.P.W., on various occasions, 1921-30; D.D.P.W., 1931; ag. D.P.W., 1931-33; D.P.W., Jan., 1934.

STEVENSON, DUNCAN.—B. 1899; ed. Lincoln Schl., Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1923; 2nd lieut., R.E., 1918; lieut., 1919; France, 1918-19; ast. conserv., forests, Br. Honduras, Jan., 1924; dep. conserv., forests, Apr., 1926; ag. conserv., Apr.-Oct., 1927; senr. ast. conserv., forests, N. Rhodesia, 1929; ast. conserv., forests, Gold Coast, Apr., 1934.

STEVENSON, MAJOR HUBERT CRADDOCK, M.C., O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1888; ed. Glengorse, Meads, Eastbourne and Harrow; war serv., 1915-19 (command batt., R.F.A.); admstv. offr., cls. IV, Nigeria, 1920; aast. sec., S. Provs. secretariat, 1926; ag. prin. aast. sec., do., 1927; aast. sec., do., 1927; ag. senr. aast. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1929; ag. res., 1933-34; admstv. serv., cls. I, 1934; ch. commnr., Ashanti, 1936.
STEVENSON, NEIL STUART.—B. 1902; ed. Lincoln Schol. and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1924; aast. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1924; conservator, Jan., 1930.
STEVENTON, L. E.—B. 1897; ed. County Schol., Wolverton and Elstow Schol., Bedford; on mily. serv. in R. Africa, Egypt, Salonika and Asia Minor; aast. divnl. supt., Nigerian rly., 1928.
STEWART, AUGUSTUS GEORGE, M.D., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Med. supt., Durban hosp., 1922.
STEWART, CHARLES DUNCAN, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1888; supt., meteorological br., survey dept., S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1929.

STEWART, DOUGLAS ROY, C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1886; ed. Gordon's coll., Aberdeen; clk., native dept., Fiji, Apl., 1905; cadet, July, 1906; passed cadet, Dec., 1909; stip. mag. of colony and ag. stip. mag., Nadroga and Colo West, and gov'ts. comsnr., Colo West, May, 1910; ag. aast. col. sec. on several occasions, 1910-1916; ag. clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Aug., 1911 and July, 1913; priv. sec. to adminstr., June, 1912; 2nd aast. col. sec., Jan., 1912; mem., bd. of examiners, Fijian language, Dec., 1913; aast. native comsnr. and gov'ts. comsnr., Rewa Prov., May, 1914; aast. sec. for native affairs, Jan., 1916; lieut., Fiji defence force, July, 1917; hon. A.D.C. to ag. gov., June, 1918; aast. col. sec., 1917; ag. sec. for native affairs, mem. of leg. coun., bd. of health, etc., 1920; prin. aast. col. sec. and editor, native newspaper "Na Mata," May, 1921; ag. col. sec. and mem., exec. and legis. couns., May, 1920 and Apr., 1921; ag. aast. gen. immigr. and editor "Rajdut" 1922 and 1923; ag. col. sec., etc., Sept., 1922 and Jan., 1924 to Apr., 1926; gov'ts. dep., June and Sept., 1924, Jan., May and Aug., 1925; dep. comsnr., W. Pacific H.C. and ag. aast. high comsnr. and gov'ts. dep., Sept.-Oct., 1925; gov'ts. dep., Jan., 1926; col. sec., Barbados, Aug., 1926; mem., exec. and leg. couns. and exec. comtee. also ch. bd. of dir., sugar industry agrl. bank; ag. gov., Apr.-July, 1927, Feb., 1929 and June-Sept., 1930; rep. col. at lst standing W. Indian Confco, 1929 and at Inter-col. Trade confco, Trinidad, June, 1931; adminstr., St. Christopher and Nevis, July, 1931; adminstr. govt., Leeward Is., 1935.

STEWART, CAPT. JOHN LIVINGSTONE, M.C., B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1894; enlisted Scottish Horse, 1914; 2nd lieut., Argyll and Sutherland Highlrs.; served with B.E.F., France, wounded, M.C., Br. War and Victory meds.; ment. in desps.; vety. offr., Gold Coast, Dec., 1923; prin. vety. offr., 1930.

STEWART, CAPT. MALCOLM JOHN.—B. 1893; 2nd lieut., Lovat Scouts Yeomanny, 1914; Indian Army, 1916-22; capt., 1919; Impl. Schol. of Instr., Zeitoun, Cairo, 1919-21; coy. offr., coy. commdr., 53rd Sikhs F.F.; aast. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1923; ag. senr. aast. treas., 1930, 1931, 1932, 1933; ag. treas. and ch. of cust., Somaliland, Sept., 1933-Mar., 1934; treas. and collr., cust. regar. gen. and harbour, Falkland Is., and O.C., F.I.D.F., 1936; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1936; dep. govt. h. July, 1936.
STEWART, NEIL, M.M.—B. 1883; Highlanders (Black Watch), 1915-19; aast. aupt., pol., Kenya, Sept., 1915; 1927.

STEWART, MAJ.-GEN. Sir ROBERT GREGOR, K.C.B. (1902)—B. 1886; en. Hazara campaign, 1888; A.D.C. to Queen Vic., Soudan, 1886; A.D.C. to Queen Vic., 1897; late commdr., R.A., south. dist. prov. gov't. of Bermuda, 1904-07.
STEWART, WILLIAM.—B. 1881; ed. Dist. High Schol. and Victoria Univ. Col., entd. N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1892; crown lands and ch. suvtt., Christchurch, valuer-gen., 1934.

STEWART, WILLIAM GORDON, B.S., A.M.I.C.E., Chld. Civ. Engrnr.—B. 1886; engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; exec. engrnr., Sept., 1929; exec. engrnr., Nov., 1929; F.M.S., May, 1930; do., S.S., Aug., 1930; senr. exec. engrnr., July, 1935.

STEYN, DANIEL, B.A. (Cape Town, Univ. of S. Africa).—B. 1893; ed. Boeshof Schol. and Gymnasium, Paarl; judge's regiar., O.F.S., 1920; pris. sc. min., 1924; Union consul-gen., Lourenço Marques, Sept., 1929; accredited rep., Union of L. of N., Mar., 1934.
STIRLING, JOHN WIGHTMAN.—B. 1888; offr., Beaufortland, 1912; prin. med. Prot., 1935.

STOCKDALE, SIR FRANK ARTHUR (1937), C.M.G. (1932), O.B.E. (1925); tab.), F.L.S.—B. 1883; ed. Withebelle Coll. Camb.; Holmes exhibition, Camb., 1901; B.A. (lst da., Nat. Sc. M.A. 1911; mycologist and lect. in science, Impl. dept. of agric. for three years, May, 1906; on sp. serv. to Trinidad, with diseases of coconuts, 1906; on sp. serv. W.I. agric. confco, 1907 and 1908; dept. of science and agric., and govt. of Guiana, Aug., 1908; dep. chmn., Guiana, 1909; mem., agric. schol., mem., banana comsnr., 1910; on sp. serv. Guiana in connection with banana comsnr., tobacco comtee., 1911; comsnr. ana at Int. rubber exhibn., London, agric., Mauritius, 1912; vice-pres. agric., Mauritius, 1913; mem., comsnr., comsnr., of govt., co-operative credit societies, J. 1913; bd. of dir., Mauritius Inst., 1914; comsnr., comtee., 1914; visited tea dists., N. India, 1914; investigation, 1925; aast. agric. adv., 1929; editor "Tropical Agric." of several repts., etc., on tracts.

STOCKS, ARTHUR HUGH.—B. 1883; Halleth Gram. Sch., Halifax; Oxford; B.A. (Oxon); wr., 1917-18; passed progress exam., dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 1919; secy. to ag. gov., 1916-17; 1922; senr. dist. comsnr., 1932.

NE, ARNOLD ALFRED PRICE DUNBAR, (1929), M.I.C.E., F.S.I.—B. 1877; eng. taman, Sierra Leone, 1912; ag. ast., Nov., 1916 to June, 1919; ag. col. eng., Jan., 1920; dist. eng., Dec., 1920; ag., May to Oct., 1922 and May to Oct., 1924; D.P.W., Cyprus, 1925; leg. coun., 1925-31; ag. gen. man. rly. in 1927.

ONEHAVEN, 1ST BARON (cr. 1925), Rt. SIR JOHN LAWRENCE BAIRD, BART., P.C., G.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. 1904, D.S.O. (b.), J.F., D.L.—B. 1874; ed. Eton and Christ rch, Oxford; ent. diplomatic service, Vienna, Cairo, 1898; Abyssinia, 1899; pvt. sec. to U. Garstyn, under-sec., pub. wks., Egypt, 1902; ag. agt. and cons. gen., Abyssinia, 1902; offr. Br. E. Africa and Abyssinian frontier rev., 1902-3; polit. offr., Abyssinian army in mainland, 1903-4 (med. and clasp, desps., I.G.); 2nd sec., Paris, 1904-6; Buenos Aires, 1914-15 (desps., D.S.O.); M.P., Rugby, 0-22; Ayr Burghs, 1922-25; parly. mem., Air., 1916-18; parly. under sec., R.A.F., 1918; ly. under sec., H.O., 1919-22; min. of transp., 1922-24; 1st commr. of wks., 1924; gov. and commr.-in-ch., Commonwealth of Australia, 1925-31.

STOODLEY, EDWIN ALAN.—B. 1890; ed. melgh Schl.; col. audit dept., Mar., 1915; audr., Nigeria, May, 1915 (seconded to E. rica, mily. audit, 1916-19; ast. audr., mainland, 1919); dep. audr., Gold Coast, July, 3; ag. audr., May, 1934.

STOOKE, GEORGE BARKSDALE, F.R.G.S., R.E.S., F.R.A.I.—B. 1897; ed. Bath; ymr. cadet, R.N., Jan., 1914; ast. naval sec., Alta Dockyard, 1915; ast. sec., rear admir. Sir R. L. Nicholson, 1917; do. to Adml. Sir L. Sykes, 1918; transf., emergency list of offrs., N., 1919; cadet, Sarawak civ. serv., 1920; ast. o., 1924; dist. offr., Kenya, 1925; ag. clk. of c. coun., 1931; clk., exec. coun. in addn., 1932; st. treas., Mauritius, 1933; dep. treas., Kenia, 36; author of various papers on anthropological objects.
