STOREY, HAROLD HAYDON, M.A., Ph.D. (antab.).—B. 1894; ed. Cambridge Univ.; mily. rr., 1914-19; mycologist, dept. of agr., Union S. Africa, Aug., 1922; plant pathologist, E. African agrl. research stn., Amalan, Tanganyika territory, Feb., 1928.

STORRS, SIR RONALD, K.C.M.G. (1929), K.T. ACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1916), C.B.E. (Mil.) (1919), B. 1881; ed. Temple Grove, Charterhouse and embroke Coll., Cambridge; classical scholarship, charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; st cls. class, tripes, 1903; ent. min. of finance, Egypt, 1904; occupied posts in secretariat, finance, dept. of mines (sec.), cust. admin., nd audit dept., Egypt, 1909; served with sir E. Gorst, Lord Kitchener, Sir H. MacMahon and Sir R. Wingate; ast. polit. offr. to Anglo-French polit. mission, E.E.F., 1917; liaison offr. for the mission in Baghdad and Mesopotamia (desps.); secretariat of war cabinet, autumn, 1917; mem., Comité, Conservation des Monuments Arabes, Cairo, also comtee., Palestine exploration fund; mily. gov. of Jerusalem, 1917-20; civ. gov., Jerusalem, Jaffa, Judsea and Southern Palestine; founder, Pro-Jerusalem Soc'y, Jerusalem schl. of music, Jerusalem chamber of commerce; chmn., Palestine local govt. comm., chmn., Palestine bd. of higher studies, vice-pres., Palestine Oriental Soc'y.; Knight of Grace of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; cmdr. of the Crown of Italy and St. Saviour of Greece; gov. and cmdr.-in-ch., Cyprus, Sept., 1926; do., Northern Rhodesia, Nov., 1932; ret., 1934; rep. Cyprus at 17th cong. of orientalists, Oxford, 1928; rep. Cyprus at C.O. confee., 1930; joint edr., Handbook of Cyprus, 1930 edn.

STOURTON, I. H. E. J.—B. 1901; ed. Stonehurst Coll., Lancs.; prob. inspr., pol., Mauritius, Oct., 1921; A.D.C. and prte. sec. to O.A.G., Apr., 1922-Mar., 1923; inspr., Jan., 1924; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., July-Dec., 1932; ch. of pol., Bermuda, 1933.

STOUTE, CYRIL EUSTACE—B. 1882; clk., dept. of agr., Barbados, Nov., 1898; after various promotions, apptd. 1st grade clk., and ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk., leg. coun., Feb., 1925; ag. col. sec., for various periods, 1926-32; man., govt. savings bank, 1932.

STOWELL, EDGER DE LA MOTE, B.A.—B. 1893; ed. Sedbergh Sch.; schl., Corpus Christi Coll., Camb.; ast. mast., F.M.S., Mar., 1921; ast. mast., Penang Free Sch., Nov., 1924; instr., Penang Normal Cls. in addn., July, 1925 and Oct., 1931; headmr., High Sch., B. Mertsjam, Jan., 1927 and Dec., 1930; ar. normal instr., Penang in addn. Apr., 1927; ast. inspr., schl., Sept., 1931; ag. supt., educn., Kedah, Mar., 1933.

STOYLE, HERBERT BLIN.—B. 1888; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Schl., Crediton, Battersea Polytechnic and L. and S.W.R.; Stan Creek rly., Br. Honduras, 1914; ag. dir., pub. wks., 1919-20; ag. dist. commr., Stan Creek, 1920; J.P.; ast. loco. supt., Tanganyika rly., 1921; dist. loco. supt., 1923; loco. supt., K.U.R.H., 1932.
STRACHAN, PETER DONALD, C.B.E. (1934), M.D.—B. 1872; med. offr., Bechuana Land Prot., 1921-23; supt., Bauteiland leper asylum, 1923.

STRACHEY, SIR CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1926), C.B. (1920).—B. 1862; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. jun. clk., F.C., 2nd Apr., 1885; 1st cl. jun. clk., 1st May, 1885; sec. Uganda rly. comtee., 10th Sept., 1895; 1st cl. clk., C.O., 20th Nov., 1898; prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1907; delegate on Anglo-German Boundary Conf. (Yola-Chad), Mar., 1906; ditto, Anglo-French (Niger-Chad), May, 1906; ditto, Anglo-German (Yola to sea), Oct., 1909; mem., W. African lands comtee., 1912; plenipotentiary at Brussels Internat. Liquor Conf., 1912; travelled in Nigeria, 1914; mem., Br. delegation, Paris Peace Conf., Jan. to Sept., 1919; Br. rep. on internat. comtees, for preparation of conventions on (1) African liquor traffic, (2) arms traffic, (3) revision of Berlin and Brussels Acts, signed at St. Germain, 10th Sept., 1919; ast. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; ast. under sec. of state, 12th Dec., 1924; ret., Sept., 1927.
STRADBROKE, (3rd EARL OF) (cr. 1821).

GEORGE EDWARD JOHN MOWBRAY ROUS, BARON ROUS (cr. 1796), VISCOUNT DUNWICH (cr. 1821), K.C.M.G. (1920), C.B. (1904), C.V.O., A.D.C., D.L., J.P.—B. 1862; ed. Harrow and Cambridge; col., 1st Norfolk Royal Garrison Art. Volunteers, 1883-1908; col., 3rd (howitzer) brig., E. Anglian divn., R.F.A., 1908-17; col., 272nd brigade, R.F.A., Jan. to Nov., 1917; gov., Victoria, 1920-26.

STRAHAN, FRANK, C.V.O., C.B.E., B.A., LL.B.—Sec., prime min's. dept., C'wealth of Australia; sec., Australian delegn. to the Imp. conf., 1923; govt. dir., Amalgamated Wireless... (A/asia) Ltd.; sec., Br. Australian and N. Z. Antarctic research expedit.

STRAHAN, KENNETH CYPRIAN.—B. 1880; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and King's Coll., Cambridge; Ceylon govt. rly., 1908-15; R.A.S.C.M.T., 1915; capt., G.H.Q., Inspection Branch, 1917; ment. in desps.; ch. mech. engr., Tanganyika rly., 1920; ch. mech. engr., Kenya and Uganda rly. and harbrs., 1930.

STRATFORD, JAMES.—B. 1869; ed. St. Aidan's Coll., Grahamstown, Cape Colony and Exeter Coll., Oxford; M.A., B.C.L., Oxon; mem., Inner Temple; called to the bar, England, 1898; Transvaal, 1902; practised, Johannesburg; K.C., 1912; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. division, 1st March, 1921; judge of appeal, 1927.

STREAK, M. J.—B. 1879; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; C.S., L.L.B.; asst. mag., Jacobsdal, 1900; Hoopstad, 1903; Heilbron, 1904; Senekal, 1908; Harrismith, 1913; mag., Bothaville, 1916; addnl. mag., Bloemfontein, 1919; mag. and mining conn., Namakgualand, 1928; addl. mag., Pretoria, 1929; mag., Ermelo, 1931; 1st civ. mag., Johannesburg, 1932; mag., Pt. Elizabeth, Mar., 1936; do., Pietermaritzburg, Oct., 1936.
STREET, SIR PHILIP WHISTLER, K.C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1863; puisne judge, N.S. Wales; judge in bankruptcy and probate jurisdictions, Feb., 1907; ch. just., 1925; lieut.-gov., 1930.

STRICKLAND, 1st BARON (cr. 1928), SIR GERALD STRICKLAND, COUNT DELLA CATENA, G.C.M.G. (1913), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1861; ed. Oscott Coll., Birmingham; lieut. C.U.R.V.; and elected mem. coun. of govt., Malta, 1886; B.A. and L.L.B., Trin. Coll., Camb., honours law tripes; attended col. conf., 1887, on behalf of Malta; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., and pres. cholera comsn., Malta, 1887; thanked by govt. for services in negotiations with the Holy See; unoffic. mem., ex. coun., and ag. asst. sec. to govt., 1888; vice-pres., bd. of health, pres. comte. of privileges, ch. sec. to govt., and major, Royal Malta militia, which he established, 1889; re-organized Malta rly., 1891; planned breakwater, 1894; pres., coun. of Malta univ., 1900; chmn., mil. and civ. drainage bd.; mem. comte. on Malta naval reserves; gov. and comdr-in-chief, Leeward Is., 1902; estab. central factories for sugar and cotton in Antigua, 1903; gov., Tasmania, 23rd July, 1904; read scientific papers as pres. of Royal Society, 1904; gov., W. Australia, 6th Apr., 1904; mem. of W. Aust. Inst. of engrns.; gov., New South Wales, 25th Nov., 1912; assumed govt., 14th Mar., 1913; ex-officio gov. of Norfolk Is., 1913-14; codified the laws of Norfolk Is., and negotiated the transfer of the dependency to the Commonwealth; hon. col., 86th W. Australia Infantry Regt.; mem. of Inst. of local govt. engrns., N.S. Wales; held dormant comsn. to administer C. of A. in absence of gov.-gen.; retired, 1917; prime min. of Malta, 1927.
STRINGER, HON. SIR THOMAS WALTER, K.T. BACH. (1928).—B. 1855; ed. high schl., Christchurch, New Zealand; barrister and solr.; K.C., 1908; judge of the sup. ct., New Zealand, 1914-27.

STRONACH, JOHN CLARK, B.A., B.A.I.—B. 1887; ed. St. Andrews Coll., Dublin, Dublin Univ. and Eng. Sch. T.C.D.; asst. engr., Indian pub. wks., 1911; exec. engr., 1919; New Delhi, 1913-26; seconded to Kenya as supl., 1926-29; transf. Kenya, 1930; ag. dir., P.W., 1930 and 1931, 1936.
STRONG, ERNEST EDWARD.—B. 1855; inspr., Basutoland, 1912; sub-insp., mounted pol., 1915; inspr., 1928.

STRONGE, SIR HERBERT (cr. 1919), K.C. (1930), K.C., Ireland.—B. 1875; ed. Schl., and Trin Coll., Dublin; priv. tutor English literature; B.A.; called to King's Inn, Dublin, 1900; joined N.E. July, 1901; stip. mag., Bahamas, 1911; adm. July to Aug., 1914, Sept. to Nov., 1914; May to Sept., 1915; also acted as chief Jan., 1915; chief just. Tonga, 1916; of Tongan govt. for services in 1916; seconded for sup. duty as pres. of com., 1921; ch. just., Leeward Is., 1925; Cyprus, 1931.

STROTHER-STEWART, ROGER B.C.L., B.LITT., Durham Univ., Grademan.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., 1906; the Tynemouth R.G.A. (T.F.), 1914; gen. offr. commdg. Tyne garrison intelligence offr., Tyne garrison, 1919; for valuable services; asst. commdg. authority, Tyne garrison, 1919-20; bar, Inner Temple, 1919; maj. R.O., 1919-27; M.P. for Swindon, 1923-24; mag., county of Victoria, Apr., 1927; temp. asst. legal adviser to Dec., 1929; legal adviser to 2nd Jan., 1930; mem., privy and nomin. coun. of health; examt. in hist. and hist., Univ. of Malta; chmn., enquiry into motor transport, 1930-32; July-Aug., 1932; chmn., traffic commission, 1930-32; chmn., trade union commission, 1932; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1932; just., Oct. Nov., 1935.

STUART, REV. CYRIL EDGAR, M.A.—ed. Dulwich Coll., Repton and S. and Ridley Hall, Cambridge; B.A., 1920; milv. serv., 1914-19, Saloon deacon, 1920; priest, 1921; curate, Hemdy Rise, 1920; chaplain at Hall, Cambridge, 1920-24; chaplain Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

STUBBS, CAPT. EDWARD (1881); ent. R.N., 1885; lieut. (temp.), 1903; commdr., 1916; squadron First Battle Squadron, 1920-22; harbmr., Portsmouth, 1923-25; H.M.S. "Agamemnon" (1925-27); capt., Mar., 1927; master att., Apr., 1927.

STUBBS, SIR REGINALD EDWARD (1928), K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1876); ed. at Radley and Coln. Oxford (exhibitioner); 1st cl. lit. hum., 1897; lat cl. lit. hum., 1899; (1920); apptd., after compet., C.O., Jan., 1900; ag. 1st cls. clk., Dec., 1910; 1st cls. clk., Dec., 1910; F.M.S. and Hong Kong to inst. of revision of civ. ser. salary list of West African lands comm., Ceylon, Jan., 1913; O.A.G., Dec., 1915 to Apr., 1916; 1918; gov., Hong Kong; assumed govt., 30th Sept., Rising Sun of Japan, 1st cls. clk., Hong Kong, 1926; hon. (temp.) Coll., Oxford, 1926; capt., Jamaica, 1926; gov. and comm., Dec., 1932, do., Ceylon.

TUBINGTON, ROBERT ALAN, P.A.S.I.—B. 3; ed. Cranleigh Sch.; surv. prob., F.M.S.; 2; asst. supt., surveys, 1919; ag. supt., rev. surveys, Pahang, 1928; Selangor, 1929; supt., land surveys, Pahang, 1930; do., Selangor, 1933; ag. senr. do., Perak, Dec., 1934.
TUCKEY, REGINALD ROBERT, C.M.G. (1932).—I.A.; pub. actuary, S. Australia, 1914; under ass., since 1923.

TUPART, SIR ROBERT FREDERIC, KT. BACH. (14).—B. 1857; ed., priv. schl.; Upper Can.; ent. Can. meteorological serv., Nov., 1872; some years was sen. inspr. and probability Toronto observ.; in charge of ch. station, Dawson's Straits, 1884-85; supt. and dir., meteorological serv. of Dom. and of magnetic observ., in Dec., 1894; fellow, Royal Soc., Can., 1901; s., Royal Astron. Soc., Can., 1902-03; pres. yal Can. Inst., 1906-07; mem., internat. theological comtee., 1907 and 1919; pres., St. George's Soc., Toronto, 1919-20; ret., 1929.
STURDY, DONALD, B.A.—B. 1905; ed. Sherborne and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; agril. course, 21-26; B.A., 1926; dist. agril. offr., Tanganyika territory, Dec., 1926.

STURGES, ROBERT SIMON MANSFIELD, O.B.E. (29).—B. 1893; ed. Radley and Christ Ch.; st. pol. offr., Iraq civ. admin., Apr., 1920; terial oriental sec. to high commr., Iraq, Feb., 21; asst. sec., Apr., 1921; polit. sec., Oct., 24.
STUTTAFORD, RICHARD.—B. 1870; M.P. for Newlands, Union of S. Africa from 1924; in. without portfolio, 1933.

SUDSBURY, EDWIN RONALD, M.A. (Oxon.).—1897; on mily. serv., Jan., 1916; cadet, Ceylon v. serv., Aug., 1921; attd. to Kurunegala Kach., sept., 1921; attd., Nuwara Eliya kach., Nov., 1921; gtr. office asst. to govt. agt., C. Prov., Jan., 1925; pte. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., Apr., 1925; fice asst. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Feb., 1927; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Nov., 1928; on sp. duties connexion with new constitution, Jan., 1930; admstve. sec., income tax dept., June, 1932; lk., bd. of review in addn.; admstve. sec., amalgamated depts., of income tax, estate duty nd stamps, and clk., bd. of review, Oct., 1933.

SUKUNA, JOSEVA LALABALAVU VANAALI, J.A. (Oxon.), Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—d. Wanganui, N.Z. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; held various appts., col. secretariat and native offices, Fiji 1907-12; served in France with the Foreign Legion and awarded the Mily. Med., 1914-16; ag. asst. sec. for native affrs. and aide-de-camp, 1916; cadet, Fiji, 1917; served in France with Fiji Labour Corps, 1917-18; called to bar, 1921; passed cadet, 1921; native lands commr., 1922; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., 1922; ast. commr. for Fiji, Br. Empire Exhib., 1924; ag. chmn. native lands commr., 1927; dist. commr., Lau, 1932; nom. native mem., leg. coun., 1932.
SULLIVAN, HON. DANIEL GILES, J.P.—B. 1882; ed. Marist Bros. schl., Christchurch, N.Z.; mem., N.Z. H. of R. since 1919; min. of rly. and min. of industries and commerce, 1935.
SULLIVAN, JOHN.—Engr., marine dept., Nigeria, 1910; Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914-16; engr., adviser to French at Duala, 1916; supt. engr., marine dept., 1927.

SULLIVAN, JOHN ALEXANDER, K.C., B.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1879; capt., R. of O. barrister-sol. and atty., former vice-pres., Montreal bar; mem., gen. coun., Quebec bar; mem., bd. of exam., do.; el. to H. of C., Canada, July, 1930; dep. P.M.G., Aug., 1935.

SUMMERS, WALTER LLOYD.—B. 1870; entd. crown lands off., S. Aust., July, 1887; clk., agri. off., May, 1892; chief clk., July, 1902; ag. sec. to min. of agri., Feb., 1910; sec. to ditto, July, 1911; also sec. to min. of industry and sec. to min. of marine, 1915.
SUMNER, JAMES A. W.—B. 1903; entd. L.P.S. (Inland Rev.), May, 1919; apptd. after compet. exam. cler. offr., London telephone serv., Apr., 1921; transfld., C.O., Oct., 1923.

SURRIDGE, BREWSTER JOSEPH.—B. 1894; ed. Felsted Coll., and Downing Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; served, N. Lancs. regt., 1914-17; ast. sec. ch. secs. office, Cyprus, Mar., 1918; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Nov., 1918; ag. ch. ast. sec., Jan. to Apr., 1919; local const., police, Apr., 1919; ag. commr., Larnaca, June, 1919; ag. A.D.C., July, 1920; ag. ast. sec., Oct., 1920; passed exam. in Turkish, Aug., 1922; ag. commr., Aug., 1922; ag. ast. sec., Sept., 1922; commr., Jan., 1924; admstve. offr.; commr. and registrar, co-op. and co-op. credit socs., Jan., 1933; registrar, co-op. and co-op. credit socs., Mar., 1936.

SURRIDGE, ERNEST REX EDWARD.—B. 1899; ed. Felsted and St. John's Coll., Oxford; mod. hist. (hon.); B.A., 1922; lieut. 7th Batt., The Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., 1917-20; France and Germany; ast. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; seconded to secretariat, 1925-28; aide-de-camp and pte. sec. to ag. gov., 1928; seconded to C.O. as ast. prin., Sept., 1930-Feb., 1933; seconded to secretariat, 1933; ag. dep. ch. sec., 1933; ast. ch. sec., 1936.

SURVEYER, HON. EDOUARD FABRE.—B. 1875; ed., Ste. Marie Coll., McGill and Laval Univs.; called to bar, 1896; vice-pres., Jun. Bar Assoc., 1902-03; pres., 1903-04; mem. of coun. of Quebec bar, 1904-05; lectr., law, McGill Univ. since 1905; judge, superior ct., Quebec, 1st Jan., 1920; edr. of practice repts., Que., since 1898; also of pocket eds. of civ. code and code of civ. procedure (the latter in both languages); pres., Can. Club, Montreal, 1909-10; vice-pres., l'alliance Francaise, Montreal.
SUTCLIFFE, MAJOR A. W., D.S.O., M.C., B.Sc. (Durham).—Ast. dist. commr., Kenya, May, 1920.

SUTHERLAND, HON. DONALD MATHESON, P.C. (Can.), M.B., D.S.O.—B. 1879; 1st el. to rep. S. Oxford in Ont. legis., g. e., May, 1902; re-el., g. e., Jan., 1905; dir., colonization and immigr., Ontario, Mar., 1909; el. at g. e., Dec., 1921; re-elect., g. e., 1925 and 1930; min. of national defence in Bennett cabinet, Aug., 1930; re-elect. by acc. after assuming office, Aug., 1930.
SUTTON, G., A.M.C.T.—B. 1902; ed. Accrington Grammar Schl. and Tech. Schl., Manchester Coll. of Technology; mast., Accra tech. schl., Gold Coast, June, 1927.

SUTTON, GEORGE GERHARD, K.C., LL.B.—B. 1880; ed. Dale Coll., King William's Town, Union of S. Africa, Manchester Grammar Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1903; admitted to bar, sup. ct., Cape Town, Oct., 1903; K.C. for Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1921; ag. judge, Cape prov. divn., on various occasions, 1922-28; judge, Cape prov. divn., Feb., 1929.
SWABEY, CHRISTOPHER.—B. 1906; ed. Winchester Coll. and Edinburgh Univ.; Younger silver med. for forestry, 1927; B.Sc. (forestry), 1928; asst. conservator, forests, Trinidad, 1928.

SWAINE, CAPT. DAVID RICHARD, M.A. (Wales).—B. 1889; asst. mast., Penang Free Schl., Sept., 1913; on milly. serv., 1915-19; twice men. in desps., M.C., asst. mast., Penang Free Schl., Feb., 1920; sr. normal instr., Penang, in addn., Mar., 1920; headmast., Northam Rd. Sch., Dec., 1924; European mast., cl. IB; prin., King Edward VII Schl., Taiping Sept., 1929; ag. ch. inspr., Eng. schls., S.S. & F.M.S., Sept., 1934; prin., Raffles Inst., S'pore, June, 1935; asupt., educn., Johore, Oct., 1935.
SWAN, GEORGE.—R. 1897; asst. govt. marine surv., Hong Kong, 1924; Hong Kong del. to safety of life at sea conf., Simla, 1931.

SWAYNE, COL. SIR ERIC JOHN EAGLES, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.B. (mil.) (1904); grand offr. of order of the Crown of Italy (1905).—B. 1863; ed. at naval coll., Gosport, and abroad; R.M.C., Sandhurst, 1882; end. Welsh regt., 1883; Bengal staff corps, 1884; adjut., 1888; capt., 1894; local lieut.-col., 1900; brevet lieut.-col., 1901; brevet-col., 1903; temp. brig.-gen., 1903-1906; retired from army, 1906; served with Burma expdn., 1883-86 (medal with clasp); intell. offr., Hukong Valley reconnaissance; in command of advanced base exploration, Turong River and Sing P'ho country, Nov., 1895 to Moh., 1896 (McGregor medal); exploration to Somaliland, Feb. to July, 1891 (thanked by resolution of Bombay govt., and appreciation by govt. of India); Gildessas affair on Abyssinian frontier, 1892 (commendation by resolution of Bombay govt.); engaged on delimitation of internat. frontier between French and British Somaliland, 1892; served for five years on intell. staff, India, as staff-capt. and D.A.Q.M.G.; apptd. 2nd-in-command, Uganda Rifles, on active serv. in Jubaland, E. Africa (medal with clasp); on active serv. in Uganda, 1899; selected to command Somaliland police force, 1900; raised and organised levies, and commanded expeditionary force in Somaliland, Nov., 1900; defeated and routed Mullah (ment. in desps., medal with clasp, brevet lt.-col., thanked by govt.); organised 6th Batt. Uganda Rifles, second expdn., 1901; again defeated Mullah (C.B., ment. in desps.); apptd. comsnr., consul-gen. and comdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., and judge of its high court, Apr., 1902; on special duty in Egypt, 1904; arranged evacuation of Somaliland, and organised control of tribes; British deleg. to conference with Italian deleg. at Lyons in connection with agreement with Italy respecting Somaliland; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, British Honduras, 1906-13; spec. duty to Canada, with reference to Indian labour (thanked by govt. of India) 1908; spec. duty to Mexico, 1909.

SWEET ESCOTT, Sir E. B., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1857; ed. Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and at Balliol Coll., Oxon; exhibitioner, Wores. Coll., Oxon, June, 1876; 2ndclass classical mods., June, 1878; 3rd class in mod. hist. finals, June, 1880; B.A., July, 1880; M.A., 1911; classical prof. at the Roy. Coll., Mauritius, June, 1881; précis writer, col. sec.'s office, Feb., and 2nd asst. col. sec., Apr., 1889; ag. col. sec., Br. Hon. author of "Thoughts on Modern Church Work."

RS, CECIL GEORGE LEWIS.—B. 1903; ed., Dul's Sch.; and Oxford; classical schol., I Coll., 1921-25; 1st cls. classical mods., 1st cls., lit., hum., 1925; apptd. after t. exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 21st 1925; pvt. sec. to permit. under-sec., for affrs., 14th Nov., 1928; asst. pvt. sec. to S. for Dom. Affrs. (Mr. J. H. Thomas), June, 1930; ditto; to Mr. J. H. Thomas of S. for Dom. affrs. and for theCols., Aug., 1931; accompanied U.K. delegation np. Economic Cnfece., Ottawa, 1932; 1st Jan., 1933; accompanied U.K. air m to Canada and U.S.A., Nov.-Dec., KES, GEORGE ROGNVALD M.A. (Liv.). 889; cadet, S. Stltltns., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd Penang, July, 1914; attd., censor's office, passed cadet, 1917; ag. asst. prot. hinese, Spore, Apr., 1917; asst. regir. and expts. (war trade dept.) Spore, 1917; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese (in addition) 1918; ceased to perform duties in war trade ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Spore, Sept., ag. 3rd mag., Spore, in addn., Dec., ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Spore, 1920; supernm. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; 1st supt., govt. monopolies and asst. prot. of Malacca, June, 1921; asst. prot., Chinese, e., Sept., 1924; ols. III., Nov., 1925; prot., Kedah, Dec., 1926; ag. prot., Chinese, Igor and Pahang, June, 1929 and Sept.; offr., cls. II., prot., Chinese, Perak, Nov.; ag. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, 1933; ag. immigrn. offr., S.S. and F.M.S., 1936.
ME, MARY KERR GODFREY.—B. 1903; ed., Ism Schl.; cadet and asst. dist. comsnr., Coast, 1928.

MES, Lieut.-Col. Sir GEORGE STEWART, M.G.(1932), K.B.E.(1928), C.M.G., D.S.O.—82; ed. Malvern Coll. and Sandhurst; ent. y. 1900; serv. S. African War (Queen's med., spas); Aden, 1903-04 (desps., D.S.O.); attd., Sudan Army, 1905 and Sudan govt., 1907; Blue expdn., 1908 (med.); promoted major, 1915 brevet lt.-col., 1917; A.D.C. to Sirdar and gov. of the Sudan, 1906-07; asst. dir. of intellige., 1908-12; pte. sec. and sec., gov. gen.'s (Sudan), 1913-16; European War (3 times t. in desps., med., brevet lt.-col.); attd. of high comsnr., Egypt, 1917-19; attd. y. of int., Egypt, 1919-20; dist. gov., Palestine, 1920; ch. sec., 1925; admstd. gov., Palestine, July to Sept., 1925 and May to Sept.; accredited rep. to mandates comsnr., 1926 and 1927; res. and commdr.-in-ch., Aden, 1928; and commdr.-in-ch., Tanganyika Territory, gov.-gen., Sudan, 1933.
YMONETTE, W. J.—B. 1878; pub. schl., her, Apr., 1898; 4th divn. comsnr., June, 1898; ag. ch. clk., recvr. gen. in treas. dept., 1922; confirmed in appt., Feb., 1924.

YMONS, CLAUDE TREVINE.—B. 1879; B.A., F.R.G.S.; vice-principal, govt. training Cevlon, Aug., 1908; asst. govt. analyst, 1912; ag. govt. analyst, in addn., Dec., govt. analyst, June, 1919; ag. regir., med. in addn., May to Dec., 1922.

ADMAN, JOHN LAD BROOKE, B.A. (Oxon).—Norwich Schl. and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford (Edsmiths' Exhibn., 1929); col. aud. dept., Feb., 1931; asst. audr., Uganda, Apr., 1931; asst. audr., Mauritius, Feb., 1936.

TAIT, HUGH NIMMO.—B. 1888; ed. at Clifton and St. John's Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. Math. Tripos, Part I. and II.; 1st cls. Nat. Science Tripos, Part I.; apptd. after compet. exam., clk., chief sec.'s office, Ireland, Oct., 1911; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mar., 1912; ag. 1st class clk., 20th July, 1917; on mil. service from 27th Sept. to 9th Dec., 1918; sec. to Rhodesian Comsn., 1919-20; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ag. asst. sec., D.O., 4th Sept., 1929; asst. sec., 24th Jan., 1930.

TAITT, HORACE VIBART.—B. 1887; ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; Island scholar, 1906; 3rd cls. class B.A., 1909; M.A., 1917; educn. offr., educn. dept., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. 2nd asst. inspr. of schools in 1914, 1915 and 1916; ag. 3rd cls. clk., educn. off., Jan.-Apr., 1917; ag. class. mast., Queen's Coll., Apr.-July, 1917; ag. asst. mast., do., Mar., 1930; asst. mast., 1934.

TATHAM, BERNARD MEABURN.—B. 1891; ed., Rossall Schl.; asst. accont., P.W.D., Uganda, May, 1921; ob. accont., Apr., 1922; asst. treas., Uganda, June, 1926, (with seniority from May, 1921); senr. asst. treas., Sept., 1934; senr. asst. treas., Gold Coast, Aug., 1936; ag. dep. treas., Sept., 1936.

TAYLOR, ARTHUR PERCY, M.C., 1914 (Mons Star).—B. 1892; ed. Colchester Royal Grammar Schl.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1926; served with R.N.V.R., R.N.D., R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1914-19; severely wounded, 1917; asst. accont., P.W.D., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; asst. commdt., Northern Territories constab., Sept., 1920; transf'd, to Gold Coast pol. asst. comsnr., pol., Nov., 1923; comsnr., pol. and in charge, C.I.D., 1926; seconded to Ashanti judiciary as ag. pol. mag., May, 1927; pol. mag., sup. ct. Gold Coast, June, 1928; crown coun., Sept., 1932; ag. solr.-gen. in 1933.

TAYLOR, EDWIN, C.M.G. (1936).—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ.ser., and assigned to C.O. Jan. 1901; served with I.Y. in S. African war, 1901-02 (medal and four clasps); re-apptd. to 2nd div., and assigned to B. of T., 1902; clk., P.W.D., B.C. Africa Prov., July, 1906; treas. asst., Uganda, Jan., 1909; asst. treas., civil admin., German East Africa (occupied territory), Jan., 1917; treas., Seychelles, May, 1919; dep. treas., Uganda, Apr., 1923; ag. treas., May 1923 to 1924; senr. asst. treas., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1925; ag. treas., in 1925, 1926, 1928 and 1929; title altered to dep. treas., Jan., 1930; mem. Freetown temp. munic. bd., Dec., 1926; pres. city coun., Freetown in addition, May, 1928; dep. treas., Hong Kong, June, 1930; treas., June, 1931.

TAYLOR, EVAN NUTTALL, Barrister-at-law, Inner Temple.—B. 1898; ent. R.M.A., 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1918, France; R. of O., 1920; cadet, F.M.S., May, 1921; attch. dist. office, Raub, June, 1921; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. V., May, 1924; asst. offr., Rembau, Dec., 1925; offr., cls. IV., May, 1928; asst. dist. offr., L. Perak, Dec., 1928; ag. asst. offr., assignee and regir., companies, Spore, Nov., 1930; offr., cls. III., May, 1933; ag. D.P.P., Penang, Mar., 1934; offr., assignee and regir., companies in addn., May 1934; D.P.P., Mar., 1936.
TAYLOR, HON. GEORGE EDWARD.—B. 1878; ed., London and Toronto; called to the bar, Ont., 1902; practised in London and Moosejaw, Sask.; K.C., Jan., 1913; pres., Sask. Bar Assoc.,
TAYLOR, GREVILLE LAUGHTON, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1902; ed. Lodge Sohl., Barbados and Oxford Univ.; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1927; clk. H. of A., Barbados, 1930; pol. mag., 1936.
TAYLOR, GREVILLE POWER.—B. 1899; ed. Elstow Schol., Bedford, and H.M.S. "Worcester"; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, March, 1925; dist. commr., Mar., 1931.
TAYLOR, H. A., F.I.C.—B. 1888; asst. analyst, Hong Kong, 1913; asst. supt., impts. and expts. dept., 1914; ag. supt. in addn. in 1930.
TAYLOR, PERCY JOHN.—B. 1881; asst. govt. marine survr., Hong Kong, 1921; ag. govt. marine survr., Mar., 1934-Jan., 1935.

TAYLOR, ROBERT STANLEY, O.B.E. (1933), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.A., M.D., B.C., D.P.H. (Cantab.), Cert., S.T.M.—B. 1875; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge; exhib., Charing Cross Hosp., London; D.M.O., Cue, West Australia; M.O.H., Cue and M.O.H., Day Dawn, West Australia; M.O., Uganda, Sept., 1914; active serv., E.A. campaign and temp. capt., U.M.S., Oct., 1914 to Nov., 1916; S.M.O., Somaliland, May, 1922; P.M.O., Somaliland, July, 1925.

TAYLOR, ROBERT WALTER, C.M.G. (1933), C.B.E. (1928), O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1883; ed. Emmanuel Schol. and King's Coll., London; clk. in crown atty's office, 1903-1906; head acctnt., Somaliland, 1906; ag. mil. paymaster, Aug., 1907, to Jan., 1908; Somaliland expdn., 1908-1910 (G.A.S. medal and clasp); asst. treas., Uganda, 1910; asst. treas., Somaliland, Mar., 1914; treas., Somaliland, July, 1915; dep. treas., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1920; ag. treas., Dec., 1921 to May, 1922; treas., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1922; ag. ch. sec., Apr. to June, 1927; gov'ts dep. 10th to 27th May, 1927; ret., Mar., 1933; recr.-gen. and treas., Bahamas, 1936.
TAYLOR, ROYDEN EBNSLOFFE.—B. 1889; ent. civ. serv., Grenada, Oct., 1905; seconded for war serv., Sept., 1915 to May, 1919; J.P., 1919; regt., sup. ct., Grenada, May, 1931.

TEMPANY, HAROLD AUGUSTIN, C.B.E. (1933), D.Sc. (Lond.), F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1881; ed. privately and Univ. Coll., London; asst. govt. chemist, Leeward Is., 1903; ag. govt. chemist and supt. of agric., July to Dec., 1906, and Jan. to Nov., 1909; govt. chemist and supt. of agric., Nov., 1909; J.P., Antigua, 1910; mem. legis. coun., Antigua, Mar. to July, 1912; represented Leeward Is. at 3rd Internat. Congress of Trop. Agric., June, 1914, and at rubber and tropical products exhibit., June-July, 1914; chmn. of trustees Antigua pub. library, 1916; mem., Antigua Defence Reserve, 1905 to 1916; dir. of agric., Mauritius, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, May, 1917; mem., bd. of dirs., Mauritius Inst., Jan., 1918; reported on S. African sugar industry, 1917; organised govt. tobacco factory, 1920; sugar experiment station, 1921; agril. coll., 1923-24; dairy, 1923; prin., Mauritius Agril. Coll. in addn. to substantive appt., 1924; reported on local sugar industry, 1926; organising vice-pres., Mauritius agril. and indus. exhib., 1926; chmn., various comtees; dir., agr. S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1929; pres., bd., R.R.I., Malaya, Apr., 1929; visited Java as del., 4th Pacific Sci. Cong., June, 1929; prin., schl. of agr., Malaya, in addn., May, 1930; chmn., comtees, rice cult., Malaya, July, 1930; del., confce. of col. dirs., of agr., Lond., July-Dec., 1931; dir. agr. S.S., and advr. agr., M.S., Jan., 1935; author of numerous papers and rept. on agril. chem., cane sugar producn., agril. educn. and trop. agr.

TENISON, EDWARD HERON RIAN.—B. Nov. on mily. serv., Apr., 1917; cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1921; attd., Kandy Kach., Dec., 1921; attd., Anuradhapura kach., Feb., 1924; attd., Badulla kach., June, 1925; ditto, Batticaloa; Nov., 1926; pol. mag., Matale, Dec., 1927; govt. agt., Badulla, Jan., 1929; addnl. asst. agt., Colombo, Nov., 1931; pol. mag., (col. Nov., 1932; addnl. asst. ch. sec., Nov., 1933.

TENNANT, MATTHEW PETTIGREW, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1881; ed. Montrose Academy; ch. engr., Jamaica govt. rly., 1905; ag. loco mot. ditto, July, 1909 to Dec., 1910; ag. ch. sec., Mar.-Nov., 1913; senr. inspgr. engr., I.W., Jamaica, Apr., 1914; ag. ast. dir., P.W., Dec., 1918 to Sept., 1921; ag. dir. of pwh., July-Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Uganda, Jul., 1921.

TERRELL, ARTHUR KOBERWEIN & BEAR, M.A. (Oxon.), Barrister-at-law.—B. 1881; Tonbridge Schol. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; clk. to bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1906; war ser., 1914-19; puisne judge, S.S. and judge, F.M.S., 1931; judge, est. of appl., Kedah, May, 1931; ch. just., F.M.S., Feb., 1933 and Apr., 1934; puisne judge, Spore, Aug., 1934.

TESTER, L. M.C.—B. 1891; war ser., 1914-18; treas. asst., Nigeria, 1919; ch. acct., P.W., 1930; Mauritius, July, 1932; ag. ch. sec., 1934; treas. and compt., cust., Zanzibar, 1934; currency offr., Jan., 1935; ag. ch. sec., July, 1936.

TETTEY, VICTOR ADOLPHUS, B.A. (Durham)—B. 1891; ed. govt. primary schl., Accra Mar. Schol., Fourah Bay Coll., Sierra Leone (Durham Univ.); 2nd cls. teacher, Accra Tech. Coll., Gold Coast, Jan., 1915; 1st cls. teacher, Apr., 1921; asst. headmast., Apr., 1923; schls., July, 1925.

TE WATER, CHARLES THEODOR.—B. 1891; ed. Normal Coll., Bedford Grammar Schol., Cambridge, B.A., LL.B.; barrister-at-law, Temple, and mem. of Pretoria bar, Pretoria (Cent.) in Union parl., 1924-25; commr. for the Union of S. Africa in 1929.

THACKER, RANSLEY SAMUEL.—B. 1891; Choir Schol. of H.M. Chapels Royal, St. Paul's, Dulwich Coll. and Univ. of London; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1913; ch. just. and ass. ch. just., St. Vincent, Nov., 1930; rep. St. Vincent confce. of W. Indian sea island cotton, Barbados, Mar. to Apr., 1937; legal advisory staff, C.O., Sept.-Oct., 1937; atty.-gen., Fiji, Nov., 1933; mem. of leg. couns.; ag. ch. just., 1935 and 1936.
THATCHER, J.—Ed. at Bristol Grazing examr. of loan acct., audit dept., O.F.S., 1904; examr. of acct., ditto, July, 1914; and., O.F.S. Prov., Apr., 1912.
THEIS, S. E., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.H. (Lond.)—B. 1903; med. offr., Territorv, Sept., 1927.

THERON, Col., Francois Henri.—B. 1891; ed. St. George's Cathedral Grammar Schol., town; ent. civ. serv., O.F.S., 1909; head horse, 1915; asst. staff offr., 4th Mounted G.S.W.A. campaign, 1915; A.D.C. to Gen. Smuts and attd. Perm. Force Staff, A.D.C. to Gen. Botha and granted tent. capt., 1916; served, G.E. Africa, July, 1916; attd. war cabinet staff as attd. Gen. Smuts, 1917; attd., 2nd S.A. Int., 1917; served in France, 1918; comp.

THOMAS, LESLIE ARDING.—B. 1891; ed. Portsmouth Gram. Schl.; pol. probt., S.S., Nov., 1910; ast. supt., pol., Sept., 1914; ch. pol. offr., P.W., Mar., 1921; dep. comsrr., pol., F.M.S., Sept., 1926; ast. supt., pol., S.S., Oct., 1926; adjutant, S.S. pol., Nov., 1926; commd., pol., depot, S.S., and ag. supt., pol., Jan., 1928; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, July, 1930; do., Selangor, Feb., 1932; dep. comsrr., pol., Perak, 1935; ch. pol. offr., Mar., 1935; ag. comsrr., pol., F.M.S., Apr., 1936.

THOMAS, SIR SAMUEL JOYCE, Kt., Bach. (1835).—B. 1875; ed. King's Coll., London; Inglis schl. and early English Text Soc'y prizeman; assoc. (with distinc.) King's Coll., 1897; B.A., Lond., 1897; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1898; enlisted, 1915; commissioned, R.A.S.C. (M.T.), and attd. to R.G.A. (145th Siege Battery), France, Flanders, Italy; invalided, 1919; ch. just., St. Vincent, Oct., 1919; ag. col sec. and admtr., St. Vincent, Feb. to Dec., 1920, and on three subsequent occasions; thanked by S. of S. for index of laws St., Vincent, 1921; mem., W. Indian ct. of appl., 1921-23; 2nd puisne judge, Trinidad, Mar., 1923; chmn., oil and water bd., 1923; ag. atty-gen., 1923; ag. 1st puisne judge on various occasions, 1923-26 and in 1928; puisne judge, Kenya, 1929; ch. just., F.M.S., Oct., 1933.

THOMAS, SIR THOMAS SHENTON WHITELEGGE, G.C.M.G., (1937), K.C.M.G.(1931), C.M.G.(1929), O.R.E. (1919).—ed. at Leatherhead and Queen's Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A., Hons. 1901; ast. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Aug., 1909; 3rd ast. sec., Apr., 1911; sen. ast. sec., Apr., 1912; ag. ast. ch. sec., Aug.-Oct., 1913, Aug., 1916-Mar., 1917, and Apr., 1917-Jan., 1919; clk. to exec. and legis. couns., 1917-19; mem. E. Africa civ. serv. comm., 1918; ast. ch. sec., Uganda, Jan., 1919; ag. ch. sec., June, 1919 to Apr., 1920; chmn., Uganda development commn., 1919; gov.'s dep., Jan.-Feb., 1920; prin. ast. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Mar., 1921; ag. dep. ch. sec., Apr.-Dec., 1921; dep. ch. sec., Jan., 1924; ag. ch. sec., in 1925 and 1926; gov.'s dep., on various occasions, 1925-26; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1927; gov., Nyasaland, 1929; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Gold Coast, 1932; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Straits Stltles, and high comsrr., Malaya States, 1934.
THOMAS, W. E., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1884; rlv. engnr., Sudan, Brazil, F.M.S., New South Wales, and India; dep. res. engnr., Benue bridge constr., Nigeria, Aug., 1930.

THOMPSON, ALDWORTH, B.Sc.—B. 1900; ed. Cork Gram. Schl., Univ. Coll., Cork and Royal Coll. of So. Lond.; ast. mycologist, agric. dept., F.M.S., Nov., 1921; mycologist, Oct., 1933; del. 3rd. Imp. Mycological Confce., London, Sept., 1934.
THOMPSON, AUBREY DENZIL FORSYTH.—B. 1897; ed. Weenen County Coll., Natal and New Coll., Oxford (B.A.); lieut., R.F.A., 1917-19; cadet, Uganda Prot., June, 1921; A.D.C., July, 1923; dist. offr., 1933.
THOMPSON, C. B.—Ast. dist. comsrr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; 1st grade admstve. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1924; ag. prov. comsrr., 1933; semi-dist. comsrr., 1934.
THOMPSON, DOWNING CHARLES, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1892; mil. serv., 1914-19; ast.

THOMPSON, EDWIN ARCHIBALD.—B. 1898; CADET, GOV'T. LAB., LEEWARD IS., DEC., 1914; 2ND JUN. ASST., GOV'T. LAB., APR., 1915; 1ST CLK., AUG., 1917; SENR. CLK., CLT., ANTIGUA, APR., 1927; TRANSF., GOV'T. LAB., LEEWARD IS., 1928; MILK ANALYST, ANTIGUA, JUNE, 1930; TRANSF., TREASY. AND CUST. DEPT., JUNE, 1935; TARIFF CLK., 1934.

THOMPSON, HENRY BURFORD.—B. 1880; CASHIER, SAVINGS BANK, ANTIGUA, APR., 1906; ACCOUNTANT, DITTO, APR., 1906; 3RD OUTDOOR OFF., TREASY., JAN., 1908; MEM., MIDDLE TEMPLE, 1912; CASHIER, TREASY., MAY, 1914; COMMER. TO ADMSTR. OATHS, ANTIGUA, JULY, 1916; SENR. CLK., 1926; POSTMTR., ST. KITTS-NEVIS, 1936.

THOMPSON, HERBERT W.—B. 1899; ENTD. C.O., 4TH OCT., 1915; ON MIL. SER., FROM 13TH AUG., 1917 TO 9TH APR., 1919; APPTD., AFTER COMPET. EXAM., ASST. CLK., C.O., 18TH JUNE, 1919; CLER. OFF., 1ST JAN., 1920; PROMOTED TO 2ND DIV. CLK., 24TH FEB., 1920; CLER. OFF., HIGHER GRADE, 10TH OCT., 1925.

THOMPSON, JOHN HARRISON.—B. 1892; DIPLOMA, ENGLISH LANGUAGE AND LIT., LONDON UNIV., 1921; APPTD. AFTER COMPET. EXAM., ASST. CLK., COMMERCIAL INTELLIGENCE BRANCH, B. OF T., 23RD JUNE, 1912; LIBRARY ASST., C.O., 6TH APR., 1914; AFTER FURTHER COMPET. EXAM., APPTD. AS CLK. OF THE 2ND DIV. AND ASSIGNED TO C.O., 24TH AUG., 1914; CLER. OFF., HIGHER GRADE, 1ST APR., 1922; ASST. LIBRARIAN, 10TH OCT., 1925; REGISTR., COL. LAWS AND ASST. LIBRARIAN, 19TH NOV., 1928.
THOMPSON, WALTER PHILIP.—B. 1909; ED. ST. LAWRENCE, RAMSGATE; POL. PROBATIONER, HONG KONG, OCT., 1929; ASST. SUPT., POL., DEC., 1931.

THOMPSTONE, CAPT. ERIC WESTBURY, M.C.—B. 1897; ED. SHREWSBURY SCHL.; 2ND LIEUT., KING'S SHROPSHIRE LIGHT INFY., 1915; SERVED IN FRANCE AND ITALY, 1916-19; WOUNDED; SILVER MED. FOR VALOUR (ITALY); ADMINIS., OFF., N.P., NIGERIA, JULY, 1919.
THOMSON, ROBERT.—B. 1892; ED. HIGH SCHL., GREENOCK AND R. TECH. COLL., GLASGOW; SERV. WITH H.M. FORCES, 1914-19; ASST. ENGNR., ZANZIBAR, JUNE, 1925; AG. DIST. ENGNR. ON VARIOUS OCCASIONS, 1927-31.

THOMSON, ROBERT LESLIE.—B. 1880; ED. PERTH ACAD.; ASST. COMMISSARY, BR. GUIANA, 1907-20; SERVED WITH H.M. FORCES IN FRANCE, 1914-18; ASST. INSPR. DISTS., 1925; 2ND GDE. OFF., DIST. ADMSTR., JAN., 1932; ASST. DIST. CONSMR., 1936.

THOMSON, WALTER MORRIS, M.A.—B. 1905; ED. EDINBURGH UNIV.; CADET, HONG KONG CIV. SERV., DEC., 1928; PASSED CADET, FEB., 1931; ASST. SERV., FOR CHINESE AFFAIRS, 1931; 3RD ASST. SEC., TO SEC. FOR CHINESE AFFAIRS, 1931; 1ST CLK., MAG., FEB., 1934; J.P., 1934; 1ST CLK. AND SENR. ASST. COL. TREAS., FEB., 1935; 1ST CLK. AND MAG., MAR., 1935; 1ST CLK. AND POL. MAG., KOWLOON, MAY., 1935; ASST. SUPT., IMPTS. AND EXPTS., DEC., 1935.

THORBY, HON. HAROLD VICTOR CAMPBELL, M.P.—M.L.A., N.S.W. FOR WAMMERAWA, 1922-27; CASTLECAUGH, 1927-30; MIN. FOR AGR., 1927-30; M.H.R. OF COMMONWEALTH OF AUSTRALIA FOR CALARE, N.S.W., SINCE DEC., 1931; ASST. MIN. IN LYONS MINY., 1934; VISITED GR. BRITAIN ON OCCASION OF SILVER JUBILEE.

THORNE, RICHARD ALFRED.—B. 1902; APPTD. AFTER COMPET. EXAM., ASST. CLK., SEC.'S OFFICE, G.P.O., 24TH OCT., 1919; CLER. OFF., 1ST JAN., 1920; TRANSF. TO C.O., 15TH OCT., 1920; CLER. OFF., HIGHER GRADE, 29TH OCT., 1930.

THORNE THORNE, RICHARD JOHN PERCY, M.A. (CANTAB.).—B. 1900; ED. CHARTERHOUSE AND CHRIST'S COLL., CAMBRIDGE; SERVED R.A.F., 1918; 2ND LIEUT., THE BUFFS, 1920; LIEUT., R.C. 26; TROPICAL AFRICAN SERVICES COURSE, CAMBRIDGE, 1926-27; CADET, UGANDA PROT., 1927; PRIV. TO GOV'T., UGANDA, 1928-29; ASST. OFF., DIST. CONSMR., 1934-36; LIEUT. R. OF O.K.I.R. 1933; CAPT., 1935; ADMSTR. OFF., CYPRUS, SEC., AUG., 1936.

THORNHILL, GEORGE KEAL, F.S.I., F.R.G.S., F.R.A.S., F.R.MET.S.—B. 1884; SUPERINT. SURVEY, CEYLON, JULY, 1901; ASST. SURVEY, JAN., 1908; SUPT., SURVEYS, 2ND GRADE, SEPT., 1911; DO., 1ST GRADE, OCT., 1914; ON MIL. SERV., 1918-19; ASST. SURVEY GEN., APR., 1927; SURVEY GEN., NOV., 1920; SURVEY GEN., SEPT., 1927.

THORNTON, LIEUT.-COL. (T.Y.P.).—B. 1906; NEWBURY, K.B.E. (1918), M.C., L.R.C.P., LOND., D.P.H. (CANTAB.), S.A.M.C., COMMANDER OF THE ORDER OF ST. JAMES, JERUSALEM (1924).—B. 1878; ED. CHELTENHAM COLL. AND LONDON HOSP.; AST. MED. OFF., YEOMANRY HOSPITALS, ANGLO-IRISH WAR, 1914-19; PLAGUE SERV., PUNJAB, 1902-03; ENT. SERV., CAPE COLONY, 1903; ADDITIONAL M.O.H., CAPE COLONY, 1906; MED. INSPIR. OF HOOPS AND CHAMBERS, AND CH. LOCAL GOV'T. INSPIR., CAPE, 1909-1911; MED. BR., SEC. I, UNION REG. OF OFFICIALS, GERMAN S.W. AFRICA, 1915; (MENT. IN DESPATCHES) LIEUT.-COL., S.A.M.C. AND O.C.S. AFRICA, 1919; AST. HOSP., RICHMOND, SURREY; MEM., COMMR. MANAGEMENT, AND SURG. TO QUEEN MARIA'S AUX. CONVALESCENT HOSP., FOR LIMBLESS SOLDIERS, RED CROSS MEM., COMMR. MANAGEMENT, QUEEN MARY'S AUX. CONVALESCENT HOSP. FOR FACIAL INJURIES, MEM., EXEC. COMMR. ON VOCATIONAL TRAINING FOR SOLDIERS IN HOSP., LONDON DIST.; REPRESENTATIVE, INTER ALLIED CONF. ON DISCHARGING PROBLEMS ARISING OUT OF THE WAR, 1919-20; AST. HEALTH OFF., FOR THE UNION, DIR. OF HEALTH SERVICES, UNION DEFENCE FORCES, AND CHAMBERS' HOSPITAL, FOR THE UNION, 1920; SEC. FOR PUBLIC HOUSING BD., FOR THE UNION, 1920; SEC. FOR CH. HEALTH OFF., FOR THE UNION, 1932.

THORNTON, GEORGE EDWIN, M.B.E.—B. 1899; WAR SERV., 1916-17 (AFRICAN SERVICE), N. RHODESIA, 1917; AG. AST. COMMR., PERIODS, 1926, 1927 AND 1928; AST. COMMR., APR., 1928; COMMR., INCOME TAX, APR., 1928; TREAS., 1933.

THORNTON, HUGH HOLME, M.B.E.—B. 1881; ED. CHRIST CHURCH, OXFORD; TAVISTOCK, AND CHRIST CHURCH, OXFORD; SEC. TO VISCOUNT MILNER, AS MEMBER OF THE WITHOUT PORTFOLIO, DEC., 1916 TO APR., 1919; ASST. OF STATE FOR WAR, 1918-19; AS SEC. OF STATE FOR COLONIES, 14TH JAN., 1919; APPTD. AS AST. OF STATE FOR COLONIES, 1ST OCT., 1920.

THORNTON, RUSSEL WILLIAM, M.A. (1936).—B. 1881; ED. GRAAF REINET SCHL. OF AGR., CAPE PROV. (DIPLOMA); SERVED IN ANGLO-BOER WAR AS S.W. AFRICA INTELLIGENCE OFFICER; JOINED DEPT. OF AGR., FIELD CROPS AND EXPERIMENT AND RESEARCH, 1905; OFFICER-IN-CHARGE, AGR. EXPERIMENT DEPT., CAPE PROV., 1908; PRIV. TO SCHL. OF AGR. AND EXPERIMENT AT CAPE CITY BY GOVERNMENT, TO ENGLAND, EUROPE, STUDY AGRICULTURAL EDUCATION, EXPERIMENTATION, ETC., TOOK CHARGE OF EXPERIMENTATION IN SOUTH AFRICA, IN CONNECTION WITH CAPE UNION, 1912; ACTIVE SERV., RESUMED CHARGE OF GROOTBOROFT, 1919; DIR., FIELD AND ANIMAL HUSBANDRY, 1923; DIR., NATIVE AGR., 1923; DIR., NATIVE AGR., 1923; ADVR. TO H. CR., 1934.

THOROGOOD, WILLIAM JESSE, M.C.—B. 96; ed. Latymer Upper Sch., and Trinity Hall, amb.; war serv., 1915-18; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 21; attd., Chinese prot., Ipoh; passed cadet, opt., 1923; offr., cls. V., Apr., 1924; asst. contrlr., lab., Seremban, Aug., 1924; asst. dist. ffr., Gopeng and Kampar, Feb., 1926; asst. sec. o res., Selangor, June, 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; asst. contrlr., lab., Klang; asst. contrlr., ab., Penang, May, 1931; ag. emigrn. comsnr. for Malaya, May, 1932; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; ag. dep. contrlr., lab., F.M.S., July, 1936.

TIBBITS, ARTHUR CHARLES KENT.—B. 1877; employed col. sec. & off.; treas. and aud. off., Antigua, Apr. to Dec., 1894; clk. sav. bank, Jan., 1895; 1st clk., post off., Jan., 1898; ag. postmtr. on various occasions, 1899-1907; postmtr. of Antigua, and federal postmtr. of Leeward Is., Apr., 1907; liby. trustee and a J.P., Antigua, 1910; income and trade tax assessor in 1908 and in 1913-15; chmn., bd. of guardians in 1911, 1913 and 1915; dir., P.O.G. fund, May, 1912; ag. asst. censor and ch. censor during war; dep. chmn., city commsnr., Dec., 1916; ag. offr. in ch. Antigua—Montserrat flashlight serv., Aug., 1913 to Feb., 1919; ag. comsnr., Virgin Is., Aug., 1918 to Feb., 1919; warden and mag., Nevis, Oct., 1925; mem., exec. and leg. couns., St. Kitts-Nevis; ag. comsnr., Montserrat and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Leeward Is., May to Aug., 1927 and Jan. to Apr., 1928; rep. Dominica at gen. leg. coun., 1928.

TIBBITT, CAPT. ERIC MONTAGUE (R. of O.)—B. 1896; served in European War, 1914-18; priv. sec. to gov., Br. Honduras, Nov., 1920; ag. clk., exec. couns., Br. Hond. Nov., 1922; asst. audr., Nigeria, Aug., 1924; audr., Br. Hond., Feb., 1928; capt., Br. Hond. Defence Force, Apr., 1928; senr. asst. audr., Uganda, Oct. 1934; audr., Cyprus, 1936.

TICKLE, ARTHUR GEORGE WARNHAM, F.R.I.B.A., F.S.I.—B. 1887; ed. Holy Trinity, Banbury, Polytechnic and Royal Acad., London; asst. engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1913; exec. engr., 1920; tech. sec., D.P.W., 1923-4 and 1928-32; J.P., 1931; asst. D.P.W., 1932; ag. D.P.W., 1933 and 1936.

TILNEY, CHARLES EDWARD, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1909; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1932; attd., office of dir. med. and sanv. services, Feb., 1932; attd., Galle kach., Sept., 1932; office asst., Ratnapura kach., June, 1933; admstive sec., dept. of med. and san. services and sec. to min. of health, Mar., 1934.

TIMMS, CHARLES GORDON, O.B.E. (1936), M.C. (three bars). L.R.C.P., M.R.C.S. Ed., L.R.F.P.S. (Glasgow), Certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1886; ed. Watson's Coll. and R. Coll., Surgeons, Edin.; served with R.A.M.C., Oct., 1914 to Mar., 1919; M.O., Uganda, Jan., 1922; transf., Somaliland, Dec., 1922.

TINDALL, BENJAMIN ARTHUR.—B. 1879; ed. Victoria Coll., Cape Prov.; B.A., LL.B.; called to bar, 1903; practised, Pretoria; K.C., 1919; presided, sp. ct., Swaziland, 1914; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., Dec., 1922.

TINWORTH, WILLIAM LEONARD, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1885; mil. serv., India and Mesopotamia, 1917-19; asst. acctnt., F.M.S. rly., 1921; ag. dep. acctnt., 1926, 1929 and 1930; ag. acctnt., pub. wks dept., 1929; dep. ch. acctnt., Tanganyika rly., 1931; ag. ch. acctnt., 1933; personal asst. to gen. man., 1937.

TISDALE, EDWARD GORDON ST.CLAIR, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Bedford Schl. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; served in France 1st, Monmouth Regt., 1915-18; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.F., Oct., 1919; ag. comsnr., mines, Kenya, 1935 and 1936.

TISDALE, HERBERT GEORGE, B.Sc., A.R.C.S., A.M. Inst. C.E., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Schl. and Imp. Coll. of Science (Whitworth schol.), eng. inspr., C.A., 1907; asst. engr., engrg. contracts dept., 1913; dep. head, engrg. contracts dept., 1926.

TITCHENER, HENRY IAN HERRICK.—B. 1900; entd. C.O., Feb., 1916; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., 24th Apr., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 13th July, 1928; asst. librarian, 14th Jan., 1929.

TODD, CEDRIC LINDLEY.—B. 1897; ed. Sherborne Schl.; served in European War, Western fronts, 1914-18 and Rhine Army, 1919-21; asst. audr., Nyassaland, Nov., 1921; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1929; senr. asst. audr., 1933.

TODD, CHARLES DOUGLAS—B. 1901; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1923-25; resigned; re-apptd., 1929; ag. treas. and ch. of cust., Somaliland, June-Oct., 1935; treas., Antigua and fedl. treas., Leeward Is., 1936.

TODD, RONALD RUSKIN.—B. 1902; ed. County High Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schol.); B.A. (hons.), 1924; M.A., 1928; cadet, Hong Kong, 1924; passed Cantonese, June, 1927; asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., 1927; asst. supt., impts. and expts., 1930; ch. asst. to sec., Chinese affrs., 1933; chmn., urban coun., 1936.
TOLMIE, GEORGE MACKENZIE, B.Sc. (Edin.)—B. 1904; ed. Inverness Royal Acad., Edinburgh Univ. and Imp. Forest Inst., Oxford; asst. conservator, forests, Gold Coast, 1926.
TOMKINSON, C.—Asst. dist. comsnr., R. Africa Prot., June, 1916; seconded as lieut., Carrier Corps.

TOMLINSON, SIR GEORGE JOHN FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1934), C.B.E. (1928), M.A. (Oxon.), Barrister-at-law.—B. 1876; ed. Charterhouse and Univ. Coll., Oxford; Transvaal educn. dept., 1903-04; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1907; seconded to Gold Coast as dir., educn., 1909; reverted to N. TOMLINSON, THOMAS FOWLER, M.C., B.Sc. A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, and Leeds University; asst. engr., constrn., Trinidad govt. rly., 1911; served with R. Engrs., France, 1915-19; res. engr., constrn., Trinidad govt. rly., 1920; temp. D.P.W., Virgin Is., 1928; dist. engr., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1927; ch. engr., govt. rly., Trinidad, 1928; supt., Trinidad govt. rly., 1933.

TOMLINSON, SIR THOMAS SYMONDS, Kt. BACH (1925).—B.A. Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.1877; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1901; town mag., Zanzibar, May, 1907; asst. judge of H.B.M. ct. for Zanzibar, Jan., 1911; ag. judge, Feb. to Nov., 1916; judge (now ch. just.), 11th Oct., 1919; seconded as legal adviser of the civil adminstr. of the occupied terry. of Ger. E. Africa, Feb., 1918; ag. atty-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920 to July, 1921; prepared revised edn. of the laws of Zanzibar, 1922; 2nd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1923; ret., 1928.

TONGUE, ERIC DAUNCEY, O.B.E. (1934); B.A., LL.B.—Ed. Taunton Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schol. and exhib.); astt. dist. commnr., Uganda, October, 1913; dist. commnr., Aug., 1923; lab. commnr., May, 1930; reverted dist. offr. on abolition of post, Oct., 1931; senr. dist. offr., Dec., 1935.

TONKIN, SYDNEY, B. Eng. (Liverpool), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., Chtd. Civ. Engnr., Chtd. Mech. Engnr.—B., 1886; astt. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., June, 1920; exec. engnr., Dec., 1925; do., F.M.S., govt. factory, June, 1929.

TOPP, WM.—B., 1884; Gambia govt. serv., 1901-07; S. Nigeria govt., 1907-08; N. TORY, GEOFFROY WILLIAM.—B., 1912; ed. King Edward VII Schl., Sheffield, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (mod. languages schol., 1931-35); med. and mod. languages trips, pt. I cl. 1, pt. 2 cls. 1; B.A., 1934; apptd., after compet. exam., astt. prin. D.O., Oct., 1935.

TOTHILL, JOHN DOUGLAS.—B., 1888; ed. Blundell's Schl.; Ontario Agr. Coll. (Toronto Univ.); B.Sc., Agr., 1919; Cornell Univ., U.S.A., Graduate Schl., 1913, 1914; Harvard Univ., D.Sc., 1922; agt. and expert, U.S. Bureau of Entomology, 1910-11; entomologist, entomological branch, Ottawa, 1911-24; seconded as dir., Levuana campaign, Fiji, for two years, Nov., 1924; ag. dir., agr., Fiji, May, 1925; dir., Levuana campaign and agr., 1926; dir., agr., Uganda, June, 1929.

TOUGHER, ROBERT ALEXANDER HUNTER.—B., 1891; ed. Royal Belfast Acad. Inst. and Queen's Univ. of Belfast, B.A. (1914); army, 1914-19; war serv., Dardanelles, Egypt and Syria, ment. in desps.; capt., gen. R. of O., 1919; solr., Ireland, 1920; astt. land offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.

TOUSSAINT, JAMES REGINALD.—B., 1879; ag. offr., Matara kach., Ceylon, Jan., 1917; sec., Buddhist temporalities ord. commn., and extra off. astt. to dir., educn., Nov., 1918; apptd. to civ. serv., July, 1919; addnl. astt. commnr. of stamps, Sept., 1921; cls. IV, Mar., 1926; astt. commnr., stamps, Mar., 1930; dep. commnr. stamps, July, 1931; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Oct., 1934; office astt., Kurunegala kach., Feb., 1935; dep. fiscal, Colombo, June, 1935.

TOVEY, DUNCAN LORIMER PATTISON.—B., 1903; ed. Clifton Coll. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; M.A.; astt. priv. sec. (appts.) to S. of S. Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Dec., 1926; to Lord Passfield, June, 1929; apptd. astt. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930.

TOWNLEY, CHARLES HENRY.—B., 1892; served with field coy., R.E.'s (T.) in Egypt, Gallipoli, France, Belgium and Germany 1914-19; astt. govt. printer, Nigeria, May, 1920; senr. supt., Aug., 1926; ag. govt. printer, May, 1927 and Nov., 1929; govt. printer, Gold Coast, 1931.

TRATMAN, EDGAR KINGSLEY, L.D.S., M.D.S.—B., 1899; ed. Clifton Coll. and Bristol Univ.; prof., dental surgery, coll. of med., S'pore, Oct., 1929; mem., dental bd., F.M.S., Apr., 1931; do., S.S., July, 1931.

TRAVERS, MICHAEL F.—B., 1897; ed. St. Mary's and O'Connell's Schls., Dublin; boy clk., Irish land commn., Dublin, 8th May, 1913; astt. clk., board of educn., 22nd Apr., 1915; ret. R. Irish Regt., 19th Apr., 1916 to 6th Dec., apptd. clar. offr., 1st Jan., 1921; transf., 7th Apr., 1926.

TREMLETZ, CAPT. JOHN DANIEL, S.S.C., M.C., M.R.C.V.S.—B., 1886; ed. Harry and R. Vety, Coll., London; do., vet. (G. and France, 1916-18; M.C., 1917; vet., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920; senr. offr., Apr., 1923.
TRENCH, ARTHUR DONALD DE PONTOUX (dip.).—Senr. coffee offr., agr. serv., Oct., 1916.

TREVOR, IVAN BERNARD.—B., 1914; Tytenhanger Lodge, Seaford; Reserv., 1915 (not taken up); cadet, Midland traffic astt., Kowloon-Canton rly., Hong. Dist., 1924; ag. traffic astt. and ch. astt. in addn., 1926 and 1927; ag. ch. astt. passed Cantonese, 1928; traffic man. storekeeper, 1932.

TRINNICK, COMDR. GEORGE WILLIAM, R.N.R.—B., 1889; merchant serv., 1904; master's B.O.T. certif.; R.N., 1914; "Diana," Home waters, near and in H.M.S. "Temeraire," North Sea and marine offr., Nigeria, 1922; lient. comdr., comdr., on retired list, 1934; ag. dir., 1935; prin. marine offr., 1935.
TRIPE, WILLIAM BOWDONALE.—B., 1889; Coll., Christchurch, N.Z.; Victoria Univ. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; astt. dist. commnr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929.

TROUGHTON, EDWARD PERCY.—B., 1889; ed. Forest Schl.; R.N.A.S. and R.N., 1917-18; elk., astt. commnr., Basutoland; clk., finan. sec., 1923; astt. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1926; senr. astt. treas., S. ag. dep. treas., Aug., 1936.
TROWBRIDGE, EDMUND.—B., 1889; model and high schla., Toronto, Ont.; clk., exec. coun., 1906; sec., Alberta, 1907; sec., Alberta pork comm., 1908; sec. and regis. of companies, prov. of Alta., 1910.

TRUPHET, JOHN SIDNEY.—B., 1890; Roan Schl. and King's Coll., London; Crown Agent's office, 1901; astt. dept., 1919; do., insurance and ch. dept., 1920; do., shipping dept., 1922; ch. shipping dept., 1927; head, ship. dept., 1931; head, do., 1933; head, ship. dept., 1934.

TRUSTED, HARRY HERBERT.—B., 1890; ed. Ellesmere Coll. and Tri. Hall; to bar, Inner Temple, 1913; (Duke of Cornwall's L.I. and staff, 1914-19; puisne judge, Leeward gen., 1927; commnr. to revise laws, Leeward Is., 1928; R.C., Leeward atty.-gen., Cyprus, Dec., 1929; advisory staff, C.O., Aug., Palestine, 1932; ch. just., 1936.

TRUTER, SIR THEODORUS (1874), C.M.G. (1918).—B., 1874; Schl., George; Normal Coll., Cape Town; Coll., Rondebosch; Cape civil service, chief examr., aucts., 1895; lieut., 1899-1902 (med. and four o'c.); Pretoria, Apr., 1901; mem., mag., Ernelo, Dec., 1904; mag., 1908; prov. sec., Transvaal, 1909; S. TUCKER, D.E.A., M.C.—Francis de Sales Fathers, G. Florimont Coll., Lancy (G.). re partie) Grenoble Univ., July, 1914; Wilts. regt. and M.G.C., 1914-20; 2nd lieut., June, 1917; H.M. cust., U.K., 1923; inspr.-in-ch., preventive serv., Tanganyika cust., 1929.

TUCKER, LESLIE.—Ed. at Liverpool Collegiate instn. and Univ. Coll., Liverpool; prelim. and inter. B.A.; 1st cls. scholarship; 1st cls. both years certif. exam.; parchment certif., 897; head master, govt. boys schl., St. Helena, 904; head master, senior schl., St. Helena, 1905; hon. sec. and treas., govt. lace schl., 1908; supervisor of govt. schls., 1910; hon. sec., bd. of educn., 1911; govt. rep. on Hussey Charity bd. of educn., 1911; J.P. and coroner and mem., bd. of health, 1912; lieut., St. Helena Rifles, Sept., 1914 to Nov., 1916; lieut., R.A.S.C., Apr., 1917 to Nov., 1919; inspr. schls., Leeward Is., Feb., 1920; chmn., gen. purposes comte., Antigua bd. of educn.; chmn., St. Kitts bd. of educn., 1922; asst. dir., educn., Jamaica, 1930.

TUCKER, L. M.—Ed. Mod. Schl. for Girls, Exeter, Homerton Coll., Cambridge and Univ. Coll., Exeter; mistress, Prince of Wales' Coll., Achimota, Gold Coast, Sept., 1926; organiser, female and infant educn., Apr., 1928; inspr. schls., Apr., 1933.

TUCKER, RICHARD WILLIAM ETHELBERT, M.A. (Cantab.), B.Ed., (Capetown).—B. 1892; ed. Wilsons Grammar Schl. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls., nat. sci. tripos pt. I (hons. chem. and zoology) 1913; 1st cls., nat. sci. tripos, pt. II (zoology) 1914; asst., S. African museum, Cape-town, 1914-21; entomologist, dept. of agr., Union of S. TULL, JOHN CADMAN.—M.D. (McGill), F.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B.1884; capt., Canadian Army Med. Coll. to Intern. cong. on cancer, London, May, 1928; deleg., leprosy confce., Manila, Dec., 1930; pathologist, Cyprus, 1936.

TUPPER, HENRY DE BEAUVIOIR, A.M., R.N., Capt., R.N. (ret.).—B. 1883; naval cadet, 1898; commdr., 1917; capt., 1928; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Otranto barrage and admv.; Legion of Honour (France), Order of Aves (Portugal) and Al Valore (Italy); habmr., Br. Guiana, 1925; do., Jamaica, Oct., 1931.

TURBETT, IVAN JOLIFFE TUNNELL.—B. 1890; ed. TURGEON, WILLIAM FERDINAND ALPHONSE, K.C., B.A.—B. 1877; ed. at New York city and Laval univ., Montreal; barrister; atty.-gen. of Sask., 23rd Sept., 1907; elec. to Sask. legis., 1907; re-elec., 1908, 1912 and 1917; atty.-gen. and prov. sec., 1915; judge, ct. of appeal, Sask., 14th Mar., 1921.

TURNER, ERNEST EDGAR.—B. 1880; clk., post office, Sheffield, 1894-1900; served in S. African war and in S. African constab. TURNER, HENRY.—B. 1882; ed. Oamaru South Pub. Schl., N.Z.; N.Z. pub. trust office, 1899; asst. pub. trustee, 1923; under-sec. of defence 1930.

TURNER, JAMES POSON.—B. 1871; clk., pub. library, St. Kitts, 1890; clk., registr.'s off., St. Kitts, 1897; comanr. to administer oaths, St. Kitts, 1899; clk. to mag., dist. "C." St. Kitts, May to Oct., 1905; 1st clk., registr.'s off., Antigua, May, 1906; comanr. to admin. oaths, Antigua, May, 1906; clk. registr. of jt. stock companies, Nov., 1906; dep. coroner, dista. "A" and "B" Antigua, Apr., 1916; dir. of P.O.G. fund, Jan., 1917; registr. and prov. marshal, Antigua, and chief registr., Sept., 1917; comanr. income tax, July, 1923; M.L.C., Antigua, Mar., 1926; addnl. mag., dist. A., Antigua, Jan., 1928; M.L.C., Antigua, Mar., 1929; J.P., May, 1930; comanr. sup. ct., Oct., 1931.

TUTE, SIR RICHARD CLIFFORD, Kt. Bach.(1937).—B. 1874; ed. St. Peter's Royal Schl., York and London Univ.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; passed open compet.exam.forIndian civ.serv.,1898; dist. and sessions judge, Allahabad, Bareilly-Meerut and other dists. from 1907; served throughout European war, retiring in 1919 with rank of major, R.G.A.; dist.judge,Galilee dist., Palestine, 1919; pres., dist. ct., Samarai, 1920; pres., land ct., Samarai, 1921; pres., land ct., Jerusalem, 1925; ch. just., Bahamas, 1931; admstd. govt. in Aug., 1932; author of "A Commentary on the Ottoman Land Code" and (as joint author) a metrical translation of Omar Khayyam.
TUTTON, MICHAEL WILLIAM LEONARD.—B. 1913; ed. Eton (K.S.) and Exeter Coll., Oxford, B.A., 1935; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1936.

TWEEDSMUIR, 1st BARON (cr. 1935) Rt. Hon. JOHN BUCHAN, C.H. (1922), M.A. (Oxon), hon. D.C.L. (Oxon), hon. LL.D. (Glasgow and St. Andrews), J.P. (Oxfordshire), D.L., J.P. (Peebleshire).—B. 1875; ed. Glasgow Univ. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (schol., 1895, M.A., fellow); Stanhope historical essay, 1897; Newdigate prize, 1898; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1899; pres., Union, 1899; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1901; pvt. sec. to Lord Milner, 1901-03; on headqrs. staff Br. TWEMLOW, GEORGE BROWNBILL.—B. 1897; ed. at Rossall Sch.; mily. serv., 1916-20; demob. with rank of capt.; re-enlisted, 1920-23; joined botanical and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1923; asst. supt., botanical and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1927.

TYLER-SMITH, H. A., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E.—B. 1887; B.Soc. (Eng.) with 1st cls. hons., civil engnr., Liverpool Univ., 1909; 2nd lieut., R.K. TYNDALE-BISCOE, CECIL JULIAN, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Glenalmond, Trent and Jesus Coll., Camb.; war ser., R.A. 1914-18 (France and Palestine); M.C., 1916; instr. Imp. Sch. of Gunnery, 1918; polit. offr. TYRWHITT-DRAKE, ROBERT HAMILTON.—B. 1901; ed. St. Andrews Coll., Grahamstown, S. Africa; Magdalen Coll., Oxford and Rhodes Univ. Coll. Grahamstown; computer, survey dept., Kenya, 1927; dist. survr., Kenya, 1930; dir., land survey and regisrn., Zanzibar, 1936.

TYSER, SIR C. R., KT. BACH (1909).—Ed. at Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb., M.A. (1872); called to bar, In. Tem., 1873; asst. ag. judic. comanr. and leg. advr., Cyprus, 1880-1; atty.-gen., Leewards, 1886-9; pres. dist. ct., Kyrenia, Cyprus, 1895; puisne judge, Cyprus, Jan., 1902; ch. just., 1906; ret., 1919; author of "Marine Insurance Losses," 1894; wrote translation of Omar Hilmi Effendi's Evkaf Law, 1899; and of the Mejelle, 1901.

UECKERMAN, ROBERT MASON.—B. 1876; 2nd cls. clk., R.M.R., Nov., 1902; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1903; examr., Jan., 1909; examr., R.M.J. office, Johannesburg, Oct., 1919; asst. regisr., deeds, Kimberley, Apr., 1924; regisr., deeds and companies, Windhoek, July, 1930; asst. regisr., deeds, n., Jan., 1932; regisr., deeds, Bloem., '34; regisr., mining titles and Rand township regisr., June, 1935.

UNWIN, ARTHUR HAROLD, O.B.E., D.Oec., M.R.F.A., F.R.G.S., M.C.—Forestry branch, dept. of interior, Canad.; asst. conservator and sen. conservator, Nigeria, 1904-17; office of woods, news dept., England, 1917-19; pm. for Cyprus, 1921; pm. UTTLEY, DENIS FLORAT, R.A.—B. 1895; St. John's, Leatherhead and Christchurch bridge; col. audit dep., May, 1930; Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1930.

July, 1931.

VALENTINE, DOUGLAS, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.),—B. 1892; ed. AN DER LINGEN, Pieter Moll, B.A.—1879; ed. Kroonstad and Stellenbosch; dmtr., Jeppie educn. instit., Johannesburg; 3; do., Middelburg secondary schl., 1908; pr., educn., Transvaal, 1913; pub. serv., npr., 1934.
VAN DER MERWE, Hermanus Zulch, B.A.—3, 1885; ed. at Ceres High Schl. and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; inspr., schla., 1912; sec., gen. dept., Cape Prov., 1934.
