VAN NIEKERK, Christiaan Andries.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schl., Wellington and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; commdt. for Kroonstad in Anglo-Boer War (wounded); del., peace confce., Vereeniging, 1902; mem., compensation comm., till 1903; mem., leg. assem., O.F.S., 1907; leg. assem., Union, 1910-24; mem., pensions grievances comm., chmn., sp. pensions bd., 1925; mem., Union senate since 1924; pres., 1930.
VAN RENEN, R.—R. 1877; clk., treasy., 1895, and income tax office, 1914; recr., rev., Benoni, 1921; do., Krugersdorp, 1929; do., E London, 1932; do., Bloemfontein, 1932.

VAN RYNEVELD, Col. Sir Pierre Herbertus Andrias, K.B.E. (1920), D.S.O. (1918), M.C. (1916), B.A., B.Sc.—R. 1891; ed. Grey Coll., Schl., Grey Univ. Coll., Bloemfontein, Imp. Coll., Univ. of London; 2nd lieut., 7th Loyal N. Lancs. Regt., Aug., 1914; lieut., R.F.C., Apr., 1915; detachment C.O., Scnussi campaign, Egypt, 1915-16; flight comdr., Palestine, 1916; ditto, Salonika, 1916; squad. comdr., London night defences, 1916-17; squad. comdr., No. 17 squad., B.E.F., 1917; severely wounded; wing comdr., XI. (Army) Wing, 2nd Army, 1917-19; ditto, Army wing, Rhine army of occupation, 1919; ment. in desig. six times, "1914-15" Star, Brit. War med., Victory med., Order of the Nile, 4th cls., Croix de Chevalier, Legion d'Honneur, France, Croix d'Officier, Ordre Leopold avec Croix de Guerre, Belgium; S. African liaison offr., Air Miny., 1919-21; lieut.-col. (temp. col.), Permanent Force Union Defence Forces, Feb., 1920; director of air services, U.D.F., Feb., 1920; col., S.A. staff corps, Nov., 1929; O.C., troops, Roberts Heights and commdt., S.A. Milv. Coll. in addn. to duties as dir., air services, Dec., 1929; brig.-gen. and apptd., ch., gen. staff, May, 1933.

VAN SCHALKWIJK, Johannes Zacharias, B.A.—B. 1883; ed. Bloemfontein and Stellenbosch; asst. Bethlehem; vice-prin., Ficksburg, 1906; do., Normal Practising Schl., Bloemfontein, 1910; prin., model schl., Bloemfontein and vice-rect., Normal Coll., 1913; inspr. of schls., O.F.S. Nov., 1917.

VAN SCHALKWIJK, L. M. A. N., B.A. (Cape), Ph.D. (Amst.).—B. 1888; ed. Normal and S. African Coll., Cape Town, also in Holland, Germany and Austria; organising inspr., Union dept. of educn. since 1921.
VAN ZEYLEN, Frederick Cornelius.—B. 1885; mech. and elec. engr., draughtsman, harbr. engrn.'s office, Nassau, Bahamas, 1923; asst. civ. engrn., 1925; dep. civ. engrn., 1925; D.P.W., May, 1928.

VAN ZIJL, Hendrik Stephanus, LL.B. (Camb.), B.A., LL.B. (Cape).—B. 1876; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1903; M.P., Cape of Good Hope, 1905-10; partly draftsman, Union of S. Africa, 1910-15; K.C., 1919; judge, sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1920; chmn., delimitation comm., July, 1927 and Mar., 1932; judge-pres., Cape prov. divn., 1935; chmn., S.W. Africa constitution comm., 1935-36.

VAN ZYL, Gideon Brand.—B. 1873; ed. Normal and South African Coll., Capetown; atty-at-law and notary pub., 1898; conveyancer, 1897; asst. dir., war recruiting, 1918; dep. chmn., comtees, 1921-24; dep. speaker and chmn., comtees, Union of S.A. H. of A., Jan., 1934.

VAN ZYL, Jacob Ernest Siebert.—B. 1885; prob. clk., R.M., Hanover, 1904; clk., deeds, Vryburg, 1904; clk., R.M., Calvinia, 1905; clk., master's off., C.T., 1906; 2nd gr. clk., Union educn. dept., 1910; 1st gr. clk., do., 1914; senr. clk., do., 1917; 2nd gr. prin. clk., do., 1920; 1st gr. prin. clk., 1921; 2nd gr. ch. clk., 1928; lat. gr. ch. clk., 1928; pub. serv., inspr., 1931; under sec., educn., July, 1934.

VAN ZYL, L. J., B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Boys' High Schl. Wellington, and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; head, High Schls., Vrededorf, Smithfield, Ladybrand, 1910-21; inspr., educn., 1921-35; sec., educn., O.F.S., 1935.

VASKESS, Henry Harrison.—B. 1891; clk., Western Pacific high comm., Apr., 1911; on milly. serv., June, 1915; clk. in ch., accts., W. Pacific, Sept., 1919; ch. clk. and acctnt., Apr., 1920; ag. sec. to W. Pacific high comm. for short periods in 1920, 1922, 1925 and 1927-29; sec., Western Pacific high comm., Dec., 1929.

VASSALL, Henry Holland.—B. 1887; ed. Bedford Grammar Sch., and Keble Coll., Oxford; asst. res., Nyassaland, 1911; res., 1919; admst. offr., 1st grade, Dec., 1923; ag. prov. commnr., Jan., 1929 to June, 1930 and July, 1930 to Feb., 1931.

VASSALLO, Edwin P., L.S. & A.—B. 1891; ed. Flore's Coll. and Malta Univ.; grad. as land survr. and architect, 1914; elec. for 7th divn. in leg. assem., Oct., 1921; re-ele., June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for pub. wks., 16th Aug., 1927; edr. "The Malta Times and Progress" until Aug., 1927.

VASSALLO, S., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Ed. Malta Lyceum and Malta Univ.; M.D., 1913; F.R.C.S. (Edin.), 1928; govt. exhib. for science, 1906-1907; ditto for medicine, 1909-1910; post-graduate work at Univ., Coll. Hosp. and London Hosp.; clinical ast., St. John's Hosp., W., and Royal Ear Hosp.; supernumerary med. offr., Leeward Is., 1914; D.T.M. and H. (Lond.) certif., L.S.T.M. (Duncan med.); temp. commr., R.A.M.C. (ment. in desps.); med. offr., Uganda, 1919; res. surg. offr., Zanzibar Prot., 1925; senr. specialist offr., 1935; 4th cls. Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1931.

VAUGHAN, John Henry, M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, B.A. (hist. trip), 1914; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-21; M.C., Arras, 1917; asst. dist. commnr., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to Br. res. and clk. of the coun., July, 1924; ag. regisr., high ct., 1927; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1929; res. mag., July, 1929; ag. judge in 1931, 1932 and 1933; asst. atty.-gen., 1933 and 1934; ag. atty.-gen. in 1934 and 1935; commnr. for revision of laws, 1934; solr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1936; author of "The Dual Jurisdiction in Zanzibar" (1935).
VEERASAWMY, John Aloysius.—B. 1891; barrister-at-law (Middle Temple), 1913; ag. stip. mag., Br. Guiana, 1922; stip. mag., 1928.

VENABLES, Oswald Eric, B.A. (Dublin).—B. 1891; ed. Campbell Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; on active ser., June, 1917; offr., cls. V., Nov., 1917; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1921; 2nd mag., S'pore, 1923; 2nd asst. sec. (A.), col. secret., S'pore, 1924; Br. res., Brunei, 1926; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1926; mag., Seremban, 1927; dist. offr., Upper Perak, 1929; collr., land rev., K. Lumpur, 1931; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1931; Br. advr., Perlis, July 1932; dist. offr., K. Kangsar, May, 1936.

VENNING, Guy, M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., and assigned to local govt. bd., May, 1899; after further compet. exam., apptd. clk. of 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 8th Jan., 1900; transf. to C.O., 16th Apr., 1903; conf. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; staff offr., 9th Jan., 1928.

VENOUR, Louis Stephen Douglas Hamilton.—B. 1885; ed. pvtly.; capt., 5th batt., Middx. Regt.; asst. commnr., pol., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; ag. staff instr., in 1916 and 1919; commnr., pol., Jan., 1917; King's Pol. Med., 1922; ag. commnr., pol. C.I.D., June to Nov., 1928; senr. commnr., pol., 1929; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., on various occasions, 1925-33.

VERTY, John.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, copyist, treasy. and cust., Br. Honduras, 1908; 3rd cls. clk., regist. gen.'s office, 1910; 2nd cls. clk., atty. gen's office, 1912; resig., 1913; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1913; dep. clk., cts., Jamaica, 1913; clk., cts., 1920; ag. judge, Kingston cts., 1922; chmn., commnr. of enquiry, land, judge, sup. ct., Turks and Caicos Is., 1936; ag. judge, high ct., 1936; 2nd puisno judge, Br. Guiana, 1936.
VERNALL, Richard John, M.A.—B. 1901; archt., P.W.D., Hong Kong; tech. sec., P.W.D., 1934.

VERNON, Roland Vinables C.—B. 1877; ed. at Clifton and Oriel, Balliol, 1895-9; 1st cls. mods., 1897; Hertford schlr.; Craven schlr., 1899; 1st cls. lit., hum., 1901; after compet. exam., clk. inse.: des. rev., Nov., 1899; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 1900; sec. to Trinidad riot. comm., managing comm., E.I.O.C.; ass. priv. Lord Elgin, Jan., 1908; priv. sec. to 16th Apr., 1908; read. S. African Univ., 1910; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1911; to Lord Denman, gov.-gen. of Australia, 1st cls. clk., 9th Nov., 1912; visited on British Solomon Islands, Nov., 1913; represented H.M.G. on Anglo-Persian Hebrides confere., May-Aug., 1914; treasur., Oct., 1914; lent to Min. of War, June, 1915; asst. gen. sec., 1916; visited Belgium on sp. serv., Feb., 1917; visited Irish Convention, June, 1917 to Mar., 1918; to treasur., May, 1918; read. dep. acct.-gen., Apr., 1920; lent to C.O., Middle East; asst. sec., finan. and economic, 1920; visited Egypt, Palestine and Transjord., sp. serv., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; to Lausanne for Turkish Peace Treaty, Dec., 1922 and June, 1923; transferred to staff of C.O. as asst. sec., Jan., 1923; returned to C.O., 1st Oct., 1928; mem. Cur. Board, 1928.

VIADER, René, M.B.E. (1930).—R. Coll., Mauritius; ent. civ. serv., Nov. 1899; regiar., col. sec's offic., finan. asst., P.W.D., Mar., 1923; sec. of sec's office, Apr., 1925; 2nd asst. sec., 1926; ag. asst. col. sec., 1925-30.

VICKERS, William John, M.B.E.—L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Camb.), Certif. L.S. of H. and Edin.; ed. King Edward's Gram. Sch.; Med. Schl.; Gt. War, Br. exp.; res. staff, gen. hosps., B'mgham; offr., dist. hosp., K. Lumpur, Selangor, offr., dist. hosp., K. Lumpur, Selangor, offr., Selangor E., May, 1926; in Perak, Sept. 1927; health offr., K. Kuala, June, 1928; ag. sr. health offr., 1934.
VICKERS-HAVILAND, Lieut., R. Fusiliers, 1914-18; K.A.R., 1918; asst. polit. offr., 1919; offr., 2nd grade, 1922; asst. attd., secretariat, Tanganyik dist. offr., 1931; seconded as 1932-33.

VIDAL, M. R. R.—Asst. Africa Prot., Dec., 1912; 1st offr., Kenya, Jan., 1924; senr. Vigne, Childlow.—B. 1898; B.A. (Oxon.), dip. for. (Oxon.), 2nd Lieut., R.A.F., 1918; ag. judge, Gold Coast, Mar., 1922; fellow, 1930-32, Yale Univ.; Coast, Oct., 1933.

VILJOEN, Philippus R.—Dr. Med. Vet. (Berne).—lgr. agr. as asst. vety. bacteriol., Pretoria, 1913; vety. res. poort and Vryburg, 1914. ence, Oct., 1914; retd. to dept. of agr., June, 5; resigned, Jan., 1919; reapptd. dept. of agr.-senr. research offr., Onderstepoort, 1920; sub- vety. research and prof. in vety. medicine, Transvaal Univ. Coll., Apr., 1922; dep. dir., v. services, 1927; under-sec., agr., Jan., 1931; agr., May, 1933.
VINCENT, WILLIAM HENRY, L.M.S.S.A., nd.—B. 1886; ed. Colet Court, St. Paul's Sch., Mary's Hosp., and London Univ.; dist. med. r., Fiji, 1915.

VISCHER, HANNS, C.M.G. (1934), C.B.E. (18)—B. 1876; ed Switzerland and Emmanuel ll., Cambridge; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1903; l., 1905; dir., educ., 1908-18; lieut., Gen. st., 1915; capt., 1915; major, 1916; Etoile sire, 1917; Legion d'Honneur, 1918; Crown of Ily, 1918; Crown of Belgium, 1918; demob., 19 with rank of major in G.R.O.; mem. and S. S. of S.'s advisory comte, on native educn. Trop. Africa, Nov., 1923.

VISSER, DANIEL HENDRIK, J.P.—B. 1877; tered Cape pub. serv., 23rd Mar., 1894; asst. ag. and ag. civil commnr., 1902-05; clk.-asst., Cape House of Assembly, 8th Apr., 1907; alk.-st., House of Assem., Union of S. Africa, 1910; clk., House of Assembly, 1st Oct., 20.
VISSER, DR. THOMAS CHRISTOFFEL—Mem., union assem., to 1929; mem., Union senate, 1930; mn. of comtees., 1930.
VISUWALINGAM, VELLUPILLAI.—B. 1883; at. Ceylon civ. serv. as ag. extra office asst., Matapura kach., Mar., 1934; extra office asst., Jaffna, Jan., 1935; ag. office asst., Jaffna kach., pr. 1935.

VLIELAND, CHARLES ARCHIBALD, B.A.—B. 80; ed. Exeter Schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford (chol.); lat. cls. hon., nat. sci., 1911; cadet, M.S., 1914; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, 1916; dist. asst. sec., fedl. secretariat, 1919; asst. dist. Ir., 1920-21; sec. to res., N. Sembilan, 1922; ute sec. to O.A.G., June, 1925; addl. col. secs. Boe, S'pore, Nov., 1926; offr., cls. III., Dec., 26; ag. dist. offr., B. Padang, July, 1927 to sept., 1928; dist. offr., K. Selangor, Apr., 1929; ipt., 1931 census, Malaya, Mar., 1930; offr. cls. I., Dec., 1931; economy offr., S.S. and F.M.S., ug., 1932; ag. under sec., S.S., Feb., 1934; offr. Is. B., Oct., 1934; finan. commnr. and audr.-en. Johore, July, 1935.

VOIGT, FREDERICK CAREL MARTHINUS, B.A.—B. 1879; clk., dept. of just., 1901; pvt. ec. to min. of nat. affrs., 1910; senr. clk., nat. ffs. dept., Pretoria, 1912; dept. of just., Feb., 913; asst. mag., Krugersdorp, 1916; prof. ass., ty. gen's. office, Bloemfontein, 1921; ditto, Tafe Town, 1926; pub. serv. inspr., 1928; asst. prov. sec., Cape admntr., 1930; prov. sec., ch. local govt. inspr. and contr., educn. finance, Mar., 1933.
VOLLET, DENYS HENRI RENE.—B. 1898; served in Great War; mod. offr., Basutoland, 1927.

VON BONDE, CECIL, Ph.D., M.A.—Ed. Univ. of Capetown; senr. lect. in Zoology, Univ. of Capetown, 1918; awarded "Purcell Memorial Prize" for Zoology in 1924; ag. prof., Zoology, Univ. of Capetown, 1926; dir., fisheries survey and govt. marine biologist, Union of S. Africa, 1928; seconded for marine biolog. survey to confce. of E. African govs. in Kenya and Zanzibar, 1928.

VOSS, GEORGE DU TOIT, K.C., B.A., LL.B., Advocate of the Sup. Ct., of S. Africa.—B. 1884; asst. regisr., sup. ct. of S. Africa (Transvaal prov. divn.), 1918; regisr., high ct. of S.W. Africa, 1919; atty.-gen., S.W. Africa, 1925; sol. gen., Eastern dista., Cape Prov., 1931; law adviser, dept. of just., Nov., 1931.
WADDELL, CECIL JAMES, B.Sc., A.M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1896; ed. Allan Glen's Sch. and Glasgow Univ.; war ser., 1915-18; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925; seconded, Kowloon-Canton rly., 1931-32.

WADDINGTON, E. J., C.M.G. (1935), O.B.E. (1919).—Asst. dist. commnr., E.A.F.P., Dec., 1913; ag. prov. commnr., Jubaland, Nov., 1919; senr. asst. sec., secretariat, Kenya, Feb. to Sept., 1923; clk. of couns., Sept., 1923; asst. sec., gov't. confce., 1925; res. commnr., Mombasa, 1928; col. sec., Bermuda, 1932; do., Br. Guiana, 1935.

WADE, A. DE V., C.M.G. (1935), O.B.E. (1931).—Ed. Lancing Coll., exhibtnr. and Keble Coll., Oxford; scholar; M.A.; asst. dist. commnr., E. Africa Prot., March, 1912; ag. dep. ch. native commnr., Kenya, 1928; prin. asst., native affrs. dept., Jan., 1928; ch. native commnr., 1932; ag. col. sec., 1933; col. sec., 1934.

WADE, CECIL HENRY, O.B.E. (1929), B.A. (hons.), 1909.—Ed. Lancing Coll. (exhibtnr.) and Keble Coll., Oxford; 3rd grade res., Nyasaland, Aug., 1911; asst. ch. sec. July, 1920; 1st asst. sec., June, 1921; examr. in Chinyanja language; ag. ch. sec., May, 1926; ag. treas., July to Dec., 1927; ag. ch. sec. May 1929 to Jan., 1930 and Apr., 1930; prin. asst. sec., Nigeria, 1931.

WADE, CAPT. T. G. ROCHFORT, M.A., F.R.G.S.—B. 1901; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and St. Columba's Coll., Dublin; B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1926; M.A., 1930; inspr., schls., St. Lucia, May, 1931; C.O., St. Lucia Volunteer Force.

WADIA, SIJAVAX HIRJI, B.A. (Bombay), B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1915; addl. Jaffna Kach., June, 1915; addl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addn., Oct., 1915; finan. asst. to chrmn., munic coun., Colombo, Jan., 1918; office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1920; pol. mag., Avissawella, June, 1920; asst. contr., rev., Apr., 1922; addl. asst. contr., rev., Aug., 1925; asst. commnr., stamps, Jan., 1926; asst. contr., rev., Nov., 1926; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Sept., 1931; asst. govt. agt. Matara, Dec., 1932; ag. excise commnr., Mar., 1934.
WADLEY, W. J. D., M.A.—B. 1903; ed. City of London Schl.; class schl., Lincoln Coll., Oxford; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Sept., 1926.

WAINWRIGHT, WILLIAM FREDERICK, O.B.E.—B. 1881; Indian pol., 1899; accompanied Sir Charles Yate on polit. mission to Mekran, S. Persia; transf'd. to Bengal pol., 1901 on sp. famine opera., 1906-08; serv. in Gt. War, Egypt and Palestine, 1916-20; major, Territorial Reserve (Yeomanry); dep. dist. comdt. of pol. Palestine, 1920; dist. supt., pol., Palestine, 1926.

WAKEFIELD, ARTHUR JOHN, B.Sc., N.D.A., N.D.D.—B. 1900; ed. Brewood Grammar Schl., Harper Adams Agrl. Coll., Reading and Edinburgh Univ.; 2nd lieut. Shropshire Light Infy., 1918; vety. dept., N. Rhodesia, 1923; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; senr. agrl. offr., 1930; ag. dir., agr., Jan.-Aug., 1933; dep. dir., agr., 1935.

WAKEFIELD, COL. HUBERT STEPHEN, O.B.E. (Mil.)—B. 1883; ed. privte. schl. and Ellemere Coll.; joined Cape Mtd. Riflemen, 1901; S.A. war, 1899-02; (Queen's med., 3 clasps); lieut., C.M.R., 1912; lieut., 1st regt., S.A.M.R., 1913; operns. in G.S.W. Africa, 1914-15; adjt., bar, King's Inns, June, 1898; K.C., Feb., 1910; Ph.D. (metaphysics and ethics, ancient languages and comparative philology, maths.); nr. and senr. crown pros., N.W. circuit and inter assizes, 1906-20; war serv., 1915-1918; county ct. judge for two circuits, midland counties; dep. recorder, Galway; dep. county ct. judge for counties Kerry, Tipperary and West Riding of Cork; pres., Br. pensions appl. tribunal (Ireland), Mar., 1920-May, 1929; pres., dist. s. Cyprus, 1929-31; ch. just., Seychelles, 1931; A.G., Seychelles, Dec., 1931-May, 1932.

WALTER, A., O.B.E. (1935), F.R.A.S.—Join magnetic and meteorological dept., royal observatory, Greenwich, 1892; asst. director, Royal Alfred Observatory, Mauritius, Apr., 1897; statistical adviser to Mauritius Royal comm., 1909; insus comm., Mauritius, 1911 and 1921; dir., royal Alfred Observatory, May, 1911 to 1921; ag. st. col. sec., Mauritius, Nov. to Dec., 1918; food, trade and export contr., Mar., 1920 to Jan., 1922; prot. of imigrts. and poor law commrs., Jan., 1922; mem. of coun. of govt., 1922; headed delegation to India for the re-opening of emigr. Mauritius, 21st Dec., 1922; statistician, Kenya, 1926; statistician to govs' conferees, 1926; dir., E. African meteorological serv., 1928; author of "Sugar Industry of Mauritius"; Insurance of crops against cyclones; editor, Mauritius almanac.

WALTERS, JOHN RADLEY, B.A. (Cantab.)—1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1911; att. Kandy kach., Dec., 1911; att. Galle kach., May, 1912; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Nov., 1912; offic. asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1913; pol. mag., Jampola, May, 1915; pol. mag., Karunegala, Dec., 1920; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Sept., 1921; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, July, 1923; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Feb., 1924; dist. judge, Matara, Oct., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Mar., 1927; ag. govt. agt., Badulla, Apr., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, June, 1928; ag. govt. agt., N.W.P., Apr., 1931; do. E.P., Nov., 1932; addnl. P.M.G., July, 1933; P.M.G., Sept., 1933.

WALTON, BRYAN STAGG.—B.A. (Oxon.)—admt., S.S. Nov., 1913; ag. ast. dist. judge and 4th mag., S'pore, Mar., 1914; ast. censor, Nov., 1914; 2nd lieut., S'pore V.R., Jan., 1916; ag. ast. supt. govt. monops., S'pore, Nov., 1916; offr., cols. V., Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.), Dec., 1917; ag. ast. supt. govt. monops., Penang, Aug., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; dist. offr., K. Selangor, May, 1924; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; supt., lands, Kelantan, Aug., 1926; 2nd mag., Penang, Dec., 1928; ag. collr., land rev. and ast. treas., Malacca, May, 1929; cls. II., Nov., 1930; 1st ast. sec. (a), S.S., Aug., 1932; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Jan., 1934; ag. advr. land office, Kedah, May, 1936.

WALTON, HON. SIR EDGAR HARRIS, K.C.M.G. (1911).—M.L.A., Cape, for Port Elizabeth, since 1898, and in Union parlmt. since 1910; treas.-gen., Feb., 1904, to Feb., 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with S. Africa Bill, 1909; is proprietor and editor of Eastern Province Herald; high commr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1921-25.

WALTON, SIR GEORGE O'DONNELL, KT. BACH. (1925).—B.1871; matriculated London Univ., 1891; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1893; practised at the bar, Barbados, 1894 to 1902; acted on several occasions as pol. mag. and judge of ast. ct. of appeal, Barbados; apptd. pol. mag., dist. "F," Barbados, Dec., 1902; lieut. of M.I., Barbados volunteers; ag. chief just. St. Lucia, 18th May to Oct., 1908, and from March to Oct., 1911; mag. and cot. dist. "C," St. Kitts, Nov., 1911; ag. 2nd puisne judge, Leeward Is., on various occasions, 1911-15; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, May, 1915; ag. adminstr. on several occasions in 1915 and in 1916 and 1917, ag. chief just., from Oct., 1916 to Mar., 1919; atty.-gen., B. Honduras, Mar., 1919; ag. chief justice, May to Nov., 1919; ch. just., Grenada, July, 1921; 1st puisne judge, Trinidad, Apr., 1926; ret., 1931.
WALWYN, ALGERNON EDWARD VERE.—B. 1888; ed. Bath Coll. and Peterhouse, Camb.; asst. dist. offr., N. Prova., Nigeria, Apr., 1915; sec., Sept., 1922; sec., N. Prova., 1933.

WALWYN, VICE ADMIRAL SIR HUMPHREY THOMAS, K.C.S.I. (1923), C.B. (1928), D.S.O. (1916).—B. 1879; ed. H.M.S. Britannia, Dartmouth; went to sea, H.M.S. Camperdown, 1896; gunnery lieut., 1904; commdr., 1912; H.M.S. Warspite, 1915-17; capt., 1916; in commd., destroyer flotillas and senr. offr., Mediterranean destroyers, 1923; dir., gunnery divn., naval staff, admv., 1924-26; commanded H.M.S. Valiant, 1926; flag offr. commdg. R. Indian Navy, 1928-34; rear-adm., 1928; vice-adm., 1932; ret. list, 1934; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Newfoundland, 1936.

WAMSLEY, R. W., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1880; olr., civ. commrs' office, Kimberley, 1903; inland rev. dept., 1911; prin. olr., 1921; recr., rev., Port Elizabeth, 1921; recr., rev., Bloemfontein, 1931; ch. rev. offr., 1932; recr., rev., Cape Town, 1933.

WARD, ESKINE RURUL LA TOURRETTE, B.A.—B. 1900; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Oxford Univ.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; pol. mag. dist. "A," Barbados, 1931; ag. regis., and judge, ast. ct. of appl., various periods, 1932-33.

WARD, ESPIRE FRANCIS, M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O.—Ed. Epsom Coll. and Queen's Univ., Belfast; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, 1912; trans., Gambia, 1918; trans., G. Coast, 1920; on special service for C.O., Oct., 1915 to Feb., 1916; temp. lieut., gen. list, Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1916; attached Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914-15; priv. secy. to ag. gov., Gambia, 1918 senr. med. offr., July, 1927; author of "Beri-Beri, Etiology, Symptoms, and Treatment." "Favourite Prescriptions" (2nd edition), "Deaths from Lightning Stroke" (Br. Med. Jnl.)

WARD, E. R.—B. 1902; ed. King's Coll., Taunton and Coll. of Estate Management, Lincoln's Inn Fields; cadet, Nigeria, 1926; ast. dist. offr., 1929; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., S. Prova., Nov. 1930-May, 1931; ditto to gov., July, 1931-Mar., 1934.
WARD, FRANK DORRINGTON, F.R.I.B.A., M.R.S.A.I.—B. 1885; ed. Kent Coll., Canterbury; P.W.D., S.S., Aug., 1920; govt. architect, S.S., Nov., 1928; registr., bd. of archts., S.S., Jan., 1932.

WARD, JOSEPH CORBET, O.B.E.—Lieut. (afterwards capt.), I.Y., S. Africa, 1900-02 (Queen's medal and 4 clasps, King's medal and 2 clasps); Transvaal civ. ser., 1902-04; Br. E. Africa, Solik expedition (med. and clasp), 1905; prin. registr., docs., land dept., E.A.F., 1905; addl. 3rd K.A.R., 1914; post. commdt., lines of commun., 1915-18; staff offr. for Br. E. Africa, 1918; temp. lieut. col. (deeps. 3 times, 3 war medals.); staff offr. and intell-offr., Kenya, 1920-25; sec., office of H.M. Eastern African Dependencies, London, 1926.

WARD, LENNOX, K.C., B.A. (lit. and phil.), LL.B., 1908 (Univ. Cape of Good Hope).—Called to bar, 1909; prof. astt., atty.-gen., Cape, 1910; prof. astt., solr.-gen., 1914; atty.-gen., mandated territory, S.W. Africa, 1919; solr.-gen., 1925; atty.-gen., Natal, 1926; judge-pres., native high ct., 1933.

WARD, LEONARD KEITH, D.Sc. (Adelaide), B.A., B.E.—Ed., Sydney Gram. Schl. and Brisbane Gram. Schl.; entd. univ. of Sydney, 1897, as Queensland govt. exhibitioner, and graduated in arts, 1900, and in engineering, 1903; in service of Broken Hill Proprietary Mining Co., till Oct., 1903; lecturer in geology, mineralogy, petrology and mining geology, Kalgoorlie schl. of mines, W. Australia, 1903-7; astt. govt. geologist and inspr. of mines, Tasmania, 1907-11; govt. geologist, S. Australia, Jan., 1912; also dir. of dept. of mines.

WARD, WILFRED ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. privately and at Christ's Hosp.; served, France with Civil Service Rifles, 16th London Regt., as lance-corp.; 2nd lieut., 3rd Lancs. Fusiliers May, 1915; seconded, M.G.C., France, Salonica and Palestine; M.C. and promoted capt.; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; passed cadet, Jan., 1922; astt. dist. offr., Krian, Mar., 1922; offr., cls. V., Nov., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1923; dist. offr., Port Dickson, Apr., 1925; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1928; dist. offr., Temerloh, July, 1929; 2nd astt. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Feb., 1930; ag. astt. treas. F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Dec., 1930; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1933; pub. trustee and offl. admtr., F.M.S., Mar., 1934.

WARD, WILLIAM ERNEST FRANK, B.A., B.Litt.—B. 1900; ed. Mercer's Schl., London, Dulwich Coll. and Lincoln Coll. Oxford; open hist. schol., Lincoln Coll., 1918; 2nd cl. hons., B.A., mod. hist., 1922; B.Litt., 1923; Goldsmith's exhib., mod. hist., 1921; Ridley Hall, Cambridge; mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

WARDLE, BRIAN.—B. 1891; ed King William's Coll., Isle of Man; Indian pol., 1911-22; King's Pol. med., 1919; seconded as intell. offr., Dublin, 1920-22; supt. in charge, C.I.D., Zanzibar, 1924; ag. astt. commdt. in 1930 and 1933; supr., pol., Tanganyika Territory, 1934.

WARDLE, JAMES MOREY, B.Sc., C.E., M.E.I.C.—B. 1888; ed. Br. Columbia Pub. Schls. and Queen's Univ., Kingston, Ont.; B.Sc., 1912; engr. on locn. and constr., Canadian N. Pac. Rly., Br. Columbia; highway engr., nat. parks, Canada, dept. of intr., 1914-18; supt., Banff nat. park, Alta., 1918-21; ch. engr., nat. parks, Canada, 1921-35; western supervsr., nat. parks, 1932-35; dep. min., dept. of intr., Ottawa, 1935.

WARD-PHICE, HENRY LEWIS, B.A., Barrister.—B. 1888; ed. Caterham Schl. and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; astt. dist. commnr., S. Nigeria, 1912; astt. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1924; astt. sec., S. Provs. secretariat, 1926; I grade I, adminstr. serv., 1929; staff grade, 1936.

WARE, THOMAS WALTER, M.B., Ch.B., Brist., D.P.H. (Lond.)—B. 1890; med. offr., Brist.; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, in ch., subord. staff families, Kennedy Town infec. dis. hosp. and astt., Victoria hosp., 1927; in ch., civ. hosp., 1928; visiting med. offr., Chinese hosps. and dispensaries, 1930; 2nd port health offr. and inspr., immigrts., Sept., 1932 and 1933; ag. port health offr., etc., 1933; ch. offr., Nov., 1934.

WARNER, BRODICK ARTHUR, B.N., Winchester Coll.; astt. dist. commnr., Prot., 1912; lab. commnr., 1922; instr. instructive offr., 1923; senr. astt. sec., prov. commnr., 1929; ag. dep. ch. sec., prov. commnr., June, 1933; late cap., City of London Regt. (T.F.); cap., Home Force Reserve.

WARREN, EDGAR HARRIS, M.R.C.S.—Served with 28th I.Y., S. African med. (medal with 2 clasps); S. African comm., 1903-1908; lieut., Transvaal H.A., 1914; customs, Nyasa land Regt., 1916; customs, 1909; also imigr. offr., 1918; imigr. offr., May, 1914; astt. comm., Apr., 1920; ag. compt., cust., Jun., 1925; compt., cust., Mar., 1925; ag. treas., Jun., 1925.
WARREN, FREDK. DEMRON.—Stock inspr., Basutoland, 1914; sub-mt. utoland mounted pol., 1922; war ser., 1914.

WARREN, JOHN JOSEPH.—B. 1890; 1910-13; inspr., pol., F.M.S., May, 1913; commnr., Mar., 1921; dir. comm., May, 1926; head, prev. serv., Nov., 1927; astt. astt., govt. monops., S.S. No. 1; senr. astt. astt., govt. monops., S.S. No. 2; senr. astt. astt., excise, 1934; ag. astt. astt. and excise, S.S. 1935.

WARRINGTON, J. C.—B. 1887; borough and Trin. Coll. Camb.; B.A.; served in France with R.E. (Signal Corps) 1918 twice ment. in despatches French Guerre; astt. dist. commnr., G. Cosi., commnr., Apr., 1924.

WAUSE-BALIYER, BASIL NOEL, M.D. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Eng.), 1929.—B. 1899; med. Guiana, 1926; G.M.O.H., pers. bact., 1926; astt. M.G.O.H., pers. surg. gen. and dep. port health offr., med. offr. of health, surg. gen.'s dist. health offr., 1931; immigr. ag. gen., 1935; ag. chmn., poor law commrs., 1935.
WATERER, RALPH RONALD, M.A.—B. 1902; passed B.A. in estate, land and dipl., forestry, Cambridge Forestry Inst., Oxford; astt. comm., 1st gr., Cyprus, Oct., 1928.

WATERMEYER, ERNEST F.—B. 1880; ed. Graaff Reinet Coll., Stellenbosch, Bath Coll. and Caius Coll., matha. schol., Caius Coll., 1899; 1902; law tripso, 1903; incott., Temple, Apr., 1904; pres., incott., 1919; ag. puline judge, Cape Prov., etc., 1920; puline judge, C.P., 1922.

WATERMEYER, THOEBOLD.—B. 1879; ed. Graaff Reinet Coll., Stellenbosch, Bath Coll., Bath Coll. (now Birmingham Univ.), appt., Cape govt. rlys., 1902; astt., 1920; rep. S. African Rlys. Congress, Rome, 1922; astt. gen. man., electrification, Cape town, and 1927; astt. gen. man., Johannesburg, 1933.

WATERS, HAROLD BERTHOLD.—B. 1896; ed. Royal Gram. Schol., St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; see (Royal West Surrey) Regt., R.E., 1914-19; astt., agric., sent to F.M.S., Java and S. oil-palm industry, 1926; de...

KINS, CHARLES ARTHUR ALBRECHT.—B. l. Br. Comm. Schl., Smyrna and pvt. survr. and ast. irrig. wpt., Cyprus; survr., ch. survr., and ast. prov. engr., 1905-08; engnr., 1920-27; on war surv.; temp. ast. engr., Egypt, 1st class, and erecting engr., P.W.D., Cyprus, July, livnl. roads engr., P.W.D., Apr., 1930; engnr., P.W.D., Mar., 1932.
KINS-PITCHFORD, H. O., M.R.C.S., (Lond.), D.T.M. & H.—B. 1900; ed. St. Coll., Surrey; St. Thomas's Hosp. Med. E. Africa med. serv., Zanzibar, Feb., 1925; offr., Kenya, 1934.

KSON, ANDREW D.—B. 1882; ed. common Baddeley Acad., Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1905; inv. surv., Canada, dept. of insurance, Feb., actuary, 1914; assoc., Inst. of Actuaries, Lond., 1919; contrib. to the jnl. of the Inst. of actuaries, and the transactions of the Actuarial Soc., 1919.

ATSON, DONALD FRANCIS, O.B.E. (1926).—1885; 2nd clk., admstr.'s office Seychelles, 1900; dispenser and clk., med. dept., 1900; clk. local bd. of health, quarantine and cemetery tee., 1906; man., govt. pharmacy, 1901; agt., lunatic asylum, May to Oct., 1920; agt., coll. of customs, chmn., marine bd., and im., profiteering comm., July, 1920 to Apr., 1921; contr., local clearing office, June, 1921; ass., postmr., collr. of cust., consmr. of income, man. of savings bank, regisr. of shipping, r., 1923.
VATSON, ERNEST SIBBLES, B.Sc. Tech.—1902; ed. Cent. High Sch., Manchester and Inst. of Technology, Manch.; B.Sc., Tech., 1923; t. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

WATSON, HON. HERBERT GORDON, I.S.O. (1922).—Clk., gov. of Zululand's office, 1895; agt., for Zululand at different periods, 1896; clk., v.s. office, Natal, Dec., 1896; transf. Cape surv. and apptd. clk., gov's. office, Mar., 1901; ch. clk., July, 1904; clk., exec. coun., Nov., 1904 to Apr., 1907; agt., priv. sec. to offr. mstg. govt., major-gen. E. S. Brook, C.B., July, 1904 to Feb., 1905; clk., exec. coun., Apr., 1907; priv. sec., major-gen. Sir Henry Scobell, C.V.O., Dec., 1909; ast. clk., exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, May, 1910; sec. to prime min. and clk., exec. coun., Feb., 1916; clk., exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, 1922; admstr., prov. Natal, Feb., 1928.

WATSON, JAMES, M.A., B.Sc.—B. 1883; ed. George Heriot's Schl. and Edinburgh Univ., I.A. 1902, B.Sc. 1904; European mast., Raffles Institution, S'pore, 1906; science mast., 1910; spr. of schl., Pahang, F.M.S., 1915; headmast., Anderson schl., Ipoh, 1920; inspr., of schl., Penang, 1921; ch. inspr. of English schl., S.S. nd F.M.S., 1923; agt. dir. educn., S.S. and F.M.S., various periods, 1930-34.

WATSON, JAMES GILBERT.—B. 1889; ed Kgl. Preuss. Forstakademie, Eberswalde, Germany; supt., govt. plantations, F.M.S., Feb., 1913; ast. conserv., forests, F.M.S., Oct., 1913; dep. conserv., forests, research, F.M.S., Nov., 1926; conserv., forests, Jan., 1930; ax. dep. dir. forestry, S.S. and dep. advr., forestry, M.S., Aug., 1935.

WATSON, OSWALD, L.S.—B. 1891; survey cadet, N.S.W. and N. Guinea, Sept., 1910-14; milt. surv., survey sec., R.E. and topo. sec., A.I.F., 1915-18; lic. land survr., Australia and N.Z., Sept., 1919; survr., S.S., Apr., 1920; do. F.M.S., Jan., 1921; ast. supt., surveys, Jan., 1926; ag. sr. ast. supt., Nov., 1933.
WATSON, P. C. M.—Treas. ast., E.A.P., May, 1914; senr. ast. treas., Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. dep. treas., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1924; prin. ast. treas., 1930.

WATSON, HON. ROBERT.—B. 1868; M.E.C. and M.H.A., Newfoundland; ed. at St. John's and Rugby; elected for Trinity, 1897, 1902, 1908 and 1909; mem. exec. coun.; col. sec., and parly. head of dept. of post office, telegraphs, educn., rlys., pub. health and pub. charities, 1909; mem. exec. comm., Diocesan Synod, 1910; ag. premier, 1910; attended Imp. Conf. and the Coronation, 1911; cashier, govt. savings bank, 1913; hon. priv. sec. to gov., 1916; ast. priv. sec. to gov., 1918; hon. corres. sec., Royal Col. Inst.

WATSON, WILLIAM LAW, A.M.I.C.E., M. Coun. Inst. Loco. Engrns.—B. 1883; ed. Grammar Schl. and Robert Gordon's Coll. (Abdn.); loco. dept., G.N.S.R., 1899; loco. dept., G.W.R., 1905; engnr. design dept., Crown agents, 1912; min. of munitions, 1915; dep. head, engnr. design dept., C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engnr. (mech.), 1928; ch. engnr. (contract), 1935.

WATT, MICHAEL HERBERT, C.B.E. (1935), M.D. (N.Z.), D.P.H. (N.Z.).—B. 1887; ed. Otago Boys' High Schl. and Otago Univ. (N.Z.); med. offr. of health, 1917; ast. ch. health offr., 1920; dep. dir.-gen. of health, 1925; dir.-gen., 1931.

WATT, SIR THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1912); C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1857; ed. at priv. schls. and Glasgow Univ.; admtd. a sol. to Scottish ct.; practised in Natal; served as subaltern in Imperial L.I., 1900; apptd. capt. and offr. commdg. Newcastle Town Guard, and ment. in desps.; M.L.A., Natal, 1901-1910; formerly min. of just. and educn., Natal; min. of posts and telegraphs and pub. wks., Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of pub. wks. and interior, 1916; min. of pub. health, 1919; min. of posts and telegraphs and of public wks., 1921-24.

WATTS, BERTRAM T., O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1883; ed. Wellingborough Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A., F.R.G.S.; inspr., Egyptian govt. survey, 1905-08; inspr. Egyptian govt. survey, 1912; on sp. surveys in the Sudan for irrign. wks.; dir. sp. irrign. surveys, Mesopotamia, for Turkish govt., 1908-11; ast. dir. surveys, Cyprus, 1912-20; ag. regisr. gen., 1913, 1918, 1919 and 1920; ag. Br. del. of Evcaf., 1919, 1923 and 1926; regisr. gen., Cyprus, 1920; passed in mod. Gk. and Turkish; title altered to dir., land registration and surveys, July, 1927; dir. surveys, land offr. and commr., mines, Uganda, 1930.

WATTS, CLIVE.—B. 1893; 2nd lieut., Northumberland Fusiliers, Sept., 1915; lieut., July, 1917; capt., June, 1918; ment. in desps., and wounded, European War; acctnt., treasy., Cyprus, 1920; acctnt. to pub. custodian and Cyprus contr. in addition, Apr., 1921 to May, 1923; ag. ast. treas., for various periods in 1924, 1925 and 1927; ag. ast. sec., col. sec's office in 1926 and 1927; ag. dep. treas., July, 1927 to Jan., 1928; dep. treas., Jan., 1928; ag. treas., for various periods, 1928-33; offr. ree. in bankruptcy, Mar. to June, 1931; Br. del. of Evcaf., Nov., 1933; treas., Mauritius, 1934.

WATTS, Guy Tracey.—B. 1892; ed. Clifton Coll.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, Apr., 1914; war serv., 1915-18; crown counsel, Nigeria, Mar., 1925; pol. mag., Calabar, 1928; atty.-gen., Bahamas, Jan., 1930; chmn., bd. of educn., mem., leg. coun.; ag. ch. just., in 1930, 1931 and 1932; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1933.

WAUCHOFF, Major-Gen. Sir Arthur Grenfell, G.C.M.G. (1933), K.C.B. (1931), C.B. (1923), C.M.G. (1917), C.I.E. (1919), D.S.O. (1900).—B. 1874; ed. St. Ninian's, Moffat and Repton; 2nd lieut., 4th Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders, 1893; 2nd Batt., The Black Watch, 1896; capt., 1901; served in Boer War (despa., Queen's med. with clasp, King's med. with two clasps); European War, 1914-18; major, 1914; commanded 2nd Batt., Black Watch in France and Mesopotamia, 34th Brigade and temporarily 7th divn., Indian Corps (despa.); bvt. lieut.-col., afterwards col., 1922; major-gen., 1923; mili. mem., Overseas Sttlmt. delegation to Australia and N.Z., 1923; ch. of Br. sec., mili. inter-allied comsn. of control, Berlin, 1924-27; G.O.C., 44th Home Counties divn., T.A., 1927; G.O.C., Northern Ireland dist., 1929; high commr. and comdr.-in-ch., Palestine, 1931.

WAYLAND, Edward James.—B. 1888; ed. Central Foundation Sch., City of London Coll., R. Coll. of Science and R. Sch. of Mines; nat. sci. studentship (C.L.C.), 1906; Natl. schl. (R.C.S.), 1907-10; post grad. & Marshall research schol., 1910; A.R.C.S., M.Inst. M.M.; F.G.S., London, etc.; Bigaby med., geol. soc., Lond., 1933; Victoria med., R.G.S., 1935; ast. min. survr., Ceylon, 1912; war serv. 254 Tunnelling Coy., R.E.; forward demolition reconnaissance offr. (IVth Army); ag. O.C., 3rd Water Boring Sec., R.E., apptd. to Uganda, Oct., 1918; dir. geological survey, 1928.

WAYNE, Richard St. John Ormerod.—B. 1904; schol. of Eton, 1917; Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1923; M.A., 1930; ast. sec., col. sec's. Office, Cyprus, May, 1927; ag. pvte. sec. to O.A.G., on various occasions, 1927-29 and 1932; ag. ch. ast. sec. in 1930 and 1933; ag. consnr., in 1931, 1934 and 1935; admstive. offr.; ast. sec., Jan., 1935; comnr., Mar., 1935.
WEAVING, L. A.—Ast. dist. comnr., Kenya, June, 1921; ag. sec., 1930.

WEBB, Ambrose Henry.—B. 1882; ed. Clifton Coll., Bath Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford, 1899 (class. schol.); 1st Victoria prizeman and John Brooke schol., King's Inns, Dublin, 1908; called to bar, 1909; K.C., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Samaria, 1921; puisne judge, Kenya, 1933.

WEBB, Cecil Richard, O.B.E. (1926), M.C.—Ast. traffic manager, Uganda rly., 1914; traffic man., Tanganyika Territory rlys., Oct., 1919; gen. man., govt. rlys., Sierra Leone, June, 1923; gen. man., Palestine rly., 1930.

WEBB, Oliver Stanley, M.C., M.Inst. M. & Cy. B., M. I. Struct. E. Chtd. Struct. Engr.—B. 1889; ed. Kingswood Coll., Grahamstown and Rhodes Univ. Coll., S. Africa; sapper, R.E., Dec., 1914; mentd. in desp., capt. commdg. 31st divn. signal co., R.K., Jan., 1919; ast. engrn., P.W.D., S.S., Feb., 1920; exec. engrn., Jan., 1927; ag. senr. exec. engrn., June, 1935.
WEBB, Hon. Patrick Charles.—Mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1913-18 and since 1933; min. of mines, 1935.

WEBB, Honour Hope Mitchell, B.A., B.L., B.C.L., Portora Royal Schl., Enniskillen, Dublin; trop. African schol., Oxford, 1928-29; cadet, Tanzania Territory, 1929; ast. ed. 1931; ag. res. mag., Apr., 1932.

WEBB, William Leslie—B. 1896; Coll., Blackheath and Guys' Hosp., L.S., Lond.; D.P.H., Lond.; M.R.C.P., Lond.; med. offr., Uganda serv., venereal diseases, 1913; temp. or med. serv., 1914-16; temp. caps., R.Y. 19; senr. med. offr., 1925; sec. offr., Mulago hosp., 1926; dep. dir. Uganda, 1928; dir. med. service, 1934.

WEBBER, Major John Warren—(R.A.R. of O.).—B. 1884; ed. Medics and tele., Transvaal, 1903; Union of S. Africa, 1913-19; war Rebellion, 1910, German S.W. Afr. France and Flanders, 1914-18; Geol. and tele., dist. survr., Feb., 1920; 1929; ch. acct., post and tel., Sept., 1931.

WEBLEY, H. L.—B. 1884; ed. Plymouth; E. African pol., Feb., 1912; European war, 1915-19; ast. commr., Nigeria, Sept., 1919; seconded to pol., inspr., prisons and sherif, Gambia, 1920; ast. commr., pol. Gambian commr., pol., etc. as prte. sec., on several occasions, 1920-23; ag. senr. commr., on several occasions, 1920-29; commr., prisons and sherif, June, 1929; ki. 1934.

WEBSTER, George Franklin—ast. polit. offr., German E. Afr. admstrn., Oct., 1916; adminstr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922; ag. senr. commr., Es-Salam dist., Jan., to Dec., 1926; offr. mem., Jan., to Apr., 1927; sec., Feb., 1928; prov. commr., Jan., 1929; senr. prov. commr., mem., leg. coun., 1935.

WEBSTER, George Henry.—B. 1887; Br. post office, 1918-20; twice mentd. in desp., dist. and tele., Palestine, 1920; numerous occasions; dep. P.W.D., 1920; ast. audir., Kenya, keeper, P.W.D., 1929; sec., Nov., 1929.

WEBSTER, Capt. John—Surg. (Vic. Univ. Manch.), Barrister-at-law, Middle Temp., med. offr., S.S., July, 1910; med. offr. for surg. wks., July, 1910; cls. IV, J.W., 1914; W.Y., 1914; in desp.); prof. of med., Sept., 1922; offr. deleg. Assoc. of Trop. Med., Trop. logist., S.S., Apr., 1930; S.pore, in addn., in 1930.

WEDDERBURN, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1895; Coll., Edinburgh, and cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Point Pedro and Charlestown, Matara, Aug., 1909; K.C., land settlmt. offr., Jan., 1912; offr. and special of ordn. offr., Ordoc., Aug., 1912; ast. land settlmt. offr., Waste Lands Off.,

WELLS, THOMAS HUSTAGE, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1888; ed. Lincoln Cathedral Choir Sch.; statnmr., F.M.S. Rly., Sept., 1913; asst. traffic inspr., Tamhii, Sept., 1915; wharf supt., Pral., Oct., 1916; asst. traffic man., July, 1918; asst. traffic man., July, 1926; senr. asst. traffic man., July, 1927; asst. sec. to gen. m., F.M.S. rly., Jan., 1932.

WELSH, ALEXANDER SIMPSON, K.C.—B. 1881; Cape civ. serv., 1899; B.A. (Cape), 1904; LL.B. (Cape), 1907; prof. assist. to crown prosecutor, Johannesburg, 1914; prof. assist. to atty.-gen., Cape, 1918; crown pros., Johannesburg, 1921; law advr., 1926; atty.-gen., Transvaal, 1929.

WENTWORTH, M. F. G., B.A.—B. 1908; ed. Haileybury and Univ. Coll., London (teacher's dipl., Lond.); inspr., schls., Aug., 1930.
WERE, MARY JOSEPHINE, O.B.E., L.R.C.P., S.L., D.P.H.—Lady med. offr., dist. hosp., K. Lumpur, May, 1921; do., infant welfare centre, May, 1923.

WEST, HAROLD.—B. 1889; ed. Christ's Coll., Blackheath; land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1910; lieut., R.G.A., 1917-19; lat. cls. land survr., 1920; prin. land survr., Kowloon and New Territories, 1922; 1st asst. supt., crown lands, 1926; asst. supt., crown lands, 1932.
WESTON, BERTRAM JOHN, M.Sc. (Agr.) S. Africa, M.A. (Cantab.), F.R.H.S.—B. 1907; ed. Ashford Grammar and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; horticulturist, (now supt., agr.), Cyprus, 1931.
