WESTON, HARRY CHARLES, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1894; ed. Chatham House Schl., Ramsgate, Goldsmiths Training Coll., Univ. of Lond., Glasgow Univ., and Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; war serv., 1914-19; ch. ast. engrnr., Zanzibar, 1921; res. engrnr., Dar-en-Salaam, 1925; prin., Accra Tech. Schl., May, 1929.

WHEATLEY, MARK.—B. 1888; ed. Nottingham Univ. Coll.; ast. mast., Victoria Inst., Selangor, Jan., 1911; headmast., govt. Eng., Schl., K. Pilah, Apr., 1927; headmast., King George V Schl., Seremban, Jan., 1928; do., Batu Rd. Schl., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1930; ag. headmast., Maxwell Schl., K. Lumpur, in addn., July-Dec., 1930.

WHRELLS, CHARLES JOHN.—B. 1887; joined Royal North West Mounted Pol., Canada, Dec., 1912; proceeded overseas with 1st Canadian contigent, Sept., 1914; on warserv., 1914-19; "1914- Star, Victory and Gen. Serv. Meds., Meritorious Serv. Med., Croix-de-Guerre (Belgian), ment. in despr., Apr., 1917; rejoined R.N.W.M. Pol., Canada, May, 1919; asst. comdt., pol., Bahamas, Apr., 1921; ag. comdt., June, 1921 to Apr., 1923 and from May to Dec., 1924; comdt. of pol., inspr. of prisons, provost marshal and ch. of fire dept., Aug., 1926.

WHEELER, LEONARD RICHMOND, Ph.D. (Lond.), M.Sc. B.A. (Lond.), M.R.S.T. — B. 1888; science master, Letchworth Schl., 1909-12; Gram. Schl., Antigua, 1912-14; ent. col. serv. as science master, Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad Dec., 1914; coman. in 1st batt., B.W.I.R., from 1st Trinidad contingent, Sept., 1915; pilot, R.A.F., 1917, served in Egypt and Salonica, 1916-19; returned to Trinidad, 1920; coman. for Boy Scouts, Trinidad, 1920; transfd. to S.S. and F.M.S. as European mast., Nov., 1921; science and maths. mast., Malay Coll., K. Kangsar, Dec., 1921; vice-prin., Sultan Idris Training Coll., T. Malim, Aug., 1924; asst. instr., schls., Perak, July, 1926 and Sept., 1930; instr., schls., Malacca, Sept., 1926; ditto, Pahang, Feb., 1928; asst. instr., schls., Penang, Oct., 1934; author of "Flora of Antigua," "Scouting in the Tropics," "The Modern Malay," etc.
WHEELER, CAPT. RICHARD CHAMBERLAIN, M.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—Veterinary offr., Kenya, July, 1913; senior veterinary officer, Apr., 1920.

WHISKARD, SIR GEOFFREY GRANVILLE, K.C.M.G. (1933), C.B. (1923), C.M.G. (1931).—B. 1886; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. moda., 1907; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1909; ent. H.O., 1911; asst. pvt. sec. and subsequently prin. pvt. sec. to successive secs. of state, 1915-20; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Dublin Castle, 1920-22; asst. sec., C.O., 1st Apr., 1924; vice-chmn., Oversea Settlement C'tee., 4th Sept., 1929; asst. under sec. of State, D.O. in addn., 1st Jan., 1931; accompanied U.K. deleg. to Imp. Economic Confce., Ottawa, 1932; high commr. in the C'wealth of Australia for H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., 1936.

WHITCOMBE, SIDNEY F.—B.1899; ent.C.O., July, 1915; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., 1917; on mil. serv., Jan., 1918 to Apr., 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, Apr., 1927; dep. regis., Nov., 1931.

WHITE, ALBERT GEORGE.—B. 1901; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., H.M.S.O. Mar., 1920; transfd. to C.O., Mar., 1928; accompanied U.K. deleg. to Imp. Economic Confce., Ottawa, 1932; seconded as regis. on staff of high commr. in S. Africa for H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., May, 1934.

WHITE, ALEXANDER, T.C.T. Teacher's Dipl. (Ireland).—B. 1883; ed. Training Coll., Kildare Place, Dublin; asst. mast., army schls., 1914-11; headmast., ditto, 1912-23; asst. mast., Saivengun, Hong Kong, 1923; ditto, King's Coll., 1926; lect., T.I. English, 1925; headmr., Ellis Kadoorie schl., 1936.

WHITE, ARTHUR REGINALD, M.C.—B. 1886; ch. drattman, Kelantan, May, 1912; on mily. serv. 1915-18; asst. supt., surveys, Kelantan, Nov., 1918; supt., surveys, Jan., 1923; supt., rev. surveys, F.M.S. and S.S., July, 1929; supt., surveys, Malacca, Mar., 1931; ag. asst. surv. gen., F.M.S., Apr., 1933; senr. supt., surveys, Perak, Aug., 1934; dep. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Nov., 1935.

WHITE, ARTHUR V.—B.1871; ed. Pub. and High Schls., and Univ. of M.E.; engrnr. with several prominent lect. in mech. drawing and mach. Toronto Tech. Schl.; later with depts., Can.; consulting engrnr., Coman. Can., until its abolition, 1921; engrnr., representing Can. to the intercoman. Lake of the Woods intercoman. the boundary waters treaty, 1911; engrnr. to Hydro-electric Coman., Ont.; has written extensively on water power development and fuel supply.
WHITE, GEORGE, B.Sc. (Eng.), AMIMech. Engr.—B. 1898; capt. M.E. prin.; junr. tech. schl., Hong Kong; trade and tech. schls., 1935.
WHITE, HUGH FORRESTER MORGAN.—B. 1891; ed. Malvern Coll. and St. Nicholas Oxford; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915-19; offr., cls. I, Sept., 1932.
WHITE, LT.-COL. THOMAS WADE, M.P., V.D.—El. to H. of R., Canada, Australia for Balclava (Vic.), 1929; trade and cust. since 1933.

WHITE, RT. HON. SIR WILLIAM TRIMBLE.—B. 1866; ed. High Schls., Oakville and Ont., Univ. of Toronto (B.A.), graduate, Ontario law schl.; apptd. manager National Ltd., Toronto, 1899; subsequently gen. mngt. and vice-pres. of the same institution; member of the Privy Council for Canada and minister in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1917; minister of National govt., Oct., 1917; reelected at general election, 1917, for Leeds-Brockville; resign. position, 1919.
WHITE, W. V. C.—B. 1897; offr., 8th Sept., 1914 to 14th Dec., 1916; through wounds, 17th Feb., 1916; offr., Food, 18th Mar., 1918; appt. cls. I, 24th Apr., 1922.
WHITEHEAD, JOHN.—B. 1891; ed. Schls. and Tech. Coll., Greenock; mem. of Inst. of Naval Architects; architect, Nigerian marine dept., 1928.

WHITELEY, GERALD CHARLES.—B. 1894; ed. Bradford and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (Oxon); 2nd cls. hons. moda., 1915; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915-19; adm. prov., 1925; adm. prov., cls. I, 1931; ag. ch. dep. ch. sec., 1934; ag. ch. dep. ch. sec., 1935.
WHITLAMSMITH, GEORGE.—B. 1903; ed. Westminster and Clapham; 2nd cls. hons., hist., 1926; admin. Tanganyika Territory, 1926-1928.

WHITLEY, MAJOR NORMAN, M.C., B.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-Law, Temple.—B.1883; called to bar on war serv., 7th Manchester Temp., 1915; demob. with rank of maj., dep. pub. pros., S.S., Jan., 1919; judge, S.S., Feb., 1930; puisne judge, S'pore, Apr., 1930; puisne judge, Penang, Apr., 1930; puisne judge, S'pore, Apr., 1932; do., Penang, ch. just., Jan., 1936.
WHITLEY, W. B.A. (Cantab.), analytical chem., Tanganyika Territory.
WHITTAKER, HARRY, C.E., A.M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1891; Honduras, 1911-20; ass't.
ITTING, CHARLES EDWARD JEWEL.—B. ed. Haileybury and St. John's Coll.; aspt., educ. dept., N. Prov., Nigeria, 1922; senr. educ. offr., Palestine, 1931; educ., Nigeria, 1933.
ITTINGDALE, WILLIAM.—B. 1885; ch. tsman and compr., surveys, Cape, Mar., prof. asst., Sept., 1920; 1st grade prof. trig. survey, Nov., 1923; dir., trig. survey, 1933.
ITTINGHAM, THOMAS WILLIAM.—B.; ed. Masonic Sch., Bushey; sub-inspr., Br. Guiana, 1924; ex. officio J.P., 1925; inspr., 1927; ag. commr., N.W. dist. in county inspr., Dec., 1932.

HITTLE, REGINALD ALFRED, M.C.—B. 1897; Manchester Grammar Schl. and Queen's Coll., rd (schol.); 1st cls. math. mods., 1919; B.A.; 2nd lieut., Manchester Regt., 1916; served France, 1917; invalided from Army, 1918; dist. commr., Uganda, 1921; asst. sec.; senr. asst. sec., 1932.

HITWORTH, STANLEY HOWARTH.—B.; ed. Melbourne Univ., Royal Vet. Coll., Univ. of Zurich; vet. serv., Kenya, May-Dec., 1930; do., Gold Coast, and miny. of land fisheries, Jan., 1931-Aug., 1933; div. resch. and vet. adv., Malaya, Sept., 1933.

HYBRO, CHARLES, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1902; Berkhaimsted Schol., London Univ., Schl. of Economics, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, economic tripso., p. I., 3rd cls., 1922; law ps., II., 3rd cls., 1923; supt., educ., Guyanika Territory, 1926.

HYTE-SMITH, THOMAS STODART.—B.; ed. Merchiston Castle Schol. and Edinburgh v.; writer to signet, 1906; 2nd lieut., the Royal m., 1915; lieut., 1916; capt., K.A.R., 1918; ed. in Nyasaland and Portuguese E. Africa, 1919; 2nd asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, Dec., 1920; J.P., 1924; asst. crown solr., 1926; ag., Kowloon, 1929 and 1930; asst. crown solr., 1930; ag. land offr. and reg. marriages, 1934; land offr., 1935.

ICKHAM, CAPT. DENIS HENRY.—B. 1896; Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; taught Rangers, Sept., 1914; served B.E.F., 1915-18; mentd. in desps.; transf. to men's Royal Regt., 1922; served with 5th A.R. Kenya, 1922-25; admstr. offr., Kenya; seconded to Somaliland, Apr., 1929.

VICKHAM, DUDLEY LEE BORRETT.—B. 1909; Br. Guiana and Barbados; cler. asst. secre., Br. Guiana, Apr., 1927; asst. clk., leg. n., Oct., 1930; 4th cls. clk., secretariat, Jan., 33; pte. voc. to O. A. G., May to Oct., 1932; clk., leg. coun., June and July, 1933; has secy. to various comtees. and bds.; clk., leg. n., Oct., 1935.

VICKHART, W. A. F.—B. 1888; clk. Crown ents' office, 1906; asst. head, gen. dept., Mar., 1922; do., stores dept., July, 1931; dep. head, piping dept., Nov., 1934; asst. sec., W. African toney bd., June, 1914; secy., Dec., 1918.

WICKRAMASINGHE, REGINALD HUGH, A., (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Lond.), F.R.A.S.—B. 1871; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1930; asst. dulla kach., Nov., 1930; office asst., Anuradhapura kach., Feb., 1932; do., Kegalla kach., ig., 1934; do., Mannar and Mullaitivu kachs., Nov., 1935.

WICKRAMASINGHA, SENA PANDUKABHAYA, A., LL.B. (Cantab.)—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1922; asst. to Matara kach., Dec., 1922; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Apr., 1923; extra office asst., Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1925; office asst., ditto, June, 1925; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Feb., 1926; addnl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, June, 1927; pol. mag., Panadura, Oct., 1927; ditto, Point Pedro, Apr., 1929; dist. judge, Tangalle, 1932; asst. attit. offr., Apr., 1934; dep. rubber contr. in addn., May, 1934.

WICKREMESINGHE, CRIL LEONARD.—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Apr., 1912; att., Badulla kach. Apr., 1912; Matara kach. Oct., 1912; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, July, 1913; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avissawella, June, 1916; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1917; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1920; addnl. ast. govt. agt., Colombo, May, 1921; ast. govt. agt., Mannar, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Nov., 1925; ast. govt. agt., Puttalam, Oct., 1926; ditto, Kalutara, Mar., 1928; ag. govt. agt., N.C.P., July, 1931; addnl. commr., lands, Sept., 1934; commr., lands, Apr., 1935.

WIDDUP, MAJOR CHARLES PONSONBY, V.D.—B. 1884; clerical asts., treas., B. Guiana, 1902; 6th cls. clk., lands and mines dept., 1903; ag. sub-inspr. of police, 1903; sub-inspr. of police, 1904; dist. inspr. of police, 1908; passed Hindustani exam., 1909; county inspr. of police, 1914; dep. inspr. gen. and supt., fire brig., B. Guiana, Dec., 1920; internat. prison confce., 1925; ag. inspr.-gen., pol. and commdt., local forces in 1924, 1926, 1927, 1930 and 1933; ag. inspr., prisons in addn., July-Nov., 1933; King's pol. med., 1934.

WIGGETT, ARCHIBALD DOUGLAS.—B. 1884; law and income tax depts., Cape Col., 1901-10; census office, 1911; accts. br., dept. of int., 1912; income tax dept., 1914; inland rev. dept., 1916; recr., rev., Kimberley, 1931; acct., 1931; recr., rev., E. London, 1932; ch. clk., income tax, Feb., 1933; recr., rev., Pretoria, 1933; ch. rev. offr., June, 1934.
WIGHTMAN, J. W. E.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, Apr., 1914; ag. res. commr., 1926.

WIGLEY, WILFRID MURRAY, O.B.E. (1931).—Ed. Derby schl.; held various clerical appts. under gov't., St. Kitts-Nevis, Feb., 1895, to Apr., 1898; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1901; practised at bar of Leeward Is. for some years; ag. admstr., St. Kitts, puisne judge, Leeward Is. and atty.-gen., on various occasions; crown atty., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918; 1st puisne judge, Leeward Is., Jan., 1935.
WILCOCKS, C., M.D., Ch.B. (Manch.)—B. 1896; milly. serv., 1915-19; med. offr., Tanganika Territory, Nov. 1927; tuberculosis research offr., 1930.

WILCOCKS, CARL THEODORUS MULLER.—B. 1861; ed. Godbolt's Comm'l Acad., Graaff Reinet; represented Fauresmith including Jagersfontein and Koffyfontein in first Union parl.; M.L.A. for Winburg (O.F.S.), 1915-24; mem., rlys. and harbors. bd., 1924; pub. debt, commr., 1925; admstr., O.F.S., Mar., 1929.

WILFORD, SIR THOMAS MASON, K.C.M.G. (1930), K.C. (1929).—B. 1870; ed. Christ Coll., Canterbury, N.Z.; M.P. for Wellington Suburbs, 1900 and for Hutt from 1903-29; chmn., Wellington harbour bd., 1908-9 (mem. 10 years); min. of just., marine, etc., in national govt., two years; leader of opposition and liberal party, 1920-25; min. of just. and defence, 1928-29; high commr. for N.Z. in London, 1929-33.
WILKIN, R. O., M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H. (Edin.).—B. 1889; mily. serv., 1914-20; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925.

WILKINSON, ALAN FREDERICK LAWDER.—B. 1892; ed. Royal Naval Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth, and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; war serv. (Navy), 1914 to 1919; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, July, 1919; dist. commr., April, 1924.
WILKINSON, PAUL BIDDULPH, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lond., M.B., B.S., Lond., M.R.C.P., Lond.—B. 1901; W.A.M.S., 1930; 2nd med. offr., St. Helena, 1934; ag. senr. med. offr., 1936.

WILLAN, HAROLD CURWEN, M.C., B.A.; (Oxon.), Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple.—B. 1896; ed. Kendall Gram. Schol. and Jesus Coll., Oxford (open exhib.); lieut., R.G.A.; M.C., June, 1917; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. V, Oct., 1923; dist. offr., Jelebu, Nov., 1923; asst. dist. offr., K. Kangar, Aug., 1925; offr., cls. IV, Oct., 1927; examur. in law for State of Perak, Nov., 1927; ag. reg. dist. judge and 1st mag., Malacca, Jan., 1932; offr., cls. III, Oct., 1932; ag. dep. legal advr., F.M.S., July, 1934; solr.-gen., Kenya, 1936.
WILLAN, REGINALD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Ed. Oakham Schol. and Guy's Hospital; med. offr. to Baro-Kano rly., Nigeria, 1909-1911; joined West African med. staff, Jan., 1912.

WILLBOURN, ERIC STEWART, M.A. (Cantab.), F.G.S.—B. 1889; asst. geologist, F.M.S., May, 1914; 2nd lieut., R.E. (Signal.), Apr., 1918; demob., May, 1919; ag. geologist, F.M.S., Nov., 1922-July, 1923 and July, 1927-Feb., 1928; dir. geol. survey, F.M.S., Dec., 1931.

WILCOCKS, MAJOR JAMES LUGARD, D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., The Black Watch, Feb., 1912; A.D.C. to G.O.C., N. Army, India, July, 1913; A.D.C. to G.O.C. Indian Army Corps, France, Sept., 1914; (twice ment. in desps.); brig. maj., 1916; brig. maj., Bermuda, Mar., 1919; ret., Feb., 1922; (placed on R. of O.); A.D.C., Kenya, Feb., 1925; ag. sec., native affrs. dept., May, 1928 to Sept., 1930; asst. commr., prisons, Apr., 1932; commr., prisons, 1933.
WILLDON, MARGARET ISABEL, M.B.E. (1925).—B. 1877; matron, Basutoland leper asylum, 1913.

WILLIAMS, ARTHUR, B.Sc. (Econ.) Hons. (Lond.), F.R.Econ.S.—B. 1899; ed. Wade Deacon Gram. Schol., Widnes, and Lond. Univ. (External); clk., supply estabn., G.P.O., May, 1920; cadet, S.S., June, 1924; jr. mag., Malacca and attchd. land office, July, 1924; passed cadet, June, 1926; offr., cls. VI, May, 1927; offr., cls. V, June, 1927; offr., cls. IV, June, 1931; passed bar final, May, 1933; lect. in the penal code, Raffles Coll., Oct., 1934; ag. reg. dist. judge and 1st mag., Malacca, Apr., 1935; offr., cls. III, June, 1936.

WILLIAMS, A. D. J. B., O.B.E. Med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1912; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Sept., 1923; ag. dep. dir. med. services, 1928; dep. D.M.S., 1928; dir. med. and many services, Nyasaland, 1933; provh. mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1936.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES EDWARD, C.R.E. (1935), O.B.E. (1918), M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1873; ed. Llandovery Coll.; asst. wks. man., Vulcan loco. works, Lance., inspr., N.Z. and W. Australian rlys.; own Agent's office, 1900; asst. engr., Johnson, loco. wks., Darlington; engr., Crown Agts., 1904; section dir., Miny. of Munitions, 1916; major, R.R. D.A.L.W.O., 1918; tech. head of engg. mun. dept., C.A., 1919; visited Nigeria, 1920; Palestine and Cyprus, 1921; ch. inspr. engr., 1922; pres., Inst. of Loco. Engrs., 1933-34.

WILLIAMS, MAJOR C. T., O.B.E.—B. 1877; ed. St. John's Leatherhead; late int. Batt. Tr. Surrey Regt.; ret. with rank of maj.; serv. in France, Belgium, Italy and Russia twice ment. in desps.; adjt., N. Terr. constab., Apr., 1925; ag. comm., N.T.C., various periods; commr., pol., Gold Coast disb.ment of N.T.C., Apr., 1929; int. Batt., Gold Coast pol., June, 1931.
WILLIAMS, CHARLES HOLMAN DAVIS, M.A., Dip. Agr., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1897; asst. mist. and later dir., Guadeloupe sugar expt. station; agronomist, Sugar expt. station; dept. agr., Br. Guiana, 1929.
WILLIAMS, D. L. GWTINNE.—B. 1888; sup. ct., Eng., 1914; served European forces 1915-18; et. marital offr., 1918; staff ser., 1919; conveyancer and reg. of titles, 1923.

WILLIAMS, EDWARD BENEDICTUS, cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; asst. dist. offr., Mar., 1910; 2nd asst. dist. offr., Dec., 1914; asst. dist. offr., Aug., 1917; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, 1918; mag., Seremban, Jan., 1920; Tampin, Mar., 1920; dist. offr., Col., 1920; collr., land rev., Seremban, Nov., 1925; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1925; pur. F.M.S., in addn., Mar., 1927; offr., F.M.S., May, 1927; offr., cls. I.B., commr., lands, F.M.S., Apr., 1931; ag. advr. in addn., Jan., 1933; reg. and offl. assignee, B.S., Jan., 1934.

WILLIAMS, ERIC TREMBAR, B. 1890; ed. Blundell's schol. and Oxford; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1913; office, Dec., 1913; dist. offr., Col., cls. IV, B.S., Nov., 1920; ag. sec., Johore, June, 1932; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1930; ag. sec., Perak, Aug., 1935.

WILLIAMS, ERNEST HILL.—B. 1899; cadet, Hon. K. cadet, Dec., 1925; asst. to affrs., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Aug., 1928; 2nd pol. mag. and dist. offr., N. dist., Nov., asst. to sec., Chinese aff., sec., Chinese aff., and menu., Aug.-Oct., 1935; ag. asst. at ag. puisne judge, May-Jun.
WILLIAMS, ERNEST STANLEY.—ed. Chatham House, Rangoon Hall, Oxford; prin., dioce. Rangoon, 1924-25; lect., Univ., 1923-25; supt., ed. tory, 1926.

WILLIAMS, FRANK LEWIS.—B. 1899; ed. Weymouth, Cambridge; cadet, F.M.S., Chinese prot., Perak, Dec., May, 1924; offr., cls. V, Nov., 1924; attchd. Ch. 1925; do., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1927; offr., cls. I., Nov., 1928; asst. p.

WILLIAMS, FRANK STANLEY.—B. 1894; ed., Paul's Sch., London; co. aud. dept., Feb., 1920; asst. aud., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; senr. asst. aud., July, 1924; ag. dep. aud., various occasions from 1926; ag. aud., Mar., Aug., 1930; senr. asst. aud., Palestine, Apr., 1931; auditor, Nyasaland, 1935.

WILLIAMS, GILBERT CLYDE.—B. 1878; ed., Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1902; consnr., income tax, Barbados, 1924; judge, asst. ct. of appl., and inspr., prisons, 1925; ac. atty.-gen., and ch. just., various periods, 1928-36.

WILLIAMS, JOHN BASIL.—B. 1906; ed., Marlborough, McGill Univ., Montreal and Cambridge; colonial exhibitioner, Trinity Coll., 1924-26; 2nd cls., nat. sci. tripus, pt. I., 1927; 2nd cls. pt., 1928, B.A., 1927; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 17th Oct., 1929; seconded to N. Rhodesia adminstr., 1935-36; prin., 3rd Mar., 1937.
WILLIAMS, J. H., M.I.C.E., M.A.M.Soc.C.E.—B. 1873; res. engr., Singapore govt. rly.; engr.-in-ch., Canton-Hankow rly.; res. engr., Benue Bridge constrn., Nigeria, 1928.

WILLIAMS, OWEN GEORGE REVELL, C.M.G.—B. 1886; ed., Lancing Coll. and Oxford; exhibitioner, Hertford Coll., 1906; 1st cls. mods., 1907; 1st cls. Lit. Hum., 1909; Aubrey Moore scholarship, 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., k. in sec.'s off., In. Rev., Oct., 1910; 2nd cls. k., C.O., Mar., 1911; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, 4th June, 1916; priv. sec. to Mr. Hewins, 26th Sept., 1917; priv. sec. to Col. Amery, 14th Jan., 1919; 1st cls. clk., 8th (ar.), 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., Feb., 1926.
WILLIAMS, PATRICK EDWARD WILLOWS.—B. 1904; ed., Shrewsbury Schl. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

WILLIAMS, PHILIP STANHOPE, B.A.—B. 1889; 1st at Harrow (head of the schl. 1907-08) and at Alias Coll., Camb. (schol. and Sayer schol.), lat. cls. classical tripus, 1911; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; passed cadet, June, 1915; offr., cl. V., Jan., 1916; asst. sec. to res., Pers., Feb., 1917; offr., s. IV., Dec., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Larut, June, 1921; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1924; ag. Br. ldr., Perla, May, 1925; 2nd mag., Penang, Jan., 1925; 2nd mag., Spore, July, 1929; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1929; ag. legal advr. and judi. comm., Kelantan, Sept., 1930; dist. offr., Klangsar, June, 1933; offr., cls. IB., Nov., 1933.

WILLIAMS, ROBERT ORCHARD.—B. 1891; curator, botanic gardens, Trinidad, 1916; supt., gr., Grenada, 1920; economic botanist, 1931; est. dir. agr., Trinidad, 1934; ch. horticultural offr., Palestine, 1935.
WILLIAMS, SIDNEY BRUCE.—B. 1880; S.A. constab., 1901; Swaziland pol., 1908; clk., 1911; J.P., 1916; dep. asst. consmr., 1928; asst. omsnr., 1930.

WILLIAMS, WILLIAM JOHN, M.I.E.E., L.I.Mech.E., M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1880; ed., Carthorpe County Schl., Univ. Coll., N. Wales and Univ. of Liverpool (engnr. dept.); attd. Portsmouth dockyard, 1909; sent to Hong Kong or electrification H.M. naval yd., May, 1909; ch. engr., Shanghai munic. elect. dept., 1911; engr.-in-ch. and man., Penang munic. elect. supply and tramways undertakings, 1920; engr. and man., Penang Hill rlya., Oct., 1923; dir., elect. dept., F.M.S., Jan., 1927; advr., elec., M.S., Nov., 1934.

WILLIAMSON, E.—B. 1884; clk., G.P.O., Transvaal, Mar., 1903; treasy., Transvaal, July, 1904; senr. clk., Jan., 1919; prin. clk., Apr., 1921; sec., cabinet comte., on expenditure, 1922-24; ch. clk., estimates, June, 1929; under-secr. for finance, July, 1931.
WILLIAMSON, J., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Cert., Lond. S. Hyg. & T.M. (distinc.)—B. 1897; mily. serv., 1915-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

WILLIMOTT, STANLEY GORDON.—B. 1899; ed., Liverpool Univ. and St. Catharine's Coll., Camb.; war serv., 1917-19; B.Sc., 1st cls., schl. of chemistry, Liv. Univ., 1921; A.L.C., 1921; Ph.D. (Liv.), 1923; research schl., St. Catharine's Coll., Camb.; Ph.D. (Cantab.) in agrio. science, 1927; research chem., Imp. Chem. Industries, 1928-29; gov't. analyst, Cyprus, Nov., 1929; asst. gov't. analyst, S.S., 1936; author of numerous papers in scientific and med. journals.

WILLINGDON, 1ST VISCOUNT (cr. 1924); 1st Baron of Ratton (cr. 1910); Rt. Hon. Freeman Freeman-Thomas, P.C. (1931); G.B.E. (1917); G.C.S.I. (1918); G.C.I.E. (1913).—B. 1866; A.D.C. to Lord Brassey when gov. of Victoria, 1895; M.P., Hastings, 1900-06; Bodmin divn. of Cornwall, 1906-10; jun. lord of treas., 1905-12; J.P.; gov. of Bombay, 1913-19; ditto, Madras, 1919-24; major, Sussex Imperial Yeomanny; lord-in-waiting to His Majesty; gov'r-gen. of the Dominion of Canada, 1926-31; viceroy of India, 1931.

WILLIS, MAURICE A., M.B.E. (1933)—B. 1900; ent. M.O.D., G.P.O., May, 1915; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., Apr., 1918; on mil. serv., May, 1918 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transfd., P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; transfd., C.O., May, 1924; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Confce., Ottawa, 1932.

WILLIS, THOMAS WILLIAM.—B. 1897; ed., London; clk., treasy. and cust. house, Turks and Caicos Is., Jan., 1925; ag. asst. treas., postmr., collr. of cust., regir. of shipping and gen. man., savings bank, May, 1928 to Aug., 1928; clk. to consmr., Aug., 1928.

WILLMOT, ARTHUR CHARLES, O.B.E. (Mily.).—B. 1881; ed., Lancing Coll. and Aspatria Agric. Coll., Cumberland; asst. survr., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1907; dist. engr., P.W.D., May, 1908; ag. asst., D.P.W., Apr.-Nov., 1913; engr.-in-charge, Kampala Port Bell rlya. constrn., Dec., 1913; served with 4th K.A.R. in E. African Campaign, May, 1915 to Dec., 1918 (twice ment. in desps.); ag. D.D.P.W., Oct., 1920 to Apr., 1922; D.D.P.W., June, 1923; ag. D.P.W. on various occasions, 1923-24, 1926-27 and 1929; D.P.W., 1933.

WILLMOT, FREDERICK CHARLES, M.D., D.P.H., L.R.C.P., V.D.—Asst. health offr., dept. of pub. health, Union of S. Africa, 1913; senr. asst. health offr., Union of S. Africa and asst. dir., med. services (defence); col., S.A. Med. Corps.

WILLOUGHBY, GODFREY POUNTNEY, M.Sc. Tech., M.S., M.I.E.E.—B. 1888; ed. Taunton Schl. and Victoria Univ., Manchester; B.Sc., 1st cls. hon., elect. engrng., 1909; Schuster Schol., 1909; tech. asst., post office, Union of S. Africa, Mar., 1913; lect., elect. engrng., phys., math., Durban Tech. Coll., Apr., 1919; asst. engr., and tech. educn. offr., post office, Union of S. Africa, Dec., 1920; elect. inspr., Kenya, and ch. teleg. engr., Kenya and Uganda, Apr., 1926; engr.-in-ch., Kenya, Uganda and Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1933; ag. P.M.G., 1936.

WILLS, DANIEL.—B. 1898; ed. King's Rochester; R.M.C., Sandhurst; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1921; ag. asst. supt., govt. monops., S'pore, Feb., 1922; ag. asst. to adm. dist. offr., P. Wellesley, Nov., 1923; offr. cls. V, Oct., 1924; ag. sec. rubber restrn., Johore, Apr., 1927; offr. cls. IV, Oct., 1928; asst. advr., K. Tinggi; do., Kukup, Apr., 1930; offr. assignee, Penang, Apr., 1931; asst. dist. offr. B. Mertajam, Aug., 1931; offr. cls. III, 2nd mag., Penang, Oct., 1933; asst. advr., Sengamat, Feb., 1936.
WILLS, JOSEPH ARTHUR, B.Sc. (Edin.).—B. 1894; on war serv., R.F.A., France and Italy; asst. consr., forests, Gold Coast, Aug., 1921; provl. forest offr., Oct., 1931.

WILMOT, AUBREY CHARLTON.—Cape civ. serv., 1900; transfd., Transvaal, 1902; ch. clk., mast. sup. ct., 1910; ch. clk., land bank, 1912; asst. gen. man., 1921; gen. man., 1924; man. dir., 1930; chmn., farmers' spec. relief bd., 1931; chmn., farmers' assistance bd., 1935; chmn., immigr. selection bd., 1935.

WILSON, CECIL SAMUEL, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub. Univ.)—B. 1897; med. offr., F.M.S., July, 1925; surg., F.M.S., Aug., 1928; surg., N. Somalian, apr., 1931; do., Perak, July, 1932 and Nov., 1933; do., Selangor, Aug., 1933.

WILSON, CAPT. CHARLES, Barrister-at-Law (King's Inns).—B. 1889; ed. Foyle Coll., Derry, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (siz., jun. and sen. ex., schol., sen. mod., B.A.); cert B., Dublin Univ. O.T.C.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1913; attd. Chinese prot. and contr. of lab. office, Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1913 to May, 1914; asst. contr. of lab., Penang, Dec., 1914; passed cadet, Jan., 1915; on mily. serv., 2nd lieut., 6th K.S.L.I., July, 1915; France, Feb., 1916; offr., cls. V, May, 1916; lieut., July, 1916; capt., Feb., 1919; demob., Mar., 1919; ment. in dep. dep. contr. lab., Seremban, June, 1919; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., coast, in addn., Sept., Nov., 1920; govt. rice agt., Kinta, in addn., Dec., 1920; 2nd lieut. and adj., M.V.I., Perak, Apr., 1921; emigr. agt. in India, Aug., 1922; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1925; accomp. contr. lab. to Delhi, Feb. to Mar., 1926 and to Siuna, Aug. to Sept., 1927; state treas., Perak, Oct., 1928; chmn. sany. bd., Larut and Matang in addn., Oct., 1928; 2nd mag., S'pore, Jan., 1929; offr., cls. II, Jan., 1930; offr., cls. IB, 1931; emigr. consmr., Malaya, Madras, Oct., 1934; ag. contr., lab., Malaya, Apr., 1936.

WILSON, COLIN E. J.—Boarding offr. and clk., Br. Solomon Is., June, 1913; ag. dist. offr., Guadalcanal, Jan., 1915 and Apr., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands, Nov., 1916; ag. collr., cust., May, 1918; ag. treas. and collr., cust., Apr., 1919 and Dec., 1924; acctnt. and 1st clk., treas. and cust., Oct., 1929; visiting dist. offr., Mar., 1923; ag. dist. offr., E. Solomons, Apr., 1923; dep. consmr. for W. Pacific, July, 1923; dist. offr., Santa Cruz, Dec., 1923; ag. govt. sec., Mar., 1925; ag. req. consmr., Dec., 1925.
WILSON, D. E., M.B., B.Ch. (Aberd.).—B. 1902; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1928.
WILSON, DONALD BAGSTER, M.A., B.Ch. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1930; pathologist, 1936.
WILSON, FRANCOIS.—B. 1876; ed. A. T. Clark; clk., clk., acctnt. gen's.

WILSON, FRANK KEESHAH, B.A.—1891; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; deo., Dec., 1914; attd., census ofc., 1915; supt., posts and telegraphy, India, 1917; regis., sup. ct., S'pore, Oct., 1918; rubber assessment comm., 1920; Aug., 1926; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1928; Penang, Aug., 1929; offr., cls. I, 1930; K. Lumpur, Jan., 1933; Muar, Oct., 1936.

WILSON, GEORGE FREDERICK, B.A.—B. 1886; ed. King Edward's Sch., London; law, 1890; advr., pub. wks., dept., Cyprus, 1901; sec.'s off., 1904; ag. asst. sec., chmn., 1905; July, 1919 and June to Oct., 1920; Sept., 1919; ag. compt., on various duties (now styled compt'r., cust. and exec. regis., Br. ships, regis., Cyprus fisheries; mem., leg. coun., 1921; partnerships, Apr., 1929.
WILSON, GEOFFREY STUBBS.—ed. Radley Coll.; pol. prob., 1932; asst. supt., pol., Mar., 1933.
WILSON, GEORGE HAROLD SUMNER.—ed. Chigwell Sch., Essex; asst. dist. comm., Coast, Aug., 1914; dist. comm., 1915.
WILSON, G. R. C., M.R.C.S. Eng. (Lond.), Cert. of London S.T.M.—R.A.M.C., May, 1915 to Feb., 1919; Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1919; offr., 1927.

WILSON, SIR HENRY FRANCIS (1908), K.B.E. (1919), C.M.G. (1919), ed. Rugby and Triu Coll., Camb., called to bar (Lincoln's Inn, 1888); the Rt. Hon. J. Chamberlain, M.P., cols., June, 1895; legal asst. in India, went to S. Africa as legal asst. o. staff, Feb., 1900; sec. to administrator, River Colony, 1901; col. sec., J. 1903; as lieut. gov., in 1903, 1904 and 1905; I.C.C., 1903-1907 (rep. O.R.C. o. ret.; was sec. to the Royal Com. 1933.
WILSON, HENRY WILSON (Cantab.).—B. 1896; ed. Upminster Coll., Cambridge; barrister-at-law, S. E. circuit; Kent sessions; Tanganyika Territory, 1929; legal occ., 1933.
WILSON, J. M. A.—B. 1900; dept., N.T., Gold Coast, Schls., Apr., 1931.

WILSON, JOHN MUNCH.—ed. coll., cust., 1895; boarding offr., S.E. dist., 1900; bondkeeper, Lautoka, and clk., 3rd clk., Suva, 1902; 2nd Levuka, 1910; sub collr., 1911; Lautoka, 1911; collr. of collr., Lautoka, 1912; dist. compt., 1913; and govt. storekeeper, 1923; dist. cust., govt. storekeeper, 1930; nom., leg. coun., 1930.
WILSON, LIEUT.-COL. J. V.D.—B. 1876; man., Tobago inspr., Trinidad constab., senr. inspr., 1916; served 1919; dep. inspr.-gen., constab., local forces on several occasions.
WILSON, LIEUT.-COL. ORME, G.C.S.I. (1929), C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O.

ILSON, CAPT. LOGIE WEIR.—B. 1894; ed. Leawick Sch., St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin; al Coll. of Science, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (Dublin); R.A.S.C., Oct., 1914; Irish Rifles, 1917; asst. dist. commr., Leone, July, 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., 1919 to Mar., 1920; ag. dist. commr., dist. commr., Aug., 1926; ag. asst. ch. sec., Apr.-July, 1935; ag. prov. commr., N., June, 1936.

ILSON, MARK.—B. 1896; ed. Mountjoy and Trinity Coll., Dublin; first schol. t. and pol. sci., 1921; B.A. (senr. modr. and medallist), 1921; LL.B., 1922; served with F., 1918-9; called to bar, King's Inns, Dublin (1st cls. hon. at final examn.), 1924; admin. serv., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; Uganda, 1926; ag. admstr.-genl. and off. on various occasions, 1926-9; ag. puisane for various periods, 1933-36; chan., Uganda, 1934-36; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Ica, 1934; senr. mag., 1935-36; puisane judge, ganyika Territory, 1936.
ILSON, PERCIVAL DOUGLAS, M.Inst.C.E.—1887; ed. Swansea Gram. Sch.; asst. nr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1913; exec. engrnr., 4.

VILSON, REGINALD CHARLES, D.Sc., F.G.S., Inst.M.M.—B. 1888; ed. Scotch Coll. and Melbourne Univ.; capt., Royal Welsh Fusiliers R.E., France, 1915-18; geologist, Portuguese Africa, 1911-14; geologist, geol. survey, 1919-27; dir., 1927; publns. on E. Africa and Nigerian Geology.

VILSON, RONALD ERIC.—B. 1898; ed. Faverham and Sandhurst; cadet, F.M.S., Sept., 1917; chd. dist. off., K. Selangor, Jan., 1922; do. t. dept., Aug., 1922; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., May, 1923; passed cadet, Dec., 1923; ag. t. sec. to res., Perak, Feb., 1924; offr. cls. V., Oct., 1924; dist. offr., Jelebu, Jan., 1925; 4th t. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1926; offr. cls. sec. to res., Pahang, Sept., 1928; asst. dist. r., K. Kangar, Oct., 1930; do., Kuantan, Oct., 1932; offr. cls. III., Sept., 1933; sec. to res. N. Umbilan, May, 1934.

WILSON, BRIG.-GEN. SIR SAMUEL HERBERT, C.M.G. (1929), K.C.B., (1927), K.C.M.G. (1923), B.E. (1921), C.R. (1918), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1873; entd. Army, R.E., 1893; capt., 1904; major 13; bt. lieut.-col., 1916; bt. col., 1917; served in Africa, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal, two clasps); European War, 1914-18; Chevalier Legion d'Honneur, 1915; Commandeur de la Couronne Belgien, 1918; Officier Legion d'Honneur, 1918; ench croix de guerre avec palme; Belgian croix de guerre; sec. to overseas defence comte., 1911; gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, 1914; gen. staff r., 1st grade, 1915; brig.-gen. gen. staff, 1916; sec. to overseas defence comte., 19th Nov., 1918; v., Trinidad, 7th Nov., 1921; gov., Jamaica, Sept., 1924; assumed govt., 29th Sept., 1924; perm. under sec. of state for the cols., 6th Aug., 1925; ret., 1933.
WILSON, WILLIAM GLASSFORD WALKER, M.I.L.—B. 1894; ed. Kilmarock Acad.; war serv., 1914-19; asst. loco. supdt., Nigerian rly., 1923; wks. man., 1927; ag. mech. engrnr., 1928.

WILTSHERE, ERIC.—B. 1883; ed. Monmouth Grammar Sch.; articled pupil to county survr. and architect, Middlesex, 1911-14; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-19; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1920; A.M.I.C.E., Oct., 1924; dist. engrnr., Nov., 1929; ag. asst. dir., P.W.D., 1927, 1928, 1930 and 1931.

WILTSHIRE, H. G., M.A., M.B., B.Chir. (Camb.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; exhib., Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (coll. prize); L.S.T.M. certif. with distinc. (2nd place); war serv., 1914-19; France, Gallipoli, Egypt, Palestine (1914 Star with clasp); bacteriological specialist, R.A.M.C., 1916-19; med. offr., Nyasaland, 1924; do., Zanzibar, 1930; pathologist, 1932.
WINSER, CYRIL LEGH, C.M.G., (1928), M.V.O. (1920).—Priv. sec. to gove. of S. Australia since May, 1915.

WINSON, VICTOR HANOTT, B.Sc., A.K.C., M.I.E.E.—B. 1887; ed. Tiffine Sch., Kingston-on-Thames and King's Coll., London (1st cls. hon., elect. engrnr.); 2nd cls. engr., G.P.O., engrng. dept., Oct., 1909; asst. engrnr., July, 1911; supdt., posts and tels., Malacca, Sept., 1913; war serv., Aug., 1917-Apr., 1919; supdt., posts and tels., Jan., 1922; senr. engrnr., posts and tels., S.S. & F.M.S., Oct., 1926; senr. engrnr., posts and tels., Penang, July, 1928; ditto, F.M.S. and S.S., Oct., 1930; asst. engrnr.-in-ch., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1930; engrnr.-in-ch., do., 1933; dir., tels. and phones, and engrnr.-in-ch., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1934.

WINSTEEDT, SIR RICHARD OLAF, K.B.E. (1935), C.M.G. (1926), M.A., Oxon., D. Litt., Oxon.—B. 1878; ed. at Magdalen College School and New Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mods.; 2nd cls. Lit. Hum., M.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1902; passed cadet, 1904; class V., Jan., 1910; sec. to comte., for Malay studies in addn., Sept., 1913; class IV., confirmed dist. offr., Dec., 1913; federal examr. in Malay in addn., Dec., 1913; class III., asst. dir., educn. S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1917; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, June to Aug., 1919, in addn.; cls. II., Nov., 1919; fed. examr. in Malay, S. Stilts., and F.M.S., June 1920; seconded to act as prin., Raffles' Coll., S'pore, June, 1921; offg. as sec. to high comte. in addn.; Fed. examr. in Malay in addn., Mar., 1923; dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., cls. IA, July, 1924; chmn., postal serv. comte. in addn., July, 1924; chmn., tech. educn. comte. in addn., Nov., 1925; rep., F.M.S., govt. at C.O. confce., Apr., to July, 1927; chmn., comte., med. research, S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., Aug., 1928; chmn., Malayan delegation to 4th Pacific serv. cong., Batavia, May, 1929; rep. F.M.S. at C.O. confce., June, 1930; gen. advr., Johore, Oct., 1931; ret., 1935; author of an "English-Malay Dictionary" (3 vols.), a "Malay Grammar" (Clarendon Press), "Colloquial Malay," a "Malay Reader" (Clarendon Press), "Malayan Memories," "Shaman, Sawa and Sufi," a study of Malay magic (London), editor and part author of "Malaya" (London, 1922), and author of various pamphlets published by the F.M.S. govt. on Malay subjects; editor of many Malay folk-tales and (with R. J. Wilkinson, C.M.G.) of Pantun Melayu.

WINSTON, V. A.—B. 1903; ag. 3rd cl., post office, Dominica, 1920; asst. cl., P.W.D., Sept., 1920; jnr. audit. cl., Sept., 1924; ag. audit cl., Jan.-Apr., 1927; senr. cl., treasury, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1934.
WIRASINHA, VICTOR LLOYD, B.A. (Hons.) Lond.—B. 1913; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan. 1935.

WISE, WILFRID, M.Inst.C.E., O.B.E. (1936), B.Sc. (Hons. Lond.), M.I.Mun.Cy.E., A.C.G.I.—B. 1886; ed. pvtly. and City and Guilds of London Cent. Tech. Coll., Univ. of Lond.; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Fiji, May, 1921; ag. commr., wks., Aug., 1923; commr., wks., Jan., 1926; on sp. duty, Tonga, May-July, 1930; commr., cent. road bd., M.L.C.; commr., town planning comte., and beaconing comte., 1930; commr., traffic control bd., 1934; title changed to D.P.W., 1936.

WISEMAN, R. A.—B. 1886; ed. Monmouth Gram. Sch., and Oxford; math. demy, Magdalen Coll., 1905-1909; 2nd cl. math. mods., 1906; 2nd cls. hist., 1908; 2nd cls. jurisprudence, 1909; apptd. after compet. exam., cl., sec.'s dept., Admiralty, Mar., 1910; 2nd cls. cl., C.O., Mch., 1911; sec. to overseas prize disposal comte., Nov., 1914; temporarily served at ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917 to Mar., 1919; sec. to international shipping comte., Feb., 1917; sec. to inter-allied chartering comte., May, 1917; priv. sec. to partly. U.S. of S. for the Cols. (Mr. Amery), 1st Apr., 1919; 1st cls. cl., 1st Jan., 1920; prtn., 1st Apr., 1920; accompanied partly. U.S. of S. for the Cols. (Hon. E. F. L. Wood) on visit to W. Indies, Dec., 1921-Feb., 1922; rep. U.K. govt. at W. Indian confce., Ottawa, June-July, 1925; ast. sec., 18th Sept., 1927; D.O., 1928.
WITHYCOMBE, ROBERT, O.B.E. (1932), M.B.E. (1918), A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1880; dir., elec. and wireless dept., Zanzibar, Dec., 1911; 3rd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar.

WOODEHOUSE, Lieut.-Gen. Sir JOSCELIN HENRAGE, G.C.B. (1913), K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1889), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1852; ed. at R.M.A., Woolwich; commr. in R.A., 1872; served in Zulu war, 1879; Afghan war, 1880; Soudan, 1883-1884; gov. of Eng. frontier, 1887-1894; commd. "I" battery, R.H.A., 1894-1898; with Malakand field force (severely wounded), 1898; in action with Egyptian forces at Argin; infantry div. at Toski; Presidency dist., 1898-1899; Secunderabad, 1900-1; Lahore, 1902; Rawal Pindi, 1903; ag. in cmd. of W. Comd., 1905; Zulu war (medal and clasps); Soudan war (medal, 3 clasps); N.W. Frontier (medal and clasp); 2nd cls. Medjidie; 2nd cls. Osmanieh; Egyptian bronze star; Order of Palm, French Govt.; gov. of Bermuda, 1907-1908; late G.O.C. Northern Army, India.

WOODEHOUSE, R. E., O.B.E., M.D., D.P.H.—B. 1883; ed. pub. schls. and Toronto Univ.; on mil. serv., Canada, France and England, 1914-19; prov. dist. med. offr., Fort William and Port Arthur in 1912 and again in 1919; exec. sec., Canadian Tuberculosis Assoc., 1921; dep. min. dept. of pensions and national health, Dom. of Canada, 1933.

WOODEMAN, GUY STANLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1909; office ast. to govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1910; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1912; ast. commr. of excise attld., the office, excise commr., Apr., 1913; ast. commr., excise, Cent. Div., Jan., 1914; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Apr., 1915; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1916; ditto, Colombo, Oct., 1921; ditto, Matare, Mar., 1926; cust., Apr., 1925; dep. commr., 1926; commr., excise, Sep., 1926; S.P., Nov., 1930; pol. coll., Colombo port coman., Nov., 1933; sec., Dec., 1934-Mar., 1935.

WOLFE, HENRY, O.B.E. (1935), Cornell Univ., U.S.A.—B. 1882; of agr., S. Africa, 1916; dep. dir. Territory, 1920; ag. dir., Mar. 1921 and Mar. to Nov., 1924; L.S., 1916-17; dep. dir. agr. (plant path.), 1931; ag. dir., agr., 1933.
WOLFFER, PHILIPPE REYNIER, Glasgow Acad.—cl., land dept., 1920; ag. office supt., 1917; land assessor titles, doc. and coast land titles, titles of enemy property, July-Nov., 1921.
WOMACK, OSWALD CRANE.—B. 1892; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Univ.; temp. capt., R.A.S.C. and R.A.C.; ast. supt., surveys, Ceylon, 1928; survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1929; do., 1932.

WOOD, BERNARD READ, Tonbridge Schl., Sid. Suss. Coll.; dipl. in forestry; ast. conserv. serv., 1914; dep. conserv., 1919; forest, Br. Guiana, 1925; conserv. forest serv., Oct., 1927; conserv. Guiana, Jan., 1928; mem. Hindi and Urdu, Apr., 1928; ast. coun., Aug., 1928; comm., loco., 30; offl. mem., exec. coun., Aug., 1931; J.P., Aug., 1931.
WOOD, FRANCIS WILLIAM, Queen Elizabeth Gram. Schls., cl. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921.

WOOD, HARRY EDWIN, Manchester Grammar Schl. and Outer (B.sc., 1902, M.sc., 1904) of physics, Manchester Univ.; Transvaal meteorological dept.; Union observatory, Johannesburg; German E. Africa campaign; astronomer, Jan., 1928.
WOOD, JAMES HERBERT, etc.—B. 1897; ast. engr., 1921; ag. senr. exec. engr., 1934.

WOOD, JOHN, M.I.C.E., schl. Timaru, and Timaru, N.Z. pub. wks., dept., 1914; ags. rest. engr., Nelson, 1914; Arthur's Pass tunnel com., ch. Westport dist., 1909; engnr., 1912; inseng. engr. for N.Z. ch., 1934; under-secr. and engnr., and comm., main bldg., 1934.

WOOD, LESLIE WILLIAM, merchant Taylors' math. trip., 1st cls. math. trip., 2nd cls. math. trip., pt. 2; B.A., 1914; Gold Coast, 1915; 1914-20; ast. col. sec., ast. sec. of couns. for various purposes, senr. ast. col. sec., 1931 in 1931 and 1932.
WOOD, CAPT. RICHARD B. 1889; E. African seconded, K.A.R., 1914.

GOODHOUSE, JOHN LESLIE.—B. 1892; ed. merchant Taylor's Schl.; asst. transport offr., rank of lieut., Uganda Transport Corps, 1914; ment. in despatches for service in German Africa, Nov., 1916; asst. dist. commr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1920; dist. June, 1926.

OODING, JOHN CONRAD—B. 1901; ed. prison Coll., and Codrington Coll. Barbados, Durham; class. hon., 1922; M.A., 1927; asst. mast., Gram. Sch., St. Kitts, Aug., 1933; ag. headm., Montserrat, and St. Kitts, various periods, 1927-33; mem., Middle Temple, 1933; ag. postmr., St. Kitts, Dec., 1935; sr. and provost marshal St. Kitts and adml. dists. C.D.H.K., Dec., 1935; mem., publishers' comte, and income tax commr., May, 1936; ag. headm., Gr. Sch., St. Kitts, Sept., 1936.

OODMAN, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1872; ed. schl., Winchester Coll. and St. Mary's Hosp.; L.S.C., L.R.C.P., L.S.A.; attd. to R.A.M.C. Fort Pitt Hosp., Chatham, during S. African War; London Schl. of Trop. Med. exam., Br. adurias, 1910-15; med. offr., hosp., gaol, etc., Hong Kong; seconded for serv. in war, 1918-19; capt., R.A.M.C. (T.), in charge of military company, Salonica and Constantinople; charge, Belize hosp., Jan. to June, 1928; Bahamas med. offr., Out Islands, June to Sept., 1930; ag. res. surg. and radiologist, Bahamas, Oct., 1928-Aug., 1929; ag. dir. med. services, g. to Nov., 1929.

WOODWARD, WILLIAM, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1892; ed. Nuneaton Gram. Sch., Birmingham Univ.; served Gt. War, 1915-18; engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921; ag. exec. engr., waterworks, 1930 and 1932-5; ag. exec. engr., waterworks maint., 1936.

WOOLLEY, CHARLES CAMPBELL, O.B.E. (34), M.C.—B. 1893; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; d. Kandy Kach., June, 1921; ag. office to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Sept., 1922; 4th col. sec., Feb., 1924; 3rd asst. col. sec., Apr., 1925; temp. attd. C.O., 1926 to June, 1926; addnl. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1926; 3rd asst. l. sec., Oct., 1926; asst. govt. agt., N'Eliya, May, 1927; asst. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun., pt., 1928; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., exec. un., Nov., 1930; sec. to gov., July, 1931; col. o., Jamaica, Aug., 1935.

WORKMAN, JOHN EDWARD—B. 1897; served Worcestershire Regt., King's Liverpool Regt., F.C., R.A.F., 1915-1918 (Mesopotamia, India and Egypt); dist. inspr. and intell. offr., I.C., 1920, platoon commdr., R. Ulster spl. instab., 1921; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, Jan., 1925; commr., Jan., 1933.

WORLEY, NEWNHAM ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1913; arrister-at-law, Inner Temple; cadet, F.M.S., ov., 1914; asst. contr. of lab., Kampar, July, 1917; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, May, 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, Negri Sembilan and ag. ep. contr. of lab., Seremban, Aug., 1920; asst. rot. of Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1921; asst. dir. of ducn. (Chinese), F.M.S., June, 1922; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1926; asst. dir. educn. (Chinese), F.M.S. nd S.S., in 1927 and 1930; 1st mag., Ipoh, Nov., 1931, offr. cls. II; registr., sup. ct., K. Lumpur, May, 1932; D.P.P., S'pore, Mar., 1934; offr. cls., IB, Jan., 1935; solr.-gen., S'pore, Mar., 1936.

WORLEDGE, JOHN LEONARD.—B. 1896; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; active service in France, Sept., 1914 to Sept., 1918; ment. in despatches; Ireland, 1919; asst. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1920; asst. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, May 1920 to Feb., 1921; ag. senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1923; senr. asst. audr., Nov., 1925; seconded to Seychelles, Oct., 1925 to Jan., 1926; on sp. duty, Seychelles, Oct.-Nov., 1929; audr., Br. Guiana, May, 1932; audr.-gen., Jamaica, 1936.
WORRELL, CECIL THEOPHILUS WILFRED EVERARD.—B. 1894; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1916; mag., St. Patrick, Trinidad, Jan., 1932; do., St. George East, Jan., 1934.

WORSLEY, RALPH MARCUS MEABURN, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1910; attd. Colombo kach., Dec., 1910; attd., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1911; extra office asst. to govt. agt., W. Prov., Oct., 1911; asst. land settm. offr., Jan., 1912; office asst. to govt. agt., Central Prov., July, 1914; asst. censor, Dec., 1914; on mily. duty, Feb., 1915; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1921; asst. attlnt. offr., Jan., 1924; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, May, 1924; asst. govt. agt., Matara, Jan., 1929; govt. agt., W. Prov., July, 1933; ag. land commr. in addn., Sept.-Dec., 1933; ag. dep. ch. sec. in addn., Oct.-Dec., 1934 and Nov. 1935-Apr., 1936.

WORSLEY, RICHARD ROBERT LE GETT, Ph.D. (Lond.), D.I.C., A.R.C.S., B.Sc.—B.1899; ed. St. Paul's and R. Coll. of Sci.; R.E. O.C.B., 1918-1919; sub. dir., chemv. sect., miny. of agr., Egypt, 1926; biochemist, E. African agr. research sta., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

WORTLEY, EDWARD JOCKLYN, C.M.G. (35), O.B.E. (1926), M.B.E.—B. 1884; ed. Jamaica Coll.; course in agric. and agric. chem. at Jamaica govt. lab. and sp. course at the Ontario agric. coll.; fellow of the chem. socy., 1900; jnr. asst. chem., Jamaica, 1902; asst. chem., 1905; lect. in agric. sci., 1906; headmstr., govt. farm schl. and stock farm, 1909; commr. for Jamaica, Canadian Nat. Exhibn., Sept., 1910, and Sept., 1912; dir. of agr., Bermuda, 1913-20; food commr., 1917; hon. sec., internat. comte, on potato industry, 1919; visited Canary Is. on behalf of Bermuda govt., 1920; dir. of agr., Nyasaland, Dec., 1920; dir. agr., Trinidad, 1930; author of "Companion to Blackie's Tropical Readers," "Poultry Diseases," and "Foods and Food Products of Jamaica."

WRIGHT, MAJOR ANDREW BARKWORTH, C.B.E. (1932), M.A., M.C. (1917), Bar (1918), R. of O.—B. 1895; ed. Haileybury and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (schol.); served in European War with Suffolk Regt., 1914-19; major, 1918; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, June, 1922; asst. priv. sec. to O.A.G., June to Oct., 1923; ag. priv. sec. to high commr., and ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., on various occasions, 1924-26; ag. col. sec. for various periods, 1927-36; ch. asst. sec., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1930; ag. commr., Mar.-June, 1932; ag. treas., Aug.-Nov., 1934; ag. Br. del. of Evkaf, 1934; admvte. offr.: ch. asst. sec., Jan., 1935.

WRIGHT, ARTHUR ALBAN, C.M.G. (37), B.A.—B. 1887; ed. St. Edmund's Schl., Canterbury; open exhibn., St. John's Coll., Oxford, 1906; lieutenant, 3rd Bn. Bedfordshire Regt. (S.R.), 1909; 2nd cls. bns. schl., mod. hist., Oxford, 1910; B.A., 1910; cadet, Fiji, 1912; ag. sub-inspr., constab., 1912; passed cadet, 1914; 2nd grade dist. commr., 1915; dist. commr., Nadi, 1915; do., Colo East, 1916; on mily. duty, 1917; 2nd lieut., 5th Bn. Rifle Brigade, 1917; on active serv., France, 1917; dist. comnr., Macuata, 1920; ditto (1st grade), Nadroga and prov. comnr., Nadroga and Colo West, 1922; ditto, Colo North, 1923; ag. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1927; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1928; nom. mem., leg. coun., 1929; lat. ast. col. sec., Jan., 1931; sec. for native affrs., Mar., 1932; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. col. sec. in 1932, 1933, 1934 and 1935; gov't. dep. on several occasions, 1932-36; ag. gov't., Mar.-Aug., 1935; admstr. and col. sec., St. Vincent, June, 1936.

WRIGHT, CHARLES HAROLD.—M.A., F.I.C., F.C.S.; B. 1880; scholar, Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Nat. Sc. Tripos), 1902; M.A. 1916; ast. analyst and science master, Trinidad, Apr., 1908; ag. gov't. analyst and prof. of chemistry, Trinidad, on two occasions; agric. chemist, Fiji, Mar., 1914; ag. dist. comnr., Kadavu, July-Aug., 1918; gov't. chem., Fiji, Jan., 1920; senr. agr. chem., Nigeria, June, 1923.

WRIGHT, OWEN FRANKLIN.—B. 1887; R.I.C. depot, 1908; sub-inspr., Jamaica constab., Nov., 1908; 3rd cls. inspr., June, 1911; 2nd cls. inspr., Aug., 1919; 1st cls. inspr., June, 1924; inspr., gen., pol., Nov., 1932.

WRIGHT-NOOTH, CAPT. RODNEY GEORGE, M.C. (1916), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. St. Paul's Sch., stud., engng. faculty, King's Coll., London, 1904-08; 2nd lieut., R.E. (S.R.), 1909; engnr. on survey and constrn. of Magadi rly. and water wks., 1911-14; R.E., 1914-22; served, Kenya, German E. Africa and Jubaland; severely wounded, twice ment. in desps., M.C.; div. offr., R.E., Londonderry and Enniskillen Divs., Ireland, Apr., 1920 to Apr., 1922; capt., Reg. Army, R. of O., 1922; supt. pub. wks., Antigua, July, 1922; mem., leg. coun., Antigua, Aug., 1922; ast., D.P.W., B. Guiana, Dec., 1926; ag. D.P.W. in 1927, 1929 and 1931; dep. D.P.W., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1931; ag. D.P.W. in 1933, 1934 and 1935; res. engrn. in ch., constrn. of Lusaka, Sept., 1933-Aug., 1935.

WUPPERMAN, RUDOLF CARL, M.B., Ch.B., Edin.—B. 1883; entered med. serv., Trinidad, Oct., 1909; ast. res. surg., colonial hosp., Port of Spain, Oct., 1914; res. surg., col. hosp., May, 1923; dist. med. offr., 1929.

WYATT, ALBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1900; ed. Leamington Coll., Ireland, Ellesmere Coll., Shropshire, and in Hanover, Munich and Edinburgh; Malayan pol. serv., 1911; ast. comnr., pol., Selangor, 1915; 2nd. lieut., Rifle Brigade, 1916; served in France 1917-18; comdt., pol. depot, Kuala Lumpur, 1920; comnr., pol., Trengganu, 1923; dep. comnr., pol., and ag. dir., crim. intell., F.M.S., 1928; comnr., pol., Johore, 1929; dir., crim. intell., S.S., June, 1931; dir., sp. br., S.S., June, 1931 and Nov., 1933; offg. ch. pol. offr., Perak, Mar., 1933; dir., sp. branch, S.S., Nov., 1933; senr., supt., and senr. dep. comnr., Malayan pol. serv., 1936.
WYNNE, COMDR. WALTER ROBERT MORANT, R.N. (Retd.).—B. 1885; King's harbr. mast., Bermuda, 1924-26; harbr. and shipping mast., Barbados, 1928.

WYNNE-JONES, EDWARD IRVINE.—B. 1898; ed. Oxford Univ. of Wales; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1919; secretariat, Nov., 1922; passed final exam., Feb., 1923; insp., May, 1923; ditto, South, Dec., 1923; secretariat, July, 1928; pol. mag., extra asst. col. sec., Nov., 1928; sec. and clk. of comrs., in 1929; North, 1930; 2nd pol. mag., Nov., 1930; pol. mag., Keruwa, 1931-33.

YATES, GEO. W.—B. 1873; ed. and high schls.; ent. cir. serv., sec. to various mins., from Feb., 1894; accompanied Hon. F. Cochrane, Ont., to Ottawa on forestry and mines, to Ottowa on his portfolio of min. of rly. and other govt.; cont. priv. sec. of min. of rly. until formation of union govt.; became ch. priv. sec. to Sir Ross, deputy minister of railways and harbours, 1920.

YATES, JOSEPH MARTYN ST. JOHN.—B. 1878; ed. Winchester Coll. and Trinity, Cambridge; called to the bar, June, 1903; northern circuit, 1903-15; H.M. stip. mag., Mar., 1919; ag. stip. mag., Mar., 1920; ag. ch. just., Feb.-Mar., 1921; circuit mag., Mar., 1921 to Sept., 1921; for serv. as ag. astt. to atty.-gen., 1922; res. mag., Jamaica, 1923; served in 1923 and 1924; ag. judge, Kingston, 1924 to Oct., 1925; ag. puisne judge, Eastern Circuit, 1925; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1926; puisne judge, Gold Coast, ch. just. on various occasions, 1926-27.
YATES, WILLIAM STEWART.—ed. Coatham Sch., and Jesus Cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1926; dist. offr., Apr., 1930; ag. res. mag., Sept., 1932.
YOUNG, ALFRED ERNEST.—L.R.C.P. (Lond.), L.D.S. (Eng.), offr., Basutoland, 1924.

YOUNG, SIR ALFRED KAY.—(1925), K.C., B.A.—Ed. Mag., called to the bar, In. Temp. clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. H.M., ag. dist. mag., Toledo dist., 1924; to Sir A. Maloney, and clk. of sec. to admstr. of the govt., Trinidad, 1908; ch. just., 1912; admstr. govt., Apr. to Nov., 1912; atty.-gen., Fiji, Mar., 1915; and Mar.-just., Leeward Is., Feb., 1923; ret. June, 1929.

YOUNG, CAPT. SIR G.C.M.G. (1916), K.C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1854; ed. Edin. Acad., Rup., comdt., mil. pol., Ky. comnr., Paphos, Nov., 1879; ditto, Fam. survey and prin. forest, coun., 1892; ch. sec., 1892 to Dec., 1895, Jan. to June, 1896; mission to St. Vincent, Nov., 1902; ool. sec., admtd. govt., Feb. to Oct., 1910; gov't., S. Sith, govt., Sept., 1911; tes and for Brunei and Brit. Agt. for N. Borneo Sarawak; ret. Nov., 1919.

YOUNG, HON. SIR FREDERICK WILLIAM, K.T., CH. (1918), LL.B.—M.H.A., S. Australia, 1909; assnr. of crown lands and immigrn., 1912-14; gen. for S. Australia, 1915-18; mem. of tee. for stimt. of ex-service men within the pire, 1917; M.P. (Co. U.), Swindon, 1918.

YOUNG, HENRY ALFRED, K.C.—Ed. Edin. iv.; called to the bar (Scotland), July, 1899; crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 22nd June, 1896; asst. atty.-gen., 1913; senr. res. mag., 7; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions; pol. g., Nigeria, Jan., 1922; puisne and circuit ge., Sierra Leone, 1930.

YOUNG, MAJOR SIR HUBERT WINTHROP, C.M.G. (1934), Kt. Bach. (1932), C.M.G. (1923), S.O. (1918).—B. 1885; ed. Eton and R. Mily. ad., Woolwich; comsmd., R. Garrison Arty., M.; Indian Army, 1908; capt., 1913; major, 1919; asst. censor, Army headqrs., Simla, July, 1914 to Feb., 1915; N.W. Frontier, Feb.-Dec., 1915 (1914-15 Star); asst. polit. offr., Mesopotamia, c., 1915 to Aug., 1917; dep. dir., local resources, Mesopotamia, Aug., 1917 to Mar., 1918 (ment. in sps.); gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, Hejaz opera., Mar. to Oct., 1918 (ment. in desps., D.S.O., 1 class Order of Nahda); pres. local resources, Damascus, Oct., 1918; seconded to F.O., b., 1919 to Mar., 1921; seconded to C.O., Middle East Dept. as asst. sec., Mar., 1921 to Dec., 1926; accompanied Mr. Churchill to Cairo nce., Apr., 1921; visited Palestine and Iraq sp. serv., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; sp. serv., usanne for Turkish peace treaty., Jan., 1922; d at Brussels and Geneva for Iraq frontier spute on various occasions during 1925 and 26; ret. from army and apptd. col. sec., braltar, Feb., 1927; couns. to high coman., sq., Sept., 1929; ag. high comsnr., on four occasions; envoy extraordinary and min. plenit. Baghdad, Oct., 1932; gov. and mmdr.-in-ch., Nyasaland Prot., 1932; do., northern Rhodesia, Mar., 1934.

YOUNG, HON. SIR JAMES ALEXANDER, C.V.O. (1935).—Mem. N.Z. parl., 1911-12; min. of health, 1926-28; min. of industries and commerce, 1928; min. of health, min. of immigrn. in coalition miny., 1931-35; min. internal affrs., 1933-35.

YOUNG, JAMES ANDERSON, M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrews and Edinburgh Unive.; late house surg., Derbyshire R. Infirmary; served with R.A.M.C. 'F.', 1915-19; granted hon. rank of major on signation; med. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1919; st. bacteriologist, Nigeria, 1927.

YOUNG, SIR MARK ATCHISON, K.C.M.G. (1934), C.M.G. (1931).—B. 1886; ed. Eton and ing's Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. r., Nov., 1909; off. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., ug., 1910; ditto, Kandy, Nov., 1911; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1913; 4th asst. col. sec., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd ditto, Feb., 1914; 2nd ditto and clk. of leg. coun., Sep., 1914; 3rd asst. sec., Oct., 1914; on mil. duty, 1915-1919; 3rd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1920; 2nd asst. col. sec., July, 1920; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Mar., 1922; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., exec. coun., Mar., 1923; col. sec., Sierra Leone, 1928; ag. gov. in 1929 and 1930; ch. sec., Palestine, 1930; admtd. govt. for various periods, 1930-32; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Barbados, June, 1933.

YOUNG, W. A., M.A., B.M., B.Ch. (Oxon.), B.Sc. (War.), Lond., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), cert., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med. (with distinction, 2nd place), late exhib., Jesus Coll., Oxford.—War serv., France, 1916-18; clin. asst., children and ear depts., St. Thomas's hosp., 1922; R.M.O., Ealing hosp., 1922-23; East Africa med. serv. (Zanzibar), Sept., 1923; ag. R.S.O., Zanzibar, 1927, 1930 and 1933; M.O.H. and ag. senr. med. offr., 1935; S.M.O., Tanganyika Territory, 1936.

YOUNG, WILLIAM, M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1892; med. offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1921; med. offr., K. Lipis, Nov., 1922; ditto, Taiping, Oct., 1924; ditto, Larut, May, 1925; ditto, B. Gajah, Mar., 1927; ditto, B. Pahang, May, 1928; ag. sr. med. offr., Pahang, Nov., 1928; med. offr., Larut, Aug., 1929; ag. ch. med. offr., soc. hyg., F.M.S., May, 1930; ag. State med. and health offr., Pahang, Jan., 1932, ag. dep. do., Selangor, Jan., 1933; ch. med. offr.; Malacca, Oct., 1935.
YOUNG, WILLIAM ARTHUR, H.Sc., M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M. and H.—B. 1890; prof. of bacteriology, Coll. of Medicine, S'pore, Mar., 1927.

YOUNG, SIR WILLIAM DOUGLAS, K.B.E. (1919); C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. at Charterhouse; clk. in govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1877; ch. clk., 1889; ag. priv. sec. on several occasions, 1877-1893, to govrs. of Br. Guiana, Trinidad, and G. Coast; ag. asst. govt. sec., Br. Guiana, 1892-3-4-5: ag. govt. sec., Dec., 1892; ag. govt. act. N.W. dist., 1895; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, 1895; ag. col. sec., 1896-7-8, 1900-01; admtd. govt., Mauritius, Aug., 1897; comsnr., Turks and Caicos Islands, 1901; admtr., Dominica, 19th Apr., 1906; ag. gov'r., Leeward Is., 1909; adminstr., St. Lucia, 1913; ag. gov', Windward Is., May to Dec., 1914; gov', Falkland Is., Dec., 1914; assumed gov', May, 1915; ret., 1920.
YOUNG, WILLIAM MARTIN.—B. 1890; N. British Rlwy., 1905-20; dep. ch. acot., Ceylon govt. rlwy., 1921; ch. acot., Palestine rlwys., 1927.
YU WAN, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1890; inspr., vernacular schls., Hong Kong, 1922; mem., bd. of examns., 1923.
ZIAI, MUSTAPHA FUAD.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1922; inspr., Cyprus mily. pol., Nov., 1906; judge, dist. ct., Apr., 1913; puisne judge, Oct., 1927.
